ref: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
text: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ n abɔ i nu ka, “Sɔnɔ n bo i siiri i aŋunfɔ ni.” Na Yesu ase i ka, “Kɔ na a kɔ yo sɔ.” Yesu ni Marata ni Mariya jɔrɛ

ref: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
text: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n wo kɔ su ni, na ba kɔ aju miɛ be su, bara be woori dɔ, bu fere i ka Marata, na wɔ de Yesu wɔfɔ i dika.

ref: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
text: Marata la i niɛma be, bu fere i ka Mariya, na wɔ tana Yesu bi, na i suti kabo Yesu kere barasu ni.

ref: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
text: Ama, na wɔ yo Marata ahoresaaki kabo yiri ŋgumi wo juma yo su ni, nyini ti, na wɔ ba ase Yesu ka, “M Mibiɛ, u cɛcɛ wo má nu ka m niɛma n ayaki juma n kɛrɛ aji mini ŋgumi saa su, nzɛn sɛ? Se i ka i ba buka m.”

ref: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
text: Na Mibiɛ n abɔ i nu ka, “M danfo Marata, a fa juma beberebe ata u ŋu nyi, ye a suwaraki a,

ref: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
text: ama, deke kun n kpein be akpiɛ u su a. Mariya yiri, deke bo i ti kpa tara i kɛrɛ ye wɔ yi i n a, na be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro de i dɔ.”

ref: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
text: Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, am sere deke bo am koro i Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na i má am. Am niɛniɛ deke bo am koro i dɔ, na am ŋú i. Am bobo anɔ ni, na i téke i ma am.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
text: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ, haari ni bo am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, am si aceeri kpam bo am fa ce am nbaam, nzu ti ye nzɛn am sereeri am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni, i koro ma má am Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a?” Kabo minɛm ŋa Yesu ni Sitana dɛɛ ti kun ni

ref: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
text: Na yiri n bo i wo sɔrɔ n ase ka, ‘Má a miɛ m. Ma tu anɔ awie, na m nbaam da a. M koro yasu má na m má u pui.’

ref: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti nbaasim ni, ŋma ye i wa sere i jombaa ye i mi kéte na i fá woo ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.

ref: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Saŋga bo am ba susere Nyɛmɛ, am se ka o, ‘Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ma minɛm bu u duma casi ni. Ma u fɛmɛya n bra.

ref: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
text: Fa ya satiɛ ce ya, dama yɛrɛ gusu fa ce bo bu do ya ŋgasi n wɔ. Má a ma deke be yo ya na ya wura satiɛ nu.’”

ref: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sere deke be Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i má i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i niɛniɛ deke be, i ŋú i wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bobo anɔ ni, i téke i ma i.

ref: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
text: I ti sɔ lɛɛ, kabo sa n bo i yo tuuri Jona n kereeri Ninɛva­fɔm ka Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i ni, sɔ ye sa n bo i bá tu mini Adamande Wa n gusu kére ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm ka Nyɛmɛ asunma m a.

ref: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
text: Saŋga bo Yesu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, bara be wo jama n nu, na wɔ se Yesu ka, “Tere­fiɛ wo bara n bo i wuuri u na i maari u nyufini n dɔ.”

ref: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
text: Saŋga be na Yesu afɔn ayi ajini afite sɔnɔ be ŋu, wɔ yo i bobo. Ajini n fite yakiiri sɔnɔ n sɔ ni, na wɔ koro ajɔjɔ. Na jama n ayo alekutura.

ref: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
text: Na nbem gusu akoro ka bu sɔ i ŋgɛɛ. Nyini ti, na ba se i ka i yo alekutura sa be bo i kere ka i yiko n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
text: Ama, Yesu siiri bu sunsunni n sɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, “Fɛmɛya kɛrɛ bo i nya kpakpaakiiri nu wieeri, nyini fɛmɛya ni ká ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Awuru gusu kɛrɛ bo i kpakpaakiiri nu wieeri, nyini awuru ni sándi wɔ.

ref: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
text: Na nzɛn Sitana dɛɛ fɛmɛya n nya kpaakiiri nu wieeri, i kóro jina a? Ai, i kóro jina má. Deke bo i ti na m se sɔ n la ka, am ŋa Bɛlazɛbuli ma m yiko ye m koro fa fɔn yi ajinim a.

ref: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
text: Tɔ, ye nzɛn am ŋa Bɛlazɛbuli maari m yiko, ye m fa fɔn yi ajinim a, ye ŋma maari am dɛɛ minɛm ni yiko ye bu bita fɔn yi bu n a? Nyini ti, kabo am minɛm yoori ni, nyini kere ka am la má nahɔrɛ.

ref: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
text: Nzɛn sɔnɔ faŋga­fɔ nya faari i biɛsɔya neŋge kɛrɛ jina ŋmindaari i dika, pui koro yo má i awuru n nu neŋge be.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
text: Ambɛrɛ Farasi­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am koro ka am tana kpirikoro tanaa­birim wɔ, am tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, na am koro ka minɛm yo am ansɛ gɔɔsu wɔ, na bu ma am jirima.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
text: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n kun ase Yesu ka, “Ya Kere­fɔ, nzɛn a seeri sɔ, a kpiɛ nzukɔ asaŋga ya kɛrɛ mmɔɔ lɛɛ.”

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
text: Nzɛn ajini nya fite yakiiri sɔnɔ, i kɔ jeŋgbe bara yɛ boro kpantain nu wɔ, deke bo nzue wo má, na i kɔ niɛniɛ woo­biri bo i tana yi i ŋumi. Nzɛn wɔ nya má woo­biri, nyini saŋga, a ti ka i ŋa, ‘Ma m kpie m nyi kɔ m woo­biri dawa ni.’

ref: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
text: Na i kaaki kɔ, i kɔ ŋu ka ba kpara dika n nyunyumi, asɛsɛ deke kɛrɛ su.

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
text: Na i nyá kɔ fa ajinim nso, bo bu ti tiɛ tara yiri ni i tii, na bu kɛrɛ bá wura woo­biri dawa n nu. Nyini saŋga, a ŋu ka sɔnɔ n woo­wa ayo tiɛ atara kabo i yo ti dawa ni.”

ref: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
text: Ama, na Yesu ase i ka, “Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu de i ni dɔ a.” Kabo Yesu keteeri ka i yo má alekutura sa bo bu koro i ni

ref: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
text: Na Farasi­fɔ n abirisi kabo Yesu aŋmisi má i ŋu ka, ye i sudi diire ni.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
text: Ambɛrɛ Farasi­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro, dama am yi am jakam ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, haari ni am maakunm, ni ŋgɛtɛm, ni nvan­nvanm kɛrɛ, ama, am yo má nahɔrɛ sam, na am kaaki koro má Nyɛmɛ. Wɔ yo daka ka am yo nyini sam saŋga kɛrɛ, na am fa jaka yii­wam buka su.

ref: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
text: Saŋga bo minɛm sutiɛn bu nɔɔ bara yɛ Yesu sɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ. Am koro ka m yo alekutura sa na am ŋu i wɔ, ama, m yó má i, má ka alekutura sa n bo Nyɛmɛ yo yoori i maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Jona ni ŋgumi.

ref: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
text: Fɛmɛ bara be yo fiteeri haari i bɔsu Seba wɔ, na i ba ti Fɛmɛ Solomon dɛɛ ŋgɔnlɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa nɔaniɛ ni. Jina cɛɛn, yiri bá yasu jina na i járaki ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm nahɔrɛ su, dama kisa, m suse am wɔ ka, mini tara Fɛmɛ Solomon, ama, am ade má m nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
text: Jona yo kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kereeri Ninɛva­fɔm na ba kaaki bu wo. Jina cɛɛn, bɛrɛ bá yasu jina na bu járaki ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm, dama kisa, m suse am wɔ ka, mini tara Jona, ama, am akete ka am kaaki má am wo.” Kabo Yesu kereeri anyumbaa kpa jɔrɛ ni

ref: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Sɔnɔ koro so má fitana su na i fa se fiɛɛ­wa nu, wara i fa gaŋga butu su. Ama, i fa taka deke su aŋgoro wɔ, adena minɛm bo bu ba wura awuru n nu n koro ŋu asi.

ref: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
text: Ya nyu­mbaa ni ti ka fitana ma ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n a. Nzɛn u nyu­mbaa nya ti kpa, u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ ta wein n wɔ lɛɛ. Ama, nzɛn i nya ti má kpa, u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ wo awosin nu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
text: Ambɛrɛ sinnzinm ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka Nyɛmɛ yoori deke kɛrɛ kunnu ni i sin a.

ref: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
text: Ambɛrɛ bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am yo faŋ­kunmam dɔŋgu fa ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ, bɛrɛ bo am nam yo kuunri bu ni.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
text: Saŋga bo Yesu jɔjɔ wieeri sɔ ni, na Farasi­fɔ be asere i ka i bra na bu di diire i dika, na Yesu akɔ awura ka i di diire ni.

ref: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
text: Na Mibiɛ n ase i ka, “Ambɛrɛ Farasi­fɔm, am ŋmisi am kɔpu ni am taalɛ sin wɔ, ama, am kunnu ayi ni anyii sa­yoo­wam, ni sunsunni tiɛm wɔ.

ref: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
text: Má nzɛn am fa deke bo i wo am kɔpu ni am taalɛ kunnu ma yaari­fɔm, má deke kɛrɛ bo am yo i, má i yo casi ma am wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
text: Am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ti ka faŋ­kunmam bo bu la má nyaari see­wa be n wɔ, na minɛm nati su na bu si má ka faŋ­kunma lɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am cici sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri sɔ minɛm wɔ, ama, ambɛrɛ ni am tii soro má su ka am fa am saa kan i.

ref: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
text: Nyini kere ka ambɛrɛ ni am tii saa woori sa n bo am nam yo yoori i n nu lɛɛ. Bɛrɛ kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a, na ambɛrɛ ata bu faŋ­kunmam.

ref: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
text: Nyini ti, ye Nyɛmɛ Alecira­fɔ n seeri ka, ‘M súnma m nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm, ni m jaramasam bu dɔ, na bu kún nbem, na bu kére nbem wuwuruwa.’

ref: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
text: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ cín ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm sui fa kɔ kabo am nam yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm, fiteeri durunya n bumboori fa ba ju haari ni kisa ni.

ref: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
text: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am akere má minɛm Nyɛmɛ atin, kabo i ti ye ni ka am kere bu ni, na ambɛrɛ ni am tii akete ka am su má i atin n su, ye am akanda bo bu koro ka bu su i su ni.”

ref: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
text: Saŋga bo Yesu yasuuri nyini dika n nu ni, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n afa i kpɔ agaya, na ba bisa i bisaa­wam beberebe,

ref: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
text: Saŋga n bo nyini n kɛrɛ sukɔ su ni, na jama beberebe aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ haari bu kɔ sutiɛntiɛn bu beŋgu ŋu. Na Yesu abo i bu ka i jɔjɔ, na wɔ du mɔ ase i susu­fɔm n ka, “Am si am ŋu sa ni Farasi­fɔm dɛɛ kpɔnɔ ayiri n bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ nu na i yasu ni. M suni bu dɛɛ burukɔɔ­nu sa­yoo­wam wɔ.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
text: Nzɛn wuram bo bu wo bɛrɛ nyuma, na ahunma a ŋú ka ba kpiɛ bu na bu yara bu, ye Nyɛmɛ niɛ bu dika, nzu ti ye i níɛ má ambɛrɛ bo am tara wuram co n a? Ambɛrɛ, am la má yarada fieo.

ref: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
text: Má am yo sɔ, ama, am kpini Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n busu ka, na i nyá ba fa nyini dekem n buka am su.”

ref: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “M danfom, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am soro minɛm bo bu koro kun aŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na nyini sin, bu koro yo má pui kun ni.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
text: Haari am tii­ŋmiɛ mmɔɔ bo i wo am tii su ni, Nyɛmɛ si i kɛrɛ nɔ. Nyini ti, má am soro sɛrɛ. Am la anyunuyi Nyɛmɛ dɔ tara njɔrɛm beberebe.”

ref: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
text: Am tɔ am deke bo am la i, na am fa ŋwaa n ma yaari­fɔm. Am kpini ajɛkɛ see­biri bo i saaki má n busu. Am fa ajɛkɛ bo i kpiɛ má su n fa se Nyɛmɛ dika n nu, dɔ ye awiefɔ koro wura wɔ má i a, na watarawam gusu koro muka má i.

ref: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
text: Dama nyini saŋga n ba juuri, Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni kére am nɔaniɛ kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka am jɔjɔ i a.” Miɛɛnu fa kɔ sɔnɔ neŋge­fɔ sinnzin be su

ref: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, “M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kan kereeri minɛm ka i ti m susu­fɔ, mini Adamande Wa n gusu bá kan kere Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm n ka i mi ti m dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
text: Saŋga bo bu ba faari am baari bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu, wara nfɛmɛm ni nfaŋga­fɔm nyunu ka bu di am jɔrɛ, má am ma am ahore saaki fa kɔ kabo am jɔjɔ barasu.

ref: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
text: Sɔnɔ be woori jama n nu, na wɔ se Yesu ka, “M Kere­fɔ, se m gɔrɔ ka i kpaaki aja bo ya si ayaki i ama ya n nu, na i fa m dɛɛ ma m.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Wɔrɔ, ŋma ayi m, ka m di am jɔrɛ na m kpaaki am aja nu ma am a?”

ref: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
text: Na wɔ sunsun i tii nu na wɔ se ka, ‘M la má deke bo m fa ahin diire n kɛrɛ se. Ye m yó sɛ wɔ?’

ref: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
text: Na wɔ se ka, ‘Kabo m yó yaa, m sún m bondom kɛrɛ ji wɔ, na m tá ŋgbiŋgbirim, na m fá m diire n ni neŋge kɛrɛ bo m la bu n wura nu.

ref: Life is more than food or clothing.
text: Dama ŋgɔɔ tara diire, na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n gusu tara suturam.

ref: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
text: Am niɛ kabo wuram bo pum barasu barasu a. Bu yo má juma be, bu kaaki tu má gise. Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, haari Fɛmɛ Solomon ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bo i yo la i ni, wɔ la má sutura bo i yo nyumi juuri nyini wura pum ni be.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
text: Dama durunya n nu­fɔm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, bɛrɛ sunsun sɔ ɔ saŋga kɛrɛ. Am Si Nyɛmɛ si ka am koro nyini dekem ni.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
text: Ama, nzɛn i ti amusui­su­fɔ tiɛ­fɔ, i súnsun ka i mibiɛ ni kpíe má i nyi ba má nde. A ŋu ka i subo amusui­su­fɔ nyiŋgam ni, bu nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram kɛrɛ, na i sudi na i sunu nzan bo,

ref: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
text: Am sunsun ka ma fa tana­laifiɛ aba durunya n nu a? Ai, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, ma fa má tana­laifiɛ baari a, ama, ma fa kpakpaaki­nu aba a.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
text: Tere­fiɛ wo nyini akɔɔm n dɔ, nzɛn bu mibiɛ n ba ŋu ka bu toko tana ŋminda i. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, i má bɛrɛ tana a, na yiri ni i tii fá diire ba ma bu na bu di.

ref: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
text: Nzɛn haari i kpieeri i nyi ba juuri kɔŋguɛ jinanjin, wara akɔnyuma bo saŋga, na i ba ŋu ka bu toko tana ŋminda i, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ.

ref: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
text: Am si tenle ka nzɛn má awuru­fɔ­biɛ n si saŋga bo awiefɔ ni bá i dika na i ba wɔ i, má i tana ŋminda a, na awiefɔ n koro ba wura má.

ref: So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
text: Ambɛrɛ gusu kpa am ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, dama mini Adamande Wa ni bá saŋga bo am sunsun má ka m bá n wɔ.”

ref: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
text: I jɔjɔɔri sɔ n na Petoro abisa i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, a bo ahin miɛɛnu n ni yɛrɛ wɔ, nzɛn a ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ?”

ref: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: Na Yesu abisa i ka, “Amusui­su­fɔ kpiɛn n bo i la dabuŋu na i kaaki la alecira n la ŋma a? Yiri la bo i mibiɛ n afa amusui­su­fɔ nyiŋgam awura i saa nu ka i kpaaki diire nu ma bu, saŋga bo i ti ye ni ka i ma bu ni.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Nzɛn i mibiɛ n ba ŋuuri ka i suyo juma n kabo i seeri i ka i yo i ni, tere­fiɛ wo nyini amusui­su­fɔ n dɔ.

ref: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nyini amusui­su­fɔ n mibiɛ ni fá i aja kɛrɛ wura i saa nu wɔ, na i níɛ i dika.

ref: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
text: dɔ na i mibiɛ n bá kpie i nyi ba cɛɛn bo i sunsun má ka i bá, na i kaaki si má ba n saŋga ni. Nyini saŋga n na i mibiɛ n cín i sui agaya, na i fá i bo minɛm bo bu yo má i yarada n su.

ref: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
text: Na nzɛn amusui­su­fɔ n si deke bo i mibiɛ n koro ka i yo i, na wɔ yo má siriya na i yo deke bo i mibiɛ n koro ka i yo ni, i bó i wɔ nyunyumi.

ref: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
text: Ama, nzɛn amusui­su­fɔ bo i si má deke bo i mibiɛ n koro ka i yo i, na i yoori deke be bo i ti ye ni ka i cin i sui, i dɛɛ sui­cin n ti kaan sɔ ɔ. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo bu ce i aceeri beberebe, i ti ye ni ka i mi fa yo deke bo wɔ yo daka ka i yo i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ce i aceeri beberebe agaya, i ti ye ni ka i mi su i kɔ tara sɔ.” Kabo Yesu ŋa wɔ fa kpakpaaki­nu aba ni

ref: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, “Ma fa Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n aba ka i yara durunya n nu­fɔm ka sin dɛɛ su wɔ. Má nyini sin n jeeri kisa, má m koro.

ref: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
text: Ama, m la wahara be bo i ti ye ni ka m di i, má ka ma di i awie, m ahore dá má.

ref: A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three.
text: Fite kisa fa kɔ, awuru kun nu­fɔm minɛ nnu kpákpaaki nu. Minɛ nsan tí bu busu bu ŋgumi wɔ, na minɛ nnyɔ tí bu busu bu ŋgumi.

ref: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
text: Baa­biɛsɔ ni i si koro má bu beŋgu jɔrɛ, na baa­bara ni i ni koro má bu beŋgu jɔrɛ, na baa n ni, ni i wa yi koro má bu beŋgu jɔrɛ.” Kabo Yesu jarakiiri minɛm ka bu si má sam bo bu suyo n bu ni

ref: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
text: Na Yesu ase jama n ka, “Saŋga bo am ŋu ka nzue yinyiŋgu atiɛn i nɔɔ wiɛ afiiri, a ti ka am ŋa, nzue bá nyuma, a ŋu ka i bá a gusu.

ref: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
text: Na saŋga bo am ŋu ka aŋuma sukpiɛ fite nyunu ndiɛma biɛ su, a ti ka am ŋa, nyuma wiɛ fíte agaya, a ŋu ka i fíte wɔ.

ref: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
text: Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, nzu dɛɛ ti ye am koro niɛ aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu na am si sam bo bu bá yo n a, ye am niɛ ye am koro si má sam bo bu suyo ahin saŋga n bu a?”

ref: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
text: Nzɛn u ni be la jɔrɛ, na nzɛn i koro ka i fa u kɔ jɔrɛ­di­fɔ dɔ, jɔkɔti tara i mi n jaa, na má jɔrɛ n ju jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n dɔ, má nyini, i mi ni cín u kɔ jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n dɔ, na yiri gusu fá u ma dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ ni, na yiri gusu nyá nyi u.

ref: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
text: Nyini saŋga n na minɛ nbem wo dɔ, na ba se Yesu kabo Galili­fɔ nbem yo kɔɔri ka bu kɔ yo yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ, na bu wo yo su ni, na Gomina Pilato gusu ama minɛm aba akun bu.

ref: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.

text: Ai, i ti má sɔ fieo. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nzɛn am akaaki má am wo, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ ɔ lɛɛ.” Miɛɛnu fa kɔ damba baka n bo wɔ su má n su

ref: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
text: Dɔ na nzɛn baka n suuri suu­wa, i ti kpa, nzɛn wɔ su má, na a koro ma m kpiɛ i ji.’” Kabo Yesu tuntuunri bara be Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ni

ref: and a woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for
text: Bara be woori dɔ, ajini wo i ŋu, wɔ bu i wi, i afɔ buru ni mɔcuɛ lɛɛ, i koro yasu jina má.

ref: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
text: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, wɔ yo daka ka m sukɔ ɔ, nyuma, ni ahunma, ni ahiɛni, dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ kɛrɛ ti ye ni ka bu kun i Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ.”

ref: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
text: Am ti fɛn. Minɛm bo bu wo mɔ kisa ni, bá ka sin, na bo bu ka sin kisa n bá du mɔ.” Kabo Yesu ahore saaki maari Yɛrusalem­fɔm barasu ni

ref: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
text: Saŋga n ba juuri, awuru­fɔ­biɛ ni yásu nyi anɔ n wɔ, nyini saŋga n na am jína anɔ n su, subobo, na am sére i jande ka, ‘Ya Mibiɛ, teke anɔ n ma ya.’ Na i gusu nyá se am ka, ‘Ai, m si má am sa.’

ref: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
text: Dɔ na am sé i ka, ‘Ya ni wɔrɔ yo diiri a, na ya nu, na a kere minɛm ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ, ya bɔsu dɔ.’

ref: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
text: Ama, na i káaki se am bekun ka, ‘Ambɛrɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, m si má am sa fieo, am cinsi m bi bɛrɛ.’

ref: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
text: Na am ŋú ka Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, ka bu wo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, na kabo ba fa ambɛrɛ atu dasin ni, dɔ na am sún, na am kán am saa.

ref: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
text: Na minɛm fíte durunya n awɔrɛ nna n kɛrɛ nu, na bu bá tana Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, na bu dí na bu nú.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: Na Yesu abo awie ase ka, “Oi, ambɛrɛ Yɛrusalem­fɔm. Ambɛrɛ kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a, na minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ sunmaari bu am dɔ ni, am atu bu yabuɛ. Saŋga kɛrɛ m koro ka m tiɛn am nɔɔ, na m fa m saa bara yɛ am, kabo akɔ tiɛn i nbaam nɔɔ, i laŋkpɛtɛ nu ni, ama, am soro má m su.

ref: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Am niɛ fɛn, Nyɛmɛ yáki am nu ji. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, fite kisa fa kɔ, am ŋú má m kun, má ka saŋga bo am bá se ka, ‘Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ.’”

ref: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.

text: Yesu ŋuuri sɔnɔ n sɔ ni, na wɔ bisa Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ni Farasi­fɔm n ka, “Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya tuntun tukpaki­fɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, nzɛn atin wo má nu?”

ref: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
text: Ama, ba bɔ má i nu, na Yesu ama sɔnɔ n anya laifiɛ, na wɔ ma i atin na wɔ kɔ.

ref: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
text: Ama, ba koro abɔ má i ahin bisaa­wa n nu. Kabo Yesu tuuri bu fɔ fa kɔ kpirikoro sam su ni

ref: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
text: Na Yesu aŋu kabo wɔfɔm nbem suniɛniɛ kpirikoro tanaa­biri na bu tana ni, dɔ na wɔ bo ahin miɛɛnu ni, akere bu kɛrɛ.

ref: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
text: I ŋa, “Saŋga kɛrɛ bo sɔnɔ be ba fere u ka a bra ba di konyaya diire i dika, a kɔ, má a tana jirima tanaa­biri ni, a yoori sɔ, a kɔ ŋu ka wɔ fere sɔnɔ be bo i tara u,

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wura i ŋu kpiri, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi kaan wɔ, na sɔnɔ bo i yo i ŋu kaan, Nyɛmɛ wúra i mi kpiri.”

ref: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
text: Dɔ na Yesu anya ase sɔnɔ n bo i baandiiri i n ka, “Saŋga bo a ŋa a fere minɛm na bu ba di diire u dika, má a fere u danfom, wara u ninbaam, wara u lamɔyam, wara u beŋgum bo bu la ajɛkɛ, nzɛn a yoori sɔ, bɛrɛ gusu bá fere u cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, nyini saŋga ni, ba tɔ u kaari awie lɛɛ.

ref: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
text: Wɔ cɛ má na akɔɔ n akɔ aba, na wɔ se ka, ‘M mibiɛ, ma yo kabo a seeri ka m yo ni, ama, fofoe toko ka.’

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
text: Yesu seeri sɔ n na wɔfɔm nu kun ase i ka, “Ŋka tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo bu bá tana Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu di diire n dɔ.”

ref: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, na wɔ yo diire beberebe, na wɔ baandi jama beberebe ka bu ba di diire ni.

ref: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
text: Na saŋga n bo diire n ba yoori siriya ni, na wɔ sunma i akɔɔ ka i kɔ se minɛm n bo wɔ du mɔ abaandi bu n ka, ‘Am bra, deke kɛrɛ ayo siriya.’

ref: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
text: Na bu kun kun kɛrɛ asere atin ka bu koro ba má diire n di. Na ciciri su dɛɛ sɔnɔ n ase akɔɔ n ka, ‘Jande, m sere u wɔ, ma to asiɛn, i ti ye ni ka m kɔ niɛ kabo i ti wɔ, m koro ba má.’

ref: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
text: Na nyiŋga n ase ka, ‘Ma to m tooram asɛsɛ busu nnu, m koro ka m kɔ fa bu yo juma kisa kisa na m niɛ, m sere u wɔ jande, m koro ba má.’

ref: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
text: Na akɔɔ n akpie i nyi akɔ ase i mibiɛ n kabo minɛm kɛrɛ ase i ni. Dɔ na yaa ati awuru­fɔ­biɛ n agaya, na wɔ se yiri akɔɔ n ka, ‘Yo ndende na a kɔ miɛ kunnu atiŋgberem kɛrɛ su, na a kɔ fere yaari­fɔm, ni wɔbukam, ni anyinsinm, ni minɛm bo bu saam ni bu jaam awu ni, na a fa bu bra.’

ref: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
text: Na awuru­fɔ­biɛ n ase i ka, ‘Fite kɔ miɛ n ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ, ni i kɔrɔkam kɛrɛ nu, na a kɔ fere minɛm ka bu bra, adena m awuru n yi ni jama.

ref: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
text: Saŋga bo Yesu wo kɔ su ni, na jama beberebe asu i su, na wɔ cɛ aniɛ bu, na wɔ se bu ka,

ref: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
text: “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ba m dɔ, i koro yo má m susu­fɔ, má ka i mi akoro m atara i si, ni i ni, ni i yi, ni i nbaam, ni i ninbaam, ni i sinbaam, ni haari yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii.

ref: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro sɔ má i yiwee baka jaanu n na i su m su, i mi koro yo má m susu­fɔ.

ref: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
text: Ma m fa i ka sɔnɔ ŋa i ta awuru, i du mɔ tana wɔ, na i bu akunta ka, na i niɛ ka i la ŋwaa bo i koro ju awuru n ta.

ref: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
text: Nzɛn i ŋwaa ju má bo i ta awuru n wie, na i boori i bu na wɔ wie má i, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sin ŋuuri sa n bo wɔ yo i ni, i mi síri wɔ,

ref: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
text: na bu sé ka, ‘Ahin sɔnɔ n boori i awuru n bu wɔ, ama, wɔ koro awie má i.’

ref: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
text: Nzɛn i ŋuuri ka i koro kɔrɔ má nu, i dú mɔ sunma sɔnɔ ɔ, na i kpá i beŋgu fɛmɛ n atin na i kɔ́ sere i ka, i má bu yo jɔrɛ n dɔŋgu.”

ref: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Njin yo fiɛ, ama, nzɛn i fiɛfiɛ nya ba saari wieeri, i koro yo má fiɛfiɛ kun.

ref: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
text: I koro buka má asiɛn ni mmɔɔ na i kaaki koro buka má nanyɛti na i yo diire, ŋka bu fa butu wɔ. Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti.”

ref: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
