text: Ama, yiri afa má i ŋu agɔ má bu ŋu, dama i si kabo adamande woo­wa ti.

ref: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
text: Má pui ti, yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii si kabo adamande ahore su ti barasu, má be se i a.

ref: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a. Sɔnɔ koro fa má Nyɛmɛ yo má i fɛmɛ, má ka ba wu i fɔfɔrɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a, sɔnɔ koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, má ka ba wu i fɔfɔrɛ fa kɔ nzue biɛɛ­wa ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ atin n su.

ref: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
text: Deke bo adamande awu i, i ti adamande wɔ, na deke bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awu i, i ti awiɛwiɛ wɔ.

ref: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
text: Má a ma i yo u alekutura fa kɔ kabo ma se u ka wɔ yo daka ka bu wu am fɔfɔrɛ ni.

ref: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ a ti ka aŋuma kpiɛ kɔ ɔ, ye a ti i naŋmiɛ ŋga a, ama, a si má deke bo i fite, na a kaaki si má deke bo i sukɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awu i ti sɔ ɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
text: Na Yesu akaaki abisa i ka, “Wɔrɔ bo a kere Yisareli­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu nyunu yi u ni, i yo sɛ ye a si má ahin n a?

ref: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a, sa bo ya si i, ye ya jɔjɔ i jɔrɛ a, na sa bo ya nyu­mbaa aŋu i, ye ya di i daani kere minɛm a, ama, am akete ya daani dii­wa ni.

ref: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
text: M suse am sam bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn wɔ lɛɛ, ama, am sude má i, ye m ba seeri am sam bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, am yó sɛ de i a?

ref: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
text: Be nya fun má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, má ka mini Adamande Wa n bo m woori i dɔ na ma jura aba ni.

ref: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
text: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori m yarada, i nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.”

ref: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
text: Nyɛmɛ akoro durunya n nu­fɔm agaya n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ fa i kunnu Wa kun n kpein n afa ama bu, adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo i yarada, i mi ká má ŋgbɛɛn, ama, i nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
text: Nyɛmɛ asunma má i Wa n durunya n nu ka i ba di bu jɔrɛ cin bu sui wɔ, ama, wɔ sunma i ka i ba de bu tii ji wɔ.

ref: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i daafiiri Nyɛmɛ Wa n ŋu, Nyɛmɛ dí má i mi n jɔrɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ da má i ŋu, Nyɛmɛ adu mɔ adi i jɔrɛ ka, dama ka wɔ da má Nyɛmɛ Wa kun n kpein n ŋu n dɛɛ ti.

ref: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ di bu jɔrɛ n la ka, wein n aba durunya n nu­fɔm dɔ, ama, bu satiɛ yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti, ba koro awosin n atara wein ni.

ref: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ kpɔ wein n wɔ, i koro má ka i kɔ wein n nu, na i satiɛm fite fofoe nu.

ref: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nahɔrɛ atin su, i fite fofoe nu wɔ, na minɛ kɛrɛ ŋu ka i sa­yoo­wa n su Nyɛmɛ atin n su wɔ. Kabo Yohane diiri Yesu ŋu daani barasu ni

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
text: Nyini sin na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n akɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu. Na ba kɔ adi le­nnyɔ dɔ, na i subiɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue.

ref: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
text: Na Yohane susu­fɔm ni Yuda­fɔ be awura akuruwa fa kɔ bu fin kpara n dɛɛ yoo­wa atin su.

ref: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
text: Na ba kɔ Yohane dɔ akɔ ase i ka, “Ya Kere­fɔ, niɛ. Sɔnɔ n bo i yo baari u dɔ Yodan kunma n wiɛ afiiri n na a yo diiri i ŋu daani kereeri minɛ ni, yiri wo dɔ subiɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue n a, bu kɛrɛ sukɔ i dɔ ɔ.”

ref: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
text: Na Yohane ase bu ka, “Sɔnɔ koro nya má deke be, má ka Nyɛmɛ afa nyini deke n ama i.

ref: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
text: Ambɛrɛ ni am tii woori m daani ka m ŋa má mini ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, ama, Nyɛmɛ sunmaari m ka m du i mɔ ɔ.

ref: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
text: Bu nya jaari konya, i ti konyafan n dɛɛ wɔ, ama, na konyafan n danfo jina suso i sui ti deke bo konyafan ni jɔjɔ se i, nyini saŋga na i danfo n ahore jɔ. Nyini ahorejɔ kun ni, wo mini gusu dɔ a.

ref: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
text: Wɔ yo daka ka i dɛɛ duma n fun, na m dɛɛ n kpiɛ su.” Yiri bo i fite aŋgoro n dɛɛ jɔrɛ

ref: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
text: Yiri bo i fite aŋgoro n tara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ. Na bo i fite durunya n nu fɛn ti durunya n nu fɛn fɔ ɔ, na i jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ ti durunya n nu fɛn jɔrɛ wɔ. Ama, yiri n bo i fite aŋgoro n tara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

ref: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
text: Deke bo wɔ ŋu i, ni bo wɔ ti i, ye i di i daani kere minɛ a, ama, be de má i daani dii­wa ni.

ref: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i deeri i daani dii­wa n asoro su akere ka Nyɛmɛ ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ lɛɛ.

ref: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
text: Dama yiri n bo Nyɛmɛ asunma i ni, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ye i kan i a, dama Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama i bo i la má aŋusu.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
text: Nyɛmɛ koro i Wa ni, na wɔ fa deke n kɛrɛ awura i saa nu.

ref: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
text: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i daari Nyɛmɛ Wa n ŋu, i la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kete i nɔaniɛ ni, i nyá má ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, ama, Nyɛmɛ fá yaa cin i sui.

ref: This time he had to go through Samaria,
text: I sukɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka i fa Samariya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu sin a.

ref: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
text: Na bara n ase i ka, “Ee, wɔrɔ, a ti Yuda­fɔ e, ye m gusu ti m Samariya­fɔ bara. Nzu ti ye a se m ka m ma u nzue a?” Dama Yuda­fɔm ni Samariya­fɔm tɔ má pui.

ref: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
text: Na bara n ase i ka, “Ya si, bura n wo ŋgo, u gusu la má goroka, ye a nya nyini nzue n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n ni wɔ?

ref: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
text: Wɔrɔ, ŋka a ŋa a tara ya na Jakɔbu n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Yiri maari ya nyini bura n a. I ni i nbaam fa saŋga ni i neŋge­ndɔrɛm kɛrɛ yo nuuri nyini bura n nu wɔ.”

ref: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
text: ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nu nzue n bo m má i ni, nzuehɔɛ tí má i mi kun fieo, dama nzue n bo m má i ni, i wó i kunnu tiɛ kabo bura nzue tiɛ n wɔ, na i má i ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.”

ref: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
text: Na bara n ase i ka, “Ya si, ma m nyini nzue n be na m nu, adena má nzuehɔɛ ti m kun, na má m kaaki ba sa nzue fɛn kun.”

ref: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Kɔ fere u hun na am bra.”

ref: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
text: Dama nbiɛsɔm nnu ye a fite bu ajaa bu n a, na haari bo u ni yiri wo bɛrɛ kisa mmɔɔ ni, i ti má u hun. A se nahɔrɛ wɔ.”

ref: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
text: Na bara n ase i ka, “Ya si, ma ŋu ka a ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ.

ref: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
text: Ya na Samariya­fɔm yo suuri Nyɛmɛ ahin buka n su wɔ. Ama, ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm ŋa wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ su Nyɛmɛ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ.”

ref: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Bara, de bo m suse u ni, saŋga suba bo am sú má ya Si Nyɛmɛ ahin buka n su, wara Yɛrusalem miɛ n nu.

ref: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
text: Ambɛrɛ Samariya­fɔm ŋa am su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, ama, am si má i. Ama, yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm si i, ye ya su i a. Dama tii­de n fite Yuda­fɔm dɔ ɔ.

ref: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
text: Saŋga suba bo wɔ ju awie, na bo bu su ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni, i Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni búka bu ye bu sú i ni nahɔrɛ su a. Dama nyini minɛm ŋusu ye ya Si Nyɛmɛ koro ka bu su i a.

ref: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
text: Nyɛmɛ ti awiɛwiɛ wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, bo bu su i ni, wɔ yo daka ka i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n búka bu na bu sú i ni nahɔrɛ su.”

ref: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
text: Na bara n ase i ka, “Tɔ, m si ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni bá. I ba juuri, i kán ya deke kɛrɛ kere ya.”

ref: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Mini bo m sujɔjɔ u dɔ ni, mini lɛɛ.”

ref: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
text: Nyini saŋga n nu na Yesu susu­fɔm n afite miɛ n kunnu aju, na jɔrɛ n amiɛ bu kabo Yesu ni bara n wo jɔjɔ su ni. Ama, bu be abisa má bara n ka, “A koro nzu i dɔ a?” Be gusu abisa má Yesu ka, “Nzu ti ye a sujɔjɔ bara n dɔ a?”

ref: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
text: Na bara n ayaki i dundu n bɛrɛ, na wɔ kpie i nyi akɔ miɛ n kunnu akɔ ase minɛm ka,

ref: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
text: “Am ba niɛ sɔnɔ be bo wɔ se m m tii su sa kɛrɛ bo ma yo i. Beni, kisa, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n lɛɛ fan?”

ref: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
text: Na minɛm n afite miɛ n nu, na bu kɔ Yesu dɔ.

ref: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
text: Bara n kɔɔri n dɛɛ sin, na Yesu susu­fɔm n asere i ka, “Jande, ya Kere­fɔ, di diire.”

ref: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “M la diire bo m di bo am si má i.”

ref: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
text: Na i susu­fɔm n abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Be afa diire aba ama i wɔ di wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?”

ref: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “M diire n la ka, m yo yiri bo wɔ sunma m dɛɛ koroo­wa na m yó i juma n wie.”

ref: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
text: Na wɔ bo bu anyundɛrɛ ase ka, “Am ŋa wɔ ka sara nna na am tambu fiesu neŋgem. Ama, mini suse am wɔ lɛɛ, am tu am tii na am niɛ, fiesu diire n abi ni kpiɛ.

ref: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
text: Sɔnɔ bo i kpiɛ diire ni, i nya i ŋkatɔ, i ti ka sɔnɔ bo i tiɛn minɛ nɔɔ, na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni, dɔ­fɔ ni kpiɛ­fɔ n kɛrɛ nya ahorejɔ.

ref: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
text: Be bu ŋa ‘Be dɔ, na be kpiɛ ni,’ i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
text: Bara n seeri minɛm bo bu fite miɛ n nu n kabo Yesu ase i tii su sa kɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti, na Samariya­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu fite miɛ n nu n ada Yesu ŋu.

ref: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
text: Nyini ti, bu ba juuri Yesu dɔ ni, na ba sere i ka i ka bu dɔ, na wɔ kɔ awo bu dɔ cɛɛn nnyɔ.

ref: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
text: Na minɛ dɔŋgu ada i ŋu abuka su fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti.

ref: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
text: Na ba se bara n ka, “Kisa, má u dɛɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ŋgumi ti ye ya da i ŋu a, ama, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii ati i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa, na ya si nu ka ahin sɔnɔ n ti bo i baari ka i ba de durunya n nu­fɔm tii wɔ, nahɔrɛ su.” Kabo Yesu maari fɛmɛ n kpiɛn be wa nyaari laifiɛ ni

ref: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
text: Dama yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii adu mɔ akan ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ la má jirima i tiɛŋgu bɔsu.

ref: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
text: Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri Galili ni, na ba de i ni bu sa nnyɔ, dama bu yo ŋuuri kabo i yoori alekutura sam cɛɛn­kpiri n saŋga, Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, dama bɛrɛ mmɔɔ yo kɔɔri nyini cɛɛn­kpiri n di ti.

ref: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
text: Na Yesu akaaki akɔ Kana, miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, deke bo i yo maari nzue kaakiiri nzan ni. Fɛmɛ n kpiɛn be woori dɔ. I wa be la má laifiɛ, i wo Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
text: Na saŋga bo kpiɛn n tiiri ka Yesu afite Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu akɔ aju Galili ni, na wɔ kɔ Yesu dɔ akɔ asere i ka i wa suwu, i ma bu kɔ na i kɔ tuntun i, na i nya laifiɛ.

ref: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Ma ŋu ka nzɛn am aŋu má alekutura sam, am yo má m yarada.”

ref: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Kɔ. U wa n anya laifiɛ.” Na wɔ de Yesu nɔaniɛ ni, na wɔ kaaki akɔ.

ref: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
text: Na saŋga bo i sukɔ awuru ni, na i akɔɔm akpa i atin aba ase i ka, “U wa n anya laifiɛ.”

ref: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
text: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, “Saŋga bɔnɔ ye i ŋuniɛ n jɔɔri a?” Na ba se i ka, “Anuma wiɛsu kerefi kun saŋga ye i ŋuniɛ n jɔɔri a.”

ref: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
text: Dɔ na baa n si n ateŋge su ka, nyini saŋga n nu lɛɛ, ye Yesu seeri i ka i wa n anya laifiɛ n a. Na i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ ada Yesu ŋu.

ref: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
text: Nyini sin na Yesu akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu na i kɔ di Yuda­fɔm cɛɛn­kpiri be.

ref: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
text: Kunma be da dodoori anɔ n bo bu fa nbɔɛm fa sin wura miɛ n nu ni. Hiburu aniɛ nu bu fere nyini kunma n ka Besata, i la kpatam nnu wɔ.

ref: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
text: Sɔnɔ be woori bu nu bo wɔ da tukpaki i afɔ abura­san ni mɔcuɛ lɛɛ.

ref: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
text: na i nɔaniɛ wo má am ahore su, dama wɔ sunma m am dɔ, na am ada má m ŋu.

ref: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
text: Yesu ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na wɔ fa nu ka sɔnɔ n tukpaki n acɛ i ŋu, na wɔ bisa i ka, “A koro ka a nya laifiɛ wɔ?”

ref: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
text: Na tukpaki­fɔ n ase Yesu ka, “M Mibiɛ, m la má buka­fɔ be bo nzue n nya ba kankan, na i kuku m wura nu. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo nzue n nya ba kankan, na m ba ŋa m wura nu, a ŋu ka be adu m mɔ.”

ref: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Yasu, fa u dasu n na a nati.”

ref: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
text: Jinaa­biri n nu bɛrɛ na sɔnɔ n anya laifiɛ, na wɔ yasu afa i dasu n sunati. Nyini cɛɛn n bo Yesu tuntuunri sɔnɔ n ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ.

ref: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
text: Nyini ti na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ase sɔnɔ n bo wɔ nya laifiɛ n ka, “Nyuma ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ. Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo má nu bo a sɔ u dasu ni.”

ref: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Sɔnɔ n bo i tuntuunri m ni, yiri seeri m ka m fa m dasu na m nati a.”

ref: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “Nyini sɔnɔ n la ŋma a, ye i seeri u ka a fa u dasu nati a?”

ref: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
text: Ama, na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ nya laifiɛ n akoro asi má i mi n bo i tuntuunri i ni, dama Yesu awura jama n nu akɔ.

ref: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
text: Nyini sin na Yesu aba aŋu i, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ se i ka, “Ti fɛn. Kisa a nya laifiɛ, má a yo satiɛ kun. Má nyini, a dí wahara bo i tára nyini n ŋusu.”

ref: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
text: Na sɔnɔ n akɔ ase Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni ka Yesu tuntuunri i a.

ref: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
text: Nyini ti, na ba kere Yesu wuwuruwa, dama wɔ tuntun sɔnɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n dɛɛ ti.

ref: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “M Si toko yo juma haari ba ju ni nyuma, nyini ti ye m gusu yo a.”

ref: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
text: Yesu jɔjɔɔri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n aboro bu nyi abuka su ka bu kun i. Má ka bo wɔ saaki Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ciire n ŋgumi dɛɛ ti, ye bu kpi ka bu kun i a, ama, bo wɔ kaaki ase ka Nyɛmɛ ti i Si n dɛɛ ti. Nyini kere ka wɔ fa i ŋu abu Nyɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Kabo Yesu yiko ti barasu ni

ref: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, mini Nyɛmɛ Wa n ni m tii, m koro bo má m tiɛɛnu yo má pui, má ka deke bo m ŋu ka m Si yo i ni, ye mini gusu yo i a.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
text: Dama m Si Nyɛmɛ koro mini i Wa ni, na i sukere m deke kɛrɛ bo i yo i ni, na i bá kere m sakpa­kpam bo m yó bu tara sɔ, na i yó am alekutura.

ref: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
text: Kabo m Si Nyɛmɛ yɛ minɛm bo ba wu n su n na i ma bu ŋgɔɔ ni, sɔ ye mini gusu ma minɛm bo ma yi bu ni ŋgɔɔ a.

ref: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
text: Dama m Si Nyɛmɛ ni i tii di má be jɔrɛ, ama, wɔ fa jɔrɛ­di atin kɛrɛ ama mini i Wa n wɔ,

ref: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
text: adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bu m kabo bu bu m Si ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bu má m, i mi abu má m Si bo wɔ sunma m n lɛɛ.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti m nɔaniɛ n na i de i, na i da Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m n ŋu, i mi la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na m dí má i mi jɔrɛ. I mi afite yiwee nu anya ŋgɔɔ lɛɛ.

ref: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m kaaki suse am a, ka saŋga suba bo wɔ ju awie, na minɛ bo ba wu ni tí mini Nyɛmɛ Wa n nɔaniɛ ni, na bo bu tiiri i na bu deeri i, bu nyá ŋgɔɔ.

ref: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
text: Dama kabo ŋgɔɔ fite yiri m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, sɔ ye wɔ ma mini i Wa n gusu yiko ka m ma minɛm ŋgɔɔ a,

ref: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
text: na wɔ kaaki ama m atin ka m di minɛ jɔrɛ, dama m ti Adamande Wa a.

ref: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
text: Má am ma ahin jɔjɔɔ­wam n yo am alekutura. Saŋga suba bo minɛ bo ba wu n kɛrɛ tí m nɔaniɛ ni,

ref: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
text: na bu téŋge bu yiwee n nu. Minɛm bo bu yo yoori sakpa ni, bu téŋge na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na minɛm bo bu gusu yo yoori satiɛ ni, bu téŋge na bu nya sui­cin.” Sam bo bu di Yesu ŋu daani ni

ref: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
text: Na Yesu asa su ase ka, “M koro bo má m tiɛɛnu yo má pui. Kabo Nyɛmɛ se m ni, sɔ ye m di minɛm jɔrɛ a. Nyini ti, m jɔrɛ dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama m yo má m dɛɛ koroo­wa, ama, m yo yiri n bo wɔ sunma m n dɛɛ koroo­wa a.

ref: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.
text: Nzɛn m di m ŋu daani kere am, am de má ka i ti nahɔrɛ,

ref: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
text: ama, be wo bɛrɛ bo i di m ŋu daani. Yiri la m Si Nyɛmɛ. Na m si ka i daani dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth.
text: Am yo sunmaari minɛm Yohane dɔ, na wɔ se am nahɔrɛ, kabo m ti barasu.

ref: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
text: M de má adamande daani dii­wa, ama, m kan kabo Yohane seeri am ni, adena am nya am tii.

ref: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
text: Yohane yo ti ka fitana bo i je na i ta a, na ambɛrɛ ayo asoro su ka am wo i wein nu ni ahorejɔ fa kɔ saŋga su.

ref: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
text: Ama, m la deke bo i di m ŋu daani bo i tara Yohane dɛɛ ni, yiri la sam bo m Si Nyɛmɛ akere m ka m yo bu wie ni. Nyini sam ni di m ŋu daani ka m Si asunma m a.

ref: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
text: Na m Si bo wɔ sunma m ni, yiri gusu adi m ŋu daani a. Am nya ti má i konvi, am kaaki nya ŋu má i kabo i ti barasu,

ref: but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
text: Haari ni nyini n mmɔɔ am akete ka am ba má m dɔ na am ba nya ŋgɔɔ.

ref: I don't care about human praise,
text: M koro má kansi adamande dɔ.

ref: but I do know that none of you love God.
text: M si am sa, na m si ka, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koro n wo má am ahore su.

ref: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
text: Mini ade m Si jinaa­biri aba, ye am ade má m, ama, nzɛn be baari ni i tiɛŋgu ba, am dé yiri wɔ.

ref: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
text: Ambɛrɛ, am koro am ŋuŋu nu kansi wɔ, na bu susu am, ama, am koro má kansi yiri n ŋgumi bo i ti Nyɛmɛ n dɔ, nyini ti, i yo sɛ ye am koro da m ŋu a?

ref: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
text: Má am sunsun ka mini kɔ́ jaraki am m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ a. Am jaraki­fɔ la Moyisi, bo am la tama ka yiri búka am ni.

ref: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
text: Moyisi yo kɛrɛɛri m ŋusu jɔrɛ wɔ. Nyini ti, nzɛn má am de i nɔaniɛ ni, má am de m gusu dɛɛ ni.

ref: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
text: Ama, am ade má kabo i yo kɛrɛɛri i ni, i yó sɛ ye am koro de bo m suse am n a?”

ref: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
text: Na ba tiɛntiɛn kpɔnɔ sinsin n bo ba di i na wɔ ka n nɔɔ, na wɔ yi kandiɛ buru ni nnyɔ.

ref: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
text: Na ba ma wɔ wura lee n nu. Jinaa­biri n nu bɛrɛ, na ba ju deke bo bu sukɔ ni. Kabo jama n suniɛniɛ Yesu busu ni

ref: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “Ye a yó nzu alekutura sa kere ya, na ya ŋu i na ya yo u yarada? A yó sa bɔnɔ ɔ?

ref: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Be koro ba má m dɔ, má ka m Si Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m ni, acin i mi adodo m, na m téŋge i mi jina cɛɛn.

ref: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
text: “Gbɛfɛnɛ kaan be wo ya nu fɛn, i bita kpɔnɔ nnu ni jombaa nnyɔ, ama, i koro ju má ahin minɛm kɛrɛ.”

ref: The ground was covered with grass, and Jesus told his disciples to tell everyone to sit down. About
text: Wura mɔnɛ sunnu bɛrɛ, na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ka, “Am ma minɛm kɛrɛ tantana wura n su.” Na ba ma minɛm kɛrɛ atantana. Nbiɛsɔm ŋgumi kɔ yasuuri minɛ akpii nnu wɔ.

ref: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
text: na ba kɔ awura lee nu ka bu kaaki kpiɛ kɔ Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu. Ale aba asan kɛrɛ, na Yesu nya ba má bu dɔ,

ref: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
text: Nyini sin, na Yesu akpiɛ akɔ Galili kunma kpiri n bo bu kaaki fere i ka Tiberiya n buekun dɔ.

ref: It was almost time for the Jewish festival of Passover, and Jesus went up on a mountain with his disciples and sat down.
text: Nyini saŋga, wɔ ka kaan, na Yuda­fɔm di bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni. Na jama beberebe asu i su, dama ba ŋu kabo wɔ yo alekutura sam atuntun tukpaki­fɔm na ba nya laifiɛ ni. Na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n afun akɔ atana buka be su.

ref: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
text: Na Filipu ase i ka, “Haari nzɛn ya faari ŋwaa beberebe toori diire maari bu, i jú má bo bu kɛrɛ koro nya kan kan di.”

ref: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
text: Na i susu­fɔm kun bo bu fere i ka Andere, na i gɔrɔ la Simon Petoro n ase Yesu ka,

ref: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
text: Na Yesu afa kpɔnɔ n na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ kpakpaaki i nu ama minɛm bo bu tantana ni, na wɔ kaaki afa jombaa n ayo i sɔ, na bu kɛrɛ anya kabo bu koro.

ref: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
text: Bu kɛrɛ diiri na bu kunnu yiiri ni, na wɔ se i susu­fɔm n ka, “Am tiɛntiɛn sinsin n bo ba di i na wɔ ka n nɔɔ. Má am ma be ka ŋgbɛɛn.”

ref: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
text: Yesu faari nu ka bu koro ka bu tara i faŋga, na bu fa i yo bu fɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yasu afite bu nu yiri ŋgumi, akɔ buka n su. Kabo Yesu natiiri nzue su ni

ref: The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day
text: Alecin na jama n bo bu kaari kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ n afa nu ka lee kun n kpein woori kunma n su anuma n a. Na ba si nu ka Yesu susu­fɔm n ŋgumi wuraari lee n nu kɔɔri a, ama, yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii awura má nu.

ref: Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.”
text: Má am ma am nyunu boro diire bo i saaki n busu, ama, am ma am nyunu boro diire bo i wie má, na i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n busu. Mini Adamande Wa ni má am nyini diire n a, dama mini ŋgumi, ye m Si Nyɛmɛ ase m nyaari akere ka m ti i dɛɛ a.”

ref: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo kɛrɛɛri lɛɛ, ka, ‘Nyɛmɛ kére sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyaari a.’ Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ keree­wa n na i siiri nu, i mi bá m dɔ.

ref: They started arguing with each other and asked, “How can he give us his flesh to eat?”
text: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu, na bu suse akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Ahin sɔnɔ ni yo sɛ koro fa i ŋuniɛ ma ya na ya di a?”

ref: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
text: I naasɔ saŋga, na Yesu susu­fɔm n ajura akɔ kunma kpiri n nu,

ref: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
text: na aŋuma sukpiɛ faŋga su, na nzue nyama gusu suyasu.

ref: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
text: Nyini saŋga ni, i susu­fɔm n wo nzue n su akɔ aju kunma kpiri n afiɛn, na ba ŋu Yesu ka i nati nzue n su suba dodo lee ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu.

ref: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Mini a, má am ma sɛrɛ ti am.”

ref: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
text: Na lee nbem afite miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Tiberiya n aba aju kunma n nɔɔ su, fa dodo deke bo Yesu sereeri Nyɛmɛ gɔɔri kpɔnɔ n su maari minɛm na bu diiri ni.

ref: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
text: Na jama n aniɛ, ba ŋu má Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n bɛrɛ, nyini ti, na ba wura leem n nu akɔ Kapɛnam kɔ suniɛniɛ Yesu busu. Kabo Yesu ŋa yiri ti diire n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n a

ref: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
text: Minɛm n kɔ ŋuuri Yesu kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ ni, na ba bisa i ka, “Ya Kere­fɔ, saŋga bɔnɔ ye a baari fɛn a?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má alekutura sam bo m yoori bu n ti ye am suniɛniɛ m busu a, ama, kabo m maari am diire na am kunnu ayi n dɛɛ ti a.

ref: “What exactly does God want us to do?” the people asked.
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “Ya yo sɛ koro yo juma n bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka ya yo i n a?”

ref: Jesus answered, “God wants you to have faith in the one he sent.”
text: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, “Juma n bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka am yo i n la ka, am yo mini bo wɔ sunma m yarada.”

ref: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
text: Dama saŋga bo ya nam yo woori boro kpantain nu ni, bu diiri diire be bo bu fere i ka mana n wɔ. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu lɛɛ, ka yiri maari bu diire n bo i fite aŋgoro ye bu diiri a.”

ref: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má Moyisi yo maari nyini diire n bo i fite aŋgoro n a, ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ maari am diire n bo i fite aŋgoro nahɔrɛ su a.

ref: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
text: Dama diire n bo Nyɛmɛ ma n la bo i fite aŋgoro, na i ba ma durunya n nu­fɔm ŋgɔɔ ni.”

ref: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
text: Na ba se i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, ŋka a ma ya nyini diire n saŋga kɛrɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Mini ti diire n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ba m dɔ, ahɔɛ ti má i mi, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, nzuehɔɛ ti má i mi kun.

ref: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
text: Ama, ma se am, haari ni bo am aŋu m n kɛrɛ, am suyo má m yarada.

ref: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo m Si fa i maari m, i bá m dɔ, na wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a ba m dɔ, m kéte má u fieo.

ref: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
text: Dama ma fite má aŋgoro aba má ka m ba yo m dɛɛ koroo­wa, ama, m baari ka m ba yo Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m dɛɛ koroo­wa a.

ref: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
text: Yiri n bo wɔ sunma m dɛɛ koroo­wa la ka, minɛm kɛrɛ bo wɔ fa ama m ni, má m ma bu be mmɔɔ mini, ama, i koro ka m teŋge bu kɛrɛ jina cɛɛn na bu nya ŋgɔɔ.

ref: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Dama m Si koroo­wa la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋu mini i Wa n na i yo m yarada, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na m téŋge i jina cɛɛn.”

ref: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
text: I seeri ka yiri ti diire n bo wɔ fite aŋgoro aba n dɛɛ ti, na minɛm ni suŋuŋuti i.

ref: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
text: Na ba se ka, “Má Yosefu wa Yesu n lɛɛ? Ya si i si ni i ni kɛrɛ e. Ye i baari sɛ, ye i ŋa i fiteeri aŋgoro baari a?”

ref: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
text: Nyini kere má ka be adu mɔ aŋu Nyɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ, má ka mini ŋgumi bo m fite m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ n aŋu i a.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, i la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

ref: I am the bread that gives life!
text: Mini ti diire n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n a.

ref: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
text: Am nam yo diiri diire n bo bu fere i ka mana n boro kpantain nu, ama, na ba wu.

ref: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
text: Ama, ahin diire n ti diire barasu be bo i fite aŋgoro wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri i, i wú má.

ref: I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
text: Mini ti diire n bo wɔ fite aŋgoro aba, na i ma ŋgɔɔ n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di ahin diire ni, i nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri. Diire n bo m má n la m ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, bo m fá ma durunya n nu­fɔm, na bu nyá ŋgɔɔ ni.”

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má ka am di mini Adamande Wa n ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na am nu m dɛɛ mbunja ni, ambɛrɛ ni am tii la má ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di m dɛɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na i nu m mbunja ni, i mi la ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, na m téŋge i mi jina cɛɛn.

ref: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di m dɛɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na i nu m mbunja ni, i mi wo ni mini, na mini gusu wo ni yiri lɛɛ.

ref: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
text: Yiri m Si bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni sunmaari m a, i dɛɛ ti, ye m la ŋgɔɔ a, sɔ ye sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di m ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, mini dɛɛ ti, ye i nyá ŋgɔɔ a.

ref: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
text: Yesu kereeri nyini keree­wa n kɛrɛ saŋga bo i wo Kapɛnam bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ nu n wɔ. Kabo Petoro seeri ka Yesu ti sɔnɔ bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n a

ref: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
text: Yesu susu­fɔm dɔŋgu tiiri i jɔjɔɔ­wa n sɔ ni, na ba se ka, “Ahin keree­wa n ti kekereke. Ŋma koro de i a?”

ref: What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from?
text: Ye nzɛn má am ŋu ka mini Adamande Wa n sukaaki sufun kɔ aŋgoro, deke bo m fite baari ni, má am se sɛ wɔ lɛɛ?

ref: The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit.
text: Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma minɛ ŋgɔɔ a. Adamande yiko be koro ma má ŋgɔɔ. Nɔaniɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ akere am ni, yiri ma am nya Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n a.

ref: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
text: Ama, am be n mmɔɔ ada má m ŋu.” Má pui ti, fite saŋga bo Yesu boori i juma n bu ni, ye i si bo bu yo má i yarada n a, ni sɔnɔ n bo i bá tɔ i ni.

ref: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma se am ka be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro ba m dɔ, má ka m Si ama atin ka i mi yo m susu­fɔ.”

ref: Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him.
text: I seeri sɔ n sin, na i susu­fɔm dɔŋgu akaaki ayaki i ka bu su má i su kun.

ref: Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him.
text: Na wɔ bisa i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni ka, “Ambɛrɛ gusu koro ka am kaaki yaki m wɔ fan?”

ref: Simon Peter answered, “Lord, there is no one else that we can go to! Your words give eternal life.
text: Na Simon Petoro abɔ i nu ka, “M Mibiɛ, ya kɔ́ ŋma dɔ a? Wɔrɔ ti sɔnɔ n bo i la nɔaniɛ n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n a.

ref: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
text: Ye ya yo u yarada, na ya si ka wɔrɔ ti sɔnɔ casi n bo a fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n a.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
text: Na Yesu abɔ nu ka, “M ŋa am buru ni nnyɔ ye m yiiri am e, má sɔ? Ama, am nu kun ti Sitana sɔnɔ ɔ.”

ref: Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
text: I jɔjɔ niiri Yudasi wɔ, Simon Isikariyoti wa ni. Yiri Yudasi tiiri buru ni nnyɔm ni kun wɔ, ama, yiri bá tɔ Yesu n a.

ref: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
text: Nyini sin na Yesu afa Galili awɔrɛ n nu subara yɛ, wɔ koro má ka i kɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, dama Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm koro ka bu kun i wɔ.

ref: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
text: Na Yesu niɛmam ase i ka, “Fite ahin awɔrɛ n nu na a kɔ Yudiya, adena u susu­fɔm gusu kɔ́ ŋu sam bo a yo bu ni.

ref: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka minɛ si kabo i ti barasu, i fa má i sa­yoo­wa fiɛ má minɛm sin, nzɛn a yo alekutura sam wɔ, ma durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ si u sa.”

ref: Even Jesus' own brothers had not yet become his followers.
text: I niɛmam n mmɔɔ ayo má i yarada, nyini ti ye bu seeri sɔ n a.

ref: Jesus answered, “My time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “M saŋga nya ju má, ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am koro, am kɔ ɔ.

ref: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
text: Ambɛrɛ ye durunya n nu­fɔm koro kpɔ má am a, ama, bu kpɔ mini wɔ, dama m se bu wɔ, ka bu sa­yoo­wam ti má kpa.

ref: Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.”
text: Am kɔ cɛɛn­kpiri n di, m kɔ́ má, dama m saŋga nya ju má.”

ref: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
text: I seeri bu sɔ ni, na wɔ ka Galili dɔ lee nnyɔ. Kabo Yesu kɔ diiri Nambue Bo cɛɛn­kpiri ni

ref: After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
text: I niɛmam kɔɔri ka bu kɔ di cɛɛn­kpiri ni, na Yesu gusu aba asa bu su, ama, wɔ yi má i ŋu fofoe nu, i fiɛ kɔɔri a.

ref: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
text: Na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni suniɛniɛ i, na ba bisa ka, “Ye i wo ni wɔ?”

ref: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
text: Na jama n sutu asuturi bu beŋgu dɔ i busu. Nbem ŋa, “I ti sɔnɔ kpa a.” Ye nbem gusu ŋa, “Ai, i dadaka minɛm wɔ.”

ref: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
text: Ama, be akoro akan má i jɔrɛ fofoe nu, dama bu kɛrɛ soro Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n wɔ.

ref: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
text: Saŋga bo ba di cɛɛn­kpiri n akɔ aju afiɛn ni, na Yesu akɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, sukere minɛm.

ref: The leaders were surprised and said, “How does this man know so much? He has never been taught!”
text: Na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ayo alekutura, na ba bisa ka, “I yo sɛ ye ahin sɔnɔ n la nyini si n ŋusu sɔ a, bo be akere má i a?”

ref: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Deke bo m fa kere am ni, i ti má m tiɛŋgu keree­wa, ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m ni, yiri kere m kabo m kere a.

ref: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i yó Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa ni, ye i sí kabo m keree­wa n ti a, nzɛn m keree­wa n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ, wara m tiɛŋgu keree­wa.

ref: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ i koroya­nu, i mi koro ka bu kansi i wɔ, ama, bo i koro ka bu kansi sɔnɔ bo i sunma i ni, nyini sɔnɔ ni jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ a, na kɔrɛ be wo má i ŋu.

ref: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
text: M ŋa Moyisi yo maari am Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n a, má sɔ? Ama, am be n mmɔɔ su má i atin su. Ye nzu ti ye am koro ka am kun m a?”

ref: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
text: Na jama n ase i ka, “Ajini wo u ŋu. Ŋma koro ka i kun u a?”

ref: Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons
text: Moyisi yo maari am nɔɔ ka am wura am nbaa­biɛsɔm kɛnkɛnnɛ le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn, nzɛn haari i tɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n mmɔɔ, am yo i, ama, má Moyisi yo boori i bu ka bu wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n a, am nam yo boori i bu a.

ref: in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath?
text: Am wura nbaa­biɛsɔm kɛnkɛnnɛ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ, adena má am bu nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari Moyisi ni ŋgbɛɛn, ye nzɛn ma tuntun sɔnɔ mɔmɔ kun Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n na wɔ nya laifiɛ, nzu ti ye am afa yaa m ŋu a?

ref: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
text: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na Yɛrusalem­fɔm nbem abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Má ahin sɔnɔ n ye Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm koro ka bu kun i n a?

ref: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
text: Am niɛ e. Wɔ yi i ŋu fofoe nu ye i jɔjɔ a. Be suse má i pui mmɔɔ. Beni, Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm asi ka yiri ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n nahɔrɛ su n a.

ref: But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.”
text: Ama, saŋga bo Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ba baari, be sí má deke bo i fite. Ahin sɔnɔ n yiri, ya si deke bo i fite.”

ref: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, “Do you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him.
text: Na saŋga bo Yesu sukere Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su ase ka, “Am sunsun ka am si m nahɔrɛ su, nzɛn sɛ? Ye am si deke bo m fite? Ma ba má ni m dɛɛ yiko nu wɔ. Yiri n bo wɔ sunma m ni ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ a, ama, am si má i.

ref: But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.”
text: Mini si i a, dama m fite i dɔ ɔ, yiri sunmaari m a.”

ref: Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
text: Nyini ti, na ba kpi ka bu tara i, ama, be afa má i saa akan má i, dama i saŋga nya ju má.

ref: A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!” Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
text: Na jama n dɔŋgu ayo i yarada, na ba se ka, “Saŋga bo Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ba baari, i yó alekutura sam tara ahin sɔnɔ n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?” Kabo bu sunmaari tintini­jina­fɔm ka bu kɔ tara Yesu ni

ref: But Jesus told them, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me.

ref: The people asked each other, “Where can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks?
text: Na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Yiri koro ka i kɔ ni wɔ, ye yɛrɛ koro ŋu má i a? I kɔ́ ya minɛm dɔ ɔ, bo ba sandi awura Giriki­fɔm asiɛn su ni, na i kɔ́ kere Giriki­fɔm ni Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni, nzɛn sɛ a?

ref: What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?” Streams of Life-Giving Water
text: I ŋa sɛ wɔ lɛɛ? Bo i ŋa ya bá niɛniɛ i busu na ya ŋú má i ni, na bekun, deke bo i sukɔ ni, ya koro kɔ má dɔ.” Kabo sɔnɔ bo i yo Yesu yarada nya nzue n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni

ref: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
text: Yesu jɔjɔ niiri Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wɔ, ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yiri Yesu yarada nyá Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni. Nyini saŋga ni, Nyɛmɛ nya fa má i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma má, dama wɔ nya wura má Yesu kpiri. Kabo Yesu dɛɛ ti, na jama n nɔɔ woori bu ŋguŋgumi ni

ref: Others said, “He is the Messiah!” Others even said, “Can the Messiah come from Galilee?

text: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni yó Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a, na bu wú i Bɛtilɛhɛm, Fɛmɛ Davidi bɔsu wɔ.”

ref: The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus.

ref: They answered, “No one has ever spoken like this man!”
