ref: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,
text: Na Nikodemo bo i ti Farasi­fɔm kun n na i yo kɔ ŋuuri Yesu ni, ase bu ka,

ref: “Our Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.”
text: “Ya dɛɛ atin n su, kana ya ba di sɔnɔ jɔrɛ jaraki i, má ka ya bisa i na ya ti sa bo wɔ yo i n ka.”

ref: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “Wɔrɔ n mmɔɔ fite Galili wɔ? Kan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n na a niɛ. A ŋú má ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n be mmɔɔ fite Galili.” [

ref: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
text: Alecin ŋgarama fuun na Yesu akaaki akɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na jama n kɛrɛ aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ. Na wɔ tana sukere bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm nbem afa bara ajaa­fɔ be bo bu kɔ taraari i ka i ni biɛsɔ da, na ba fa i ajina bu afiɛn.

ref: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
text: Na ba se Yesu ka, “Ya Kere­fɔ, ya kɔ atara ahin bara n ka i susaaki ajaa ciire wɔ.

ref: The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?”
text: Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari Moyisi n kere ka, nyini nbaram ŋusu sɔ, ya tu bu yabuɛ bo kun bu. Nyini ti, ye wɔrɔ ŋa sɛ?”

ref: They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
text: Bu seeri sɔ n na bu sɔ i ŋgɛɛ, na bu jaraki i wɔ. Na Yesu abiri i tii asiɛŋgu sufa i saa maraka sukɛrɛ asiɛŋgu.

ref: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
text: Bu wo i bisa n su ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su ase bu ka, “Bo i ŋa wɔ nya yo má satiɛ am nu, ma i mi du mɔ tu i yabuɛ.”

ref: Once again he bent over and began writing on the ground.
text: Na wɔ kaaki abiri i tii sukɛrɛ asiɛŋgu.

ref: The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
text: Bu tiiri sɔ ni, na bu kɛrɛ alɔrɔki kun kun akɔ. Nkpiɛnm ni du mɔ a. Na wɔ ka Yesu ni bara ni ŋgumi.

ref: Jesus stood up and asked her, “Where is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?”
text: Na Yesu ayɛ i tii su, na wɔ bisa bara n ka, “Bara, bu wo ni? Be ase má ka i ti ye ni ka bu kun u, nzɛn sɛ?”

ref: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
text: Na bara n abɔ i nu ka, “Ai, m Mibiɛ, be ase má ka i kún m.” Na Yesu ase i ka, “Mini mmɔɔ se má ka be kún u. Kɔ, ama, má a yo satiɛ kun.”] Kabo Yesu ŋa yiri ti durunya n nu­fɔm wein n ni

ref: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase jama n ka, “Mini ti durunya n nu­fɔm wein a. Bo i su m su kɛrɛ wo má awosin nu kun, ama, i nyá wein bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni.”

ref: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
text: Na Farasi­fɔm n ase i ka, “Wɔrɔ, a sudi u ŋu daani wɔ, be de má u daani n ka i ti nahɔrɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Haari nzɛn m di m ŋu daani n mmɔɔ, m daani dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama m si deke n bo m fite baari, na m kaaki si deke bo m sukɔ ni. Ama, ambɛrɛ, am si má deke bo m fite, na am kaaki si má deke bo m sukɔ n gusu.

ref: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
text: Ambɛrɛ, am di jɔrɛ kabo durunya n nu­fɔm di bu beŋgum jɔrɛ n wɔ, ama, mini ni m tii, m di má be jɔrɛ.

ref: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
text: Ama, haari nzɛn m di minɛ jɔrɛ n mmɔɔ, m jɔrɛ dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama má mini ŋgumi di minɛm jɔrɛ a, ama, mini ni m Si bo i sunmaari m ni di a.

ref: I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
text: Mini ni m tii di m ŋu daani a, na m Si bo wɔ sunma m gusu di m daani.”

ref: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “U si wo ni?” Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, “Am si má m ni m Si n be n mmɔɔ. Nzɛn má am si mini, má am si m Si n mmɔɔ.”

ref: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
text: Saŋga bo Yesu seeri sɔ ni, i wo minɛm kere su Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, fa dodo deke bo bu yi bu saraka ŋwaa fa ma Nyɛmɛ n wɔ. Ama, be akoro atara má i, dama i saŋga nya ju má. Kabo Yesu seeri ka i fite má durunya n nu fɛn ni

ref: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, “M bá kɔ yaki am, na am bá niɛniɛ m busu, ama, am ŋú má m, na am bá wu ni am satiɛ ni. Deke bo m sukɔ ni, am koro kɔ má dɔ.”

ref: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
text: Na Yuda­fɔm n abisa ka, “I ŋa deke bo i sukɔ ni, ya koro kɔ má dɔ. Nyini la ka, i kún i ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ a?”

ref: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ, ama, mini fite aŋgoro wɔ. Ambɛrɛ ti durunya n nu fɛn fɔm wɔ, ama, mini fite má durunya n nu fɛn.

ref: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
text: Nyini ti ye ma se am ka, am wú ni am satiɛ n a, dama nzɛn am yo má m yarada ka mini la sɔnɔhɔrɛ n bo m se am ka m ti ni, am wú ni am satiɛ n wɔ.”

ref: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “Ye wɔrɔ la ŋma a?” Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Kabo m se am saŋga kɛrɛ ni, m ti sɔ ɔ.

ref: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
text: M la sa dɔŋgu bo m jɔjɔ fa kɔ am dɔ, na m la sa dɔŋgu bo m fa di am jɔrɛ jaraki am, ama, yiri bo i sunmaari m ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ, na deke bo i se m ni, nyini ye m kan kere durunya n nu­fɔm a.”

ref: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
text: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, ba si má ka i jɔjɔ niiri i Si Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

ref: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Am bá yɛɛri mini Adamande Wa n su baka jaanu n su, ye am sí ka mini la sɔnɔhɔrɛ n bo m se am kabo m ti n a, na m yo má deke be m dɛɛ yiko nu, ama, m se kabo m Si akere m wɔ.

ref: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
text: Yiri bo i sunmaari m n wo m dɔ, wɔ nya yaki má m cɛɛn be, dama saŋga kɛrɛ m yo deke bo i sun i nu wɔ.”

ref: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
text: I jɔjɔɔri sɔ ni, na jama n dɔŋgu ayo i yarada. Kabo Yesu seeri Yuda­fɔm ka bu satiɛ dɛɛ ti ma ba yo akɔɔm n a

ref: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
text: Na Yesu ase Yuda­fɔm bo bu yo i yarada n ka, “Nzɛn am de m nɔaniɛ ni, am ti m susu­fɔm wɔ nahɔrɛ su.

ref: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
text: Na am sí deke bo i ti nahɔrɛ ni, na nahɔrɛ ni má am nya am tii a.”

ref: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
text: Na ba se i ka, “Ya ti Abraham anumam wɔ, be nya fa má ya yo má i akɔɔm. Ye nzu ti ye a ŋa ya nyá ya tii a?”

ref: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ, i mi ti satiɛ akɔɔ.

ref: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
text: Akɔɔ­baa koro ka má awuru n nu saŋga kɛrɛ, ama, awuru­baa yiri wo awuru n nu wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: If the Son gives you freedom, you are free!
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn mini awuru­baa n nya deeri am tii jiiri, am nyá am tii nahɔrɛ su.

ref: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
text: M si ka am ti Abraham anumam wɔ, ama, am koro ka am kun m wɔ, dama am ade má m nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
text: Deke bo mini aŋu i m Si dɔ n ye m suse am a, ama, ambɛrɛ, deke bo am ti i am si dɔ, ye am yo a.”

ref: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
text: Na ba se i ka, “Ya si la Abraham.” Na wɔ se bu ka, “Nzɛn má am ti Abraham nbaam, má am yo i sa­yoo­wa a.

ref: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
text: Kabo i ti ni, ma se am nahɔrɛ sam bo m tiiri i Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, ama, am koro ka am kun m wɔ. Abraham ayo má nyini be sɔ.

ref: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
text: Ambɛrɛ, am yo am si sa­yoo­wa a.” Na ba se i ka, “Yɛrɛ, ya ti má soma nbaam. Ya si la Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi wɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nzɛn má Nyɛmɛ ti am Si, má am koro m, dama m fiteeri Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ye m baari fɛn n a. Ma ba má ni m dɛɛ yiko nu, ama, yiri sunmaari m a.

ref: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
text: Nzu ti ye am ti má m jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu a? Dama am koro de má m nɔaniɛ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
text: Sitana ti am si wɔ, na am koro ka am yo kabo am si koro n wɔ, i fite ka i bumboori kɛrɛ, i ti sɔnɔ kun­fɔ ɔ, na i su má nahɔrɛ su, dama nahɔrɛ haari kun mmɔɔ wo má i dɔ, i dɛɛ la ka i bu kɔrɛ wɔ, dama yiri ti kɔrɛ­fɔ a, na yiri kaaki ti kɔrɛ­fɔm kɛrɛ si a.

ref: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
text: Ama, mini se nahɔrɛ n ti, am de má m nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
text: Am ŋma koro kere m satiɛ bo ma yo i a? Nzu ti ye m se am nahɔrɛ, ye am de má m nɔaniɛ n a?

ref: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ. Deke bo i ti na am ti má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n la ka, am ti má i dɛɛm wɔ.” Kabo Yesu ti kpiri tara Abraham ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni

ref: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
text: Na Yuda­fɔm n ase i ka, “Bo ya ŋa a ti Samariya­fɔ ɔ, na ajini wo u ŋu ni, i ti má nahɔrɛ wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Ajini wo má m ŋu, ama, m bu m Si n wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ bu má m.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bita m nɔaniɛ ni, i mi wú má fieo.”

ref: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
text: Na ba se i ka, “Kisa, ye ya si ka ajini wo u ŋu a. Abraham ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni kɛrɛ awu, ama, wɔrɔ ŋa sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bita u nɔaniɛ ni, i mi wú má fieo.

ref: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
text: A tara ya si Abraham bo wɔ wu n wɔ? I ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ awu. A bu u ŋu sɛ wɔ?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nzɛn m wura m ŋu kpiri, i ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. M Si wura m kpiri a, yiri bo am ŋa i ti am Nyɛmɛ ni,

ref: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
text: ama, am si má i. Mini si i a. Haari nzɛn m ŋa m si má i, m yó kɔrɛ­fɔ ka ambɛrɛ wɔ. Ama, m si i, na m bita i nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, kana bu ba wuuri Abraham ni, mini wo bɛrɛ wɔ.”

ref: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
text: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba fa yabuɛ ka bu bo kun i, ama, na wɔ lɔrɔki afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu.

ref: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
text: Na saŋga bo Yesu wo natiiri su ni, na wɔ ŋu sɔnɔ be, bu wuuri i ni, i ti anyinsin wɔ.

ref: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
text: Na i susu­fɔm n abisa i ka, “Ya Kere­fɔ, nzu ti ye bu wuuri ahin sɔnɔ ni anyinsin a? I wu­fɔm yoori satiɛ, nzɛn i dɛɛ satiɛ a?”

ref: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “I ti má i ni i wu­fɔm be mmɔɔ satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ama, Nyɛmɛ koro ka minɛm ŋu kabo i yo alekutura sa fa ma i a.

ref: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
text: Kabo wiɛ toko wo bɛrɛ ni, am ma ya yo yiri n bo wɔ sunma m juma ni. Ale bá san bo be koro yo má juma be.

ref: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
text: Kabo m toko wo durunya n nu ni, mini ti durunya n nu­fɔm wein a.”

ref: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
text: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ yi nanzue agɔ asiɛŋgu, na wɔ fa akpita naatiɛ afa abɔrɔki anyinsin nyu­mbaa nnyɔ n kɛrɛ.

ref: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
text: Na wɔ se i ka, “Kɔ na a kɔ ŋmisi u nyunu Silowam bura n nu.” Silowam n bu la ka, ba sunma i wɔ. Na wɔ kɔ aŋmisi i nyunu n na wɔ ŋu asi, na wɔ kaaki aba awuru.

ref: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
text: Na i lamɔkɔm ni minɛm bo bu yo ŋuuri i saŋga kɛrɛ ka i sere ni, abisa ka, “Má ahin sɔnɔ ni yo tana fɛn sere n a?”

ref: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
text: Nbem ŋa, “Yiri a.” Nbem gusu ŋa, “Má yiri a. I ferema­fɔ a.” Na yiri n mmɔɔ ni i tii suse bu ka, “Mini a.”

ref: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Sɔnɔ n bo bu fere i ka Yesu ni, yiri kpitaari naatiɛ bɔrɔkiiri m nyu­mbaa n a. Na wɔ se m ka m kɔ Silowam bura n nu, na m kɔ ŋmisi m nyunu. Na ma kɔ aŋmisi, kpeŋga na ma ŋu asi.”

ref: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “Ye yiri n wo ni?” Na wɔ se bu ka, “M si má.” Kabo Farasi­fɔm bisaari sɔnɔ n fa kɔ i nyu­mbaa tekee­wa n su ni

ref: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n bo Yesu kpitaari naatiɛ na i fa tekeeri anyinsin nyu­mbaa n tiiri Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ.

ref: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
text: Nyini ti, na Farasi­fɔm gusu akaaki abisa i ka, “I yoori sɛ ye a ŋuuri asi a?” Na wɔ se bu ka, “I faari naatiɛ bɔrɔkiiri m nyu­mbaa n a, na ma ŋmisi i, na ma ŋu asi.”

ref: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
text: Na Farasi­fɔm nbem ase ka, “Ahin sɔnɔ n fite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, dama i bita má Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ciire ni.” Ama, na nbem gusu ase ka, “I yo sɛ ye satiɛ­yo­fɔ koro yo alekutura sam ka ahin ŋusu sɔ a?” Na bu kɛrɛ nɔɔ awo i ŋguŋgumi.

ref: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
text: Na ba kaaki abisa sɔnɔ n bo i yo tiiri anyinsin bekun ka, “Ye wɔrɔ bo wɔ teke u nyu­mbaa ni, a bu i sɛ wɔ?” Na wɔ se ka, “I ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ.”

ref: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
text: Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ade má ka i yo tiiri anyinsin wɔ, ye i ŋu asi kisa, má ka bu ba fereeri i si, ni i ni,

ref: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
text: na ba bisa bu ka, “Am wa n bo am ŋa am wuuri i na i ti anyinsin ni, yiri la ahin? Ye i yoori sɛ ye i ŋu asi kisa a?”

ref: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
text: Na i si, ni i ni abɔ bu nu ka, “Ya si i ka i ti ya wa a, ya wuuri i anyinsin wɔ.

ref: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
text: Ama, ya si má kabo i yoori barasu na i koro ŋu asi kisa ni, yɛrɛ gusu kaaki si má sɔnɔ bo i tekeeri i nyu­mbaa ni. Wɔ ju i tiɛŋgu sɔnɔ, am bisa i, yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii sé am.”

ref: The man's parents said this because they were afraid of their leaders. The leaders had already agreed that no one was to have anything to do with anyone who said Jesus was the Messiah.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye i si, ni i ni seeri ka, “Wɔ ju i tiɛŋgu sɔnɔ, am bisa yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii n a.”

ref: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
text: Na ba kaaki afere sɔnɔ n bo i yo tiiri anyinsin n bekun, na ba se i ka, “Niɛ Nyɛmɛ ti, na a jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ. Yɛrɛ si ka ahin sɔnɔ n ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ.”

ref: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Nzɛn i ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, i ti má satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, mini si má. Deke bo m si i n la ka, m yo tiiri anyinsin wɔ, ama, kisa m ŋu asi.”

ref: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
text: Na ba bisa i ka, “I yoori u sɛ wɔ, ye i tekeeri u nyu­mbaa n sɛ wɔ?”

ref: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, “Ma du mɔ ase am kabo i tekeeri m nyu­mbaa ni, ye am ade má. Ye nzu ti ye am koro ka m kan kere am bekun a? Ambɛrɛ gusu, am koro ka am yo i susu­fɔm wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?”

ref: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
text: Na ba tɔ i ŋu ni nzukɔ kpiɛ ase i ka, “Wɔrɔ ti nyini sɔnɔ n susu­fɔ a, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti Moyisi dɛɛ susu­fɔm wɔ.

ref: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
text: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ yo jɔjɔɔri Moyisi dɔ a, ama, nyini sɔnɔ n yiri, ya si má i fitee­biri n mmɔɔ.”

ref: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Ahin ti alekutura wanla, am si má i fitee­biri, ama, wɔ teke m nyu­mbaa.

ref: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
text: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ de má satiɛ­yo­fɔ nyɛmɛsere, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su Nyɛmɛ atin su, na i yo i sa koroo­wa, Nyɛmɛ de i sere wɔ.

ref: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
text: Fite durunya n bumboori, sɔnɔ be nya ti má ka be ateke sɔnɔ bo bu wuuri i na i ti anyinsin nyu­mbaa.

ref: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
text: Nzɛn má ahin sɔnɔ n afite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, má i koro yo má pui.”

ref: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
text: Na ba se i ka, “Wɔrɔ yiri, bu wuuri u satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, ye wɔrɔ koro ka a kere yɛrɛ a?” Na ba fɔn i afite. Kabo Yesu ŋa bo bu de má Adamande Wa n ti anyinsinm ni

ref: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
text: Yesu tiiri ka ba fɔn ayi sɔnɔ n ni, na wɔ kɔ aŋu i, na wɔ bisa i ka, “A yo Adamande Wa n yarada a?”

ref: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
text: Na sɔnɔ n abisa i ka, “M Mibiɛ, yiri la ŋma a? Se m na m yo i yarada.”

ref: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “A ŋu i e, yiri la mini n bo m sujɔjɔ u dɔ ni.”

ref: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
text: Na sɔnɔ n ase i ka, “M Mibiɛ, ma yo u yarada.” Na wɔ kutu Yesu nyunu akansi i.

ref: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
text: Na Yesu ase ka, “M baari durunya n nu ka m ba di minɛm jɔrɛ wɔ, na bo bu ŋu má asi n ŋú asi, na bo bu ŋu asi n yó anyinsinm.”

ref: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nzɛn má am ti anyinsinm, má am la má ŋgasi, ama, kabo am nya ŋa am ŋu asi ni, am toko la ŋgasi lɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
text: Na Yesu ase ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, sɔnɔ bo i lɔrɔki wura nbɔɛm sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ fa má anɔ n nu awura má, i mi ti awiefɔ ɔ, na i kaaki ti sɔnɔ bo i nati fin minɛm di wɔ.

ref: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
text: Anɔ niɛ­fɔ ni teke anɔ n ma i a, na i fere i dɛɛ nbɔɛm n duma, na i nbɔɛm ni ti i konvi, na i fa bu fite.

ref: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
text: Nzɛn i nya faari i nbɔɛm kɛrɛ fiteeri, i du bu mɔ ɔ, na i dɛɛ nbɔɛm n su i su, dama bu si i konvi.

ref: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
text: Sɔnɔ bo bu si má i, bu sú má i su fieo, bu ŋmáti yaki i wɔ, dama bu si má i konvi.”

ref: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
text: Na Yesu abo ahin miɛɛnu n akere bu, ama, ba ti má i bu. Kabo Yesu ŋa yiri ti bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpa n ni

ref: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
text: Bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu duuri m mɔ baari, na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu niɛ nbɔɛm su ni, bu ti awiefɔm wɔ, ni minɛm bo bu nati fin minɛm di wɔ, ama, nbɔɛm n ade má bu jɔjɔɔ­wa ni.

ref: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
text: Mini ti anɔ n a, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i fa m dɛɛ anɔ n wuraari, i nyá tii­de, na i wúra na i fíte, na i nyá i diire di.

ref: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
text: Awiefɔ dɛɛ la ka i ba wɔ ɔ, na i kun, na i saaki. Mini aba ka bu nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má aŋusu.

ref: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
text: Mini ti bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpa n a. Bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpa n tɔ i ŋgɔɔ ɔ, na i wu ma i nbɔɛm ni.

ref: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
text: Pa­di­fɔ n bo ba fa i ka i sasa nbɔɛm n ti má bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ nahɔrɛ su, na nbɔɛm n ti má i dɛɛm. Nyini ti, nzɛn i ba ŋuuri ka bedi suba, i ŋmati yaki nbɔɛm wɔ. Na bedi n wura bu nu tara nbem, na bo ba ka n sandi.

ref: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
text: M la nbɔɛ nyiŋga nbem bo bu ti m dɛɛm, bo bu wo má ahin nbɔɛm n nu. Wɔ yo daka ka m fa bu gusu ba wura ahinm n nu. Bɛrɛ gusu tí m konvi na bu dé i, na bu yó kun na bu sasa­fɔ yó kun.

ref: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
text: M tɔ m dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ fa ma m minɛm, na m káaki de i bekun, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m Si koro m n a.

ref: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
text: Be sude má m dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ ni m dɔ, ama, mini ni m tii sutɔ i m koroya­nu a. M la atin bo m tɔ m dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ ni, na m kaaki la atin bo m de i bekun. M Si maari m nɔɔ ka m yo sɔ a.”

ref: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
text: Yesu seeri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, na Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nɔɔ awo i ŋguŋgumi bekun.

ref: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
text: Na bu dɔŋgu n ase ka, “Ajini wo i ŋu, nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ bo fiɛ a. Nzu ti ye am so am sui i dɔ a?”

ref: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
text: Ama, nbem ŋa, “Má sɔnɔ bo ajini wo i ŋu jɔjɔɔ­wa la ahin. Ajini yo sɛ koro teke anyinsin nyu­mbaa?” Kabo Yuda­fɔm keteeri Yesu ni

ref: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
text: Na Yesu sunati bara yɛ Solomon Lɛlɛ n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni.

ref: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
text: Na Yuda­fɔ nbem atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i, na ba bisa i ka, “Saŋga bɔnɔ ye a bá kan teke ya nyi a? Nzɛn wɔrɔ ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, se ya nahɔrɛ kpain.”

ref: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Ma se am, ye am ade má a. Alekutura sam bo m yo bu m Si duma n nu ni, di m ŋu daani a.

ref: But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me.
text: Ama, am yo má m yarada, dama am ti má m dɛɛ nbɔɛm.

ref: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
text: M dɛɛ nbɔɛm ti m konvi. M si bu, na bu su m su.

ref: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
text: M ma bu ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na bu ká má ŋgbɛɛn cɛɛn be fieo. Be gusu koro fin má bu fite má m saa nu.

ref: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
text: M Si bo i faari bu maari m ni, tara deke kɛrɛ faŋga, na be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fin bu fite m Si saa nu.

ref: and I am one with the Father.
text: Mini ni m Si ti deke kun wɔ.”

ref: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm afa yabuɛ bekun ka bu bo kun i.

ref: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Ma yo alekutura sakpam dɔŋgu, bo m Si kereeri m na am aŋu i, ye sa bɔnɔ ti ye am ŋa am kun m n a?”

ref: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
text: Na Yuda­fɔm n ase i ka, “Má sakpam bo a yo bu n be dɛɛ ti ye ya ŋa ya kún u a, ama, bo a jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu n ti, dama a ti adamande wɔ, ama, na a se ka a ti Nyɛmɛ wɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied: In your Scriptures doesn't God say, “You are gods”?
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Ba kɛrɛ i am Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, ‘Am ti ka Nyɛmɛ wɔ.’

ref: You can't argue with the Scriptures, and God spoke to those people and called them gods.
text: Be koro bu má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi ni ŋgbɛɛn. Nyɛmɛ yo seeri bu ka bu ti ka Nyɛmɛ wɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni,

ref: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
text: ama, mini yiri, m Si Nyɛmɛ ayi m, ye wɔ sunma m durunya n nu a. Ye nzu ti ye ambɛrɛ ŋa ma kan ciire jɔrɛ, dama m ŋa mini ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a?

ref: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
text: Nzɛn m yo má m Si juma ni, má am yo m yarada.

ref: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
text: Ama, nzɛn m yo bu na haari am yo má m yarada mmɔɔ a, am yo m juma n yarada, adena am si nahɔrɛ su ka mini ni m Si n ti deke kun wɔ.”

ref: Again they wanted to arrest Jesus. But he escaped
text: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba kpi ka bu tara i bekun, na wɔ lɔrɔki afite bu nu.

ref: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
text: Na Yesu akaaki akpiɛ akɔ Yodan kunma n buekun dɔ akɔ aju deke bo Yohane yo biɛɛri minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, na wɔ wo dɔ.

ref: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
text: Na jama beberebe aba i dɔ, na ba se ka, “Yohane ayo má alekutura sa be, ama, jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ bo i yo jɔjɔ fa kɔɔri ahin sɔnɔ n ŋusu n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.”

ref: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
text: Na minɛ dɔŋgu ayo i yarada dɔ.

ref: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
text: Na i ninbaa nbaa­baram n asunma ka bu kɔ se Yesu ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, u danfo n bo a koro i agaya n ada tukpaki.”

ref: But he stayed where he was for two more days.
text: Ama, i tiiri ka Lazarosi ada tukpaki ni, na wɔ ka deke bo i woori n cɛɛn nnyɔ abuka su.

ref: Then he said to his disciples, “Now we will go back to Judea.”
text: Nyini sin na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ka, “Am ma ya kaaki kɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.”

ref: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
text: Na i susu­fɔm n ase i ka, “Ya Kere­fɔ, má anzɔni fɛn ye Yuda­fɔm ŋa bu tu yabuɛ bo kun u n a? Ye a sukoro ka ya kpie ya nyi kɔ dɔ bekun n a?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Fa ŋgarama kɔ ju naasɔ, m ŋa i ti wiɛ kun wɔ e. Sɔnɔ bo i nati wiɛsu bo má i jaa, dama durunya n nu wein ni ma i ŋu asi a.

ref: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
text: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i nati kɔŋguɛ bo i jaa, dama wein wo má i dɔ.”

ref: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
text: Yesu jɔjɔɔri sɔ n sin, na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, “Ya danfo Lazarosi adaafi, ama, m sukɔ na m kɔ teŋge i lɛɛ.”

ref: They replied, “Lord, if he is asleep, he will get better.”
text: Na i susu­fɔm n ase i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, nzɛn wɔ daafi wɔ, i nyá laifiɛ.”

ref: Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
text: Kabo Yesu seeri n la ka, Lazarosi awu wɔ, ama, i susu­fɔm bɛrɛ sunsun ka i ŋa wɔ daafi nahɔrɛ su wɔ.

ref: I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.”
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo m fiɛ ka ma wo má dɔ, adena am yo yarada. Am ma ya kɔ i dɔ.”

ref: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
text: Na Tomasi bo bu fere i ka Ndaa n ase i lamɔkɔ susu­fɔm n ka, “Am ma yɛrɛ gusu kɔ, nzɛn bu kɔ kuunri i a, na ya ni yiri kɛrɛ awu.” Kabo Yesu ŋa yiri teŋge minɛm yiwee nu ni

ref: When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
text: Saŋga bo Yesu kɔ juuri ni, i ŋuuri ka ba se Lazarosi i le­nna lɛɛ.

ref: Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem,
text: Betani ni Yɛrusalem miɛm waari má ni i beŋgu.

ref: and many people had come from the city to comfort Martha and Mary because their brother had died.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm dɔŋgu aba Marata, ni Mariya dɔ ka bu ba dada bu ahore, bu niwa n bo wɔ wu n dɛɛ ti.

ref: When Martha heard that Jesus had arrived, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed in the house.
text: Marata tiiri ka Yesu suba ni, na wɔ kpa i atin, ama, na Mariya aka awuru.

ref: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
text: Na Marata ase Yesu ka, “M Mibiɛ, nzɛn má a woori fɛn, má m niwa n awu má.

ref: Jesus told her, “Your brother will live again!”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “U niwa ni téŋge i yiwee n nu bekun.”

ref: Martha answered, “I know he will be raised to life on the last day, when all the dead are raised.”
text: Na Marata ase i ka, “M si ka i bá teŋge i yiwee nu jina cɛɛn, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee nu ni.”

ref: Jesus then said, “I am the one who raises the dead to life! Everyone who has faith in me will live, even if they die.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Mini ti sɔnɔ bo i teŋge minɛm bu yiwee nu, na i má bu ŋgɔɔ n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, haari nzɛn i wuuri, i nyá ŋgɔɔ.

ref: “Yes, Lord!” she replied. “I believe you are the Christ, the Son of God. You are the one we hoped would come into the world.”
text: Na Marata ase i ka, “M Mibiɛ, ma yo yarada ka wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, bo i ŋa i súnma i durunya n nu ni.” Kabo Yesu suunri ni

ref: After Martha said this, she went and privately said to her sister Mary, “The Teacher is here, and he wants to see you.”
text: Marata seeri sɔ n sin, na wɔ kaaki akɔ afere i niwa Mariya bɛnlɛ, na wɔ se i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ n aba, i ŋa a bra.”

ref: As soon as Mary heard this, she got up and went out to Jesus.
text: Mariya tiiri sɔ ni, i kaba bereki yasuuri wɔ, na wɔ kɔ i dɔ.

ref: He was still outside the village where Martha had gone to meet him.
text: Nyini saŋga, na Yesu nya ju má miɛ n nu, i toko wo deke bo Marata kɔ tuuri i n wɔ.

ref: Many people had come to comfort Mary, and when they saw her quickly leave the house, they thought she was going out to the tomb to cry. So they followed her.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm bo bu woori awuru n nu Mariya bi, na bu sudada i ahore ni, bu ŋuuri kabo i bereki yasu fiteeri ni, na ba su i su. Bu sunsuunri ka i sukɔ deke bo bu seeri Lazarosi n wɔ, na i kɔ sun.

ref: Mary went to where Jesus was. Then as soon as she saw him, she knelt at his feet and said, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
text: Saŋga bo Mariya kɔ juuri deke bo Yesu wo n na i ŋuuri i ni, na wɔ kutu i nyunu, na wɔ se i ka, “M Mibiɛ, nzɛn má a woori fɛn, má m niwa n awu má.”

ref: When Jesus saw that Mary and the people with her were crying, he was terribly upset
text: Yesu ŋuuri ka i susun ni, ni kabo Yuda­fɔm n bo bu suuri i su baari n gusu wo sun su ni, na i ahore asaaki, na wɔ mi i ŋumi.

ref: and asked, “Where have you put his body?” They replied, “Lord, come and you will see.”
text: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, “Am seeri i ni wɔ?” Na ba se i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, bra na a ba niɛ.”

ref: Jesus started crying,
text: Na Yesu asun.

ref: and the people said, “See how much he loved Lazarus.”
text: Nyini ti na Yuda­fɔm n ase ka, “Am niɛ kabo i koro i agaya barasu a.”

ref: Some of them said, “He gives sight to the blind. Why couldn't he have kept Lazarus from dying?”
text: Ama, na bu nbem ase ka, “Yiri n bo i teke anyinsin n nyu­mbaa ni, má i koro tuntun má sɔnɔ ni, na i wu má, nzɛn sɛ?” Kabo Yesu teŋgeeri Lazarosi i yiwee nu ni

ref: Jesus was still terribly upset. So he went to the tomb, which was a cave with a stone rolled against the entrance.
text: Na Yesu ahore asaaki bekun, na wɔ kɔ deke bo bu seeri Lazarosi ni. I ti yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma wɔ, ye bu faari i seeri nu a, na ba fa yabuɛ nyiŋga atɔ anɔ ni.

ref: Then he told the people to roll the stone away. But Martha said, “Lord, you know that Lazarus has been dead four days, and there will be a bad smell.”
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Am ba yi yabuɛ ni.” Na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ wu n niwa Marata ase i ka, “M Mibiɛ, kisa wɔ bun, dama bu seeri i nyuma le­nna lɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “Didn't I tell you that if you had faith, you would see the glory of God?”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Ma se má u ka, nzɛn a yoori m yarada, a ŋú Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa kabo i ti barasu?”

ref: After the stone had been rolled aside, Jesus looked up toward heaven and prayed, “Father, I thank you for answering my prayer.
text: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba lakati yabuɛ ni. Na wɔ yɛ i tii su aŋgoro, na wɔ se ka, “M Si, m da u asi ka a de m sere.

ref: I know that you always answer my prayers. But I said this, so the people here would believe you sent me.”
text: M si ka saŋga kɛrɛ a de m sere wɔ, ama, ahin minɛm bo bu jina fɛn n dɛɛ ti, ye m seeri sɔ a, adena bu yo m yarada ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.”

ref: When Jesus had finished praying, he shouted, “Lazarus, come out!”
text: I sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri ni, na wɔ tiɛn ase ka, “Lazarosi, fite.”

ref: The man who had been dead came out. His hands and feet were wrapped with strips of burial cloth, and a cloth covered his face. Jesus then told the people, “Untie him and let him go.” The Plot To Kill Jesus (Matthew
text: Na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ wu n afite faŋ­kunma n nu. Tani pɔɔndiiri i saa, ni i jaa, na tani kun pɔɔndiiri i tii. Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Am nyaŋgi yi tani n na am ma i kɔ.” Kabo bu sɛsɛɛri su ka bu kun Yesu ni

ref: Many of the people who had come to visit Mary saw the things Jesus did, and they put their faith in him.
text: Yuda­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu baari Mariya dɔ ŋuuri sa n bo Yesu yoori i ni, na ba yo i yarada.

ref: Others went to the Pharisees and told what Jesus had done.
text: Na bu nbem akɔ Farasi­fɔm n dɔ akɔ ase bu kabo Yesu ayo ni.

ref: Then the chief priests and the Pharisees called the council together and said, “What should we do? This man is working a lot of miracles.
text: Na Farasi­fɔm ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n afere Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm ni, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ. Na ba se ka, “Ye ya yo sɛ wɔ? Am niɛ e, ahin sɔnɔ ni suyo alekutura sa­yoo­wam dɔŋgu.

ref: If we don't stop him now, everyone will put their faith in him. Then the Romans will come and destroy our temple and our nation.”
text: Nzɛn ya yaki i na i suyo sɔ, minɛm kɛrɛ yó i yarada a, na Roma yiko­fɔm bá saaki ya Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni, na bu kún ya minɛm ni.”

ref: One of the council members was Caiaphas, who was also high priest that year. He spoke up and said, “You people don't have any sense at all!
text: Na bu nu sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Kayafasi ni, bo i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn nyini afɔ n ase ka, “Ambɛrɛ, am si má pui.

ref: Don't you know it is better for one person to die for the people than for the whole nation to be destroyed?”
text: Am si má kabo i ti n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ka nzɛn am ma sɔnɔ kun wu maari minɛm kɛrɛ, i ti ye ni bo ya minɛm kɛrɛ bá wu ni.”

ref: Caiaphas did not say this on his own. As high priest that year, he was prophesying that Jesus would die for the nation.
