ref: Yet Jesus would not die just for the Jewish nation. He would die to bring together all of God's scattered people.
text: Na má bɛrɛ ŋgumi dɛɛ ti ye i wú a, ama, adena i tiɛn Nyɛmɛ nbaam nɔɔ, na bu yo kun, bɛrɛ bo ba sandi akɔ asiɛn nbem n su.

ref: From that day on, the council started making plans to put Jesus to death.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, fite nyini cɛɛn n fa kɔ, na ba sɛsɛ su ka bu kun Yesu.

ref: Because of this plot against him, Jesus stopped going around in public. He went to the town of Ephraim, which was near the desert, and he stayed there with his disciples.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yi má i ŋu fofoe nu anati má Yuda­fɔm nu kun. Ama, i fiteeri bɛrɛ ni, na wɔ kɔ awɔrɛ be bo i wo dodo boro kpantain dɛɛ bue ni, na i ni i susu­fɔm n akɔ awo miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Efirayim n su.

ref: It was almost time for Passover. Many of the Jewish people who lived out in the country had come to Jerusalem to get themselves ready for the festival.
text: Nyini saŋga, wɔ ka kaan, na Yuda­fɔm di bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni, na minɛ dɔŋgu afite ahɛmɛm aba Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu ba kpakpara bu ŋu fin ka, na cɛɛn­kpiri n nya ju.

ref: They looked around for Jesus. Then when they were in the temple, they asked each other, “You don't think he will come here for Passover, do you?”
text: Na ba niɛniɛ Yesu busu, na saŋga bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Am sunsun sɛ wɔ? I bá cɛɛn­kpiri ni? Ya de má ka i bá.”

ref: The chief priests and the Pharisees told the people to let them know if any of them saw Jesus. This is how they hoped to arrest him.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Farasi­fɔm n ama nɔɔ ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i si deke bo Yesu wo, i se bu na bu kɔ tara i.

ref: If you serve me, you must go with me. My servants will be with me wherever I am. If you serve me, my Father will honor you.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su m, wɔ yo daka ka i mi su m su, na deke bo m wo ni, m amusui­su­fɔ gusu wó dɔ ɔ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i suuri m, m Si Nyɛmɛ wúra i mi kpiri.” Kabo Yesu jɔjɔ fa kɔɔri i yiwee n jɔrɛ su ni

ref: Judas did not really care about the poor. He asked this because he carried the moneybag and sometimes would steal from it.
text: I ti awiefɔ n dɛɛ ti ye i seeri sɔ a, má ka i cɛcɛ wo yaari­fɔm nu wɔ. Yiri ti bu ŋwaa bita­fɔ a, na i suwɔ ŋwaa n be saŋga saŋga.

ref: they took palm branches and went out to greet him. They shouted, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord! God bless the King of Israel!”
text: Na ba kpiɛkpiɛ kpakpa nyawa akɔ akpa i atin, na bu subo kuyigindi, bu ŋa, “Hosana. Ya kansi Nyɛmɛ. Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ. Tere­fiɛ wo Yisareli­fɔm fɛmɛ n dɔ.”

ref: Jesus found a donkey and rode on it, just as the Scriptures say,
text: Na Yesu aŋu kaako­baa be, na wɔ fun atana su, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni,

ref: Mary took a very expensive bottle of perfume and poured it on Jesus' feet. She wiped them with her hair, and the sweet smell of the perfume filled the house.
text: Na Mariya afa tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani bo i la gɔ agaya tuwa bambaka kun, na wɔ fa agɔ Yesu jaa su, na wɔ fa i tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin anunuka i jaam kɛrɛ su, na tulaalɛɛ ni nvani ade awuru n kɛrɛ.

ref: A lot of people came when they heard that Jesus was there. They also wanted to see Lazarus, because Jesus had raised him from death.
text: Yuda­fɔm beberebe tiiri ka Yesu woori dɔ ni, na ba ba i dɔ, má ka Yesu ŋgumi ti ye bu baari a, ama, bu baari ka bu ba ŋu Lazarosi n bo Yesu teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu n wɔ.

ref: A meal had been prepared for Jesus. Martha was doing the serving, and Lazarus himself was there.
text: Na ba su Yesu wɔfɔ dɔ. Marata faari diire n ba maari bu a, Lazarosi gusu woori minɛm bo bu ni Yesu diiri diire n nu.

ref: A disciple named Judas Iscariot was there. He was the one who was going to betray Jesus, and he asked,
text: Na Yesu susu­fɔm kun bo bu fere i ka Yudasi Isikariyoti bo i bá tɔ Yesu n abisa ka,

ref: Jesus replied, “Leave her alone! She has kept this perfume for the day of my burial.
text: Na Yesu ase ka, “Yaki Mariya bɛnlɛ, i dɛɛ nzee bo i sufa yo m fa kɔ m yiwee cɛɛn lɛɛ.

ref: You will always have the poor with you, but you won't always have me.” A Plot To Kill Lazarus
text: Dama am ni yaari­fɔm ni wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, ama, am ni mini koro wo má bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.”

ref: So the chief priests made plans to kill Lazarus.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n asɛsɛ su ka bu kun Lazarosi gusu.

ref: Philip from Bethsaida in Galilee was there too. So they went to him and said, “Sir, we would like to meet Jesus.”
text: Na ba ba Filipu dɔ, i fite miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Besayida na i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu n wɔ. Na ba se i ka, “Njabiɛ, ya koro ka ya ŋu Yesu wɔ.”

ref: But the Pharisees said to each other, “There is nothing we can do! Everyone in the world is following Jesus.” Some Greeks Want To Meet Jesus
text: Na Farasi­fɔm n ase bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Am aŋu má i? Ya koro yo má pui kun. Yiri ye durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ susu i su n a.” Kabo Giriki­fɔ nbem ba bisaari Yesu busu ni

ref: So Father, bring glory to yourself. A voice from heaven then said, “I have already brought glory to myself, and I will do it again!”
text: M dɛɛ bo m se la ka, m Si, kere kabo u jirima woo­wa ti.” Na akonvi be atiɛn afite aŋgoro ase ka, “Ma du mɔ akere kabo m jirima woo­wa ti, na m káaki kere i bekun.”

ref: “The Lord has blinded the eyes of the people, and he has made the people stubborn. He did this so that they could not see or understand, and so that they would not turn to the Lord and be healed.”
text: “Nyɛmɛ ama ba yo anyinsinm na má bu ŋu asi, na wɔ ma bu ahore ayo kekereke na má bu ti i nɔaniɛ n bu, adena má bu kaaki ba i dɔ na bu nya laifiɛ.”

ref: The next day a large crowd was in Jerusalem for Passover. When they heard that Jesus was coming for the festival,
text: Na alecin na jama n bo bu baari ka bu ba di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n ati ka Yesu suba Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: “People of Jerusalem, don't be afraid! Your King is now coming, and he is riding on a donkey.”
text: “Ambɛrɛ Siyon­fɔm, má am soro sɛrɛ. Am niɛ, am fɛmɛ ni suba n a, i fun kaako­baa wɔ.”

ref: A crowd had come to meet Jesus because they had seen him call Lazarus out of the tomb. They kept talking about him and this miracle.
text: Nyini alekutura sa n bo i yoori i na minɛm tiiri n dɛɛ ti, na ba kpa i atin.

ref: Philip told Andrew. Then the two of them went to Jesus and told him. The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up
text: Na Filipu akɔ ase Andere, na bu nnyɔ n kɛrɛ akɔ ase Yesu.

ref: Jesus said: The time has come for the Son of Man to be given his glory.
text: Na Yesu ase ka, “Saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ wúra mini Adamande Wa n kpiri.

ref: I tell you for certain that a grain of wheat that falls on the ground will never be more than one grain unless it dies. But if it dies, it will produce lots of wheat.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má ka alɛ­mbaa awura fa nu, na wɔ kpɔrɔ, i ti i tii wɔ. Ama, nzɛn i wuraari fa nu na i kpɔrɔɔri, ye i wúra mbaa beberebe a.

ref: If you love your life, you will lose it. If you give it up in this world, you will be given eternal life.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i ŋgɔɔ, i mi bá fun i, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i ŋgɔɔ durunya n nu fɛn, i mi bá nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

ref: Now I am deeply troubled, and I don't know what to say. But I must not ask my Father to keep me from this time of suffering. In fact, I came into the world to suffer.
text: Na Yesu ase ka, “Kisa, m ahore asaaki, ye m se sɛ wɔ? M koro se ka, m Si Nyɛmɛ, yi m fite ahin anyum­boro bo i suba m dɔ n nu, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, m koro se má sɔ, dama nyini dɛɛ ti ye m baari a.

ref: When the crowd heard the voice, some of them thought it was thunder. Others thought an angel had spoken to Jesus.
text: Jama n bo bu jina Yesu bi n tiiri akonvi ni ŋga ni, na bu nbem ase ka, “Nzue akpura.” Na nbem gusu ase ka, “Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ ajɔjɔ i dɔ a.”

ref: Then Jesus told the crowd, “That voice spoke to help you, not me.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye akonvi n jɔjɔɔri a, má mini dɛɛ ti a.

ref: This world's people are now being judged, and the ruler of this world is already being thrown out!
text: Saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ dí durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ, na i fɔ́n yi Sitana bo i ti bu fɛmɛ ni.

ref: If I am lifted up above the earth, I will make everyone want to come to me.”
text: Ama, mini, bu ba yɛɛri m su jinaari aŋgoro baka jaanu n su, m cín sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ba m dɔ.”

ref: Jesus was talking about the way he would be put to death.
text: Kabo i yo seeri sɔ n kere yiwee n bo i bá wu i n wɔ.

ref: The crowd said to Jesus, “The Scriptures teach that the Messiah will live forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
text: Na jama n ase i ka, “Ya ti i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ. I wú má. Ye i yoori sɛ ye wɔrɔ ŋa, wɔ yo daka ka bu bá yɛɛri Adamande Wa n su jinaari aŋgoro baka jaanu n su a? Ye Adamande Wa n la ŋma a?”

ref: Jesus answered, “The light will be with you for only a little longer. Walk in the light while you can. Then you won't be caught walking blindly in the dark.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Mini bo m ti wein ni, m wo am nu kisa, ama, m cɛ́ má. Nyini dɛɛ ti, am nya wo ni wein ni, am sunati wein nu wɔ, na má awosin tu am. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo awosin nu, i mi si má deke bo i sukɔ ni.

ref: Have faith in the light while it is with you, and you will be children of the light.” The People Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus After Jesus had said these things, he left and went into hiding.
text: Am nya wo ni wein ni, am yo i yarada, na m má am yo wein dɛɛ nbaam.” Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ fite bu nu akɔ afiɛ deke be, na má be ŋu i. Kabo Yuda­fɔm ayo má Yesu yarada ni

ref: He had worked a lot of miracles among the people, but they were still not willing to have faith in him.
text: Yesu yo yoori alekutura sam dɔŋgu na ba ŋu i, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ba yo má i yarada.

ref: This happened so that what the prophet Isaiah had said would come true, “Lord, who has believed our message? And who has seen your mighty strength?”
text: Nyini ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya jɔrɛ n bo i yo kɛrɛɛri n yo yii­wa. I ŋa, “M Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ, ŋma ade jɔrɛ n bo ya kan i n a? Ye ŋma ye a yi u yiko akere i a?”

ref: The people could not have faith in Jesus, because Isaiah had also said,
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ba koro ayo má Yesu yarada n a. Kabo Esaya akaaki ase ka,

ref: Isaiah said this, because he saw the glory of Jesus and spoke about him.
text: Esaya yo ŋuuri Yesu jirima woo­wa n dɛɛ ti, ye i kaanri i ŋu jɔrɛ n a.

ref: Even then, many of the leaders put their faith in Jesus, but they did not tell anyone about it. The Pharisees had already given orders for the people not to have anything to do with anyone who had faith in Jesus.
text: Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm dɔŋgu mmɔɔ yoori Yesu yarada, ama, bu soroori Farasi­fɔm n ahore n dɛɛ ti, ba koro ayi má bu ŋu fofoe nu. Nzɛn bu yiiri bu ŋu ka ba yo i yarada, Farasi­fɔm ni fɔ́n bu fite bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu wɔ.

ref: And besides, the leaders liked praise from others more than they liked praise from God. Jesus Came To Save the World
text: Dama Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n yo koroori adamande kansi taraari Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ. Kabo Yesu seeri ka i nɔaniɛ ni dí minɛm jɔrɛ ni

ref: In a loud voice Jesus said: Everyone who has faith in me also has faith in the one who sent me.
text: Na Yesu atiɛn ase ka, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, má mini ŋgumi ye i yo m yarada, ama, i yo bo wɔ sunma m n gusu yarada a.

ref: And everyone who has seen me has seen the one who sent me.
text: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i niɛ na i ŋuuri m, ti ka wɔ ŋu m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i sunmaari m n lɛɛ.

ref: I am the light that has come into the world. No one who has faith in me will stay in the dark.
text: Mini bo m ti wein n aba durunya n nu, adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, i wó má awosin nu.

ref: I am not the one who will judge those who refuse to obey my teachings. I came to save the people of this world, not to be their judge.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti m nɔaniɛ n na i de má i, m di má i mi jɔrɛ, dama ma ba má ka m ba di durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, m baari ka m ba de bu tii ji wɔ.

ref: But everyone who rejects me and my teachings will be judged on the last day by what I have said.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kete m, na i de má m nɔaniɛ ni, i jɔrɛ­di­fɔ wo bɛrɛ wɔ. Nɔaniɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ i ni, yiri dí i mi jɔrɛ jina cɛɛn n a.

ref: I don't speak on my own. I say only what the Father who sent me has told me to say.
text: Dama má m koroya­nu ye m kan sɔ a, ama, m Si n bo wɔ sunma m ni, yiri maari m nɔɔ, kereeri m nɔaniɛ kun kun kɛrɛ bo m jɔjɔ i n a.

ref: I know that his commands will bring eternal life. This is why I tell you exactly what the Father has told me.
text: M si ka i nɔaniɛ maa­wa n ti deke bo i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo m Si seeri m ni, sɔ ye m jɔjɔ a.”

ref: It was before Passover, and Jesus knew that the time had come for him to leave this world and to return to the Father. He had always loved his followers in this world, and he loved them to the very end.
text: Alecin bo má bu di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n ni, na Yesu asi nu ka saŋga n aju bo i fíte durunya n nu kaaki kɔ i Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Na i dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm bo bu wo durunya n nu na i koro bu ni, i koro bu sɔ ɔ haari i kɔ ju i faŋga nyaari.

ref: Even before the evening meal started, the devil had made Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, decide to betray Jesus.
text: Na kɔŋguɛ saŋga bo bu wo diire n di su ni, na Sitana awura Yudasi, Simon Isikariyoti wa n tii nu ka i tɔ Yesu ma i kpɔ­fɔm.

ref: Jesus knew he had come from God and would go back to God. He also knew that the Father had given him complete power.
text: Na Yesu asi nu ka i Si Nyɛmɛ afa deke n kɛrɛ awura i saa nu, na i kaaki si ka i fiteeri Nyɛmɛ dɔ baari a, na i káaki kɔ i dɔ ɔ.

ref: So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
text: Na wɔ nya yasu ajina, na wɔ yi i tanlɛɛ n ase, na wɔ fa papawo abo i wi nu.

ref: He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
text: Na wɔ sa nzue agɔ nyaŋga nu, na wɔ bo i bu suŋmisi i susu­fɔm n jaa, na wɔ fa papawo n bo i bo i wi nu ni sunuka bu jaam.

ref: But when he came to Simon Peter, this disciple asked, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
text: I ba kpuraari Simon Petoro, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, “M Mibiɛ, na wɔrɔ ba ŋmisi m dɛɛ jaa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “You don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Sa bo m suyo i ni, a koro ti má i bu kisa, ama, fa kɔ fa ba, a bá ti i bu.”

ref: “You will never wash my feet!” Peter replied. “If I don't wash you,” Jesus told him, “you don't really belong to me.”
text: Na Petoro ase i ka, “Ai, a ŋmísi má m jaa haari durunya n ba yasu.” Na Yesu ase i ka, “Nzɛn ma ŋmisi má u jaa, m ni wɔrɔ akpaaki lɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Sɔnɔ anya abiɛ nzue, ŋka i ŋmisi i jaa a, i biɛ má nzue kun, dama i ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ ti casi wɔ. Ambɛrɛ, am ti casi wɔ, ama, má am kɛrɛ.”

ref: Jesus knew who would betray him. That is why he said, “except for one of you.”
text: Yesu siiri bo i bá tɔ i ma i kpɔ­fɔm ni, nyini ti ye i ŋa má bu kɛrɛ ti casi n a.

ref: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
text: I ŋmisi bu jaam wieeri ni, na wɔ fa i tanlɛɛ n awura, na wɔ kɔ atana i tanaa­biri ni. Na wɔ bisa bu ka, “Kabo ma yo am ni, am ati i bu?

ref: You call me your teacher and Lord, and you should, because that is who I am.
text: Am fere m ka am Kere­fɔ ɔ, na am kaaki fere m ka am Mibiɛ. Am jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama m ti sɔ ɔ.

ref: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
text: Dama ma yo akere am lɛɛ, na am gusu yo am beŋgum kabo ma yo am ni.”

ref: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, akɔɔ koro tara má i mibiɛ, na bekun sunma­fɔ gusu koro tara má bo i sunmaari i ni.

ref: I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, “The man who ate with me has turned against me!”
text: M sujɔjɔ ni má am kɛrɛ wɔ, dama m si bo ma du mɔ ayi bu ni. Ama, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, i ti ye ni ka i yo yii­wa, ka, ‘Sɔnɔ n bo m ni yiri saŋga di diire taalɛ kun nu, yiri akaaki m jaa tu­fɔ n a.’

ref: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
text: M suse am sa bo i bá yo wɔ lɛɛ, na i ba yoori, na am yo m yarada ka mini la sɔnɔhɔrɛ n bo ma se am kabo m ti n a.

ref: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
text: Na yiri n acin adodo Yesu, na wɔ bisa i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, a ni ŋma a?”

ref: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
text: Yesu seeri bu sɔ wieeri ni, na i ahore asaaki akɔ asin su, na wɔ se bu kpain ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, am nu kun bá tɔ m ma m kpɔ­fɔm a.”

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
text: Bu nu kun be bo Yesu koro i agaya n tana mata Yesu.

ref: and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant.
text: Na Simon Petoro abo i nyu­mbaa i dɔ ka i bisa Yesu ka, i ni ŋma a?

ref: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
text: Yudasi tiiri bu ŋwaa bita­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, bu nbem sunsun ka Yesu ŋa i kɔ to deke be na bu fa di cɛɛn­kpiri n wɔ, wara i kɔ yi ŋwaa n be fa ma yaari­fɔm wɔ.

ref: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
text: Yudasi diiri too n wieeri ni, na wɔ yasu afite akɔ. Nyini saŋga ni, i ti kɔŋguɛ wɔ. Kabo Yesu maari bu nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ ni

ref: Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
text: Na nzɛn bu wuraari Nyɛmɛ kpiri m dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ gusu wúra m kpiri, na Nyɛmɛ má i yo kisa bɛrɛ.”

ref: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “M nbaam, m cɛ́ má am nu fɛn. Am bá niɛniɛ m busu fie wɔ, na kabo m yo seeri Yuda­fɔm ni, sɔ ye m suse am kisa, ka am koro kɔ má deke bo m sukɔ ni.

ref: Simon Peter asked, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “You can't go with me now, but later on you will.”
text: Na Simon Petoro abisa i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, a kɔ́ ni wɔ?” Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, “Deke bo m sukɔ ni, a koro su má m su kisa, ama, saŋga suba bo a bá su m su.”

ref: Peter asked, “Lord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!”
text: Na Petoro akaaki abisa i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, nzu ti ye m koro su má u su kisa? Nzɛn i yo yiwee mmɔɔ a, m wú u dɛɛ ti.”

ref: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
text: Na Yesu abisa i ka, “A koro wu m dɛɛ ti? Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a, kana akɔnyuma bá bo, a kéte kpe nsan su ka a si má m.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
text: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, “Má am ma am ahore saaki. Am yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na am yo m gusu yarada.

ref: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
text: M Si Nyɛmɛ dika n nu, sɔɔm sunnu dɔ, nzɛn má i ti má sɔ, má m se má am. M kɔ́ na m kɔ sɛsɛ woo­biri su se am.

ref: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
text: Na nzɛn m kɔ sɛsɛ woo­biri n su seeri am, m káaki ba fa am kɔ deke bo m kɔ́ wo ni, na deke bo m wó ni, am gusu wó dɔ ɔ.

ref: You know the way to where I am going.”
text: Na deke bo m sukɔ ni, am si atin ni.”

ref: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
text: Na Tomasi ase i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, ya si má deke bo a sukɔ ni, ye ya yo sɛ si atin n a?”

ref: “I am the way, the truth, and the life!” Jesus answered. “Without me, no one can go to the Father.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Mini ti atin n a, ni nahɔrɛ, ni ŋgɔɔ n a. Mini ŋgumi ti atin n bo sɔnɔ koro fa i na i kɔ m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

ref: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
text: Nzɛn má am siiri m, má am si m Si mmɔɔ, ama, fa nyuma fa kɔ, am asi i, na am aŋu i.”

ref: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.”
text: Na Filipu ase i ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, kere ya u Si ni, deke bo ya koro i kɛrɛ lɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
text: Na Yesu abisa i ka, “Filipu, bo m ni ambɛrɛ awo bɛrɛ acɛ sɔ n na a si má m? Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ ŋu mini aŋu m Si lɛɛ, ye nzu ti ye am ŋa m kere am m Si n a?

ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
text: A yo má yarada ka m ni m Si n ti deke kun wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Jɔrɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ ama am ni, má m tiɛŋgu jɔjɔɔ­wa, ama, m Si bo m ni yiri ti kun ni, yo i juma.

ref: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
text: Bo m ŋa, m ni m Si n ti deke kun ni, am yo m yarada. Nzɛn má nyini, am yo m yarada sam bo m yo bu n dɛɛ ti.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, yiri mmɔɔ yó ahin sam bo m yo bu ni, na i yó bo bu tara ahin mmɔɔ, dama m sukɔ m Si dɔ ɔ.

ref: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
text: Na deke kɛrɛ bo am fa m duma fa sereeri i, m yó i ma am, adena mini Baa ni má minɛm wura m Si kpiri.

ref: I will do whatever you ask me to do. The Holy Spirit Is Promised
text: Na nzɛn am ba faari m duma fa sereeri m deke be, m yó i ma am.” Kabo Yesu jɔjɔ fa kɔɔri Awiɛwiɛ Casi n su ni

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Nzɛn am koro m, am dé m nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni.

ref: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
text: M sére m Si Nyɛmɛ, na i má am Buka­fɔ fɔfɔrɛ bo i bá wo am dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
text: Yiri la Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i se nahɔrɛ ni. Durunya n nu­fɔm koro de má i, dama bu ŋu má i, na bekun bu kaaki si má i, ama, ambɛrɛ si i, dama i wo am dɔ, na i bá wo am nu.

ref: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
text: M kɔɔri, m yaki má am na am ka ayikam, m káaki ba am dɔ.

ref: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
text: Wɔ ka kaan na durunya n nu­fɔm ŋu má m kun, ama, ambɛrɛ ŋú m. M la ŋgɔɔ n dɛɛ ti, am gusu nyá ŋgɔɔ.

ref: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n ye am sí ka mini ni m Si n ti deke kun a, na mini ni ambɛrɛ gusu ti deke kun wɔ.

ref: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de m nɔaniɛ maa­wam, na i su i su, i mi ti bo i koro m a, na bo i koro m ni, m Si gusu koro yiri wɔ, na mini mmɔɔ kóro i mi, na m yí m ŋu kere i.”

ref: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
text: Na Yudasi, má Yudasi Isikariyoti ni, ase ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, nzu ti ye a yí u ŋu kere yɛrɛ a, ye a yí má u ŋu kere má durunya n nu­fɔm a?”

ref: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro m, i dé m nɔaniɛ ni, na m Si mmɔɔ kóro i mi, na ya bá, na ya bá wo i dɔ.

ref: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má m, i dé má m nɔaniɛ ni. Nɔaniɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ i ni, i ti má m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ, i ti m Si n bo i sunmaari m dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
text: M toko wo am dɔ, ye m suse am ahin kɛrɛ a,

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
text: ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ súnma Buka­fɔ ni, m duma n nu, yiri la Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni, yiri kére am deke kɛrɛ a, na i káaki teŋge am su fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo ma kere am ni.”

ref: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Ahoreda n ye m sufa ma am n a. M dɛɛ ahoreda n ye m sufa ma am a. Má kabo durunya n nu­fɔm ma am ahoreda ye m suma am n a. Má am ma am ahore saaki, má am soro sɛrɛ.

ref: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
text: Am ati ka ma se ka, ‘M kɔ́ na m káaki ba am dɔ bekun.’ Nzɛn má am koro m, má am ahore jɔ ka m sukɔ m Si n dɔ, dama m Si tara m.

ref: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
text: Sa bo wɔ nya yo má lɛɛ, ye ma se am n a, adena i bá yoori, na am yo m yarada.

ref: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
text: M jɔ́jɔ má am dɔ jɔrɛ beberebe kun, dama Sitana bo i ti durunya n nu­fɔm tii ni suba. Ama, i koro yo má m pui.

ref: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
text: M koro ka durunya n nu­fɔm si ka, m koro m Si ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m yo deke kɛrɛ bo i se m ka m yo i n a.” Na wɔ se bu ka, “Am yasu na ya kɔ.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Mini ti baka n bo i su suu­wa nahɔrɛ su a, ye m Si Nyɛmɛ ti nyini baka n fie niɛ­fɔ.

ref: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
text: Asa­mbaa kɛrɛ bo i wo m su na i su má, m Si kpiɛ i ji wɔ, na asa­mbaa kɛrɛ bo i wo m su na i su, i kpiɛkpiɛ i nyawa n yo i dɔŋgu wɔ, na i su nyunyumi.

ref: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
text: Jɔrɛ n bo m jɔjɔ maari am ni, yiri adu mɔ ayo am casi a.

ref: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
text: Am wo ni mini na m gusu wo ni ambɛrɛ. Kabo baka asa­mbaa be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro su i ŋgumi, má ka i wo baka n su. Sɔ ye am gusu koro su má, má ka am wo m dɔ.

ref: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
text: Mini ti baka n a, ye ambɛrɛ ti asa­mbaam ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo m dɔ, na mini mmɔɔ wo i dɔ, i mi su suu­wa nyunyumi wɔ, dama am koro yo má pui, má ka am awo m dɔ.

ref: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo má m dɔ, bu kpiɛ i ji wɔ, kabo bu kpiɛ baka asa­mbaa na i wu n wɔ, na bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ wura sin nu na bu je ni.

ref: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
text: Nzɛn am wo m dɔ, na m nɔaniɛ n wo am ahore su, deke kɛrɛ bo am koroori i, na am sereeri Nyɛmɛ, am nyá i.

ref: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
text: Nzɛn am su suu­wa nyunyumi, nyini kere ka am ti m susu­fɔm a, na i wura m Si kpiri.

ref: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
text: Kabo m Si koro m ni, sɔ ye mini mmɔɔ koro am a, am wo ni m koro ni.

ref: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
text: Ma de m Si nɔaniɛ maa­wam n dɛɛ ti ye i koro m a. Nzɛn am gusu de m nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, m gusu kóro am sɔ ɔ.

ref: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
text: Ma se am ahin sam na am ahore jɔ kabo m ahore ajɔ n wɔ, na am ahore jɔ n kɔ sin su.

ref: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
text: M nɔaniɛ maa­wa n la ka o, am koro am beŋgu kabo m koro am ni.

ref: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
text: Koro be wo má ahin koro n sin, ka sɔnɔ wu i danfom ti.

ref: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
text: Nzɛn am su m nɔaniɛ maa­wa n su, am ti m danfom wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
text: M fere am m danfom wɔ, m fere má am ka m akɔɔm kun, dama sɔnɔ akɔɔ si má i mibiɛ sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ, ama, deke kɛrɛ bo ma ti i m Si dɔ ni, ma fa akere am.

ref: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Má ambɛrɛ yiiri m a, ama, mini ayi am ase ka am kɔ su suu­wa bo i wó bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ, na deke kɛrɛ bo am bá fa m duma n sereeri m Si, i yó i ma am.

ref: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
text: Nzɛn má am ti durunya n nu­fɔm dɛɛ minɛm, má bu koro am kabo bu koro bu beŋgu n wɔ. Ama, bo ma yi am afite durunya n nu­fɔm nu, na am ti má durunya n nu­fɔm dɛɛ minɛm n ti, ye bu kpɔ am n a.

ref: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
text: Má am ma am wara fi ka ma se am ka, akɔɔ tara má i mibiɛ. Nzɛn bu yo kereeri m wuwuruwa, sɔ ye bu kére am gusu wuwuruwa a. Na nzɛn bu yo deeri m nɔaniɛ, bu dé am gusu dɛɛ ni.

ref: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
text: Bu yó am nyini n kɛrɛ, m dɛɛ ti, dama bu si má m Si bo i sunmaari m ni.

ref: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
text: Nzɛn má ma ba akan má m ŋu jɔrɛ akere má bu, má bu si má ka ba yo satiɛ, ama, kisa na ma kan akere bu ni, bu koro kan fite má nu ka ba yo má satiɛ.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kpɔ m, i kpɔ mini ni m Si n kɛrɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
text: Ma yo alekutura sam bo ba ŋu i. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro yo nyini alekutura sam sɔ. Nzɛn má ma yo má nyini alekutura sam na ba ŋu má i, má be fa má bu ŋgasi ka bu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm. Ama, kisa, ba ŋu nyini n kɛrɛ, na bu toko kpɔ m ni m Si ni.

ref: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
text: Nyini ma kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n ayo yii­wa n a, ka, ‘Bu kpɔ m kpɔ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.’

ref: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
text: Na Buka­fɔ n bo m súnma i fite m Si dɔ na i bá am dɔ ni, yiri ti Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ti nahɔrɛ su n a, yiri dí m ŋusu daani kere am a.

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text: Na am gusu di m daani, dama m ni ambɛrɛ wo bɛrɛ a, fite m juma n bumboori ba ju ni kisa.

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: M suse am ahin sam kɛrɛ bo bu bá yo n wɔ, na bu ba yoori, na má am kaaki am sin bo m.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
text: Bu bá fɔn yi am fite bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, ama, saŋga suba bo bu bá kun am, na bu súnsun ka bu susu Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

ref: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
text: Bu si má m ni m Si n dɛɛ ti, ye bu yó nyini sam kɛrɛ n a.

ref: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
text: Ma se am sam bo bu bá yo lɛɛ, na i saŋga ba juuri, na am téŋge su ka m yo seeri am.” Kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n juma ti barasu ni Na Yesu asa su ase bu ka, “M ni ambɛrɛ yo woori bumboori n dɛɛ ti, ye ma yo ase má am ahin kɛrɛ a.

ref: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
text: Ama, kisa m sukaaki kɔ yiri bo i sunmaari m n dɔ ɔ, ye am be akoro abisa má m ka, ‘Ye a sukɔ ni wɔ?’

ref: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
text: Bo ma se ka m kɔ́ yaki am dɛɛ ti, am ahore asaaki agaya.

ref: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
text: Ama, nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, i ti am buka su wɔ ka m kɔ, dama nzɛn má m kɔ má, má Buka­fɔ ni bá má, ama, nzɛn m kɔɔri, m súnma i am dɔ.

ref: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
text: I baari, i kére durunya n nu­fɔm deke bo i ti satiɛ, na i kére bu deke bo i ti sakpa na i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na i káaki kere bu kabo Nyɛmɛ bá di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ barasu.

ref: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
text: I kére bu deke bo i ti satiɛ, dama bu yo má m yarada.

ref: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
text: Na i kére bu deke bo i ti kpa na i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, dama m sukɔ m Si n dɔ ɔ, na am ŋú má m kun.

ref: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
text: Na i káaki kere bu kabo Nyɛmɛ bá di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ adi Sitana bo i ti durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ tii n jɔrɛ ayo i ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
text: M toko la jɔrɛ dɔŋgu bo má m se am, ama, am koro de má i kɛrɛ kisa.

ref: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
text: Ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i se nahɔrɛ n ba baari, yiri kére am nahɔrɛ atin kɛrɛ a, dama jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i kán kere am ni, má i dɛɛ yiko nu ye i fá kan, ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ kére i ni, sɔ ye i kán, na i kére am sam bo bu bá yo ni.

ref: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
text: Yiri wúra m kpiri a, dama i kán m ŋu jɔrɛ kere am.

ref: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
text: Deke kɛrɛ bo i ti m Si dɛɛ, i ti m dɛɛ wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma se am ka, i kán m ŋu jɔrɛ kere am n a.” Kabo Yesu kaanri ahoresaaki ni ahorejɔ jɔrɛ ni

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Wɔ ka kaan na am ŋú má m, ama, i ba yoori kaan buka su na am ŋú m bekun.”

ref: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
text: Na i susu­fɔm n nbem abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Nzu jɔrɛ ye i jɔjɔɔri i sɔ n a? I ŋa, wɔ ka kaan na ya ŋú má i, ama, i ba yoori kaan buka su, ya ŋú i bekun. Ye wɔ kaaki ase bekun ka, i sukɔ i Si dɔ ɔ.”

ref: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
text: Na ba kaaki abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Ye ahin dɛɛ, i ba yoori kaan n bu la ka sɛ wɔ lɛɛ? Ya si má i jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu.”

ref: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
text: Na Yesu afa nu ka bu koro ka bu bisa i nyini jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu wɔ. Na wɔ bisa bu ka, “Bo m seeri ka wɔ ka kaan na am ŋú má m, ama, i ba yoori kaan buka su, am ŋú m bekun n ye am subisa am ŋuŋu nu, na am si i bu, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, ambɛrɛ bá sun na am kóko, ama, na durunya n nu­fɔm bɛrɛ ahore jɔ́. Ambɛrɛ ahore bá saaki, ama, am ahoresaaki ni bá kaaki ahorejɔ.

ref: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
text: Nzɛn bara anya akutu ka i wu baa, i la ahoresaaki, dama i wahara saŋga ye wɔ ju a, ama, i ba wuuri baa n wieeri, i téŋge má i wahara n bo wɔ sin n su kun, dama baa n bo wɔ wu i durunya n nu n ti, i ahore ajɔ.

ref: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
text: I ti sɔ ɔ, am gusu dɔ, kisa, am la ahoresaaki, ama, m bá ŋu am bekun, na am ahore jɔ́ bo sɔnɔ koro dé má am ahorejɔ n am dɔ.

ref: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Nyini cɛɛn ni, am bísa má deke be busu m dɔ. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, deke kɛrɛ bo am sereeri m Si ni, m duma n nu, i yó i ma am.

ref: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
text: Am nya sere má m Si deke be m duma n nu haari ni nyuma. Kisa am sere i, na am nyá i, na am ahore jɔ́ kɔ sin su.” Kabo Yesu akunma durunya n ni

ref: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Ma se am ahin n kɛrɛ anyundɛrɛ nu wɔ lɛɛ, saŋga suba bo m bó má am anyundɛrɛ kun, ama, m kán m Si ŋu jɔrɛ kɛrɛ kpain kere am a.

ref: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
text: Nyini cɛɛn ni, am fá m duma n fa sere Nyɛmɛ, má mini sere Nyɛmɛ ma am a.

ref: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
text: Yiri m Si ni i tii koro am a, dama am koro m na am ayo yarada ka m fite i dɔ ɔ.

ref: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
text: M fiteeri m Si dɔ ɔ baari durunya n nu a, ye kisa m sufite durunya n nu kaaki kɔ i dɔ a.”

ref: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
text: Na Yesu susu­fɔm n ase i ka, “Kisa a jɔjɔ ya dɔ wein­wein wɔ, a bo má ya anyundɛrɛ kun.

ref: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
text: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, “Am ayo yarada kisa?

ref: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
text: Ama, saŋga suba bo wɔ ju awie, bo am kɛrɛ bá sandi yaki mini ŋgumi, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kɔ́ i dika, ama, m wo má m ŋgumi, dama m Si Nyɛmɛ wo m dɔ.

ref: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
text: Ma se am ahin kɛrɛ na am la ahoreda m dɔ. Am ŋú kekereke durunya n nu, ama, am fa ahoresi, dama ma kunma durunya ni.”

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
text: Yesu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su aniɛ aŋgoro na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ. I ŋa, “M Si, saŋga n aju. Wura mini u Wa n kpiri, adena m gusu wura u kpiri.

ref: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
text: Dama a ma m yiko ka m niɛ minɛm kɛrɛ su, na bo a fa bu ama m ni, m má bu ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

ref: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
text: Wɔrɔ ŋgumi ti Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su a. Na minɛ bo bu si u, na bu si mini Yesu Kristo n bo a sunmaari m ni, bɛrɛ la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.

ref: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
text: Ma wura u kpiri durunya n nu, dama ma yo juma n bo a ma m ka m yo i n awie.

ref: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
text: M Si, saŋga bo durunya nya bo má i bu ni, m yo woori u dɔ ɔ, ye a wura m kpiri a. Kisa, kaaki wura m kpiri u dɔ bekun.

ref: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
text: Minɛm n bo a yiiri bu fiteeri durunya n nu fɛn, ma kere bu kabo a ti barasu. Bu tiiri u dɛɛm wɔ, ye a faari bu maari m a, ye ba de u nɔaniɛ n a.

ref: They know that you gave me everything I have.
text: Kisa bu si ka deke kɛrɛ bo a fa ama m ni, fite u dɔ ɔ.

ref: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
text: Dama nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ bo a jɔjɔ fa maari m ni, m gusu ajɔjɔ ama bu, ye ba de i, ye ba si ka i ti nahɔrɛ ka m fiteeri u dɔ ɔ, ye ba yo yarada ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.

ref: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
text: M susere u ma bɛrɛ wɔ, má bo bu wo durunya n nu ni, ama, minɛm bo a fa bu ama m ni, bɛrɛ ye m sere u ma bu a, dama bu ti u dɛɛ minɛm wɔ.

ref: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
text: M minɛm kɛrɛ ti u dɛɛm wɔ, ye u dɛɛm gusu kɛrɛ ti m dɛɛm, ye ba ma minɛm aŋu m jirima woo­wa ni.

ref: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
text: Kisa, m suba u dɔ lɛɛ, m ká má durunya n nu fɛn kun, ama, bɛrɛ ká fɛn wɔ. M Si, wɔrɔ bo a ti casi ni, yo bu kanda u duma n bo a fa maari m n yiko nu, na bu yo deke kun, kabo mini ni wɔrɔ ti kun ni.

ref: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
text: Saŋga bo m woori bu dɔ ni, m yoori bu kanda u duma n bo afa ama m yiko nu wɔ. M niɛɛri bu su, na bu be aka má ŋgbɛɛn, má ka bo wɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn haari i ni kunnu ni, adena kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n yo yii­wa.

ref: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
text: Kisa m suba u dɔ lɛɛ, ama, m toko wo durunya n nu ye m jɔjɔ ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n na bu nya m dɛɛ ahorejɔ n kɔ sin su.

ref: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
text: Ma fa u nɔaniɛ n afa ama bu, na kabo m ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɔ be ni, sɔ ye bu gusu ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɔm a, nyini dɛɛ ti, durunya n nu­fɔm koro má bu jɔrɛ.

ref: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
text: M susere má u ka a yi bu fite durunya n nu wɔ, ama, m susere u ka a kanda Sitana satiɛ­yo­fɔ ni, na i yaki bu.

ref: Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours.
text: U nɔaniɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Ma u nɔaniɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n yo bu casi.

ref: I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me.
text: Kabo a sunmaari m durunya n nu ni, sɔ ye m gusu asunma bu a.

ref: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
text: Bɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, ye ma fa m ŋu ama u a, adena bɛrɛ gusu fa bu ŋu ma u nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
text: M susere má u ma má bɛrɛ ŋgumi wɔ, ama, m susere u ma minɛm bo bu bá yo m yarada ahinm dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kaan­wa n ti wɔ.

ref: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
text: M Si, m sere u ka a ma bu kɛrɛ yo kun, kabo a wo m dɔ, na m gusu wo u dɔ ni, ma bɛrɛ gusu wo ya dɔ, adena durunya n nu­fɔm yo yarada ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.

ref: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
text: U jirima woo­wa n bo a maari m ni, ma fa ama bu, na bu yó kun kabo m ni wɔrɔ ti kun ni.

ref: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
text: M wo bu dɔ, na u gusu wo m dɔ, nyini ma bu yo deke kun kabo a koro ka bu ti n a, na durunya n nu­fɔm si ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a, na kabo a koro m ni, a koro u minɛm gusu sɔ ɔ.

ref: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
text: M Si, m koro ka a ma minɛm bo a fa bu ama m n bá wo m dɔ, deke bo m wo ni, na bu ŋú m jirima woo­wa n bo a fa maari m ni, dama saŋga bo a nya ayi má durunya n ye a koro m a.

ref: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
text: M Si, wɔrɔ yo sa kɛrɛ bo i kɔ atin su n a, durunya n nu­fɔm si má u, ama, mini si u, na ahinm gusu si ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.

ref: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
text: Ma kere bu kabo a ti barasu, na m toko sukere bu wɔ, adena koro n bo a koro m n wó bu nu, na m ni bɛrɛ yó kun.”

ref: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
text: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n akɔɔ be, i dika­fɔ la sɔnɔ n bo Petoro kpiɛ yiiri i sui ni, na wɔ se Petoro ka, “Ma ŋu má u ka a wo i bi baka fie n nu dɔ?”

ref: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
text: Na wɔ kaaki abisa bu bekun ka, “Am koro ŋma a?” Na ba se i ka, “Ya koro Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n wɔ.”

ref: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Ma se am ka yiri la mini. Nzɛn am koro mini wɔ, am ma ahin minɛm bɛrɛ kɔ.”

ref: Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‘I did not lose anyone you gave me.’ ”
text: I seeri sɔ ni, na i nɔaniɛ bo i yo jɔjɔɔri i n ayo yii­wa, ka, “Minɛm bo a fa bu ama m ni, m ma má bu be ka má ŋgbɛɛn.”

ref: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
text: Nyini saŋga Simon Petoro jina bita takobe, na wɔ cin awɔrɔ i akpiɛ sɔnɔ be sui, i saa fɔmbɔrɔ dɛɛ ni, nyini sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n akɔɔ ɔ. Bu fere i ka Malikusi.

ref: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
text: Na sojam, ni bu kpiɛn, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n tintini­jina­fɔm n atara Yesu acici i nyama.

ref: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
text: Ama, na Petoro yiri aka ajina dasin anɔ n nu, na yiri susu­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n si i ni afite, na wɔ ba afani n bo i jina kanda anɔ n dɔ, na wɔ sere i atin na wɔ fa Petoro awura.

ref: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
text: Ayɛrɛ saŋga lɛɛ, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn ni ngbɛfɛnɛm, ni tintini­jina­fɔm atɔrɔ sin wo tun su, na Petoro gusu ajina bu bi sutun sin n be. Kabo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kpiɛn n diiri Yesu jɔrɛ ni

ref: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
text: Ye nzu ti ye a bisa m a? Bisa minɛm bo bu tiiri m keree­wa n ka m seeri bu sɛ wɔ. Bɛrɛ si kabo m seeri bu n a.”

ref: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
text: Na Anasi ama ba fa i ni nyama cicii­wa n sɔ akɔ Kayafasi bo i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kpiɛn n dɔ. Kabo Petoro keteeri bekun ka i si má Yesu ni

ref: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
text: Na alecin ŋgarama fun na ba fa Yesu afite Kayafasi dika n nu, na ba fa i akɔ gomina n dika, bu fere gomina n ka Pilato. Yuda­fɔm awura má gisan nu, adena má bu yo bu ŋu fin, na bu koro di má Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni.

ref: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
text: Na ba se i ka, “Nzɛn má wɔ do má ŋgasi be, má ya fa má i ba má u dɔ.”

ref: And so what Jesus said about his death would soon come true.
text: Bu seeri sɔ ni, adena Yesu nɔaniɛ n bo i seeri kabo i bá wu barasu n yo yii­wa.

ref: Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
text: Na Pilato akaaki awura gisan n nu, na wɔ fere Yesu abisa i ka, “Wɔrɔ ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n a?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
text: Na Yesu abisa i ka, “U dɛɛ sunsunni nu lɛɛ, nzɛn be seeri u a?”

ref: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
text: Na Pilato abisa i ka, “M ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ? U minɛm ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni faari u baari m dɔ a. A do nzu ŋgasi wɔ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “M fɛmɛya n ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɛmɛya. Nzɛn má m fɛmɛya n ti durunya n nu fɛn fɛmɛya, má m susu­fɔm bá kun luɛ m busu, na má be koro tara má m wura má Yuda­fɔm saa nu. Ama, m fɛmɛya n ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ.”

ref: “So you are a king,” Pilate replied. “You are saying that I am a king,” Jesus told him. “I was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.”
text: Na Pilato abisa i bekun ka, “Ŋka a ti fɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ?” Na Yesu ase i ka, “Bo a ŋa m ti fɛmɛ ni, i ti sɔ ɔ. Bu wuuri m durunya n nu ka m ba di nahɔrɛ n daani wɔ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nahɔrɛ n su, i de m nɔaniɛ n wɔ.”

ref: And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?”
text: Ama, am la am yoo­wa be bo i ma m atin ka m yi dan­saraka kun ma am Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n di saŋga. Am koro ka m yi ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ ni ji ma am a, nzɛn sɛ?”

ref: They shouted, “No, not him! We want Barabbas.” Now Barabbas was a terrorist.
text: Na ba tiɛn ase ka, “Má yiri. Ya koro Barabasi wɔ.” Barabasi ti sɔnɔ bo i nati fin minɛm di wɔ.

ref: Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip.
text: Na Pilato ama ba fa Yesu akɔ afin i ŋgbere.

ref: The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him.
text: Na sojam n awura i tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom ka fɛmɛya dɛɛ su, na ba fa buwe aŋɔ i ka kaba dɛɛ su, na ba fa aforo i tii.

ref: They came up to him and said, “Hey, you king of the Jews!” They also hit him with their fists.
text: Na bu suba i dɔ suyo i anzasi se i ka, “Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ, ya yo u ansɛ.” Na ba bo i nyɔɔnu.

ref: Once again Pilate went out. This time he said, “I will have Jesus brought out to you again. Then you can see for yourselves that I have not found him guilty.”
text: Na Pilato akaaki afite aba Yuda­fɔm dɔ aba ase bu ka, “Am ti fɛn, m sufa i fite ba kere am wɔ, na am si ka ma ŋu má ŋgasi bo wɔ do i.”

ref: Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, “Here is the man!”
text: Yesu tiɛ fiteeri ni tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom ni, ni i tii buwe kaba n ni, na Pilato ase bu ka, “Am niɛ, sɔnɔ n yaa.”

ref: When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, “Nail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!” Pilate told them, “You take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.”
text: Deke bo i tiɛ fiteeri na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni tintini­jina­fɔm ŋuuri i sɔ ni, kpeŋga na ba tiɛn, bu ŋa, “Am kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu. Am kpama i.” Na Pilato ase bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii fa i kɔ kpama i e. Mini, ma ŋu má ŋgasi bo wɔ do i.”

ref: The crowd replied, “He claimed to be the Son of God! Our law says that he must be put to death.”
text: Na ba se i ka, “Ya la nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa be, wɔ yo daka ka i wu, dama i ŋa i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a.”

ref: He went back inside and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus did not answer.
text: Na wɔ kaaki awura gisan n nu abisa Yesu ka, “A fite ni wɔ?” Ama, Yesu abɔ má i nu.

ref: “Why won't you answer my question?” Pilate asked. “Don't you know I have the power to let you go free or to nail you to a cross?”
text: Na Pilato akaaki abisa i ka, “A bɔ má m nu? A si má ka m la yiko bo m koro ma bu fa u ji, na m kaaki la yiko bo m koro ma bu kpama u baka jaanu n su?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If God had not given you the power, you couldn't do anything at all to me. But the one who handed me over to you did something even worse.”
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Nzɛn má Nyɛmɛ ama má u atin, maala má yiko bo a koro yo m pui. Nyini ti, sɔnɔ n bo i faari m wuraari u saa nu ni, i satiɛ yoo­wa tara u dɛɛ.”

ref: Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, “If you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.”
text: I seeri sɔ ni, na Pilato akpi kabo i yo na i yaki Yesu nu ji. Ama, na Yuda­fɔm sutiɛntiɛn suse ka, “Nzɛn a yakiiri i nu jiiri, u ni Fɛmɛ Siza danfoya awie lɛɛ, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo i ŋu ka i ti fɛmɛ, i mi ti Fɛmɛ Siza kpɔ­fɔ ɔ.”

ref: When Pilate heard this, he brought Jesus out. Then he sat down on the judge's bench at the place known as “The Stone Pavement.” In Aramaic this pavement is called “Gabbatha.”
text: Pilato tiiri sɔ ni, na wɔ fa Yesu afite, na wɔ tana jɔrɛ dii­biri n su bimbindi n su. Yuda­fɔm aniɛ nu bu fere i ka Gabata, i bu la ka, deke bo ba fa yabuɛ asɛsɛ su ayo i dɔŋgu ni.

ref: It was about noon on the day before Passover, and Pilate said to the crowd, “Look at your king!”
text: Nyini cɛɛn n tiiri bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n kpokpo cɛɛn wɔ, wiɛsu kerefi buru ni nnyɔ saŋga a. Na Pilato ase Yuda­fɔm ka, “Am fɛmɛ n yaa.”

ref: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be nailed to a cross. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
text: Bu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na Pilato anya afa i ama bu ka bu kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su. Kabo bu kpamaari Yesu baka jaanu n su ni Na ba de Yesu akɔ.

ref: The soldiers first broke the legs of the other two men who were nailed there.
text: Nyini ti, na sojam n akɔ abo abubu sɔnɔ n kun jaam, ni nyiŋga n gusu dɛɛ, bɛrɛ n bo bu kpamaari bu dodoori Yesu ni.

ref: So the chief priests went to Pilate and said, “Why did you write that he is King of the Jews? You should have written, ‘He claimed to be King of the Jews.’ ”
text: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n akete ase Pilato ka, “Má a kɛrɛ ka, ‘Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n lɛɛ,’ ama, kɛrɛ ka, ‘Ahin sɔnɔ ŋa, “Mini ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n a.”’ ”

ref: When Jesus saw his mother and his favorite disciple with her, he said to his mother, “This man is now your son.”
text: Yesu niɛ ŋuuri i ni ni, ni i susu­fɔ n bo i koro i agaya n ka bu jina dodo i ni, na wɔ se i ni n ka, “M ni, niɛ fɛn, u wa la ahin.”

ref: and he carried his cross to a place known as “The Skull.” In Aramaic this place is called “Golgotha.”
text: Na yiri ni i tii asɔ i baka jaanu n akɔ awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Tere­awa Woo­biri ni, Yuda­fɔm aniɛ nu bu fere dɔ ka Gologota.

ref: There Jesus was nailed to the cross, and on each side of him a man was also nailed to a cross.
text: Na ba kpama i baka jaanu n su dɔ, na ba kaaki akpama minɛ nnyɔ nbem adodo i, bu kpamaari kun i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na ba kpama kun i saa biɛ su, na Yesu awo bu afiɛn.

ref: Pilate ordered the charge against Jesus to be written on a board and put above the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, King of the Jews.”
text: Na Pilato akɛrɛ kɛrɛɛ­wa be amata i tii su baka jaanu n su. Kɛrɛɛ­wa n la ka, “Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n bo i ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n lɛɛ.”

ref: The words were written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek. The place where Jesus was taken wasn't far from the city, and many of the people read the charge against him.
text: Bu kɛrɛɛri nyini kɛrɛɛ­wa n Yuda­fɔm, ni Roma­fɔm, ni Giriki­fɔm dɛɛ aniɛm kɛrɛ nu wɔ, na Yuda­fɔm dɔŋgu aŋu i, na ba kan i, dama deke bo bu kpamaari i n waari má ni miɛ kunnu.

ref: But Pilate told them, “What is written will not be changed!”
text: Na Pilato ase bu ka, “Bo ma kɛrɛ i ni, nyini lɛɛ wɔ.”

ref: The soldiers said to each other, “Let's not rip it apart. We will gamble to see who gets it.” This happened so the Scriptures would come true, which say, “They divided up my clothes and gambled for my garments.” The soldiers then did what they had decided.
text: Na ba se ka, “Má am ma ya sɔnsɔn i nu, ama, am ma ya tu mbaa na ya si bo i dí i.” Bu yoori sɔ adena Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n yo yii­wa, bo ba kɛrɛ i ka, “Ba kpaaki m neŋge wuraa­wam nu, na ba tu mbaa m tanlɛɛ n bo i wo m bu n busu ni.” Nyini ti ye sojam yoori sɔ n a.

ref: Jesus' mother stood beside his cross with her sister and Mary the wife of Clopas. Mary Magdalene was standing there too.
text: Na Yesu ni, ni Yesu ni niɛma­bara, ni Kolopasi yi Mariya, fa kɔ tuuri Mariya n bo i fite Magadala n jina dodo deke bo bu kpamaari Yesu ni.

ref: But when they came to Jesus, they saw he was already dead, and they did not break his legs.
text: Ama, saŋga bo bu ba kpuraari Yesu ni, bu ŋuuri ka yiri awu, nyini dɛɛ ti, ba bubu má i jaam.

ref: In the place where Jesus had been nailed to a cross, there was a garden with a tomb that had never been used.
text: Deke bo bu kpamaari Yesu ni, baka fie be wo dɔ. Nyini baka fie n nu yabuɛ buɛ bo ba lokoti i ayo i faŋ­kunma wo dɔ, bo bu nya se má fuin nu ni.

ref: Jesus knew that he had now finished his work. And in order to make the Scriptures come true, he said, “I am thirsty!”
text: Nyini sin na Yesu asi nu ka wɔ yo i juma kɛrɛ awie, na wɔ se ka, “Nzuehɔɛ ti m wɔ.” I seeri sɔ ni, adena Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n yo yii­wa.

ref: A jar of cheap wine was there. Someone then soaked a sponge with the wine and held it up to Jesus' mouth on the stem of a hyssop plant.

text: Nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka i mi ti sɔnɔ bo i woori ya nu, saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu woori bɛrɛ na ya nati tiɛn ya nɔɔ i dɔ ni, fite saŋga n bo Yohane biɛɛri Yesu Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, haari fa kɔ juuri cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ maari Yesu fun kɔɔri aŋgoro yakiiri ya ni.”

ref: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
text: Dɔ na ba sere Nyɛmɛ, bu ŋa, “Ya Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ si kabo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sunsunni ti barasu a. Ahin minɛ nnyɔm ni nu, kere ya bo a koro ka ya yi i,

ref: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd

ref: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,

text: nyini ti, na wɔ warakpin, na wɔ jina i jaa su, na wɔ bo i bu sunati bara yɛ. Dɔ na wɔ su bu su awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na i sunati, na i suwarakpin, na i sukansi Nyɛmɛ.

ref: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
text: Saŋga bo minɛm ni siiri nu ka, yiri bo i yo tana Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n Anɔ Nyunyumi n nu sere n lɛɛ ni, na ba miɛ atana, na sa n bo i yoori sɔnɔ n ayo bu alekutura. Kabo Petoro kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni

ref: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Saŋga bo wɔbuka n bita Petoro ni Yohane barasu ni, na minɛ kɛrɛ ayo alekutura, na ba ŋmati aba bu dɔ awɔrɛ be bo bu fere i ka Solomon dɛɛ Lɛlɛ n nu.

ref: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
text: Saŋga bo Petoro ŋuuri minɛm sɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, “Yisareli­fɔm, nzu ti ye am fa ahin sa n yo alekutura ye am niɛ ya sɔ a? Am sunsun ka ya dɛɛ yiko nu ye ahin sɔnɔ n anati a, wara am sunsun ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n dɛɛ ti, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, i ti má sɔ.

ref: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
text: Nyɛmɛ bo Abraham ni Izaki ni Jakɔbu ni ya nam suuri i ni, yiri ye wɔ wura i amusui­su­fɔ Yesu n kpiri bambaka a. Ama, am faari i wuraari yiko­fɔm saa nu wɔ, na am akete i Gomina Pilato nyunu, ka am koro má i, haari ni bo má gomina n ŋa i yaki i nu ji ni.

ref: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
