ref: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
text: Ahin sɔnɔ bo am ŋu i na am si i ni, Yesu dɛɛ duma n yiko nu ma i jaam kɛrɛ ayo faŋga n a. Deke n bo am aŋu i ni, i fite ka sɔnɔ n ayo Yesu duma n yarada n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma wɔ nya laifiɛ ye am kɛrɛ aŋu i n a.”

ref: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
text: Na Petoro anya ase bu ka, “M ninbaam, m si ka ambɛrɛ ni am tere­tere­fɔm kɛrɛ be mmɔɔ asi má sam bo am yo yoori bu ni.

ref: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am kaaki am wo, na am su Nyɛmɛ, na i fá am satiɛ ce am,

ref: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
text: na i má am nyá ŋumi­yi, na i nyá sunma Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo wɔ yi i ka i bá am dɔ ni, yiri la Yesu.

ref: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
text: Wɔ yo daka ka i wó Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ má deke kɛrɛ yo yii­wa, kabo dawa saŋga na i maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ casim n nɔɔ na bu seeri ni.

ref: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
text: Moyisi yo seeri ka, ‘Am Micɛra Nyɛmɛ súnma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ kabo i sunmaari mini Moyisi n wɔ. Nyini sɔnɔ ni yó am dɛɛ tiɛŋgu sɔnɔ bo i fite am nu wɔ. I ti ye ni ka am de i nɔaniɛ, na am yo deke kɛrɛ bo i se ka am yo i.

ref: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n nɔaniɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ yí i mi fite i dɛɛ minɛm nu wɔ, na i ká ŋgbɛɛn.’

ref: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu jɔjɔɔri fite Samuwɛli saŋga haari ba ju ni nyuma, bu kɛrɛ jɔjɔɔri fa kɔ ahin sam bo bu suyo nyuma n su wɔ.

ref: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
text: Ambɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm a, am dɛɛ la sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ yo diiri i di ni am nam ni. Na wɔ se ya na Abraham ka, ‘U busu nu­fɔm dɛɛ ti ye m yó durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ kisi a.’

ref: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i du mɔ yiiri i amusui­su­fɔ n ye i sunmaari i am dɔ n a, ka i bá yo am kisi, nyini la ka, i má am kaaki am wo, na i yí am fite am satiɛ kɛrɛ nu.”

ref: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
text: Petoro ni Yohane toko jɔjɔ jama n dɔ ni, na minɛm nbem aba bu dɔ, bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nbem, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu tintini­jina­fɔm kpiɛn, ni Sadusi­fɔm nbem.

ref: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
text: Bu baari ni yaa su wɔ, kabo Yesu jaramasa nnyɔm ni sukere minɛm ka Yesu wuuri ni, i teŋgeeri i yiwee nu wɔ, nyini kere ka minɛm wu, bu teŋge bu yiwee nu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
text: Nyini ti, na ba tara Petoro ni Yohane. Bu taraari bu naasɔ saŋga a, na ba nyi bu haari ale akɔ acin.

ref: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
text: Ama, na minɛ dɔŋgu bo bu tiiri Yesu jaramasa ni keree­wa n ayo yarada. Nyini ti, bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu yoori yarada ni, bu nbiɛsɔm kɔ yasuuri minɛ akpii nnu wɔ.

ref: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
text: Alecin ŋgarama na Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm, ni bu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
text: Na ba fa Petoro ni Yohane aba ajina bu afiɛn, na ba bisa bu ka, “Am yoori sɛ yoori ahin sa n a? Am la yiko bɔnɔ, wara ŋma dɛɛ duma nu ye am fa tuntuunri sɔnɔ n ye wɔ nya laifiɛ n a?”

ref: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
text: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura Petoro ŋu, na wɔ se bu ka, “Ya tere­tere­fɔm ni ya nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm,

ref: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
text: sakpa n bo ya yo i ama wɔbuka ni, ni kabo i yoori barasu na wɔ nya laifiɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye am sudi ya jɔrɛ bisa ya sɔ a, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
text: I ti ye ni ka ambɛrɛ ni Yisareli­fɔm kɛrɛ si ka o, ahin sɔnɔ n bo i jina bɛrɛ na wɔ nya laifiɛ ni, i fite Yesu Kristo Nazarɛti­fɔ n duma n nu wɔ. Yiri ye am kuunri i, ye Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i fiteeri i yiwee n nu n a.

ref: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
text: Ba kɛrɛ i ŋusu jɔrɛ Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, ‘Yabuɛ n bo ambɛrɛ bondo ta­fɔm faari i tuuri ni, yiri ye wɔ kaaki bondo n jaa yabuɛm kɛrɛ tii a.’

ref: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
text: Yiri ŋgumi ye Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i ba de sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tii a, duma be wo má durunya n kɛrɛ nu bo i koro de ya tii ji, má ka Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma ni ŋgumi.”

ref: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
text: Na Yuda­fɔ yiko­fɔm ni tere­tere­fɔm n aŋu kabo Petoro ni Yohane la ahoresi barasu ni, na ba si ka ba kɔ má kara be mmɔɔ, na wɔ yo bu alekutura. Dɔ na ba si nu ka bu yo suuri Yesu su wɔ.

ref: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
text: Bu ŋuuri ka sɔnɔ n bo wɔ nya laifiɛ n jina Petoro ni Yohane bi ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ba koro ase má jɔrɛ be.

ref: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
text: Na ba se Petoro ni Yohane ka bu fite sɔɔ n nu. Bu fite wieeri ni, na bɛrɛ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n abo Petoro ni Yohane n ŋusu anzama.

ref: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
text: Ama, nzɛn ya koro má ka ahin jɔrɛ n bara yɛ deke n kɛrɛ, am ma ya ŋɔŋɔnɔ bu ka, má bu se ka bu bo Yesu duma mmɔɔ na be ti.”

ref: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
text: Nyini ti, na ba kaaki afere Petoro ni Yohane awura, na ba ma bu nɔɔ ka, má bu se ka bu bo Yesu duma, wara bu kan i ŋusu jɔrɛ kere minɛm.

ref: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
text: Sɔnɔ n bo bu yoori nyini alekutura sa n maari i na i nyaari laifiɛ ni, i tara afɔ abura­na. Kabo yarada­yo­fɔm n sereeri Nyɛmɛ ka i ma bu ahoresi ni

ref: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
text: Saŋga bo Petoro ni Yohane nyaari bu tii ni, bu kaba kpieeri bu nyi kɔɔri bu beŋgu Yesu susu­fɔm n dɔ ɔ, na ba kan jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛnm, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n seeri ni.

ref: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: Yarada­yo­fɔm ni tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ sɔ ni, na bu kɛrɛ ayo anɔɔkun, na ba sere Nyɛmɛ, bu ŋa, “Ya Micɛra Yiko­fɔ, wɔrɔ yiiri aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu a, ni tieku n ni dekem kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni.

ref: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
text: Wɔrɔ yo ma u Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wuraari ya na Davidi bo i ti u dɛɛ amusui­su­fɔ n ŋu a, na wɔ se ka, ‘Nzu ti ye minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm afa yaa agaya sɔ a. ye minɛm bita sunsunni bo i la má kpɛrɛ a?

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
text: Ye durunya n nu nfɛmɛm ayasu ajina, na bu yiko­fɔm atiɛn bu nɔɔ, ka bu tu luɛ ni Micɛra Nyɛmɛ ni, ni Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i n a.’

ref: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
text: Tɔ, kabo Fɛmɛ Davidi yo kaanri ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka Fɛmɛ Herode, ni Gomina Pɔntiyo Pilato fa saŋga ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm yo tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ahin miɛ n nu wɔ, na ba kete Yesu, u amusui­su­fɔ casi n bo a yiiri i ka yiri ti Tii­de­fɔ ni.

ref: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
text: Bu tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ɔ, na ba yo dekem kɛrɛ bo u dɛɛ yiko n ni u koroya­nu na a du mɔ acici ka bu yo ni.

ref: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
text: Ya Mibiɛ, ti kabo kisa bu ŋɔŋɔnɔ ya barasu, nyini ti, ma yɛrɛ u akɔɔm n ahoresi, na ya kan u dɛɛ labari kpa n na má ya soro sɛrɛ.

ref: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
text: Fa u yiko n tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ, na a ma ya yiko na ya tuntun minɛ, na ya yo alekutura sam barasu barasu, u amusui­su­fɔ casi Yesu n dɛɛ duma nu.”

ref: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
text: Saŋga bo bu sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri sɔ ni, na dika n bo bu wo nu n akpisi. Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura bu kɛrɛ ŋu, na ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni ahoresi. Kabo Yesu susu­fɔm ni yoori anɔɔsɛ barasu ni

ref: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
text: Yarada­yo­fɔm n yo yoori anɔɔsɛ wɔ, na ba saŋga ayo deke n kɛrɛ kun. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i se ka i neŋge be tiiri i tiɛŋgu dɛɛ. Ama, bu kɛrɛ kpaaki deke n kɛrɛ bo bu la i nu ma bu beŋgu wɔ.

ref: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
text: Na Yesu jaramasam n akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni yiko bambaka, na ba kan akere minɛm kabo Yesu teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni. Na Nyɛmɛ ama bu kɛrɛ adi ahɔrɔma beberebe.

ref: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
text: Be awo má bu nu bo i miɛɛri deke be busu. Bo bu laari asiɛnm, ni awurum tɔɔri bu wɔ, na ba fa ŋwaa kɛrɛ bo bu nyaari i ni,

ref: Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means “one who encourages others.”
text: Nyini sɔnɔ n tɔɔri i asiɛn n bo i la i, na wɔ fa ŋwaa n aba ama Yesu jaramasam ni.

ref: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
text: Ama, na yiri ni i yi n ayo bu nɔɔkun, na wɔ yi ŋwaa n be afiɛ, na wɔ fa bo i kaari n ama Yesu jaramasam ni.

ref: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
text: Na Petoro abisa i ka, “Ananiyasi, nzu ti ye a ma Sitana awura u sunsunni nu, ye a fa ŋwaa n bo a tɔɔri u asiɛn n na a nyaari i n be afiɛ a, ye a ŋa a dadaka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a?

ref: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
text: Saŋga n bo a nya tɔ má i ni, i ti u dɛɛ wɔ, kisa gusu bo a tɔ i ni, ŋwaa n ti u dɛɛ wɔ. Ye nzu ti ye a yo ahin sa n a? Má adamande ŋgumi ye a ŋa a dadaka a, ama, a ŋa a dadaka Nyɛmɛ wɔ.”

ref: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
text: Ananiyasi tiiri nyini nɔaniɛ n sɔ ni, i kaba kpaasiiri wɔ, na wɔ wu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tiiri nyini sa n bo wɔ yo ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya.

ref: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
text: Na ngbɛfɛnɛm ayasu afa tani apɔɔndi i, na ba fa i afite akɔ ase i.

ref: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
text: Wɔ cɛ má, na i yi gusu aba, wɔ si má sa bo wɔ yo ni.

ref: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
text: Na Petoro abisa i ka, “U ni u hun aja n bo am tɔɔri i n ŋwaa n kɛrɛ lɛɛ?” Na wɔ bɔ Petoro n nu ka, “Ɛɛn, i kɛrɛ lɛɛ.”

ref: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
text: Dɔ na Petoro abisa i ka, “Nzu ti ye u ni u hun ayo am nɔɔkun na am yo Micɛra n Awiɛwiɛ n na am niɛ a? Niɛ fɛn, minɛm bo bu faari u hun kɔ seeri i ni, bu jaa busu wo anɔ n nu, bu fá wɔrɔ mmɔɔ fite lɛɛ.”

ref: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text: Jina n nu bɛrɛ, na wɔ kaba akpaasi Petoro jaa bu, na wɔ wu. Bo bu seeri i hun n ba ŋuuri ka yiri gusu awu, na ba fa i akɔ ase i adodo i hun ni.

ref: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
text: Na Kristo jama n kɛrɛ, ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri nyini sa n bo wɔ yo ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu kɛrɛ agaya. Kabo Yesu jaramasam n yoori alekutura sam barasu ni

ref: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Na Yesu jaramasam n ayo alekutura sam barasu barasu dɔŋgu minɛm n nu bo i kere ka Nyɛmɛ wo bu dɔ. Na yarada­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ Solomon Lɛlɛ n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni.

ref: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
text: Minɛm bo ba yo má Yesu yarada ni soroori sɛrɛ wɔ, ba koro asaŋga má bu nu, ama, na minɛm n awura bu kpiri.

ref: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
text: Na minɛ beberebe ayo Mibiɛ n yarada, subuka bu su saŋga kɛrɛ, bu sunnu, nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram.

ref: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
text: Na alekutura sam bo Yesu jaramasam yoori bu n dɛɛ ti, na minɛ asɔ bu tukpaki­fɔm, ni bu dasum aba ase atiŋgberem nɔɔ su, adena Petoro ba sunati sin, na haari i awiɛwiɛ mmɔɔ tɔ gɔ bu nbem ŋu, na bu nyá laifiɛ.

ref: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
text: Na minɛ beberebe afite miɛ miɛm bo bu bara yɛ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n su ni, na ba fa tukpaki­fɔm ni minɛm bo ajinim yaki má bu n aba, na bu kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ. Kabo bu kereeri Yesu jaramasam n wuwuruwa barasu ni

ref: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn ni, ni i beŋgum bo bu su Sadusi­fɔm atin su ni, aba asi Yesu jaramasam ni kɔrɛ agaya.

ref: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
text: Na ba tara Yesu jaramasam ni, akɔ anyi bu dan­saraka.

ref: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
text: Ama, nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ aba ateke dan­saraka anɔ ni, na wɔ yi Yesu jaramasam n afite, na wɔ se bu ka,

ref: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
text: Na jaramasam n ade i nɔaniɛ ni, na alecin ŋgarama fuun, na ba kɔ ajina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na ba bo i bu sukere minɛ. Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ni i dɛɛ jama nbaam n ba juuri ni, na ba fere Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm ni, ni Yisareli nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ na bu fa sɛsɛ­su, dɔ na ba ma nɔɔ ka bu kɔ yi Yesu jaramasam ni dan­saraka n nu bra.

ref: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
text: Ama, minɛm n bo bu sunmaari bu n kɔ juuri ni, ba ŋu má jaramasam ni dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu, na ba kpie bu nyi aba ase bu.

ref: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
text: Bu ŋa, “Saŋga bo ya kɔ juuri ni, dan­saraka anɔ n kɛrɛ nyi wɔ kenken, tintini­jina­fɔm gusu jina anɔ n nu bɛrɛ, ama, saŋga bo ya ba tekeeri anɔ n ni, ya ŋu má sɔnɔ kun n kpein mmɔɔ sɔɔ n nu.”

ref: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
text: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n kɛrɛ nkpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu tintini­jina­fɔm kpiɛn n tiiri sɔ ni, na ba birisi, ba si má sa bo wɔ yo.

ref: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
text: Dɔ na sɔnɔ be aba ase bu ka, “Am ti fɛn. Minɛm bo am taraari bu kɔ nyiiri bu ni, bu wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu wo minɛm kere su.”

ref: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
text: Nyini ti, na Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu tintini­jina­fɔm kpiɛn n afa i minɛm akɔ, na ba kɔ atara Yesu jaramasam n aba. Bu kɔ faari bu ni, ba miɛ má bu, dama bu soroori ka minɛm ni tu bu yabuɛ wɔ.

ref: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
text: I ŋa, “Ya maari am nɔɔ nahɔrɛ su ka, má am se ka am kere keree­wa be ahin sɔnɔ n duma nu. Ama, am niɛ kabo am ayo a. Am afa am keree­wa n abara ayɛ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n kɛrɛ nu, ye am koro ka am se ka yɛrɛ kuunri i a.”

ref: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
text: Na Petoro ni jaramasa nyiŋgam n abɔ bu nu ka, “Wɔ yo daka ka ya de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ, má adamande dɛɛ.

ref: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
text: Nyɛmɛ bo i ti ya nam dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ ni, yiri teŋgeeri Yesu fiteeri i yiwee n nu, yiri bo am kuunri i na am kpamaari i baka jaanu n su ni.

ref: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
text: Yiri Yesu, ye Nyɛmɛ afa i atana i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, ka i yo ya Mɔ­fɔ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ, na i ma yɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm atin, na ya kaaki ya wo, na Nyɛmɛ fa ya satiɛ kɛrɛ ce ya.

ref: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
text: Yɛrɛ ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni di ahin sam n daani kan kere minɛ a. Yiri Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ye Nyɛmɛ afa ama minɛm bo bu de i nɔaniɛ n a.”

ref: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
text: Saŋga bo minɛm bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu agaya, má bu koroori ka bu kun Yesu jaramasam wɔ.

ref: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
text: Ama, na bu nu kun, i ti Farasi­fɔ ɔ, bu fere i ka Gamɛli, i kaaki ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere­fɔ ɔ, bo minɛm kɛrɛ ma i jirima agaya. Na wɔ yasu ajina, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu fa jaramasam n fite dasin kaan ka.

ref: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
text: Dɔ na wɔ se jama n kɛrɛ ka, “M lamɔkɔ Yisareli­fɔm, am niɛ am tii su dɔŋgu fa kɔ sa n bo am koro ka am fa yo ahin minɛm ni.

ref: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
text: Nyini sin, saŋga bo bu ŋa bu kɛrɛ asiɛn n su jama duma na bu si bu nɔ ni, na sɔnɔ be gusu aba, yiri la Yudasi bo i fite Galili asiɛn n su ni. Na jama beberebe asu i su, ama, na minɛ akun yiri gusu, na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ aŋmati asandi.

ref: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
text: Nyini ti, kabo i ti ni, m suse am wɔ, má am se ka am yo ahin minɛm ni pui. Am yaki bu bɛnlɛ. Nzɛn atin n bo bu bita i n ti adamande dɛɛ, i ká ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
text: Ama, nzɛn i ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, am kóro yo má ahin minɛ ni pui. Am bá ŋu ka am si má na am sukun luɛ ni Nyɛmɛ.”

ref: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
text: Na ba de Gamɛli fɔ­tuu­wa ni. Na ba fere Yesu jaramasam n awura, na ba fin bu ŋgbere, na ba ma bu nɔɔ ka, má bu se ka bu bo Yesu duma mmɔɔ kere minɛ kun. Na ba yaki bu, na ba kɔ.

ref: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
text: Yesu jaramasam ni fiteeri minɛm bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n dɔ wieeri ni, na bu ahore ajɔ agaya, ka Nyɛmɛ abu bu awura be nu, dama ba di nyinsɛ Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu.

ref: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Na cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na bu suwura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, ni minɛm dika, na bu yaki má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere ni, na bu sukan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n kere bu ka Yesu ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

ref: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
text: Na fa kɔ saŋga su, na Yesu susu­fɔm n asunnu abuka su, na Yuda­fɔm bo bu jɔjɔ Giriki aniɛ n ni aŋuŋuti Yuda­fɔm bo bu jɔjɔ Hiburu aniɛ ni. Bu ŋa cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn na bu ce nkuna­fɔm diire ni, bu bu má bu dɛɛm wura má nu.

ref: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
text: Nyini ti, na Yesu jaramasam buru ni nnyɔm n afere susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ pepere atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na ba se bu ka, “I ti má ye ni ka yɛrɛ ni ya tii yaki Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n kan, na ya suniɛ diire jɔrɛ su.

ref: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
text: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, am niɛ am ŋuŋu nu, na am yi minɛ nso bo bu la anyunuyi, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo bu dɔ, na bu la alecira, na bu kaaki la sunsunni kpa, na ya má bɛrɛ niɛ nyini sereyam n kɛrɛ su.

ref: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
text: Na yɛrɛ ni ya tii nyá ya ŋu, na ya nyunu bóro nyɛmɛsere, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan su.”

ref: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
text: Na jama n afa bu aba Yesu jaramasam n dɔ, na ba fa bu saa anana bu ŋu, na ba sere Nyɛmɛ ama bu.

ref: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
text: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni susandi subuka su. Na minɛ beberebe ayo yarada abuka Yesu susu­fɔm su Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu. Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm gusu dɔŋgu ayo yarada. Kabo bu taraari Satɛfano barasu ni

ref: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
text: Satɛfano ti sɔnɔ bo i diiri Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma wɔ beberebe, na Nyɛmɛ ama i yiko nahɔrɛ su, na wɔ yo alekutura sa barasu barasu minɛm n nu.

ref: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
text: Ama, na Yuda­fɔ nbem ayasu ajina ase akuruwa ni yiri. Nyini minɛm wo asɛsɛ n bo bu fere i ka Ma De M Tii tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu wɔ. Bu nbem fite miɛ kpirim n bo bu fere bu ka Sirene, ni Alɛkizanderiya n wɔ. Na bu nbem gusu fite Silisiya ni Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu wɔ.

ref: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
text: Ama, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ama Satɛfano alecira, na wɔ koro akan ayi bu.

ref: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba ma minɛ nbem ŋwaa ka bu kpiɛ kɔrɛ sɔ Satɛfano ka, ba ti ka wɔ kpiɛ Moyisi ni Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ.

ref: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
text: Bu seeri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, na ba yɛ minɛm yaa su, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, na ba tara i akɔ Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm dɔ.

ref: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
text: Dɔ na ba fa minɛ nbem aba, na ba kpiɛ kɔrɛ asɔ i. Bu ŋa, “Saŋga kɛrɛ ahin sɔnɔ n jɔjɔ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn wɔ fa kɔ ya Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru casi n su, na i kaaki jɔjɔ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa su.

ref: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
text: Ya ti ka i ŋa, Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ ni bó fufu ya Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n kɛrɛ gɔ, na i kaaki ya nana sa­yoo­wam kɛrɛ bo Moyisi fa kereeri ya ni.”

ref: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
text: Na bɛrɛ tere­tere­fɔm n kɛrɛ afa bu nyu­mbaa akpama Satɛfano ŋu, na ba ŋu ka i nyunu­awa akaaki wo ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ dɛɛ su.

ref: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
text: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn n abisa Satɛfano ka, “Ahin jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo ba kan i n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ?”

ref: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
text: Na Satɛfano abɔ i nu ka, “M sim ni m ninbaam, am so am sui na am ti fɛn. Kana ya na Abraham kɔ wo Harani miɛ kpiri n nu ni, Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n yo yiiri i ŋu kereeri Abraham Mesopotamiya dɛɛ asiɛn n su wɔ.

ref: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
text: Na wɔ se i ka, ‘Fite yaki u asiɛn n bo a tana su ni, ni u minɛm, na m kére u asiɛn bo m koro ka a kɔ tana su ni.’

ref: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
text: Nyini ti, na Abraham ayasu Kaladiya asiɛn n su, na wɔ kɔ awo Harani miɛ kpiri n nu. I woori dɔ ye i si ba wuuri a. Nyini sin, na Nyɛmɛ ama wɔ yasu dɔ aba atana ahin asiɛn n bo ya wo su kisa ni.

ref: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
text: Nyini saŋga, Nyɛmɛ nya fa má asiɛn n haari kampiti sɔ mmɔɔ ma má Abraham na i se ka i ti i dɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i ka i fá asiɛn n ma i ni i busu nu­fɔm wɔ. Saŋga bo i seeri i sɔ ni, na Abraham nya wu má baa.

ref: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
text: Na Nyɛmɛ ase i ka, ‘U busu nu­fɔm bá kɔ yo wɔfɔm wɔ asiɛn be su, na bu fá bu yo nkɔkɔm, na bu kére bu wahara agaya kɔ ju afɔ yaa nna.’

ref: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
text: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ase ka, ‘M bá cin minɛm n bo bu fá u minɛm yo bu nkɔkɔm n sui, na m nyá yi u minɛm fite nyini asiɛn n su, na bu káaki ba fɛn, na bu bá su m.’

ref: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
text: Na Jakɔbu nbaam n asi bu niɛma Yosefu kɔrɛ, na ba tɔ i afa ama Ijipiti asiɛn n su­fɔm. Ama, na Nyɛmɛ awo i dɔ,

ref: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
text: na wɔ yi i afite kekereke kɛrɛ bo i tuuri i nu, na wɔ ma i alecira, na wɔ di Ijipiti Fɛmɛ Faaro ahɔrɔma. Na fɛmɛ n afa i dika n kɛrɛ, ni Ijipiti asiɛn n ama i ka i niɛ i dika.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
text: Na ahɔɛ aba atɔ Ijipiti, ni Kanana asiɛnm n su, abi aboro minɛ ŋu, bo ya nam anya má diire bo bu di.

ref: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
text: Na bu si Jakɔbu ati ka diire wo Ijipiti asiɛn n su, na wɔ sunma i nbaam. Ciciri su dɛɛ sunma ni, na ba kɔ ato diire n aba.

ref: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
text: Bu ba kpieeri bu nyi kɔɔri ajaari nnyɔ su ni, na Yosefu ayi i ŋu akere i sinbaam ni, na Fɛmɛ Faaro gusu asi Yosefu dika­fɔm.

ref: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
text: Na Jakɔbu, ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ akɔ Ijipiti asiɛn n su, dɔ ye i ni i nbaam n kɛrɛ kɔ wuuri a.

ref: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
text: Na ba fa bu aba Sɛkɛm miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba se bu faŋ­kunma n bo Abraham toori i jɛtɛ ŋwaa beberebe Hamɔ nbaam dɔ ni.

ref: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
text: Saŋga n ba dodoori bo deke n bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i yo ma Abraham n yo yii­wa ni, nyini saŋga ni, na ya minɛm ni atɛrɛ ade Ijipiti asiɛn n kɛrɛ su.

ref: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
text: Bo woori dɔ sɔ ɔ, haari na sɔnɔ be aba adi Ijipiti asiɛn n su fɛmɛ, yiri asi má Yosefu.

ref: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
text: Na yiri ni afa sunsunni tiɛ adadaka ya nam, na wɔ bita bu ni tiɛ bita, na wɔ se ya nam ka bu fa bu nbaam fa tu, adena bu wu.

ref: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
text: Nyini saŋga n ye bu wuuri Moyisi a, i yo ti baa bo i yo nyumi agaya Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ. Na ba bita i i si dika haari sara nsan,

ref: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
text: na ba nya ayi i afite, na Fɛmɛ Faaro wa baa­bara afa i ayo i kunnu wa.

ref: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
text: Na ba kere i Ijipiti­fɔm ŋgɔnlɛ ni alecira barasu barasu kɛrɛ, na wɔ yasu, na i duma ata fa kɔ i sa­yoo­wa ni i jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ nu.

ref: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
text: Na wɔ ŋu ka Ijipiti­fɔm be sukere Yisareli­fɔ kun be wuwuruwa. I ŋuuri i sɔ ni, wɔ sun má i nu, na wɔ kɔ abo akun Ijipiti­fɔ ni.

ref: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
text: Moyisi sunsuunri ka, i dɛɛ minɛm ni tí i bu wɔ, ka, Nyɛmɛ ye wɔ yi i ka i ba de bu tii ji a, ama, ba ti má i bu sɔ.

ref: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
text: I alecin, na wɔ ŋu ka Yisareli­fɔm minɛ nnyɔ sudi aluwa n bu ŋuŋu nu, na wɔ kɔ akpata bu, na wɔ se bu ka, ‘Njam, am ti fɛn, m ŋa am kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ e, ye nzu ti ye am sudi aluwa?’

ref: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
text: Ama, na yiri n bo i sukere i beŋgu n wuwuruwa n asun Moyisi akɔ i sin, na wɔ bisa i ka, ‘Ŋma fa u yoori ya niɛsu­fɔ ni ya jɔrɛ­di­fɔ a?

ref: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
text: A koro ka a kun mini gusu, kabo anuma a kuunri Ijipiti­fɔm wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a?’

ref: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
text: Moyisi tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na wɔ ŋmati afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su akɔ awo Madiya asiɛn n su akaaki wɔfɔ dɔ. Dɔ na wɔ ba aja bara, na wɔ wu nbaa­biɛsɔm nnyɔ.

ref: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
text: Moyisi ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na wɔ yo alekutura, na wɔ cin adodo ka i niɛ i nyunyumi. Dɔ na Micɛra n ajɔjɔ i dɔ, ase i ka,

ref: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
text: ‘Mini ti u nam Nyɛmɛ a, u na Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu.’ Na sɛrɛ ati Moyisi, na i ŋuniɛ subobo i, wɔ bo má su ka i niɛ i kun.

ref: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
text: Na Micɛra n ase Moyisi ka, ‘Deke bo a jina n ti m tiɛŋgu dɛɛ asiɛn casi wɔ, cin yi u gbagbaa ni.

ref: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
text: Ma ŋu kabo m minɛm wo kekereke nu Ijipiti asiɛn n su barasu. Ma ŋu kabo bu fiɛfiɛ barasu, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma jura aba ka m ba de bu tii ji. Bra na m sunma u Ijipiti asiɛn n su.’

ref: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
text: Tɔ, yiri Moyisi ye Yisareli­fɔm keteeri i n a, na ba bisa i ka, ‘Ŋma fa u yoori ya niɛsu­fɔ ni ya jɔrɛ­di­fɔ a?’ Moyisi, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i mɛrɛkɛ i dɔ baka n bo i je sin fofoe na i yara má n su, na mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka i kɔ de minɛm tii ji na i yo bu kpiɛn n a.

ref: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
text: Yiri Moyisi yo seeri Yisareli­fɔm ka, ‘Nyɛmɛ bá yi am busu nu­fɔ be na i yó i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ, kabo m ti ni.’

ref: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
text: Yiri Moyisi, i ni ya na Yisareli­fɔm yo woori boro kpantain n nu n a, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n jɔjɔɔri i dɔ Sinayi Buka n su n a, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n maari i ka i fa ma ya nam n a.

ref: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
text: Ama, ya nam akoro má ka bu de i, na ba kete i na ba koro ka má bu kpie bu nyi kɔ Ijipiti asiɛn n su.

ref: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
text: Na ba yiyi bu sikam, na ba fa ata amɔɛ ka nambaa dɛɛ su, na ba yo tiɛ ama i, na ba su i, na ba di cɛɛn­kpiri fa kɔ nyini deke n bo bɛrɛ ni bu tii ayo i n su.

ref: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
text: Nyini ti, na Nyɛmɛ akaaki i sin abo bu, na wɔ ma bu susu asara­mbaam bo bu wo aŋgoro ni, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n nu lɛɛ. Bu ŋa, ‘Ambɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm, am yo yoori yoo­wam boro kpantain n nu afɔ abura­na ni, am yo maari mini wɔ?

ref: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
text: Ai, am yo sɔɔri amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Mɔlɛki na am afa tani abo i nambue n sɔ i nati wɔ, ni amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Rɛfani na i ti ka asara­mbaa be ni. Bɛrɛ ye am ta bu ka amɔɛm ye am su bu a. Nyini ti, ye m má bu taraari am kɔ miɛ be bo i sin Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n ŋu n a.’

ref: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
text: Ya nam ni, bɛrɛ yo laari nambue n bo i kere ka Nyɛmɛ wo bu dɔ n boro kpantain n nu a. Bu yoori i kabo Nyɛmɛ kereeri Moyisi ka i yo i n wɔ.

ref: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
text: Ya nam yo faari nyini nambue n maari bu sin­fɔm wɔ, na bu ni bu mɔ­fɔ Yosuwa afa i akɔ asiɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ fɔn yiiri minɛm su na i fa maari bu ni. Na nyini nambue n awo bu dɔ haari Fɛmɛ Davidi saŋga.

ref: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
text: Na Fɛmɛ Davidi adi ahɔrɔma Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ka i ma i ta awuru bo Jakɔbu ni i dika­fɔm fa su Nyɛmɛ. Fɛmɛ Davidi akoro ata má i,

ref: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
text: ama, i wa Fɛmɛ Solomon yo taari awuru n maari Nyɛmɛ a.

ref: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
text: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n yiri tana má awuru bo adamande ata i nu, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n ajɔjɔ i ni. I ŋa,

ref: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
text: ‘Micɛra n ŋa o, Aŋgoro ni ti m fɛmɛya tanaa­biri a, na asiɛŋgu nya ti deke bo m jaa gbagbaa tiɛn. Ye am ta nzu awuru barasu ma m a? Am nya nyini fofoe n ni ye m wo nu a?

ref: I have made everything.”
text: Má mini yoori ahin dekem ni kɛrɛ a?’”

ref: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ bɔnɔ ye am nam akere má i wuwuruwa? Na ba kun minɛm ni, bo bu seeri ka Sɔnɔ n bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ni bá ni.

ref: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
text: Ambɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ yo sunmaari i mɛrɛkɛm ye bu faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa maari am n a, ama, am ade má i.” Kabo bu tuuri Satɛfano yabuɛ bo kuunri i barasu ni

ref: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
text: Kabo Satɛfano jɔjɔɔri sɔ ni, na yaa ati Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n agaya, na ba kan bu nɔɔ awura nu, na bu sukpɛsɛ bu jee.

ref: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
text: Ama, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura Satɛfano ŋu, na wɔ tu i tii aniɛ aŋgoro, na wɔ ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ woo­wa ata papasi barasu. Na wɔ ŋu ka Yesu jina Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

ref: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”

ref: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
text: Nyini ti, na Petoro ayasu asu bu su na bu kɛrɛ akɔ. Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri ni, na ba fa i afun akɔ aŋgoro sɔɔ n nu, na nkuna­fɔm kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i, na bu susun, na ba fa tanlɛɛm ni tani barasu barasu bo Dɔkasi la ŋgɔɔ na i kpa maari bu n akere yiri Petoro.

ref: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
text: Na Petoro afɔn bu kɛrɛ afite sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ kutu i naŋgoroma su, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ, na wɔ kaaki i nyunu awɔ fuin ni, na wɔ se i ka, “Tabita, yasu.” Saŋga bo i tekeeri i nyu­mbaa ni, na wɔ ŋu Petoro, na wɔ yasu atana.

ref: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
text: Na Petoro aso i saa nu abuka i, na wɔ yasu ajina, na wɔ fere Nyɛmɛ minɛm n ni nkuna­fɔm ni kɛrɛ, na wɔ fa i akere bu ka wɔ teŋge.

ref: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
text: Na nyini labari ni abara ayɛ Jopa miɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na minɛ beberebe ayo Mibiɛ n yarada.

ref: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
text: Na Petoro awo Jopa le­nnyɔ, i yo woori jɛrɛkɛ be dika a, bu fere i ka Simon.

ref: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
text: Sɔnɔ be yo woori Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu, bu fere i ka Koneliyo, i ti Roma­fɔ sojam n kpiɛn wɔ, i niɛ soja mbaa yaa su a. Bu fere nyini soja jama mbaa n ka Itali sojam.

ref: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
text: Koneliyo ti sɔnɔ bo i su Nyɛmɛ atin su wɔ, i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ, na bu soro Nyɛmɛ. Na i suyo sakpa ma Yuda­fɔm bo bu ti yaari­fɔm ni, na i susere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
text: Na cɛɛn n be wiɛsu kerefi nsan saŋga, na wɔ kaba aŋu Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be, na mɛrɛkɛ n aba ase i ka, “Koneliyo.”

ref: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Na wɔ niɛ i gbirim, na sɛrɛ ati i, na wɔ bisa i ka, “M Mibiɛ, nzu a?” Na mɛrɛkɛ n abɔ i nu ka, “Nyɛmɛ ade u sere, wɔ ŋu sakpa n bo a yo ama yaari­fɔm ni, i wara koro fi má nyini sam kɛrɛ.

ref: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
text: I wo jɛrɛkɛ be dika a, bu fere i gusu ka Simon, i dika n wo fa dodo tieku n wɔ.”

ref: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
text: Mɛrɛkɛ n jɔjɔɔri i dɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ kɔ, na Koneliyo afere i ngbɛfɛnɛm nnyɔ, ni soja kun be bo i su Nyɛmɛ atin su na i wo i dika ni.

ref: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
text: Na alecin ŋgarama, na ba de atin na bu kɔ Jopa, bu kɔ sudodo dɔ fa kɔ wiɛ kekete saŋga, na Petoro gusu ayasu afun sɔɔ su ka i sere Nyɛmɛ.

ref: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
text: Na ahɔɛ ati i, na wɔ koro deke be i di. Saŋga bo bu wo diire n yo su ni, na deke be aba i dɔ ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su.

ref: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
text: Na wɔ ŋu ka aŋgoro n ateke, na wɔ ŋu deke be i ti ka tani bambaka wɔ, ba cici i bue nna n kɛrɛ, i sucɔɔndi ba asiɛŋgu.

ref: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
text: Ajaa­nna barasu kɛrɛ, ni cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ, ni laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ ka bu wo nu.

ref: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Na wɔ ti akonvi be ŋga, i ŋa, “Petoro, yasu na a kun di.”

ref: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Ama, na Petoro ase i ka, “M Mibiɛ, ai. M nya di má deke bo i ti ciire na i la fin.”

ref: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Na akonvi n akaaki ase i bekun ka, “Deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i casi, má a se ka i ti fin.”

ref: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
text: Deke n yoori sɔ kɔ juuri kun kun kpe nsan a, na wɔ kaba afun akɔ aŋgoro.

ref: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
text: Saŋga n bo Petoro subirisi fa kɔ deke n bo wɔ ŋu i n su ni, nyini saŋga, minɛm bo Koneliyo sunmaari bu ni, abisa asi Simon dika, na ba ka ajina awuru n anɔ nu.

ref: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
text: Na ba fere sɔnɔ be, abisa i ka, Simon Petoro wo dɔ ɔ?

ref: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
text: Petoro toko wo sunsun su, fa kɔ deke bo i baari i dɔ ka alɛlɛ nu n bu. Dɔ na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase i ka, “Ti fɛn. Minɛ nsan nbem wo fɛn suniɛniɛ u busu.

ref: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
text: Nyini ti, yasu na a jura, na u ni bɛrɛ kɔ, má a yo sika, dama mini sunmaari bu a.”

ref: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
text: Na Petoro ajura akɔ minɛm n dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, “Mini ye am niɛniɛ m n a. Am natiiri bu la ka sɛ wɔ?”

ref: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
text: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, “Sojam kpiɛn Koneliyo sunmaari ya u dɔ a. I ti sɔnɔ kpa a, na i soro Nyɛmɛ, na Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ bu i agaya. Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be seeri i ka i fere u, na a ba i dika na a ba kan jɔrɛ bo a la i kere i a.”

ref: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
text: Nyini ti, na Petoro ade minɛm n awura, na ba da i dɔ. Alecin ŋgarama, na Petoro ayasu, na i ni minɛm n akɔ, bu sukɔ ni, na yarada­yo­fɔm nbem bo bu wo Jopa n gusu asu i su akɔ.

ref: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
text: Na i alecin fɔfɔrɛ, na ba ju Sizariya, bu kɔ ŋu ka Koneliyo atiɛn i lamɔsim, ni i danfo kpam nɔɔ suŋminda yiri Petoro.

ref: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
text: Saŋga bo Petoro kɔ ŋa i wura awuru n nu ni, na Koneliyo akpa i atin akutu i jaa bu haari asiɛŋgu, na wɔ su i.

ref: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
text: Ama, na Petoro ayɛ i su ajina, na wɔ se i ka, “Mini ni m tii mmɔɔ, ti adamande wɔ.”

ref: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
text: Petoro ni Koneliyo bitaari anzama sɔ ɔ haari bu kɔ wuraari awuru n nu, dɔ na wɔ ŋu ka minɛ beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ.

ref: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka, i ti ciire wɔ ka yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm fa ya ŋu saŋga ni daanfiɛ barasu be nu, wara ya wura bu dika. Ama, Nyɛmɛ akere m ka má m sinndi sɔnɔ be ka i woo­wa ti fin.

ref: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
text: Na Koneliyo ase i ka, “I nyuma le­nna lɛɛ, ka ahin saŋga n sɔ, fa kɔ wiɛsu kerefi nsan, m wo m dika n nu wo nyɛmɛsere su, na sɔnɔ be akaba aba ajina m nyunu, i wura sutura fofoe papasi,

ref: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: na wɔ se m ka, ‘Koneliyo, Nyɛmɛ ade u sere, ye wɔ teŋge u sakpa bo a yo ama yaari­fɔm n su.

ref: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
text: Nyini ti, sunma na bu kɔ Jopa, na bu kɔ fere Simon Petoro ni. I wo jɛrɛkɛ be dika a, bu fere i gusu ka Simon, i dika n wo dodo tieku n wɔ.’

ref: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
text: Nyini ti, ye m kaba sunmaari ka bu fere u n a, ye wɔrɔ gusu a yo sakpa ye a ba. Kisa, ya kɛrɛ gusu atiɛn ya nɔɔ fɛn Nyɛmɛ nyunu, na ya ti deke n kɛrɛ bo Mibiɛ n ama u nɔɔ ka a fa ma ya ni.” Kabo Petoro kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n Koneliyo dika ni

ref: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
text: Na Petoro anya ase ka, “Kisa, ma si nahɔrɛ su ka adamande kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i tambu má nu.

ref: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i soro Nyɛmɛ na i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, i mi koro i ti nzu daanfiɛ sɔnɔ, Nyɛmɛ de i wɔ.

ref: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
text: Am si labari n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari Yisareli­fɔm, nyini labari kpa n kere ka minɛm nya tana­laifiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, yiri ti sɔnɔ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a.

ref: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
text: Am kɛrɛ si sa bo i boori i bu Galili dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, saŋga bo Yohane kaanri Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛ n jɔrɛ ni, haari na wɔ bara ayɛ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

ref: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
text: Am si Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n jɔrɛ ni, kabo Nyɛmɛ faari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni yiko n wuraari i ŋu ni, ni kabo i nati bara yɛɛri deke kɛrɛ suyo sakpa sutuntun minɛm kɛrɛ bo Sitana miɛ bu n na bu nya laifiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ woori i dɔ a.

ref: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
text: Yɛrɛ, ya ŋuuri deke n kɛrɛ bo i yoori bu Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ asiɛn su, ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni. Na ba kpama i baka jaanu n su, na wɔ wu.

ref: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
text: Ama, i le­san cɛɛn, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i afite i yiwee n nu, na Nyɛmɛ ama minɛm aŋu i.

ref: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
text: Má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋuuri i a, ama, yɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri ya ka ya di i daani ni, yɛrɛ ŋuuri i a, na ya ni yiri asaŋga adi, na ya nu, saŋga bo i teŋgeeri ni.

ref: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
text: Na wɔ ma ya nɔɔ ka ya kɔ kan labari kpa n kere minɛm na ya di daani ka, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i bá di ŋgɔɔ­fɔm, ni bo ba wu n kɛrɛ jɔrɛ a.

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
text: Saŋga bo Petoro toko jina wo jɔjɔ su ni, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aba awura minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu jina suti labari n ŋu.

ref: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm bo bu ti yarada­yo­fɔm na bu suuri Petoro su fiteeri Jopa miɛ kpiri n nu baari n ayo alekutura, kabo Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura minɛm n bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm mmɔɔ ŋu ni.

ref: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
text: Na bɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm ni, ati ka minɛm n sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu sukansi Nyɛmɛ. Na Petoro ase ka,

text: “Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura ahin minɛm n ŋu, kabo i fa wuraari ya ŋu ni. Ye ŋma koro kete ka má ya biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue a?”

ref: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
text: Nyini ti, na Petoro ama nɔɔ ka bu biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, Yesu Kristo duma n nu. Na ba sere i ka i wo bu dɔ le­nnyɔ.

ref: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
text: Nyini ti, saŋga bo Petoro kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Yuda­fɔ yarada­yo­fɔm nbem ajaraki i.

ref: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
text: Bu ŋa, “Nzu ti ye a kɔ minɛm bo bu wura má kɛnkɛnɛ n dika ye u ni bɛrɛ kɔ saŋga di diire a?”

ref: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
text: I ŋa, “M wo Jopa miɛ kpiri n nu, wo nyɛmɛsere su ni, na ma ŋu deke be, i yo m ka alɛlɛ dɛɛ su wɔ. M ŋuuri deke be, i ti ka tani bambaka wɔ, ba cici i bue nna n kɛrɛ, i sucɔɔndi fite aŋgoro n nu suba asiɛŋgu, na wɔ ba ajina m bi.

ref: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
text: Na ma niɛ i nu nyunyumi, na ma ŋu ajaa­nnam barasu kɛrɛ, ni cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ, ni laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ ka bu wo nu.

ref: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Na ma ti akonvi be ŋga, i ŋa, ‘Petoro, yasu na a kun di.’

ref: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Ama, na ma se i ka, ‘M Mibiɛ, ai. M nya di má deke bo i ti ciire na i la fin.’

text: Na ma kaaki ati akonvi ni ŋga bekun, i fite aŋgoro wɔ, i ŋa, ‘Deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i casi, má a se ka i ti fin.’

text: Deke n yoori sɔ kɔ juuri kun kun kpe nsan a, na wɔ kaaki afun akɔ aŋgoro.

ref: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
text: Nyini saŋga n nu, na minɛ nsanm n bo bu sunmaari bu m dɔ n afite Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu aba aju awuru n bo m wo nu ni.

ref: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
text: Na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase m ka m yasu na m su bu su kɔ, má m yo sika. Na ma su bu su, na ya kɛrɛ akɔ. Na ahin yarada­yo­fɔ nsɛnm n gusu asunma m afite Jopa, na ya kɛrɛ akɔ Sizariya, na ya kɔ awura Koneliyo dika.

ref: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
text: Na wɔ se ya kabo i ŋuuri Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ i dika barasu ni, ni kabo mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka i sunma na bu kɔ Jopa, na bu kɔ fere sɔnɔ n bo bu fere i ka Simon Petoro ni,

ref: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
text: na i bá kan jɔrɛ be kere u ni u dika­fɔm kɛrɛ na am nya tii­de.

ref: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.

ref: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Na ma teŋge su kabo Mibiɛ n yo seeri ni. I ŋa, ‘Yohane yo faari nzue biɛɛri am a, ama, mini fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ba wura am ŋu a.’

ref: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka Nyɛmɛ faari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wuraari yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm bo ya yo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo yarada n ŋu, ye wɔ fa awura bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm gusu ŋu. Mini la ŋma bo m koro kanda Nyɛmɛ ka má i yo sa bo i koro ka i yo i a?”

ref: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
text: Saŋga bo bɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm tiiri sɔ ni, ba se má jɔrɛ be kun, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ, bu ŋa, “Nyɛmɛ ama minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n atin ka bɛrɛ gusu kaaki bu wo na bu nya ŋgɔɔ.” Kristo jama n bo i wo Antiyoki n jɔrɛ

ref: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
text: Fite saŋga n bo bu kuunri Satɛfano n dɛɛ sin, na ba kere yarada­yo­fɔm wuwuruwa, nyini ti, na ba sandi abara ayɛ deke kɛrɛ, na nbem akɔ Fonisiya awɔrɛ n nu, ni Sipurusi asiɛn n su, ni Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu kɔ sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n sukere Yuda­fɔm ŋgumi.

ref: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
text: Ama, bu nbem wo nu, bu fite Sipurusi, na nbem gusu fite Sirene miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba kɔ Antiyoki, na bu sukan ya Mibiɛ Yesu labari kpa n jɔrɛ sukere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

ref: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
text: Na Mibiɛ n yiko awo bu dɔ nahɔrɛ su, na minɛ beberebe ayo yarada, na ba kaaki bu wo, na ba su Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su.

ref: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.

ref: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
text: Saŋga bo i juuri na i ŋuuri sakpa bo Nyɛmɛ ayo ama minɛm sɔ ni, na i ahore ajɔ, na wɔ tu bu fɔ ka bu ma bu nyunu boro yarada n bo bu bita i fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su ni, na bu su i su ni bu ahore­mbaa kun.

ref: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
text: Banabasi tiiri sɔnɔ kpa a, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awo i dɔ, na i la yarada agaya. Na minɛ beberebe aba ayo Mibiɛ n yarada.

ref: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
text: Na Banabasi akɔ Tarasu miɛ kpiri n nu ka i kɔ niɛniɛ Sɔɔlu.

ref: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
text: Saŋga bo Banabasi kɔ ŋuuri Sɔɔlu ni, na wɔ fa i na ba kɔ Antiyoki, na bɛrɛ minɛ nnyɔ n kɛrɛ akɔ awo dɔ afɔ kun, na ba tiɛn Kristo jama n nɔɔ, na ba kere minɛ beberebe. Antiyoki dɔ ye bu du mɔ fereeri yarada­yo­fɔm n ka Kristo­fɔm a.

ref: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
text: Nyini saŋga n nu, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nbem afite Yɛrusalem aba Antiyoki dɔ.

ref: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
text: Bu fere bu nu kun ka Agabusi, dɔ na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama i yiko, na wɔ yasu ajina ajɔjɔ ka, ahɔɛ bambaka be bá tɔ durunya n kɛrɛ nu. Nyini ahɔɛ n ba tɔɔri saŋga bo Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri bo bu fere i ka Kalodisi n diiri i fɛmɛya n a.

ref: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
text: Na ba yiyi ŋwaa ni, na ba fa ŋwaa n asunma Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu ka bu fa kɔ ma Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni.

ref: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
text: Fa kɔ nyini saŋga n nu, na Fɛmɛ Herode abo i bu sukere Kristo jama minɛ nbem wuwuruwa.

ref: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
text: Na wɔ ma ba fa takobe akun Yohane gɔrɔ Yakobo.

ref: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
text: I yoori saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm sudi bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n wɔ.

ref: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
text: Nyini ti, na wɔ ka dan­saraka n nu, na Kristo jama n asere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro ama i. Kabo Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n yiiri Petoro fiteeri dan­saraka n nu ni

ref: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ n bo i alecin na Fɛmɛ Herode má bu yi Petoro fite na bu di i jɔrɛ ni, i da sojam nnyɔ afiɛn wɔ. Na ba wura i jɔrɔkɔ aminda bu i bue nnyɔ n kɛrɛ, na nbem gusu kaaki jina dan­saraka anɔ n nu ŋminda.

ref: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
text: Kpeŋga na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be aba ajina dan­saraka n nu, na sɔɔ n ata wein. Na mɛrɛkɛ n abo Petoro gbɛngbɛn, na wɔ teŋge i, na wɔ se i ka, “Yasu ndende.” Na jɔrɔkɔ n bo i wo Petoro saa n afite atɔ.

ref: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka, “Bo u dambara ni, na a fa u gbagbaa n wura.” Na Petoro ayo sɔ, na wɔ kaaki ase i ka, “Fa u tanlɛɛ n wura, na a su m su.”

ref: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
text: Na ba kɔ asin sojam ciciri su dɛɛ n ŋu, na ba kɔ asin nnyɔ su dɛɛ n gusu ŋu, na ba kɔ aju bulaari anɔ n bo bu teke i ka na bu sin kɔ miɛ n kunnu ni. Na anɔ n ni i tii ateke, na ba fite. Bu toori atiŋgbere n nu kɔɔri mɔ kaan, na mɛrɛkɛ n akaba ayaki Petoro.

ref: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
text: Dɔ na Petoro nyunu akoro, na wɔ se ka, “Kisa, ma si ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka Micɛra n ye wɔ sunma i mɛrɛkɛ n ka i yi m fite Fɛmɛ Herode saa nu, ni deke n kɛrɛ bo Yuda­fɔm sunsun ka bu yó m ni.”

ref: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
text: I faari nyini sa n kɛrɛ nu sɔ ni, na wɔ kɔ Mariya dika, i wa la Yohane n bo bu kaaki fere i Marako ni, nyini saŋga, jama beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ wo nyɛmɛsere su.

ref: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
text: Na wɔ si akonvi n ka Petoro lɛɛ, i ahore ajɔ agaya n dɛɛ ti, wɔ teke má anɔ ni, na wɔ ŋmati akpie i nyi, na wɔ kɔ ase minɛm bo bu wo awuru n nu n ka Petoro jina dasin.

ref: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
text: Na ba se i ka, “A bo fiɛ wɔ.” Ama, na wɔ kaaki ajɔjɔ ni anyum­boro ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Dɔ na ba se ka, “Kisa, i mɛrɛkɛ a.”

ref: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
text: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ Petoro toko jina anɔ n nu wo bobo su. Kasin, na ba ba ateke anɔ ni, na saŋga bo bu ŋuuri ka yiri ni, na ba yo alekutura.

ref: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
text: Na Petoro afa i saa ayo bu ka bu yaki naŋmiɛ. Na wɔ kan akere bu kabo Mibiɛ n yiiri i fiteeri dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu barasu ni. Na wɔ se bu ka, “Am kɔ kan ahin jɔrɛ n kere Yakobo ni yarada­yo­fɔ nyiŋgam n kɛrɛ.” Na wɔ fite bu bi bɛrɛ akɔ deke be.

ref: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
text: Ale ba ciinri ni, na jɔrɛ n amiɛ sojam ni, ba si má kabo bu yo. Na ba se ka, “Ye nzu ayo Petoro a?”

ref: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
text: Na Fɛmɛ Herode ama nɔɔ ka bu niɛniɛ Petoro busu, ama, ba ŋu má i. Nyini ti, na wɔ bisa sojam bu jɔrɛ ni, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu kun bu. Nyini sin, na Fɛmɛ Herode afite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu, na wɔ kɔ awo Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu fa kɔ saŋga su. Kabo Fɛmɛ Herode wuuri barasu ni

ref: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
text: Na Fɛmɛ Herode afa yaa agaya Tiro ni Sidon miɛ n nu­fɔm ŋu, nyini ti, na ba fa sɛsɛ­su, na ba kɔ ka bu ŋu i. Bu du mɔ kɔɔri sɔnɔ be dɔ ɔ ka, bu fere i ka Blasitusi, na bu ni i dɛɛ ayo kun ka. Blasitusi ti Fɛmɛ Herode gisan n niɛ­fɔ kpiɛn wɔ. Na wɔ nya fa bu akɔ Fɛmɛ Herode dɔ, na ba kɔ asere ka i ma bu ni yiri nya tana­laifiɛ. Deke bo i ti na bu yoori sɔ n la ka, dama bu diire kɛrɛ fite yiri fɛmɛ n dɛɛ asiɛn n su wɔ.

ref: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
text: Cɛɛn n bo bu kpamaari i ba juuri ni, na Fɛmɛ Herode awura i fɛmɛya sutura, na wɔ tana i fɛmɛya biɛ n su, na wɔ jɔjɔ minɛm n dɔ.

ref: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
text: I jɔjɔ wieeri ni, na minɛ kɛrɛ atiɛn, bu ŋa, “Ahin yiri, nyɛmɛ be ajɔjɔ a, má adamande.”

ref: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
text: Kpeŋga na Micɛra n mɛrɛkɛ akaba abo i aji, dama wɔ ma má Nyɛmɛ jirima n dɛɛ ti. Na wɔ da tukpaki, na kakabam awura i, na wɔ wu.

ref: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu yoori bu juma n Yɛrusalem miɛ nu wieeri ni, na ba fa Yohane bo bu kaaki fere i ka Marako ni, na ba fite Yɛrusalem na ba kaaki akɔ Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
text: Saŋga bo bu cici bu nɔɔ na bu wo Mibiɛ n su su ni, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase bu ka, “Am yi Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu ma m, na bu yo juma n bo ma yi bu ka bu yo i ni.”

ref: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
text: Bu ciciiri bu nɔɔ, na bu sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri ni, na ba fa bu saa anana bu ŋu, na ba ma bu atin na ba kɔ. Kabo Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu kɔɔri Sipurusi ni

ref: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
text: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n asunma Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu na ba kɔ aju miɛ kpiri be su, bu fere i ka Sɛlusiya, na ba fa lee bambaka dɔ, na ba kɔ miɛ be bo i wo lui bambaka be su, bu fere nyini lui n ka Sipurusi n su.

ref: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
text: Na ba fa bɛrɛ asin akɔ aju lui bambaka n wiɛ atɔɔri dɛɛ bue ni, miɛ kpiri be su, bu fere i ka Pafɔsi. Na ba kɔ aŋu boboyaya­fɔ be dɔ, bu fere i ka Baari Yesu, i ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, i yo i ŋu ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ, ama, i ti má i.

ref: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
text: I yo wo lui bambaka n su gomina n dɔ ɔ, bu fere gomina n ka Sɛgosi Pɔɔlu, i ti si­fɔ ɔ. Na wɔ fere Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu, dama i koroori ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ.

ref: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
text: Ama, boboyaya­fɔ ni, bu kaaki fere i duma Giriki aniɛ nu ka Elamasi ni, na wɔ boro i nyi ka i kanda gomina n na má i yo Yesu yarada.

ref: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
text: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura Sɔɔlu, bo bu kaaki fere i ka Pɔɔlu ŋu, na wɔ niɛ boboyaya­fɔ n gbirim, na wɔ se i ka,

ref: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
text: “Wɔrɔ, a ti Sitana dɛɛ wa a. A ti deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa n kpɔ­fɔ ɔ. Kɔrɛ ni satiɛ­yo asunnu u ŋu. A ti Mibiɛ n atin kpa n saaki­fɔ ɔ. Cɛɛn bɔnɔ ye a yaki i a?

ref: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
text: Ti fɛn, Mibiɛ ni bó u ji kisa, na a yó anyinsin fa kɔ saŋga su, na a ŋú má wiɛ ka i ta kun.” Kpeŋga na i nyunu akaba asi biri, na i suturiŋgi suniɛniɛ sɔnɔ bo i kere i atin.

ref: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
text: Saŋga bo gomina n ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na i tiiri kabo bu kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na wɔ yo i alekutura, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yo Mibiɛ n yarada. Kabo Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi kɔɔri Pisidiya dɛɛ Antiyoki miɛ n nu ni

ref: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
text: Na ba fite Pɛrɛga akɔ aju Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Pisidiya awɔrɛ n nu ni. Na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn, na ba kɔ awura atana bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu.

ref: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
text: Saŋga bo bu kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kadasi n bo Moyisi kɛrɛɛri i ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n wieeri ni, na bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n asunma ase Pɔɔlu ka, “Njam, nzɛn am la jɔrɛ be bo am kan kere jama ni, na i ma bu ahoresi, am kan kere bu.”

ref: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
text: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu ayasu ajina, na wɔ yɛ i saa su, na wɔ bo i bu na i jɔjɔ, i ŋa, “M lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm, ni ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm na am su Nyɛmɛ ni, am ti fɛn.

ref: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
text: Yisareli­fɔm Nyɛmɛ ni, yiri yo yiiri ya nam a, na wɔ ma ba tɛrɛ abuka su, saŋga bo bu yo ti wɔfɔm Ijipiti asiɛn su ni. Na Nyɛmɛ afa i dɛɛ yiko bambaka n na wɔ yi bu afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su,

ref: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
text: Am si am ŋu sa, adena má sam bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo seeri n be tu am.

ref: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
text: Na wɔ butu daanfiɛ barasu nso n bo bu wo Kanana asiɛn n su ni, na wɔ fa bu asiɛn n ama Yisareli­fɔm ka bu fa yo bu tiɛŋgu dɛɛ.

ref: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
text: Na Nyɛmɛ ayi Sɔɔlu fɛmɛya n nu, na wɔ fa Fɛmɛ Davidi bo i ti Jese wa n ajina i jinaa­biri. Na Nyɛmɛ adi i ŋusu daani, i ŋa, ‘Yiri Davidi, Jese wa n ti sɔnɔ bo m ahore atara i a, yiri yó m sa koroo­wa kɛrɛ a.’

ref: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
text: Yiri Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu nu ye Nyɛmɛ seeri ka Yisareli­fɔm Tii­de­fɔ Yesu ni fite n a.

ref: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
text: Kana Yesu ba ba, Yohane du mɔ ba kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kereeri Yisareli­fɔm wɔ, ka bu kaaki bu wo na i biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue.

ref: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: Na saŋga bo Yohane ba sukoro ka i wie i juma n ni, na wɔ se minɛm n ka, ‘Am sunsun ka mini la ŋma a? Ai, má mini la sɔnɔ n bo am ŋminda i ka i bá n a. Ama, am ti fɛn. Yiri bo i wo m sin suba ni, m ti kaan mmɔɔ ni bo m dodo i.’

ref: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
text: M ninbaam, ambɛrɛ bo am ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm ni, ni ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm na am su Nyɛmɛ ni, yɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ ma ba fa ahin tii­de labari n ama ya a.

ref: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
text: Dama Yɛrusalem­fɔm ni bu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ asi má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n bu, haari ni bo bu kan i bu Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni. Ba kaaki asi má kabo Yesu ti barasu, na ba ma ba kun i. Nyini ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kadasi n ayo yii­wa a.

ref: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
text: Na saŋga bo bu yoori sa n kɛrɛ bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu fa kɔ i ŋusu jɔrɛ ni, na ba jura i baka jaanu n su, na ba fa i awura yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma nu.

ref: But God raised him from death!
text: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i yiwee n nu,

ref: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
text: na wɔ yi i ŋu bo i la má nɔ akere minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu suuri i su fiteeri Galili awɔrɛ n nu kɔɔri Yɛrusalem ni. Bɛrɛ n bo bu ŋuuri sam bo bu yoori ni, kisa bɛrɛ sudi i daani kere ya minɛm n a.

ref: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
text: Nyini ti, ya ba ka ya kan labari kpa sam n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fa ma ya nam ni kere am a,

ref: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
text: ye i ma nɔaniɛ n ayo yii­wa ama yɛrɛ bo ya ti bu busu nu­fɔm n a, wɔ teŋge Yesu i yiwee n nu, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Kansi Jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n nu, i jue nnyɔ su ni. Nyɛmɛ ŋa, ‘Wɔrɔ ti m Wa a, nyuma, m káaki u Si.’

ref: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
text: Bekun, kabo i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu na i kpɔrɔ má faŋ­kunma nu ni, Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase ka, ‘M fá deke kpa n bo i ti casi na i la anyunuyi n ma u, yiri ye m seeri ka m fa ma Fɛmɛ Davidi n a.’

ref: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
text: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i Kansi Jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n awɔrɛ be nu, ka, ‘A yáki má na u Amusui­su­fɔ Casi n wu kpɔrɔ.’

ref: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
text: Fɛmɛ Davidi yo yoori Nyɛmɛ kun koroo­wa haari na wɔ ba awu, na ba se i deke bo bu seeri i nam ni, na i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n akpɔrɔ faŋ­kunma n nu.

ref: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
text: Ama, yiri n bo Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, wɔ kpɔrɔ má.

ref: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
text: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, m koro ka am si ka yiri Yesu dɛɛ ti ye bu kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri am a, ka Nyɛmɛ afa am satiɛ ace am.

ref: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
text: Bu yo seeri ka, ‘Ambɛrɛ bo am yo Nyɛmɛ anzasi ni, am ti fɛn, am yo alekutura na am ka ŋgbɛɛn, dama m suyo juma be am dɛɛ saŋga nu, haari má be kan kereeri am, am dé má i.’”

ref: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
text: Na saŋga bo Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi sufite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu ni, na minɛm n asere bu ka bu kaaki bra Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n bo i suba ni, na bu ba kan nyini jɔrɛm bo ba kan akere bu ni, na bu tí bekun.

ref: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
text: Saŋga bo minɛm ni ba sandiiri tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n ni, na Yuda­fɔm nbem, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba kaaki bu woo­wa na bu su Yuda­fɔm atin n su ni, na bu dɔŋgu asu Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi su. Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi atu bu fɔ, ka má bu kaaki bu sin bo sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ ayo ama bu ni.

ref: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
text: Saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni ŋuuri jama n sɔ ni, na ba si Pɔɔlu kɔrɛ, na bu ni yiri awura akuruwa, na ba kpiɛ i nzukɔ.

ref: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi ajɔjɔ ni ahoresi, bu ŋa, “I ti ye ni ka ya du mɔ kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere ambɛrɛ wɔ ka. Ama, kabo am akete i na am koro má ka am nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, ya yáki am wɔ, na ya kɔ́ kan kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

ref: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
text: Dama nɔaniɛ maa­wa bo Mibiɛ n fa maari ya n lɛɛ. I ŋa, ‘Ma yi u ka a yo wein ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, adena durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ nya tii­de.’”

ref: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
text: Saŋga bo minɛm n bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm tiiri ahin nɔaniɛ n sɔ ni, na bu ahore ajɔ, na ba kansi Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ ni. Na minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ka bu nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n ayo yarada.

ref: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
text: Na Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ n asandi abara ayɛ nyini awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

ref: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
text: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n awura minɛm bu sin, bɛrɛ la nbaram bo bu la wo na bu su Nyɛmɛ ni, ni nbiɛsɔm bo bu wo miɛ n nu na bu se na i bi ni. Na ba kere Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi wuwuruwa, na ba fɔn bu afite bu asiɛn n su.
