ref: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
text: Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi akpɔnkpɔn bu jaa tuturi agɔ bu ŋu, nyini kere ka ba kete miɛ n nu­fɔm lɛɛ, na ba kɔ miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Ikoniyom n su.

ref: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
text: Na Yesu susu­fɔm bo bu wo Antiyoki dɔ ni, bu ahore ajɔ agaya, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awo bu dɔ.

ref: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
text: Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi kɔ juuri Ikoniyom miɛ kpiri n nu ni, bu kaaki yoori sɔ bekun, na ba wura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n ni anyum­boro haari na minɛ beberebe ayo yarada, Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ.

ref: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
text: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm nbem bo bu yo má yarada n awura minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm bu sin akpura bu ni yarada­yo­fɔm n tii.

ref: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
text: Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi awo dɔ ayo le­nnyɔ. Na bu sujɔjɔ Mibiɛ n jɔrɛ ni ahoresi, na Mibiɛ n ama bu faŋga, na wɔ ma bu atin na ba yo alekutura sa barasu barasum, adena minɛ ŋu bu na bu si ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
text: Na kpakpaaki­nu aba miɛ n nu­fɔm dɔ, na nbem ajina Yuda­fɔm n sin, na nbem gusu ajina Yesu jaramasam n sin.

ref: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
text: Na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm ni, fa saŋga ni bu mɔ­fɔm n afa sɛsɛ­su atara kabo bu yo Yesu jaramasam ni tiɛ, na bu tu bu yabuɛ.

ref: But when the two apostles found out what was happening, they escaped to the region of Lycaonia. They preached the good news there in the towns of Lystra and Derbe and in the nearby countryside. Paul and Barnabas in Lystra
text: Dɔ na ba kan labari kpa n akere minɛm. Kabo Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi kɔ woori Lisitira miɛ kpiri n nu jɔrɛ ni

ref: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
text: Sɔnɔ be woori Lisitira miɛ kpiri n nu, i jaa awu, kabo bu wuuri i ni, i ti wɔbuka a, wɔ nati má.

ref: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
text: Na wɔ tana aso i sui ati kabo Pɔɔlu sujɔjɔ ni. Na Pɔɔlu afa i nyu­mbaa akpama i ŋu, na wɔ fa nu ka sɔnɔ n ayo yarada, ka i koro tuntun i na i nya laifiɛ.

ref: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
text: Na wɔ tiɛn kpa ase i ka, “Yasu jina cencen.” Na sɔnɔ n ayasu kungere­kun awarakpin sunati bara yɛ.

ref: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
text: Saŋga bo jama n ŋuuri sa n bo Pɔɔlu ayo i sɔ ni, na ba tiɛn kpa Likawoniya aniɛ n nu, bu ŋa, “Ya nyɛmɛm akaaki ka adamandem wɔ, ye ba jura aba ya dɔ a.”

ref: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
text: Na ba fere Banabasi duma ka Ziyosi, bu amɔɛ kpiri duma lɛɛ, na ba fere Pɔɔlu ka Hɛrɛmesi, dama yiri ti amɔɛ n cɛm a.

ref: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
text: Na amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Ziyosi n sɔɔ jina miɛ n ahɛmɛ, na amɔɛ n fɔ n ni miɛ n nu­fɔm anyɔndi tooram ni baka pum afa aba miɛ n anɔ nu ka bu fa yo tiɛ ma Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi.

ref: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
text: Dawa saŋga ni, yiri Nyɛmɛ yo maari atin wɔ ka daanfiɛ kɛrɛ yo kabo i koro.

ref: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
text: Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ, saŋga kɛrɛ i yo sakpa ma am wɔ, i ma nzue tɔ ma am wɔ, na am alɛ­mbaa bo am dɔ bu n yo nyunyumi, na am di, na am kunnu yi, na i ma am ahorejɔ. Nyini kɛrɛ kere am ka i wo bɛrɛ a.”

ref: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
text: Haari ni nyini jɔjɔɔ­wa n kɛrɛ n mmɔɔ ni, i yoori kekereke maari Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi ye bu koro kandaari jama n ye ba yo má tiɛ ama bu n a.

ref: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
text: Na Yuda­fɔ nbem afite Antiyoki ni Ikoniyom miɛ kpirim n nu aba Lisitira, na ba ma minɛm ade bu nɔaniɛ, na ba tutu Pɔɔlu yabuɛ, bu sunsun ka wɔ wu wɔ, na ba cin i afite miɛ n nu.

ref: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
text: Ama, na Yesu susu­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i, na wɔ yasu, na wɔ kpie i nyi awura miɛ n nu. Na alecin fɔfɔrɛ, na yiri ni Banabasi afite dɔ, na ba kɔ Dɛrɛbi miɛ kpiri n nu. Kabo Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi kaaki kɔɔri Siriya dɛɛ Antiyoki n su ni

ref: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
text: Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi akan labari kpa n Dɛrɛbi miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba nya susu­fɔm beberebe dɔ. Na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ Lisitira, ni Ikoniyom miɛ kpirim n nu, na ba nya akɔ Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Pisidiya awɔrɛ n nu ni.

ref: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
text: Na ba se yarada­yo­fɔm ni ka, bu ma bu yarada n yo bii­wa, na ba ma bu ahoresi. Na ba se bu ka, “Wɔ yo daka ka bu kere ya wuwuruwa beberebe ka, na ya nya koro wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.”

ref: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
text: Miɛ kɛrɛ bo bu wɔɔri su, bu yi nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm wɔ Kristo jama n nu, na bu cici bu nɔɔ, na bu sere Nyɛmɛ, na bu fa bu wura yiri Mibiɛ n bo ba yo i yarada n saa nu ka i niɛ bu dika.

ref: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
text: Bu fite dɔ ni, na ba fa Pisidiya awɔrɛ n nu akɔ atiɛ Pamfiliya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

ref: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
text: Dɔ na ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Pɛrɛga miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba nya akɔ Ataliya miɛ kpiri n nu,

ref: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
text: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri Antiyoki ni, na ba tiɛn Kristo jama n nɔɔ, na ba kan sam n bo Nyɛmɛ ayo bu ni, ni kabo wɔ teke atin ama bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba yo yarada barasu ni.

ref: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
text: Na ba wo yarada­yo­fɔm n dɔ miɛ n nu acɛ.

ref: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
text: Tɔ, na minɛ nbem afite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu aba Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu sukere yarada­yo­fɔm ni bu dɛɛ keree­wam. Bu ŋa, “Má ka ba wura am kɛnkɛnnɛ kabo Moyisi yo kereeri ni, am koro nya má tii­de.”

ref: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
text: Na Pɔɔlu, ni Banabasi, ni bɛrɛ ase akuruwa nyannyan fa kɔ nyini jɔrɛ n bu su. Nyini ti, na ba fa sɛsɛ­su ka Pɔɔlu, ni Banabasi, ni yarada­yo­fɔm nbem bo bu wo Antiyoki ni, ka bu kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu na bu kɔ bisa Yesu jaramasam ni, ni Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nyini jɔrɛ ni.

ref: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
text: Na Kristo jama n akaari bu na ba kɔ. Bu wo kɔ su ni, na ba fa Fonisiya, ni Samariya awɔrɛm n nu, na ba kan akere dɔ­fɔm kabo minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n ayo Nyɛmɛ yarada barasu ni, na nyini labari n ama yarada­yo­fɔm n ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su.

ref: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
text: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri Yɛrusalem ni, na Kristo jama ni, ni Yesu jaramasam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ade bu ni bu saa nnyɔ, na ba kan akere bu fa kɔ deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ma ba koro ayo i ni.

ref: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
text: Ama, na yarada­yo­fɔ nbem bo bu su Farasi­fɔm atin n su n ayasu ajina, ase ka, “Wɔ yo daka ka minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba yo Yesu yarada n awura kɛnkɛnnɛ ka, na bu nya ma bu nɔɔ ka bu su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa maari Moyisi n su.”

ref: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
text: Na Yesu jaramasam ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na bu kpokpo jɔrɛ n nu nyunyumi.

ref: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
text: Na ba se nyini jɔrɛ n akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu acɛ, na Petoro ayasu ajina, na wɔ se bu ka, “M ninbaam, am si ka mini ye Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri m fiteeri am nu, ka m ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n ti labari kpa ni, adena bu yo yarada.

ref: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ bo i si kabo adamande ahore­mbaa ti barasu ni, akere ka ba yo yarada nahɔrɛ su, dama wɔ fa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama bu, kabo i yo fa maari yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni.

ref: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
text: Nyɛmɛ dɔ, yɛrɛ ni bɛrɛ la má kpakpaaki­nu lɛɛ, dama yarada n bo ba yo i n dɛɛ ti, wɔ ŋmisi ayi satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo bu ahore su ni.

ref: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
text: Kisa, nzu ti ye am koro ka am yo Nyɛmɛ na am niɛ a, ye am susɔ yarada­yo­fɔm sɔɔri bo ya nam, ni yɛrɛ ni ya tii akoro asɔ má i a?

ref: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
text: Ai. Yɛrɛ ayo yarada ka ya Mibiɛ Yesu ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye ya nya tii­de a, nyini ma bɛrɛ gusu nya tii­de a.”

ref: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
text: Na minɛm kɛrɛ atɔ amuna, na bu ti kabo Banabasi ni Pɔɔlu sukan kere bu fa kɔ alekutura sa barasu barasum bo Nyɛmɛ ma bu koro yo maari minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

ref: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
text: Saŋga bo bu jɔjɔ wieeri ni, na Yakobo gusu ayasu ajɔjɔ. I ŋa, “M ninbaam, am ti fɛn.

ref: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
text: Simon ateŋge ya su kabo Nyɛmɛ ni i tii adu mɔ akere ka i cɛcɛ wo minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm nu ni, ni kabo wɔ yi bu nbem afa bu ayo i dɛɛ minɛm ni.

ref: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
text: Micɛra n ŋa, ‘Nyini sin, m káaki ba yɛ Fɛmɛ Davidi awuru n bo wɔ tɔ n su, na m tá i bɔnfɔ ni, na m yó i dɔŋgu.

ref: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
text: Na Yakobo akaaki ase bu ka, “Mini dɛɛ sunsunni la ka, minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu suba Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, wɔ yo má daka ka ya miɛ bu.

ref: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Ama, i ti ye ni ka ya kɛrɛ kadasi ma bu, na ya se bu ka, má bu di tiɛ niɛ, na má bu yo sakaraya, na má bu di niɛ bo ba kpiɛ má i kɔm, na má bu di niɛ mbunja.

ref: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
text: Dama ba du mɔ akan Moyisi nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n jɔrɛ ya nam saŋga, haari ni nyuma bu toko kan i kadasi miɛ n kɛrɛ su wɔ, na bu sukan i na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn kɛrɛ.” Kabo bu kɛrɛɛri kadasi maari Antiyoki­fɔm ni

ref: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
text: Dɔ na Yesu jaramasam ni, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Kristo jama n kɛrɛ afa sɛsɛ­su asoro su ka bu yi bu nu nbem, na bu sunma bu ni Pɔɔlu, ni Banabasi Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu, bɛrɛ la Yudasi bo bu fere i ka Basabasi ni, ni Silasi. Nyini minɛ nnyɔm kɛrɛ ti Kristo jama n mɔ­fɔm wɔ.

ref: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
text: Na ba kɛrɛ kadasi ama bu ka bu fa kɔ. Kɛrɛɛ­wa n la ka, “Yɛrɛ am ninbaam, ni Kristo jaramasam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni subisa am dɔŋgu, ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm na am wo Antiyoki ni, ni bo bu wo Siriya ni Silisiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm nu ni.

ref: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
text: Ya ti ka ya nbem afite ya nu fɛn aba am dɔ, aba akan jɔrɛ nbem akere am bo wɔ miɛ am, na wɔ yo am sunsunni agaya. Yɛrɛ ni ya tii asunma má bu.

ref: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
text: Nyini ti, ye ya kɛrɛ atana asɛsɛ su, asoro su ayi minɛm asunma bu ni ya danfo kpam Banabasi ni Pɔɔlu am dɔ.

ref: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Bɛrɛ ye ba fa bu ŋgɔɔ ama ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti n a.

ref: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
text: Nyini ti, ya súsunma Yudasi ni Silasi am dɔ, na bɛrɛ ni bu tii bá teke bu nɔɔ, na bu kán jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo kadasi n nu kere am.

ref: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
text: Má am di tiɛ niɛ, má am di niɛ mbunja, má am di niɛ bo ba kpiɛ má i kɔm, na má am yo sakaraya. Nzɛn am tara am ŋu na am ayo má ahin sam ni, i yó kpa ma am. Tɔ, ya ka saŋga lɛɛ.”

ref: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
text: Na minɛm bo bu sunmaari bu n akɔ aju Antiyoki. Bu kɔ juuri ni, na ba nya atiɛn yarada­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ nɔɔ, na ba fa kadasi n ama bu.

ref: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
text: Saŋga bo bu kaanri kadasi n wieeri ni, na bu ahore ajɔ, na wɔ kaaki ama bu ahoresi.

ref: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
text: Na Yudasi, ni Silasi bo bɛrɛ ni bu tii ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ beberebe akere bu, na bu ahore asi, na wɔ buka bu faŋga.

ref: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
text: Na ba wo dɔ acɛ, na yarada­yo­fɔm n akaari bu ni ahoreda, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ bɛrɛ n bo bu sunmaari bu n dɔ. [

ref: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi aka Antiyoki, na bu ni bu beŋgu dɔŋgu akan na ba kere minɛm Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ ni. Kabo Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi kpaaki yakiiri bu beŋgu ni

ref: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
text: Tɔ, fa kɔ saŋga su, na Pɔɔlu ase Banabasi ka, “Ma ya kpie ya nyi kɔ miɛm kɛrɛ bo ya kaanri Mibiɛ n jɔrɛ kereeri bu n su, na ya kɔ niɛ kabo ya ninbaam ti barasu.”

ref: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Má Banabasi koro ka bu fa Yohane bo bu kaaki fere i ka Marako n na bu kɛrɛ kɔ ɔ,

ref: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
text: ama, na Pɔɔlu akete ka i sú má bu su, dama saŋga bo bu wo Pamfiliya awɔrɛ n nu ni, i ŋmati yakiiri bu wɔ, wɔ su má bu su abuka má bu ni juma ni.

ref: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
text: Dɔ na akuruwa bambaka aba bu afiɛn, na ba kpaaki ayaki bu beŋgu, na Banabasi afa Marako abuka i ŋu su, na ba fa lee bambaka akɔ Sipurusi asiɛn n su,

ref: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
text: na Pɔɔlu gusu afa Silasi ka i su i su, na yarada­yo­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ asere Nyɛmɛ ama bu, ka bu di Mibiɛ n ahɔrɔma.

ref: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
text: Na wɔ kɔ abara ayɛ Siriya, ni Silisiya awɔrɛm n nu, na wɔ ma Kristo jama n ahoresi.

ref: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
text: Na Pɔɔlu anati haari na wɔ kɔ aju Dɛrɛbi, ni Lisitira miɛ kpirim n nu, sɔnɔ be woori dɔ, i ti Kristo susu­fɔ ɔ, bu fere i ka Timoti. I ni ti Yuda­fɔ bara bo wɔ yo Kristo yarada wɔ, ama, na i si ti Giriki­fɔ.

ref: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
text: Na yarada­yo­fɔm bo bu woori Lisitira ni Ikoniyom miɛ kpirim n nu n kɛrɛ ase ka Timoti ti sɔnɔ kpa a.

ref: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
text: Pɔɔlu koroori ka i fa Timoti buka i ŋu su wɔ, na bu sunati, na wɔ wura i kɛnkɛnnɛ. Má deke be dɛɛ ti a, ama, Yuda­fɔm bo bu woori dɔ n dɛɛ ti, dama bu kɛrɛ si ka Timoti si ti Giriki­fɔ ɔ.

ref: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
text: Na ba nati miɛ miɛm su, na ba kan akere yarada­yo­fɔm fa kɔ jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Yesu jaramasam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n asɛsɛ su asoro su ka bu yo i n su. Na ba se bu ka bu su nyini atinm su nyunyumi.

ref: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
text: Nyini ti, na Kristo jamam n anya faŋga, na bu yarada ayo bii­wa abuka su, na bu sunya minɛm subuka su ale kɛrɛ bo i cin. Kabo Pɔɔlu ŋuuri sɔnɔ be ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su ni

ref: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
text: Na ba fa Firigiya, ni Galatiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu asin, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama má bu atin na bu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

ref: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
text: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri Masiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ awieeri ni, má bu koroori ka bu wura Bitiniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu wɔ, ama, Yesu dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ n asoro má bu su ka bu wura dɔ.

ref: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
text: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ, na Pɔɔlu aŋu deke be ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su, ka sɔnɔ be wo Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu, jina susere i, i ŋa, “Jande, bra Makɛdoniya fɛn na a ba buka ya.”

ref: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
text: Pɔɔlu ŋuuri nyini deke n ka alɛlɛ nu n dɛɛ ti, ya kaba yoori siriya a, na ya kɔ Makɛdoniya, dama ya si ka Nyɛmɛ ye wɔ se ka ya kɔ dɔ na ya kɔ kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm n bo bu wo dɔ n a. Kabo Lidiya yoori Yesu yarada ni

ref: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
text: Na ya fite bɛrɛ akɔ miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Filipiya n su, yiri ti Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nyini bue n miɛm kɛrɛ tii a. Roma­fɔm niɛ i su a. Na ya yo le­nnyɔ dɔ.

ref: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
text: Na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, na ya fite miɛ n nu akɔ kunma nu, dɔ ye ya sunsun ka ya kɔ ŋu ka deke bo Yuda­fɔm n tiɛn bu nɔɔ sere Nyɛmɛ a. Na ya tana, na ya ni nbaram bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n abo anzama.

ref: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
text: Bara be woori bu nu, bu fere i ka Lidiya, i fite miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Tiyatira n su wɔ, i tɔ sanvuin kpakpam bo i la gɔ, i ti bara bo i su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ ateke i sunsunni, na wɔ so i sui nyunyumi, na wɔ ti kabo Pɔɔlu suse ni.

ref: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
text: Na ba biɛ i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue, na wɔ sere ya jande ka, “Nzɛn am asoro su ka ma yo Mibiɛ n yarada nahɔrɛ su, am ba wo m dika.” I yoori ya awaraye n dɛɛ ti, na ya soro su. Kabo bu nyiiri Pɔɔlu ni Silasi dan­saraka nu ni

ref: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
text: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, ya sukɔ deke bo Yuda­fɔm n tiɛn bu nɔɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ni, na ya ni suŋguru be ayɛ, i ti afani wɔ, ajini wo i ŋu, na i nati yi munzue. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na i to­fɔm anya ŋwaa beberebe afite i munzue yi n nu.

ref: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
text: Na i nati susu ya ni Pɔɔlu su, na i sutiɛn suse ka, “Ahin minɛm ti Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n dɛɛ amusui­su­fɔm wɔ. Bɛrɛ kere am kabo am yo na am nya tii­de a.”

ref: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
text: I nati yoori sɔ ɔ, cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, haari na yaa aba ati Pɔɔlu, na wɔ kaaki aniɛ i, na wɔ tiɛn ajini n bo i wo suŋguru n ŋu n na wɔ se i ka, “Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n dɛɛ yiko nu, fite yaki i.” Kpeŋga, na ajini n afite ayaki i.

ref: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
text: Tɔ, saŋga bo afani n to­fɔm ŋuuri ka bu koro nya má ŋwaa fite má i n dɔ kun ni, na ba tara Pɔɔlu ni Silasi, na ba cin bu akɔ miɛ n kunnu afa bu awura yiko­fɔm saa nu.

ref: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
text: Na jama n kɛrɛ atɔ Pɔɔlu ni Silasi ŋu ni jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn. Na jɔrɛ­di­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ama ba yi Pɔɔlu ni Silasi suturam, na ba ma nɔɔ ka bu fin bu ŋgbere.

ref: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba bo bu nyunyumi, na ba kɔ anyi bu dan­saraka, na ba ma dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n nɔɔ ka i tu anɔ n na i niɛ bu dika nyunyumi.

ref: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
text: Dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n deeri nyini nɔaniɛ n wieeri sɔ ni, na wɔ fa bu akɔ awura dan­saraka n sɔɔ n bo i wo mɔ n nu, na wɔ bo bu tobarim.

ref: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
text: Na fa kɔ kɔŋguɛ jinanjin saŋga, na Pɔɔlu ni Silasi susere Nyɛmɛ, na bu sudi jue kansi i, na bu beŋgu dan­sarakam ni suso bu sui suti.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
text: Jina n nu bɛrɛ, na asiɛn n akpisi agaya, haari na dan­saraka sɔɔ n jaafa bo bu fa boori i bu n kɛrɛ akpisi, na anɔm n kɛrɛ ateke, na tobari n kɛrɛ n bo bu fa boori bu n akporo atɔtɔ.

ref: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
text: Na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n abo nu ayasu, na wɔ ŋu ka dan­saraka sɔɔ anɔm n kɛrɛ ateke, i sunsun ka dan­sarakam kɛrɛ aŋmati asandi wɔ, nyini ti, na wɔ cin awɔrɔ i takobe ka i kun i ŋu wɔ.

ref: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu atiɛn kpa ase ka, “Má a ma u ŋu mandim. Ya kɛrɛ wo fɛn.”

ref: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
text: Na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n afere ka bu fa sin bra, na wɔ bereki awura Pɔɔlu ni Silasi sɔɔ n nu, na sɛrɛ ati i, na i ŋuniɛ subobo i, na wɔ kɔ akutu bu jaa bu.

ref: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
text: Dɔ na wɔ fa bu afite, na wɔ bisa bu ka, “M mibiɛm, m yo sɛ ye m nya tii­de a?”

ref: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
text: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, “Yo Mibiɛ Yesu yarada, na a nyá tii­de, wɔrɔ ni u dika­fɔm kɛrɛ.”

ref: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
text: Na ba kan Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ n akere i, i ni bo bu wo i dika n kɛrɛ.

ref: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ n kɛrɛ, na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n afa Pɔɔlu ni Silasi akɔ, na wɔ kɔ aŋmisi bu kani n kɛrɛ, na ba kaba abiɛ yiri ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue.

ref: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
text: Dɔ na wɔ fa bu akɔ i dika, na wɔ ma bu diire, na ba di. Na yiri ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ ahore ajɔ agaya ka ba yo Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti.

ref: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
text: Alecin ŋgarama, na jɔrɛ­di­fɔm n asunma npulisim ka bu kɔ se dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n ka i yaki nyini minɛm nu ji na bu kɔ.

ref: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
text: Nyini ti, na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n asere Pɔɔlu ka, “Jɔrɛ­di­fɔm n ama nɔɔ ka ya yaki am na am kɔ. Nyini ti, am kɔ ni ahoreda.”

ref: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ase npulisim ni ka, “Haari ni bo ya ti Roma­fɔm ni, ba di má ya jɔrɛ aŋu má ya ŋgasi be, na ba bo ya jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, na ba nyi ya dan­saraka. Ye kisa bu sukoro ka bu fiɛ yi ya ji bɛnlɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Nyini koro yo má, má ka bɛrɛ ni bu tii aba na bu ba yi ya ji.”

ref: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
text: Na npulisim n akɔ ase jɔrɛ­di­fɔm ni. Bu tiiri ka Pɔɔlu ni Silasi ti Roma­fɔm ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu.

ref: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
text: Nyini ti, na ba ba atara Pɔɔlu ni Silasi jaa, na ba nya ayi bu afite dan­saraka n nu, na ba sere bu jande ka bu fite miɛ n nu.

ref: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
text: Na ba fite dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu, na ba kaaki akɔ Lidiya dika, na ba kɔ aŋu yarada­yo­fɔm ni, na ba ma bu ahoresi, na ba nya afite miɛ n nu akɔ.

ref: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
text: Na Pɔɔlu ni Silasi afa asin Amfipolisi, ni Apoloniya miɛ kpirim su, na ba kɔ aju Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu. Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ wo dɔ.

ref: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
text: Nyini ti, kabo i nati yo ni, na Pɔɔlu awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu akɔ aju Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn nsan, na i ni bɛrɛ subo anzama fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n su,

ref: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
text: na i sukan, na i suyi i bu kere bu, kabo wɔ yo daka ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ba di wahara na i teŋge i yiwee nu ni. Na wɔ se bu ka, “Yiri Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n la Yesu n bo m kan i jɔrɛ kere am ni.”

ref: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm nbem ade jɔrɛ n bo Pɔɔlu kaanri i ni, ni Giriki­fɔm beberebe bo bu su Nyɛmɛ ni, ni nbaram beberebe bo bu la anyunuyi ni, na bu kɛrɛ afa bu ŋu amata Pɔɔlu ni Silasi.

ref: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
text: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm n asi bu kɔrɛ agaya, na ba fere minɛm ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn nbem bo bu la má kpɛrɛ na bu nati tana gɔɔsu ni, na ba tiɛn jama dɔŋgu nɔɔ. Na ba kpisi miɛ ni, na ba kɔ sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Jason n dika, akɔ ajina i dika anɔ nu, deke bo Pɔɔlu ni Silasi wo ni, na bu sutiɛntiɛn ka i yi bu ma bu na bu fa bu kɔ jama n nu.

ref: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
text: Ama, bu kɔ niɛniɛɛri na ba ŋu má bu ni, na ba tara Jason ni yarada­yo­fɔ nbem acin bu afa bu akɔ miɛ n nu yiko­fɔm dɔ, na bu sutiɛn suse ka, “Ahin minɛm n bo ba saaki durunya n abara ayɛ ni, aba ya miɛ n nu fɛn,

ref: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
text: haari Jason ade bu awura i dika. Ba kete Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n nɔaniɛ n bo wɔ fa ama ka ya su i su ni, bu ŋa fɛmɛ be wo bɛrɛ bekun, bo bu fere i ka Yesu.”

ref: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
text: Bu seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ yɛ yiko­fɔm ni jama n ahore su.

ref: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
text: Na yiko­fɔm n akpiɛ Jason ni i beŋgum kɔbuka, na ba tɔ kaari, na ba nya ayaki bu aji. Kabo Pɔɔlu woori Bɛriya miɛ kpiri n nu ni

ref: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ n kɛrɛ, na yarada­yo­fɔm n ase Pɔɔlu ni Silasi atin nu akɔ aju miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Bɛriya n su. Bu kɔ juuri dɔ n na ba kɔ awura Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu.

ref: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm ni dɔŋgu ayo yarada, fa saŋga ni Giriki­fɔm beberebe, nbaram bo bu la anyunuyi n ni nbiɛsɔm.

ref: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
text: Ama, saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm bo bu wo Tɛsalonika n tiiri ka Pɔɔlu akɔ akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Bɛriya dɔ gusu ni, na ba ba dɔ aba awura minɛm bu sin, na ba yɛ bu yaa su.

ref: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
text: Kpeŋga na yarada­yo­fɔm n ayo ndende afa Pɔɔlu akɔ ase haari tieku n nɔɔ su, ama, na Silasi ni Timoti yiri aka Bɛriya dɔ.

ref: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
text: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu wo Atensi miɛ kpiri n nu suŋminda Silasi ni Timoti ni, na i ahore asaaki agaya kabo i ŋuuri ka amɔɛm asunnu miɛ n nu ni.

ref: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kɔ awura Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na i ni bɛrɛ, ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu su Nyɛmɛ ni, subo anzama. Na i sukɔ gɔɔsu cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na i sukan kere minɛm bo bu wo dɔ ni.

ref: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
text: Sifɔm asɛsɛ nbem wo miɛ n nu, bu fere bu kun ka Epikurifɔm, na bu fere nyiŋga n ka Sitɔwa­fɔm, na bu suse akuruwa ni Pɔɔlu, na bu nbem abisa ka, “Ye ahin jɔrɛ beberebe­fɔ n ŋa sɛ wɔ lɛɛ?” Na bu nbem abɔ nu ka, “I jɔjɔ fa kɔ amɔɛ fɔfɔrɛ nbem jɔrɛ wɔ.” Deke bo i ti na bu seeri sɔ n la ka, Pɔɔlu sukan Yesu ni yiwee teŋge n su jɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
text: Ahin keree­wa n bo a sukere i ni, ya si má i, ya koro ka a ma ya ti i bu wɔ.”

ref: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
text: Dama Atensi­fɔm ni wɔfɔm bo bu wo miɛ n nu n dɛɛ la ka, saŋga kɛrɛ bu sukan na bu suti keree­wa fɔfɔrɛm wɔ.

ref: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
text: Na Pɔɔlu ayasu ajina miɛ n nu tere­tere­fɔm n afiɛn, na wɔ se ka, “Ma ŋu kabo ambɛrɛ Atensi­fɔm su amɔɛm na am yo munzue sam ni anyum­boro.

ref: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
text: Dama kabo m nati bara yɛ niɛ yi kɔrɛsi ni, ma ŋu dekem bo am su bu ni, na ma ŋu bimbindi be bo am akɛrɛ su ka, ‘Nyɛmɛ n bo ya si má i n dɛɛ woo­biri lɛɛ.’ Tɔ, yiri n bo am su i na am si má i ni, yiri ye m kan i jɔrɛ kere am a.

ref: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
text: Nyɛmɛ bo i yiiri durunya n ni deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo nu ni, yiri ti aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a, i wura má amɔɛ sɔɔm bo adamande afa i saa ata bu n nu na i da.

ref: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
text: Sasu bekun, i fun má deke be bo i se adamande ka i yo ma i, dama yiri ni i tii ma adamande ŋgɔɔ ni ŋumi a, ni deke n kɛrɛ a.

ref: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
text: Yiri ma adamande n kɛrɛ fite sɔnɔ kun busu n nu wɔ, na wɔ ma ba tɛrɛ ade durunya n kɛrɛ. Na wɔ nya akpama saŋga saŋga ama bu kɛrɛ, na wɔ kaaki akere bu kabo bu asiɛn nyaari ti.

ref: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
text: Deke n bo i ti na i yoori sɔ n la ka, bu bá turiŋgi niɛniɛ i busu na beni bu ŋú i. Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ ni, i wo má dede ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ be.

ref: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
text: Dama yiri dɛɛ ti ye ya la ŋgɔɔ ye ya wo bɛrɛ ye ya nati a. Kabo am jue­di­fɔ nbem gusu ase ni, ka, ‘Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ti i nbaam wɔ.’

ref: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo ya nya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam ni, má ya sunsun ka i woo­wa ti ka sika, wara jɛtɛ, wara yabuɛ nyunyumi, wara deke be bo juma si­fɔm afa bu alecira ayo i.

ref: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
text: Dawa saŋga ni, Nyɛmɛ ayo aniɛ má minɛm fa kɔ bu sa­yoo­wam su, dama bu yo si má. Ama, kisa, wɔ ma nɔɔ ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo deke kɛrɛ kaaki i wo na i yaki i satiɛ.

ref: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
text: Dama wɔ du mɔ akpama cɛɛn na wɔ yi sɔnɔ be ka i bá di durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ ni nahɔrɛ. Deke bo i kere ya kɛrɛ ka i bá yo sɔ n la bo i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni.”

ref: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
text: Saŋga bo bu tiiri ka Pɔɔlu sukan fa kɔ yiwee teŋge n su n jɔrɛ ni, na bu nbem ayo i anzasi. Ama, na bu nbem ase ka, “Ya koro ka a kaaki ba kan ahin jɔrɛ n bekun na ya ti.”

ref: When Paul left the council meeting,
text: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu akɔ ayaki bu.

ref: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
text: Na bu nbem asu i su na ba yo yarada. Bɛrɛ la Diyonusiyɔsi, i ti Aropagɔsi­fɔm n nu kun wɔ, ni bara be bo bu fere i ka Damarisi ni, ni minɛm nbem.

ref: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
text: Na wɔ kɔ atu Yuda­fɔ sɔnɔ be dɔ, bu fere i ka Akuyila, i ti Pontusi asiɛn su fɔ ɔ, i ni i yi, bu fere i ka Pirisila, bu fiteeri Itali asiɛn su baari a, wɔ nya cɛ má ye Pɔɔlu ŋuuri bu n a, dama Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kalodisi n ama nɔɔ ka Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ fite i asiɛn n su. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Pɔɔlu akɔ ka i kɔ niɛ bu busu,

ref: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
text: na wɔ kɔ aseke bu wɔfɔ, na i ni bɛrɛ asaŋga ayo juma, dama bu kɛrɛ ti jɛrɛkɛm ni tani wu­fɔm wɔ.

ref: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
text: Na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn kɛrɛ, i suwura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na i subo anzama ni bɛrɛ, ni Giriki­fɔm kɛrɛ, adena i kaaki bu sunsunni na bu yo yarada.

ref: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
text: Ama, Yuda­fɔm n keteeri i nɔaniɛ n wɔ, na ba kpiɛ i nzukɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kpɔnkpɔn i sutura wuraa­wa tuturi agɔ bu ŋu, na wɔ se bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ, nzɛn am nya má tii­de, i ti am dɛɛ jɔrɛ wɔ, má m dɛɛ. Fite kisa fa kɔ, m kɔ́ minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n dɔ ɔ lɛɛ.”

ref: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
text: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu afite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu ayaki bu, na wɔ kɔ sɔnɔ be dika, i ti má Yuda­fɔ, ama, i su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, bu fere i ka, Titosi Jusitosi, i dika n mata tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n wɔ.

ref: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
text: Na sɔnɔ n be bo bu fere i ka Kirisipusi na i ti bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n kpiɛn n ayo Mibiɛ n yarada, i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ, na Kɔrinto­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu tiiri labari n bo Pɔɔlu kaanri i ni, na bu kɛrɛ ayo yarada, na ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue.

ref: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
text: Cɛɛn n be kɔŋguɛ sɔ ɔ, na Mibiɛ n ajɔjɔ Pɔɔlu dɔ ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su, i ŋa, “Má a soro sɛrɛ, ama, kɔ su na a sukan labari kpa n kere bu, má a mɔ u nɔɔ,

ref: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
text: dama m wo u dɔ. Be koro fa má i saa kan má u na i yo u pui. Niɛ fɛn, m la minɛ beberebe miɛ n nu fɛn bo bu ti m dɛɛ.”

ref: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
text: Na wɔ wo bu dɔ akɔ aju afɔ kun ni kpaaki nu, na i sukere bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
text: Na ba se ka, “Ahin sɔnɔ ni suboro i nyi sukere minɛm keree­wa bo i wo i ŋgumi ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni.”

ref: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
text: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu tekeeri i nɔɔ ka i jɔjɔ ni, na Gomina Galiyo ase Yuda­fɔm ka, “Nzɛn má i ti ka wɔ yo satiɛ be, wara wɔ do ŋgasi be, ye am sujaraki i a, má m ti i bu ma am.

ref: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
text: Ama, kabo i nya ti bisaa­wam fa kɔ nɔaniɛm, ni dumam, ni am dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wam n su ni, ambɛrɛ ni am tii kɔ yo i dɔŋgu. Mini, m koro di má nyini jɔrɛ ni.”

ref: Then he sent them out of the court.
text: Na wɔ ma ba fɔn ayi bu afite jɔrɛ dii­biri sɔɔ n nu.

ref: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
text: Na bu kɛrɛ akaba atɔ sɔnɔ n bo bu fere i ka Sositeni n ŋu na bu subo i wɔ, jɔrɛ dii­biri n nyunu bɛrɛ, yiri ti tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ nkpiɛn a. Ama, Gomina Galiyo cɛcɛ awo má nyini n be nu. Kabo Pɔɔlu kpieeri i nyi kɔɔri Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu ni

ref: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
text: Na Pɔɔlu awo Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu acɛ, nyini sin na wɔ kaari yarada­yo­fɔm ni na i ni Pirisila ni Akuyila akɔ. Bu kɔ juuri miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kenciriya n su ni, na Pɔɔlu ayi i tii dɔ, dama wɔ du mɔ atan ndiɛ n dɛɛ ti. Na ba wura lee bambaka be nu dɔ, i sukɔ Siriya awɔrɛ n nu.

ref: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
text: Bu kɔ juuri Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ yaki Pirisila ni Akuyila dɔ, ama, na yiri akɔ awura Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na i ni bɛrɛ abo anzama fa kɔ labari kpa n su.

ref: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
text: Na ba sere i ka i wo bu dɔ le­nnyɔ, ama, wɔ soro má su.

ref: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
text: Na wɔ yo le­nnyɔ dɔ, na wɔ nya akaaki ayasu dɔ, abara ayɛ miɛ miɛm bo bu wo Galatiya, ni Firigiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu n kɛrɛ, na wɔ ma yarada­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ ahoresi. Kabo Apolosi kɔɔri Ɛfɛso ni Kɔrinto miɛ kpirim n nu ni

ref: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
text: Nyini saŋga ni, Yuda­fɔ sɔnɔ be aba Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu, bu fere i ka Apolosi, bu wuuri i Alɛkizanderiya miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ. I yo la si fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n su, na i koro jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ su.

ref: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
text: Ba du mɔ akere i keree­wam fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin su, na i ahore abi na i ŋuniɛ ti nyakanyaka, na i sukan na i kere minɛm Yesu jɔrɛ nahɔrɛ su. Ama, i si má nzue biɛɛ­wa be, má ka Yohane dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛɛ­wa ni ŋgumi.

ref: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
text: Na wɔ bo i bu na i sukan i ni ahoresi Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu. Saŋga bo Pirisila ni Akuyila tiiri i keree­wam n sɔ ni, na ba fa i akɔ awuru, na ba kere i Nyɛmɛ atin n su jɔrɛ nyunyumi abuka su.

ref: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Nyini sin, na Apolosi akoro ka i kɔ Akaya awɔrɛ n nu, na yarada­yo­fɔm ni bo bu wo Ɛfɛso n ama i ahoresi, na ba kɛrɛ kadasi ama bu ninbaam bo bu wo Akaya n ka bu de i wɔfɔ nyunyumi. Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri dɔ ni, na wɔ buka minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n nu na ba yo yarada ni.

ref: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm seeri akuruwa n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ fa Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n na wɔ kan ayi bu fieo fieo jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, ka, Yesu ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

ref: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
text: Saŋga bo Apolosi toko wo Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Pɔɔlu anati abara ayɛ dɔ dɛɛ ahɛmɛ miɛ miɛm n kɛrɛ su, haari na wɔ kɔ aju Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu. Dɔ na wɔ kɔ atu Yesu susu­fɔ nbem,

ref: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
text: na wɔ bisa bu ka, “Saŋga bo am yoori yarada ni, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura am ŋu?” Na ba bɔ i nu ka, “Ai, ya nya ti má ka Awiɛwiɛ Casi wo bɛrɛ mmɔɔ.”

ref: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu ase bu ka, “Yohane nzue biɛɛ­wa n bu la ka, minɛ kaaki bu wo na bu yaki bu satiɛ wɔ. Na wɔ se minɛm n ka bu yo sɔnɔ n bo i wo i sin suba n yarada, yiri la Yesu.”

ref: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Bu tiiri nyini n sɔ wieeri ni, na ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue ya Mibiɛ Yesu n dɛɛ duma nu.

ref: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
text: Na Pɔɔlu afa i saa anana bu ŋu, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura bu ŋu. Na ba jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu nbem, na ba kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ akere minɛm.

ref: There were about twelve men in this group.
text: Bu kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri nbiɛsɔm buru ni nnyɔ ɔ.

ref: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
text: Ama, na bu nbem afa ahore­mbaa kekereke, na ba kete ka bu yo má yarada. Na ba jɔjɔ jɔrɛ tiɛ fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su, jama n kɛrɛ nyunu. Nyini ti, na Pɔɔlu ayaki bu, na wɔ fa yarada­yo­fɔm n afite akɔ Tiranusi tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ bambaka n nu, na i ni bɛrɛ sutiɛn bu nɔɔ subo anzama, fa kɔ Yesu dɛɛ atin n su cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn.

ref: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ ama Pɔɔlu ayo alekutura sam bo bu la má ŋusu.

ref: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
text: Haari na sanvuinm ni suturam kɛrɛ bo ba kan i ŋuniɛ, na bu fa kɔ nana tukpaki­fɔm ŋu, bu nya laifiɛ wɔ, na ajinim fite yaki minɛm bo bu wo bu ŋu ni.

ref: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
text: Na Yuda­fɔm nbem bo bɛrɛ gusu nati jeŋgbe fɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm ŋu ni, aboro bu nyi ka bu fa Yesu duma n fɔn yi ajinim. Bu ŋa, “Yesu n bo Pɔɔlu kan i jɔrɛ n dɛɛ duma nu, ye m se am ka am fite yaki sɔnɔ n a.”

ref: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
text: Ama, na ajini n ase bu ka, “M si Yesu, na m kaaki si Pɔɔlu, ama, ye ambɛrɛ la ŋma n wɔ?”

ref: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
text: Na sɔnɔ n bo ajini n wo i ŋu ni, atɔ bu ŋu na wɔ kunma bu kɛrɛ faŋga, na wɔ bo bu na wɔ ma bu mandim, na ba ŋmati afite i dika n nu bu bu ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu n kɛrɛ tiiri nyini sa n sɔ ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu, na ba kansi Mibiɛ Yesu n duma agaya.

ref: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
text: Na minɛ dɔŋgu bo ba yo yarada n afite ajina, na ba kan bu ŋgasim ni bu satiɛm kɛrɛ bo ba yo bu n akere jama ni.

ref: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
text: Nyini n kɛrɛ sin, na Pɔɔlu afa sunsunni ka i kɔ Makɛdoniya ni Akaya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu, na i nya sin kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu. Na wɔ se ka, “Nzɛn m nya ŋuuri m natiiri n kɛrɛ nyaari, wɔ yo daka ka m kɔ́ Roma miɛ kpiri n nu gusu.”

ref: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
text: Na wɔ sunma i buka­fɔ nnyɔm ni, bɛrɛ la Timoti ni Irasitusi ka bu kɔ Makɛdoniya, na yiri aka Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, ayo le­nnyɔ.

ref: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
text: Nyini saŋga n ye Mibiɛ n atin n su su n ayo jɔrɛ agaya Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu n a.

ref: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
text: Jɛtɛ tunmi be yo wo dɔ, bu fere i ka Demetirɔsi, i fa jɛtɛ wɔ, na i fa yo sɔɔ kan kanm, bu ti ka amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Atɛmisi n wɔ, na i sufa tɔ ma minɛm. Na minɛm bo bu yo nyini juma n anya kpɛrɛ beberebe afite nu.

ref: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
text: Tɔ, na yiri Demetirɔsi afere minɛm bo bu yo nyini juma atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, “Njam, am si ka ya nya ya dɛɛ ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ fite ahin juma n bo ya yo i n nu wɔ e.

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: Yiri Pɔɔlu ni, kisa, am aŋu i e, am ati kabo wɔ koro akan ateke minɛ beberebe nyi ni, má bo bu wo Ɛfɛso fɛn ŋgumi, ama, ni bo bu wo Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu kɛrɛ, i ŋa amɔɛm bo adamandem yo bu n ti má nyɛmɛ ni.

ref: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Jama n tiiri ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu, na ba tiɛn kpa, bu ŋa, “Yɛrɛ Ɛfɛso­fɔm dɛɛ amɔɛ Atɛmisi ni, yiri ti yiko­fɔ a.”

ref: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
text: Na miɛ n abo nu, na minɛm suyo naŋmiɛ. Na jama n akaba akɔ atara Gayosi ni Arisitakusi, bɛrɛ bo bu suuri Pɔɔlu su fiteeri Makɛdoniya baari ni. Na ba cin bu akɔ tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ fofoe paa n nu.

ref: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
text: Má Pɔɔlu ni i tii koroori ka i kɔ deke bo jama n atiɛn bu nɔɔ n wɔ, na i jɔjɔ bu dɔ, ama, yarada­yo­fɔm n asoro má i su.

ref: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
text: Haari na nyini awɔrɛ n nu yiko­fɔm bo bu ti i danfom n asunma asere i jande ka má i kɔ tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ fofoe n nu.

ref: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
text: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ ni, bu nyunu ata, na jama n sutiɛntiɛn, suyo naŋmiɛ, nbem ŋa sɔ, nbem gusu ŋa ahin, ama, bu dɔŋgu asi má deke n bo i ti na ba ba atiɛn bu nɔɔ ni.

ref: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm asun sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Alɛkizanda n adu jama n nyunu, nyini dɛɛ ti, minɛ nbem sunsun ka yiri faari nyini sam n kɛrɛ baari a. Dɔ na wɔ yɛ i saa su ka jama n yaki naŋmiɛ, na i sukan na minɛm si deke bo i wo nu.

ref: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Ama, saŋga bo bu faari nu ka i ti Yuda­fɔ ni, na bu kɛrɛ atiɛn kungere­kun, bu ŋa, “Yɛrɛ Ɛfɛso­fɔm dɛɛ amɔɛ Atɛmisi ni, yiri ti yiko­fɔ a.” Ba tiɛntiɛn sɔ ɔ acɛ haari saŋga su.

ref: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
text: Na sɔnɔ be bo i ti bu miɛ n nu kadasi kɛrɛ­fɔ n aba bu dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, “Am yaki naŋmiɛ.” Dɔ na wɔ nya se bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ Ɛfɛso­fɔm, am ti fɛn, ŋma wo bɛrɛ bo i si má ka Ɛfɛso­fɔm niɛ Atɛmisi amɔɛ kpiri sɔɔ n ni yabuɛ n bo i fiteeri aŋgoro tɔɔri n su a?”

ref: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
text: Ahin minɛm bo am afa bu aba fɛn ni, ba wɔ má puitiri­pui afite má amɔɛ sɔɔ n nu, ba kaaki akpiɛ má nzukɔ be ajaraki má ya amɔɛ ni.

ref: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
text: Nzɛn Demetirɔsi ni i beŋgu juma­yo­fɔm ni, la jaraki fa kɔ bu be dɔ, jɔrɛ­di­fɔm ni gomina nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm wo bɛrɛ, bu koro jaraki nyini minɛm bu dɔ.

ref: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
text: Ama, nzɛn sa be wo bɛrɛ buka su na am koro ka ya yo i dɔŋgu a, am fa bra saŋga bo miɛ n nu jama n kɛrɛ la bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ ni.

ref: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
text: Naŋmiɛ n bo am ayo i nyuma ni, i la má kpɛrɛ, i koro fa ya wura jɔrɛ nu, dama ya koro kan má deke n bo i ti na am ayo sɔ ni.

ref: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
text: I jɔjɔɔri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ ma jama n asandi.

ref: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
text: Minɛm ni yoori naŋmiɛ n wieeri ni, na Pɔɔlu afere yarada­yo­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na wɔ ma bu ahoresi, na wɔ kaari bu, na wɔ kɔ Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu.

ref: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
text: Na wɔ fa nyini awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na wɔ jɔjɔ yarada­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ dɔ, na wɔ ma bu ahoresi, na wɔ fa dɔ akɔ aju Giriki­fɔm dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu,

ref: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
text: na wɔ wo dɔ haari sara nsan. Na saŋga bo i wo sɛsɛ­su su ka i kɔ Siriya awɔrɛ n nu ni, na wɔ ti ka Yuda­fɔm akpa bu ŋu akɔ aŋminda i atin nu, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yo i sunsunni ka i kpie i nyi fa Makɛdoniya a.

ref: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text: Na minɛ nbem asu i su akɔ, bɛrɛ la Sopateri bo bu fere i si ka Pirihusi na i fite Bɛriya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Arisitakusi, ni Sekundosi bo bu fite Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Gayosi bo i fite Dɛrɛbi miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Tikikusi, ni Torofimosi bo bu fite Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni, ni Timoti.

ref: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
text: Na bɛrɛ kɛrɛ adu mɔ akɔ aŋminda ya Tɔrasi miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
text: Na Yuda­fɔm bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ cɛɛn, na ya kɛrɛ atiɛn ya nɔɔ ka ya saŋga di diire. Na Pɔɔlu ajɔjɔ minɛm dɔ, haari kɔŋguɛ jinanjin, dama i koroori ka nyini cɛɛn alecin i kaari bu wɔ, na i kɔ yaki bu.

ref: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
text: Ya tiɛɛnri ya nɔɔ aŋgoro sɔɔ be nu wɔ, fitanam sunnu nu dɔ.

ref: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
text: Na gbɛfɛnɛ be, bu fere i ka Itikusi, na wɔ fun atana sɔɔ aŋgoro nsan n tukoro n su, na daafiri ati i. Pɔɔlu jɔjɔ cɛɛri na wɔ daafi, na wɔ fite aŋgoro aba atɔ asiɛŋgu. Bu jura kɔ maari i su ni, nyini saŋga wɔ wu.

ref: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu ajura akɔ asiɛŋgu, na wɔ fun abutu i ŋu, na wɔ fa i saa abara ayɛ i, na wɔ se bu ka, “Má am ma sɛrɛ ti am, dama i la ŋgɔɔ.”

ref: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
text: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu akpie i nyi afun akɔ aŋgoro sɔɔ n nu dɔ, na i ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ asaŋga adi diire, na ba bo anzama, haari ale akɔ acin, na wɔ kaari bu na wɔ kɔ.

ref: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
text: Na ba nya afa gbɛfɛnɛ n akɔ awuru ni laifiɛ, na bu ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su. Kabo Pɔɔlu fiteeri Tɔrasi kɔɔri Miletosi ni

ref: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
text: Na Pɔɔlu afa sɛsɛ­su ka yɛrɛ fa lee bambaka du mɔ ɔ, na yiri nati na ya ni yiri kɔ saa nu Asosi miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
text: Na Pɔɔlu aboro i nyi ka i ju Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ka, na bu di Pɛntɛkoti cɛɛn­kpiri ni, nyini ti, wɔ koro má ka i cɛ Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ nu, na wɔ san asin Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n dɛɛ bue ni. Kabo Pɔɔlu kaariiri Ɛfɛso dɛɛ Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni

ref: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
text: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu wo Miletosi miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ sunma afere Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu n ka bu ma bu ni yiri yɛ.

ref: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
text: Saŋga bo bu ba juuri i dɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, “Ambɛrɛ ni am tii si kabo m woo­wa ti barasu, saŋga kɛrɛ bo m woori am dɔ ni, fite cɛɛn gbingbin n bo m ba juuri Esiya awɔrɛ n nu ni.

ref: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
text: M yoori m ŋu kaan wɔ, na ma yo Mibiɛ n juma n ni nyunvuin. Haari ni bo Yuda­fɔm sɛsɛɛri su ka bu kere m wuwuruwa barasu n kɛrɛ ni, m mindi deeri i wɔ.

ref: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
text: Am si ka deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa na i buka am ni, m kaanri i kereeri am wɔ jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, ma fa afiɛ má am su, na m sunati awuru awurum n kɛrɛ nu na m sukere am.

ref: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
text: M kan kereeri Yuda­fɔm ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ wɔ, ka bu kaaki bu wo fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na bu yo ya Mibiɛ Yesu n yarada.

ref: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
text: Tɔ, kisa Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma m sukɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n a, ama, m gusu si má deke bo i kɔ́ yo m dɔ.

ref: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
text: Deke bo m si i n la ka, miɛ kɛrɛ bo m kɔ su ni, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n se m ka bu kére m wuwuruwa, na bu nyí m dan­saraka.

ref: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Ama, m fa má m tiɛŋgu ŋgɔɔ yo má pui. Deke bo i kpiɛ m daafiri n kɛrɛ la ka, má ka ma yo juma n bo Mibiɛ Yesu n afa ama m n awie. Yiri la ka, m kan labari kpa n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama minɛm ni.

ref: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
text: Kisa m si ka am be mmɔɔ ŋú má m kun, ambɛrɛ bo m yo nati am nu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ fa kɔ i fɛmɛya n su kere am ni.

ref: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ nyuma, ka wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a kaari ŋgbɛɛn, i ti má m jɔrɛ.

ref: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
text: Nyini ti, am bita am ŋu nyunyumi, na am kaaki niɛ Kristo jama minɛm kɛrɛ su, bɛrɛ bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama am ka am niɛ bu dika ni, kabo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ niɛ i nbɔɛm su ni. Nyini minɛm ye Nyɛmɛ faari i tiɛŋgu Wa mbunja deeri bu jiiri n a.

ref: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
text: M si tenle su ka m kɔ yakiiri am, minɛ tiɛm ba wura am nu, kabo bedi wura nbɔɛm nu sandi bu n wɔ.

ref: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am kpa am ŋu, na am teŋge su ka afɔ nsan lɛɛ bo m nati bɔbɔ am ni nyunvuin, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu.

ref: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
text: Kisa, m sufa am wura Nyɛmɛ saa nu wɔ lɛɛ, na i niɛ am dika, na m kaaki fa am wura i dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n bo i ma am ahɔrɔma nu, na i buka am na i má am nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i tiɛŋgu minɛm kɛrɛ ni.

ref: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
text: M konviki awo má be ŋwaa, wara sutura nu.

ref: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
text: Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka m yo faari m saa yoori m juma a, ye m fa bukaari m ŋu ni m lamɔkɔm n kɛrɛ a.

ref: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
text: Kabo ma yo juma n sɔ ni, m sukere am ka am gusu yo i sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, na am buka yaari­fɔm, na am teŋge su kabo ya Mibiɛ Yesu n ni i tii seeri ni. I ŋa, ‘Tere­fiɛ wo sɔnɔ bo i ce aceeri n dɔ tara sɔnɔ bo i de aceeri ni.’”

ref: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
text: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu jɔjɔ wieeri ni, na i ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ akutu bu jaa naŋgoroma su, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ.

ref: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
text: Na bu ahore asaaki agaya, dama i seeri bu ka bu ŋú má i kun. Na ba se i atin nu, na wɔ kɔ awura lee bambaka n nu.

ref: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
text: Dɔ na ya ŋu lee bambaka be ka i sukɔ Fonisiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na ya wura i akɔ.

ref: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
text: Ya sukɔ ni, na ya ŋu Sipurusi lui bambaka ni, na ya yaki ya saa biɛ su, na ya sin akɔ Siriya awɔrɛ n nu. Na ya nya akɔ ajina Tiro miɛ kpiri n nu, dɔ ye bu yi bu sɔɔri n lee n nu a.

ref: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
text: Ama, ya yasu cɛɛn n ba juuri ni, na ya fite ka ya kɔ, na bu ni bu yim, ni bu nbaam ase ya atin nu afite miɛ n nu akɔ tieku n nɔɔ su. Dɔ na ya ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ akutu ya jaa naŋgoroma su, na ya sere Nyɛmɛ.

ref: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
text: Nyini sin, na ya kaari ya beŋgu, na yɛrɛ awura lee bambaka n nu, na bɛrɛ gusu akpie bu nyi akɔ awuru.

ref: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
text: Na ya fite Tiro akɔ aju miɛ kpiri n nu, na ya kɔ aniɛ yarada­yo­fɔm ni busu, na ya da bu dɔ le­kun.

ref: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
text: Ya woori dɔ yoori le­nnyɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ be aba dɔ, bu fere i ka Agabusi, i fite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu wɔ.

ref: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
text: Na wɔ ba ya dɔ, na wɔ fa Pɔɔlu dambara, acici i dɛɛ jaam ni i saam. Na wɔ nya ase ka, “Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ŋa o, kabo Yuda­fɔm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem ni cíci ahin dambara n fɔ lɛɛ, na bu nyá fa i ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.”

ref: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
text: Saŋga bo ya tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na ya ni minɛm n bo bu wo dɔ n asere Pɔɔlu jande ka má i kɔ Yɛrusalem.

ref: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
text: Ama, na wɔ bisa, “Nzu ti ye am suyo sɔ na am saaki m ahore a? Mini, ma yo siriya na m kɔ Yɛrusalem, na bu kɔ́ cici m nyama, má nyini ŋgumi, ama, haari na bu kún m, ya Mibiɛ Yesu n dɛɛ ti.”

ref: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
text: I seeri sɔ ni, ya koro akanda má i ka má i kɔ, na ya mɔ ya nɔɔ, na ya se i ka, “Tɔ, ma Mibiɛ n dɛɛ koroo­wa yo.”

ref: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
text: Na Yesu susu­fɔm nbem bo bu fite Sizariya n asu ya su, na ba fa ya akɔ ka ya kɔ wo sɔnɔ be dika, bu fere i ka Manesoni, i ti Sipurusi­fɔ ɔ, wɔ su Yesu dɛɛ atin n su acɛ. Kabo Pɔɔlu kɔ niɛɛri Yakobo busu ni

ref: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
text: Saŋga bo ya kɔ juuri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na ya ninbaam n ade ya ni ahorejɔ nyunyumi.

ref: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
text: I alecin na Pɔɔlu afa ya akɔ Yakobo dɔ, na ya kɔ atu Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ ka bu wo i dɔ.

ref: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
text: Bu tiiri nyini nɔaniɛ n sɔ ni, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ. Ama, na ba se i ka, “Ya niwa Pɔɔlu, m ŋa a ŋu Yuda­fɔm akpii beberebe bo bu wo ya nu fɛn na ba yo Yesu yarada, ye bu gusu nyunu aboro ka bu su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su.

ref: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
text: Ba ti ka a kere Yuda­fɔm bo bu tana minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n nu ni, ka má bu su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Moyisi fa maari n su, ye a se bu ka má bu wura bu nbaam kɛnkɛnnɛ, na má bu kaaki su Yuda­fɔm nana sa­yoo­wam su.

ref: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
text: Nahɔrɛ, bu tí ka a ba. Ye ya yo sɛ wɔ?

ref: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
text: Ti fɛn, kabo ya yo n la ka, minɛ nna nbem wo ya nu fɛn bo ba tan ndiɛ.

ref: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
text: Su bu su na a kɔ biɛ fin kabo bu yoo­wa kere ni, na a tɔ bu tii su kaari, adena bu koro yi bu tii. Nyini ni má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo bu kan i fa kɔ u dɔ n ti kɔrɛ wɔ, na bu si ka wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ.

ref: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Ama, minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba yo yarada n yiri, ya kɛrɛ kadasi ama bu, ka má bu se ka bu di tiɛ niɛ, wara mbunja, wara deke bo wɔ wu i ŋgumi na ba kpiɛ má i kɔm, na má bu yo sakaraya.”

ref: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
text: I alecin, na Pɔɔlu afa minɛm n akɔ na ba kɔ ayo fin biɛɛ­wa yoo­wa ni, na wɔ nya awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ jɔjɔ akere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ni, cɛɛn n bo bu ba biɛ fin n wie, na bu kun kun n kɛrɛ yo bu tii su sa­yoo­wa ni. Kabo bu taraari Pɔɔlu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni

ref: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
text: Saŋga bo wɔ ka kaan na le­so cɛɛn n wie ni, na Yuda­fɔ nbem afite Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu aba dɔ, na ba ŋu Pɔɔlu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na ba yɛ jama n kɛrɛ ahore su, na ba tara Pɔɔlu.

ref: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
text: Na ba tiɛn, bu ŋa, “Yisareli­fɔm, am buka ya. Ahin sɔnɔ ni nati jeŋgbe deke n kɛrɛ kere minɛm kɛrɛ ɔ, ka má bu bu Yisareli­fɔm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni. Ye wɔ kaaki afa minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm aba awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ yo ahin woo­biri casi n fin.”

ref: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
text: Na miɛ n abo nu, na minɛm kɛrɛ aŋmati atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na ba tara Pɔɔlu acin ayi i afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na ba kaba anyi Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n anɔ ni ndende.

ref: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
text: Má bu koroori ka bu kun Pɔɔlu wɔ. Dɔ na labari n atu Roma­fɔm sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ka Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n abo nu.

ref: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text: Kpeŋga na wɔ yasu ndende, na wɔ fa sojam ni bu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, na ba ŋmati aju dɔ. Saŋga bo jama n ŋuuri soja kpiɛn n ni i sojam ni, na ba yaki Pɔɔlu, ba bo má i kun.

ref: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
text: Na sojam kpiɛn n akɔ atara Pɔɔlu, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu wura i jɔrɔkɔ nnyɔ. Na wɔ bisa ka, “Ahin sɔnɔ ti nzu sɔnɔ ɔ? Ye wɔ yo nzu wɔ?”

ref: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
text: Na jama ni nbem sutiɛntiɛn sujɔjɔ, bu jɔjɔɔ­wa n woori i ŋgumi ŋgumi wɔ, ba jɔjɔ má jɔrɛ kun. Bu naŋmiɛ dɛɛ ti, wɔ koro asi má jɔrɛ n bu. Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu fa Pɔɔlu kɔ bɛrɛ sojam dika bambaka n nu.

ref: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
text: Bu faari Pɔɔlu kɔ juuri bimbindi bo bu tiɛntiɛn su fun na bu wura gisan nu ni, na i boori i bu ka i fun kɔ sɔɔ n aŋgoro ni, sojam n kukuuri i wɔ, dama jama n biɛkpa n dɛɛ ti.

ref: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
text: Na bu suŋmati su i su, sutiɛn kpa, bu ŋa, “Am kun i, am kun i.” Kabo Pɔɔlu kaanri i jɔrɛ n kereeri jama n ni

ref: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
text: Saŋga bo bu sukoro ka bu fa Pɔɔlu wura sojam dika bambaka n nu ni, na Pɔɔlu ase sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ka, “Ma m kan jɔrɛ be na a ti.” Na sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n abisa i ka, “A ti Giriki aniɛ?

ref: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, “Ai, mini, m ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, m ti miɛbaa a, m fite Tarasu wɔ, miɛ kpiri n bo i la duma na i wo Silisiya awɔrɛ n nu ni. Jande, ma m jɔjɔ minɛm ni dɔ.”

ref: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
text: Na sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ama i atin ka i jɔjɔ, na Pɔɔlu ajina bimbindi n bo bu tiɛntiɛn su fun na bu wura gisan nu ni, na wɔ fa i saa ayo minɛm n ka bu yaki naŋmiɛ. Na ba tɔ amuna, na Pɔɔlu ajɔjɔ bu dɔ Hiburu aniɛ nu.

ref: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
text: I ŋa, “M sim ni m ninbaam, am ti fɛn, m koro ka m kan m jɔrɛ n na am ti.”

ref: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
text: Saŋga bo bu tiiri ka i sujɔjɔ bu dɔ Hiburu aniɛ nu ni, na ba yaki naŋmiɛ na ba mɔ bu nɔɔ surum. Na Pɔɔlu ase ka,

ref: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
text: “M ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, bu wuuri m Tarasu miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ bo i wo Silisiya awɔrɛ n nu ni, ama, bu taari m Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu fɛn wɔ, Gamɛli kereeri m kara a, ye m sɔnsɔɔnri ya nam sa­yoo­wa atin n kɛrɛ ni anyum­boro. Na ma fa m ŋu kɛrɛ ama Nyɛmɛ, kabo ambɛrɛ bo am jina fɛn nyuma n afa am ŋu ama i ni.

ref: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
text: Na ma kere minɛm bo bu su ahin atin n su n wuwuruwa, haari na ma kun bu nbem. Na ma tara bu nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram anyi bu dan­saraka.

ref: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
text: Na Pɔɔlu asa su ase bu ka, “Saŋga bo m wo kɔ su n na m kɔ dodoori Damasiko miɛ kpiri n ni, wiɛ kekete saŋga lɛɛ, na sin be akaba afite aŋgoro ata abara ayɛ m.

ref: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Na ma kpaasi haari asiɛŋgu, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga, i ŋa, ‘Sɔɔlu, Sɔɔlu e, nzu ti ye a kere m wuwuruwa sɔ a?’

ref: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
text: Na ma bisa i ka, ‘M Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ ŋma a?’ Na wɔ bɔ m nu ka, ‘Mini Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n a, mini ye a kere m wuwuruwa n a.’

ref: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
text: Na minɛm bo bu su m su n aŋu sin ni, ama, ba ti má akonvi n yiri ŋga.

ref: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
text: Na ma bisa i ka, ‘M Mibiɛ, ye a ŋa m yo nzu wɔ?’ Na wɔ se m ka, ‘Yasu na a kɔ Damasiko, dɔ ye bu sé u deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka a yo i ni.’

ref: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
text: Sin n bo i ta wuraari m nyu­mbaa nu agaya n dɛɛ ti, na ma yo anyinsin, na minɛm ni bo bu su m su n aso m saa nu afa m awura Damasiko.

ref: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
text: Tɔ, sɔnɔ be woori miɛ n nu, bu fere i ka Ananiyasi, i su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, na i su i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, i ti sɔnɔ n bo Yuda­fɔm bo bu wo miɛ n nu n kɛrɛ ma i jirima agaya a.

ref: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
text: Na wɔ ba m dɔ ajina adodo m, na wɔ se m ka, ‘M niwa Sɔɔlu, niɛ, a ŋú asi.’ Jina n nu bɛrɛ na ma ŋu asi bekun, na ma niɛ aŋu i.

ref: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, ‘Nyɛmɛ n bo ya nam su i ni, yiri ye wɔ yi u a, na a si i sa koroo­wam, ye wɔ yi Sɔnɔ n bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n akere u, na a ti yiri ni i tii nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa.

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
text: Dama deke bo a ŋu i na a ti i ni, wɔrɔ kán kere minɛm kɛrɛ.

ref: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
text: na ma ŋu Yesu ka i sujɔjɔ m dɔ, i ŋa, ‘Yasu na a fite Yɛrusalem ndende, dama minɛm bo bu wo fɛn ni dé má m jɔrɛ n bo a kan kere bu ni.’

ref: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
