text: Sasu bekun, saŋga bo bu sukun Satɛfano bo i diiri u ŋusu daani kereeri minɛm ni, m yo jinaari bɛrɛ, na ma soro su, mini niɛɛri minɛm bo bu kuunri i n suturam su a.’

ref: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
text: Na Mibiɛ n ase m ka, ‘Wɔrɔ kɔ, m súnma u dede minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni dɔ.’”

ref: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
text: Na bu sutiɛntiɛn, yaa ati bu n dɛɛ ti, na bu sufikindi bu suturam tu, na bu susa fa sutu sandi aŋgoro.

ref: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
text: Na Roma­fɔm soja kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ama nɔɔ ka bu fa Pɔɔlu wura bu dika bambaka n nu, na bu bisa i bisaa­wam na bu fin i ŋgbere na bu si deke bo i ti na Yuda­fɔm ni tiɛntiɛn sɔ ni.

ref: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
text: Ama, saŋga bo bu ciciiri Pɔɔlu nyama n ka bu bo i ni, na wɔ bisa soja kpiɛn n bo i jina dodo i ka, “Atin wo nu ka am bo Roma miɛbaa na am asi má satiɛ bo wɔ yo i?”

ref: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
text: Saŋga bo soja kpiɛn n tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na wɔ kɔ sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n dɔ, akɔ abisa i ka, “A koro ka a yo ahin sɔnɔ n sɛ wɔ? I ti Roma miɛbaa a.”

ref: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
text: Nyini ti, na sojam kpiɛn n akɔ Pɔɔlu dɔ, akɔ abisa i ka, “A ti Roma miɛbaa a, nahɔrɛ su?” Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, “Ɛɛn.”

ref: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
text: Na sojam kpiɛn n ase i ka, “Mini, m tɔɔri ŋwaa beberebe wɔ ye m nya kaakiiri Roma miɛbaa a.” Na Pɔɔlu ase i ka, “Ama, mini yiri, bu wuuri m miɛbaa a.”

ref: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
text: I seeri sɔ ni, kpeŋga na sɛrɛ ati sojam bo ba yi bu ka bu bisa i bisaa­wa ni, na ba kɔ bu sin. Na sɛrɛ ati soja kpiɛn ni mmɔɔ, dama wɔ du mɔ abo Pɔɔlu jɔrɔkɔ ka, ye i nya siiri ka i ti Roma miɛbaa a. Kabo bu faari Pɔɔlu kɔɔri Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm dɔ ni

ref: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.

ref: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”

ref: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu ase i ka, “Nyɛmɛ bó wɔrɔ mmɔɔ, wɔrɔ bo a tana dɔ ka kobe dawa bo ba bo i ŋgoro fofoe ni. A tana na a di m jɔrɛ, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ atin su, ama, wɔrɔ ni u tii asu má i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su na a tana ma nɔɔ ka bu bo m.”

ref: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
text: Na minɛm bo bu jina dodo Pɔɔlu n ase i ka, “Wɔrɔ la ŋma na a kpiɛ i nzukɔ a, yiri Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn ni?”

ref: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ bu nu ka, “M ninbaam, ma si má ka yiri ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n a. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, ‘Má a jɔjɔ sɔnɔ bo i niɛ u minɛm su jɔrɛ tiɛ.’”

ref: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
text: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu faari nu ka, minɛm ni nbem ti Sadusi­fɔm, na bu nbem gusu ti Farasi­fɔm ni, na wɔ tiɛn kpa jama n nu, i ŋa, “M ninbaam, m ti Farasi­fɔ ɔ. M sim mmɔɔ suuri nyini atin ni su a. Deke n bo i ti na ya tiɛn ya nɔɔ n la ka, m la tama ka minɛm wu, bu téŋge bu yiwee nu wɔ.”

ref: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
text: Pɔɔlu seeri sɔ wieeri na akuruwa aba Sadusi­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm n afiɛn, na bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ n akpaaki nu nnyɔ.

ref: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
text: Sadusi­fɔm n ŋa, sɔnɔ wu, i téŋge má i yiwee nu kun, ye bu kaaki ŋa, Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm, wara awiɛwiɛm wo má bɛrɛ. Ama, Farasi­fɔm bɛrɛ yo nyinim n kɛrɛ yarada a.

ref: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”

ref: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu niwa bara be wa ati sɛsɛ­su n bo ba fa i ni, na wɔ kɔ awura sojam sɔɔ bambaka n nu, na wɔ se Pɔɔlu.

text: Na am nya kpɔŋgɔ ma Pɔɔlu gusu, na am fa i haari na am kɔ ju Gomina Felika dɔ, na má pui yo i.”

ref: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
text: Na i alecin fɔfɔrɛ na sojam bo bu nati bu jaa su n akpie bu nyi akɔ sojam sɔɔ bambaka n nu, na ba ma soja kpɔŋgɔ­fun­fɔm n afa i akɔ aju deke bo bu sukɔ ni.

ref: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
text: Bu kɔ juuri Sizariya ni, na ba fa kadasi n ama gomina ni, na ba nya afa Pɔɔlu awura i saa nu.

ref: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.

ref: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
text: Na Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ ajina su ka, jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo bu jɔjɔ i n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Kabo Pɔɔlu kaanri i nahɔrɛ Gomina Felika n nyunu ni

text: Bɛrɛ gusu koro kan kere má u deke n bo i ti na kisa bu sujaraki m ni.

ref: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
text: Ama, deke bo m soro i su ka i ti nahɔrɛ n la ka, m su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, kabo ya nam su i ni, nyini atin n gusu ye bu ŋa i ti má nahɔrɛ n a. Ama, m yo deke bo ba kɛrɛ i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu n yarada, na m kaaki yo deke bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n nu n yarada.

ref: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ m boro m nyi wɔ, na m sunsunni ti casi fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ ni adamandem dɔ.

ref: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
text: M fiteeri Yɛrusalem, wɔ cɛ, nyini sin, na ma kpie m nyi akɔ dɔ ka m kɔ fa ŋwaa ce m minɛm bo bu ti yaari­fɔm n na m yo m dɛɛ yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ.

ref: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
text: M wo nyini yoo­wa n yo su ye bu ŋuuri m Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu a. Nyini saŋga ni, ma yo fin biɛ yoo­wa n awie mmɔɔ. Jama be awo má m dɔ, be gusu ayo má naŋmiɛ be.

ref: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
text: Ama, Yuda­fɔm nbem bo bu fite Esiya awɔrɛ n nu ni, yo woori dɔ a. Wɔ yo daka ka má bɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni bu tii wo fɛn, na bu kan m ŋgasi bo ma do i n kere u.

ref: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
text: Nzɛn má nyini, yaki na ahin minɛm bo bu jina fɛn n kan ŋgasi bo m doori, saŋga bo m jinaari bu tere­tere­fɔm nyunu na bu diiri m jɔrɛ ni.

ref: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
text: Má ka deke kun n kpein be bo bu yo sudi m jɔrɛ na m jinaari bu nyunu ni. Yiri la bo m seeri ka minɛm wu, bu káaki teŋge bu yiwee nu ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye a sudi m jɔrɛ nyuma n a.”

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
text: Na Gomina Felika si Mibiɛ n atin n sereya n kɛrɛ nyunyumi n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ se bu ka, “Am kɔ ka, na sojam kpiɛn Lisiyasi ba baari, na m nyá di jɔrɛ ni.”

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka soja kpiɛn n niɛ Pɔɔlu dika nyunyumi, ama, i ma i atin na i nati i koroya­nu, na i ma i danfom gusu atin na bu koro ba buka i. Kabo bu faari Pɔɔlu jinaari gomina n ni i yi n nyunu ni

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: I yoori le­nnyɔ, na Gomina Felika akaaki aba, i ni i yi Durusila, i ti Yuda­fɔ baa a. Na wɔ sunma afere Pɔɔlu na i ba kan kabo sɔnɔ yo Yesu Kristo yarada barasu ni. Na wɔ so i sui ati.

ref: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
text: Ama, saŋga bo Pɔɔlu wo jɔjɔ su fa kɔ sa bo i kɔ atin su, ni tara­buŋu, ni kabo Nyɛmɛ bá di minɛm n jɔrɛ ni, na sɛrɛ ati Gomina Felika na wɔ se ka, “Tɔ, kisa, wɔ sɛ su. A koro kɔ, na m ba nya saŋga na m fére u.”

ref: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
text: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ ni, i sunsunni la ka Pɔɔlu má i ŋwaa a, nyini ti ye saŋga saŋga i fere Pɔɔlu, na bu subo anzama n a.

ref: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
text: Na afɔ nnyɔ aba asin, na sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Pɔkiyɔsi Fɛsitosi ade Felika jinaa­biri ni. Na Gomina Felika akoro ka i jɔ Yuda­fɔm n ahore, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yaki Pɔɔlu dan­saraka n nu, i ma ba yi má i.

ref: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
text: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n aba akan ŋgasi bo Pɔɔlu ado i n akere Gomina Fɛsitosi. Na ba sere i jande

ref: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
text: ka i boro i nyi na i ma bu fa Pɔɔlu ba Yɛrusalem. Deke n bo i ti na bu seeri sɔ n la ka, ba sɛsɛ su ka bu kɔ ŋminda i atin nu wɔ na bu kun i.

ref: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
text: Na Gomina Fɛsitosi abɔ bu nu ka, “Pɔɔlu wo dan­saraka nu Sizariya, mini ni m tii, m kpie m nyi kɔ dɔ kisa.

ref: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
text: Am ma am nu nbem bo bu la yiko n su m su, na ya kɔ Sizariya, na nzɛn wɔ do ŋgasi be a, na bu koro kan i na bu jaraki i.”

ref: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
text: Na Gomina Fɛsitosi awo bu dɔ, le­mɔcuɛ ni le­buru afiɛn, na wɔ nya aba Sizariya. I alecin na wɔ kɔ atana jɔrɛ dii­biri ni, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu fa Pɔɔlu bra.

ref: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
text: Saŋga bo bu faari Pɔɔlu ba juuri n ni, na Yuda­fɔm bo bu fiteeri Yɛrusalem baari n atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i. Na ba kan i ŋgasi tiɛtiɛ beberebe bo bu ŋa wɔ do i, bu gusu akoro akere má ŋgasi n bo bu ŋa wɔ do i n tenle su.

ref: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu ase ka, “Ma do má ŋgasi be fa kɔ Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, ma do má ŋgasi be fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n su, ma kaaki ado má ŋgasi be fa kɔ Roma­fɔm Fɛmɛ Kpiri Siza n dɔ.”

ref: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
text: Ama, na Gomina Fɛsitosi akoro ka i buka Yuda­fɔm ni, na i da bu ahore, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ bisa Pɔɔlu ka, “A soro su ka ya kɔ Yɛrusalem na bu kɔ di u jɔrɛ n dɔ m nyunu, fa kɔ ahin sereyam kɛrɛ su?”

ref: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, “M jina Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n jɔrɛ dii­biri wɔ lɛɛ, fɛn ye wɔ yo daka ka bu di m jɔrɛ a. Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii si tenle su ka ma do má Yuda­fɔm ŋgasi be.

ref: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
text: Nzɛn ma do ŋgasi be bo wɔ yo daka ka bu kun m, ma kete má ka má bu kun m. Ama, nzɛn jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo ba kan i n be ti má nahɔrɛ, be la má atin bo i koro fa m wura bu saa nu. Nyini ti, m sere ka a fa m jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ.”

ref: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
text: Na Gomina Fɛsitosi ni i nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n atana aŋu bu ŋu, na ba soro su, na wɔ se Pɔɔlu ka, “A sere ka ya fa u jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ. Tɔ, ya fá u kɔ i dɔ.” Kabo bu faari Pɔɔlu baari Fɛmɛ Agiripa n nyunu ni

ref: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
text: Na wɔ yo le­nnyɔ, na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ni i siwa baa­bara Bɛrɛnisi aba Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu, ka bu ba niɛ Gomina Fɛsitosi busu.

ref: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
text: Bu woori dɔ cɛɛri kaan n dɛɛ ti, na Gomina Fɛsitosi akan Pɔɔlu jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ n akere fɛmɛ ni. I ŋa, “Felika yo yakiiri sɔnɔ be dan­saraka n nu fɛn.

ref: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
text: Ama, na ma se bu ka yɛrɛ Roma­fɔm, ya yo má sɔ, nzɛn minɛm ajaraki sɔnɔ, ya ma bu kɛrɛ tiɛn bu nɔɔ ɔ, na i mi si deke n bo i ti na bu jaraki i ni, na i gusu nya kan i dɛɛ bo i la i ni.

ref: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
text: Saŋga bo bu suuri m su ba juuri fɛn ni, ma wura má nnyɔ nu, i alecin m kaba kɔ tanaari jɔrɛ dii­biri n wɔ, na ma ma nɔɔ ka bu fa sɔnɔ n bra.

ref: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
text: Saŋga bo bu yasu jinaari na bu jaraki i ni, ba kan má satiɛ bo má m sunsun ka wɔ yo i na bu fa jaraki i ni.

ref: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
text: Ama, i ti akuruwa ye bu seeri a, fa kɔ bu nyɛmɛsu atin su, ni sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Yesu n busu, bu ŋa wɔ wu, ama, Pɔɔlu ŋa i toko la ŋgɔɔ.

ref: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
text: Ma si má kabo m tara ahin jɔrɛ n tii ni i jaa barasu, na ma bisa Pɔɔlu ka, i koro ka ya fa i kɔ Yɛrusalem na bu kɔ di i jɔrɛ n dɔ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu asere ka, i koro ka i ka dan­saraka n nu haari i ba kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ. Nyini ti, na ma ma nɔɔ ka bu subita i haari m ba nya atin na m ma bu fa i kɔ fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ.”

ref: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
text: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Gomina Fɛsitosi ka, “M koro ka mini ni m tii ti ahin sɔnɔ n jɔrɛ ni.” Na Gomina Fɛsitosi ase i ka, “Ahunma a tí i jɔrɛ ni.”

ref: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
text: Tɔ, na i alecin, na Fɛmɛ Agiripa, ni Bɛrɛnisi afite ni bu fɛmɛya jirima bambaka, na bu ba wura tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ bambaka n nu, bu ni soja nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni miɛ n nu minɛ anyunuyi­fɔm. Na Gomina Fɛsitosi ama nɔɔ, na ba fa Pɔɔlu awura.

ref: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
text: Ama, m gusu afiiti i jɔrɛ n nu, ma ŋu má i ŋgasi bo wɔ do i na i ti ye ni ka i wu. Nyini ti, kabo yiri ni i tii asere ka m fa i jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ ni, m fá i kɔ dɔ ɔ.

ref: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
text: Ama, m la má deke be tenle su bo m kɛrɛ fa ma fɛmɛ kpiri ni, fa kɔ ahin n jɔrɛ n su. Nyini ti ye ma fa i aba wɔrɔ Fɛmɛ Agiripa dɔ a, adena m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ niɛ i jɔrɛ n nu, na m si deke bo m kɛrɛ kɔ ma fɛmɛ kpiri ni.

ref: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
text: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Pɔɔlu ka, “Atin wo nu ka wɔrɔ ni u tii jɔjɔ.” Na Pɔɔlu ayɛ i saa su, na wɔ bo i jɔrɛ n bu, i ŋa,

ref: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
text: “Fɛmɛ Agiripa, wɔ yo m fiɛ nyuma ka m jina u nyunu, na m kan m jɔrɛ n nahɔrɛ su fa kɔ deke n kɛrɛ bo Yuda­fɔm jaraki m n su.

ref: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
text: Dama wɔrɔ ni u tii asi kabo Yuda­fɔm nana sa­yoo­wam n ti, ni kabo ya se bu akuruwam barasu ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, m sere u wɔ, ka a fa sawɔrɛ na a ti m jɔrɛ ni.

ref: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
text: Bu si m sa kabo m woo­wa yo ti, má nyuma. Nzɛn bu ŋa bu kan nahɔrɛ, bu se u ka m yo ti Farasi­fɔm be wɔ, na ma su ya dɛɛ nyini atin n bo i su ti kekereke n su ni anyum­boro.

ref: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
text: Nyini n ye ya anɔ buru ni nnyɔ n bo ya su Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ n ŋminda ka ya nya i n a. Fɛmɛ, ti fɛn, nyini tama n bo m la i n dɛɛ ti, ye Yuda­fɔm jaraki m n a.

ref: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
text: Nzu ti ye am de má ka Nyɛmɛ koro teŋge minɛm bu yiwee nu a?

ref: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
text: Nahɔrɛ mini ni m tii yo sunsuunri ka wɔ yo daka ka m yo sam dɔŋgu na m saaki Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n duma.

ref: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
text: Kabo m yo yoori Yɛrusalem lɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm yo maari m yiko, na ma tara Nyɛmɛ minɛm beberebe anyi bu dan­saraka, na saŋga bo bu diiri bu jɔrɛ na bu ŋa bu kun bu ni, mini mmɔɔ jina bu sin wɔ.

ref: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga, m nati wura ya tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu wɔ, na m sucin bu sui, na m sumiɛ bu ka bu kete Yesu. Na ma fa yaa bu ŋu agaya, haari na m sukɔ miɛ nyiŋgam su, kɔ sukere bu wuwuruwa.” Kabo Pɔɔlu kaanri kabo i kaakiiri i wo ni

ref: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
text: Na Pɔɔlu asa su ase ka, “Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ma sere atin Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n dɔ, na ba ma m yiko ka m kɔ Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
text: Na yɛrɛ kɛrɛ akpaasi haari asiɛŋgu, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga, ka i sujɔjɔ m dɔ Hiburu dɛɛ aniɛ n nu, i ŋa, ‘Sɔɔlu, Sɔɔlu e, nzu ti ye a kere m wuwuruwa sɔ a? A sukere u ŋu yaari ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, kabo bu fa toora dɔdɔɔri­fɔ sudɔ na i kete, na bu fin i baka n wɔ.’

ref: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Na ma bisa ka, ‘M Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ ŋma a?’ Na Mibiɛ n abɔ nu ka, ‘Mini Yesu ye a sukere m wuwuruwa n a.

ref: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
text: Ama, yasu jina. Deke n bo i ti na ma yi m ŋu akere u n la ka, m fa u yo m amusui­su­fɔ, na a di m daani kere minɛm fa kɔ deke bo a ŋu i nyuma n su, ni dekem bo m bá yi m ŋu kere u bekun n su.

ref: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
text: Na m dé u tii fite minɛm saa nu, Yuda­fɔm ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, dama m susunma u bɛrɛ dɔ a,

ref: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
text: na a teke bu nyi, na a yi bu fite awosin nu ba wein nu, na a yi bu fite Sitana saa nu, na a fa bu wura Nyɛmɛ saa nu, adena bu yo m yarada na m fa bu satiɛ ce bu, na bu nya jinaa­biri Nyɛmɛ minɛ n nu, na bu kɛrɛ saŋga wo.’” Kabo Pɔɔlu kaanri kabo i yoori i juma n ni

ref: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
text: Nyini ti, m du mɔ boori i bu Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ma kan akere bu ka bu kaaki bu wo na bu su Nyɛmɛ, na bu yo sakpa bo i kere ka ba kaaki bu wo. Na ma kɔ akan akere Yɛrusalem­fɔm n gusu, na ma nya akɔ akan akere Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu fɔm kɛrɛ. Nyini sin, na ma kɔ akan akere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n gusu.

ref: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Yuda­fɔm taraari m Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, ka bu kun m n a.

ref: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ abuka m dɛɛ ti, ye m toko wo bɛrɛ haari ni nyuma n a, adena m jina fɛn di daani kere nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam kɛrɛ, ni minɛ barasum kɛrɛ. M sukan má jɔrɛ fɔfɔrɛ be wɔ, má ka sam bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni Moyisi yo seeri ka bu yó ni,

ref: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
text: ka, wɔ yo daka ka Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni bá di wahara. Yiri ti sɔnɔ bo i wú na i dú mɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu a. Na i kán wein n jɔrɛ kere Yuda­fɔm, ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ.”

ref: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
text: Pɔɔlu wo nyini jɔjɔɔri n su na i kan i nahɔrɛ ni, na Gomina Fɛsitosi atiɛn i ŋu kpa, i ŋa, “Pɔɔlu, a bo fiɛ wɔ. U kara sɔnsɔn ni u si agaya n ma u tii yo má juma.”

ref: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
text: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, “Gomina Fɛsitosi jirima­fɔ, ma bo má fiɛ, ama, m jɔjɔ anyuŋkoro ni nahɔrɛ jɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
text: Fɛmɛ Agiripa si ahin jɔrɛm kɛrɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m la ahoresi jɔjɔ i nyunu n a. M si tenle su ka i si ahin sam n kɛrɛ bo m sukan kere am ni, dama i be mmɔɔ ayo má fiɛɛ­wa nu.

ref: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
text: Fɛmɛ Agiripa, a yo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm jɔrɛ n yarada a? M si tenle su ka a yo yarada.”

ref: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
text: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Pɔɔlu ka, “Ahin saŋga kaan nu, a sunsun ka a koro jɔjɔ na a ma m kaaki Kristo susu­fɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ?”

ref: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, “Nzɛn i cɛ o, i cɛ má o, mini yiri, m nyɛmɛsere la ka, wɔrɔ ni minɛm n kɛrɛ bo m sujɔjɔ am dɔ nyuma ni, am ba kaaki na am yo kabo m ti barasu ni, ama, bu wura má am jɔrɔkɔ kabo ba wura m ni.”

ref: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
text: I seeri sɔ ni, na fɛmɛ n ayasu ajina, yiri ni gomina ni, ni Bɛrɛnisi, ni nyiŋgam n kɛrɛ bo bu tana bɛrɛ ni.

ref: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
text: Bu yasuuri na bu wo kɔ su ni, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Ahin sɔnɔ n ayo má sa be bo i ti ye ni ka bu kun i, wara bu nyi i dan­saraka nu.”

ref: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
text: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Gomina Fɛsitosi ka, “Nzɛn má ahin sɔnɔ n asere má ka bu fa i jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ, má ya koro yaki i nu ji.”

ref: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
text: Na i alecin, na ya kɔ aju Sidon miɛ kpiri n nu, na Juliyosi ayo Pɔɔlu sakpa, i maari i atin wɔ, na wɔ kɔ aniɛ i danfom busu, adena i kɔ nya deke bo i koro.

ref: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
text: Ya yasuuri Sidon ka ya kɔ ni, ya faari Sipurusi lui bambaka n dɛɛ bue n wɔ, ya koro fa má deke bo má ya sukoro ka ya fa ni, dama aŋuma n cin ya kɔ ya sin wɔ.

ref: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
text: Dɔ na soja kpiɛn n aŋu lee bambaka be ka i fite Alɛkizanderiya miɛ kpiri n nu sukɔ Itali, na wɔ fa ya awura nu.

ref: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
text: Ya yasuuri bɛrɛ ka ya kɔ ni, aŋuma n kpa ya atin wɔ, ya koro akɔ má ndende. I yoori kekereke ye ya nya kɔ dodoori miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Kinidosi n a. Aŋuma n dɛɛ ti, ya koro akɔ má deke bo má ya sukɔ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ya fa lui bambaka n bo bu fere i ka Kereti dɛɛ bue ni, akpiɛ akɔ adodo miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Salamone ni.

ref: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
text: Ya faari bɛrɛ ni, i yoori ya kekereke ye ya nya kɔ juuri miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Dika Nyunyumi n a, lee ŋgbiŋgbirim jinaa­biri ni, i mata miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Lasiya n wɔ.

ref: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
text: Na ya wo dɔ acɛ, haari na Yuda­fɔm nɔɔ cici n aba asin, bu ŋa nyini saŋga ni, tieku n su natiiri ti tiɛ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Pɔɔlu abɔbɔ bu, i ŋa,

ref: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
text: “Njam, ma ŋu ka nzɛn ya ŋa ya kɔ ya nyunu, i yó kekereke, na deke dɔŋgu sáaki, má lee bambaka n ni neŋgem ŋgumi, ama, ya ŋgɔɔ mmɔɔ wo nu.”

ref: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
text: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, soja kpiɛn n keteeri Pɔɔlu nɔaniɛ n wɔ, na wɔ de lee­kan­fɔ n ni lee n fɔ n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
text: Deke bo ya wo ni, i ti má kpa ka ya wo dɔ haari fufuku ba tu ya, nyini dɛɛ ti, na ya dɔŋgu asoro su ka ya yasu dɔ, na ya niɛ, beni, ya koro kɔ ju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Foniki n su na i wo Kereti lui bambaka n su ni. Lee ŋgbiŋgbirim jinaa­biri wo dɔ ɔ, i nɔɔ wɔ wiɛ atɔɔri dɛɛ bue n wɔ, ya koro kɔ wo dɔ, haari na fufuku n ba sin. Kabo aŋuma bambaka be yasuuri tieku n nu ni

ref: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
text: Na aŋuma fafari be sukpiɛ fite ya nyunu ndiɛma biɛ su. Na ba sunsun ka bu koro kɔ deke bo ba fa bu tii awɔ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba cin atu bulaari n bo bu fa kpamaari lee ni, na ya san tieku n ahɛmɛ akɔ adodo Kereti lui bambaka n dɛɛ bue ni.

ref: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
text: Ya kpa aŋuma n atin wɔ, ya koro akɔ má deke bo ya fa ya tii awɔ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ya yaki lee bambaka ni, na wɔ su aŋuma n su.

ref: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
text: Na ya kɔ adodo lui be bo bu fere i ka Kawuda ni, na ya fa i sin. Lee kaan be sa lee bambaka n su, i yoori kekereke ye ya nya koro taraari i jinaari a.

ref: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
text: Na ba nya acin i afun awura lee bambaka n nu. Na ba fa nyama awura lee bambaka n bu, na ba cici i kenken, na i bu n ayo faŋga. Bu soro sɛrɛ ka lee ni kpama tɛtɛkɛ n dika wɔ, miɛ kaan be wo bɛrɛ, bu fere i ka Siritisi. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba jura lee tani n bo i bo aŋuma na lee n kɔ ni, na ba nyanya i, na lee n asu aŋuma n su.

ref: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
text: Kabo aŋuma n kereeri ya wahara sɔ ni, i alecin, na ba yiyi neŋgem bo bu wo lee n nu ni nbem ajiji nzue n nu.

ref: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
text: I le­san cɛɛn, na bu kɛrɛ afa bu saa ayi lee n dɛɛ neŋge kɛrɛ aji nzue n nu.

ref: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
text: Na wɔ cɛ bo ya ŋu má wiɛ, wara asara­mbaam, na aŋuma n sukpiɛ agaya sɔ, haari na ya ba ayi tama ka ya fite má nu.

ref: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
text: Tɔ, wɔ cɛ bo minɛm n adi má diire n dɛɛ ti, na Pɔɔlu ayasu ajina bu afiɛn, na wɔ se bu ka, “Njam, nzɛn má am tiiri m nɔaniɛ ni, má ya yasu má Kereti, má ya neŋgem ni saaki má, na bu ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ.

ref: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
text: Ama, kisa, m sutu am fɔ ɔ, am ma am ahoresi. Am be mmɔɔ ŋgɔɔ ká má nu, má ka lee bambaka ni ŋgumi saaki a.

ref: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
text: Dama anuma kɔŋguɛ, Nyɛmɛ n bo m su i na i dɛɛ la m ni, i mɛrɛkɛ be ba jinaari m bi,

ref: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
text: na wɔ se m ka, ‘Pɔɔlu, má a ma sɛrɛ ti u. Wɔ yo daka ka a kɔ́ jina Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n nyunu wɔ, na i dí u jɔrɛ. Ti fɛn, Nyɛmɛ afa minɛm kɛrɛ bo u ni bɛrɛ saŋga wo lee n nu n ama u.’

ref: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
text: Nyini ti, njam, am fa ahoresi. Dama m daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu ka i yó kabo i seeri m n wɔ.

ref: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
text: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, aŋuma ni sún ya kɔ jina lui bambaka be su wɔ.”

ref: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
text: Na ba nyaŋgi lee bambaka n bulaari nɔnɔɔrim n aji nzue n nu, na ba nyaŋgi bakam bo bu fa kan lee n dɛɛ nyamam ni, na ba yɛ tani n bo i wo lee n tii su n aŋgoro ka aŋuma n fa lee n kɔ, na bu sukan sukɔ ahɛmɛ.

ref: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
text: Na lee juma­yo­fɔm n akoro ka bu wɔ ŋmati, na ba yi lee kaan n afa ase nzue n su, na ba kpiɛ mbɛnlɛ ka bu suyi bulaari nɔnɔɔri nbem lee bambaka n tii dika a.

ref: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
text: Na sɛrɛ ati bu, bu sunsun ka lee bambaka ni kɔ́ si yabuɛm wɔ, na i sandi nu, nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba cɔɔndi lee n sin bulaari nɔnɔɔri nna asiɛŋgu. Na bu susere Nyɛmɛ ka ale cin bu.

ref: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ase soja kpiɛn n ni i sojam ni ka, “Má ka ahin minɛm n aka lee n nu, am be mmɔɔ fíte má nu.”

ref: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
text: Na sojam n akpiɛ nyamam bo bu bita lee kaan n aji, na wɔ kɔ.

ref: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
text: Nyini ti, m sere am wɔ, am di diire, adena i buka am na am nya ŋgɔɔ. Haari am be mmɔɔ tii­ŋmiɛ kun n kpein ká má ŋgbɛɛn.”

ref: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
text: Pɔɔlu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi bu kɛrɛ nyunu, na wɔ bu i nu, na wɔ di.

ref: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
text: Na bu kɛrɛ afa ahoresi, na bɛrɛ gusu adi diire.

ref: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
text: Bɛrɛ kɛrɛ diiri na bu kunnu yiiri ni, na ba sasa diire mbaa n bo wɔ ka n agɔ nzue n nu, adena lee bambaka n ka fafaki. Kabo lee bambaka n sandiiri nu barasu ni

ref: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
text: Saŋga bo ale ba ciinri ni, lee juma­yo­fɔm ŋuuri asiɛn be, ba si má i, ama, na ba ŋu ka nzue be ati ada bɛrɛ, ŋmiɛ abara ayɛ i. Na ba fa sunsunni ka nzɛn má bu koro yo, na ba boro bu nyi na bu fa lee bambaka n kɔ wura ŋmiɛ n nu na i kpama.

ref: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
text: Ama, na ba kɔ aju kunma kpiri saŋgaa­biri, na ba wura ŋmiɛ be nu, na lee bambaka n tii dika n aka akpama, wɔ koro akɔ má. Na nzue nyama ni subo lee ni, na wɔ paparaki i sin dika ni.

ref: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
text: Na sojam n asɛsɛ su ka bu kun dan­sarakam n kɛrɛ wɔ, adena má be wiɛ kpiɛ na i ŋmati kɔ.

ref: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
text: Ama, soja kpiɛn n koroori ka i de Pɔɔlu tii ji n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kete bu ŋu ka má bu yo sɔ. Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka, bo bu si nzue wiɛ n kɛrɛ, bu du mɔ ti to nu na bu wiɛ kpiɛ kpɛrɛ su,

ref: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
text: na minɛm bo bu kaari n kɛrɛ nya so baka sinsinm, ni lee buebuem n nu, na bu wiɛ kɔ ju ahɛmɛ. Kabo i yoori na ya kɛrɛ koro fiteeri nu, na pui ayo má be n lɛɛ.

ref: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
text: Saŋga bo ya fiteeri nzue n nu wieeri ni, na ba se ya ka bu fere lui bambaka n ka Malite.

ref: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
text: Na miɛ n nu­fɔm ade ya na ba yo ya sakpa agaya. Nzue sutɔ na ayɛrɛ wo bɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti, na ba tɔrɔ sin ama ya, na ya kɛrɛ atun.

ref: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
text: Na Pɔɔlu atiɛntiɛn yee nɔɔ agɔ sin n nu, jaa pumpuni wo yee n nu, na sin n waraŋgu awura i, na wɔ fite akan i saa aka asense su.

ref: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
text: Miɛ n nu­fɔm ni ŋuuri ka woo n akan asense Pɔɔlu saa su sɔ ni, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Ahin sɔnɔ n ti sɔnɔ kun­fɔ ɔ nahɔrɛ su, haari ni bo wɔ koro afite n nzue n nu ni, i nyɛmɛ atin su, wɔ yo má daka ka i nyá ŋgɔɔ.”

ref: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
text: Ama, na Pɔɔlu akpisi woo n aji sin n nu, wɔ ŋu má yaari be.

ref: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
text: Na minɛm n asunsun ka i cɛ́ má, i ŋuniɛ wúra a, wara i kába kpaasi wu. Ama, bu ŋminda cɛɛri na bu ŋu má ka deke be ayo i ni, na ba kaaki bu sunsunni, na ba se ka, “I ti nyɛmɛ be wɔ.”

ref: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
text: Deke bo ya sekeeri ni, fie be wo dodo dɔ, i fɔ n ti lui bambaka n su sɔnɔ anyumbaa­fɔ ɔ, bu fere i ka Pubiliyosi. Na wɔ de ya wɔfɔ nyunyumi, na ya ka ada i dika haari le­san.

ref: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
text: Nyini saŋga Pubiliyosi si gusu la má laifiɛ, i da sɔrɔ, i ŋuniɛ ado, na i suje tɔtɔkɔ. Na Pɔɔlu awura sɔɔ n bo i da nu ni, na wɔ fa i saa anana i ŋu, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ama i, na wɔ nya laifiɛ.

ref: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
text: I yoori sɔ n dɛɛ sin, na tukpaki­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo lui bambaka n su n gusu aba Pɔɔlu dɔ, na wɔ tuntun bu, na ba nya laifiɛ.

ref: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
text: Ya woori dɔ haari sara nsan, na ya nya fa lee bambaka be bo i fite Alɛkizanderiya dɛɛ miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ. Amɔɛ ndaam anyaari see­wa mata i. Fufuku saŋga n dɛɛ ti, wɔ jina dɔ acɛ.

ref: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
text: Na ya ŋu yarada­yo­fɔ nbem dɔ, na ba sere ya ka ya wo bu dɔ le­so. Na ya nya yasu aba Roma miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
text: Saŋga bo ya kɔ juuri Roma miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na ba ma Pɔɔlu atin ka i nya awuru be na yiri ni i tii suwo nu, na ba ma i soja kun ka i suniɛ i dika.

ref: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
text: I woori dɔ i le­san, nyini sin, na Pɔɔlu afere Yuda­fɔm bo bu la anyunuyi na bu wo miɛ n nu ni, ka bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ. Bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ se bu ka, “M ninbaam, ma yo má ya minɛm n sa be, ma bu má ya nana sa­yoo­wa n be ŋgbɛɛn, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, bu taraari m Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ba nya afa m awura Roma­fɔm saa nu.

ref: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
text: Bɛrɛ gusu bisaari m bisaa­wam wieeri ni, má bu ŋa bu fa m ji wɔ, dama ba ŋu má satiɛ bo ma yo i na i ti ye ni ka bu kun m.

ref: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
text: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm n akete ka má Roma­fɔm n yaki m nu ji, na wɔ yo daka ka m sere ka bu fa m jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ, haari ni bo m la má jɔrɛ be bo m fa jaraki m tiɛŋgu minɛm ni.

ref: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m fere am a, na m jɔjɔ am dɔ na am si bo i wo bɛrɛ, ka, sɔnɔ n bo Yisareli­fɔm yo i tama n dɛɛ ti, ye m wo ahin jɔrɔkɔ n nu a.”

ref: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
text: Na ba se ka, “Ya nya de má kadasi be Yudiya­fɔm dɔ fa kɔ u jɔrɛ su. Ya minɛm bo bu fiteeri dɔ baari n gusu, be nya kan má, wara i jɔjɔ jɔrɛ tiɛ be fa kɔ u dɔ.

ref: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
text: Ama, ya koro ka ya ti fite u dɔ kabo u sunsunni ti, dama ya si ka nyini atin n bo a bita i ni, deke kɛrɛ bo a kɔ, minɛ jaraki i wɔ.”

ref: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
text: Na ba kpama cɛɛn ka bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ. Nyini cɛɛn n na minɛ beberebe aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ deke bo Pɔɔlu da ni. Na wɔ kan akere bu kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n atin ti barasu. Fite ŋgarama haari kɔŋguɛ, na wɔ boro i nyi na i ma bu si kabo Yesu ti barasu, fite kabo ba kɛrɛ i Moyisi dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kadasi n nu ni, ni kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n nu ni.

ref: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
text: Na bu nbem ade i nɔaniɛ ni, ama, na nbem akete, ba yo má yarada.

ref: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
text: Nyini ti, bu nɔɔ ayo má kun, na ba yasu ka bu kɔ awuru, na Pɔɔlu ase bu jɔrɛ kun be abuka su, i ŋa, “Nahɔrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔjɔɔri am jɔrɛ n a. I yo maari Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya seeri am nam ka,

ref: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
text: ‘Kɔ na a kɔ se nyini minɛm ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, Am só am sui ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, am tí má deke bo am so am sui ka am ti i n bu. Am káaki niɛ ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, am ŋú má pui.

ref: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
text: Dama ahin minɛm tii ti kekereke, ba tɔ bu sui na má bu ti, ba kata bu nyu­mbaa na má bu ŋu. Má nyini, bu nyu­mbaa ŋú i wɔ, na bu sui tí, na bu sí bu ahore su, na bu káaki bu wo, na m má bu nya laifiɛ.’”

ref: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
text: Na Pɔɔlu kaanri sɔ wieeri ni, na Yuda­fɔm n ayasu afite akɔ ase akuruwa nyannyan bu ŋuŋu nu.]

ref: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
text: Na Pɔɔlu ade sɔɔ, na i sutɔ kaari, na wɔ wo nu haari afɔ nnyɔ, na i sude minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ba i dɔ ni,

ref: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
text: na i sukan Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ŋusu jɔrɛ, na i sukere minɛm ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo jɔrɛ ni ahoresi, be akanda má i.

ref: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
text: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n fa ma am a. M ti Yesu Kristo akɔɔ ɔ, ye wɔ fere m ka m kaaki i jaramasa, na wɔ yi m ka m kan labari kpa n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n jɔrɛ.

ref: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
text: Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ faari i yiko teŋgeeri i yiwee nu ni, nyini ma Awiɛwiɛ Casi n kere ka i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a, yiri la ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ni.

ref: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Yiri dɛɛ ti, ma di Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma, ye wɔ ma ma yo i jaramasa, adena i duma n dɛɛ ti, na m ma daanfiɛ barasu kɛrɛ de i nɔaniɛ ni, na bu yo i yarada.

ref: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ bo am wo Roma miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ye m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma am a. Ambɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro am na wɔ fere am na am ayo i dɛɛ minɛm ni, ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ma am wura bu ahɔrɔma nu na am nya ahoreda. Kabo má Pɔɔlu koroori ka i kɔ niɛ Roma­fɔm busu ni

ref: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
text: Ciciri su, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, m da m Nyɛmɛ asi ma am kɛrɛ, dama durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ati kabo am ayo Yesu Kristo yarada ni.

ref: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
text: Nyɛmɛ bo m su i ni m ahore­mbaa kun ni, na m kan i Wa n labari kpa n jɔrɛ ni, yiri di m daani a, kabo m nati sere Nyɛmɛ ma am ni.

ref: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ m sere i wɔ, ka nzɛn i ti i dɛɛ koroya­nu, i teke atin ma m kisa, na i awieeri su na m koro ba niɛ am busu.

ref: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
text: Dama m nyunu aboro ka m bá ŋu am wɔ, adena m fa aceeri n bo i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ ba ma am na am koro jina faŋga kenken ni nyɛmɛsu ni.

ref: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
text: Nahɔrɛ, i ti deke bo ya fa ma ya beŋgu na ya nya ahoresi wɔ, m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ, kabo ya yo Yesu yarada n dɛɛ ti.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
text: M ninbaam, m koro ka am si ka saŋga kɛrɛ, má m koro ka m bá niɛ am busu wɔ, ama, m nya má atin. Má m koro ka m ba nya minɛ am nu ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, kabo ma nya am beŋgu nbem bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

ref: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m ahore abi ka m ba kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere ambɛrɛ gusu bo am wo Roma miɛ kpiri n nu n a. Yiko bo i wo labari kpa n nu ni

ref: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Nyinsɛ ti má m fa kɔ labari kpa n su, dama yiri ti Nyɛmɛ yiko bo i ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yarada n nya tii­de a, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni du mɔ a, na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n nya tɔ su.

ref: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
text: Nyɛmɛ wo aŋgoro akere kabo wɔ fa yaa acin minɛm sui ni, bɛrɛ bo bu sunsunni ti tiɛ na bu yo satiɛ ni, bu satiɛ n dɛɛ ti, ba kete ka má minɛm si Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su.

ref: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
text: Deke bo i ti na wɔ fa yaa n la ka, ba ŋu deke n kɛrɛ bo i ma bu si Nyɛmɛ ni, dama yiri ni i tii ayi i kpain afite fofoe nu.

ref: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
text: Haari ni bo bu si i n kɛrɛ, ba kete ka bu ma má i jirima ka i ti Nyɛmɛ, na bu kaaki da má i asi. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bu sunsunni kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn ye ŋgɔnlɛ akpiɛ bu su, ye bu ahore­mbaa ayo biri n a.

ref: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
text: Bu bu bu ŋu ka bu ti ŋgɔnlɛ­fɔm wɔ, ama, bu ti sinnzinm wɔ.

ref: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
text: Jirima bo má bu fa ma Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, ba fa ama amɔɛm bo ba yo bu na bu ferema ni adamandem, ni laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm, ni ajaa­nnam, ni cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm wɔ.

ref: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ayaki bu ye bu yo bu kunnu koroo­wa n a, na bu suyo daŋgba sam bu ŋuŋu nu suwura bu ŋu nyinsɛ.

ref: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
text: Dama ba kete Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ­fɔ ni, na bu nya susu kɔrɛ su. Na bu su dekem bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ni, bu su má yiri bo i yiiri deke n kɛrɛ ni. Yiri dɛɛ la kansi saŋga kɛrɛ a. Ami.

ref: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
text: Ba kete Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ yaki bu a, na bu suyo nyinsɛ sam kabo bu kunnu koro n a, haari na nbaram yaki nbiɛsɔm dɔ daari, na bu sufa bu beŋgu nbaram da, na bu yo kabo má wɔ yo má daka ka bu yo ni.

ref: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
text: Na nbiɛsɔm n gusu yaki nbaram dɔ daari, na bu la konviki agaya fa kɔ bu beŋgu nbiɛsɔm dɔ, na bu ni bɛrɛ saŋga da ka bara ni biɛsɔ dɛɛ su. Nyini nyinsɛ sam dɛɛ ti, ye ba nya sui­cin kabo wɔ yo daka ka bu nya i n a.

ref: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
text: Kabo ba kete ka bu si má Nyɛmɛ ni, nyini ma wɔ yaki bu, ye bu sunsunni tiɛ n ma bu yo sa bo má wɔ yo má daka ka bu yo i n a.

ref: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
text: Bu ahore­mbaa ayi ni satiɛ barasu kɛrɛ ŋgumi wɔ, bu konviki wura bu beŋgu neŋgem nu wɔ, na bu suyo bu beŋgum tiɛ, na bu susi bu beŋgum kɔrɛ, na bu sukun bu beŋgum, na bu sudi aluwa, na bu sudadaka bu beŋgum, na bu sukpɔ bu beŋgum, na bu sukan bu beŋgum jɔrɛ,

ref: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
text: na bu susaaki bu beŋgum duma, na bu sukpɔ Nyɛmɛ, na bu sukpiɛ bu beŋgum nzukɔ, na bu la karambani, na bu sufɔ, na bu susunsun kabo bu yo na bu yo satiɛm, na bu sukete bu wu­fɔm nɔaniɛ,

ref: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
text: Bu si kabo Nyɛmɛ akere ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nati yo sɔ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi wú ni, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, bu toko yo nyini sam wɔ, ama, bo i kɔ tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, bu jina minɛ bo bu gusu nati yo nyini sam ni sin wɔ.

ref: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
text: Tɔ, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a kan nyini minɛm jɔrɛ ni, a la má kabo a yo na a kan fite nu Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Dama jɔrɛ n bo a kan jaraki u beŋgu na wɔrɔ ni u tii yo nyini sam ni, nyini ma a kan jaraki u ŋu n a.

ref: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
text: Ya si kabo Nyɛmɛ di minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu yo sɔ n jɔrɛ, na i jaraki bu ni, i jɔrɛ dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ ayo sakpa bambaka ama u na i la mindi ni sawɔrɛ ni, nzu ti ye a bu i ŋgbɛɛn a? A si má ka deke bo i ti na i yoori nyini sakpa n la ka, i koro ka a kaaki u wo wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
text: Ama, kabo a yo u ahore­mbaa kekereke na a kete ka a kaaki má u wo ni, a sukpini sui­cin ma u ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ bá di minɛm jɔrɛ nahɔrɛ su, na i cín bu sui na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋú i ni.

ref: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
text: Na i tɔ́ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋkatɔ kabo i sa­yoo­wa ti barasu.

ref: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
text: Minɛ nbem dɛɛ la ka, bu mindi nati yo sakpa, na bu kpini kansi, ni jirima, ni aŋuniɛ­mbaa bo i kpɔrɔ má n busu ni, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ má bu ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.

ref: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
text: Ama, minɛm bo bu nati yo anyii sam, na bu kete deke kɛrɛ bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na bu su satiɛ atin su ni, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ fá yaa bu ŋu na i cín bu sui n a.

ref: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu nati yo satiɛ ni, bu dí wahara na bu ŋú anyum­boro, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni dú mɔ ŋu nyini kekereke n a, na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm gusu nyá tɔ su.

ref: God doesn't have any favorites!
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ tambu má nu.

ref: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
text: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu la má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n na bu yoori satiɛ, bu ká ŋgbɛɛn, haari ni bo bu la má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ni. Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu la Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n na bu yoori satiɛ, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ fá i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n di bu jɔrɛ, na i bó bu asi a.

ref: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am ŋa am ti Yuda­fɔm, na am daafi Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ŋu, na am fa fɔ ka am si Nyɛmɛ ni,

ref: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
text: na am si kabo Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa ti, na am sunsun nyunyumi, na am yi sa bo i ti kpa, dama am asi Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ awie,

ref: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
text: am yo am ŋu ka ambɛrɛ bita anyinsinm kpama a, na am ti wein ma minɛm bo bu wo awosin nu ni.

ref: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am kere minɛm ni, nzu ti ye am kere má am ŋu a? Ye ambɛrɛ bo am se minɛm ka má bu wɔ ni, am wɔ, nzɛn am wɔ má?

ref: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
text: Am ŋa má be saaki ajaa ciire ni, ye am saaki i, nzɛn am saaki má i? Am ŋa am koro má amɔɛ jɔrɛ ni, ye am wɔ neŋgem amɔɛ sɔɔm n nu, nzɛn am wɔ má?

ref: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am bita Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n fa fɔ ka am si i, na am su má i su ni, ambɛrɛ kaaki wura Nyɛmɛ nyinsɛ a.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
text: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ ti, ye minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu a.”

ref: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
text: Nzɛn am yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka am yo ni, nyini yiri, am kɛnkɛnnɛ n bo am awura i n la kpɛrɛ. Ama, nzɛn am yo má kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ni, i ti ka am awura má kɛnkɛnnɛ lɛɛ.

ref: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
text: Nzɛn bo ba wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ n yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka bu yo ni, Nyɛmɛ de bu ka ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
text: Nyini ti, i sukere ka, bɛrɛ n bo ba wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ na bu yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ni, bu dí ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm jɔrɛ jaraki am, dama am awura kɛnkɛnnɛ na am la nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, ama, am yo má kabo i kere ni.

ref: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
text: Dama má Yuda­fɔm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa su ni ŋgumi ti Yuda­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

ref: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i ti Yuda­fɔ nahɔrɛ su la bo wɔ kaaki wo i ahore­mbaa su ni, na kɛnkɛnnɛ wuraa­wa nahɔrɛ su fite ahore­mbaa su wɔ, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni kaaki sɔnɔ a, má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni. Nyini sɔnɔ sɔ, Nyɛmɛ kansi i a, má adamande.

ref: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
text: Tɔ, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, bu la nzu kpɛrɛ wɔ? Ye kɛnkɛnnɛ wura buka nzu wɔ?

ref: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
text: Nahɔrɛ, Yuda­fɔm tara minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni deke dɔŋgu nu. Ciciri su, Nyɛmɛ du mɔ faari i nɔaniɛm n wuraari bɛrɛ saa nu a, ka bu niɛ i su.

ref: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
text: Ye nzɛn Nyɛmɛ fa wuraari bɛrɛ saa nu a, ye bu nbem ayo má yarada, nyini kere ka Nyɛmɛ la má dabuŋu lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
text: Ai, má sɔ. Haari ni bo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti kɔrɛ­fɔm ni, Nyɛmɛ yiri ti i nahɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Deke bo a kan ni, bu ŋú ka nyini ti nahɔrɛ a, na nzɛn bu di u jɔrɛ jarakiiri u, a kúnma bu.”

ref: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
text: Be koro se ka nzɛn m bu kɔrɛ, na i ma minɛm ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ ti barasu, adena bu ma i jirima, ye nzu ti ye i di m jɔrɛ cin m sui ka m ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ a?

ref: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
text: I ti kabo minɛ nbem nati kan wɔ, bu ŋa, am ma ya yo satiɛ adena Nyɛmɛ ba ma i kaaki sakpa. Bu kpiɛ m nzukɔ ka mini mmɔɔ se sɔ ɔ. Bu bu kɔrɛ wɔ. Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ na i cín bu sui. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su saŋga kɛrɛ

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
text: Tɔ, ya se sɛ wɔ? Yɛrɛ bo ya ti Yuda­fɔm ni, ya tara bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n nzu nu wɔ? Ai, ya tara má bu pui, dama ma du mɔ ase am ka yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm ni, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ.

ref: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
text: Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i la ŋgɔnlɛ, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i niɛniɛ Nyɛmɛ busu.

ref: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
text: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ ka, “Bu konvi ti ka faŋ­kunma bo ba teke i su wɔ, na bu fa bu taferema sudadaka minɛm.” “Na jɔrɛ bo i fite bu nɔɔ nu yo ya ka pumpuni bɔrɛ wɔ.”

ref: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
text: “Nzukɔlɛɛ ni jɔrɛ tiɛ ŋgumi fite bu nɔɔ nu a.”

ref: These people quickly become violent.
text: “Bu cɛ má na bu yi minɛ ŋgɔɔ ndende.

ref: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
text: Deke kɛrɛ bu kɔ, bu dɛɛ la ka bu yo minɛm ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, na bu saaki bu.

ref: They don't know how to live in peace.
text: Bu dɛɛ la ka bu nati sukpini jɔrɛ, bu koro má tana­laifiɛ.”

ref: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
text: Ya si ka deke n kɛrɛ bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu ni, i jɔjɔ ni Yuda­fɔm bo bu su i su n wɔ, adena má be koro teke i nɔɔ kan jɔrɛ be, ama, durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ bá jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu na i dí bu jɔrɛ na i cín bu sui.

ref: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
text: Ama, kisa Nyɛmɛ ayi akere ya kabo ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, má yiri la bo ya su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ni, ama, yiri la bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm diiri i ŋusu daani ka i yó n wɔ.

ref: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
text: dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ayo satiɛ afun Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa ni,

ref: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
text: ama, Yesu Kristo ade sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tii aji, nyini dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ŋa sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ayo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a, i ti Nyɛmɛ aceeri wɔ.

ref: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, nzu deke ye ya koro fa fɔ a? Deke be wo má bɛrɛ. Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni ma minɛm yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ a, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ. Ama, bo minɛm yo Yesu yarada n dɛɛ ti a.

ref: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka Nyɛmɛ ti má Yuda­fɔm ŋgumi Nyɛmɛ, i ti bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n gusu Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

ref: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
text: Tɔ, am ma ya niɛ ya na Abraham jɔrɛ. Ya busu nu, yiri ti ya kɛrɛ na a.

ref: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
text: Nzɛn má i sakpa yoo­wa dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, má i koro fa fɔ yanfɔya, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ti má sɔ.

ref: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
text: Nzɛn sɔnɔ yo juma na ba tɔ i kaari, i ti má ka bu ŋa ba ce i aceeri wɔ, i ti i juma ni ŋkatɔ ɔ.

ref: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
text: Fɛmɛ Davidi mmɔɔ yo kaanri nyini minɛm ŋusu jɔrɛ, ka i mi la tere­fiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, má ka i sakpa n dɛɛ ti a. I ŋa,

ref: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
text: Tere­fiɛ­fɔ la sɔnɔ bo Micɛra n ŋa i la má satiɛ be ni.”

ref: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
text: Tɔ, minɛm bo bu wuraari kɛnkɛnnɛ n ŋgumi la nyini tere­fiɛ ni, nzɛn sɛ a? Ai, minɛm bo bu wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni mmɔɔ la i. Dama ya du mɔ ase ka, Abraham yarada n dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a.

ref: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
text: Saŋga bɔnɔ ye Nyɛmɛ seeri sɔ n a? Nyini saŋga Abraham awura kɛnkɛnnɛ, nzɛn wɔ nya wura má? Ai, nyini saŋga wɔ nya wura má.

ref: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
text: Yiri gusu kaaki ti minɛm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n si a, má ka ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n dɛɛ ti, ama, bo ba yo yarada kabo i yoori yarada saŋga bo i nya wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni.

ref: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
text: Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Abraham ni i busu nu­fɔm ka i fá durunya n kɛrɛ ce bu ni, má ka i su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su dɛɛ ti a, ama, kabo wɔ yo Nyɛmɛ yarada n ti ye Nyɛmɛ deeri i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin n su i dɔ a.

ref: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
text: Nzɛn minɛ su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ye bu nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i cé bu ni, nyini la ka yarada­yo n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ, na kabo Nyɛmɛ ase n gusu la má kpɛrɛ.

ref: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
text: Nyini ti, deke bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i fá ma ni, i baari minɛm bo bu yo yarada n dɛɛ ti wɔ. I yoori sɔ ni, adena deke n bo i yo seeri ka i yó n yó aceeri kpama ma Abraham busu nu­fɔm kɛrɛ, má bo bu su nɔaniɛ n kpamaa­wa n su ŋgumi, ama, bo ba yo yarada kabo Abraham yoori ni. Yiri ti ya kɛrɛ si a,

ref: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
text: kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, “M má a yo minɛ barasu dɔŋgu si.” Nyɛmɛ ŋuuri ka Abraham ayo yarada ka yiri Nyɛmɛ teŋge minɛm bo ba wu n yiwee nu, na yiri ma deke n bo i yo wo má bɛrɛ n wo bɛrɛ a.

ref: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
text: Nyini ti, saŋga bo Abraham yoori yarada na i la tama ni, i ti kekereke na i yo tama ka i bá yo minɛ barasu dɔŋgu si, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “U busu nu­fɔm bá sunnu beberebe ni.”

ref: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
text: Wɔ yo má sika fa kɔ deke bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó i n su, ama, yarada n bo i yoori i n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ nya faŋga na wɔ kansi Nyɛmɛ.

ref: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
text: Na wɔ si tenle su ka Nyɛmɛ koro yo deke n bo i yo seeri ka i yó i ni.

ref: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
text: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i ka, Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, má Abraham ŋgumi dɛɛ ti ye bu kɛrɛɛri i sɔ a,

ref: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
text: ama, yɛrɛ gusu dɛɛ ti ye bu kɛrɛɛri sɔ n a, yɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ bá de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n na ya yo i yarada ni, yiri bo i teŋgeeri ya Mibiɛ Yesu i yiwee n nu ni.

ref: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
text: Ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti ye Yesu wuuri a, na wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu, adena Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

ref: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
text: Tɔ, ya yo yarada, na Nyɛmɛ ade ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, ye ya ni yiri afiɛn ayo dɔŋgu a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
text: Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti ma ya la atin jina deke bo ya wo kisa n a, yiri la Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama ya ni, na ya la ahorejɔ, dama ya la tama ka ya nyá Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be.

ref: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
text: Na nzɛn ya gusu la ahorejɔ saŋga bo ya di wahara, ya si ka wahara di ni ma ya mindi a.

ref: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
text: Na tama n bo ya la i ni, i ma ya di má nyinsɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya, yiri ma ya si ya ahore su kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ya agaya barasu a.

ref: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
text: Saŋga bo ya la má faŋga mmɔɔ ni, nyini saŋga n ye Kristo ba wu maari satiɛ­yo­fɔm n a.

ref: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ yiri akere ya kabo i koro ya barasu, dama saŋga bo ya toko yo satiɛm ni, ye Kristo ba wu maari ya a.

ref: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, i yiwee n bo i wu maari ya ni, ye Nyɛmɛ ade ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a, nyini ti, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka i dé ya fite sui­cin n bo Nyɛmɛ fá cin satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni.

ref: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
text: Saŋga bo ya yo ti Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔm ni, ye i wa Yesu wu maari ya n a, na wɔ yo ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu, kisa na ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn ayo dɔŋgu ni, i dé ya tii ji nahɔrɛ su, dama Yesu la ŋgɔɔ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
text: Má nyini ŋgumi a, ama, ya la ahorejɔ na ya bo tuntun Nyɛmɛ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, dama wɔ yo ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu. Adam ni Yesu Kristo jɔrɛ

ref: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
text: Ama, haari nyini mmɔɔ, fite Adam saŋga n kɛrɛ kɔ ju Moyisi saŋga ni, yiwee la yiko kun minɛm sɔ ɔ, haari ni bo ba yo má satiɛ kabo Adam yoori ni. Adam woo­wa kere ya deke be fa kɔ yiri n bo i bá ba n jɔrɛ a.

ref: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
text: Ama, aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ni, i ti má ka Adam dɛɛ satiɛ ni. Sɔnɔ kun satiɛ dɛɛ ti ye minɛ dɔŋgu wuuri a, ama, deke bo i tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, minɛ dɔŋgu anya ahɔrɔma bambaka, i ti aceeri bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, sɔnɔ kun ahɔrɔma dɛɛ ti. Yiri la Yesu Kristo.

ref: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
text: Bekun, Nyɛmɛ aceeri n ti má ka sɔnɔ kun n dɛɛ satiɛ ni, dama sɔnɔ kun n dɛɛ satiɛ ni, nyini ma Nyɛmɛ di minɛ jɔrɛ cin bu sui a, ama, minɛ dɔŋgu satiɛ bo ba yo i n dɛɛ sin, ye Nyɛmɛ ace bu aceeri a, na wɔ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

ref: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
text: Ya ŋu ka sɔnɔ kun dɛɛ satiɛ ti, ye yiwee la yiko kun minɛ sɔ n a, yiri sɔnɔ kun n satiɛ dɛɛ ti. Ama, deke bo i tara i kɛrɛ n la kabo minɛ anya ŋgɔɔ adi fɛmɛya n wɔ, dama ba nya ahɔrɔma bambaka n bo i ti Nyɛmɛ aceeri ni, Nyɛmɛ ade bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. I kɛrɛ fite sɔnɔ kun n dɛɛ ti wɔ, yiri la Yesu Kristo.

ref: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
text: Nyini ti, kabo sɔnɔ kun dɛɛ satiɛ ti na Nyɛmɛ di minɛm kɛrɛ jɔrɛ cin bu sui ni, sɔ bekun ye sɔnɔ kun dɛɛ sakpa­yo ni ma Nyɛmɛ de minɛm kɛrɛ ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ na bu nya ŋgɔɔ a.

ref: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
text: Na kabo sɔnɔ kun keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na minɛm kɛrɛ ayo satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, sɔ bekun ye sɔnɔ kun gusu ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a, nyini ye Nyɛmɛ dé minɛm kɛrɛ ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a.

ref: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
text: Nyɛmɛ faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kereeri a, na minɛ si kabo satiɛ­yo asunnu barasu a, ama, deke bo satiɛ­yo sunnu, Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama n sunnu tara sɔ.

ref: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
text: Nyini ti, kabo satiɛ la yiko na i ma yiwee kun minɛm sɔ ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye i de minɛm ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
text: Tɔ, ye ya se sɛ wɔ? Ya koro kɔ su suyo satiɛ, adena Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma buka su, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
text: Ai. I koro yo má sɔ. I ti ka ya ti minɛm bo ba du mɔ awu wɔ, fa kɔ satiɛ su. Ye nzu ti ye ya toko yo satiɛ bekun a?

ref: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
text: Am si má Nyɛmɛ nzue n biɛ n bu wɔ? Yɛrɛ bo bu biɛɛri ya Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun lɛɛ, na ya ni yiri asaŋga awu yiwee kun lɛɛ.

ref: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
text: Saŋga bo bu biɛɛri ya Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, i ti ka ya ni Yesu asaŋga awu, na ba kaaki ase ya ni yiri wɔ lɛɛ. Na kabo ya Si Nyɛmɛ Yiko­fɔ n teŋgeeri Yesu i yiwee nu ni, sɔ ye i má ya gusu nya nyini ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ n a.

ref: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
text: Ya ti kabo i ti n wɔ, saŋga bo i wuuri ni, sɔ ye wɔ yo daka ka ya bá ti kabo i ti n a, i yiwee teŋge n nu.

ref: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
text: Ya si ka, ya woo­wa dawa ni, ba saŋga akpama i ni Kristo i baka jaanu n su, adena má ya yo akɔɔm fa kɔ satiɛ su kun, dama aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i yo satiɛ n wo má bɛrɛ kun.

ref: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ wu, wɔ de i tii afite satiɛ nu lɛɛ.

ref: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
text: Tɔ, kabo ya ni Kristo saŋga wuuri ni, sɔ ye ya yo yarada ka ya ni yiri sáŋga nya ŋgɔɔ a.

ref: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
text: Ya si ka Kristo teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, i wú má kun, dama yiwee la má i ŋusu yiko kun.

ref: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
text: Yiwee n bo i wuuri ni, i wuuri kpe­kun n kpein wɔ, na i yi satiɛ, ama, ŋgɔɔ n bo i la i ni, i wo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wo wɔ.

ref: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
text: Sɔ ye i ti ye ni ka ambɛrɛ ni am tii si ka am awu fa kɔ satiɛ su a, na am ni Yesu ayo kun, ye am la ŋgɔɔ wo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wo a.

ref: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
text: Nyini ti, má am ma satiɛ la yiko fa kɔ am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n su, na am yo am kunnu koroo­wa.

ref: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
text: Má am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa be ma satiɛ, na i kaaki deke bo i yo satiɛ, ama, am fa am ŋu ma Nyɛmɛ ka minɛm bo ba teŋge yiwee nu anya ŋgɔɔ kisa, na am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ ma Nyɛmɛ na i fa am yo deke bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su.

ref: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
text: Satiɛ la má yiko fa kɔ am dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má am su kun, ama, Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni niɛ am su a. Minɛm bo ba kaaki sa bo i kɔ atin su akɔɔm

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
text: Tɔ, nzu bekun a? Kisa, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má ya su kun, ama, Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni niɛ ya su a. Nyini ti, ya koro yo satiɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ.

ref: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
text: Saŋga be am yo ti satiɛ akɔɔm wɔ, ama, kisa ya da Nyɛmɛ asi ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo bu fa kereeri am, na am ade i ni am ahore­mbaa kɛrɛ ni,

ref: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
