text: am ade am tii afite satiɛ nu lɛɛ, am akaaki sa bo i kɔ atin su n akɔɔm wɔ.

ref: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
text: M fa adamande dɛɛ woo­wa, ye m fa kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ti a, dama am suti má i bu. Am yo fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n be yoori daŋgba sam wɔ, ni satiɛ barasum wɔ, nyini ma am saaki nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n a, sɔ ye kisa am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaam yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, nyini ma am yo Nyɛmɛ minɛ casim a.

ref: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
text: Saŋga bo am yo ti satiɛ akɔɔm ni, be miɛ má am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su.

ref: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
text: Satiɛm bo am yo yoori bu ni, nzu kpɛrɛ ye am nyaari i a? Pui. Kisa, bu ti am nyinsɛ wɔ, na i awieeri kɛrɛ la yiwee wɔ.

ref: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
text: Ama, kisa na am ade am tii afite satiɛ nu, na am ayo Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm ni, kpɛrɛ bo am anya i ni, yiri la bo am ayo i minɛ casim ni, na i awieeri, am nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
text: Dama satiɛ ŋkatɔ la yiwee wɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ aceeri n bo wɔ fa ama ya n la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri na i wo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo nu n wɔ.

ref: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
text: Ya fa i ka, nzɛn bara aja, na i hun toko la ŋgɔɔ, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ma má atin ka i yaki i ajaa, ama, nzɛn i hun n nya wuuri, i la atin bo i koro kɔ ja biɛsɔ fɔfɔrɛ.

ref: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
text: Saŋga bo ya yo su ya adamande woo­wa su ni, ya koroo­wa la ka, ya sufa ya ŋuniɛ­mbaam suyo satiɛm, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ ti. Nyini satiɛm n awieeri la yiwee wɔ.

ref: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
text: Ama, kisa, ya nya ya tii afite nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i yo niɛ ya su ni, dama ya wu wɔ. Nyini ti, ya su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, ya su má atin dawa n bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo ba kɛrɛ ase n su, ama, ya su Nyɛmɛ ni atin fɔfɔrɛ n bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama ya ni su a. Kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ma minɛ yo satiɛ barasu ni

ref: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
text: Tɔ, ye ya se sɛ wɔ? Ya koro se ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti satiɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ. Nzɛn má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n awo má bɛrɛ, má m si má kabo satiɛ ti. Dama nzɛn má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ase má ka, “Má a ma u konviki wura be dɛɛ neŋge nu,” má m si má ka nzɛn u konviki wuraari be dɛɛ neŋge nu, i ti satiɛ wɔ.

ref: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
text: Saŋga be m yo la ŋgɔɔ ɔ, na m si má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n jɔrɛ, ama, saŋga bo nɔaniɛ maa­wa n baari ni, na wɔ yɛ satiɛ su m ahore su,

ref: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
text: na ma wu. Na ma ŋu ka nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo má i ma ŋgɔɔ ni, mini dɔ, i faari yiwee baari a.

ref: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
text: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n dɛɛ ti, ye satiɛ anya atin awura m ahore su adadaka m, ye i kun m a.

ref: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
text: Ya si ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti awiɛwiɛ jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, mini yiri, m bita adamande dɛɛ woo­wa a, na m ti satiɛ akɔɔ.

ref: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
text: Sam bo m yo bu ni, m koro si má deke n bo i ti na m yo bu ni, dama deke n bo m koro ka m yo bu ni, m yo má nyini, ama, deke n bo m kpɔ bu ni, m yo nyini wɔ.

ref: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn m yoori satiɛ be, na m koro má i, ma soro su ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti kpa wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Ma ŋu ka má m koroya­nu ye m yo nyini satiɛm n a, ama, satiɛ n bo i wo m ahore su ni ma m yo sɔ a.

ref: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
text: M si ka woo­wa n bo m bita i ni, i ti má kpa haari kaan sɔ, i ti adamande woo­wa a, nyini ti, sakpa­yo wo m ahore su, ama, m koro yo má i.

ref: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Satiɛ n bo m yo bu na m koro má ka m yo bu ni, nyini la ka, má mini ni m tii yo bu a, ama, satiɛ n bo i wo m ahore su ni yo bu a.

ref: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
text: Ma ŋu ka nyini jɔrɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka, saŋga kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m yo sakpa, nyini saŋga ye m yo satiɛ a.

ref: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
text: Dama m sunsunni ni m ahore su, m koro Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni.

ref: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
text: Ama, ma ŋu ka nɔaniɛ barasu be wo m ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ nu, nyini nɔaniɛ tiɛ n kun luɛ ni nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i wo m sunsunni ni m ahore su n wɔ, na i kunma m ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ nu, na i tara m nyi m.

ref: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
text: Oi. Mini aŋunfɔ­fɔ sɔ. Ŋma dé m tii ji a, na i yí m fite ahin adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu a, bo i sucin m kɔ yiwee n nu, na m ka ŋgbɛɛn n a?

ref: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
text: M da Nyɛmɛ asi, dama wɔ de m tii aji, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Tɔ, mini dɔ, m sunsunni ni m ahore su, m su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ, ama, m adamande dɛɛ woo­wa su, m su satiɛ atin su wɔ.

ref: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
text: Nyini nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n la má faŋga, dama adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n ayɔrɔki. Deke bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n akoro ayo má i ni, Nyɛmɛ ayo i, yiri la bo i sunmaari i tiɛŋgu Wa durunya n nu fɛn, ni aŋuniɛ­mbaa bo i ferema ni adamande dɛɛ n bo i yo satiɛ ni, na wɔ di satiɛ bo i wo adamande ŋuniɛ n nu n jɔrɛ, na wɔ yo i ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
text: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu su adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin su ni, bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo adamande sa koroo­wa su wɔ, ama, minɛm bo bu su Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ atin n su ni, bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔrɛ su wɔ.

ref: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
text: Bo i sunsunni wo adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n su, i mi wú ɔ, ama, bo i sunsunni wo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔrɛ su, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ, na i nyá ahoreda.

ref: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, am su má adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin n su, am su Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ atin ni su a, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am nu n wɔ. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la má Kristo Awiɛwiɛ ni, i mi ti má Kristo dɛɛ.

ref: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ bo Kristo wo am dɔ ni, am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n bá wu wɔ, satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ama am ŋgɔɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i de am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ.

ref: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
text: Dama nzɛn am su adamande dɛɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin su, am wú wɔ lɛɛ, ama, nzɛn am ma Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bukaari am, na am yakiiri adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa ni, am nyá ŋgɔɔ.

ref: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
text: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ma Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n kere bu atin, bɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam a.

ref: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
text: Kabo ya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam ni, ya nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i minɛm ni, na ya ni Yesu Kristo gusu sáŋga nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i minɛm n a. Nzɛn ya ni yiri saŋga diiri wahara, ya ni yiri gusu sáŋga nya i jirima woo­wa ni. Kabo ya woo­wa bá ti kabo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ n ti ni

ref: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
text: M sunsun ka wahara n bo ya sudi i kisa ni, i ti má pui, ya koro fa má i sunsun má jirima woo­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ bá yi i kere ya ni.

ref: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
text: Deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu n ŋminda ni anyum­boro wɔ, na bu bá ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ yí i nbaam fite fofoe n nu.

ref: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
text: Ya si ka deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ni, wo waraki ni yaari nu wɔ, haari ni kisa, kabo bara ba suwu baa na i di yaari ni.

ref: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
text: Má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ni ŋgumi wo waraki su a, ama, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ bo ya de i Awiɛwiɛ bo i ti ciciri su aceeri ni, ya wo waraki su suŋminda ni anyum­boro wɔ, ka Nyɛmɛ bá fa ya yo i nbaam, na i de ya ŋuniɛ ni.

ref: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
text: Tii­de n bo ya nya i ni, nyini ma ya la tama n a. Nzɛn a la deke, a se má ka a la tama ka a nya i. Deke bo sɔnɔ la i, i se má ka i la i tama.

ref: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
text: Ama, nzɛn ya la tama ka ya nyá deke be na ya nya nya má i, ya mindi ŋminda ni anyum­boro wɔ, na ya bá nya i.

ref: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
text: I ti sɔ lɛɛ, saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya yɔrɔki na ya si má kabo ya yo ni, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ba buka ya, beni, ya si má kabo ya sere Nyɛmɛ barasu, ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni i tii waraki sɔ ɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ma ya, ni waraki bo adamande koro jɔjɔ má i.

ref: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
text: Nyɛmɛ fiiti adamande ahore­mbaa su na i si kabo i ti barasu a, yiri si deke bo i wo i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n sunsunni nu a, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n su kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni su a, na i sere Nyɛmɛ ma i minɛm.

ref: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
text: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ wo minɛm bo bu koro i n dɔ, na i buka bu, adena i yo deke kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma bu, bɛrɛ la minɛm bo i du mɔ sɛsɛɛri su ka i fere bu ni, na wɔ fere bu.

ref: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
text: Nyɛmɛ du mɔ siiri nyini minɛm wɔ, na wɔ cici ka bu bá yo kabo i Wa n ti ni, nyini ma i Wa n yoori baa­kpiɛn, i nbaam kɛrɛ nu n a.

ref: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
text: Tɔ, kisa, ye ya se sɛ wɔ bekun a, fa kɔ ahin sam kɛrɛ su a? Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ wo ya dɔ, ŋma koro yo ya sa be a?

ref: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
text: Nyɛmɛ akete má ka i fa má i tiɛŋgu Wa ni mmɔɔ ma má ya, ama, i faari i maari ya kɛrɛ wɔ. Nyini ti, i yo sɛ kete ka i má má ya deke n kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa n a?

ref: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
text: Nyini ti, ŋma koro di ya jɔrɛ cin ya sui a? Be wo má bɛrɛ. Dama Kristo ni i tii wuuri wɔ, nahɔrɛ na wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu, i tana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, susere i ma ya.

ref: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, bu kun ya sɔ ɔ, ya ti ka nbɔɛm wɔ, ye bu kpiɛ ya kɔm a.”

ref: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
text: Haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni, ya di nasara a, na ya toko di nasara buka su, Kristo bo i koro ya n dɛɛ ti.

ref: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
text: Tɔ, deke bo m sukan kere am ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, m bu má kɔrɛ, m ti Kristo dɛɛ wɔ, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n gusu kere m sunsunni nu a, ye i di daani m ahore su a.

ref: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
text: Yiri la ka, m ahore asaaki agaya, na saŋga kɛrɛ i suyo m yaa

ref: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
text: fa kɔ m ninbaa Yisareli­fɔm dɛɛ busu. Nzɛn má mini ni m tii kaari ŋgbɛɛn, na m kpaaki yakiiri Kristo, adena i buka m ninbaam, m dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm ni, má m koro.

ref: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
text: Bɛrɛ la Yisareli­fɔm bo Nyɛmɛ maari bu atin ka bu yo i nbaam, na bu ŋuuri i jirima woo­wa ni, na i faari sɛsɛ­sum diiri i di ni bɛrɛ ni, na i faari nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari bu ni, na i kereeri bu kabo bu su i barasu ni, na i seeri ka i yó sakpa beberebe ma bu ni.

ref: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
text: I ti má ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ti má nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama má bu kɛrɛ bo bu wuuri bu Yisareli­fɔm busu nu ni n ti Yisareli­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

ref: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
text: Nyini la ka, má nbaam kɛrɛ bo Abraham wuuri bu kabo adamande wu nbaam ni ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam nahɔrɛ su a, ama, i nbaam bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i wú bu ni ŋgumi, ti i busu nu­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

ref: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
text: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i n yaa, i ŋa, “Ti fɛn, ŋgɔɔ cɛɛn ka buusɔ, m káaki ba na m bá ŋu ka u yi Saara bita baa­biɛsɔ.”

ref: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Má nyini ŋgumi a, am teŋge su ka ya na Izaki yo maari i yi Rɛbɛka akunnu, ye i wuuri ndaam n a.

ref: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
text: Saŋga bo Rɛbɛka nya wu má i ndaam ni, na bu nya yo má sakpa wara satiɛ be ni, nyini saŋga n ye Nyɛmɛ seeri i ka, “Baa­kpiɛn ni bá su baa­kan n a.” Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu ye i yoori sɔ n a, yiri koro i sɔnɔ ye i yiiri i a, má ka bu be sa­yoo­wa dɛɛ ti a.

ref: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
text: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “Jakɔbu, ye m koro i a, ama, Isawu yiri, m kpɔ i wɔ.”

ref: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
text: Nyini ti, má adamande koroya­nu, wara deke bo i boro i nyi yo i ni ti jɔrɛ a, ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ si aŋunfɔ ni, nyini ti jɔrɛ a.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Ijipiti Fɛmɛ Faaro ka, “Deke n bo i ti na m yiiri u tanaari fɛmɛya biɛ n su n la ka, m kére m yiko u ŋgɔɔ su, na m duma tá bara yɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu.”

ref: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
text: Nyini kɛrɛ kere ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i si i aŋunfɔ, ye i si i mi aŋunfɔ a, na minɛm bo i koro ka i yo bu ahore­mbaa kekereke ni, na i yo bu. Nyɛmɛ yaa­fa ni i aŋunfɔ si n jɔrɛ

ref: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
text: M ŋa, wɔrɔ la ŋma na a se Nyɛmɛ akuruwa a? Dundu koro bisa má yakati ta­fɔ n ka, “Nzu ti ye a taari m sɔ a?”

ref: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
text: Yakati ta­fɔ n la atin bo i fa i yakati n ta dundu bo i sufa yo juma cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na i kaaki la atin bo i fa be ta dundu bo i fa yo juma cɛɛn kpiri cɛɛn.

ref: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
text: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ la atin bo i koro fa yaa, na minɛm ŋu kabo i yiko ti barasu. I gusu koro mindi agaya fa kɔ minɛm bo bu ma yaa ati i ni, bɛrɛ bo má wɔ yo daka ka bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni.

ref: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
text: Na i gusu la atin bo i fa i jirima woo­wa bo i la má aŋusu n fa kere minɛm bo wɔ si bu aŋunfɔ ni, na wɔ du mɔ asɛsɛ su ka bu bá nya i jirima woo­wa n be ni.

ref: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
text: I ti kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Hoseya akɛrɛ i dɛɛ kadasi n nu n wɔ ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “Minɛm bo bu yo ti má m minɛm ni, m fére bu ka m minɛm. Na yiri n bo ma koro má i ni, m fére i ka m sɔnɔ koroo­wa.

ref: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
text: Dika n bo i nu na m fereeri bu ka, ‘Am ti má m minɛm ni,’ dɔ ye bu fére bu ka Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ nbaam n a.”

ref: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
text: Tɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya abo awie fa kɔ Yisareli­fɔm jɔrɛ su, i ŋa, “Haari ni bo Yisareli­fɔm sunnu beberebe ka ŋmiɛ ni, bu nu minɛ kaan sɔ nyá tii­de a.

ref: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
text: Dama sa n bo Micɛra ni yó i durunya n nu ni, i yó i wie ndende.”

ref: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
text: I ti kabo Esaya du mɔ seeri n wɔ, i ŋa, “Nzɛn má Micɛra Yiko­fɔ n ayaki má ya busu nu­fɔm nbem, má i yo ya kabo i yoori Sodom­fɔm ni Gomora­fɔm wɔ, miɛ tiɛm bo Nyɛmɛ saaki bu ni.” Yisareli­fɔm ni labari kpa n jɔrɛ

ref: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
text: Tɔ, ye ya se sɛ wɔ? Minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, akpini má atin kabo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, kisa, Nyɛmɛ ade bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, bu yarada n dɛɛ ti.

ref: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
text: Nzu ma i yoori sɔ n a? Dama ba kpini má atin kabo bu yo yarada na Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, ama, bu kpiniiri atin ka bu sa­yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. Na ba bo bu jaa yabuɛ n su na ba kpaasi.

ref: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
text: Kabo ba du mɔ akɛrɛ i ase ni, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “Am ti fɛn, m sufa yabuɛ be se Siyon miɛ kpiri n nu lɛɛ, nyini yabuɛ ni, minɛm bó bu jaa su na bu kpáasi. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, i mi dí má nyinsɛ.”

ref: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
text: M ninbaam, deke bo m koro i m ahore su la ka, m minɛm Yisareli­fɔm nya tii­de, m nyɛmɛsere lɛɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
text: Moyisi yo kɛrɛ fa kɔɔri kabo minɛ koro su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, na bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ŋa, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ su, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.”

ref: But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?”
text: Ama, fa kɔ kabo yarada dɛɛ ti, na sɔnɔ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, i ŋa, “Má a sunsun u ahore su ka, ŋma fún kɔ aŋgoro a?” Nyini la ka, i kɔ́ fa Kristo jura ba lɛɛ.

ref: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
text: Wara “Ŋma júra kunma ŋgoŋgo n bo i la má awieeri nu a?” Nyini la ka, i kɔ́ fa Kristo fun fite bo ba wum n nu ba lɛɛ.

ref: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
text: Deke bo i suse n la ka, “Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n waari má ni wɔrɔ. I wo u nɔɔ nu, ni u ahore­mbaa mmɔɔ su.” Yiri la labari n bo ya kaanri i n fa kɔ yarada­yo su ni.

ref: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
text: Nzɛn a teke u nɔɔ jɔjɔɔri minɛm nyunu ka, Yesu ti u Mibiɛ nahɔrɛ su, na a yoori yarada u ahore su ka Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu, a nyá tii­de.

ref: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, i mi dí má nyinsɛ.”

ref: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
text: Nyini ti, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, bu la má kpakpaaki­nu, Micɛra kun ni ti bu kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a. Yiri ma minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu bo fere i duma deke kpa bo i la má aŋusu n a.

ref: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
text: Tɔ, bu yo sɛ koro bo fere i duma, má ka ba du mɔ ayo i yarada? Ye bu yo sɛ koro yo i yarada, má ka ba du mɔ ati i jɔrɛ? Ye bu yo sɛ koro ti i jɔrɛ a, má ka be adu mɔ akan i labari kpa n akere bu?

ref: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
text: Ye bu yo sɛ koro kan i labari kpa n kere minɛm a, má ka be asunma bu minɛm dɔ? Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu, ka, “Bo bu suba kan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n ma ya ni, bu natiiri yo fiɛ.”

ref: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
text: Ama, má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ soroori su deeri labari kpa n a, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo seeri ni, ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, ŋma ayo ya labari n yarada? Ai, be ade má i.”

ref: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
text: Ama, minɛ bo ba de má labari kpa ni, ba ti má jɔrɛ n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a? Nahɔrɛ, ba ti i. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Ba bo ndoori abara ayɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu, na bu nɔaniɛ aju asiɛn n kɛrɛ su.”

ref: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
text: M koro ka m bisa bekun ka, Yisareli­fɔm ni, ba si má? Ba si. Moyisi du mɔ seeri ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “M má am si kɔrɛ fa kɔ minɛm bo bu ti má daanfiɛ n nahɔrɛ su ni, m má am fa yaa fa kɔ minɛm bo bu la má ŋgɔnlɛ n ŋu.”

ref: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
text: Na Esaya ayo ahoresi, na wɔ se bu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “Haari minɛm bo bu yo niɛniɛ má m busu ni, m ma ba ŋu m. Na minɛm bo bu yo bisa má m busu ni, ma bɔ bu nu afa m ŋu akere bu.”

ref: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
text: Na Esaya akaaki ase fa kɔ Yisareli­fɔm su ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “Ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, m teŋge m saa fere nyini minɛm n wɔ, ama, bu ti atii kekereke ni ketema­fɔm wɔ.”

ref: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
text: M koro ka m bisa ka, Nyɛmɛ akete i minɛm wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a? Ai, wɔ kete má bu e. Am ti fɛn. Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii, m ti Yisareli­fɔ ɔ. M ti Abraham busu nu­fɔ ɔ, m fite Bɛnjamin dɛɛ lɛɛ anɔ n nu wɔ.

ref: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
text: Nyɛmɛ akete má i minɛm bo i du mɔ yiiri bu ni. Am nya kan má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya n su, kabo i jarakiiri Yisareli­fɔm Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
text: Bo i ŋa, “Micɛra, ba kun u nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ, ba kaaki afufu u yoo­wa yoo­birim n kɛrɛ, wɔ kɔ aka mini ŋgumi kpein wɔ, ye bu koro ka bu kun m gusu n a.”

ref: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
text: Nzu bekun a? Yisareli­fɔm n anya má deke n bo bu kpiiri i busu ni, ama, minɛm n bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri bu ni, bɛrɛ nyaari i a, na bo bu kaari n kɛrɛ ahore­mbaa ayo kekereke.

ref: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kisa gusu, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ ayi Yisareli­fɔm nu minɛ kaan sɔ ɔ, ye wɔ fere bu a, fa kɔ i ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti.

ref: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: I ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ yi bu a, má ka bu sa­yoo­wa dɛɛ ti a. Nzɛn má i ti ka bu sa­yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti, má ahɔrɔma n ti má aceeri kun.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
text: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ, ka, “Nyɛmɛ ama bu ahore­mbaa nɔnɔɔri, na i ma bu nyu­mbaa ŋu má asi, na i ma bu sui ti má asui, haari ni nyuma.”

ref: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
text: Na Fɛmɛ Davidi ase ka, “Ma bu cɛɛn­kpiri diire dii­biri n kaaki toroku ni diɛndiɛ tara bu. Ma bu kpaasi, na bu nya bu satiɛ ŋkatɔ.

ref: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
text: Ma bu nyunu si biri na bu koro ŋu má asi, ma bu wi biri saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga ka ba sɔ sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri wɔ.” Kabo bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n anya tii­de ni

ref: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
text: M koro ka m bisa ka, bo bu boori bu jaa kpaasi ni, bu yasu má kun wɔ? Ai, má sɔ. Ama, nahɔrɛ, bu kpaasi sɔ n dɛɛ ti, ye minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm nyaari tii­de a, adena Yuda­fɔm ŋu i na bu si bu kɔrɛ.

ref: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
text: Kisa m sujɔjɔ ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni gusu dɔ a. Juma n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ni, i sunmaari m ka m yo i jaramasa ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm dɔ ɔ, nyini ti ye m fa m juma n fa fɔ a,

ref: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
text: na i má m tiɛŋgu minɛm si kɔrɛ, adena bu nbem nya tii­de.

ref: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
text: Dama saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ keteeri bu ni, ye i ni durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ayo kun a, ama, nzɛn i kaaki deeri bu bekun, i tí sɛ wɔ? I yó ka ba nya ŋgɔɔ afite yiwee nu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
text: Nzɛn be faari i alɛ­mbaa fɔfɔrɛ be fa yoori yoo­wa na i fa maari Nyɛmɛ, nyini la ka, i alɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Ye nzɛn baka bunlui n ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, nyini la ka, i asa­mbaa n kɛrɛ gusu ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
text: Nahɔrɛ, Yisareli­fɔm ti ka baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n wɔ, ye ba kpiɛ i asa­mbaa ni nbem atu, na ambɛrɛ ti ka boro baka bo i suu­wa la má faŋga, ye ba kpiɛ am asa baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n dɛɛ asa­mbaa n su a, nyini ti, am ni baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n asa­mbaa n kɛrɛ saŋga nya ŋgɔɔ fite i bunlui n dɔ ɔ.

ref: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
text: Nzɛn i nya ti sɔ, má am fa fɔ ka am ti kpa tara baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n ni i tii, dama má asa­mbaa ni ma bunlui ni ŋgɔɔ a, ama, bunlui ni ma asa­mbaa ni ŋgɔɔ a.

ref: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
text: Beni, a se ka, “Ba kpiɛ baka n bo i su suu­wa n dɛɛ asa­mbaa n be, adena mini nya sasuu­biri a.”

ref: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
text: Tɔ, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, Yuda­fɔm ayo má yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ kpiɛɛri bu jiiri n a, ye u yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye a jina jinaa­biri n bo a jina n a. Nyini ti, má a yo kpirikoro, ama, si u ŋu sa.

ref: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
text: Dama nzɛn Nyɛmɛ ayaki má baka kpa n ni i tii dɛɛ asa­mbaa ni, beni, i bá yaki má wɔrɔ boro baka ni mmɔɔ dɛɛ asa­mbaa ni.

ref: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
text: M ŋa a ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ yo sakpa barasu ni, ni kabo i di minɛ jɔrɛ na i cin bu sui ni anyum­boro barasu ni. Minɛm bo bu yo má i yarada n ye i di bu jɔrɛ cin bu sui ni anyum­boro n a, ama, wɔrɔ yiri, i yo sakpa ma u wɔ, nyini ti, susu i sakpa n bo i yo ma u n su, má nyini, i kpíɛ wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ji wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
text: Nzɛn Yuda­fɔm kaakiiri bu wo, na bu bá yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada, i koro fa bu sa bu woo­biri dawa n su, dama i la yiko bo i koro yo sɔ.

ref: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
text: Ti fɛn. Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii ti ka boro baka asa­mbaa n bo ba kpiɛ i afa asa baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n su wɔ. Nyini ti, a si ka Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ti má kekereke na i káaki ba fa baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n asa­mbaam n ba sa bu woo­biri dawa n su bekun. Kabo Nyɛmɛ si sɔnɔ kɛrɛ aŋunfɔ n jɔrɛ

ref: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
text: M ninbaam, asiri nu jɔrɛ be yaa, ye m koro ka am si i a, adena má am yo am ŋu kpiri ka am la si. Yiri la ka, kabo Yisareli­fɔm nbem ayo bu ahore­mbaa kekereke ni, bu bíta i sɔ haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ bá ba Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
text: Tɔ, i yó sɔ ɔ, na Yisareli­fɔm kɛrɛ bá nya tii­de, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Tii­de­fɔ ni fíte Siyon miɛ kpiri n nu ba, na i bá yi deke n kɛrɛ bo i kɔ má atin su fa tu, na i yó Jakɔbu busu nu­fɔm casi.

ref: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
text: Kabo m fá sɛsɛ­su di i di ni bɛrɛ lɛɛ, saŋga bo m ba yiiri satiɛ n bo i wo bu ŋu ni.”

ref: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
text: Kabo Yuda­fɔm n akete labari kpa n ni, nyini ma ba kaaki Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔm kisa n a, ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm n dɛɛ ti. Ama, fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ fereeri bu nam na i yiiri bu ni, bu ti i minɛ koroo­wam wɔ.

ref: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
text: Dama nzɛn Nyɛmɛ faari i aceeri fa ceeri minɛ nbem, wara nzɛn wɔ fere sɔnɔ, i kaaki má i sunsunni.

ref: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
text: Ambɛrɛ, am yo keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ, ama, kisa, wɔ si am aŋunfɔ, bɛrɛ bo ba kete i nɔaniɛ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
text: Tɔ, kisa, i ti sɔ ɔ, bɛrɛ akete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, dama Nyɛmɛ asi am aŋunfɔ, adena i awieeri su, na i bá si bɛrɛ gusu aŋunfɔ.

ref: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ abo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kun ka bu kɛrɛ kete i wɔ, adena i awieeri su na i bá si bu kɛrɛ aŋunfɔ. Nyɛmɛ kansi jɔrɛ

ref: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
text: Nahɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ alecira ni i si bo i la i n wo ŋgo. Kabo i di i jɔrɛ ni, be koro fiiti má nu na i ti i bu, i atin n gusu kɛrɛ, sɔnɔ koro si má i bu.

ref: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
text: “Ŋma nya ce Nyɛmɛ deke be a, adena Nyɛmɛ kaaki tɔ i mi kaari a?”

ref: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
text: Dama deke kɛrɛ fite yiri dɔ a, deke kɛrɛ ti i dɛɛ wɔ, na deke kɛrɛ bá yo i dɛɛ wɔ. Yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

ref: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
text: Má am ma am woo­wa ti kabo durunya n nu­fɔm woo­wa ti barasu ni, ama, am kaaki am sunsunni fɔfɔrɛ, na am kaaki am wo, nyini ma am koro si Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu sa a, bo i ti kpa, na i sun i nu, na i ti kabo i koro ka i ti ni.

ref: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
text: Tɔ, Nyɛmɛ aceeri n bo wɔ fa ama m dɛɛ ti, m sutu am kɛrɛ fɔ ɔ ka, má be wura i ŋu kpiri tara kabo i ti ni, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sunsun na i niɛ i ŋu kabo i ti nahɔrɛ su barasu, na i si kabo Nyɛmɛ ama i mi yarada barasu ni.

ref: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
text: Adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa n ti mɔmɔ kun wɔ, ama, i la bue bue, i kɛrɛ ni i juma bo i yo i.

ref: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
text: Ya gusu dɔ i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ya sunnu, ama, ya ni Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti, ya ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa mɔmɔ kun wɔ, na ya kɛrɛ ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be bo ya buka ya beŋgu wɔ.

ref: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
text: Am koro minɛm ni akunnu fofoe, am kpɔ satiɛ, na am su deke bo i ti kpa su.

ref: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
text: Am koro am beŋgu Kristo­fɔm ka am ti nibaam wɔ, na am bu am beŋgum ka bu tara am.

ref: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
text: Am su Mibiɛ n ni anyum­boro, má am yo nviɛya, am ma am ahore­mbaa bi ni nyɛmɛsu.

ref: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
text: Am la ahoresi n dɛɛ ti, am ma am ahore jɔ. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am sudi wahara, am mindi, na am sere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni anyum­boro.

ref: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
text: Am kpaaki am neŋgem nu ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu la má be ni, na am boro am nyi sude minɛm wɔfɔ.

ref: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
text: Am sere deke kpa Nyɛmɛ dɔ ma minɛm bo bu kere am wuwuruwa ni, am sere deke kpa ma bu, má am sere deke tiɛ ma bu.

ref: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
text: Nzɛn minɛm wo ahorejɔ nu, am ma am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ ahore ti jɔɔ­wa. Nzɛn minɛm wo ahoresaaki nu, am ma am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ wo ahoresaaki nu.

ref: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
text: Am ma am ni am beŋgum saŋga wo ni sawɔrɛ, má am koro kpiri, ama, am fa am ŋu mata tarakam. Má am se ka ambɛrɛ ŋgumi la ŋgɔnlɛ a.

ref: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
text: Nzɛn am koro yo i, am boro am nyi na am ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tana ni laifiɛ.

ref: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
text: M danfo kpam, nzɛn be ayo am satiɛ, má am wɔrɔ tiɛ tɔ i kaari, ama, am yaki i, na Nyɛmɛ cín i mi sui, dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Micɛra n ŋa, “Mini cín bu sui a, mini tɔ́ bu kaari a.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
text: Ama, am yo kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Nzɛn ahɔɛ ati u kpɔ­fɔ na a la diire, ma i na i di. Nzɛn nzuehɔɛ ati i, ma i nzue na i nu. Nzɛn a yoori sɔ, i ti ka a tɔrɔ sin­mɔnɛ agɔ i mi tii su wɔ lɛɛ.”

ref: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
text: Má a ma satiɛ yoo­wa kunma u, ama, ma u kpa­yo n kunma satiɛ ni.

ref: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
text: I ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ de tere­tere­fɔm nɔaniɛ, bɛrɛ bo bu la yiko na bu niɛ am su ni, dama yiko be wo má bɛrɛ bo i fite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Bo bu la yiko kisa n kɛrɛ, Nyɛmɛ akpama bu a.

ref: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sakpa soro má tere­tere­fɔm ni sɛrɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori satiɛ ni, yiri soro tere­tere­fɔm n a. Nyini ti, nzɛn a koro má ka a soro tere­tere­fɔm ni, yo sakpa, na bu kansi u.

ref: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
text: Nyɛmɛ ma tere­tere­fɔm wo bɛrɛ yo bu juma a, na bu buka u na a yo sakpa. Ama, nzɛn a yoori satiɛ, ma sɛrɛ ti u, dama Nyɛmɛ ama bu yiko ka bu yo bu juma ma yiri Nyɛmɛ a, na bu cin minɛm bo bu yo satiɛ n sui. Bu cin má minɛm sui ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka am de tere­tere­fɔm n nɔaniɛ, má ka am soro ka bu cin am sui n ŋgumi dɛɛ ti a, ama, am ahore su, ni am sunsunni kere ka am yo sɔ n dɛɛ ti a.

ref: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, i kaaki ti ye ni ka am tɔ am lampom, dama gominam n yo bu juma ni anyum­boro wɔ, Nyɛmɛ akere ka bu yo sɔ a.

ref: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Dama am si Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n kɛrɛ, ka, “Má a saaki ajaa ciire, má a kun sɔnɔ, má a wɔ,” ni bo i ŋa, “Má a ma u konviki wura be dɛɛ neŋge nu,” haari ni bo ba ka n kɛrɛ jina nyini nɔaniɛ kun ni su a, ka, “Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.”

ref: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i beŋgu yo má i mi satiɛ. Nyini kere ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ jina koro su wɔ lɛɛ. Minɛm bo bu wo wein nu n jɔrɛ

ref: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
text: I ti ye ni ka am yo sɔ, dama am si kabo ahin saŋga n ti ni. Saŋga n aju, i ti ye ni ka am teŋge, má am daafi. Dama saŋga n bo ya nyá tii­de n adodo, i waari má ka saŋga bo ya du mɔ yoori yarada ni.

ref: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
text: Wɔ ka kaan na ya fíte kɔŋguɛ nu, ale sucin lɛɛ. Nyini ti, am ma ya yaki awosin nu juma kɛrɛ, na ya yo wein sa­yoo­wa, kabo luɛ­kun­fɔm sana bu biɛsɔya neŋgem na bu kɔ kun luɛ ni.

ref: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
text: Ya wo wiɛsu wɔ lɛɛ, nyini ti, am ma ya woo­wa kɔ atin su. Má ya sudi ya tii, sunu nzan subo, suyo ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, má ya suyo sakaraya, suyo konvikiya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, má ya sukpini jɔrɛ, na má ya susi kɔrɛ.

ref: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
text: Ama, am ma ya fa ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo n yo deke bo ya fa wura, kabo sɔnɔ fa sutura wura barasu ni, na má ya su ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa n su.

ref: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
text: Am de minɛm bo bu yarada ti má bii­wa n ni am saa nnyɔ, má am se bu akuruwa fa kɔ deke n bo bu ci bu n su na am tanda bu ahore.

ref: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
text: Ma ya fa i ka, nbem la yarada bo bu koro di deke kɛrɛ bo bu koro, ama, nbem gusu wo bɛrɛ, bu yarada ti má bii­wa, bu ciire la niɛ, bu di toro nyawa n ŋgumi wɔ.

ref: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
text: Wɔrɔ la ŋma na a jaraki be dɛɛ gbɛfɛnɛ? I dɛɛ tiɛŋgu mibiɛ ni sé ka i jína wara i kpáasi a. I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka i jína a, dama i mibiɛ ni koro ma i jina nyunyumi a.

ref: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
text: Bekun, minɛ nbem dɔ, bu sunsun ka cɛɛn nbem ti kpa tara nbem, ama, nbem gusu sunsun ka cɛɛn n kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ, be tara má i beŋgu. I ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ su kabo i sunsunni nu kere ka i ti kpa n su.

ref: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
text: Dama nyini dɛɛ ti ma Kristo wuuri, ye i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu n a, adena i yó sɔnɔ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ, bo bu la ŋgɔɔ n ni bo ba du mɔ awu n kɛrɛ.

ref: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
text: Wɔrɔ la ŋma, wɔrɔ bo a jaraki u beŋgu na a bu i ŋgbɛɛn ni? A si má ka ya kɛrɛ bá jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu na i dí ya jɔrɛ?

ref: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
text: Nyini ti, ya kɛrɛ bá kan ya sa bo ya yo i n kɛrɛ kere Nyɛmɛ. Má a yo sa bo i ma u beŋgu Kristo­fɔ bo i jaa na i kpaasi

ref: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
text: Kabo m ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu ayo kun ni, nyini ma m si ka deke be mmɔɔ ni i tii ti má ciire n a. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ sunsun ka deke be ti ciire i dɔ, má i mi di i.

ref: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
text: Nzɛn diire n bo a di i n dɛɛ ti, na a saaki u beŋgu Kristo­fɔ n ahore, nyini yiri, a suyo má koro sa lɛɛ. Kristo yo wuuri i dɛɛ ti wɔ, má a ma i mi ka ŋgbɛɛn, u diire n bo a di i n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
text: Má am ma be jɔjɔ jɔrɛ tiɛ fa kɔ deke kpa n bo am si i n su.

ref: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
text: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ niɛ i minɛm su ni, i ti má di ni nu jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, i ti aceeri bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama minɛm wɔ, yiri la ka, ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ni tana­laifiɛ, ni ahorejɔ.

ref: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
text: Má am saaki Nyɛmɛ juma ni, diire dɛɛ ti. Diire kɛrɛ ti kpa a. Ama, nzɛn be di diire be na i ma i beŋgu bo i jaa na i kpaasi, nyini yiri, i ti má kpa.

ref: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
text: Nzɛn a di niɛ, wara a nu nzan, wara a yo nyini n be sɔ, na i ma u beŋgu Kristo­fɔ bo i jaa na i kpaasi, i ti má ye ni ka a yo sɔ.

ref: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ba sudi diire barasu nbem na i yo sika, Nyɛmɛ adi i mi jɔrɛ ajaraki i ka wɔ yo satiɛ, dama diire n bo i sudi i ni, wɔ yo má yarada. Deke kɛrɛ gusu bo sɔnɔ yo i, na i la má yarada, i ti satiɛ wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
text: Yɛrɛ bo ya la faŋga fa kɔ yarada su ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya yo sawɔrɛ ni bɛrɛ n bo bu yarada la má faŋga ni, na ya buka bu, na ya dada bu ahore, má ya yo sa bo i sun yɛrɛ ni ya tii nu.

ref: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ yo deke bo i sun i beŋgu nu, na i yo sakpa, na i kɔ mɔ ni i nyɛmɛsu ni.

ref: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
text: Nyɛmɛ ma ya mindi ni ahoresi a, yiri ma am la anɔɔsɛ ni am beŋgu a, kabo Yesu Kristo kereeri am ni,

ref: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
text: adena am kɛrɛ tiɛn am nɔɔ fa anɔɔsɛ, na am kansi Nyɛmɛ, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Si. Kabo minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm gusu ade Kristo labari kpa n ni

ref: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am de am beŋgum, ni am saa nnyɔ kabo Kristo ade am ni, nyini ma minɛ kansi Nyɛmɛ a.

ref: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
text: Dama m suse am ka Kristo baari ka i ba buka Yuda­fɔm n wɔ, i yoori sɔ n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yo ma Yuda­fɔm nam n ayo bii­wa nahɔrɛ su n a.

ref: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
text: Bekun i baari ka i ba buka minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm wɔ, na bu gusu kansi Nyɛmɛ fa kɔ kabo wɔ si bu aŋunfɔ ni. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Nyini dɛɛ ti, m kánsi wɔrɔ Nyɛmɛ, m kánsi u duma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm nu, na m dí jue ma u.”

ref: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
text: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i bekun ka, “Ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, am ma am ahore jɔ ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo wɔ yi bu ni.”

ref: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
text: Bekun, i ŋa, “Ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, am kansi Micɛra ni, na am ma daanfiɛm kɛrɛ kansi Nyɛmɛ.”

ref: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
text: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya gusu ase ka, “Sɔnɔ be bá fite Jese dɛɛ lɛɛ anɔ n nu, i ti kabo baka tiɛ i bunlui su n wɔ, na i bá yo daanfiɛm kɛrɛ fɛmɛ, yiri má bu nya ahoresi a.”

ref: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
text: Nyɛmɛ ma ya tama, m sere i ka i ma am ahorejɔ ni tana­laifiɛ kɔ sin su, kabo am ayo i yarada n dɛɛ ti, adena Awiɛwiɛ Casi n yiko ma am tama bo i la má aŋusu. Deke bo i ti na Pɔɔlu kɛrɛɛri ahin kadasi n ni

ref: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
text: Tɔ, m ninbaam, m si tenle su ka am la wo kpa, na am la si dɔŋgu, na am kaaki koro fa tu am beŋgu fɔ.

ref: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
text: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ma fa ahoresi, ye m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n fa ma am a, adena m teŋge am ahin sam n su, dama Nyɛmɛ ayo sakpa ama m,

ref: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
text: ka m yo Yesu Kristo amusui­su­fɔ ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, adena m kan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n kere bɛrɛ bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu ti i dɛɛ minɛ casim. M yoori sɔ ni, i ti ka Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n bo i yo yoo­wa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu ni.

ref: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
text: Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, m la atin bo m koro fɔ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ juma n bo wɔ fa ama m su.

ref: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
text: I ma m faari alekutura sam, ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu fa yoori nyini juma n a. M boori i bu Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ma bara ayɛ akɔ aju haari Ilirikum awɔrɛ n nu, na ma kan Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ akere bu.

ref: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
text: Deke bo m nyunu boro su agaya n la ka, m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm awɔrɛm n kɛrɛ bo bu nya ti má Kristo duma ni, dama m koro má ka m ta be dɛɛ ataari su,

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
text: kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Haari ni bo minɛm nya kan má labari kpa n kere má bu ni, bu ŋú i, haari ni bo bu nya ti má labari kpa ni, bu tí i, na bu sí i bu.” Kabo Pɔɔlu ŋa i koro ka i kɔ niɛ Roma­fɔm busu ni

ref: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
text: Ama, kisa, miɛ fɔfɔrɛ be wo má bɛrɛ bo m kɔ dɔ na m kɔ yo juma ahin awɔrɛm n nu, nyini ti, kabo má saŋga kɛrɛ na m koro ka m ba niɛ am busu ni,

ref: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
text: m la tama ka m ba susin kɔ Esipani dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, m jína am dɔ na m niɛ am busu, na m ni ambɛrɛ nya tana, na am buka m na m koro kɔ ju deke bo m sukɔ ni.

ref: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
text: Dama Kristo­fɔm bo bu wo Makɛdoniya ni, ni bo bu wo Akaya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu ni, wɔ yo bu fiɛ ka bu yiyi ŋwaa, na m kɔ fa ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem na bu ti yaari­fɔm ni.

ref: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
text: Bɛrɛ ni bu tii afa sunsunni kpa aŋu ka Yɛrusalem­fɔm kaari su bu su, dama Yɛrusalem­fɔm du mɔ faari Nyɛmɛ deke kpa maari bɛrɛ n bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka bɛrɛ gusu nya durunya n nu neŋge ma Yɛrusalem­fɔm ni.

ref: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
text: Tɔ, nyini ti, nzɛn m koro yoori nyini juma n kɛrɛ wieeri, na bu nya nyini buka n kɛrɛ, m bá niɛ am busu, na m nyá sin kɔ Esipani.

ref: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
text: M si tenle su ka m baari, m fá deke kpa fite Kristo dɔ ba ma am.

ref: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
text: M ninbaam, kabo Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo wo ya dɔ, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n koro ya agaya n dɛɛ ti, m sere am wɔ, ka am buka m na ya sere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro,

ref: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
text: ka i dé m fite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu­fɔ nbem bo bu yo má yarada n saa nu, na Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem n dé buka n bo m sufa kɔ ma bu ni.

ref: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
text: Na nzɛn i ti Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa, na m bá am dɔ ni ahorejɔ, na m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ koro dada ya beŋgu ahore.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
text: Nyɛmɛ bo i ma tana­laifiɛ n buka ya kɛrɛ. Ami.

ref: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
text: Am de i kabo má am de Nyɛmɛ minɛm barasu ni, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, na am buka i ni deke kɛrɛ bo i koro, dama yiri ni i tii abuka minɛ dɔŋgu, haari ni mini mmɔɔ.

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
text: Am bisa Pirisila ni Akuyila dɔŋgu ma m. M ni bɛrɛ saŋga yo juma ma Yesu Kristo wɔ.

ref: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
text: Bu yo tɔɔri bu ŋgɔɔ maari m wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m da bu asi n a, má mini ŋgumi yo bu ansɛ a, ama, Kristo jamam kɛrɛ bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm gusu yo bu ansɛ.

ref: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
text: Am bisa Kristo jama n kɛrɛ dɔŋgu, bo bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ bu dika ni. Am bisa m danfo kpa Ɛpanɛtosi n dɔŋgu ma m, yiri du mɔ yoori Kristo yarada Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu a.

ref: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
text: Am bisa Mariya dɔŋgu, bo wɔ yo juma agaya abuka am ni.

ref: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
text: Am bisa Andɔrɔnikusi ni Juniya dɔŋgu, m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm bo m ni bɛrɛ yo woori dan­saraka nu ni, bu la dumaye Kristo jaramasam kɛrɛ nu, bɛrɛ du mɔ yoori Kristo susu­fɔm ka, ye m nya ba yoori a.

ref: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
text: Am bisa m danfo Ampiliyatusi dɔŋgu, m Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, m koro i agaya.

ref: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
text: Am bisa Wuribanisi dɔŋgu ma m, bo yiri ni yɛrɛ saŋga yo juma ma Kristo ni, na am kaaki bisa m danfo Sitakisi dɔŋgu.

ref: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
text: Am bisa Apɛlɛsi dɔŋgu, bo wɔ yi i ŋu ka i ti Kristo susu­fɔ nahɔrɛ su ni, na am bisa Arisitobulosi dika­fɔm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

ref: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
text: Am bisa m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔ n bo bu fere i ka Herodiyon n dɔŋgu, na am bisa Naracisi dika­fɔm bo bu ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ minɛm n buka su.

ref: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
text: Am bisa Tirifena ni Tirifosa dɔŋgu, nbara nnyɔm bo bu yo juma ma Mibiɛ ni, ni m danfo Pɛrisisi, yiri mmɔɔ ayo juma agaya ama Mibiɛ n ni.

ref: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
text: Am bisa Rufusu dɔŋgu, yiri bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i yo i juma n ni, ni i ni bo i ti mini mmɔɔ ni ni.

ref: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
text: Am bisa ahin minɛm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma m, bɛrɛ la Asinkiritusi, ni Felegon, ni Hɛrɛmesi, ni Patorobasi, ni Hɛrɛmasi, ni ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo bu dɔ ni.

ref: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
text: Am bisa Filolokusi ni Juliya dɔŋgu, ni Nɛrɛyusi, ni i niɛma­bara ni, ni Olimpasi, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo bu dɔ ni.

ref: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
text: Am bisa am beŋgu dɔŋgu ni koro. Kristo jamam kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n bisa am dɔŋgu. Awieeri su dɛɛ fɔ­tuu­wa

ref: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
text: M ninbaam, m sere am wɔ, am si am ŋu sa ni minɛm bo bu saaki keree­wam bo am adu mɔ ade i ni, na bu fa kpakpaaki­nu ba ni, na bu ma minɛm kpaasi fa kɔ bu yarada n su ni. Má am dodo bu.

ref: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
text: Dama nyini minɛm sɔ, bu su má ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo su, ama, bu su bu dɛɛ kunnu koroo­wa su wɔ, na bu bo minɛm bo bu si má pui n anɔɔfiɛ, na bu dadaka bu.

ref: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ati kabo am ade Kristo nɔaniɛ nahɔrɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, m ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su. M koro ka am la si fa kɔ deke n bo i ti kpa n su, ama, m koro má ka am la si haari kaan sɔ mmɔɔ fa kɔ deke n bo i ti má kpa n su.

ref: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Nyɛmɛ bo i ma tana­laifiɛ ni, i cɛ́ má, i má am yiko na am fún tiɛntiɛn Sitana ŋu mɛrɛki i. Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am.

ref: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
text: Timoti bo m ni yiri saŋga yo juma n bisa am dɔŋgu, na Lukiyosi, ni Jason, ni Sosipateri, m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm n gusu bisa am dɔŋgu.

ref: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
text: Mini Tɛrɛtiyusi gusu bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ duma n nu, mini ye Pɔɔlu ma m kɛrɛɛri ahin kadasi n sufa ma am n a.

ref: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
text: Am ma ya kansi Nyɛmɛ. Yiri koro ma am jina faŋga kenken fa kɔ Yesu Kristo labari kpa n bo m kaanri i jɔrɛ na am ati i n su a. I ti jɔrɛ bo bu yo fa fiɛɛri dada kɛrɛ wɔ, ama, kisa wɔ fite fofoe nu.

ref: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛɛri i n a, na Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n ama daanfiɛ kɛrɛ ati i, na ba de i, na ba yo i yarada.

ref: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
text: Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi la alecira a, yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i a, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu bo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu na wɔ fere m ka m yo Yesu Kristo jaramasa ni, m ni ya niwa Sositeni ni sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi ma am a.

ref: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am tana­laifiɛ. Sakpam bo Pɔɔlu ŋa Nyɛmɛ ayo ama bu ni

ref: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ m da Nyɛmɛ asi wɔ, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, fa kɔ sakpa n bo wɔ yo ama am na i fite Yesu Kristo dɔ ni.

ref: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
text: Daani n bo ya diiri i fa kɔ Kristo ŋusu jɔrɛ ni, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
text: Yiri má am faŋga haari kɔ ju awieeri a, na am la má ŋgasi be ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ cɛɛn ni.

ref: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
text: Nyɛmɛ la dabuŋu, yiri afere am ka am ni i Wa ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo n la wo fiɛ ni am beŋgu a. Kpakpaaki­nu n bo i woori Kristo jama n afiɛn ni

ref: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
text: M ninbaam, m sere am wɔ, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n nu, ka am kɛrɛ fa anɔɔsɛ na má am ma kpakpaaki­nu be wo am nu, ama, am ma am sunsunni ni am nɔaniɛ yo kun nahɔrɛ su.

ref: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
text: M ninbaam, deke bo i ti na m se sɔ ni, Kolowe dika­fɔm nbem ase m ka am la akuruwa am ŋuŋu nu.

ref: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
text: Yiri la ka, am nbem ŋa, “Mini su Pɔɔlu su a,” nbem ŋa, “Mini su Apolosi su a,” nbem ŋa, “Mini su Petoro su a,” ye nbem gusu ŋa, “Mini su Kristo su a.”

ref: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
text: Kristo akpakpaaki i ŋu nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ye bu kpamaari Pɔɔlu baka jaanu n su maari am a? Bu biɛɛri am Nyɛmɛ nzue Pɔɔlu dɛɛ duma nu wɔ? Ai.

ref: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
text: M da Nyɛmɛ asi ka ma biɛ má am be Nyɛmɛ nzue, má ka Krisipusi ni Gayosi n ŋgumi.

ref: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, be koro se má ka m biɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ nzue m dɛɛ duma nu.

ref: I did baptize the family of Stephanas, but I don't remember if I baptized anyone else.
text: Ɛɛnhɛɛn, ma teŋge su bekun ka m biɛɛri Satɛfanasi dika­fɔm n gusu Nyɛmɛ nzue. Sin nyini sin, m koro teŋge má su ka m biɛɛri be Nyɛmɛ nzue kun.

ref: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
text: Dama Kristo asunma má m ka m ba biɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue wɔ, ama, i sunmaari m ka m ba kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm wɔ. Ma fa má adamande dɛɛ alecira, ye m fa kan kereeri minɛm a, nzɛn má m yo sɔ, má yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri baka jaanu n su n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Kristo ti Nyɛmɛ yiko ni alecira a

ref: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
text: Dama nzɛn ya kan yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri i baka jaanu n su n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu suka ŋgbɛɛn ni, bɛrɛ dɔ i ti sinnzinya jɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Ama, yɛrɛ n bo ya nya tii­de ni, i ti Nyɛmɛ yiko sa bo wɔ yo ama ya a lɛɛ.

ref: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “M yó alecira­fɔm alecira ni ŋgbɛɛn, na m tɔ́ si­fɔm si n atin.”

ref: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
text: Tɔ, alecira­fɔm wo ni? Ye si­fɔm wo ni? Ye bo bu fiiti na bu si ahin durunya n nu jɔrɛ n wo ni? Durunya n nu alecira ye Nyɛmɛ afa akaaki sinnzinya n a.

ref: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
text: Nyɛmɛ la alecira, be koro fa má durunya n nu alecira na i fa si Nyɛmɛ. Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu ye i ma ya kan labari kpa n bo i ti ka sinnzinya jɔrɛ n kere minɛm a, na bo ba yo yarada ni, Nyɛmɛ ade bu tii aji.

ref: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
text: Ama, yɛrɛ kan Kristo Tii­de­fɔ labari kpa n jɔrɛ wɔ, kabo bu kpamaari i baka jaanu n su ni. Yuda­fɔm dɔ, i ti ciire wɔ, ye bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n dɔ, i ti sinnzinya jɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
text: Ama, minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ afere bu ni, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, ni bo bu ti Giriki­fɔm ni, Kristo ti Nyɛmɛ yiko ni alecira a.

ref: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
text: M ninbaam, am sunsun na am niɛ kabo am yo ti na Nyɛmɛ ba fereeri am ni. Nzɛn ya ŋa ya niɛ, durunya n nu­fɔm yo bu má ka am dɔŋgu la alecira, am dɔŋgu yo ti má yiko­fɔm, na am dɔŋgu gusu yo fite má nfaŋga­fɔm n busu nu.

ref: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ ayi dekem bo bu ti sinnzinya sa durunya n nu ni, na wɔ fa awura alecira­fɔm nyinsɛ, na wɔ yi dekem bo bu la má faŋga durunya n nu ni, na wɔ fa awura nfaŋga­fɔm nyinsɛ.

ref: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
text: Nyɛmɛ ayi bo bu ti tarakam durunya n nu fɛn, na wɔ yi minɛm bo bu bu má bu ni, ni dekem bo bu ti ŋgbɛɛn ni, na i ma dekem bo bu la faŋga n ka ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
text: Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ yoori i sɔ n la ka, adena má be koro fɔ yiri Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i fɔ, i ti ye ni ka i fɔ Mibiɛ n ti.”

ref: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
text: M ninbaam, saŋga bo m baari am dɔ na m ba kaanri labari kpa n kereeri am, na am si Nyɛmɛ asiri nu n jɔrɛ ni, ma fa má adamande alecira ni si jɔrɛ ajɔjɔ akere má am.

ref: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
text: Dama m yo baari ni, ma koro má ka m kan jɔrɛ be am dɔ, má ka Yesu Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n dɛɛ jɔrɛ, ni kabo i wuuri baka jaanu n su ni.

ref: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
text: M nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ni labari kpa n bo m kaanri i ni, i ti má adamande alecira ni si jɔrɛ, ama, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni yoori alekutura sam kereeri ka m nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
text: Adena má am yo yarada adamande alecira n dɛɛ ti, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n dɛɛ ti. Nyɛmɛ alecira jɔrɛ

ref: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
text: Tɔ, ama, ya jɔjɔ alecira jɔrɛ fa kere minɛm bo ba bi fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin n su n a. I ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ alecira, wara i ti má alecira bo durunya n nu nfaŋga­fɔm la i ni, bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
text: Durunya n nu nfaŋga­fɔm be mmɔɔ asi má nyini alecira ni, nzɛn má bu siiri i, má bu kpama má ya Mibiɛ Jirima­fɔ n baka jaanu n su.

ref: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
text: Ama, i ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ, ka, “Deke bo be nyu­mbaa nya ŋu má i ni, wara deke bo be sui nya ti má i ni, wara deke bo be nya sunsun má i, i ahore­mbaa su ni, nyini ye Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ i su, na i fá ma bo bu koro i n a.”

ref: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
text: Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, Nyɛmɛ ama i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ayi nyini n sɔ akere ya, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni fiiti si deke n kɛrɛ a, haari ni Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ bo i wo ŋgo ni.

ref: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
text: Adamande be koro si má sa bo i wo i beŋgu adamande ahore su, má ka i mi ni i tii awiɛwiɛ ni si deke bo i wo i ahore su a. Sɔ ye be koro si má sa bo i wo Nyɛmɛ ahore su a, má ka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni ŋgumi si a.

ref: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
text: Awiɛwiɛ n bo yɛrɛ anya i ni, i ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ, ama, i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, adena ya si aceerim kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ni.

ref: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
text: Nyini ye ya fa kere minɛm a. I fite má adamande alecira keree­wa n nu, ama, i ti deke bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni kereeri ya a, ye ya fa kere minɛm bo bu la Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a.

ref: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
text: Sɔnɔ bo i la Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni, i mi koro fiiti deke n kɛrɛ a, ama, be koro fiiti má i mi yiri.

ref: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
text: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu barasu n wɔ ka, “Ŋma asi sa bo i wo Micɛra n sunsunni nu, na i kére i kabo i yo a?” Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya si deke bo i wo Kristo sunsunni nu.

ref: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
text: M ninbaam, ma koro ajɔjɔ má am dɔ ka am ti minɛm bo bu si Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔrɛ, ama, m jɔjɔɔri am dɔ ka am ti bo bu bita adamande woo­wa a, Kristo dɛɛ atin n su, am ti ka nbaatumam wɔ.

ref: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
text: Dama am toko bita adamande woo­wa n wɔ. Am si am beŋgu kɔrɛ wɔ, na am suse akuruwa am ŋuŋu nu. Kabo am yo sɔ ni, durunya n nu adamande woo­wa atin lɛɛ, ye am bita i n a.

ref: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
text: Dama nzɛn am nbem ŋa, “Mini su Pɔɔlu su a,” ye nbem gusu ŋa, “Mini su Apolosi su a,” am ti má ka durunya n nu­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
text: Apolosi la ŋma? Wara Pɔɔlu la ŋma? Ya kɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ amusui­su­fɔm bo ya ba abuka am wɔ, ya kɛrɛ ni ya juma bo wɔ fa ama ya na ya yo, ye am ayo yarada a.

ref: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
text: Bo i dɔ mbaa n ni bo i gɔ nzue n kɛrɛ yo juma kun wɔ. Bu kɛrɛ nyá bu ŋkatɔ kabo bu juma­yoo­wa ti barasu n wɔ.

ref: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
text: Dama ya kɛrɛ saŋga yo juma kun ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ gusu ti ka Nyɛmɛ fie ye ya dɔ i a, na am kaaki ti ka Nyɛmɛ awuru ye ya ta i a.

ref: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
text: Aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ti ka sɔnɔ bo i si ataari ta nyunyumi wɔ, ye m se sɔɔ n bumboori fa n a. Ye be gusu dɛɛ la ka i ta taka nyini n su a. Ama, wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si i ŋu sa kabo i ta taka su barasu.

ref: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
text: Ama, jina cɛɛn, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ juma­yoo­wa bá fite fofoe nu, na bu ŋú i kabo i ti, dama sin bá yara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ juma, na sin ni yó i na i níɛ kabo i ti.

ref: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo sin n diiri i juma­yoo­wa ni, i nyá má ŋkatɔ, ama, yiri ni i tii nyá tii­de, se i ti ka sin n afundi i mi wɔ.

ref: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
text: Am si má ka am ti ka awuru bo Nyɛmɛ wo am nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Am si má ka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am nu wɔ?

ref: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i saakiiri Nyɛmɛ awuru ni, Nyɛmɛ sáaki i mi, dama Nyɛmɛ awuru n ti casi wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ ni am tii ti ka nyini awuru n a.

ref: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
text: Má be se ka i dadaka i ŋu. Nzɛn be wo am nu, na i sunsun ka i la durunya n nu alecira, ma i mi yo i ŋu ka i ti sinnzin wɔ, adena i kaaki alecira­fɔ nahɔrɛ su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
text: Dama deke bo durunya n nu­fɔm bu i ka i ti alecira ni, Nyɛmɛ dɔ i ti sinnzinya a. Nyini ti ye ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Alecira­fɔm ye Nyɛmɛ fa bu ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ fa tara bu n a.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
text: Ye ba kaaki akɛrɛ i ka, “Micɛra ni si deke kɛrɛ bo i wo alecira­fɔm sunsunni nu a, i si ka i ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.”

ref: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
text: Mini Pɔɔlu ti am dɛɛ wɔ, ni Apolosi, ni Petoro gusu, ni durunya, ni ŋgɔɔ, ni yiwee, ni kisa dɛɛ saŋga n bo ya wo nu ni, ni saŋga bo i bá ba ni, deke n kɛrɛ ti am dɛɛ wɔ,

ref: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text: na ambɛrɛ gusu ti Kristo dɛɛm, ye Kristo gusu ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ.

ref: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
text: Tɔ, nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka am niɛ ya ka ya ti Kristo amusui­su­fɔm wɔ, ya ti minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ afa i asiri nu jɔrɛ n awura ya saa nu wɔ.

ref: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
text: Jɔrɛ n bo i kaaki wo bɛrɛ la ka, wɔ yo daka ka amusui­su­fɔ yo i juma ni nahɔrɛ su ma i mibiɛ.

ref: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
text: Mini yiri, nzɛn am di m jɔrɛ, wara adamande jɔrɛ­di­fɔm di m jɔrɛ, m cɛcɛ wo má nu, haari mini mmɔɔ ni m tii di má m ŋu jɔrɛ.

ref: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
text: M sunsunni ni m ahore su, ma yo má satiɛ be, ama, nyini kere má ka m la má ŋgasi be wɔ lɛɛ, má ka Mibiɛ ni ŋgumi ti bo i di m jɔrɛ a.

ref: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, má sɔnɔ be di i beŋgu jɔrɛ, dama saŋga nya ju má bo Mibiɛ ni bá. I ba baari, yiri yí deke kɛrɛ bo i wo awosin nu na i fiɛ n fite fofoe nu a, na i yí deke kɛrɛ bo i wo adamande ahore su fite fofoe nu. Nyini saŋga ni, ye sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyá i kansi Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
text: Tɔ, m ninbaam, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye m kaanri ahin n kɛrɛ fa kɔ m ni Apolosi jɔrɛ n a, na am niɛ ya woo­wa, na am su kabo ba kɛrɛ i ase ni ŋgumi su, adena má am be yo kpirikoro ka mɔ­fɔ be tara i lamɔkɔ nyiŋga ni.

ref: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
text: Ŋma ase am ka am tara am beŋgu a? Nzu deke ye am la i, ye Nyɛmɛ afa ace má am a? Deke bo am deeri i ni, ye nzu ti ye am koro kpiri ka Nyɛmɛ afa ace má am a?

ref: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
