text: Am sunsun ka am anya deke n kɛrɛ bo am koro i n lɛɛ fan. Am ayo neŋge­fɔm lɛɛ fan. Am akaaki nfɛmɛm ayaki ya lɛɛ fan. Nzɛn má am adi fɛmɛya nahɔrɛ su, má i yo m fiɛ, adena ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ saŋga di fɛmɛya ni.

ref: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
text: Mini dɔ, yɛrɛ n bo ya ti Kristo jaramasam ni, i ti ka Nyɛmɛ ma ya ka sin wɔ. Ya ti ka minɛm bo ba di ya jɔrɛ abo ya asi ye ya wu a, na ya di nyini nyinsɛ n na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyu­mbaa ŋu i, mɛrɛkɛm ni adamandem kɛrɛ.

ref: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
text: Yɛrɛ ti sinnzinm wɔ, Kristo dɛɛ ti, ama, ambɛrɛ ti anyunuteke­fɔm wɔ, Kristo atin n su. Yɛrɛ ti daburuka­fɔm wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ ti nfaŋga­fɔm. Bu bu ambɛrɛ wɔ, ama, yɛrɛ yiri, bu bu má ya.

ref: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
text: Haari fa ba ju ni kisa, ahɔɛ ni nzuehɔɛ sukun ya a, ya la má sutura wuraa­wa kpa, bu bita ya bo wɔ, ya kaaki la má deke bo ya da.

ref: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
text: Ya yo ya saa nu juma ni anyum­boro wɔ, ye ya di a. Nzɛn minɛm kpiɛ ya nzukɔ, ya sere awieeri kpa ma bu wɔ. Nzɛn bu kere ya wuwuruwa, ya mindi de i wɔ.

ref: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
text: Nzɛn bu saaki ya duma, yɛrɛ jɔjɔ kpa ma bu wɔ. Ya ti ka wura tiɛ bo durunya n nu­fɔm fa gɔ bɔnfɔ su n wɔ, haari ni kisa.

ref: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
text: I ti má ka m koro ka m wura am nyinsɛ dɛɛ ti, ye m sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛm ma am a, ama, am ti m nbaa koroo­wam dɛɛ ti, ye m subɔbɔ am a.

ref: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
text: Dama nzɛn haari má am la kere­fɔm akpii buru mmɔɔ fa kɔ Kristo dɛɛ atin n su, má am la má sim beberebe, mini ti am si a, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ atin n su, labari kpa n bo m kan kereeri am dɛɛ ti.

ref: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye m susunma Timoti am dɔ n a. Yiri ti m wa koroo­wa nahɔrɛ su a, na i kaaki la dabuŋu Mibiɛ n atin n su. I téŋge am su kabo m woo­wa ti Kristo atin n su, ni kabo m kere minɛm ka bu wo barasu, deke kɛrɛ bo m kɔ ni, Kristo jamam n kɛrɛ nu.

ref: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
text: Am nbem sunsun ka m ba má am dɔ kun, nyini ti, ba fa kpirikoro.

ref: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
text: Ama, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ soro su, i cɛ́ má, m bá niɛ am busu, na m bá si nyini minɛm bo bu la kpirikoro ni, má ka bu nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ni ŋgumi, ama, m níɛ kabo bu yiko ti barasu.

ref: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
text: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ niɛ i minɛm su ni, i ti má nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n wɔ, ama, i ti yiko wɔ.

ref: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
text: Ahin jɔrɛ nnyɔm nu, am koro bɔnɔ ɔ? Am koro ka m bá cin am sui, nzɛn sɛ? Wara am koro ka m bá am dɔ bɛnlɛ, ni koro wɔ?

ref: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
text: Ma ti jɔrɛ be, ka daŋgba sa wo am nu, yiri la ka sɔnɔ be bita i si yi da a. Haari bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni mmɔɔ yo má nyini daŋgba sa ni.

ref: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
text: Am ayo nyini sa n ye am toko koro kpiri n a fan? Ye am ahore saaki má n a? Wɔ yo daka ka am yi nyini sɔnɔ n fite am nu.

ref: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
text: Haari ni bo mini wo má am dɔ ni, m awiɛwiɛ wo am dɔ, na sɔnɔ bo i yoori nyini sa ni, ma du mɔ adi i jɔrɛ ajaraki i ya Mibiɛ Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu, kabo má mini ni m tii wo am dɔ na m yo ni. Nzɛn am ba tiɛɛnri am nɔɔ, na m awiɛwiɛ wo am dɔ, na ya Mibiɛ Yesu yiko n wo ya dɔ,

ref: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
text: am fa nyini sɔnɔ n fa wura Sitana saa nu, na i kere i kekereke fa kɔ i adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa n su, adena i awiɛwiɛ n nya tii­de cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ ni bá di minɛm jɔrɛ ni.

ref: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
text: Am yanfɔya bo am nati fɔ i ni, i ti má kpa. Am si má ka ayiri kaan sɔ ma kpɔnɔ n yasu a, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
text: Nyini ti, am ma ya bita Butara cɛɛn­kpiri nyunyumi, na ya yaki satiɛ bo i ti ka ayiri dawa ni, ama, am ma ya di i ka ya ti minɛm bo ya ti casi na ya la nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo i ti ka kpɔnɔ bo ba gɔ má ayiri nu ni.

ref: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
text: Kadasi n bo m yo du mɔ kɛrɛ maari am ni, m seeri am ka, má am ni minɛm bo bu yo daŋgba sam saŋga wo.

ref: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
text: Tɔ, m sukan má durunya n nu­fɔm ni jɔrɛ a, bɛrɛ bo bu nati yo daŋgba sam ni, ni bo bu nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su ni, ni bo bu fin bu beŋgum ni, ni bo bu su amɔɛm ni. Nzɛn má i ti sɔ yiri, ŋka má am fite yaki durunya n wɔ.

ref: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
text: Nzɛn am be ni i beŋgu Kristo­fɔ be nyaari jɔrɛ be, i ti ye ni ka i mi fa jɔrɛ n kɔ Nyɛmɛ minɛm n dɔ, má i se ka i fa kɔ durunya n nu jɔrɛ­di­fɔm bo bu si má Nyɛmɛ n dɔ fieo.

ref: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
text: Am si má ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm bá di durunya n kɛrɛ jɔrɛ a, nzɛn sɛ? Ye nzɛn am bá di durunya n kɛrɛ jɔrɛ, nzu ti ye am koro yo má am ŋuŋu nu jɔrɛ mumukum dɔŋgu a?

ref: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
text: Am si má ka ya bá di haari ni mɛrɛkɛm jɔrɛ? Ye nzu ti ye am koro di má jɔrɛm fa kɔ dekem bo bu wo durunya n nu n su a?

ref: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
text: Ambɛrɛ, nzɛn am la nyini jɔrɛ n be sɔ, ye nzu ti ye am fa kɔ minɛm bo Kristo jama n bu má bu n dɔ a?

ref: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
text: Deke bo m sukan i ni, i ti am nyinsɛ wɔ. Alecira­fɔ haari kun n kpein mmɔɔ wo má am nu bo i koro di am ŋuŋu nu jɔrɛm, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
text: Am si má ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, nzɛn sɛ? Má am dadaka am ŋu, minɛm bo bu yo daŋgba sa koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, wara bo bu su amɔɛm, wara bo bu saaki ajaa ciire, wara nbiɛsɔm bo bu fa bu ŋu kaaki ka nbaram na bu ni bu beŋgu nbiɛsɔm saŋga da ka bara ni biɛsɔ dɛɛ su ni,

ref: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
text: wara awiefɔm, wara bo bu nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su, wara bo bu nu nzan na bu bo, wara bo bu nati kan minɛm jɔrɛ, wara bo bu nati fin minɛm ni, bɛrɛ be mmɔɔ koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

ref: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
text: Am nbem yo ti sɔ ɔ. Ama, Nyɛmɛ aŋmisi ayi am satiɛm ni, na wɔ fa am ayo i dɛɛ minɛ casim, na wɔ de am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo duma n dɛɛ ti, ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n dɛɛ ti. Am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa yo deke bo i ma Nyɛmɛ jirima

ref: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
text: Am nbem se ka, “M la atin bo m koro yo deke kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m yo i,” ama, má deke kɛrɛ la kpɛrɛ a. Mini mmɔɔ koro se ka m la atin bo m koro yo deke kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m yo i, ama, m yaki má na deke be kunma m.

ref: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
text: Nbem gusu ŋa, “Diire ti akunnu dɛɛ wɔ, na akunnu gusu ti diire dɛɛ.” I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ bá ma akunnu n ni diire n kɛrɛ ka ŋgbɛɛn a. Ama, aŋuniɛ­mbaa n yiri, i ti má sakaraya dɛɛ, ama, i ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ wɔ, na Mibiɛ n gusu ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa n dɛɛ.

ref: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
text: Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n nu ye i teŋgeeri Mibiɛ ni i yiwee nu a. I yiko n nu ye i bá teŋge ya gusu a.

ref: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
text: Am si má ka am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n ti Kristo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ye m koro fa Kristo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be yo sakaraya, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, atin wo má nu.

ref: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari i ŋu mataari Mibiɛ n na bu saŋga woori ni, i ni yiri dɛɛ awiɛwiɛ n ayo kun lɛɛ.

ref: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
text: Am ŋmati fite sakaraya nu. Má satiɛ kɛrɛ bo sɔnɔ yo i ye i saaki i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n a. Ama, bo i yo sakaraya ni, i mi saaki i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ.

ref: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
text: Am si má ka am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n ti ka awuru bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo nu n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ fa maari am n a, má am dɛɛ la am ŋu.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
text: Nyɛmɛ ato am atɔ kaari wɔ, nyini ti, am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n fa yo deke bo i ma Nyɛmɛ jirima.

ref: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
text: Tɔ, jɔrɛ n bo am kɛrɛ maari m ni, am ŋa, “I ti ye ni ka má biɛsɔ si bara.”

ref: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ama, mini dɔ, sakaraya dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka biɛsɔ kɛrɛ la i tiɛŋgu yi, na bara kɛrɛ la i tiɛŋgu hun.

ref: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
text: Má bara n dɛɛ la i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, i ti i hun n dɛɛ wɔ, sɔ ye i kaaki ti a, má biɛsɔ n gusu dɛɛ la i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, i ti i yi n dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text: Má am kete am beŋgu daari, má ka am ayo anɔɔkun ka am fa nyini saŋga kaan sere Nyɛmɛ, nyini sin, na am kaaki saŋga bekun, adena má Sitana yo am niɛ na i nya am, dama am koro tara má am ŋu dɛɛ ti.

ref: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
text: I ti má ka m suma am nɔɔ ka am yaki i wɔ, ama, m suma am atin ka am koro yo sɔ ɔ.

ref: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
text: Kisa m sujɔjɔ ambɛrɛ nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram bo am nya ja má ni dɔ a, ni nkuna­fɔm, nzɛn má am wo kabo m wo ni, na am ja má, i ti kpa.

ref: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
text: Ama, nzɛn am koro tara má am ŋu, am ja. Nzɛn am yo sɔ, i ti ye ni bo saŋga kɛrɛ am la konviki agaya fa kɔ bara ni biɛsɔ dika, na am koro tara má am ŋu ni.

ref: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
text: Ahin ti nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram ajaa­fɔm dɛɛ jɔrɛ ye m sukan i n a, má mini suse a, ama, i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa a, ka má bara yaki i hun ajaa.

ref: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
text: Minɛm bo ba ka ni, m dɛɛ fɔ ye m sufa tu am n a, má Nyɛmɛ seeri sɔ a. Nzɛn Kristo­fɔ be yi ti má yarada­yo­fɔ, na wɔ soro su ka i tana i hun n ajaa bu, má i yaki i ajaa, i bita i.

ref: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
text: Nzɛn bara be ti Kristo­fɔ, na i hun ti má yarada­yo­fɔ, na biɛsɔ n asoro su ka i ni i yi n saŋga tana, má bara n yaki i ajaa, i tana.

ref: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
text: Ama, nzɛn bo i ti má yarada­yo­fɔ n kete ka i ja má, yaki i na i kɔ, nyini saŋga, Kristo­fɔ n la má ŋgasi be. Nyɛmɛ afere ya ka ya wo ni tana­laifiɛ wɔ.

ref: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
text: Wɔrɔ bara ni, beni, wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, u hun ni nyá tii­de, na wɔrɔ biɛsɔ n gusu, beni, wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, u yi ni nyá tii­de. Kabo ya yo ti na Nyɛmɛ fereeri ya ni, ya ti sɔ

ref: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
text: Tɔ, am ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wo kabo Mibiɛ n kereeri i saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ fereeri i ni. Deke kɛrɛ bo m kɔ Kristo jamam n nu, kabo m kere bu ka bu yo lɛɛ.

ref: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
text: I ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wo kabo i yo ti barasu na Nyɛmɛ nya ba fereeri i ni.

ref: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
text: Nzɛn a ti akɔɔ, ye Nyɛmɛ afere u, má a ma i yo u yaa, ama, nzɛn a si ka a koro nya atin na a de u tii, a koro yo sɔ.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
text: Nyɛmɛ ato am atɔ kaari wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, má am yo adamandem akɔɔm.

ref: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
text: Fa kɔ minɛm bo ba nya aja má ajaa n dɔ ni, m la má keree­wa bo i fite Mibiɛ n dɔ fa kɔ nyini n dika, ama, Mibiɛ n asi m aŋunfɔ na m la dabuŋu n dɛɛ ti, ye m koro ka m se am deke bo i wo m sunsunni nu n a.

ref: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
text: Miɛ n bo ya wo nu kisa n dɛɛ ti, mini dɔ, nzɛn má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro wo i wo, má i ti kpa.

ref: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
text: Nzɛn a du mɔ aja bara, má a kpini atin na a yaki ajaa ni. Nzɛn a nya ja má gusu, má a kpini atin na a ja.

ref: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
text: Nzɛn a ti biɛsɔ ye a ja bara, a yo má satiɛ, nzɛn a ti suŋguru gusu ye a ja biɛsɔ, a yo má satiɛ. Ama, wahara n bo ajaaja­fɔm di i durunya n nu n ye m koro má ka am di i be a.

ref: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
text: M ninbaam, deke bo m suse n la ka, saŋga n waari má kun. Nyini ti, fa kisa fa kɔ, bo bu la nbaram ni, i ti ye ni ka bu wo ka bu la má be.

ref: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
text: Na bo bu sun ni, ma bu wo ka bu sun má, na bo bu ahore ajɔ ni, ma bu wo ka bu ahore ajɔ má, na bo bu to neŋgem ni, ma bu wo ka má bu dɛɛ la neŋge ni.

ref: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
text: Na bo bu fa durunya n nu neŋgem fa yo bu jumam ni, ma bu wo ka bu cɛcɛ wo má bu nu, dama ahin durunya n ni deke kɛrɛ bo i wo nu n ti siin­wa a.

ref: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ bo wɔ ja bara, i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo durunya n nu dɛɛ dekem ni su a, i dɛɛ la ka, i yo sa bo i sun i yi nu wɔ,

ref: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
text: nyini ti, i sunsunni ti nnyɔ nnyɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Na bara bo i ja má, wara suŋguru bo i si má biɛsɔ, i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo Mibiɛ n juma ni su a, adena i wo casi i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni i awiɛwiɛ nu. Ama, bara bo i la hun, i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo durunya n nu dɛɛ dekem ni su a, i dɛɛ la ka, i yo sa bo i sun i hun nu wɔ.

ref: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
text: M koro ka m buka am wɔ, nyini ti ye m se sɔ n a, i ti má ka m koro ka m kanda am atin wɔ. Ama, m koro ka am yo sa kɛrɛ bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, na am fa am ŋu ma Mibiɛ n nahɔrɛ su, na am su i nyunyumi.

ref: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
text: Ama, nzɛn suŋguru be wo bɛrɛ na wɔ nya si má biɛsɔ, na ba ce i, na i ni biɛsɔ n kɛrɛ ayo bu sunsunni nu ka bu ja má, ama, kasin na biɛsɔ n ba ŋu ka i koro tara má i ŋu, na bara n aju ajaa, i koro ja i, i ti má satiɛ.

ref: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jaari i suŋguru cee­wa ni, nyini ti kpa, ama, bo i ja má i fieo ni, i mi yo sakpa tara bo i ja i ni.

ref: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
text: Nzɛn bara ni i hun wo bɛrɛ, na biɛsɔ n nya wu má, bara n la má atin bo i fite i ajaa n bu, ama, nzɛn i hun wuuri, i la atin bo i kɔ ja sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro, ama, wɔ yo daka ka i mi ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ sɔnɔ.

ref: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
text: M dɛɛ sunsunni nu, nzɛn má i wo sɔ na i ja má, má i la tere­fiɛ agaya tara bo i ja. M niɛ ka mini mmɔɔ ni m tii la Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni.

ref: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
text: Tɔ, fa kɔ dekem bo bu kpiɛ bu kɔm yo tiɛ fa ma amɔɛm dɛɛ jɔrɛ, ya si ka ya kɛrɛ la si. Ama, si ka nyini n sɔ ma minɛ wura bu ŋu kpiri wɔ, ama, koro yiri, i buka sɔnɔ ɔ, na i woo­wa ti kpa.

ref: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro Nyɛmɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ si yiri wɔ.

ref: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
text: Nyini ti, fa kɔ kabo minɛm di amɔɛ tiɛ diirem ni, ya si ka amɔɛ ti má pui durunya n nu, na nyɛmɛ be wo má bɛrɛ sin ya Nyɛmɛ ni ŋgumi kpein ŋu.

ref: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
text: Haari nzɛn dekem bo minɛm ŋa bu ti amɔɛm wo aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu fɛn, na nzɛn i ti nahɔrɛ ka amɔɛm ni mibiɛm dɔŋgu wo bɛrɛ mmɔɔ,

ref: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
text: yɛrɛ dɔ, Nyɛmɛ kun n kpein wo bɛrɛ a, yiri la ya Si Nyɛmɛ, yiri yo yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, ya wo bɛrɛ ma yiri wɔ. Ya kaaki la Mibiɛ kun n kpein wɔ, yiri la Yesu Kristo, Nyɛmɛ ma yiri yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, i dɛɛ ti ye ya wo bɛrɛ a.

ref: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
text: Ama, má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la nyini si n a, nbem wo bɛrɛ bo ba su amɔɛm acɛ, nyini ti, haari ni kisa nzɛn bu diiri nyini diire n be, bu sunsun ka bu toko di amɔɛ tiɛ niɛ wɔ, na bu sunsunni suma bu nnyɔ nnyɔ ɔ, ka bu ti má casi.

ref: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
text: Nyɛmɛ de má ya diire dɛɛ ti, nzɛn ya diiri i, i buka má ya pui, nzɛn ya di má i gusu, i kpiɛ má ya su.

ref: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
text: Nzɛn sɔnɔ bo i sunsunni n ti nnyɔ nnyɔ n ŋu ka wɔrɔ bo a la nyini si n sɔ wo amɔɛ awuru n nu sudi amɔɛ diire be, nyini ma i ahoresi ka i di be na i niɛ a, má sɔ? Ɛɛn, i mi dí ɔ.

ref: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn diire bo m di i, na i ma m niwa Kristo­fɔ bo i jaa kpaasi, m dí má niɛ bekun fieo, adena má m niwa n bo i jaa kpaasi.

ref: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
text: Haari nzɛn nyiŋgam de má m ka m ti jaramasa, ambɛrɛ si ka m ti i jaramasa a, dama kabo am ni Mibiɛ n ayo kun ni, nyini kere am ka m ti i jaramasa a.

ref: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
text: Ya la má atin ka nzɛn ya sukɔ atin, ya koro fa ya yim bo bu ti yarada­yo­fɔm bita nu, kabo ya beŋgu Kristo jaramasa nbem yo ni, ni kabo ya Mibiɛ niɛmam, ni Kefasi bo bu kaaki fere i ka Petoro gusu yo ni, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
text: Wara m ni Banabasi ŋgumi ye i ti ye ni ka ya yo ya saa nu juma ka, na ya nya di a?

ref: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
text: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa maari Moyisi n nu ka, “Nzɛn a bita toora subobo ŋgani, má a cici i nɔɔ.” Tɔ, naanim ŋgumi ye Nyɛmɛ cɛcɛ wo bu nu a, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má bɛrɛ ŋgumi.

ref: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
text: Nahɔrɛ, ya ti ka dɔdɔɔri­fɔm wɔ, yɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye bu kɛrɛɛri sɔ a, adena bo i dɔ ŋgani n ni bo i bobo i n kɛrɛ la tama ka bu nyá be na bu kpáaki nu.

ref: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
text: Yɛrɛ kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni kereeri am a, kabo dɔdɔɔri­fɔ dɔ alɛ­mbaa ni, na ambɛrɛ ade i. Ye nzɛn i ti sɔ, yɛrɛ la má atin bo ya koro nya deke be am dɔ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
text: Nzɛn nyiŋgam la atin ka bu de neŋgem am dɔ, yɛrɛ la má atin bo ya de tara sɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ama, yɛrɛ akere má ya yiko ka ya de nyini n be sɔ, dama ya koro má ka ya kanda minɛm, na má bu de Kristo labari kpa ni.

ref: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
text: Am si má ka, minɛm bo bu yo juma Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni di nyini nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Na bo bu nati yo juma Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n su ma minɛm gusu di nyini nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
text: Sɔ ye Micɛra n ama nɔɔ a, ka bo bu kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ n nya deke bo bu di i fite labari kpa n kan nu a.

ref: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
text: Ama, haari ni bo má m la atin ka m yo sɔ ni, ma yo má nyini n be sɔ, na m sukɛrɛ má ahin kadasi n ka m de deke be am dɔ ɔ, dama nzɛn m wuuri, má i ti ye tara bo be se ka m fɔ yanfɔya ŋgbɛɛn ni.

ref: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
text: Nzɛn m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛ, m la má atin bo m fɔ, dama i ti m juma bo Nyɛmɛ ama m ka m yo i wɔ. Nzɛn m keteeri ka m yo má i, m nyá sui­cin.

ref: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ m koroya­nu, m nya ŋkatɔ, ama, i ti má m koroya­nu, dama Nyɛmɛ afa nyini juma n ama m ka m yo i a.

ref: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
text: M dɛɛ ŋkatɔ la bɔnɔ? Yiri la ka m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ fa kere minɛm ŋgbɛɛn, m la atin bo m sere deke be bu dɔ, ama, m yo má i.

ref: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
text: Haari ni bo ma de m tii na m ti má be dɛɛ akɔɔ ni, m fa m ŋu yo akɔɔ ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ, adena m koro nya minɛ dɔŋgu na bu su Kristo su.

ref: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
text: Nzɛn m kaaki kɔɔri minɛm bo bu la má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɔ, m yo kabo bu yo n wɔ, i ti má ka m su má kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere n su wɔ, ama, Kristo dɛɛ nɔaniɛ ni niɛ m su a, adena m koro nya minɛm bo bu la má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n na bu nbem su Kristo su.

ref: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
text: M yo nyini n kɛrɛ sɔ ɔ, labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, adena m ni nyiŋgam kɛrɛ saŋga di i kpɛrɛ.

ref: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, mini, m ŋmati má ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn ka sɔnɔ bo i si má bo i wo nu ni. M ti má sɔnɔ bo m bo acɔrɛ na m bo fun.

ref: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
text: Ama, m kere m ŋuniɛ n wahara a, na i yo kabo m koro ni, adena má m yo sɔnɔ bo m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm, na mini mmɔɔ fun aceeri ni.

ref: This was like being baptized and becoming followers of Moses.
text: Kabo i yoori sɔ ni, i yo ti ka ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, afa bu ama Moyisi wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
text: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, bu dɔŋgu sa­yoo­wa asun má Nyɛmɛ nu, nyini dɛɛ ti na ba wu aka ŋgbɛɛn boro kpantain n nu.

ref: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
text: Tɔ, ahin sam kɛrɛ bo bu yo yoori ni, bu ti deke bo bu sufa kere ya a, ka má ya sufa konviki, na ya su satiɛm su, kabo bu yo yoori ni.

ref: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
text: Na má am su amɔɛm kabo bu nbem yo yoori ni. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Minɛm n yo tanaari wɔ, na ba di, na ba nu, na ba nya ayasu agɔ agɔrɛ.”

ref: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
text: Má am ma ya yo sakaraya kabo bu nbem yo yoori, na cɛɛn kun nu na bu minɛm kɔ akpii abura­nyɔ ni akpii nsan wuuri ni.

ref: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
text: Má am ma ya yo Mibiɛ n na ya niɛ, kabo bu nbem yo yoori i na bu niɛ, na woom kan kuunri bu ni.

ref: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
text: Má am suŋuŋuti kabo bu nbem yo ŋuŋutiiri na mɛrɛkɛ saaki­fɔ n ba kuunri bu na bu kaari ŋgbɛɛn ni.

ref: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
text: Ahin sam kɛrɛ bo bu yo yoori ni, bu ti deke bo bu sufa kere ya wɔ, nyini ma bu kɛrɛɛri bu seeri n a, na bu fa bɔbɔ yɛrɛ n bo ya wo ahin awieeri su saŋga n nu ni.

ref: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sunsun ka i jina nyunyumi, i ti ye ni ka i mi si i ŋu sa, na má i kpaasi.

ref: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
text: Gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ be wo má bɛrɛ bo wɔ tu am, na i be nya tu má adamande. Nyɛmɛ la dabuŋu, i yáki má na gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ bo i kúnma am bá tu am, ama, haari nzɛn gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ be bá tuuri am, i kpíni atin ma am, na i má am koro mindi de i.

ref: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaa koroo­wam, am ŋmati fite amɔɛ su nu.

ref: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
text: M sujɔjɔ am dɔ ka am ti minɛm bo bu nyunu ateke wɔ, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii, am si deke n bo m sujɔjɔ i n bu.

ref: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
text: Nzɛn ya sudi ya Mibiɛ n diire ni, na ya faari awa n ni nzan n na ya yo kisi gɔ su, na ya kɛrɛ saŋga nuuri i, i ti Kristo mbunja wɔ lɛɛ, ye ya nu i n a. Kpɔnɔ n gusu bo ya kpiɛkpiɛ i nu na ya saŋga di i ni, i ti Kristo ŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ lɛɛ, ye ya di i n a.

ref: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
text: Kpɔnɔ n bo ya kɛrɛ saŋga di i n ti mɔmɔ kun wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, yɛrɛ bo ya ti minɛ beberebe ni, ya ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun wɔ lɛɛ, dama ya kɛrɛ saŋga di kpɔnɔ kun n wɔ.

ref: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
text: Am niɛ kabo Yisareli­fɔm yoori ni. Minɛm bo bu di dekem bo bu fa yo yoo­wa Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n su n ni, bu ni Nyɛmɛ la wo fiɛ bo i ma bu yo anɔɔkun, fa kɔ bu nyɛmɛsu atin n su.

ref: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
text: M suse má ka dekem bo bu yo tiɛ n ma bu n wo má bɛrɛ wɔ, wara amɔɛm ni bu tii wo má bɛrɛ. Ai, má sɔ.

ref: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
text: Ama, dekem bo amɔɛ­su­fɔm yo tiɛ ma bu ni, bu yo ma ajinim wɔ, bu yo ma má Nyɛmɛ. M koro má ka am di amɔɛ tiɛ diirem, na am ni ajinim saŋga.

ref: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
text: Nahɔrɛ, m koro ka am si ka am koro nu má ya Mibiɛ ni nzan awa n nu, na am kaaki nu ajinim dɛɛ gusu buka su, nyini koro yo má, na am kaaki koro di má ya Mibiɛ n diire ni, na am kaaki di ajinim dɛɛ buka su, nyini koro yo má,

ref: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
text: wara ya koro ka Mibiɛ n si ya kɔrɛ na i fa yaa ya ŋu wɔ. Ya la faŋga tara i wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, ya lá má faŋga. Ya yo sa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu

ref: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
text: Niɛ kɛrɛ bo naŋkɔsim tɔ i ni, am di i, má am yo sika.

ref: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Durunya n ni deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo nu n ti Micɛra n dɛɛ wɔ.”

ref: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
text: Nzɛn sɔnɔ bo i yo má yarada baandiiri am ka am ba di diire, na am soro su, am di deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa ma am ni, má am kɛrɛ nu, sika dɛɛ ti.

ref: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
text: I mi n dɛɛ sunsunni nnyɔ nnyɔ n dɛɛ ti, má a di i, má u dɛɛ ti a. M dɛɛ la m tii, ye nzu ti ye m ma be dɛɛ sunsunni nnyɔ nnyɔ n di m jɔrɛ, na má m yo kabo m koro a.

ref: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn am diiri diire, wara am nuuri deke nuu­wa, wara deke kɛrɛ bo am yoori i, am yo i na i wura Nyɛmɛ kpiri.

ref: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
text: Má am yo sa bo i ma minɛm bo bu jaa na bu kpaasi, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, haari ni Kristo jama ni.

ref: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
text: Am sɔn m su, na am ma am woo­wa ti kabo m woo­wa ti barasu n na m sɔn Kristo su ni. Tii kata su jɔrɛ fa kɔ nyɛmɛsere su

ref: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
text: Tɔ, m kansi am wɔ, dama saŋga kɛrɛ am nati teŋge m su wɔ, na am bita keree­wam bo m fa kereeri am n ni nahɔrɛ su ni.

ref: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
text: Ama, deke bo m koro ka am si i la ka, biɛsɔ kɛrɛ tii­fɔ la Kristo wɔ, na bara kɛrɛ tii­fɔ la i hun, na Kristo gusu tii­fɔ la Nyɛmɛ.

ref: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
text: Nyini ti, biɛsɔ kɛrɛ bo i susere Nyɛmɛ, wara i sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, na i kata i tii su, i mi awura i tii­fɔ n nyinsɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
text: Na bara kɛrɛ bo i susere Nyɛmɛ, wara i sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, na wɔ kata má i tii su, nyini bara n suwura i tii­fɔ nyinsɛ wɔ lɛɛ. I ti ka wɔ yi i tii wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
text: Nzɛn bara kete ka i kata má i tii su, nyini yiri, i ti ye ni ka i kpiɛkpiɛ i tii ni, ama, nzɛn i ti i nyinsɛ ka i kpiɛkpiɛ i tii, wara i yi i, nyini yiri, i ti ye ni ka i fa deke kata su.

ref: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
text: Nyini ti, i ti má ye ni ka biɛsɔ susere Nyɛmɛ na i fa deke be kata i tii su, dama i ferema ni Nyɛmɛ wɔ, i de Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa a, ama, bara n yiri, i de biɛsɔ n jirima woo­wa a.

ref: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ afa má bara ye i fa yoori biɛsɔ a, ama, i du mɔ yiiri biɛsɔ ɔ ka, na wɔ nya ayi bara afite i ŋuniɛ nu.

ref: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
text: Má bara dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ yoori biɛsɔ a, ama, biɛsɔ dɛɛ ti, ye i yoori bara a.

ref: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka bara cin acici, na i kere ka be la i yiko, mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ ti.

ref: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
text: Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ ni, fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su, bara wo má i ŋgumi, na biɛsɔ gusu wo i ŋgumi.

ref: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
text: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ yo yiiri bara fiteeri biɛsɔ ŋuniɛ nu ni, sɔ ye bara gusu wu biɛsɔ a, ama, deke n kɛrɛ fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

ref: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
text: Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii, am sunsun na am niɛ, i ti kpa ka bara sere Nyɛmɛ na má i cin acici?

ref: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
text: Ama, nzɛn bara la tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin ye i la jirima a, dama Nyɛmɛ ama i tii­ŋmiɛ n ka i fa kata i tii su wɔ.

ref: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
text: Nzɛn be wo am nu na i yo i ŋu ka i se akuruwa fa kɔ kabo m suse ni, m koro ka m se am ka, yɛrɛ, ya su má nyini yoo­wa n su, haari ni deke kɛrɛ bo Kristo jamam tiɛn bu nɔɔ ni, be yo má sɔ. Ya Mibiɛ n diire n jɔrɛ

ref: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
text: Ciciri su, ma ti ka saŋga bo am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ na am su Nyɛmɛ ni, am la kpakpaaki­nu wɔ. Ma yo yarada ka, beni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
text: Nahɔrɛ, wɔ yo daka ka am la kpakpaaki­nu am nu, adena am si bo bu ti yarada­yo­fɔm nahɔrɛ su ni.

ref: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
text: Dama saŋga bo am ba sudi ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ di i tiɛŋgu diire wɔ, be ŋminda má i beŋgu, na ahɔɛ suti nbem, na nbem nu nzan subo.

ref: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
text: Nyini la ka, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ba sudi ahin kpɔnɔ n na am sunu ahin nzan ni, am subo Mibiɛ n yiwee ni ndoori wɔ lɛɛ, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo i bá kaaki ba bekun ni.

ref: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri kpɔnɔ n na i nuuri nzan n na wɔ si má ka ya Mibiɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa n lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ bá di i mi jɔrɛ jaraki i.

ref: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
text: Bu diiri diire n wieeri ni, na wɔ kaaki afa awa n ni nzan ni, na wɔ se bu ka, “Ahin nzan n ti sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ sufa m mbunja n sudi i di wɔ. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ba sunu nyini nzan ni, am teŋge m su.”

ref: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
text: Dama deke n bo mini ni m tii deeri i fiteeri Mibiɛ n dɔ ni, nyini ye ma fa akere am n a. Kɔŋguɛ n bo bu tɔɔri ya Mibiɛ Yesu maari i kpɔ­fɔm ni, na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ,

ref: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
text: na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ bubu i nu, na wɔ se bu ka, “Ahin kpɔnɔ n ti m ŋuniɛ­mbaa lɛɛ ye m sufa ma am n a. Am yo ahin sɔ adena am fa teŋge m su.”

ref: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri ya Mibiɛ n kpɔnɔ ni, wara i nuuri i nzan ni, na i bu má i, Nyɛmɛ dí i mi jɔrɛ jaraki i, dama wɔ fa má ya Mibiɛ ŋuniɛ n ni i mbunja n ayo má juma.

ref: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
text: Wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ niɛ i ahore­mbaa su ka, na i nya di ahin kpɔnɔ ni, na i nya nu nzan ni.

ref: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye am nu, minɛ dɔŋgu ayɔrɔki ada tukpaki, haari nbem awu n a.

ref: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
text: Ama, nzɛn Mibiɛ n fiiti ya na i cin ya sui, i yo sɔ ɔ adena i di má ya jɔrɛ na i yo ya ŋgbɛɛn, cɛɛn n bo i bá di durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ ni.

ref: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaam, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ, na am di, am kɛrɛ ŋminda am beŋgu, na am saŋga di.

ref: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
text: Nzɛn ahɔɛ ati be, i ti ye ni ka i mi di diire awuru ka, na i nya ba, adena nzɛn am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ, na má Nyɛmɛ di am jɔrɛ cin am sui. Jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo wɔ ka ni, nzɛn m ba baari, m yó i dɔŋgu.

ref: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
text: Am si ka saŋga bo am yo su má Nyɛmɛ ni, bu yo taari am nyi wɔ, na am susu amɔɛm bo bu koro jɔjɔ má ni.

ref: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
text: Nyini ti, m koro ka am si ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo i dɔ, i mi koro se má ka, “Yesu aka ŋgbɛɛn,” na be gusu koro se má ka “Yesu ti m Mibiɛ,” má ka Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo i mi dɔ.

ref: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
text: Aceerim barasu barasu wo bɛrɛ, ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ti kun wɔ.

ref: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
text: Amusuim bo ya yo buka minɛm wo bɛrɛ barasu barasu, ama, ya su Mibiɛ kun wɔ.

ref: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la atin barasu barasu kabo i mi koro fa yo i juma, ama, Nyɛmɛ kun ni yo nyini n kɛrɛ a.

ref: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
text: Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma sɔnɔ be jɔjɔ alecira jɔrɛ a, nyini Awiɛwiɛ ni ma be gusu jɔjɔ si jɔrɛ a.

ref: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
text: Nyini Awiɛwiɛ ni ma be yiko ye i koro yo alekutura sam a, yiri ma be yiko ye i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm a, yiri ma be yiko ye i koro si jɔrɛ n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ a, yiri ma be yiko ye i koro jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu a, yiri gusu ma be yiko ye i koro kere aniɛ barasu barasum bu a.

ref: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
text: Nyini Awiɛwiɛ kun ni yo nyinim kɛrɛ a, yiri ce sɔnɔ kɛrɛ aceeri barasu barasu n kabo i koro a. Aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun n ni i bue barasu n jɔrɛ

ref: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
text: Awiɛwiɛ kun n ye ba fa abiɛ ya kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue a, ye ya kɛrɛ ayo aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, bo bu ti akɔɔm, ni bo bu ti má akɔɔm, ya kɛrɛ ade nyini Awiɛwiɛ kun ni ya ahore su a.

ref: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
text: Tɔ, aŋuniɛ­mbaa n la bue barasu barasu beberebe wɔ, bu ti má kun.

ref: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
text: Nzɛn má ajaari n seeri ka, “M ti má asaa, nyini ti, m ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n be fieo,” nyini kere má ka i ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be kun wɔ.

ref: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
text: Na nzɛn má asui n gusu seeri ka, “M ti má anyumbaa, nyini ti, m ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n be fieo,” nyini kere má ka i ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be kun wɔ.

ref: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
text: Nzɛn má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue n kɛrɛ tiiri anyumbaa, má bu yo sɛ koro ti deke ŋga a? Ye nzɛn má bu kɛrɛ tiiri asui, má bu yo sɛ koro de nvan a?

ref: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
text: Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro n ye wɔ fa ama aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasu n kɛrɛ a, ye wɔ yo i aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun a.

ref: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
text: Nzɛn má i kɛrɛ ti bue barasu kun, má i ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa lɛɛ.

ref: It takes many parts to make a single body.
text: Kabo i ti ni, aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasu n sunnu, ama, i ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun wɔ.

ref: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
text: Nyini ti, anyumbaa n koro se má asaa n ka, “Wɔrɔ, a kun má m kpɛrɛ be, m koro má u.” Wara atii n koro se má ajaari n ka, “Wɔrɔ, a kun má m kpɛrɛ be, m koro má u.”

ref: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
text: Ama, haari aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasum n bo ya ŋa bu la má faŋga ni mmɔɔ kun kpɛrɛ wɔ.

ref: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
text: Na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue n bo bu wo fofoe nu ni, ya niɛ má bu dika nyunyumi. Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ yoori aŋuniɛ­mbaa n ni, deke n bo yɛrɛ fa má i yo má pui ni, yiri ye wɔ wura i jirima a,

ref: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
text: adena má i la kpakpaaki­nu, ama, na bue barasu n kɛrɛ cɛcɛ wo i beŋgu nu.

ref: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
text: Nzɛn aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue kun diiri yaari, aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue n kɛrɛ sudi yaari n a, ye nzɛn aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be sunya kansi, aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue n kɛrɛ ahore ti jɔɔ­wa wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
text: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ ti Kristo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ, na am kɛrɛ kun kun n ti i bue barasu be wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti jaramasa a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan kere minɛm a? Wara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro kere a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la yiko koro yo alekutura sam a? Ai, i ti má sɔ.

ref: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la yiko koro tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro yi aniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa barasu barasu n bu a? Ai.

ref: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
text: Ama, am kɛrɛ boro am nyi na am nya aceerim n bo bu la má aŋusu ni. Kisa, m koro ka m kere am atin n bo i ti kpa tara i kɛrɛ ni.

ref: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text: Nzɛn m jɔjɔ adamande aniɛ barasu barasu n kɛrɛ, na nzɛn m jɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛm aniɛ, na m koro má minɛm, m ti ka adaŋwore wɔ, wara m ti ka coco bo i bobo i beŋgu na i yo naŋmiɛ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
text: Na nzɛn m faari m neŋgem kɛrɛ fa kpaaki nu maari yaari­fɔm, na m faari m ŋuniɛ­mbaa fa maari, na bu yara m sin, na m koro má minɛm, nyini n kɛrɛ bo m yo i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
text: Ama, nzɛn Kristo ba baari, nyini saŋga deke n bo ya si má i kɛrɛ ni aba i awieeri lɛɛ.

ref: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
text: Saŋga bo m yo ti baatuma ni, m yo jɔjɔ baatumaya jɔrɛ wɔ, na m sunsun kabo baatuma sunsun ni, ama, m nyunu ba tiiri ni, na ma yaki baatumaya jɔrɛm n kɛrɛ.

ref: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
text: Kisa dekem bo ya ŋu i ni, i ti ka ya niɛ ŋu i niɛɛnu nu wɔ, ama, ya bá ŋu i fofoe nu, kabo ya ŋu ya ŋu n wɔ. Kisa m si má deke n kɛrɛ, ama, saŋga suba bo m bá si deke n kɛrɛ, kabo Nyɛmɛ si m nahɔrɛ su ni.

ref: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
text: Tɔ, kisa ahin deke nsanm ni wo bɛrɛ a, bɛrɛ la yarada, ni tama, ni koro, ama, bo i tara bu kɛrɛ n la koro wɔ.

ref: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
text: Tɔ, am bita koro atin na am koro am beŋgu ni anyum­boro, na am kpini Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim busu, maala bo i ma am koro kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni.

ref: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
text: Dama bo i jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu n jɔjɔ má adamandem dɔ, ama, i jɔjɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ a. Nahɔrɛ, be koro ti má kabo i sujɔjɔ n bu, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko n nu ye i jɔjɔ asiri nu jɔrɛm a.

ref: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, i jɔjɔ ma minɛm wɔ, na i buka bu na bu nya faŋga buka su, na i ma bu ahoresi, na i kaaki ma bu ahoreda.

ref: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
text: Tɔ, nzɛn má am kɛrɛ koro jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu, má i yo m fiɛ. Ama, bo i yo m fiɛ kɔ tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, am koro kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm. Sɔnɔ bo i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm n tara bo i jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum ni, má ka be akoro akere nyini aniɛ barasu barasum bu, na i buka Kristo jama n na i nya faŋga.

ref: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text: M ninbaam, nzɛn kisa na m baari am dɔ, na m jɔjɔɔri aniɛ barasu barasum, nzu kpɛrɛ ye am nyá i a? Ai, am nyá má kpɛrɛ be. Má ka ma kan sa bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i akere m n ama am, wara m kan si jɔrɛ be kere am, wara m kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere am, wara m kan keree­wa bo i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ kere am.

ref: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
text: Sɔ ye i ti a, nzɛn be fitaari tere, wara be bo biɛku, na wɔ tɔ má su, bu yo sɛ koro di jue soro su a?

ref: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
text: Bekun nzɛn be kaanri luɛ awiɛ, na wɔ sun má kabo luɛ awiɛ sun ni, bu yo sɛ koro cici luɛ a?

ref: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
text: I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ dɔ, nzɛn am jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu, na i la má bu, minɛ yo sɛ koro si kabo am suse n a? I ti ka am sujɔjɔ gɔ nzue awa sin wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
text: Tɔ, nyini ti, nzɛn be jɔjɔ aniɛ be na m ti má i, m ti daanfiɛ barasu be wɔ lɛɛ i mi dɔ, ye i gusu ti daanfiɛ barasu be wɔ lɛɛ m dɔ.

ref: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
text: Kabo am nyunu aboro ka am nyá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim ni, am boro am nyi ni aceerim bo bu koro buka Kristo jama n na i nya faŋga buka su.

ref: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum, i ti ye ni ka i sere Nyɛmɛ na i ma i aceeri n bo i koro yi i bu kere minɛm ni.

ref: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
text: Dama saŋga kɛrɛ bo m sere Nyɛmɛ na m sere aniɛ barasu barasu be nu, m awiɛwiɛ susere Nyɛmɛ a, ama, m ti má i bu m sunsunni nu.

ref: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
text: Nyini ti, deke bo i ti ye ni ka m yo la ka, m sere Nyɛmɛ fite m awiɛwiɛ nu wɔ, na m kaaki sere i fite m sunsunni nu, na m di jue fite m awiɛwiɛ nu kansi Nyɛmɛ, na m kaaki di jue fite m sunsunni nu kansi Nyɛmɛ.

ref: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
text: Nzɛn a sukansi Nyɛmɛ na i fite u awiɛwiɛ nu, na be wo u bi bo i nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin ni, i mi yo sɛ koro ti u nyɛmɛsere n bu a, na i soro su se ka, “Ami” a?

ref: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
text: Haari nzɛn u nyɛmɛsere ti kpa, i buka má yiri n bo i ti má i bu ni.

ref: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
text: M da Nyɛmɛ asi ka m jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum tara am kɛrɛ.

ref: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
text: Ama, nzɛn m ni Kristo jama n aba atiɛn ya nɔɔ susu Nyɛmɛ, na m jɔjɔɔri jɔrɛ kaan bo i fite m sunsunni nu, na bu tiiri i bu, i tara bo m jɔjɔ jɔrɛ beberebe aniɛ barasu nu ni.

ref: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
text: M ninbaam, má am sunsun kabo nbaatumam sunsun ni, ama, fa kɔ satiɛ­yo dika, am yo kabo nbaatumam yo ni, na am ma am sunsunni ti kabo anyunuti­fɔm dɛɛ ti ni.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu ka, Micɛra n ŋa, “M fá minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ daanfiɛ aniɛ barasu barasum na m má bu jɔjɔ m tiɛŋgu minɛm dɔ. Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ, bu dé má m nɔaniɛ ni.”

ref: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
text: Nyini ti, aniɛ barasu barasum jɔjɔɔ­wa n kere minɛm bo ba nya yo má Kristo yarada ni nyaari a, má bo ba yo yarada ni. Na bo bu kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, bu kere bo ba yo yarada ni nyaari a, má bo bu yo má yarada ni.

ref: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n atiɛn am nɔɔ, na am kɛrɛ sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum, na bo bu nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin ni, wara bo bu nya yo má yarada wuraari am nu, bu sé ka am nyunu akan am wɔ, má sɔ?

ref: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
text: Ama, nzɛn am atiɛn am nɔɔ, na am kɛrɛ sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na bo i nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin ni, wara, bo i ti má yarada­yo­fɔ aba awura am nu, na i ti kabo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sujɔjɔ ni, i mi n sunsunni nu kere i ka i ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n di i jɔrɛ jaraki i.

ref: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
text: Nyini ma i mi n kan asiri jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo i ahore su n fite fofoe nu a, na i kutu i jaa su, na i kansi Nyɛmɛ, na i se ka, “Nyɛmɛ wo am dɔ nahɔrɛ su.” Kabo i ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tara i ŋu Kristo jama n nu barasu ni

ref: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
text: Nzɛn nbem ba sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum, i ni i agaya kɛrɛ, bu yo minɛ nnyɔ, wara minɛ nsan, má bu tara sɔ. Bu ŋa bu jɔjɔ, bu jɔjɔ kun kun, i ti ye ni ka be gusu kaaki yi i bu kere am.

ref: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
text: Dama am kɛrɛ koro kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kun kun kere am beŋgu wɔ, adena am kɛrɛ sɔnsɔn deke be nu, na i ma am ahoresi.

ref: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ ti má bo i fa kpakpaaki­nu ba i minɛm dɔ, ama, i ma bu la tana­laifiɛ wɔ. I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, Kristo jama n kɛrɛ nu,

ref: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
text: Nzɛn bu koro ka bu bisa jɔrɛ be, i ti ye ni ka bu bisa bu hunm awuru, dama i ti nyinsɛ wɔ ka bara jɔjɔ Kristo jama n nu.

ref: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n du mɔ fite ambɛrɛ dɔ a, nzɛn sɛ? Wara ambɛrɛ ŋgumi ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a?

ref: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
text: Nzɛn be sunsun ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ, wara i sunsun ka i si Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim jɔrɛ, i ti ye ni ka i mi si tenle su ka ahin nɔaniɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, i ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa a.

ref: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
text: Nzɛn be wo am nu, na i de má ahin jɔrɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bu i mi ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
text: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, i ti ye ni ka am boro am nyi na am sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, na má am kanda minɛm ka má bu jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum ni.

ref: But do everything properly and in order.
text: Na am yo deke kɛrɛ nyunyumi na i kɔ atin su.

ref: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
text: Nzɛn am bitaari labari kpa n bo m kan kereeri am kenken, nyini ma am tii­de a, nzɛn má nyini yiri, am yarada n bo am ayo i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
text: Dama labari kpa n bo m deeri i na m kan kereeri am ni, i jɔrɛ anyumbaa la ka, Kristo wuuri ya satiɛ ti wɔ, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu lɛɛ,

ref: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
text: ka i wuuri na bu seeri i ni, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i yiwee n nu i le­san cɛɛn, kabo Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kere lɛɛ.

ref: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
text: Nyini sin, na wɔ yi i ŋu akere ya ninbaam nbem, bu tara minɛ yaa nnu, bu kɛrɛ saŋga wo woo­biri kun wɔ. Bu nu, bu dɔŋgu toko la ŋgɔɔ, nbem gusu awu.

ref: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
text: Na wɔ yi i ŋu akere Yakobo, na wɔ ba akaaki ayi i ŋu akere i jaramasam kɛrɛ.

ref: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
text: Awieeri su, na wɔ yi i ŋu akere mini, bo m ti ka baa bo ba wu i na i saŋga nya ju má ni.

ref: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
text: Dama mini ti jaramasam kɛrɛ nu kaan, haari má wɔ ti má ye ni ka bu fere m jaramasa mmɔɔ, dama m yo kereeri Kristo jama n wuwuruwa wɔ.

ref: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
text: Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye m ti kabo m ti n a. Ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama m ni, i ti má ŋgbɛɛn, ama, m koro se ka ma yo juma atara nyiŋgam kɛrɛ mmɔɔ. Má mini ni m tii yoori i a, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama m ni, nyini ma m koro yoori i a.

ref: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
text: Nyini ti, mini, wara bɛrɛ o, labari kpa n ye ya kan i a, labari kpa n gusu dɛɛ ti ye am yoori yarada n a. Kabo Nyɛmɛ minɛm bá teŋge yiwee nu ni

ref: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
text: Tɔ, labari kpa n bo ya kan i ni, ya ŋa, “Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu.” Ye nzu ti ye am nbem ŋa yiwee teŋge wo má bɛrɛ a?

ref: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
text: Nzɛn yiwee teŋge wo má bɛrɛ, nyini la ka, Nyɛmɛ ateŋge má Kristo mmɔɔ i yiwee n nu lɛɛ.

ref: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
text: Má nyini ŋgumi, nyini la ka, ya bu kɔrɛ asɔ Nyɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ, dama ya ŋa wɔ teŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu, ama, bu ŋa bo ba wu n teŋge má yiwee nu.

ref: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
text: Na nzɛn bo ba wu n teŋge má bu yiwee nu, nyini la ka, wɔ teŋge má Kristo mmɔɔ i yiwee n nu lɛɛ.

ref: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
text: Na nzɛn wɔ teŋge má Kristo i yiwee n nu, yarada n bo am ayo i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ, na am toko wo am satiɛm nu wɔ lɛɛ,

ref: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
text: na bo bu yo yoori Kristo yarada na ba wu n kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ.

ref: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
text: Nzɛn durunya n nu fɛn ŋgumi ye ya la tama n fa kɔ Kristo dɔ, nyini yiri, ya ti minɛm bo ya la aŋunfɔ tara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

ref: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
text: Ama, nahɔrɛ su, Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu. Minɛm bo ba wu n kɛrɛ nu, yiri tiiri bo i du mɔ teŋgeeri i yiwee nu a.

ref: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
text: Adamande kun dɛɛ ti, ye yiwee kun sɔnɔ kɛrɛ n a, adamande kun gusu dɛɛ ti, ye minɛ bá teŋge bu yiwee n nu a.

ref: Adam brought death to all of us, and Christ will bring life to all of us.
text: Na kabo Adam dɛɛ ti na adamande kɛrɛ wu ni, sɔ ye Kristo dɛɛ ti ma adamande kɛrɛ bá teŋge bu yiwee nu na bu nyá ŋgɔɔ a.

ref: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ni i saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge i. Bo ba wu n kɛrɛ nu, Kristo ti bo Nyɛmɛ du mɔ teŋgeeri i yiwee nu a, na bo bu ti i minɛm n gusu bá teŋge saŋga bo i bá kaaki ba ni.

ref: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
text: Nyini sin na deke n kɛrɛ bá awieeri, na i nyá fa fɛmɛya n wura i Si Nyɛmɛ saa nu, nyini saŋga, wɔ kunma fɛmɛya kɛrɛ, ni nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ, ni yiko­fɔm kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

ref: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
text: Dama wɔ yo daka ka i di fɛmɛya ni haari i bá kunma i kpɔ­fɔm, na i nana bu kɛrɛ ŋu.

ref: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
text: I kpɔ­fɔ n bo i bá kunma i awieeri su n la yiwee wɔ.

ref: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
text: Na saŋga bo i ba faari deke n kɛrɛ wuraari i saa nu ni, na yiri Baa n ni i tii nya fa i ŋu wura i Si Nyɛmɛ saa nu. Nyini saŋga Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ la deke n kɛrɛ, na deke n kɛrɛ wo bɛrɛ yiri ti wɔ.

ref: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
text: Tɔ, nzɛn bu ŋa yiwee teŋge wo má bɛrɛ, ye bo bu de minɛm bo ba wu n jinaa­biri na bu biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue ma bu n di, bu yo sɛ wɔ? Nzɛn bo ba wu n teŋge má bu yiwee nu, nzu ti ye bu biɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue ma bu a?

ref: And why do we always risk our lives
text: Ye nzu ti ye yɛrɛ tɔ ya ŋgɔɔ saŋga kɛrɛ n a?

ref: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
text: Ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, m wo yiwee nu wɔ, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, m ninbaam, m fa ambɛrɛ bo m hoe ye m se sɔ n a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
text: Am kaaki sunsun nyunyumi, na má am yo satiɛ kun, dama am nbem si má Nyɛmɛ. Nyini n bo m se sɔ ni, i ti am nyinsɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Bo bu bá teŋge bu yiwee n nu n dɛɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa n jɔrɛ

ref: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
text: Tɔ, beni, be koro bisa ka, bo ba wu ni, bu teŋge bu yiwee nu sɛ wɔ? Aŋuniɛ­mbaa barasu bɔnɔ ye bu fá ba?

ref: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
text: Wɔrɔ sinnzin ni, a si má ka nzɛn a dɔ deke mbaa, má ka i wu wɔ ka, ye i nya fi a, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
text: Nyɛmɛ ma i kɛrɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa n kabo i koro a, deke mbaa barasu n kɛrɛ, ni i tiɛŋgu aŋuniɛ­mbaa.

ref: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
text: Dekem bo bu wo aŋgoro n wo bɛrɛ, na a yaki bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn ni. Aŋgoro dɛɛm nyumi wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi, na durunya n nu fɛn n dɛɛm gusu nyumi wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi.

ref: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
text: Wiɛ nyumi wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi, sara nyumi wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi, asara­mbaam nyumi wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi, ama, bu nbem yo nyumi tara nbem.

ref: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
text: Tɔ, kabo i ti lɛɛ fa kɔ yiwee teŋge n su. Aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo bu se i ni, i kpɔrɔ ɔ, na bo i teŋge, i kpɔrɔ má fieo.

ref: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
text: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ, ka, “Ciciri su dɛɛ adamande ŋgɔɔ­fɔ n la Adam wɔ,” na Adam n bo i ka sin n ti awiɛwiɛ bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni.

ref: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
text: Bo i ti awiɛwiɛ ni, má yiri du mɔ baari a, ama, bo i ti adamande ŋgɔɔ­fɔ ni du mɔ baari a, na bo i ti awiɛwiɛ n anya tɔ su.

ref: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
text: Ciciri su dɛɛ adamande n fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ, Nyɛmɛ yo faari natiɛ yoori i a, ama, bo i tɔ su nnyɔ su n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro baari a.

ref: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
text: Minɛm bo bu ti durunya n nu­fɔm ni, bu ti kabo sɔnɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ faari natiɛ yoori i n ti n wɔ. Na bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ minɛm n gusu ti kabo sɔnɔ n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro n ti ni.

ref: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
text: Kabo ya ferema ni sɔnɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ faari natiɛ yoori i ni, sɔ ye ya bá ferema ni sɔnɔ n bo i fite aŋgoro n a.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
text: M ninbaam, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i ti niɛ ni mbunja ni, i koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, na deke n bo i kpɔrɔ n koro nya má deke n bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

ref: I will explain a mystery to you. Not every one of us will die, but we will all be changed.
text: Am ti fɛn, m suse am asiri nu jɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má ya kɛrɛ wú a, ama, ya kɛrɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni bá kaaki fɔfɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
text: I yó kungere­kun wɔ, anyumbaa kata ni i teke, saŋga bo bu bá fitaari awieeri su dɛɛ awiɛ ni, na bo ba wu n téŋge bu yiwee nu, bu kpɔ́rɔ má kun, na yɛrɛ gusu ŋuniɛ­mbaa n káaki fɔfɔrɛ.

ref: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
text: Saŋga bo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i kpɔrɔ n bá kpɔrɔ má kun ni, na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i wu n gusu bá wu má kun ni, nyini saŋga Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni yó yii­wa lɛɛ, bo ba kɛrɛ i ka, “Ba di nasara yiwee ŋu ayo i ŋgbɛɛn ni.”

ref: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
text: Deke n bo yiwee bita kan n la satiɛ wɔ, ye satiɛ nya i yiko n fite Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu wɔ.

ref: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
text: Ama, ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, dama ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, i ma ya di nasara.

ref: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaa koroo­wam, am jina kenken na má am fa am ŋu ji, am boro am nyi suyo Mibiɛ n juma n saŋga kɛrɛ, dama am si ka juma n bo am suyo i ma Mibiɛ n ti má ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
text: Tɔ, fa kɔ ŋwaa n bo am yiyi i na am fa kɔ buka Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni, am yo kabo m kereeri Kristo jamam n bo bu wo Galatiya awɔrɛ n nu n ka bu yo ni.

ref: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
text: Yuda­fɔm bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ kɛrɛ mɔnɛ, am niɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ awura am abaraka fa kɔ dekem bo am la bu n su, na am suyiyi ŋwaa be se, adena nzɛn m bá baari, am yi má ŋwaa kun.

ref: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
text: Nyini saŋga na m bá juuri, m kɛ́rɛ kadasi ma minɛm n bo am ayi bu ni, na m fá sunma bu na bu fá am aceerim kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
text: Na nzɛn i ti ye ni ka mini mmɔɔ kɔ, na m ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ kɔ́. Kabo Pɔɔlu koro ka i kɔ niɛ Kɔrinto­fɔm busu ni

ref: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
text: M koro ka m fa Makɛdoniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na m sin ba niɛ am busu.

ref: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
text: Beni, m yó le­nnyɔ am dɔ, wara fufuku ni sín ka, ye m nyá ba yasu a, adena am buka m na m kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m kɔ ni.

ref: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
text: Dama m koro má ka m bá jina aŋgoro niɛ am busu na m sin, m la tama ka nzɛn Mibiɛ n soroori su, m yó le­nnyɔ am dɔ.

ref: I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
text: M wó Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu fɛn haari Butara Cɛɛn­kpiri n saŋga.

ref: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
text: Dama atin bambaka ateke ama m, na m yo juma n nahɔrɛ su, ama, m kpɔ­fɔm gusu asunnu.

ref: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
text: Nzɛn Timoti bá baari am dɔ, am de i ni am saa nnyɔ, má am ma i ahore saaki, yiri mmɔɔ yo Mibiɛ n juma n kabo m yo i n wɔ.

ref: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
text: Má be se ka i bu i ŋgbɛɛn, ama, am kaari i ni ahoreda na i ba tu m, dama m suniɛ i ni ya ninbaam n atin nu ka bu bá.

ref: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
text: Fa kɔ ya niwa Apolosi yiri, ma sere i ni anyum­boro ka i yo aniɛ, na i ni ya ninbaam bá na bu bá niɛ am busu, ama, wɔ soro má su ka i bá kisa, ama, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ ba maari i atin, i bá. Awieeri su jɔrɛ ni ansɛ

ref: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
text: Má am fa am ŋu ji, am jina kenken ni am yarada ni, na am yo nbiɛsɔm, na am fa ahoresi.

ref: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
text: M ninbaam, am si Satɛfanasi ni i dika­fɔm jɔrɛ, bɛrɛ duuri mɔ yoori Kristo yarada Akaya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu a, na ba fa bu ŋu asu Nyɛmɛ minɛm amusui, nyini ti, m sere am wɔ jande,

ref: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
text: ka am bu nyini minɛm bo bu ti sɔ ni, ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu buka su, na bu yo bu juma n ni anyum­boro ni.

ref: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
text: Dama kabo bu baari ni, bu ma m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ anya ahoresi, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka am bu minɛm bo bu ti sɔ ni.

ref: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
text: Kristo jamam bo bu wo Esiya awɔrɛ n nu n kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu. Akuyila, ni i yi Pirisila, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ bu dika n kɛrɛ gusu bisa am dɔŋgu, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
text: Ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n bisa am dɔŋgu. Am gusu bisa am beŋgum dɔŋgu ni koro.

ref: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má Mibiɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi ŋgbɛɛn. Marana ta, i bu la ka, Ya Mibiɛ, bra.

ref: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu buka am kɛrɛ.

ref: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
text: M koro am kɛrɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Ami.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu bo m ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu ni, m ni ya niwa Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma am a. Ya sukɛrɛ i na ya fa ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n bo am wo Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Akaya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na am nya tana­laifiɛ. Kabo Pɔɔlu daari Nyɛmɛ asi ni

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
text: Am ma ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Si ni. Yiri ti ya Si bo i si ya aŋunfɔ a. Yiri ti Nyɛmɛ bo i dada ya ahore a.

ref: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
text: Dama kabo ya ni Kristo susaŋga di i wahara n agaya ni, sɔ ye yiri dɛɛ ti, ye ya nya ahoreda agaya n a.

ref: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya ŋu kekereke, i ma am nya ahoreda ni tii­de wɔ. Na saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya nya ahoreda ni, i ma am ahoreda a, na am yo sawɔrɛ mindi de nyini wahara n bo ya di i n be.

ref: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
text: Ya la tama fa kɔ am dɔ, ya yo má sika. Dama ya si ka kabo am ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ susaŋga di ya wahara n be ni, sɔ ye ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ susaŋga nya ya ahoreda n a.

ref: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
text: M ninbaam, ya koro ka am si kabo ya ŋuuri kekereke Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu barasu ni, ya ŋuuri anyum­boro bo i la má aŋusu, haari na ya yi tama ka ya wu awie.

ref: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
text: Nahɔrɛ, ya sunsun ka ya wu wɔ. Ama, i yo sɔ na má ya daafi ya ŋu su, ama, na ya daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu, dama yiri teŋge bo ba wu n a.

ref: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
text: Wɔ yi ya afite nyini yiwee n bo i la má aŋusu n nu, na i yí ya fite nu bekun. Ya yo yiri tama ka i toko súyi ya fite nu bekun a,

ref: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
text: kabo am buka ya ni am nyɛmɛsere ni. Nyini má minɛ dɔŋgu bá da Nyɛmɛ asi, fa kɔ kabo wɔ de minɛ dɔŋgu nyɛmɛsere n na wɔ yo sakpam ama ya ni. Kabo Pɔɔlu kaakiiri i sunsunni ka i kaaki ba má bu dɔ ni

ref: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
text: Deke n bo ya fa fɔ n la ka, ya sunsunni nu kere ya ka ya bo má anɔɔfiɛ, na ya ahore­mbaa ti casi wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ durunya n nu, maala fa kɔ am dɔ, má ka ya su durunya n nu alecira su n dɛɛ ti a, ama, Nyɛmɛ yo bukaari ya n dɛɛ ti a.

ref: just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
text: dama am adu mɔ asi ya ŋusu jɔrɛ kaan, nyini ma am bá koro fa ya fɔ n a, kabo ya bá fa am fɔ, cɛɛn n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá kaaki ba n a.

ref: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
text: M la ahoresi fa kɔ nyini n su dɛɛ ti, ma sɛsɛ su ka m du mɔ ba niɛ am busu, adena má am nya buka kpe nnyɔ.

ref: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
text: Nyini n bo m sɛsɛɛri su sɔ ni, m la má jinakun­su wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Wara m sɛsɛ su kabo durunya n nu­fɔm n yo n wɔ, na m se ka “Ɛɛn,” na m kaaki se ka “Ai,” nzɛn sɛ?

ref: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
