text: Nyɛmɛ la dabuŋu ni nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti, nɔaniɛm bo ya kan kereeri am ni, i ti má “Ɛɛn,” na i kaaki ti má “Ai”.

ref: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ Wa Yesu Kristo n bo ya kaanri i jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, mini ni Silivanusi ni Timoti, yiri Yesu dɛɛ jɔrɛ ti má “Ɛɛn,” na i kaaki ti má “Ai,” ama, i deke kɛrɛ ti “Ɛɛn” ɔ.

ref: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
text: Dama dekem kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó bu ni, bu kɛrɛ ti “Ɛɛn” ɔ, Yesu dɛɛ ti. Nyini ti, i dɛɛ ti ye saŋga bo ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, ya se ka, “Ami” n a.

ref: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
text: Tɔ, Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ma ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ koro jina kenken a, kabo ya ni Kristo ayo kun ni. Yiri ayi ya ka ya su i n a.

ref: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
text: Nyɛmɛ di m daani m ahore su a, ma koro má ka m kan am ndiɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma kaaki aba má Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu n a.

ref: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
text: I ti má ka ya sumiɛ am kabo am yo yarada n wɔ, ama, ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ buka ya beŋgu wɔ, na am nya ahorejɔ, dama yarada n dɛɛ ti ye am jina kenken n a.

ref: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
text: Nyini ti, na ma yo m sunsunni nu ka m ba niɛ má am busu kun na am ahore saaki.

ref: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
text: Dama nzɛn m ma am ahore saakiiri, ŋmam ma m ahore da a? Má ambɛrɛ bo ma saaki am ahore ni?

ref: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
text: Deke bo i ti na m kɛrɛɛri sɔ n la ka, ma koro má ka m ba na má am ma m ahore saaki, ambɛrɛ bo má i ti ye ni ka am ma m ahore jɔ ni. M la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ, ka deke n bo i ma m ahore jɔ ni, nyini má am gusu kɛrɛ ahore jɔ n a.

ref: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
text: Dama m woori ahoresaaki, ni ahore­mbaa yaari, ni nyunvuin agaya nu, ye m kɛrɛɛri am n a. I ti má ka m koroori ka m saaki am ahore wɔ, ama, m koroori ka am si kabo m koro am agaya barasu n wɔ. Kabo Pɔɔlu ŋa bu fa ce be bo wɔ do ŋgasi ni

ref: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
text: Kabo be ayo ahoresaaki sa ni, má mini ye wɔ saaki m ahore a, ama, wɔ saaki am kɛrɛ ahore a, nzɛn má nyini, wɔ saaki am nu be ahore. M gusu koro má ka m jɔjɔ jɔrɛ n na i kɔ sin su agaya.

ref: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
text: Sui­cin n bo ambɛrɛ minɛ beberebe n kɛrɛ fa kereeri i mi ni, wɔ sɛ i su sɔ.

ref: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
text: Deke bo i ti na m kɛrɛɛri am n la ka, m koro ka m yo am na m niɛ ka am de deke n kɛrɛ bo m se am ka am yo i ni.

ref: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
text: Ya yo sɔ ɔ, adena má Sitana koro kunma ya. Dama ya si tenle su kabo i la ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ barasu ni. Kabo i miɛɛri Pɔɔlu ka i ti bu jɔrɛ Titosi dɔ ni

ref: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
text: Tɔ, m kɔ juuri Tɔrasi miɛ kpiri n nu ka m kan Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere bu ni, na ma ŋu ka Mibiɛ n adu mɔ ateke atin ama m.

ref: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
text: Haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ, m ahore ada má, dama ma ŋu má ya niwa Titosi dɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ma kaari bu, na ma sin akɔ Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu.

ref: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
text: Ama, ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, dama kabo Kristo adi nasara na ya ni yiri ayo kun ni, saŋga kɛrɛ, yiri Nyɛmɛ ma ya nati su Kristo su, i nasara natiiri nu a, na deke kɛrɛ bo ya kɔ ni, yiri fa ya bara yɛ a, na ya ma minɛm si i jɔrɛ, kabo deke nvani bara yɛ deke kɛrɛ ni.

ref: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
text: Bo bu suka ŋgbɛɛn ni, bɛrɛ bu ya ka nva­nvani n bo i sufa yiwee ba bu dɔ ɔ, ama, bo bu sunya tii­de ni, bɛrɛ bu ya ka nva­nvani n bo i suma bu nya ŋgɔɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ. Ahin juma n yiri, ŋma koro fa i tiɛŋgu faŋga yo i a?

ref: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
text: Ya ti má ka minɛ dɔŋgu bo bu bita Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n yo wata na bu di i kpɛrɛ ni, ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti nahɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ, haari na ya ti minɛ bo wɔ yi ya asunma ya wɔ, na ya jina i nyunu ye ya kan i jɔrɛ a, kabo ya ni Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
text: Ya sukaaki kan kere am kabo ya ti na am si ya, nzɛn sɛ? Wɔ yo má daka ka ya yo kabo nbem yo ni, ka ya fa kadasi bo i kere kabo ya ti barasu n bita nu, wara ya de kadasi am dɔ na i kere minɛ nyiŋgam kabo ya ti barasu, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
text: Ai, ya kadasi n la ambɛrɛ ni am tii wɔ, kadasi n bo ba kɛrɛ i awura ya ahore su ni, na minɛ kɛrɛ koro kan i na bu si kabo i ti.

ref: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
text: Na bu koro ŋu ka ambɛrɛ ti kadasi n bo Kristo akɛrɛ awura ya saa nu afa asunma ya n a. Wɔ fa má deke kɛrɛɛ­wa nzue fa kɛrɛɛri i a. Ama, Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ n ye i fa kɛrɛɛri i a. Wɔ kɛrɛ má i agɔ má yabuɛ pɛpɛrɛpɛ su wɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ yo kɛrɛɛri i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari Moyisi ni, ama, i kɛrɛɛri i gɔɔri adamandem ahore su wɔ.

ref: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
text: Deke bo i ti na ya koro jɔjɔ sɔ n la ka, ya la ahoresi Nyɛmɛ dɔ, Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
text: Ya la má deke bo i kere ka yɛrɛ ni ya tii koro yo juma n a. Deke bo i ma ya koro yo i n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

ref: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
text: Yiri Nyɛmɛ ama ya koro ayo i amusui­su­fɔm a, fa kɔ sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo wɔ di i di n su a. Nyini sɛsɛ­su n fite má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo wɔ kɛrɛ i n dɔ, ama, i fite i Awiɛwiɛ n dɔ ɔ. Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kɛrɛɛ­wa n fa yiwee ba, ama, Awiɛwiɛ n ma ŋgɔɔ ɔ.

ref: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
text: Tɔ, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ yo kɛrɛɛri i gɔɔri yabuɛ pɛpɛrɛpɛ n su na i fa maari Moyisi ni, nyini saŋga, i yoori sɔ ni, i jirima bambaka n taari wɔ, na Yisareli­fɔm n akoro aniɛ má Moyisi nyunu­awa dika, dama i nyunu­awa taari wein agaya, haari ni bo i ba saari wɔ. Kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa yiwee ba n ti n na Nyɛmɛ boori i juma n bu ni jirima bambaka n sɔ ni,

ref: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
text: i Awiɛwiɛ ni yó i dɛɛ juma n ni jirima bambaka bo i tara nyini ni, má sɔ?

ref: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
text: Nahɔrɛ, deke n bo i yo la jirima bambaka ni, i la má jirima kun, deke n bo i la jirima bambaka tara sɔ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
text: Kabo deke n bo i ti siin­wa n ti n na i yo la jirima bambaka sɔ ni, nyini yiri, deke n bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ jirima bambaka n tara sɔ.

ref: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
text: Yɛrɛ ti má ka Moyisi bo i yo faari sanvuin kataari na má Yisareli­fɔm ŋu i nyunu­awa bo i ta wein na i susaari ni.

ref: The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
text: Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni, bu sui yo tiiri tan­tanantan a. Haari ni nyuma, saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu ti ka be sukan sɛsɛ­su dawa n dɛɛ kadasim ni, i yoori bu ka nyini sanvuin n toko kata bu sunsunni su wɔ, má ka Kristo ŋgumi koro yi i a.

ref: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
text: Tɔ, deke n bo bu kɛrɛɛri i ka “Mibiɛ n” ni, bu kɛrɛ niiri i Awiɛwiɛ n wɔ. Deke bo Mibiɛ n Awiɛwiɛ n wo ni, dɔ ye minɛ nya bu tii a.

ref: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
text: Nyini ti, kabo Nyɛmɛ asi ya aŋunfɔ na wɔ fa ahin juma n ama ya ni, ya ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki má ya.

ref: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
text: Yɛrɛ, ya kete deke kɛrɛ bo i ti nyinsɛ sa na bu kata i su asiri nu ni, ya dadaka má be, ya kaaki má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni. Ama, ya kan nahɔrɛ n fofoe nu wɔ, nyini ma ya fa kere sɔnɔ kɛrɛ na i si i sunsunni nu kabo ya wo ti Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

ref: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
text: Na haari nzɛn deke be akata labari kpa n bo ya kan i n su, bɛrɛ n bo bu suka ŋgbɛɛn ni ŋgumi dɔ, ye deke be akata i su a.

ref: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
text: Dama Sitana bo i ti durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ nyɛmɛ n ata bu nyi bu sunsunni nu na má bu yo yarada, adena má bu ŋu labari kpa n dɛɛ wein n fa kɔ Kristo jirima woo­wa n su, yiri bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ ti ni.

ref: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
text: Ya kan má yɛrɛ ni ya tii jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, ya kan Yesu Kristo jɔrɛ a, ka yiri ti sɔnɔ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a. Yɛrɛ, ya ti am dɛɛ akɔɔm wɔ, yiri Yesu dɛɛ ti.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ bo i yo seeri ka, “Ma wein ta fite awosin nu” ni, yiri ma i wein ata ya ahore su a, na i ma ya si i jirima woo­wa n bo i ta fite Yesu Kristo nyunu­awa dika ni.

ref: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
text: Bu miɛ ya sɔ ɔ deke kɛrɛ nu, ama, Nyɛmɛ yi ya nu wɔ. Ya tanda sɔ ɔ, ama, ya yi má tama.

ref: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
text: Bu kere ya wuwuruwa sɔ ɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ yaki má ya. Bu bo ya ji asiɛŋgu sɔ ɔ, ama, ya ka má ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
text: Nahɔrɛ, yɛrɛ bo ya la ŋgɔɔ ni, saŋga kɛrɛ bu fa ya wura yiwee woo­biri wɔ, Yesu dɛɛ ti, adena bu koro ŋu ŋgɔɔ n gusu bo Yesu la i n ya ŋuniɛ nu.

ref: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
text: Tɔ, yɛrɛ, ya wo yiwee nu wɔ, ama, ambɛrɛ wo ŋgɔɔ nu wɔ, Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
text: Dama ya si tenle su ka Nyɛmɛ bo i teŋgeeri Yesu i yiwee n nu ni, yiri sáŋga teŋge ya ni Yesu yiwee n nu a, na i fá ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ kɔ i nyunu.

ref: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
text: Ahin n kɛrɛ ma am nya kpɛrɛ a, adena minɛ dɔŋgu sunya Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni subuka su, na i la má aŋusu, nyini ma bu suda Nyɛmɛ asi subuka su a, na i wura Nyɛmɛ kpiri. Ya la yarada n ye ya nati a

ref: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ya ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki má ya. Haari ni bo ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa sugbaraki ni, ama, ya ahore su, ya sunya faŋga fɔfɔrɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ale kɛrɛ bo i cin.

ref: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
text: Ahin waharam bo ya sudi bu ni, bu ti má pui, na bu cɛ má, nyini ni má ya nya jirima woo­wa bo i la má aŋusu na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n a.

ref: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
text: Ya fa má ya nyu­mbaa kpama má dekem bo ya ŋu bu n su, ama, dekem bo ya ŋu má bu ni, ya fa ya nyu­mbaa kpama bɛrɛ su a, dama bo ya koro ŋu i ni, i cɛ má, ama, bo ya koro ŋu má i ni, i wo bɛrɛ wɔ, saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
text: Tɔ, ya si durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa n jɔrɛ, i ti ka nambue wɔ, nzɛn wɔ saaki, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la ataari kpa bo Nyɛmɛ ata i afa ase i. Má adamande taari i a, nyini awuru n wo aŋgoro wɔ, i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ wɔ.

ref: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
text: Ya koro i sɔ ɔ, na ya wura i ka sutura na má ya nati ya bu ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
text: Yɛrɛ bo ya toko wo ahin nambue n nu ni, ya waraki wɔ, na ya wo ahore tanda nu sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. I ti má ka ya koro ka ya yi i se wɔ, ama, ya koro ka ya wura aŋgoro dɛɛ n nu wɔ, na i kata ya ŋu, adena Nyɛmɛ bá kaaki ahin aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i wu ni, na i ma ya i dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ ni.

ref: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
text: Nyɛmɛ ni i tii adu mɔ asɛsɛ nyini deke n su ase ya a. Na wɔ fa i Awiɛwiɛ n ama ya, na i kere kabo i má ya deke n kɛrɛ bo i seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

ref: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
text: Tɔ, nyini ma ya la ahoresi saŋga kɛrɛ n a. Ya si tenle su ka kabo ya toko wo ahin aŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu ni, ya nya wo má Mibiɛ n dika n nu lɛɛ.

ref: But we live by faith, not by what we see.
text: Kisa ya la yarada ye ya nati a, má ka ya nyu­mbaa ye ya fa ŋu asi nati a.

ref: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
text: Yɛrɛ, ya la ahoresi n dɛɛ ti, ye ya ŋa ya yaki ahin aŋuniɛ­mbaa n a, na ya kɔ awuru Mibiɛ n dɔ.

ref: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
text: Nyini ti, yɛrɛ yiri, nzɛn ya wo ya awuru n nu, wara ya wo má nu, ya boro ya nyi ka ya yo sa bo i sun Mibiɛ n nu wɔ.

ref: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
text: Ya si kabo i ti na ya soro Mibiɛ n ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ya boro ya nyi na ya ma minɛm kaaki bu sunsunni fa kɔ i jɔrɛ n su a. Nyɛmɛ si kabo ya woo­wa kɛrɛ ti barasu. Na m la tama ka am sunsunni nu, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ si ya woo­wa.

ref: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
text: Ya sukaaki kan kere má am bekun kabo ya ti wɔ, ama, ya sukere am deke bo i ti na am koro fa ya fɔ n wɔ, adena nzɛn minɛm bá koro ka bu fɔ deke bo bu koro ŋu i n su, na i fite má ahore­mbaa su, dama bu koro ka bu bu am ŋgbɛɛn n dɛɛ ti, tɔ, am koro bɔ bu nu ni jɔrɛ kpa.

ref: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
text: Nzɛn bu ŋa ya bo fiɛ wɔ, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ti ye ya bo fiɛ a, ye nzɛn ya nyunu ti koroo­wa gusu a, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye ya nyunu ti koroo­wa a.

ref: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
text: Koro n bo Kristo fa koro ya ni, yiri niɛ ya su a, dama ya yo ya sunsunni ka sɔnɔ kun n bo i wu maari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti ni, nyini la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ awu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
text: I wu maari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ, adena bo bu wo ni ŋgɔɔ n bita bu ŋgɔɔ n ka i ti má bu dɛɛ, ama, i ti i dɛɛ wɔ, yiri n bo i wu maari bu na wɔ kaaki ateŋge i yiwee n nu bekun ni.

ref: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, fa i kisa fa kɔ, kabo durunya n nu­fɔm n bu sɔnɔ ni, yɛrɛ, ya bu má be sɔ kun, haari ni bo ya yo buuri Kristo mmɔɔ sɔ ni, kisa ya bu má i sɔ kun.

ref: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
text: Nyini yiri, nzɛn be ni Kristo ayo kun, i mi n ayo deke fɔfɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ, i woo­wa dawa n asin, na i deke n kɛrɛ akaaki fɔfɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
text: Nyini n kɛrɛ fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ. Yiri ama Kristo ayo ya ni yiri ni i tii afiɛn dɔŋgu a, ye wɔ ma ya juma n bo i yo i ni minɛm afiɛn dɔŋgu.

ref: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
text: Nyini la ka, Nyɛmɛ ma Kristo yo i ni durunya n nu­fɔm afiɛn dɔŋgu a, na i teŋge má bu satiɛ jɔrɛ su, haari kaan sɔ, ye wɔ fa nyini afiɛn dɔŋgu­yo n jɔrɛ n awura ya saa nu, ka ya kan i.

ref: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ya ti sunma­fɔm bo ya jina Kristo jaa nu wɔ. I ti ka Nyɛmɛ ni i tii sufa ya susere am jande a. Ya jina Kristo dɛɛ jaa nu susere am wɔ, ka, am ma yiri yo am ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu.

ref: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Yɛrɛ bo ya ni Nyɛmɛ saŋga yo juma ni, ya sere am wɔ, ahɔrɔma n bo am ade i ni, má am ma i ka ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “Saŋga kpa n ba juuri ni, ye m deeri am sere a, na tii­de cɛɛn n ye m bukaari am a.” Am ti fɛn, kisa ti saŋga kpa n a. Am ti fɛn, kisa ti tii­de cɛɛn n a.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
text: Ya sukanda má be atin wɔ, adena má be jaraki ya fa kɔ ya juma n bo ya yo i n su.

ref: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
text: Ama, deke n kɛrɛ nu, ya kere ka ya ti Nyɛmɛ amusui­su­fɔm wɔ. Dama haari nzɛn ya wo wahara nu, ni kekereke nu, ni anyum­boro nu, ya mindi de i wɔ.

ref: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
text: Haari na ba bo ya, na ba nyi ya dan­saraka, na ba di aluwa ya busu, na ya yo juma kekereke, na ya kpiɛ daafiri, na ya da ahɔɛ,

ref: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
text: nyini n kɛrɛ ni, na ya kere ka ya woo­wa ti casi wɔ, na ya la si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na ya mindi, na ya yo kpa, na ya wo ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni, na ya la koro nahɔrɛ su.

ref: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
text: Ya kan nahɔrɛ jɔrɛ wɔ, na ya yo juma n ni Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n nu. Ya bita biɛsɔya neŋge bo i ti sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, i wo ya saa fɔmbɔrɔ, ni ya saa biɛ nu wɔ.

ref: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
text: Bu wura ya kpiri wɔ, na bu kaaki yo ya kaan. Bu kansi ya a, na bu kaaki jaraki ya. Bu ŋa ya dadaka minɛm wɔ, ama, bu kaaki ŋa ya kan nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
text: Ya ti ka minɛm bo be si má ya wɔ, ama, deke kɛrɛ minɛm si ya sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Ya ti ka ya wu wɔ, ama, ya la ŋgɔɔ sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Bu bo ya ni anyum­boro sɔ ɔ, ama, ya wu má.

ref: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
text: Ya wo ahoresaaki nu sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ama, saŋga kɛrɛ ya la ahorejɔ ɔ. Ya wo ka ya ti yaari­fɔm wɔ, ama, ya fa ajɛkɛ ma minɛm dɔŋgu wɔ. Ya ti ka ya la má pui wɔ, ama, deke n kɛrɛ ti ya dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
text: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ ya danfo Kɔrinto­fɔm, ya fa má deke be afiɛ má am su, ya teke ya ahore­mbaa ama am.

ref: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
text: Yɛrɛ, ya kpiɛ má am ya koro, ambɛrɛ yiri, am akpiɛ ya am koro.

ref: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
text: Am ti ka m nbaam dɛɛ ti ye m suse am sɔ n a, ka am gusu teke am ahore­mbaa ma ya, kabo ya teke ya dɛɛ ama am ni. Má am fa am ŋu bo minɛm bo bu yo má Kristo yarada n su

ref: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
text: Má am fa am ŋu bo minɛm bo bu yo má Kristo yarada n su, dama bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n ni ketema­fɔ ni, i yo sɛ ye bu koro saŋga wo a? Wara wein ni awosin yo sɛ koro saŋga wo a? Ai, i koro yo má.

ref: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
text: Nzu anɔɔsɛ wo Kristo ni Sitana afiɛn a? Wara nzu tana wo sɔnɔ bo wɔ yo Kristo yarada n ni bo wɔ yo má i yarada n afiɛn a? Ai, bu la má tana.

ref: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
text: Nzu anɔɔsɛ wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n ni amɔɛm afiɛn a? Ai, bu la má anɔɔsɛ be. Yɛrɛ ti ka Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n a, ye yiri Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n wo nu n a. Kabo Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ase ni, ka, “M wó bu dɔ, na m ni bɛrɛ sáŋga nati, na m yó bu Nyɛmɛ, na bu yó m minɛm.”

ref: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
text: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase ka, “Am fite yaki bu, na am kpaaki yaki bu, Micɛra ni seeri sɔ a. Na má am fa am ŋu mata deke bo i ti má casi, na m dé am.

ref: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
text: Na m yó am Si, na ambɛrɛ gusu yó m dɛɛ nbaam. Kabo Micɛra Kpiri n ase lɛɛ.”

ref: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, ahin deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i yó ma ya n dɛɛ ti, am ma ya kpakpara ya ŋu fite deke kɛrɛ bo i ma ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n ni ya awiɛwiɛ n yo fin ni, na am ma ya soro Nyɛmɛ na ya woo­wa kɛrɛ ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni. Kabo bu sa­yoo­wa ma Pɔɔlu ahore jɔɔri ni

ref: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
text: Am teke am ahore­mbaa na am de ya. Ya do má am be ŋgasi, ya saaki má am be, ya sisi má am be.

ref: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
text: Deke bo i ti na m se ahin sɔ ni, i ti má ka m sujaraki am wɔ, dama ma du mɔ ase am ka am jɔrɛ wo ya ahore su, ya ni ambɛrɛ ti kun wɔ, haari yiwee nu wara ŋgɔɔ nu.

ref: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
text: M la ahoresi agaya fa kɔ am dɔ, na m fa am fɔ agaya, na m la ahoreda agaya ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, haari ni wahara n bo ya di i n kɛrɛ ni, m la ahorejɔ bo i la má aŋusu.

ref: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
text: Dama saŋga bo ya ba juuri Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu ni, ya nya má ŋumi­yi haari kaan sɔ mmɔɔ, ama, ya ŋuuri kekereke wɔ deke kɛrɛ nu. Minɛm fa aluwa kpura ya sɔ ɔ, na sɛrɛ suti ya, ya ahore su.

ref: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ yiri, yiri dada minɛm bo bu ahore asaaki n ahore a, yiri adada ya ahore a, Titosi baawa n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
text: Má ka i baawa ni ŋgumi dɛɛ ti a, ama, ahoreda n bo am ni yiri kɛrɛ nyaari i n dɛɛ ti a. Wɔ se ya kabo am wara ado m na am koro ka am ŋu m barasu ni, ni kabo am ahore asaaki fa kɔ jɔrɛ n su ni, ni kabo am ahore abi m busu barasu ni, nyini ma m ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su n a.

ref: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
text: Haari nzɛn kadasi n bo m yo kɛrɛ maari am n asaaki am ahore, ma yo má m si i n sɔ, fa kɔ nyini n dika. Nzɛn má m ŋuuri kabo nyini kadasi n ama am ahore asaaki, haari ni bo wɔ cɛ má ni, má m yoori m si i n sɔ.

ref: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
text: Kisa m ahore ajɔ, má ka am ahore asaaki n dɛɛ ti a, ama, am ahoresaaki ni, wɔ ma am akaaki am wo n dɛɛ ti a. Nyini ahoresaaki ni, yiri ama am akaaki kabo Nyɛmɛ koro n a, adena má am kpaasi fieo, yɛrɛ dɛɛ ti.

ref: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
text: Dama ahoresaaki bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni, yiri ma ya kaaki ya wo ye ya nya tii­de a, na ya yo má m si i n sɔ a. Ama, durunya n nu dɛɛ ahoresaaki n fa yiwee ba.

ref: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
text: Deke bo i ti na m yo kɛrɛɛri kadasi n maari am ni, má yiri n bo i doori ŋgasi n dɛɛ ti a, wara sɔnɔ n bo wɔ do i ŋgasi n dɛɛ ti a, ama, deke n bo i ti na m yo kɛrɛɛri i sɔ n la ka, adena Nyɛmɛ ma am si kabo am cɛcɛ wo ya nu ni anyum­boro su barasu ni.

ref: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
text: Nyini n kɛrɛ adada ya ahore a. Má nyini ahoreda n ŋgumi ye ya la i a, ama, deke bo i ma ya ahore ajɔ agaya n la kabo Titosi ahore ajɔ ni, dama ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ adada i ahore a.

ref: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
text: Nyini ma ya ahore ajɔ sɔ a, dama ma du mɔ afa am afɔ kabo am ti barasu n akere yiri Titosi, na am ama ma di má nyinsɛ. Kabo jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo ya kan kereeri am n ti nahɔrɛ ni, sɔ ye minɛm koro ŋu kabo ya fɔɔri am ŋusu jɔrɛ n kereeri Titosi n gusu n ti nahɔrɛ a.

ref: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
text: Deke bo i ma yiri Titosi koro am agaya ni, maala bo i teŋge su kabo am kɛrɛ deeri i nɔaniɛ n ni, ni kabo am deeri i ni am saa nnyɔ n na sɛrɛ ti am agaya na am ŋuniɛ subobo am ni.

ref: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
text: M ahore ti jɔɔ­wa, dama deke n kɛrɛ nu, m la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ.

ref: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Tɔ, m ninbaam, ya koro ka am si kabo Nyɛmɛ ama Kristo jamam bo bu wo Makɛdoniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu n adi i ahɔrɔma barasu ni.

ref: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
text: M koro di daani ka bu maari bu faŋga nyaari wɔ, haari ba ma atara kabo má bu ma ni mmɔɔ. Bu koroya­nu,

ref: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
text: na ba sere ya jande ka ya ma bɛrɛ mmɔɔ nya atin na bu saa wo nyini juma n nu, bo ya de aceerim fa ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni.

ref: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
text: Haari na ba yo akɔ atara kabo má ya sunsun ka bu yo ni. Bu du mɔ faari bu ŋu maari Mibiɛ n wɔ ka, na ba nya fa bu ŋu ama yɛrɛ gusu, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni.

ref: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
text: Tɔ, kabo Titosi ti sɔnɔ bo i boori ahin aceeri juma n bu am nu ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya sere i jande na i suyo i sɔ ma am, haari i kɔ wie.

ref: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
text: Ambɛrɛ, am boro am nyi deke n kɛrɛ nu wɔ, bɛrɛ la yarada n bo am la i ni, ni kabo am koro jɔjɔ ni, ni kabo am la si ni, ni kabo am cɛcɛ wo deke n kɛrɛ nu ni, ni kabo am la koro ma ya ni. Kabo i ti sɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka am boro am nyi yo ahin aceeri juma n gusu.

ref: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
text: I ti má ka m suma am nɔɔ ka am yo sɔ ɔ, ama, m koro ka m kere am kabo nyiŋgam la koro ni anyum­boro na bu buka n wɔ, adena m yo am na m niɛ ka am la koro nahɔrɛ su.

ref: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
text: Am si juma kpa n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo yoori i ni, i yo ti ajɛkɛ­fɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ama, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti na wɔ kaaki yaari­fɔ, adena am kaaki ajɛkɛ­fɔm Nyɛmɛ dɔ, kabo wɔ kaaki yaari­fɔ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
text: i ti ye ni ka am yo juma n bo am ŋa am yo i n kɔ wie kisa, adena kabo am yo la anyum­boro am sunsunni nu, na am koro ka am yo i ni, sɔ ye am kere anyum­boro a, na am fa deke n bo am la i n fa yo i wie.

ref: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
text: Nzɛn be la anyum­boro na i koro ka i ce aceeri, Nyɛmɛ de i aceeri n fa kɔ deke bo i koro ce n su wɔ, má deke bo i koro ce má ni.

ref: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
text: I ti má ka ya koro ka ya yi nbem fie nu, na ambɛrɛ fie wɔ. Ai, ama, ya koro ka i sɛ su ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ.

ref: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
text: Tɔ, kisa am la i, na nbem la má i ni, am buka bu. Na nzɛn bu gusu bá la i, na am la má i, bu gusu buka am. Sɔ ye i sɛ su ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ a.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
text: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu wɔ, ka, “Bo i tambuuri beberebe ni, wɔ sunnu asin má su, ye bo i tambuuri kaan n gusu, wɔ kpiɛ má i su.” Titosi ni i buka­fɔm n jɔrɛ

ref: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
text: Ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, yiri ma Titosi nyunu aboro na i cɛcɛ wo am dɔ a, kabo mini mmɔɔ dɛɛ ti ni.

ref: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
text: Ya sere yiri Titosi ka i ba am dɔ, na wɔ soro su, ama, yiri ni i tii dɛɛ koroya­nu ye i ŋa i kɔ a, dama i cɛcɛ du mɔ wo am dɔ.

ref: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
text: Bo i buka i su, ya susunma ya niwa be gusu am dɔ. I ti sɔnɔ bo i yo labari kpa n juma a, nyini dɛɛ ti, Kristo jama n kɛrɛ kansi i wɔ.

ref: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
text: Má nyini ŋgumi, ama, yiri ye Kristo jama n kɛrɛ ayi i ka i susu ya su buka ya a, na ya niɛ ahin aceeri cee­wa juma n su n a. Ahin juma n wura Nyɛmɛ kpiri wɔ, na i kere kabo ya koro ka ya buka minɛm ni anyum­boro ni.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
text: Ya koro má ka be jaraki ya fa kɔ kabo ya niɛ ahin aceeri beberebe n su ni.

ref: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
text: Nyini ti, ya boro ya nyi wɔ, na ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, má Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi dɔ, ama, adamande mmɔɔ dɔ.

ref: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
text: Ya kaaki susunma ya niwa be buka bu su. I ti sɔnɔ bo ya yo i aniɛ atin barasu nu, aŋu ka i la anyum­boro fa kɔ deke dɔŋgu su. Kisa ya ŋu ka i la anyum­boro tara sɔ, dama i la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ.

ref: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
text: Titosi yiri, i ti m mata­fɔ bo m ni yiri saŋga yo juma ma am wɔ. Ya ninbaa nnyɔm bɛrɛ yiri, bu ti minɛm bo Kristo jamam n asunma bu wɔ, na bu kaaki wura Kristo kpiri.

ref: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
text: Nyini ti, am yi am ŋu fofoe nu, na am kere Kristo jamam ni kabo am la koro ni, na bu si deke n bo i ti na ya fa am fɔ ni.

ref: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
text: Tɔ, aceerim bo am fa kɔ buka Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni, mini dɔ, wɔ ti má ye ka m kɛrɛ i jɔrɛ.

ref: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
text: Dama m si kabo am koro ka am buka barasu ni, haari ma du mɔ afa am afɔ Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu­fɔm dɔ, ka ambɛrɛ Akaya awɔrɛ n nu­fɔm n adu mɔ akpa am ŋu haari afara kɛrɛ. Kabo am nyunu aboro na am koro ka am ce ni, wɔ yo bu dɔŋgu gusu ŋuniɛ nyanyaka ye bu koro ka bu ce n a.

ref: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
text: Nyini ye m susunma ahin ya ninbaam am dɔ n a, adena kabo ya fa am afɔ akere bu ni, na má i ka ŋgbɛɛn, ama, am yo siriya, kabo ma du mɔ ase bu ka am yo siriya ni.

ref: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
text: Má nyini, nzɛn Makɛdoniya­fɔm nbem suuri m su baari, na bu ba ŋu ka am nya yo má siriya, i ti ya nyinsɛ wɔ, ya kan má am dɛɛ yiri, kabo ya yo am yarada n dɛɛ ti.

ref: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ma ŋu ka wɔ yo daka ka m sere ya ninbaam ni, na bu du m mɔ am dɔ, na bu buka am, na am sɛsɛ am aceeri n bo am yo sereeri ka am yi fa ma n su. Nyini ma i yo koro aceeri a, i ti má ka bu miɛ am wɔ.

ref: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
text: Am teŋge su, ka sɔnɔ bo i dɔ kaan, i bá kpiɛ kaan wɔ. Na sɔnɔ bo i dɔ dɔŋgu, i bá kpiɛ dɔŋgu wɔ.

ref: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ce aceeri n kabo wɔ du mɔ asunsun i ahore su ka i ce barasu ni, má ka bu miɛ i wɔ, ama, i mi ma ni ahorejɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ koro bo i ce ni ahorejɔ ɔ.

ref: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
text: Yiri Nyɛmɛ ni, yiri koro ma am nya i aceeri barasu kɛrɛ a, na am nya deke kɛrɛ bo am koro saŋga kɛrɛ, na i sɛ am su, na am nya i buka su, adena am koro yo juma kpa kɛrɛ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
text: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Wɔ sandi i aceerim afa ama yaari­fɔm ni, na i kpa­yo n wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.”

ref: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
text: Nyɛmɛ ti bo i ma dɔdɔɔri­fɔ ni alɛ­mbaa n a, na i kaaki ma minɛm diire na bu di a, yiri ma am alɛ­mbaa n a, na i ma i sunnu buka su, na i kaaki ma am kpa­yo subuka su saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
text: Na i ma am sunya beberebe deke n kɛrɛ nu, adena am koro suyo kpa ma minɛm saŋga kɛrɛ, nyini ma minɛm da Nyɛmɛ asi, fa kɔ deke n bo ya yo i n su a.

ref: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
text: Ahin aceeri juma n bo am yo i ni, i ti má ka i subuka Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo ba fun i ni ŋgumi wɔ, ama, i kaaki ma minɛm dɔŋgu da Nyɛmɛ asi wɔ.

ref: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
text: Ahin juma n bo am suyo i kɔ wie ni, nyini kere kabo am woo­wa ti barasu a. Na minɛm ŋu i, na bu kansi Nyɛmɛ kabo am ade yiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na am kan kere minɛm nahɔrɛ, ka am su Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ n su ni. Bo i buka nyini n su la kabo am yo kpa, na am kpaaki am neŋgem nu ma bɛrɛ ni nyiŋgam kɛrɛ ni.

ref: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ ama am anya i ahɔrɔma akɔ asin su ni, bɛrɛ, bu koro am ni anyum­boro na bu sere Nyɛmɛ ma am.

ref: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
text: Am ma ya da Nyɛmɛ asi fa kɔ i aceeri n bo wɔ fa ama ya na i la má aŋusu ni.

ref: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii, m susere am wɔ, Kristo sawɔrɛ n ni i bɛnlɛ woo­wa n dɛɛ ti, mini bo nbem ŋa nzɛn m wo am dɔ, m san nya ni, ama, nzɛn m wo má am dɔ yiri, m la ahore wɔ.

ref: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
text: Deke bo i ti na m susere am n bu la ka, m koro má ka am ma m yo ahore, saŋga bo m ba baari am dɔ ni, na m yo ahoresi jɔjɔ am, kabo m sunsun ka wɔ yo daka ka m jɔjɔ bu ni, bɛrɛ bo bu ŋa ya su durunya n nu­fɔm woo­wa n su ni.

ref: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
text: Haari ni bo ya wo durunya n nu ni, ya kun má luɛ n kabo durunya n nu­fɔm kun luɛ ni.

ref: And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
text: Ambɛrɛ, am deeri i nɔaniɛ n wieeri, ye ya bá cin bu kɛrɛ sui a, bɛrɛ bo ba kete ka bu de má i nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
text: Ambɛrɛ, deke bo am nyu­mbaa ŋu i ni, nyini ye am sunsunni wo i su a. Nzɛn be la ahoresi ka i ti Kristo dɛɛ sɔnɔ, i ti ye ni ka i mi kaaki sunsun ka yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ti Kristo minɛm wɔ, kabo yiri mmɔɔ ti ni.

ref: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
text: Haari nzɛn m fɔ yanfɔya tara kabo má m fɔ ni, fa kɔ yiko n bo Mibiɛ n afa ama ya n su, i la má m nyinsɛ, nyini yiko n ye Mibiɛ n maari ya, ka ya fa buka am, na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu ni, wɔ ma má ya ka ya fa saaki am ji wɔ.

ref: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
text: M koro má ka i yo ka m sukɛrɛ am kadasim n na m wura am sɛrɛ wɔ.

ref: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
text: Dama nbem bo bu kan m jɔrɛ ni, bu ŋa, m kadasim wo nɔ na bu la faŋga, ama, saŋga bo mini ni m tii ba wo am dɔ, m ti sɔnɔ yɔrɔkii­wa a, kabo m jɔjɔ ni, be fa yo má pui.

ref: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
text: I ti ye ni ka nyini minɛm sɔ n si ka, kabo saŋga bo ya wo má dɔ na ya kɛrɛ kadasim n nu ni, sɔ ye ya yó a, saŋga bo ya wo am dɔ ni.

ref: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
text: Yɛrɛ yiri, ya wura má ya ŋu kpiri, na ya fɔ tara kabo i ti ye ni ka ya fɔ ni. Ya yo kɔ jina ya juma yoo­biri nyaari wɔ, deke bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ka ya yo ni, haari ya yo aba aju ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ dɔ.

ref: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
text: Yɛrɛ, ya gusu fa má nbem juma fa fɔ má, na má ya fɔ tara ya juma yoo­biri nyaari. Ama, ya la tama ka kabo am yarada subuka su ni, ya juma ni sandi bara yɛ am nu, kabo ya juma yoo­biri nyaari ti ni.

ref: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
text: Adena ya koro kɔ kan labari kpa n kere minɛm, bo bu wo asiɛnm bo bu wo am nyunu dɔ ni. Ya koro má ka ya yo wura nbem dɛɛ juma yoo­biri bo ba du mɔ ayo i n nu, na ya fa fɔ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.”
text: Ama, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i fɔ, i fɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti.”

ref: I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ.
text: M si am kɔrɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ si am kɔrɛ ni, dama am ti m dɛɛ nbaam wɔ, ka baa­bara bo i nya si má biɛsɔ, ye ma fa am ace biɛsɔ kun, yiri la Kristo.

ref: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
text: Dama nzɛn be ba baari am dɔ na i ba kaanri Yesu be jɔrɛ, na i ti má Yesu n bo ya kan i jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, wara nzɛn am deeri awiɛwiɛ be, na i ti má Awiɛwiɛ n bo am du mɔ deeri i ya dɔ ni, wara labari kpa barasu be bo i ti má bo am deeri i ni, ambɛrɛ koro yo nyini sawɔrɛ na am de nyini n sɔ a.

ref: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
text: Haari ni bo m ti má sɔnɔ bo i koro jɔjɔ kabo bu jɔjɔ ni, m la si yiri, ma du mɔ akere am ka i ti sɔ ɔ, deke n kɛrɛ nu ni saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
text: Saŋga bo m yo wo am dɔ na m la má pui ni, ma fa má m fie afie má be, dama ya ninbaam bo bu fite Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu yo bukaari m ni deke kɛrɛ bo wɔ kpiɛ m su ni. Ma koro má ka m fa m fie fie am be deke be nu. M woori sɔ ɔ, na m toko wo sɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

ref: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
text: Kristo dɛɛ nahɔrɛ n bo i wo m nu n dɛɛ ti, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kanda m na m fɔ má nyini yanfɔya ni, Akaya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

ref: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
text: Nzu ti a? Ka m koro má am, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, Nyɛmɛ si ka m koro am.

ref: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
text: Nyini ti, deke n bo m yo i ni, m kɔ́ su yo i sɔ ɔ, adena m tɔ bu atin, bɛrɛ kɔrɛ­fɔm ni bo bu koro ka bu nya atin na bu fɔ, na bu se ka ya ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ juma­yoo­wa sɛ ni.

ref: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
text: Dama nyini minɛm sɔ ti jaramasa kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ, bu nati bu kɔrɛ wɔ, fa kɔ bu juma­yoo­wa su, na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo jaramasam wɔ.

ref: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
text: Nyini n sɔ n ti má alekutura, dama haari Sitana ni i tii mmɔɔ yo i ŋu ka i ti mɛrɛkɛ bo i ta wein n wɔ.

ref: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
text: Nyini ti, i ti má alekutura, ka i amusui­su­fɔm mmɔɔ yo bu ŋu ka bu su nahɔrɛ atin su wɔ. Ama, bu awieeri bá kere kabo bu juma­yoo­wa ti a. Kabo Pɔɔlu diiri wahara i juma­yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti ni

ref: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
text: M koro ka m kaaki se am bekun wɔ, ka má be sunsun ka m ti sinnzin wɔ, ama, nzɛn am sunsun sɔ yiri, am yo sawɔrɛ na am de m ka m ti sinnzin wɔ, adena mini mmɔɔ koro fɔ kaan sɔ.

ref: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
text: Ahin ahoresi n bo m fa fɔ sɔ ni, i fite má Mibiɛ n dɔ, ama, m jɔjɔ ka m ti sinnzin wɔ.

ref: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
text: Kabo minɛm dɔŋgu fɔ kabo durunya n nu­fɔm yo ni, sɔ ye mini gusu fɔ́ n a.

ref: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
text: Am aŋu má i? Ambɛrɛ la alecira a, am mindi de minɛm bo bu ti sinnzin n wɔ, ni ahoreda a.

ref: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
text: Bu ŋa bu ti Hiburu­fɔm wɔ? Tɔ, mini mmɔɔ, m ti sɔ ɔ. Bu ŋa bu dɛɛ la Yisareli­fɔ nyɛmɛsu atin ni? Tɔ, mini mmɔɔ, m su nyini n su a. Bu ŋa bu ti Abraham anumam wɔ? Tɔ, mini mmɔɔ, m ti sɔ ɔ.

ref: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
text: Bu ŋa bu ti Kristo amusui­su­fɔm wɔ? Tɔ, m tara bu. Am niɛ, i ti ka m nyunu akan m ye m jɔjɔ sɔ n a. Ama, nahɔrɛ, ma yo juma atara bu, ba nyi m dan­saraka bo i la má nɔ, bu boori m haari m ŋgɔɔ ba kaari kaan sɔ ɔ, saŋga dɔŋgu, kaan sɔ, má ma wu. Nyinim kɛrɛ nu, m tara bu.

ref: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
text: Kpe nsɛn lɛɛ bo ba fin m ni bulaari ŋgbere. Ba tu m yabuɛ kpe­kun. Kpe nsan lɛɛ bo m wo lee bambakam nu tieku n su na ba sandi nu, haari na ma da tieku n nu kpe­kun, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu.

ref: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
text: Ma yo juma nahɔrɛ su adi wahara, ma kpiɛ daafiri saŋga dɔŋgu, ma da ahɔɛ ni nzuehɔɛ, ma fun diire saŋga dɔŋgu, na ayɛrɛ ati m, na ma nati m ŋuniɛ ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
text: Bu nu ŋma agbaraki ye i gbarakii­wa n asa má m a? Bu nu ŋma ye bu ma i yo satiɛ ye i yo má m yaa?

ref: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
text: Tɔ, saŋga bo wɔ yo daka ka m fɔ, m fɔ fa kɔ dekem bo bu kere ka m la má faŋga n su wɔ.

ref: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
text: Nyɛmɛ si ka m bu má kɔrɛ, yiri ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Si a, yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ a.

ref: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
text: Ama, na minɛm n afa m awura kandiɛ bambaka nu, na ba fa m acɔɔndi kobe n tukoro nu, ajura asiɛŋgu, na ma foro gomina n saa nu.

ref: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
text: Tɔ, wɔ yo daka ka m fɔ, haari ni bo i la má kpɛrɛ be ni. M la deke nbem bo m kan buka su, bɛrɛ la kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri deke nbem kereeri m ka alɛlɛ nu ni, ni kabo i yiiri sa­yoo­wam kereeri m ni.

ref: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
text: Am ti fɛn, kisa ma sɛsɛ m ŋu su ka m ba niɛ am busu kpe nsan su lɛɛ, ama, m toko koro má ka m ba fa m fie fie am. M suba má ka m koro am neŋge be wɔ, ama, m koro ambɛrɛ wɔ. Dama má nbaam kpini neŋgem se bu wu­fɔm a, ama, bu wu­fɔm ni kpini se bu nbaam n a.

ref: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
text: Haari nzɛn má m ŋa m fɔ mmɔɔ, má m ti má sinnzin wɔ, dama má m kan nahɔrɛ wɔ. Ama, m yaki i sɔ ɔ, adena má am be wura m kpiri tara dekem bo i ŋu ka m yo bu, ni dekem bo i ti ka m kan bu ni.

ref: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
text: Tɔ, fa kɔ sa­yikeree­wa beberebe n bo Nyɛmɛ fa kereeri m su, wɔ koro má ka nyini dɛɛ ti na m wura m ŋu kpiri. Nyini ti, i maari Sitana faari tɔrɛ be wuraari m ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu, i ti ka buwe ye i wɔwɔ m a, na i sukere m wahara, adena má m wura m ŋu kpiri.

ref: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
text: Kpe nsan lɛɛ bo ma sere Mibiɛ n fa kɔ ahin jɔrɛ n su, ka i ma i fite yaki m.

ref: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
text: Ama, na wɔ se m ka, “M ahɔrɔma n asɛ u su, dama saŋga bo a la má faŋga ni, ye m kere m yiko kabo m koro a.” Kabo i seeri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma m la ahoreda na m fɔ fa kɔ kabo m la má faŋga n a, adena Kristo yiko n wo m dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ a.

ref: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
text: Tɔ, kabo m jɔjɔ ni, ma yo ka sinnzin wɔ lɛɛ, ambɛrɛ miɛ m a, ye m yo sɔ n a. Má ambɛrɛ ti minɛm bo am kan kereeri kabo m ti n a, má i ti ye. Dama bɛrɛ n bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo jaramasam na bu la má aŋusu ni, mini yiri, haari ni bo m ti má pui ni, m ti má kaan ni bɛrɛ fieo.

ref: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
text: Nahɔrɛ, sam bo bu kere ka m ti jaramasa ni, ye m yoori bu am dɔ n a, ni mindi kɛrɛ, ni alekutura sam, ni yiko sam, ni abonɔ sam n a.

ref: You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
text: Nzu deke ye m yo maari Kristo jamam bo bu wo miɛ nyiŋgam su n ye ma yo ama má am a? Má ka bo ma fa má m fie be afie má am ni. Tɔ, ma do ŋgasi, am fa ce m.

ref: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
text: Tɔ, am nbem ŋa ma fa má m fie be afie má am, ama, bu ŋa m fa ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ wɔ, na ma dadaka am anya am taraa­biri.

ref: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
text: M yo sereeri Titosi wɔ, na wɔ ba am dɔ, na ma ma ya niwa n asunma i. Titosi yo sisiiri am, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, wɔ sisi má am. Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ aŋu ka m ni yiri kɛrɛ sunsunni ti kun wɔ, na ya kɛrɛ su atin kun su a.

ref: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
text: Beni, nyini n kɛrɛ bo ya jɔjɔ i sɔ ni, am sunsun ka ya sukan de ya tii wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, i ti má sɔ. M ninbaa koroo­wam, kabo ya ni Kristo ayo kun na ya jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu n ye ya se sɔ n a. Deke n kɛrɛ bo ya yo i ni, i buka am na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu n wɔ.

ref: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
text: M soro sɛrɛ wɔ, m koro má ka m ba baari am dɔ, na má m ba tu am kabo m koro ka am ti ni, wara na ambɛrɛ gusu ŋu m kabo má am koro ka m ti ni. M soro sɛrɛ ka, beni, m bá ŋu ka, am suse akuruwa, na am susi kɔrɛ, na am sufa yaa, na am suyo anyii sam, na am sukan am beŋgu jɔrɛ susaaki bu, na am sukpura tere, na am suyo kpirikoro, na am la kpakpaaki­nu.

ref: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
text: M soro sɛrɛ ka m ba juuri, na má Nyɛmɛ ma m yo m ŋu kaan am nyunu, na má m ahore saaki fa ma bu, bɛrɛ bo ba du mɔ ayo satiɛ, na bu nya kaaki yaki má bu daŋgba sa­yoo­wam, ni sakaraya, ni konvikiya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm ni.

ref: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
text: Tɔ, ahin baawa n bo m súba ni, yiri yo m kpe nsan su bo m ba niɛ am busu a. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo ba kan i na i ti nahɔrɛ, má ka minɛ nnyɔ, wara minɛ nsan adi i daani.”

ref: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
text: Saŋga bo m yo baari am dɔ ba niɛɛri am kpe nnyɔ su ni, m yo bɔbɔɔri am nbem. Kisa na m wo má am dɔ ni, m koro ka m bɔbɔ bu bekun fa kɔ nyini n su, ka nzɛn m baari, m yáki má bɛrɛ n bo bu yo yoori satiɛ ni, ni bo bu buka bu su kisa n kɛrɛ.

ref: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
text: Dama am ŋa am koro ka am ŋu deke bo i kere ka Kristo fa m su, ye i jɔjɔ am dɔ sɔ a. Tɔ, am aŋu ka yiri ti má bo i la má faŋga, ama, i la yiko am nu.

ref: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
text: Nahɔrɛ, saŋga bo bu yo kpamaari i baka jaanu n su ni, i yo la má faŋga, ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ, kisa i la ŋgɔɔ wo bɛrɛ, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n dɛɛ ti. Sɔ ye i ti a, yɛrɛ bo ya ni yiri kɛrɛ ayo kun ni, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ la má faŋga lɛɛ, ama, Nyɛmɛ yiko n dɛɛ ti, ye ya ni yiri súwo a, na ya yo juma ma am.

ref: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
text: Ambɛrɛ ni am tii, am fiiti am ŋu na am niɛ ka am woo­wa kere ka am ayo yarada nahɔrɛ su, wara am ayo má. Am si má ka Yesu Kristo wo am dɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ? I wo am dɔ yiri, ŋka am woo­wa kere ka am ayo má yarada nahɔrɛ su wɔ.

ref: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
text: Yɛrɛ, ya koro yo má deke bo i jaraki nahɔrɛ ni, ama, ya yo deke bo i su nahɔrɛ ni ŋgumi su a.

ref: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
text: Ya ahore ti jɔɔ­wa a, saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya la má faŋga, na ambɛrɛ la yiko Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Ahin n ye ya sere Nyɛmɛ ma am a, ka, i ma am woo­wa yo kabo i koro ni.

ref: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
text: Nyini mmɔɔ dɛɛ ti, ye m wo má am dɔ ye m sukɛrɛ am ahin kadasi n sɔ n a, adena m ba baari am dɔ, na má m fa Mibiɛ n yiko n fa yi am anyum­boro, nyini yiko n ye i maari m ka m fa buka am, na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu n a, wɔ ma má m ka m fa saaki am ji wɔ.

ref: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
text: M ninbaam, jɔrɛ bo wɔ ka n la ka, am ma am ahore jɔ, am boro am nyi na am wo wo kpa, am ti kabo m sereeri am ni, am la anɔɔsɛ ni am beŋgum, am wo ni tana­laifiɛ. Nyɛmɛ ti tana­laifiɛ ni koro­fɔ a, yiri wó am dɔ a.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
text: Am bisa am beŋgu dɔŋgu ni koro. Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

ref: From the apostle Paul and from all the Lord's followers with me. I was chosen to be an apostle by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised him from death. No mere human chose or appointed me to this work. To the churches in Galatia:
text: Mini Pɔɔlu bo m ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa n ni ya ninbaam bo bu wo m dɔ fɛn n kɛrɛ, sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama minɛm bo am wo Galatiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu n wɔ. Yesu Kristo ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ bo i teŋgeeri Kristo i yiwee n nu ni, afa m ayo i jaramasa, má adamande yoori m a, bekun má minɛm gusu faari juma n maari m a.

ref: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am ahoreda.

ref: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ ma Yesu ade ya jinaa­biri awu ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti, adena i de ya fite ahin durunya tiɛ n bo ya wo nu n nu.

ref: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
text: Yiri ti ye ni ka minɛm kɛrɛ kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami. Labari kpa kun n kpein n wo bɛrɛ a

ref: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
text: Labari kpa nyiŋga be wo má bɛrɛ, deke bo i ti na m se sɔ n la ka, minɛm nbem koro ka bu ta am nyi wɔ, na bu fa keree­wa barasu nbem kaaki Kristo labari kpa ni.

ref: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
text: Ama, haari nzɛn yɛrɛ be, wara nzɛn Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be fiteeri aŋgoro ba kaanri labari barasu be, na i ti má kabo ya du mɔ kaanri i ni, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
text: Kabo ya du mɔ ase am ni, nyini ye m kaaki suse am bekun n a, ka, nzɛn be ba kaanri labari barasu be bo i wo i ŋgumi ni bo am ade i ni, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
text: Bu ŋa m suyo m juma n ka minɛ kansi m wɔ, ama, i ti má sɔ, m koro ka m yo bo Nyɛmɛ kansi m wɔ, m suyo má sa bo minɛ se ka m ti kpa. Nzɛn má m ŋa m yo sa bo i sun minɛ nu, má m koro yo má Kristo akɔɔ. Kabo Pɔɔlu ba yoori Kristo jaramasa barasu ni

ref: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
text: M ninbaam, m koro ka am si ka labari kpa n bo m kaanri i jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, i ti má deke bo i fite adamande sunsunni nu wɔ.

ref: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
text: I ti má ka m deeri i fiteeri adamande dɔ ɔ, má be gusu kaaki fa kereeri m a. Ama, Yesu Kristo ni i tii yiiri i kereeri m a.

ref: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
text: Am yo tiiri kabo m woo­wa yo ti barasu, saŋga bo m yo suuri Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ atin n su ni. M yo kereeri Kristo jama n wuwuruwa agaya, na má m butu bu.

ref: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
text: M yo suuri Yuda­fɔm atin n su nahɔrɛ su taraari m sara su­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu su nyini atin n su ni. M nyunu yo boroori agaya kabo m yo na m su ya nana sa­yoo­wam su.

ref: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
text: na wɔ yi i Wa n akere m, adena m kɔ kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, ye ma kɔ má be dɔ na i kɔ kere m kabo m yo n a.

ref: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
text: Ma kaaki akɔ má Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu na m kɔ ŋu minɛm bo bu du mɔ ti Kristo jaramasam ni. M kɔɔri Arabiya asiɛn n su wɔ, na ma fite dɔ, na ma nya akaaki akɔ Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
text: Nɔaniɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, m bu má kɔrɛ. Nyɛmɛ ti m daani­fɔ a.

ref: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
text: Nyini sin na ma fite Yɛrusalem, na ma kɔ Siriya ni Silisiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu.

ref: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
text: Nyini saŋga Kristo jamam n bo bu wo Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu n be nya ŋu má m na bu ba si m,

ref: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
text: se ba ti ka minɛ ŋa, “Ahin sɔnɔ n yo kere ya wuwuruwa a, na i kun minɛ bo ba de Kristo labari kpa n na i mini bu, kisa i nati kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛ wɔ.”

ref: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
text: Deke n bo i ti na m kɔɔri n la ka, Nyɛmɛ kereeri m ka m kɔ a. Na m ni Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n atiɛn ya nɔɔ ya ŋgumi deke be, na ma kan ateke bu nyi, akere bu fa kɔ labari kpa n jɔrɛ bo m kan kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni. Dama ma koro má ka juma n bo m suyo i n kɛrɛ ka ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
text: Tɔ, haari ni bo Titosi yo woori m dɔ na i ti má Yuda­fɔ ni, ba miɛ má i ka i wura kɛnkɛnnɛ.

ref: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
text: Ama, be abisa má ya i jɔrɛ be, se minɛ nbem ba saŋgaari ya nu, bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo dɛɛ minɛm wɔ. Bɛrɛ seeri ka wɔ yo daka ka i wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n a. Bu faari bu ŋu mataari ya a, adena bu niɛ kabo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti na ya nya ya tii ni. Má bu koro ka ya su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ, na ya yo akɔɔm.

ref: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
text: Ama, na ya fa nu, ya ma má bu anyunu fieo, adena má be kaaki i sin bo labari kpa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ, na ya kan kere am su.

ref: Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message.
text: Bɛrɛ n bo minɛ bu bu ka bu ti nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, m cɛcɛ awo má bu nu ka bu ti nkpiɛnm, dama Nyɛmɛ tambu má nu, nyini nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n gusu ala má kaakii­wa bo bu kaaki m fa kɔ m labari kpa n su.

ref: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
text: Se deke bo bu ŋuuri i n la ka, Nyɛmɛ ye wɔ yi m ka m kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n a, kabo wɔ yi Petoro ka i kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere Yuda­fɔm ni.

ref: God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ ama m yiko ka m kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, kabo wɔ ma Petoro yiko ka i kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere Yuda­fɔm ni.

ref: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
text: Na Yakobo, ni Petoro, ni Yohane bo minɛ bu bu ka bu ti Kristo jama mɔ­fɔm n aŋu ka, Nyɛmɛ ama m nyini juma n ka m yo i a, nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba fa bu saa awura m ni Banabasi saa nu, na ba de ya ni bu saa nnyɔ. Na ya kɛrɛ asoro su ka m ni Banabasi kɔ yo juma ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na bɛrɛ gusu kɔ yo juma ni Yuda­fɔm ni.

ref: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
text: Ama, jɔrɛ n bo bu seeri ya n la ka, má ya wara fi yaari­fɔm sa. Nyini ye m yo la anyum­boro ka m yo i a. Kabo Pɔɔlu jarakiiri Petoro barasu ni

ref: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
text: M ŋuuri ka bu su má Yesu labari kpa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n su ni, na ma se Petoro jama n kɛrɛ nyunu ka, “Wɔrɔ, a ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, ama, u woo­wa ti ka bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm wɔ. Ye nzu ti ye a koro ka a miɛ minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu wo kabo Yuda­fɔm wo barasu n a?” Yarada dɛɛ ti ye sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nya tii­de a

ref: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
text: Kabo ya boro ya nyi na ya su Kristo su na Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, nyini kere ka yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm gusu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm ka minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ti n wɔ. Ama, nyini n sukere má ka Kristo subuka minɛm na bu yo satiɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Ai, i ti má sɔ.

ref: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
text: M yo su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ, ama, ma yaki i, nzɛn kisa na m ŋa m kaaki bo i bu su i su bekun, nyini saŋga, ma fa m ŋu ayo satiɛ­yo­fɔ lɛɛ nahɔrɛ su.

ref: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
text: Mini dɔ, fa kɔ nyini nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, i yo m ka ma wu wɔ, m su má i su kun, adena m nya ŋgɔɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. I ti ka m ni Yesu Kristo asaŋga awu baka jaanu n su a.

ref: I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me.
text: Ahin ŋgɔɔ n bo m la i ni, durunya n nu fɛn ni, yiri Nyɛmɛ Wa n koro m dɛɛ ti, na wɔ wu ama m, na ma yo i yarada, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m la ŋgɔɔ n a.

ref: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
text: Ma kete má kabo Nyɛmɛ ayo ya sakpa akere i koro barasu ni. Ama, deke bo m suse n la ka, nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋa i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, na nyini kere ka wɔ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, nyini la ka, yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you?
text: Ambɛrɛ Galatiya­fɔm, am la má ŋgɔnlɛ wɔ. Ŋma adadaka am a? M yo seeri am, kabo Yesu Kristo wuuri baka jaanu n su barasu ni, ma fiɛ má am su.

ref: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
text: Am ma m bisa am ahin bisaa­wa ni, am suuri nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su n dɛɛ ti, ye am deeri Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a? Wara nzɛn bo am tiiri Yesu labari kpa n jɔrɛ na am yoori yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye am deeri i a?

ref: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
text: Nzu ti ye am la má ŋgɔnlɛ sɔ a? Am boori i bu ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wɔ, ye i baari sɛ ye am koro ka am fa am faŋga wiewie i a?

ref: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
text: Deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i na am aŋu i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Mini dɔ, i ti má ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
text: Bo am ati labari kpa n na am ayo yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ n ama am, ye Nyɛmɛ yoori alekutura sam am nu a, nzɛn bo am su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su n a?

ref: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
text: Nyini kere ya ka, bo ba yo Nyɛmɛ yarada ni, bɛrɛ ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

ref: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
text: Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kere ka, minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm bá yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na i de bu ka ba yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ. Nyɛmɛ du mɔ kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri Abraham wɔ, na wɔ se i ka, “Wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, ye m yó adamande kɛrɛ kisi a.”

ref: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yarada, Nyɛmɛ yó i kisi, kabo Abraham yoori yarada, na Nyɛmɛ yoori i kisi barasu ni.

ref: But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, Nyɛmɛ nɔɔ dí i mi, nyini dɛɛ ti ye Kristo kaari ŋgbɛɛn wuuri baka jaanu n su deeri ya tii jiiri n a. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Nyɛmɛ yó sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo ba sense i baka n su ŋgbɛɛn.”

ref: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
text: Deke n bo i ti na Yesu Kristo wuuri nyini yiwee n la ka, nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni Abraham ka i yó minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm kisi ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, nzɛn ya yo Kristo yarada, Nyɛmɛ ŋa i ma ya Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo wɔ di i di ka i fa ma ya ni. Sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni Abraham n jɔrɛ

ref: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
text: M ninbaam, m koro ka m fa adamande woo­wa na m fa kere kabo Nyɛmɛ woo­wa ti barasu. Nzɛn minɛ nnyɔ afa sɛsɛ­su adi i di ka bu yo deke be, sɔnɔ wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kete i, wara i kaaki i.

ref: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
text: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ diiri nyini di n ni Abraham ni i busu nu­fɔ n wɔ, wɔ se má ka i busu nu­fɔm kɛrɛ, ama, i ŋa i busu nu­fɔm kun, yiri la Kristo.

ref: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
text: Sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ceeri Abraham aceeri n a. Má ka bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su dɛɛ ti ye i ce i aceeri n a.

ref: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
text: Nzɛn i ti sɔ, nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ ma Moyisi yo faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari minɛm a? Nyɛmɛ koro ka minɛ si kabo satiɛ n ti wɔ, ye i faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari a, fa kɔ ju saŋga bo Abraham busu nu­fɔ ni bá ba. Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni Abraham n a. Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari i mɛrɛkɛm wɔ, na ba kan akere Moyisi bo i wo Nyɛmɛ ni i minɛm afiɛn, na i gusu anya akan akere minɛm ni.

ref: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
text: Ama, ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, adena minɛm bo bu yoori Yesu yarada ni, bɛrɛ nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma yarada­yo­fɔm n a.

ref: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
text: Nyini ti, kana Yesu bá ba ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti ka sɔnɔ bo i bita ya a, haari na Yesu aba aba, adena Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, ya yarada n dɛɛ ti.

ref: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
text: Ama, ahin saŋga n bo ya yo Yesu yarada ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bita má ya kun.

ref: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am ayo Yesu Kristo yarada ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye am ayo Nyɛmɛ nbaam a.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
text: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu biɛɛri am Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, am ni Kristo ayo kun lɛɛ, nyini kere ka am ade i dɛɛ woo­wa n wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
text: Nyini ti, kabo am ni Kristo ayo kun ni, am kɛrɛ ti deke kun wɔ lɛɛ. Bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, bo bu ti akɔɔ­nbaam ni bo bu ti má akɔɔ­nbaam, nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram kɛrɛ akaaki deke kun wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
text: Kisa am ti Kristo dɛɛ minɛm wɔ, nyini la ka, am ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm wɔ, bo am nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

ref: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
text: Saŋga bo i toko ti kaan ni, i wo i minɛm saa nu wɔ, ye bu niɛ i su a, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo i si seeri ka i di aja ni.

ref: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
text: Ya gusu yo ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, dama saŋga bo ya yo si má Yesu jɔrɛ ni, ya yo ti akɔɔm wɔ, fa kɔ durunya n nu satiɛ yoo­wam su.

ref: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
text: Ama, saŋga n ba juuri ni, na Nyɛmɛ asunma i Wa, na ba wu i kabo bu wu adamande ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni bita i a,

ref: so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children.
text: adena i koro de minɛm bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bita bu n tii ji, na bu yo Nyɛmɛ nbaam nahɔrɛ su.

ref: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
text: Ya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam dɛɛ ti ye i sunmaari i Wa n dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ n ka i ba wura ya ahore­mbaa su. Nyini ti, ye ya koro bo fere Nyɛmɛ ka, “ Abba,” i bu la ka, “M Si.”

ref: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
text: Nyini ti, a ti má akɔɔ kun, a ti Nyɛmɛ wa a. Nzɛn a nya ti Nyɛmɛ wa, nyini la ka, i má u deke n kɛrɛ bo i fa ma i nbaam ni. Kabo Pɔɔlu cɛcɛ woori Galatiya­fɔm nu ni

ref: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
text: Saŋga bo am nya si má Nyɛmɛ ni, am yo suuri dekem bo bu ti má Nyɛmɛ n wɔ, ye am ayo bu akɔɔm a.

ref: My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me
text: M ninbaam, m susere am wɔ, am ma am woo­wa ti kabo m woo­wa ti barasu ni, dama m woo­wa ti kabo am woo­wa ti n wɔ. Am ado má mini ŋgasi be.

ref: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
text: Am si ka saŋga bo m du mɔ ba kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, m yo la má laifiɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye m koro nyaari atin a.

ref: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
text: Haari ni bo m yo la má laifiɛ n kɛrɛ ni, na i yoori kekereke ma am ni, am akete má m, abu má m ŋgbɛɛn, ama, am deeri m ni am saa nnyɔ ɔ, kabo má am de Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ wara Yesu Kristo barasu n wɔ.

ref: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
text: Nzu ti ye kisa am ahore ti má jɔɔ­wa kabo i yo ti dawa n a? Nyini saŋga ni, mini mmɔɔ ni m tii si ka, nzɛn má m yo seeri ka am lokoti yi am nyu­mbaa fa ma m, na má am koro yo i, má am yó i wɔ.

ref: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
text: Bo m tu am fɔ seeri am nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye kisa ma kaaki am kpɔ­fɔ a?

ref: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
