text: Nyini minɛm bo bu boro bu nyi na bu cɛcɛ wo am nu ni, bu sunsunni ti má kpa. Bu koro ka am kaaki yaki Kristo atin n wɔ, na am boro am nyi su bu dɛɛ atin n su.

ref: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
text: Nzɛn má am nyunu aboro nahɔrɛ su ka am yo sakpa saŋga kɛrɛ, má i ti kpa, nzɛn haari m wo má am dɔ mmɔɔ.

ref: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
text: M ahore su, má m koro ka m wo am dɔ kisa, na m da m bɔɛ na ya bo anzama, dama ma tanda fa kɔ am dɔ. Saara ni Haga woo­wa n dɛɛ jɔrɛ

ref: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am koro ka am su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ni, am si má kabo i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
text: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Abraham yo la nbaa nbiɛsɔm nnyɔ ɔ, baa n bo afani n wuuri i ni, ni baa n bo awuru­bara wuuri i ni.

ref: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
text: Baa n bo afani n wuuri i ni, kabo bara kɛrɛ nyinsɛ, na bu wu baa ni, i nyinsɛ wuuri i sɔ ɔ. Ama, awuru­bara n dɛɛ wa n bo i wuuri i ni, kabo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Abraham n ye i wuuri i a.

ref: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
text: Kabo nbara nnyɔm woo­wa n ti má kun na bu woo­wa wo i ŋgumi ni, i la bu wɔ. Bu ti ka sɛsɛ­su nnyɔm bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di n wɔ. Kun la Haga, afani n bo i dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti akɔɔm ni. I kere kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i yo fa maari Moyisi Sinayi Buka n su n maari minɛm yoori akɔɔm barasu n wɔ.

ref: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
text: Haga ade Sinayi Buka n bo i wo Arabiya asiɛn n dɛɛ jinaa­biri wɔ, i ti ka Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n bo i wo durunya n nu fɛn kisa n wɔ, i ni i busu nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ti akɔɔm wɔ.

ref: But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave.
text: Ama, Yɛrusalem n bo i wo aŋgoro n dɛɛ minɛm anya bu tii, bɛrɛ ti awuru­bara Saara dɛɛ nbaam a, yiri ti ya ni a.

ref: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Wɔrɔ bara ŋɔrɔka ni, bo a wu má baa ni, ma u ahore jɔ kɔ sin su. Tiɛn, na a siri ni ahorejɔ, wɔrɔ bara n bo a si má baa­wu yaari ni, dama bara n bo i hun akete i n la nbaam tara bara n bo i la hun ni.”

ref: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
text: Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n se sɛ wɔ? I ŋa, “Fɔn yi ahin afani n ni i wa n fite fɛn. Wɔ ti má ye ni ka ahin bara n wa n nya aja neŋge n be. Ama, i ti ye ni ka awuru­bara n wa ni fá i kɛrɛ a.”

ref: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
text: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, ya ti má afani n dɛɛ nbaam, ama, ya ti awuru­bara n dɛɛ nbaam wɔ.

ref: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
text: Kristo ade ya tii aji, nyini ti, am wo ni nyini tii­de n bo am anya i n nu, am bita i kenken na má am wo ka am ti akɔɔm kun.

ref: And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am sunsun ka am su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, na am ŋa nyini ma Nyɛmɛ de am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, am ni Kristo akpaaki lɛɛ, am di má Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n kun.

ref: But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ.
text: Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya yo yarada, ye ya la ahoresi ŋminda ka Nyɛmɛ bá de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

ref: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
text: Nzɛn ya ni Kristo ayo kun, na ya wura kɛnkɛnnɛ, i ti má pui, nzɛn ya wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ gusu, i ti má pui. Ama, nzɛn ya yo Yesu Kristo yarada, na ya koro ya beŋgum na ya yo sakpa, nyini ti jɔrɛ n a.

ref: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
text: Nyini nɔaniɛ n bo ba fa akaaki am sunsunni n fite má Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ fere am ka am ba su i n dɔ.

ref: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
text: Ama, m la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ, kabo ya ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ayo kun ni, ka, am káaki má am sunsunni na am yaki atin n bo ya bita i ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka am kaaki am sunsunni ni, Nyɛmɛ cín i mi sui.

ref: My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised.
text: M ninbaam, nbem ŋa m toko nati kan i ka i ti ye ni ka Kristo minɛm kɛrɛ wura kɛnkɛnnɛ. Nzɛn má i ti nahɔrɛ ka m kan i sɔ ɔ, ye nzu ti ye Yuda­fɔm kere m wuwuruwa n a? Nzɛn má m kan i sɔ, ye haari nzɛn m kan fa kɔ yiwee n bo Yesu wuuri i baka jaanu n su ni, má i ti má jɔrɛ.

ref: I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
text: Minɛm bo bu miɛ am ka am wura kɛnkɛnnɛ ni, má i yo m fiɛ ka bu sa bu ŋu na má bu biɛsɔ dika yo juma.

ref: My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love.
text: M ninbaam, Nyɛmɛ afere am ka i de am tii ji wɔ. Ama, má am niɛ ka am anya am tii n dɛɛ ti, na am yo am kunnu koroo­wa, ama, am koro am beŋgu nahɔrɛ su, na am buka am beŋgu.

ref: All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ jina nɔaniɛ maa­wa kun su a, yiri la ka, “Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.”

ref: But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves. God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
text: Ama, nzɛn saŋga kɛrɛ na am sudi aluwa na am ŋumi sukpi am beŋgu ŋu, na am asi má am ŋu sa, am sáaki am ŋuŋu nu wɔ lɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n ni adamande woo­wa

ref: But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
text: Nzɛn am yo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n koro ka am yo ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má am su kun.

ref: People's desires make them give in to immoral ways, filthy thoughts, and shameful deeds.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si kabo minɛm bo bu su adamande woo­wa su n ti barasu. Yiri la sakaraya, ni daŋgba sa­yoo­wam, ni konvikiya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm.

ref: They worship idols, practice witchcraft, hate others, and are hard to get along with. People become jealous, angry, and selfish. They not only argue and cause trouble, but they are
text: Na bu su amɔɛ, ni ayiri di ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, na bu sukpɔ minɛ, na bu sukpini jɔrɛ, na bu susi kɔrɛ, na bu sufa yaa, na bu suyo anyii sam, na bu suse akuruwa kpakpaaki bu ŋuŋu nu,

ref: envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
text: na bu susi bu beŋgum kɔrɛ, [na bu sukun minɛ,] na bu sunu nzan bo, na bu suyo ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kabo nzan nu­fɔm yo barasu ni, na bu suyo sa barasu nbem. Kabo ma du mɔ ase am ni, m toko bɔbɔ am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo bu yo nyini sam ni koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

ref: gentle, and self-controlled. There is no law against behaving in any of these ways.
text: na bu yo bu ŋu tarakam, na bu la tara­buŋu. Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa be wo má bɛrɛ bo i jaraki minɛm bo bu bita nyini woo­wa atin ni.

ref: God's Spirit has given us life, and so we should follow the Spirit.
text: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya ŋgɔɔ. Nyini ti, am ma ya wo kabo i kere ya ka ya wo ni.

ref: But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
text: Má am ma ya wura ya ŋu kpiri, na má am ma ya yo sa bo i yɛ minɛ ahore su, na má ya si kɔrɛ.

ref: My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself.
text: M ninbaam, nzɛn am ŋu ka am nu be suyo satiɛ, ambɛrɛ bo am wo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n kere ka am wo barasu ni, am tara am ŋu bɛnlɛ, na am buka i mi na i yo sa bo i ti kpa. Na am si am ŋu sa, na má am gusu yo satiɛ be.

ref: If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong.
text: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a ti má pui, na a sunsun ka a ti be ni, a dadaka u ŋu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Do your own work well, and then you will have something to be proud of. But don't compare yourself with others.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ niɛ i sa­yoo­wa, nzɛn i mi ŋu ka i sa­yoo­wa ti kpa, nyini saŋga, i mi koro kansi i ŋu. Ama, má i niɛ ka i sa­yoo­wa tara i beŋgum dɛɛ ti, na i fa fɔ.

ref: We each must carry our own load.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la i tiɛŋgu sa­yoo­wa.

ref: Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo bu sukere am Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ni, am buka bɛrɛ n bo bu kere am ni, ni deke kɛrɛ bo am la i.

ref: You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant.
text: Má am dadaka am ŋu, na am sunsun ka be koro dadaka Nyɛmɛ, dama deke kɛrɛ bo sɔnɔ yoori, Nyɛmɛ fá nyini tɔ i mi kaari a.

ref: Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up.
text: Nyini ti, má am ma ya fie ni sakpa­yo, dama nzɛn ya fie má, na saŋga n ba juuri, Nyɛmɛ tɔ́ ya ŋkatɔ.

ref: You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
text: Am niɛ, mini ni m tii sufa m saa sukɛrɛ ahin kɛrɛɛ­wa n ŋgbiŋgbiri n kɛrɛ ma am a.

ref: Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ.
text: Minɛm bo bu miɛ am ka am wura kɛnkɛnnɛ ni, bu koro ka minɛ kansi bu wɔ. Deke n bo i ti na bu yo sɔ n la ka, bu koro má ka minɛ kere bu wuwuruwa fa kɔ yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri i baka jaanu n su ni.

ref: But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
text: Ama, mini yiri, m fɔ má yanfɔya fa kɔ deke be su, má ka fa kɔ yiwee n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo wuuri baka jaanu n su ni. Nyini yiwee n bo i wuuri i n dɛɛ ti, mini dɔ, m bu durunya n ka i ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, na durunya n gusu bu m ka m ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
text: Nyɛmɛ ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su ahin atin n bo m kere n su ahoreda, na i si bu aŋunfɔ, dama bɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu ti Yisareli­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

ref: On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
text: Kisa m koro má ka be kpini jɔrɛ be ma m kun, dama m la deke bo i wo m ŋuniɛ su sukere ka m ti Yesu dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
text: M ninbaam, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am kɛrɛ. Ami.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. To God's people who live in Ephesus and are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu bo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu na m ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa ni, sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo am wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu, na am bita Yesu Kristo atin n nahɔrɛ su, na am ni yiri ayo kun ni.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
text: Ya si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am na bu ma am ahoreda. Kabo Nyɛmɛ ayo ya kisi ni

ref: Before the world was created, God had Christ choose us to live with him and to be his holy and innocent and loving people.
text: Dama saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ nya yi má durunya ni, ye i yiiri ya ka ya yo i minɛm a, na ya ni Kristo yo kun, adena ya yo casi, na má be koro jaraki ya Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Koro n bo i koro ya n dɛɛ ti,

ref: God was kind and decided that Christ would choose us to be God's own adopted children.
text: wɔ du mɔ acici ka i fá ya yo i kunnu nbaam Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Deke bo i koro ka i yo i ni, i koroo­wa lɛɛ.

ref: Christ sacrificed his life's blood to set us free, which means our sins are now forgiven. Christ did this because of God's gift of undeserved grace to us. God has great wisdom and understanding,
text: Kabo ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun ni, i mbunja n bo i fiteeri i ŋuniɛ nu saŋga bo i wuuri n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ade ya tii aji, afa ya satiɛ kɛrɛ ace ya. Wɔ buka ya agaya, ama ya ahɔrɔma bo i la má aŋusu, wɔ ma ya alecira ni si barasu kɛrɛ.

ref: Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
text: Nyini deke n bo Nyɛmɛ koroori ka i yo i n la ka, nzɛn saŋga n ba juuri, i bó deke kɛrɛ kun, bo bu wo aŋgoro, ni bo bu wo asiɛŋgu n kɛrɛ, na i má Yesu Kristo yo i kɛrɛ tii.

ref: He did this so we Jews would bring honor to him and be the first ones to have hope because of him.
text: Adena minɛm ŋu ya woo­wa, yɛrɛ bo ya du mɔ afa ahoresi ni Kristo ni, na bu kansi Nyɛmɛ na bu wura i kpiri.

ref: Christ also brought you the truth, which is the good news about how you can be saved. You put your faith in Christ and were given the promised Holy Spirit to show you belong to God.
text: I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ am gusu dɔ. Am tiiri labari kpa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ na i ma am nya tii­de n jɔrɛ ni, ye am yoori Kristo yarada a, ye Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase am nyaari, kabo i yo seeri ni, yiri kere ka am ti i dɛɛ minɛm a.

ref: I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, fite saŋga bo m tiiri ka am ayo Kristo yarada ni, ni kabo am koro Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ barasu ni,

ref: So I never stop being grateful for you, as I mention you in my prayers.
text: am woo­wa dɛɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ m da Nyɛmɛ asi, na m susere i ma am.

ref: I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God.
text: M sere ya Si Nyɛmɛ, i jirima woo­wa la má aŋusu. Yiri ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Nyɛmɛ a. M sereeri i ka i ma i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n buka am, na am nya alecira, na i teke am nyi, bo am si Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su.

ref: My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
text: M kaaki sere i ka i teke am nyi na am ŋu i wein ni, adena am si deke n bo i ti na wɔ fere am n na am la ahoresi fa kɔ i busu ni, na am kaaki si deke n bo i ti kpa na i la jirima agaya, bo wɔ se ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

ref: I want you to know about the great and mighty power that God has for us followers. It is the same wonderful power he used
text: Na am si kabo i yiko n la má aŋusu barasu ni, na i koro yo juma ma yɛrɛ n bo ya yo i yarada ni. Nyini yiko ni ti bambaka la faŋga a,

ref: when he raised Christ from death and let him sit at his right side in heaven.
text: yiri ye Nyɛmɛ fa teŋgeeri Kristo i yiwee n nu, afa i atana aŋgoro i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n a.

ref: There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well.
text: Deke bo i wo lɛɛ, i la yiko tara yiko­fɔm kɛrɛ, amɔɛ o, ayiri o, biɛfɔya o, mɛrɛkɛ o, jina o, ŋumi o, ni ajini o, na i duma n tara duma kɛrɛ bo i wo ahin durunya n bo ya wo nu ni, ni durunya n bo i suba ni.

ref: God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything.
text: Nyɛmɛ afa deke n kɛrɛ awura Kristo saa nu ka i niɛ bu su, na wɔ ma yiri Kristo bo i niɛ deke n kɛrɛ su n ayo Kristo jama n kɛrɛ tii a.

ref: The church is the body of Christ and is filled with Christ who completely fills everything.
text: Kristo jama n kɛrɛ ti i ŋuniɛ n a, ye i wo bu nu, ye i woo­wa ade deke n kɛrɛ a.

ref: In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God.
text: Saŋga bo am yo keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ na am yo satiɛ ni, am ti ka am awu wɔ.

ref: You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God.
text: Nyini saŋga ni, am yo satiɛ kabo kisa durunya n nu­fɔm yo n wɔ. Na am sude Sitana bo i bita asiɛn ni aŋgoro afiɛn n yiko na am de i nɔaniɛ. Yiri bita minɛm kɛrɛ bo ba kete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a.

ref: But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you.
text: ya yo ti ka ya wuuri wɔ, fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ n bo ya keteeri n su ni, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, i koro n dɛɛ ti, wɔ saŋga ama ya ni Kristo ŋgɔɔ. I dɛɛ ahɔrɔma ni ma am nyaari tii­de a.

ref: God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven.
text: Ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ ateŋge ya yiwee nu kabo i teŋgeeri Kristo i yiwee n nu ni, na ya ni yiri kɛrɛ saŋga tana Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

ref: God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ yoori sɔ n la ka, fa i kisa fa kɔ, i kére i ahɔrɔma n bo i la má aŋusu ni, yiri la kabo i ma Yesu Kristo ayo sakpa abuka ya barasu ni.

ref: God planned for us to do good things and to live as he has always wanted us to live. This is why he sent Christ to make us what we are. United by Christ
text: Kabo ya ti kisa ni, Nyɛmɛ yiiri ya sɔ a. Ya ni Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ ma ya ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ a, ka ya fa yo sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ yo fa seeri ya ka ya yo i n a. Kristo ni i minɛm kɛrɛ ayo kun

ref: At that time you did not know about Christ. You were foreigners to the people of Israel, and you had no part in the promises God had made to them. You were living in this world without hope and without God,
text: Am teŋge su ka saŋga bo am yo si má Kristo ni, am wo dede ni yiri wɔ, na am ti má minɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu n be. Sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ yo diiri i di seeri ka i búka i minɛm ni, am wo má nu. Am yo wo durunya n nu wɔ, ama, am yo la má ahoresi be, na am kaaki si má Nyɛmɛ.

ref: and you were far from God. But Christ offered his life's blood as a sacrifice and brought you near God.
text: Ama, kisa, na am ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun ni, ambɛrɛ bo má am yo wo dede ni Nyɛmɛ ni, Kristo mbunja n bo i fiteeri i ŋuniɛ n nu, saŋga bo i wuuri ni, acin am adodo Nyɛmɛ a.

ref: Christ has made peace between Jews and Gentiles, and he has united us by breaking down the wall of hatred that separated us. Christ gave his own body
text: Dama Kristo ti sɔnɔ bo wɔ ma ya sɛsɛ­su ni tana­laifiɛ a. Yiri yo faari i ŋu yoori yoo­wa yiiri kpɔ bo i ti ka kobe na i wo Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n afiɛn a, na wɔ yo bu kun.

ref: On the cross Christ did away with our hatred for each other. He also made peace between us and God by uniting Jews and Gentiles in one body.
text: I yiwee n bo i wuuri baka jaanu n su n dɛɛ ti, wɔ yi kpɔ bo má i wo ya afiɛn ni, ayo ya minɛ kunm akaaki ya aba Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: Christ came and preached peace to you Gentiles, who were far from God, and peace to us Jews, who were near God.
text: Kristo ba kaanri labari kpa n bo i yo minɛm afiɛn dɔŋgu n maari ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm, na am wo dede ni Nyɛmɛ n wɔ, ni Yuda­fɔm bo bu dodo i ni.

ref: And because of Christ, all of us can come to the Father by the same Spirit.
text: Kristo dɛɛ ti, ye Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ atin ka ya koro ba ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

ref: You Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens with everyone else who belongs to the family of God.
text: Nyini ti, ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, kisa am ti má wɔfɔm kun Nyɛmɛ minɛm dɔ, ama, am anya atin abo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm su, ye am ayo i dika­fɔm.

ref: Christ is the one who holds the building together and makes it grow into a holy temple for the Lord.
text: Sɔɔ n deke n kɛrɛ wo yiri su a, ye i maari i yo woo­biri casi ma Nyɛmɛ a.

ref: And you are part of that building Christ has built as a place for God's own Spirit to live.
text: Am ni yiri kɛrɛ ayo kun n dɛɛ ti, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n wɔ lɛɛ, bo i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo nu ni.

ref: Christ Jesus made me his prisoner, so I could help you Gentiles.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu, m woori dan­saraka nu, Yesu Kristo ni ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm n dɛɛ ti wɔ.

ref: You have surely heard about God's gift of undeserved grace in choosing me to help you.
text: Nahɔrɛ, m niɛ ka am ati kabo Nyɛmɛ asi m aŋunfɔ ka m yo ahin juma n ma am ni.

ref: As you read the letter, you will also find out how well I really do understand the mystery about Christ.
text: Nzɛn am kaanri kadasi n bo ma kɛrɛ i ni, am ŋu kabo m koro siiri Kristo ŋusu jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo asiri nu ni.

ref: And the mystery is this: Because of Christ Jesus, the good news has given the Gentiles a share in the promises God gave to the Jews. God has also let the Gentiles be part of the same body.
text: Asiri bo i wo bɛrɛ n la ka, ya de labari kpa n na ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ni bo ti má Yuda­fɔm, ya kɛrɛ sáŋga nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ fá ma i minɛm, na ya sáŋga yo i minɛm, na ya sáŋga nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

ref: God treated me with kindness. His power worked in me, and it became my job to spread the good news.
text: Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, wɔ ce m aceeri ayi m ka m yo i labari kpa n amusui­su­fɔ, ye wɔ ma m yiko ka m fa yo i juma ni.

ref: I am the least important of all God's people. But God was kind and chose me to tell the Gentiles that because of Christ there are blessings that cannot be measured.
text: Nyɛmɛ minɛm n nu, mini ti bu kɛrɛ kaan, ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ ayi m ama m ahin atin n ka m kɔ kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na bu si sakpa bo Kristo ayo i, na be koro si má i kɛrɛ bu ni.

ref: God did this according to his eternal plan. And he was able to do what he had planned because of all Christ Jesus our Lord had done.
text: Nyɛmɛ yoori nyini kɛrɛ, kabo dawa na i sunsun ka i yo i ni, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, ye i ma i kɛrɛ yoori yii­wa a.

ref: Christ now gives us courage and confidence, so we can come to God by faith.
text: Kabo ya yo Kristo yarada na ya ni yiri ayo kun ni, nyini ma ya la ahoresi koro ba Nyɛmɛ nyunu na ya soro má sɛrɛ n a.

ref: This is why you should not be discouraged when I suffer for you. After all, it will bring honor to you. Christ's Love for Us
text: Nyini ti, m sere am wɔ, má am ma am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am, fa kɔ wahara n bo m sudi i ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ni, i ti deke bo i wura am kpiri wɔ. Koro bo Kristo fa koro i minɛm barasu ni

ref: All beings in heaven and on earth receive their life from him.
text: Yiri ti lamɔsi kɛrɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu n kɛrɛ Si a.

ref: God is wonderful and glorious. I pray that his Spirit will make you become strong followers
text: M nyɛmɛsere la ka, i la jirima woo­wa bo i la má aŋusu n dɛɛ ti, i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma am nya yiko bambaka am ahore su,

ref: and that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. Stand firm and be deeply rooted in his love.
text: na kabo am yo Kristo yarada ni, i wo am ahore su, na m kaaki sere i ka am la koro nahɔrɛ su, na am jina kenken, fa kɔ koro n bo am la i n su.

ref: I pray that you and all God's people will understand what is called wide or long or high or deep.
text: Adena am ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ saŋga fiiti ni anyum­boro, na am si kabo Kristo koro am agaya, ni kabo i koro la má aŋusu ni.

ref: I want you to know all about Christ's love, although it is too wonderful to be measured. Then your lives will be filled with all that God is.
text: M nyɛmɛsere bekun la ka, i ma am kaaki si kabo Kristo koro am ni, haari ni bo adamande koro si má nyini koro n bo i la má aŋusu ni, na m sere i ka i ma am woo­wa kɛrɛ ti kabo yiri Nyɛmɛ woo­wa ti barasu ni.

ref: I pray that Christ Jesus and the church will forever bring praise to God. His power at work in us can do far more than we dare ask or imagine. Amen.
text: Nyɛmɛ ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga, yɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo ya ti Kristo jama n na ya ni Kristo ayo deke kun ni. Ami.

ref: As a prisoner of the Lord, I beg you to live in a way that is worthy of the people God has chosen to be his own.
text: Nyini ti, mini Pɔɔlu bo m su Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti na m wo dan­saraka nu ni, m susere am jande ka, am wo wo bo i kɔ atin su, kabo Nyɛmɛ fereeri am na i kereeri am ka am wo barasu ni.

ref: Always be humble and gentle. Patiently put up with each other and love each other.
text: Am yo am ŋu kaan na am yo bɛnlɛ, na am yo sawɔrɛ, na am la mindi de am beŋgu ni koro, saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Try your best to let God's Spirit keep your hearts united. Do this by living at peace.
text: Anɔɔsɛ n bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama am na am ayo kun n dɛɛ ti, am boro am nyi na am tana laifiɛ na am bita nyini anɔɔsɛ n ni am beŋgu.

ref: All of you are part of the same body. There is only one Spirit of God, just as you were given one hope when you were chosen to be God's people.
text: Yɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo ya ti Kristo minɛm ni ayo ka aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun wɔ. Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi kun ni wo ya kɛrɛ dɔ a, na ya kɛrɛ kaaki la ahoresi fa kɔ deke kun n bo i ti na wɔ fere ya ni.

ref: There is one God who is the Father of all people. Not only is God above all others, but he works by using all of us, and he lives in all of us.
text: Nyɛmɛ kun wo bɛrɛ a, yiri ti ya kɛrɛ Si a, yiri kaaki ti ya kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a, i ma ya kɛrɛ yo i juma a, na i wo ya kɛrɛ dɔ.

ref: Christ has generously divided out his gifts to us.
text: Ama, Kristo akpaaki i aceeri n ama ya kun kun n kɛrɛ kabo i koro ni.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “When he went up to the highest place, he led away many prisoners and gave gifts to people.”
text: Nyini ti, ye ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Saŋga bo i fun kɔɔri aŋgoro ni, na wɔ fa domuɛm akɔ, na wɔ ce minɛ aceerim.”

ref: This also means that the one who went deep into the earth is the same one who went into the highest heaven, so he would fill the whole universe.
text: Yiri n bo i jura baari asiɛŋgu n la bo wɔ fun akɔ aŋgoro dede n wɔ, ye i woo­wa ade deke n kɛrɛ a.

ref: This will continue until we are united by our faith and by our understanding of the Son of God. Then we will be mature, just as Christ is, and we will be completely like him.
text: Na ya kɛrɛ fa anɔɔsɛ fa kɔ kabo ya yo Nyɛmɛ Wa n yarada ni, na ya si i nahɔrɛ su subuka su, na ya yarada yo bii­wa, na ya kɔ ya nyunu buka su, na ya woo­wa kɛrɛ ti kabo Kristo wo ti barasu ni.

ref: We must stop acting like children. We must not let deceitful people trick us by their false teachings, which are like winds that toss us around from place to place.
text: Na má ya yo ka nbaatumam, adena má kɔrɛ­fɔm ba fa bu ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ kere ya na bu dadaka ya, na ya mini.

ref: Love should always make us tell the truth. Then we will grow in every way and be more like Christ, the head
text: Ama, am ma ya koro ya beŋgu, na ya sujɔjɔ nahɔrɛ, na ya kɔ ya nyunu buka su, na ya woo­wa kɛrɛ ti kabo Kristo woo­wa ti barasu ni, dama yiri ti ya tii a.

ref: Their minds are in the dark, and they are stubborn and ignorant and have missed out on the life that comes from God. They no longer have any feelings about what is right,
text: Bu wo awosin nu n dɛɛ ti, bu la má ŋgɔɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ma i minɛm ni, dama bu si má be, na bu ahore­mbaa kaaki ti kekereke n dɛɛ ti.

ref: But this isn't what you were taught about Jesus Christ. He is the truth, and you heard about him and learned about him.
text: M niɛ ka am adu mɔ asɔnsɔn i ŋusu jɔrɛ kɛrɛ, am nya ti i susu­fɔm ni, ba kere am ka Yesu ti bo i la nahɔrɛ n a.

ref: You were told that your foolish desires will destroy you and that you must give up your old way of life with all its bad habits.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am yaki am woo­wa dawa n bo i ti má kpa n ji. Am woo­wa dawa n koro ka i daka am na i saaki am a, dama i ma am yo dekem bo bu ti má kpa a, na bu mini am.

ref: Let the Spirit change your way of thinking
text: Am ma Nyɛmɛ kaaki am sunsunni ni am ahore­mbaa fɔfɔrɛ.

ref: and make you into a new person. You were created to be like God, and so you must please him and be truly holy. Rules for the New Life
text: Na am fa wo fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama am na i ferema ni yiri Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ woo­wa ni. Nyini woo­wa ni kɔ atin su a, na i kaaki ti casi nahɔrɛ su a.

ref: We are part of the same body. Stop lying and start telling each other the truth.
text: Nyini ti, má am bu kɔrɛ, am kan nahɔrɛ ma am beŋgu, dama ya kɛrɛ ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun n bue barasu be wɔ.

ref: Don't get so angry that you sin. Don't go to bed angry
text: Nzɛn am be fa yaa, má i yo satiɛ, má i fa yaa haari wiɛ kɔ tɔ.

ref: and don't give the devil a chance.
text: Má am ma Sitana atin haari kaan sɔ, na i ma am yo satiɛ.

ref: If you are a thief, quit stealing. Be honest and work hard, so you will have something to give to people in need.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo wɔ, i ti ye ni ka i mi yaki awie, na i yo i tiɛŋgu juma ni anyum­boro su, na i nya i koroo­wa, adena i koro buka yaari­fɔm.

ref: Don't make God's Spirit sad. The Spirit makes you sure that someday you will be free from your sins.
text: Má am saaki Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ahore, dama yiri ye Nyɛmɛ afa i ase am nyaari a, ka i bá de am tii ji cɛɛn be a.

ref: Stop being bitter and angry and mad at others. Don't yell at one another or curse each other or ever be rude.
text: Má am ma am kunnu yo wiwi, má am ma yaa ti am, má am kan am beŋgu ndiɛ, má am se akuruwa, má am kpiɛ am beŋgu nzukɔ, má am kpɔ am beŋgu deke be ti.

ref: Instead, be kind and merciful, and forgive others, just as God forgave you because of Christ.
text: Ama, am yo am beŋgu kpa, na am si am beŋgu aŋunfɔ, na am fa am beŋgum ŋgasi ce bu kabo Nyɛmɛ afa ace am Kristo dɛɛ ti ni.

ref: Let love be your guide. Christ loved us and offered his life for us as a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: I ti ye ni ka am ma koro wo am dɔ, kabo Kristo koro ya barasu na i faari i ŋu yoori yoo­wa maari ya na i suunri Nyɛmɛ nu ni.

ref: You are God's people, so don't let it be said that any of you are immoral or indecent or greedy.
text: Kabo am ti Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni, má am yo sakaraya, má am yo daŋgba sam, má am se ka am nyunu boro neŋge kpini su, má am bo nyini sereyam n be mmɔɔ anzama am nu.

ref: Being greedy, indecent, or immoral is just another way of worshiping idols. You can be sure people who behave in this way will never be part of the kingdom that belongs to Christ and to God. Living as People of Light
text: Am si tenle su ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sakaraya, ni daŋgba sam, ni bo i nyunu boro neŋge kpini su, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sɔ, i mi su má Nyɛmɛ, i su neŋgem wɔ. Kristo ni Nyɛmɛ bá niɛ má i su, na i nya má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

ref: Don't let anyone trick you with foolish talk. God punishes everyone who disobeys him and says foolish things.
text: Má am ma be fa i jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn be dadaka am, dama nyinim sɔ dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ fá yaa cin minɛm bo ba kete ka bu de má i nɔaniɛ n sui n a.

ref: So don't have anything to do with anyone like that.
text: Nyini ti, má am ni nyini minɛm sɔ saŋga wo.

ref: You used to be like people living in the dark, but now you are people of the light because you belong to the Lord. So act like people of the light
text: Saŋga be, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii yo woori awosin nu yo satiɛ wɔ, ama, kisa, na am akaaki ya Mibiɛ Yesu minɛm ni, am ti wein nu­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ. Nyini ti, am ma am woo­wa kere ka am ti wein nu­fɔm wɔ.

ref: Don't take part in doing those worthless things that are done in the dark. Instead, show how wrong they are.
text: Má am fa am ŋu bo minɛm bo bu wo awosin nu n su, na am yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn n bo bu yo i ni, ama, am yi bu nyini sa­yoo­wam fite fofoe nu.

ref: It is disgusting even to talk about what is done in the dark.
text: I ti nyinsɛ wɔ mmɔɔ, ka ya bo su fa kɔ bu nyini sam bo bu yo bu asiri nu ni.

ref: Light shows up everything, just as the Scriptures say, “Wake up from your sleep and rise from death. Then Christ will shine on you.”
text: Dama deke kɛrɛ bo i nya fiteeri fofoe nu ti wein wɔ. Nyini ti ye ba se ka, “Wɔrɔ daafiri­fɔ ni, yasu na a teŋge u yiwee nu, na Kristo dɛɛ wein n ta gɔ u ŋu.”

ref: Don't be stupid. Instead, find out what the Lord wants you to do.
text: Nyini ti, má am yo ka sinnzinm, ama, am boro am nyi, na am si deke bo Mibiɛ n koro ka am yo i.

ref: Don't destroy yourself by getting drunk, but let the Spirit fill your life.
text: Má am nu nzan bo, dama nzan saaki sɔnɔ tii wɔ, ama, am ma Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Always use the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to thank God the Father for everything. Wives and Husbands
text: Am da ya Si Nyɛmɛ asi saŋga kɛrɛ, fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ su, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Nbaram ni bu hunm jɔrɛ

ref: Honor Christ and put others first.
text: Am soro Kristo n dɛɛ ti, am de am beŋgu nɔaniɛ.

ref: A wife should put her husband first, as she does the Lord.
text: Nbaram, am de am hunm nɔaniɛ kabo am de Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ barasu ni.

ref: A husband is the head of his wife, as Christ is the head and the Savior of the church, which is his own body.
text: Dama biɛsɔ la yiko niɛ i yi su, kabo Kristo la yiko niɛ i jama n su barasu ni. Kristo jama n ti i ŋuniɛ­mbaa a, yiri ni i tii, ti bu Tii­de­fɔ a.

ref: Wives should always put their husbands first, as the church puts Christ first.
text: Kabo Kristo jama de i nɔaniɛ barasu ni, sɔ ye i ti ye ni ka nbaram de bu hunm nɔaniɛ deke kɛrɛ nu n a.

ref: A husband should love his wife as much as Christ loved the church and gave his life for it.
text: Nbiɛsɔm, am koro am yim kabo Kristo koro i jama n na i tɔɔri i ŋgɔɔ wu maari bu barasu ni,

ref: He made the church holy by the power of his word, and he made it pure by washing it with water.
text: adena i yo bu casi wɔ. Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo bu tiiri i ni, ye bu biɛɛri bu Nyɛmɛ nzue yiiri satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo bu ahore su a,

ref: Christ did this, so he would have a glorious and holy church, without faults or spots or wrinkles or any other flaws.
text: adena i fa bu bo i ŋu su na bu wo i dɔ, na bu yo nyumi agaya, na bu yo casi na bu la má fin be wara bu la má ŋgasi be, na be koro jaraki má bu.

ref: In the same way, a husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself. A husband who loves his wife shows he loves himself.
text: Sɔ ye i ti ye ni ka nbiɛsɔm koro bu yim kabo bu koro bu ŋu barasu n a. Dama biɛsɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i yi koro i ŋu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: None of us hate our own bodies. We provide for them and take good care of them, just as Christ does for the church,
text: Sɔnɔ be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro má i ŋu, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ niɛ i ŋu su wɔ nyunyumi, kabo Kristo niɛ i jama su barasu ni,

ref: because we are each part of his body.
text: dama ya kɛrɛ ti Kristo ŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother to get married, and he becomes like one person with his wife.”
text: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Nyini dɛɛ ti, biɛsɔ yaki i si, ni i ni, na i fa i ŋu mata i yi, nyini saŋga bu minɛ nnyɔ n ayo deke kun lɛɛ.”

ref: This is a great mystery, but I understand it to mean Christ and his church.
text: Asiri nu jɔrɛ bambaka be wo ahin nɔaniɛ n nu, na ma ŋu i la ka, i sukan fa kɔ Kristo ni i jama n jɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: So each husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself, and each wife should respect her husband.
text: Ama, jɔrɛ n bo m koro ka m se am n la ka, i ti ye ni ka biɛsɔ kɛrɛ koro i yi, kabo i koro i ŋu ni, na bara kɛrɛ bu i hun.

ref: Children, you belong to the Lord, and you do the right thing when you obey your parents. The first commandment with a promise says,
text: Nbaatumam, am de am wu­fɔm nɔaniɛ, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, dama nyini ti kpa a.

ref: Parents, don't be hard on your children. Raise them properly. Teach them and instruct them about the Lord. Slaves and Masters
text: Nbaa­sim, má am yo sa bo i yɛ am nbaam yaa su. Ama, am ta bu ataari bo i ti kpa, na am tu bu fɔ fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su. Akɔɔm ni bu mibiɛm jɔrɛ

ref: Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. You are slaves of Christ, so with your whole heart you must do what God wants you to do.
text: Má am yo ka bu niɛ am dɛɛ ti, na am koro ka am yo na bu kunnu jɔ am ŋu, ama, am yo bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu, ni am ahore­mbaa kun, ka am ti Kristo dɛɛ akɔɔm ti.

ref: Gladly serve your masters, as though they were the Lord himself, and not simply people.
text: Am yo am juma kɛrɛ ma am mibiɛm ni ahorejɔ, ka am suyo ma ya Mibiɛ Yesu wɔ, ama, má ka am suyo ma adamande wɔ.

ref: You know that you will be rewarded for any good things you do, whether you are slaves or free.
text: Nahɔrɛ, ye m ŋa am si ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i juma nahɔrɛ su, Mibiɛ ni tɔ́ i mi ŋkatɔ, nzɛn i ti akɔɔ o, wara i ti má akɔɔ o.

ref: Slave owners, you must treat your slaves with this same respect. Don't threaten them. They have the same Master in heaven that you do, and he doesn't have favorites. The Fight against Evil
text: Ambɛrɛ gusu bo am ti bu mibiɛm ni, am bita bu nyunyumi, má am suwura bu sɛrɛ. Má am ma am wara fi ka am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ n bo i wo aŋgoro n ti kun wɔ. I tambu má nu. Biɛsɔya neŋge bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya n jɔrɛ

ref: Put on all the armor that God gives, so you can defend yourself against the devil's tricks.
text: Am fa biɛsɔya neŋge n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama am n sana, adena am ba koro jina kenken na am kunma Sitana ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ kɛrɛ bo i fa dadaka am ni.

ref: We are not fighting against humans. We are fighting against forces and authorities and against rulers of darkness and powers in the spiritual world.
text: Dama má ya ni adamande kun luɛ n a, ama, ya kun luɛ n ni Sitana ni yiko­fɔm barasu barasum kɛrɛ bo bu wo aŋgoro ni, ni bo bu la yiko bita minɛm bo bu wo awosin nu durunya n nu fɛn n wɔ.

ref: So put on all the armor that God gives. Then when that evil day comes, you will be able to defend yourself. And when the battle is over, you will still be standing firm.
text: Nyini ti, am fa biɛsɔya neŋgem bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama am sana, adena saŋga kɛrɛ bo Sitana ba kun am luɛ, am koro kete i na am kunma i nahɔrɛ su, na am jina kenken.

ref: Your desire to tell the good news about peace should be like shoes on your feet.
text: Na am ma labari kpa n bo i ma ahoreda n yo ka gbagbaa bo am koro fa wura jina kenken.

ref: Let your faith be like a shield, and you will be able to stop all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
text: Am yoori nyini wieeri na am nya fa yarada yo deke bo am fa kanda de Sitana awiɛm kɛrɛ bo i tu bu na bu je ka sin ni.

ref: Let God's saving power be like a helmet, and for a sword use God's message that comes from the Spirit.
text: Am fa tii­de n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama am n yo ka bulaari kere bo am fa butu kanda am tii, na am fa Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n yo ka takobe bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama am.

ref: I pray that God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will give peace, love, and faith to every follower!
text: M sere ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ka bu ma ya ninbaam kɛrɛ ahoreda, ni koro, ni yarada.

ref: May God be kind to everyone who keeps on loving our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Nyɛmɛ buka ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am koro ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo n ni koro bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: From Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus. To all God's people who belong to Christ Jesus at Philippi and to all your bishops and deacons.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu ni Timoti bo ya ti Yesu Kristo akɔɔm ni, yɛrɛ sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo Filipiya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na ba de Yesu Kristo na bu ni yiri ayo kun ni, fa kɔ tu Kristo jama mɔ­fɔm, ni bu buka­fɔm kɛrɛ.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul's Prayer for the Church in Philippi
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am kɛrɛ na bu ma am ahoreda. Kabo Pɔɔlu sereeri Nyɛmɛ maari Filipiya­fɔm ni

ref: Every time I think of you, I thank my God.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo m ba teŋge am jɔrɛ su, m da Nyɛmɛ asi ma am wɔ.

ref: And whenever I mention you in my prayers, it makes me happy.
text: Na saŋga kɛrɛ gusu bo m sere Nyɛmɛ ma am, m sere i ni ahorejɔ ɔ,

ref: This is because you have taken part with me in spreading the good news from the first day you heard about it.
text: dama fite cɛɛn n bo am deeri Kristo labari kpa haari ba ju ni nyuma ni, am abuka m bo ma koro akan Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ akere minɛm.

ref: God is the one who began this good work in you, and I am certain that he won't stop before it is complete on the day that Christ Jesus returns.
text: Nyini ti, m la ahoresi ka Nyɛmɛ wo am dɔ, yiri boori i bu yoori nyini sakpa ni am nu a, yiri súyo i sɔ a, haari kɔ ju cɛɛn n bo Yesu Kristo bá kaaki ba ni.

ref: You have a special place in my heart. So it is only natural for me to feel the way I do. All of you have helped in the work God has given me, as I defend the good news and tell about it here in jail.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ am jɔrɛ wo m ahore su wɔ, nyini ti, kabo m sunsun fa kɔ am dɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka m yo sɔ. Dama am kɛrɛ abuka m fa kɔ juma n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ka m yo i ni. Haari ni bo kisa m wo dan­saraka nu ni, wara saŋga bo m nati m koroya­nu ni, am bukaari m wɔ, na ma kan akere minɛ ka labari kpa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, adena bu yo yarada nahɔrɛ su.

ref: God himself knows how much I want to see you. He knows I care for you in the same way Christ Jesus does.
text: Nyɛmɛ di m daani ka am sa wo m ahore su saŋga kɛrɛ a, ka m koro am ni koro bo Yesu Kristo fa koro ya ni.

ref: I pray that your love will keep on growing and you will fully know and understand
text: M nyɛmɛsere la ka, am sukoro am beŋgu buka su saŋga kɛrɛ, na am susi Nyɛmɛ atin n kɛrɛ, na am si nahɔrɛ atin,

ref: how to make the right choices. Then you will still be pure and innocent when Christ returns. And until that day,
text: adena am si na am koro tambu yi deke bo i ti kpa nahɔrɛ su, na am yo casi na am la má ŋgasi be fa kɔ cɛɛn bo Kristo ba kaaki ba ni.

ref: Jesus Christ will keep you busy doing good deeds that bring glory and praise to God. What Life Means to Paul
text: Na m susere Nyɛmɛ ka Yesu Kristo buka am, na am wo wo kpa, na am suyo sakpa saŋga kɛrɛ, na minɛ ŋu i na bu kansi Nyɛmɛ na bu wura i kpiri. Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti ye m la ŋgɔɔ a

ref: My dear friends, I want you to know that what has happened to me has helped to spread the good news.
text: M ninbaam, m koro ka am si ka sa bo wɔ tu m na m wo dan­saraka n nu ti má tiɛ, nyini ma labari kpa n akoro abara ayɛ a.

ref: The Roman guards and all the others know I am here in jail because I serve Christ.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, tintini­jina­fɔm ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n asi ka, m ti Kristo dɛɛ amusui­su­fɔ dɛɛ ti ye m wo dan­saraka nu a.

ref: Some are preaching about Christ because they are jealous and envious of us. Others are preaching because they want to help.
text: Nahɔrɛ, kɔrɛsi ni akuruwa se ti, ye bu nbem kan Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ n a, ama, nbem gusu kan i ni akunnu fofoe wɔ.

ref: But the ones who are jealous of us are not sincere. They just want to cause trouble for me while I am in jail.
text: Nyiŋgam ni, bu kan má Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ ni bu sunsunni kpa, ama bu bita anyii sunsunni wɔ, adena bu má m di wahara dan­saraka n nu.

ref: But that doesn't matter. All that matters is that people are telling about Christ, whether they are sincere or not. This is what makes me glad. I will keep on being glad,
text: Nyini n kɛrɛ bo bu suyo i ni, i be mmɔɔ kpiɛ má m daafiri. Ama, deke bo i yo m fiɛ n la ka, bu ma Kristo labari kpa ni susandi bara yɛ buka su wɔ. Nzɛn bu kan má i ni bu sunsunni kpa, wara bu kan i ni bu akunnu fofoe, m dɛɛ la ka, saŋga kɛrɛ m ahore ti jɔɔ­wa.

ref: because I know that your prayers and the help that comes from the Spirit of Christ Jesus will keep me safe.
text: Dama m si ka nyɛmɛsere bo am sere Nyɛmɛ ma m saŋga kɛrɛ n ni buka bo m nya i fite Yesu Kristo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ ni, nyini ma m nya m tii a.

ref: I honestly expect and hope I will never do anything to be ashamed of. Whether I live or die, I always want to be as brave as I am now and bring honor to Christ.
text: M fa ahoresi ni tama yo m juma ni anyum­boro saŋga kɛrɛ, na má nyinsɛ ti m, adena nzɛn m wuuri, ni bo ma wu má kɛrɛ, m woo­wa koro ma minɛ kansi Kristo dɛɛ duma.

ref: If I live, it will be for Christ, and if I die, I will gain even more.
text: Mini dɔ, nzɛn m la ŋgɔɔ, Kristo dɛɛ ti a, nzɛn ma wu, i ti m kpɛrɛ wɔ.

ref: It is a hard choice to make. I want to die and be with Christ, because this would be much better.
text: Ma ka afiɛn ni afiɛn wɔ, dama m koro ka má m wu wɔ na m kɔ wo Kristo dɔ, m ŋu ka nyini n ti ye tara durunya n nu fɛn bo m wo ni.

ref: But I know that all of you still need me. This is why I am sure I will stay on to help you grow and be happy in your faith.
text: Tɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, m si tenle su ka m wú má kisa, m wó bɛrɛ wɔ, na m búka am na am koro yo Kristo yarada ni nahɔrɛ, na am ahore jɔ.

ref: Then, when I visit you again, you will have good reason to take great pride in Christ Jesus because of me.
text: Adena nzɛn m ba kaaki ba niɛɛri am busu bekun, nyini saŋga, am lá deke bo am koro fa kansi Yesu Kristo, fa kɔ sa n bo wɔ yo ama m ni.

ref: Above all else, you must live in a way that brings honor to the good news about Christ. Then, whether I visit you or not, I will hear that all of you think alike. I will know that you are working together and are struggling side by side to get others to believe the good news.
text: Tɔ, kisa, jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ la ka, am ma am woo­wa ti kabo Kristo labari kpa n kere ka am wo barasu ni, na nzɛn m ba koro ba niɛɛri am busu, wara ma koro aba má n kɛrɛ, na m ti am jɔrɛ ka Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma am la jinakun­su, na am la sunsunni kun ni anɔɔsɛ, suboro am nyi sutu ŋgoni kan Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm na bu yo yarada.

ref: Be brave when you face your enemies. Your courage will show them that they are going to be destroyed, and it will show you that you will be saved. God will make all of this happen,
text: Na má am soro sɛrɛ fa kɔ am kpɔ­fɔm dɔ, am ma am ahore ti sii­wa saŋga kɛrɛ, nyini má bu ŋu nu na bu sí ka bu ká ŋgbɛɛn a, ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, am nyá tii­de, dama Nyɛmɛ má am nyá tii­de a.

ref: and he has blessed you. Not only do you have faith in Christ, but you suffer for him.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ ace am aceeri Kristo dɛɛ ti, ka am yo Kristo yarada, má nyini ŋgumi, ama, am kaaki di wahara ma i.

ref: You saw me suffer, and you still hear about my troubles. Now you must suffer in the same way.
text: Nyini ti, kisa am koro buka m na ya kɛrɛ kun nyini luɛ ni. Yiri ye am yo ŋuuri ka m tu i ŋgoni n a, ye kisa am ti ka m toko tu i ŋgoni n a.

ref: Now make me completely happy! Live in harmony by showing love for each other. Be united in what you think, as if you were only one person.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m sere am wɔ, jande, am kɛrɛ fa sunsunni kun, na am koro am beŋgu, na am fa anɔɔsɛ, na m ahorejɔ kɔ́ sin su.

ref: and think the same way that Christ Jesus thought:
text: Am ma am sunsunni ti kabo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti barasu ni.

ref: Then God gave Christ the highest place and honored his name above all others.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ awura i kpiri agaya ayɛ i su aŋgoro ama i duma bo i tara duma kɛrɛ n a,

ref: So at the name of Jesus everyone will bow down, those in heaven, on earth, and under the earth.
text: adena minɛ ni deke kɛrɛ bo bu wo aŋgoro, ni bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn, ni bo bu wo asiɛn bu n tiiri Yesu duma ni, bu kútu i na bu sú i.

ref: And to the glory of God the Father everyone will openly agree, “Jesus Christ is Lord!” Lights in the World
text: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ teke i nɔɔ kan ka Yesu Kristo ti deke n kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a, nyini wura ya Si Nyɛmɛ kpiri a. Am ma ya woo­wa ta wein durunya n nu

ref: My dear friends, you always obeyed when I was with you. Now that I am away, you should obey even more. So work with fear and trembling to discover what it really means to be saved.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaa koroo­wam, am yo deeri m nɔaniɛ n wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, kisa bo m wo má am dɔ ni, nyini ti ye ni ka am de m nɔaniɛ n tara kabo m yo wo am dɔ n a. Tii­de n bo am anya i n dɛɛ ti, am yo Nyɛmɛ juma n ni anyum­boro, bo i kere ka am anya tii­de, na am soro Nyɛmɛ.

ref: God is working in you to make you willing and able to obey him.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ suyo i juma am ahore su a, na i ma am de i nɔaniɛ ni, na am yo i koroo­wa.

ref: Then you will be the pure and innocent children of God. You live among people who are crooked and evil, but you must not do anything they can say is wrong. Try to shine as lights among the people of this world,
text: adena am yo minɛ casim bo be koro jaraki má am, na am yo Nyɛmɛ nbaam bo am la má ŋgasi be na am ma am woo­wa ti wein, na am wo nyuma­fɔ satiɛ­yo­fɔ ŋgbɛɛnm n nu, na am ta ka asara­mbaam.

ref: as you hold firmly to the message that gives life. Then on the day when Christ returns, I can take pride in you. I can also know that my work and efforts were not useless.
text: Na am bita Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, yiri ma minɛm ŋgɔɔ a. Nzɛn am yo sɔ, nyini ma m koro bo m hoe fa kɔ am woo­wa n su a, cɛɛn n bo Kristo bá kaaki ba ni, na m si ka juma n bo m yiiri anyum­boro yoori am nu ni, aka má ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: Your faith in the Lord and your service are like a sacrifice offered to him. And my own blood may have to be poured out with the sacrifice. If this happens, I will be glad and rejoice with you.
text: Kabo am ayo Kristo yarada, na am sukun am ŋu yo i juma n ni nahɔrɛ su ni, na haari nzɛn m wuuri mmɔɔ na am yarada buka su, m ahore ti jɔɔ­wa, na i yo m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ fiɛ,

ref: In the same way, you should be glad and rejoice with me. Timothy and Epaphroditus
text: am gusu ma am ahore jɔ kabo m ahore ti jɔɔ­wa barasu ni. Timoti ni Ipaforoditosi jɔrɛ

ref: I want to be encouraged by news about you. So I hope the Lord Jesus will soon let me send Timothy to you.
text: Nzɛn ya Mibiɛ Yesu soro su, m la ahoresi ka i cɛ má, m súnma Timoti am dɔ na i ba niɛ am busu, na i ba kaaki baari, na m gusu tí kabo am wo ti na m ahore da.

ref: The others think only about what interests them and not about what concerns Christ Jesus.
text: Bo ba ka n kɛrɛ, bu cɛcɛ wo má Kristo juma n nu, se bu cɛcɛ wo bu sa koroo­wam nu a.

ref: I hope to send him to you, as soon as I find out what is going to happen to me.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn m ŋuuri m jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nyaari, m súnma i kisa bɛrɛ na i ba niɛ am busu.

ref: And I feel sure the Lord will also let me come soon.
text: Nzɛn Mibiɛ n soro su, m la ahoresi ka i cɛ́ má, mini mmɔɔ ni m tii bá niɛ am busu.

ref: I think I ought to send my dear friend Epaphroditus back to you. He is a follower and a worker and a soldier of the Lord, just as I am. You sent him to look after me,
text: Ma ŋu ka, i ti ye ni ka m súnma ya niwa Ipaforoditosi am dɔ. M ni yiri saŋga yo juma na ya ti ka sojam bo bu buka bu beŋgu luɛ n nu wɔ, ya Mibiɛ Yesu dɛɛ atin su. Na ambɛrɛ ni am tii sunmaari i m dɔ, na wɔ ba abuka m ni deke kɛrɛ bo m koro ni.

ref: but now he is eager to see you. He is worried, because you heard he was sick.
text: I wara ado am, ka i ba niɛ am busu, i ahore akaaki asaaki, dama am yo tiiri ka wɔ da tukpaki.

ref: In fact, he was very sick and almost died. But God was kind to him, and also to me, and he kept me from being burdened down with sorrow.
text: Nahɔrɛ, i tukpaki n wuraari i, kaan sɔ, má wɔ wu. Ama, na Nyɛmɛ asi i aŋunfɔ, má yiri ŋgumi, m ni yiri kɛrɛ, adena má m ahore saaki buka su.

ref: Now I am more eager than ever to send Epaphroditus back again. You will be glad to see him, and I won't have to worry any longer.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m koro ka i ba am dɔ ndende, na am ŋu i na am ahore kaaki jɔ bekun, na má m ahore saaki kun.

ref: Be sure to give him a cheerful welcome, just as people who serve the Lord deserve.
text: Am de i ni am saa nnyɔ ni ahorejɔ, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti. Na am bu minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu woo­wa ti kabo i woo­wa ti barasu ni.

ref: Finally, my dear friends, be glad that you belong to the Lord. It doesn't bother me to write the same things to you that I have written before. In fact, it is for your own good.
text: M ninbaam, jɔrɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ la ka, am ma am ahore jɔ kabo am ni Mibiɛ n ayo kun ni. I ti ye ni ka m kaaki kɛrɛ na m teŋge am su fa kɔ kabo m yo kɛrɛ seeri am ni, dama i ti deke bo i buka am wɔ, na má am wura kekereke nu.

ref: Watch out for those people who behave like dogs! They are evil and want to do more than just circumcise you.
text: Am si am ŋu sa, ni minɛm bo bu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm na bu ti ka caam ni, na bu ŋa nzɛn sɔnɔ awura má kɛnkɛnnɛ, i nya má tii­de ni.

ref: But we are the ones who are truly circumcised, because we worship by the power of God's Spirit and take pride in Christ Jesus. We don't brag about what we have done,
text: Yɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ nahɔrɛ su a, má bɛrɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni buka ya ye ya koro su Nyɛmɛ na ya la ahorejɔ a, fa kɔ kabo ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun ni. Nyini ti, ya daafi má sa­yoo­wa be ŋu fa kɔ aŋuniɛ­mbaa su.

ref: I was circumcised when I was eight days old, and I am from the nation of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a true Hebrew. As a Pharisee, I strictly obeyed the Law of Moses.
text: Bu wuuri m ni, m le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn bu wuraari m kɛnkɛnnɛ wɔ. M ti Yisareli­fɔ baa a, na m fite Bɛnjamin dɛɛ lɛɛ anɔ n nu wɔ, na m ti Hiburu­fɔm anuma a nahɔrɛ su. M yo ti Farasi­fɔm kun wɔ, na m su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su kabo i ti ye ni ka bu su i su barasu ni.

ref: And I was so eager I even made trouble for the church. I did everything the Law demands in order to please God.
text: M yo bita i ni anyum­boro wɔ, kabo m yo na m kere Kristo jama n wuwuruwa ni. Fa kɔ kabo sɔnɔ de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n na i su i su nahɔrɛ su, mini la má ŋgasi be.

ref: All I want is to know Christ and the power that raised him to life. I want to suffer and die as he did,
text: Deke bo m koro i n kɛrɛ la ka, m sí Kristo, na m nyá i yiko n bo i koro teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, na m dí i dɛɛ wahara n bo i diiri n be, na m wú i dɛɛ yiwee n be.

ref: so that somehow I also may be raised to life. Running toward the Goal
text: Nyini ma m la tama ka mini gusu téŋge m yiwee nu a. Kabo ya boro ya nyi na ya nya Kristo aceeri ni

ref: I have not yet reached my goal, and I am not perfect. But Christ has taken hold of me. So I keep on running and struggling to take hold of the prize.
text: M suse má ka ma du mɔ aju su na m ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka m ti ni, wara ma du mɔ ayo casi wɔ, ama, m toko boro m nyi wɔ, adena m nya deke n bo i ti na Yesu Kristo afa m ayo i dɛɛ sɔnɔ ni.

ref: I run toward the goal, so I can win the prize of being called to heaven. This is the prize God offers because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: Nyini ti, m miɛ m nyi wɔ na m kɔ m nyunu, adena m kɔ ŋu i awieeri, na m nya aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ afere m ama m ŋgɔɔ i dɔ ni, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: All of us who are mature should think in this same way. And if any of you think differently, God will make it clear to you.
text: I ti ye ni ka yɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo ya yarada ti bii­wa ni, ya nyunu boro fa kɔ nyini dika. Na nzɛn am sunsunni ba woori i ŋguŋgumi, fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ n su, Nyɛmɛ yí i nyi kere am, na am tí i bu.

ref: But we must keep going in the direction that we are now headed.
text: Ama, bo i wo bɛrɛ la ka, kabo ya bita Nyɛmɛ atin ba ju haari ni nyuma ni, am ma ya toko bita i sɔ.

ref: My friends, I want you to follow my example and learn from others who closely follow the example we set for you.
text: M ninbaam, am ma am woo­wa ti kabo m dɛɛ n ti barasu ni. Woo­wa kpa n bo ya du mɔ afa akere am ni, am niɛ minɛm bo bu bita nyini woo­wa kpa n ni, na am sɔn bu su.

ref: I often warned you that many people are living as enemies of the cross of Christ. And now with tears in my eyes, I warn you again
text: Dama saŋga kɛrɛ m nati se am ka, nbem woo­wa kere ka bu ti Kristo kpɔ­fɔm wɔ, fa kɔ i yiwee n bo i wuuri i baka jaanu n su ni. Ye kisa m kaaki suse am bekun ni nyunvuin n a.

ref: that they are headed for hell! They worship their stomachs and brag about the disgusting things they do. All they can think about are the things of this world.
text: Nyini minɛm sɔ, bu nyɛmɛ la bu kunnu wɔ, na deke bo má i ti bu nyinsɛ ni, bu fa nyini fa yo yanfɔya. Bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo durunya n nu fɛn neŋgem ŋgumi sɔ a, nyini ti, bu awieeri, bu ká ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: But we are citizens of heaven and are eagerly waiting for our Savior to come from there. Our Lord Jesus Christ
text: Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti Nyɛmɛ dika n bo i wo aŋgoro n nu nbaam wɔ, ye ya suŋminda ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo, ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, ni anyum­boro, ka i fíte Nyɛmɛ dika n bo i wo aŋgoro n nu ba.

ref: Dear friends, I love you and long to see you. Please keep on being faithful to the Lord. You are my pride and joy. Paul Encourages the Lord's Followers
text: M ninbaam, m koro am agaya, na m ahore abi ka m ŋu am ni, ambɛrɛ ti, ye m ahore ti jɔɔ­wa saŋga kɛrɛ a, ambɛrɛ ti m kpɛrɛ bo ma di i m juma­yoo­wa n nu a. Nyini ti, am bita Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n ni nahɔrɛ su.

ref: Euodia and Syntyche, you belong to the Lord, so I beg you to stop arguing with each other.
text: Yuwodiya, ni Sintiki, m sere am wɔ jande, ka, am fa anɔɔsɛ, dama am ti nibaam wɔ, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: And, my true partner, I ask you to help them. These women have worked together with me and with Clement and with the others in spreading the good news. Their names are now written in the book of life.
text: Tɔ, wɔrɔ gusu bo a ti m mata­fɔ nahɔrɛ su ni, m sere u wɔ, ka, a buka nyini nbara nnyɔm ni, dama bɛrɛ yiiri anyum­boro bukaari m, ye ya kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ n a, fa saŋga ni Kerementi, ni nyiŋgam bo m ni bɛrɛ saŋga yo juma ni. Nyɛmɛ akɛrɛ bu kɛrɛ duma awura ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu.

ref: Always be glad because of the Lord! I will say it again: Be glad.
text: Ya Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, am ma am ahore jɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, m kaaki suse am bekun wɔ ka, am ma am ahore jɔ.

ref: Always be gentle with others. The Lord will soon be here.
text: Am ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋu ka am ti bɛnlɛ, dama Mibiɛ ni káaki ba kisa.

ref: Don't worry about anything, but pray about everything. With thankful hearts offer up your prayers and requests to God.
text: Má am koko fa kɔ deke be su, ama, am kan am sa bo i ti am miɛɛ­wa kɛrɛ fa kere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ, na am sere i, na am da i asi am ahore su.

ref: Then, because you belong to Christ Jesus, God will bless you with peace that no one can completely understand. And this peace will control the way you think and feel.
text: Am yoori sɔ, Nyɛmɛ má am ahoreda bo i la má aŋusu, na i súniɛ am dika fa kɔ am ahore su ni am sunsunni nu, dama am ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti.

ref: You know the teachings I gave you, and you know what you heard me say and saw me do. So follow my example. And God, who gives peace, will be with you. Paul Gives Thanks for the Gifts He Was Given
text: Dekem bo am sɔnsɔn bu fiteeri m dɔ ni, ni bo am deeri m dɔ ni, bɛrɛ la m nɔaniɛ ni, ni m sa­yoo­wa ni, am su bu su na am fa yo juma. Nyɛmɛ la ahoreda, yiri wó am dɔ a. Kabo Pɔɔlu yoori bu ansɛ fa kɔ bu buka n su ni

ref: The Lord has made me very grateful that at last you have thought about me once again. Actually, you were thinking about me all along, but you didn't have any chance to show it.
text: Ya Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, m ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su, dama wɔ cɛ bo m ti má am ŋga, ama, kisa am akaaki akere ka am cɛcɛ wo m nu. M si ka saŋga kɛrɛ am cɛcɛ wo m dɔ, se kabo am yo na am buka m n ye am anya má atin a.

ref: I know what it is to be poor or to have plenty, and I have lived under all kinds of conditions. I know what it means to be full or to be hungry, to have too much or too little.
text: M si kabo yaari ti sɔnɔ na i ti barasu, na m kaaki si kabo sɔnɔ nya i agaya na i ti barasu. Deke kɛrɛ bo m wo, na sa be atu m, nzɛn m di na m kunnu yi o, wara nzɛn m da ahɔɛ o, nzɛn m la neŋge beberebe o, wara i ti kaan o, m si kabo m tara m ŋu barasu na m nyunu yi i.

ref: Christ gives me the strength to face anything.
text: Yesu Kristo maari m faŋga n dɛɛ ti ye m koro yo deke n kɛrɛ a.

ref: It was good of you to help me when I was having such a hard time.
text: Ama, am ayo sakpa, kabo miɛ tuuri m na m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ saŋga di wahara ni.

ref: My friends at Philippi, you remember what it was like when I started preaching the good news in Macedonia. After I left there, you were the only church that became my partner by giving blessings and by receiving them in return.
text: Ambɛrɛ Filipiya­fɔm si ka fite saŋga bo m boori i bu ba kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, nyini sin, na m ba fiteeri Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu ni, Kristo jama kun n kpein be mmɔɔ ace má m aceeri be, má ka ambɛrɛ ŋgumi ceeri m aceeri a.

ref: I am not trying to get something from you, but I want you to receive the blessings that come from giving.
text: I ti má ka m koro ka saŋga kɛrɛ m sude aceeri fite am dɔ ɔ, ama, m koro ka am yo sɔ, na Nyɛmɛ yo am kisi.

ref: I have been paid back everything, and with interest. I am completely satisfied with the gifts you sent with Epaphroditus. They are like a sweet-smelling offering or like the right kind of sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Deke n kɛrɛ bo am fa sunmaari m dɔ ni, m saa akan i, am ace m atara kabo má m koro ni mmɔɔ. Kisa m koro má pui kun, dama Ipaforoditosi afa am aceeri n kɛrɛ aba ama m. I ti ka yoo­wa bo ba yo i ama Nyɛmɛ na wɔ sun i nu ade i ni akunnujɔ wɔ.

ref: I pray that God will take care of all your needs with the wonderful blessings that come from Christ Jesus!
text: M Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ ni, yiri la neŋge ni ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ a, i kɛrɛ wo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ jirima nu wɔ, i má am deke kɛrɛ bo am fun i.

ref: May God our Father be praised forever and ever. Amen. Final Greetings
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni, yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami. Kabo Pɔɔlu yoori bu ansɛ awieeri su ni

ref: Give my greetings to all who are God's people because of Christ Jesus. The Lord's followers here with me send you their greetings.
text: Am bisa Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma m. Bɛrɛ ti Yesu Kristo dɛɛm a. Ya ninbaam bo bu wo m dɔ fɛn n bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

ref: All of God's people send their greetings, especially those in the service of the Emperor.
text: Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu, maala bo bu yo juma Roma­fɔm Fɛmɛ Kpiri Siza dika ni.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is also a follower.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu bo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu na m ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa ni, m ni ya niwa Timoti

ref: To God's people who live in Colossae and are faithful followers of Christ. I pray that God our Father will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo wɔ yi bu Kolosiya miɛ kpiri n nu n a, na bu ti ya ninbaam bo bu su Kristo su nahɔrɛ su ni. Ya Si Nyɛmɛ buka am na i ma am ahoreda. Kabo Pɔɔlu sereeri Nyɛmɛ na i daari i asi ni

ref: Each time we pray for you, we thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ ya sere Nyɛmɛ ma am wɔ, yiri ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Si a, na ya da i asi fa kɔ kabo am woo­wa ti barasu n wɔ,

ref: We have heard of your faith in Christ and of your love for all God's people,
text: dama ya ti kabo am ayo Yesu Kristo yarada, ni kabo am koro Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ barasu ni.

ref: from our good friend Epaphras. He works together with us for Christ and is a faithful worker for you.
text: Ya lamɔkɔ akɔɔ Ipafarasi bo ya koro i ni, yiri du mɔ kereeri am ahin labari kpa n a. Yiri ti Kristo amusui­su­fɔ bo i la dabuŋu na i yo i juma buka am a,

ref: He is also the one who told us about the love that God's Spirit has given you. The Person and Work of Christ
text: yiri seeri ya kabo Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n abuka am na am koro minɛm barasu n a.

ref: We have not stopped praying for you since the first day we heard about you. In fact, we always pray that God will show you everything he wants you to do and that you may have all the wisdom and understanding his Spirit gives.
text: Nyini ti, fite cɛɛn bo ya tiiri kabo Nyɛmɛ ayo sakpa n ama am ni, saŋga kɛrɛ ya sere Nyɛmɛ ma am wɔ, ka i ma am si nahɔrɛ su kabo i koro ka am yo barasu ni, na i kaaki ma Awiɛwiɛ Casi ma am nya si ni alecira barasu kɛrɛ.

ref: Then you will live a life that honors the Lord, and you will always please him by doing good deeds. You will come to know God even better.
text: Nyini ma am koro wo kabo i ti ye ni ka Ya Mibiɛ minɛm wo barasu n a, na am yo kabo i koro n kɛrɛ, na am yo sakpa barasu barasu kɛrɛ, na am si Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su buka su.

ref: I pray that you will be grateful to God for letting you have part in what he has promised his people in the kingdom of light.
text: Na am da ya Si Nyɛmɛ asi, dama yiri ama am ayo minɛm bo am aju su ka am saŋga nya deke n bo i fá ma i minɛm bo bu wo wein n nu ni.

ref: God rescued us from the dark power of Satan and brought us into the kingdom of his dear Son,
text: Yiri ye wɔ yi ya afite awosin dɛɛ fɛmɛya yiko n kɛrɛ nu, ye wɔ fa ya awura i Wa koroo­wa n dɛɛ fɛmɛya n nu a.

ref: Christ is exactly like God, who cannot be seen. He is the first-born Son, superior to all creation.
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ bo ya ŋu má i n woo­wa ti ni, yiri Kristo woo­wa ti sɔ ɔ, yiri ti Nyɛmɛ Wa kpiɛn n bo i ti deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu n tii a.

ref: Everything was created by him, everything in heaven and on earth, everything seen and unseen, including all forces and powers, and all rulers and authorities. All things were created by God's Son, and everything was made for him.
text: Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ maari i yiiri deke kɛrɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu n a, ni dekem bo ya ŋu bu, ni bo ya ŋu má bu ni, ni yiko­fɔm kɛrɛ, ni nfaŋga­fɔm, ni nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ. Nyɛmɛ maari yiri yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, adena deke n kɛrɛ wura i kpiri.

ref: God's Son was before all else, and by him everything is held together.
text: Yiri du mɔ woori bɛrɛ wɔ ka, ye Nyɛmɛ nya ba yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a. Yiri ma deke n kɛrɛ toko wo bɛrɛ a.

ref: He is the head of his body, which is the church. He is the very beginning, the first to be raised from death, so that he would be above all others.
text: Yiri ti i jama n kɛrɛ tii a, ye i jama n ti i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, yiri ti i jama n bumboori a, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ adu mɔ ateŋge i yiwee nu a, adena i yo deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu n mɔ­fɔ.

ref: God himself was pleased to live fully in his Son.
text: Dama wɔ sun Nyɛmɛ nu ka, i dɛɛ woo­wa kɛrɛ wo i Wa n nu.

ref: And God was pleased for him to make peace by sacrificing his blood on the cross, so that all beings in heaven and on earth would be brought back to God.
text: Na Baa n dɛɛ ti ma Nyɛmɛ akoro akaaki deke n kɛrɛ bo bu wo aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu ama bu ni Nyɛmɛ ayo kun, i mbunja n bo i fiteeri i ŋuniɛ nu baka jaanu n su ni, ayo bu ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu a.

ref: You used to be far from God. Your thoughts made you his enemies, and you did evil things.
text: Ambɛrɛ, saŋga be, am yo woori dede ni Nyɛmɛ wɔ, am yo ti i kpɔ­fɔm wɔ, dama am yo yo satiɛm wɔ, na am sunsunni ti má kpa,

ref: But his Son became a human and died. So God made peace with you, and now he lets you stand in his presence as people who are holy and faultless and innocent.
text: ama, kisa i Wa n woori ka adamande wɔ, na i wuuri baka jaanu n su ni, nyini ma am ni yiri ayo kun a, adena i yó am casi, na má be koro jaraki am, na am lá má ŋgasi be Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: But you must stay deeply rooted and firm in your faith. You must not give up the hope you received when you heard the good news. It was preached to everyone on earth, and I myself have become a servant of this message. Paul's Service to the Church
text: Ama, i ti ye ni ka am subita yarada n bo am la i kenken saŋga kɛrɛ, na má am yaki i, má am ma deke be kanda am fa kɔ kabo am tiiri labari kpa n bo i maari am yoori i tama n jɔrɛ ni. Nyini labari n ye bu kaanri i jɔrɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu n a, ye mini Pɔɔlu mmɔɔ ti i amusui­su­fɔ, ye m kan i jɔrɛ n a. Kabo Pɔɔlu yo juma ma Kristo jama n barasu ni

ref: I am glad I can suffer for you. I am pleased also that in my own body I can continue the suffering of Christ for his body, the church.
text: Ama, kisa wɔ yo m fiɛ kabo m sudi wahara ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ni, na wahara n bo m di i m ŋuniɛ n nu ni, m sudi i sɔ haari m kɔ wie i, kabo Kristo gusu diiri wahara ni, m dí i ma i jamam bo bu ti i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ.

ref: God's plan was to make me a servant of his church and to send me to preach his complete message to you.
text: Nyɛmɛ ayi m ka m yo Kristo jama n amusui­su­fɔ a, ye wɔ ma m ahin juma n ka m yo buka am a. Nyini la bo i ŋa m kan i nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ pepere kere am ni.

ref: For ages and ages this message was kept secret from everyone, but now it has been explained to God's people.
text: I ti jɔrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ yo fa fiɛɛri dawa­fɔm kɛrɛ su wɔ, ama, kisa wɔ fa i akere i minɛm bo wɔ yi bu ni.

ref: God did this because he wanted you Gentiles to understand his wonderful and glorious mystery. And the mystery is that Christ lives in you, and he is your hope of sharing in God's glory.
text: Bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ koro ka i kere bu jɔrɛ bo i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu n a, yiri la alekutura sa bo i la má aŋusu na i koro ka i yo ma ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni. Yiri la ka, Kristo wo am nu, na am la ahoresi ka am nyá i dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be.

ref: We announce the message about Christ, and we use all our wisdom to warn and teach everyone, so all of Christ's followers will grow and become mature.
text: Ya kan Kristo jɔrɛ kere sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ, na ya kere bu na ya bɔbɔ bu, ni alecira bo wɔ fa ama ya ni, na bu ba i dɔ ba su i ka minɛm bo bu nyunu ati Kristo dɛɛ atin su.

ref: I want you to know what a struggle I am going through for you, for God's people at Laodicea, and for all of those followers who have never met me.
text: M koro ka am si kabo m tu ŋgoni yo juma n ni anyum­boro ma ambɛrɛ, ni Lawodikaya­fɔm, ni minɛm bo bu nya si má m ni.

ref: I do it to encourage them. Then as their hearts are joined together in love, they will be wonderfully blessed with complete understanding. And they will truly know Christ. Not only is he the key to God's mystery,
text: M koro ka m yo sɔ na i wura am kpɛrɛ kekereke, na am la anɔɔsɛ, na am koro am beŋgu ni nahɔrɛ su, na am si Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su, na am de i nɔaniɛ ni, na i ma am ahorejɔ, na am si jɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ yo fa fiɛɛri ni. Yiri la Yesu Kristo.

ref: I tell you these things to keep you from being fooled by fancy talk.
text: M koro má ka be fa anɔɔfiɛfiɛ daka am, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m se am sɔ n a.

ref: Even though I am not with you, I keep thinking about you. I am glad to know that you are living as you should and your faith in Christ is strong. Christ Brings Real Life
text: Haari ni bo m wo má am dɔ ni, m sunsunni yiri wo am dɔ, kabo am ayo Kristo yarada nahɔrɛ su na am la anɔɔsɛ bita i atin kenken ni, nyini ayo m fiɛfiɛ agaya. Am ma am woo­wa ti kabo Kristo dɛɛ ti ni

ref: You have accepted Christ Jesus as your Lord. Now keep on following him.
text: Kabo am asoro su ade Yesu Kristo ka am Mibiɛ ni, am ma am woo­wa ti ka minɛm bo bu ni yiri ayo kun ni.

ref: Plant your roots in Christ and let him be the foundation for your life. Be strong in your faith, just as you were taught. And be grateful.
text: Am bita i kekereke, na am subuka su fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin su, na am ma am yarada yo bii­wa nahɔrɛ su kabo ba kere am ni. Na am suda i asi agaya.

ref: Don't let anyone fool you by using senseless arguments. These arguments may sound wise, but they are only human teachings. They come from the powers of this world and not from Christ.
text: Am si am ŋu sa, na má be fa adamande ŋgɔnlɛ, ni sa ŋgbɛɛn be dadaka am na am su bu su. Bɛrɛ la adamande keree­wam, ni durunya n nu satiɛ yoo­wam bo bu fa kere minɛm na i ti má Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa ni.

ref: And you are fully grown because you belong to Christ, who is over every power and authority.
text: Kabo am ni yiri ayo kun ni, nyini ma am anya deke kɛrɛ pepere a. Yiri ti yiko­fɔm, ni nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ tii a.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was the same as being buried with Christ. Then you were raised to life because you had faith in the power of God, who raised Christ from death.
text: Saŋga bo bu biɛɛri am Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, i kere ka ba saŋga ase am ni Kristo wɔ lɛɛ, na am ni yiri asaŋga ateŋge i yiwee n nu lɛɛ, dama am la yarada ka Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n nu ye wɔ teŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu a.

ref: You were dead, because you were sinful and were not God's people. But God let Christ make you alive, when he forgave all our sins.
text: Saŋga be, am yo ti fuinm wɔ, dama am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, na am ayo awura má kɛnkɛnnɛ na am su má Nyɛmɛ. Ama, kisa Nyɛmɛ ama am ni Kristo asaŋga anya ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ. Wɔ fa ya satiɛ kɛrɛ ace ya.

ref: God wiped out the charges that were against us for disobeying the Law of Moses. He took them away and nailed them to the cross.
text: Satiɛ kɛrɛ bo ya yo yoori na ba kɛrɛ i ka i ti ŋgasi jɔrɛ ni, Yesu yiwee n bo i wuuri baka jaanu n su n dɛɛ ti, wɔ ŋmisi ayi nyini ŋgasi sa n kɛrɛ,

ref: There Christ defeated all powers and forces. He let the whole world see them being led away as prisoners when he celebrated his victory.
text: na wɔ kunma yiko­fɔm ni nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ, awura bu nyinsɛ fofoe nu.

ref: Don't let anyone tell you what you must eat or drink. Don't let them say you must celebrate the New Moon festival, the Sabbath, or any other festival.
text: Nyini ti, má am ma be jaraki am fa kɔ sa­yoo­wa be su, kabo am yo fa kɔ diire dii­wam ni bu ciirem, wara deke nuu­wam ni bu ciirem, wara cɛɛn­kpiri ciirem, wara sara fɔfɔrɛ ciire yoo­wam, wara Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ciirem.

ref: These things are only a shadow of what was to come. But Christ is real!
text: Nyini yoo­wam kɛrɛ ferema ni deke n bo i suba n wɔ, yiri la Kristo.

ref: Don't be cheated by people who make a show of acting humble and who worship angels. They brag about seeing visions. But it is all nonsense, because their minds are filled with selfish desires.
text: Am si am ŋu sa ni minɛm bo bu ŋa ba ŋu deke yikeree­wa nbem, na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti tarakam, na bu su mɛrɛkɛm ni. Adamande dɛɛ sunsunni nu kpirikoro la nyini n kɛrɛ bo bu yo sɔ ni. Má am ma bu ŋu am ŋgasi be.

ref: They are no longer part of Christ, who is the head of the whole body. Christ gives the body its strength, and he uses its joints and muscles to hold it together, as it grows by the power of God. Christ Brings New Life
text: Nyini minɛm sɔ da má Kristo bo i ti i jama n tii n ŋu. I jama n ti i ŋuniɛ­mbaa a. Yiri niɛ i ŋuniɛ­mbaa barasu barasu n kɛrɛ suyo i kun a, na i ma i wura i beŋgu nu, na i sasa suyo faŋga, na i buka nyɛmɛsu atin su, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni.

ref: You died with Christ. Now the forces of the universe don't have any power over you. Why do you live as if you had to obey such rules as,
text: Kabo am ni Kristo asaŋga awu ni, am anya am tii afite durunya n nu satiɛ yoo­wam kɛrɛ nu. Nzu ti ye am su bu nyini ciirem su a?

ref: After these things are used, they are no longer good for anything. So why be bothered with the rules that humans have made up?
text: Nyini ciirem kɛrɛ ti adamande dɛɛ keree­wam wɔ, nzɛn sɔnɔ suuri bu su, bu kɛrɛ kaari ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: Obeying these rules may seem to be the smart thing to do. They appear to make you love God more and to be very humble and to have control over your body. But they don't really have any power over our desires.
text: Nyini ciirem ni, minɛ sunsun ka bɛrɛ n bo bu su nyini n kɛrɛ su n ti alecira­fɔm wɔ, ama, bu ti má alecira­fɔm, bu su dekem bo bu ahore­mbaa koro ni su a, na bu yo bu ŋu taraka, na bu sukere bu ŋu wahara, ama, nyini n kɛrɛ bo bu yo i ni, bu koro tara má bu ŋu fa kɔ adamande konviki dika.

ref: You have been raised to life with Christ. Now set your heart on what is in heaven, where Christ rules at God's right side.
text: Nyini ti, kabo am ni Kristo asaŋga ateŋge i yiwee n nu ni, am ma am ahore­mbaa kɛrɛ wo dekem bo bu wo aŋgoro n su, dika n bo Kristo tana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su ni.

ref: Think about what is up there, not about what is here on earth.
text: Am ma am sunsunni kɛrɛ wo dekem bo bu wo aŋgoro n su, ama, má dekem bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn ni.

ref: You died, which means that your life is hidden with Christ, who sits beside God.
text: Dama am ni Kristo asaŋga awu wɔ, ye am ni yiri ayo kun a, ye Nyɛmɛ afa am ni yiri ŋgɔɔ afiɛ yiri ni i tii dɔ a.

ref: Christ gives meaning to your life, and when he appears, you will also appear with him in glory.
text: Kristo ti am ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su a, i ba baari, am ni yiri kɛrɛ sáŋga fite a, na am nyá i jirima woo­wa ni.

ref: Don't be controlled by your body. Kill every desire for the wrong kind of sex. Don't be immoral or indecent or have evil thoughts. Don't be greedy, which is the same as worshiping idols.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am yaki am ŋuniɛ­mbaa sa koroo­wa kɛrɛ, má am yo sakaraya, am yaki daŋgba sa­yoo­wam, am yaki konviki ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, má am ma am nyunu boro satiɛ­yo su. Má am ma am nyunu boro neŋge kpini su, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sɔ, i mi su má Nyɛmɛ, i su neŋgem wɔ.

ref: And this is exactly what you did, when you lived among people who behaved in this way.
text: Saŋga be, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii yo yo nyini sam wɔ, dama kabo am woo­wa yo ti lɛɛ.

ref: But now you must stop doing such things. You must quit being angry, hateful, and evil. You must no longer say insulting or cruel things about others.
text: Ama, kisa, am yaki nyini kɛrɛ fieo, yaa­fa ni aŋunkpini, na má am ma nzukɔ kpiɛ ni jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn fite am nɔɔ nu,

ref: And stop lying to each other. You have given up your old way of life with its habits.
text: má am bu kɔrɛ sɔ am beŋgu, dama am ayaki am dawa dɛɛ yoo­wa n kɛrɛ fieo fieo,

ref: Each of you is now a new person. You are becoming more and more like your Creator, and you will understand him better.
text: am afa wo fɔfɔrɛ. Nyɛmɛ suma am nyini woo­wa fɔfɔrɛ n a, adena i má am woo­wa ti kabo i dɛɛ woo­wa ti barasu ni, na am koro si i nahɔrɛ su.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are a Greek or a Jew, or if you are circumcised or not. You may even be a barbarian or a Scythian, and you may be a slave or a free person. Yet Christ is all that matters, and he lives in all of us.
text: Nyini woo­wa fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i n dɛɛ ti, Yuda­fɔm ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ti kun wɔ, minɛm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ o, ni bo ba wura má kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ, ni minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu nbem, ni minɛm bo bu nyunu nya teke má, ni akɔɔm ni awuru­nbaam, bu kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ. Ama, bo i kɔ tara bu kɛrɛ n la Kristo, yiri wo bu kɛrɛ ahore su a.

ref: Put up with each other, and forgive anyone who does you wrong, just as Christ has forgiven you.
text: na am yo sawɔrɛ de am beŋgum. Nzɛn be ni i beŋgu anya jɔrɛ, am fa ce am beŋgu. Kabo Kristo afa am ŋgasi ace am ni, ambɛrɛ gusu fa am beŋgum ŋgasi ce bu.

ref: Love is more important than anything else. It is what ties everything completely together.
text: Bo i tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, am koro am beŋgu, dama koro ma am yo kun a, na am yo anɔɔsɛ nahɔrɛ su.

ref: Each one of you is part of the body of Christ, and you were chosen to live together in peace. So let the peace that comes from Christ control your thoughts. And be grateful.
text: Kristo afere am ayo am deke kun ama am ahoreda, nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma nyini ahoreda n wo am dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na am suda Nyɛmɛ asi.

ref: Whatever you say or do should be done in the name of the Lord Jesus, as you give thanks to God the Father because of him. Some Rules for Christian Living
