text: Am sa­yoo­wa, ni am jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ nu, am yo i ya Mibiɛ Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu, na am da ya Si Nyɛmɛ asi Kristo dɛɛ ti. Kabo i ti ye ni ka Kristo susu­fɔm bita bu awuru barasu ni

ref: A wife must put her husband first. This is her duty as a follower of the Lord.
text: Nbaram, am de am hunm nɔaniɛ kabo i ti ye ni ka Mibiɛ n susu­fɔm yo ni.

ref: A husband must love his wife and not abuse her.
text: Nbiɛsɔm, am koro am yim, má am bita bu ni joko tiɛ.

ref: Parents, don't be hard on your children. If you are, they might give up.
text: Nbaa­sim, má am yo sa bo i yɛ am nbaam yaa su, na bu ahore saaki.

ref: Slaves, you must always obey your earthly masters. Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. Honor the Lord and serve your masters with your whole heart.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti akɔɔm ni, am de am mibiɛm bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn n nɔaniɛ deke kɛrɛ nu, má am yo ka bu niɛ am dɛɛ ti, na am koro ka am yo na bu ahore jɔ am ŋu, ama, am yo deke kɛrɛ bo bu ŋa am yo i n ni am ahore­mbaa kun, ka am su Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Do your work willingly, as though you were serving the Lord himself, and not just your earthly master.
text: Am yo am juma kɛrɛ ni am ahore­mbaa kun, ka am suyo ma Mibiɛ n wɔ, ama, má ka am suyo ma adamande wɔ.

ref: In fact, the Lord Christ is the one you are really serving, and you know he will reward you.
text: Am si ka am nyá ŋkatɔ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i minɛm ni. Dama am Mibiɛ n bo am su i ni, yiri la Kristo wɔ.

ref: But Christ has no favorites! He will punish evil people, just as they deserve.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori sa bo i ti má kpa, Nyɛmɛ tɔ́ i mi kaari, dama Nyɛmɛ tantambu má nu.

ref: Never give up praying. And when you pray, keep alert and be thankful.
text: Am susere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni anyum­boro, má am fa am ŋu ji, am suda i asi.

ref: Be sure to pray that God will make a way for us to spread his message and explain the mystery about Christ, even though I am in jail for doing this.
text: Nzɛn am sere Nyɛmɛ, am sere ma ya gusu, na Nyɛmɛ teke atin ma ya na ya kan labari kpa n fa kɔ Kristo jɔrɛ n bo i yo wo fiɛɛ­wa nu n kere minɛm. Dama labari kpa n dɛɛ jɔrɛ ti, ye m wo dan­saraka nu n a.

ref: Be pleasant and hold their interest when you speak the message. Choose your words carefully and be ready to give answers to anyone who asks questions. Final Greetings
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ŋa am jɔjɔ minɛm dɔ, am ma am nɔaniɛ fite kpa na i yo bu fiɛfiɛ, na am si kabo am bɔ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nu barasu. Kabo Pɔɔlu yoori bu ansɛ awieeri su ni

ref: Tychicus is the dear friend, who faithfully works and serves the Lord with us, and he will give you the news about me.
text: Ya niwa Tikikusi bá kan m ŋusu jɔrɛ kɛrɛ kere am. Yiri ye ya koro i agaya, i bita nahɔrɛ fa kɔ i juma su, i ti ya lamɔkɔ akɔɔ ɔ, fa kɔ Mibiɛ n juma n su.

ref: I am sending him to cheer you up by telling you how we are getting along.
text: Nyini ti, ye m susunma i am dɔ n a, ka i ba kan kere am kabo ya kɛrɛ ti barasu, na am ahore si.

ref: Onesimus, the dear and faithful follower from your own group, is coming with him. The two of them will tell you everything that has happened here.
text: I ni Onisimɔ suba a. Yiri mmɔɔ ti ya niwa bo ya koro i wɔ, i bita nahɔrɛ fa kɔ i juma su, i ti am be wɔ. Bu bá kan deke kɛrɛ bo i suyo fɛn kere am.

ref: Aristarchus is in jail with me. He sends greetings to you, and so does Mark, the cousin of Barnabas. You have already been told to welcome Mark, if he visits you.
text: Arisitakusi bo m ni yiri kɛrɛ wo dan­saraka nu fɛn ni, bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu. Banabasi niɛma Marako gusu bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu. Ma du mɔ ase am kabo am yo barasu, ka i baari, am de i ni am saa nnyɔ.

ref: Jesus, who is known as Justus, sends his greetings. These three men are the only Jewish followers who have worked with me for the kingdom of God. They have given me much comfort.
text: Yesu n bo bu kaaki fere i ka Jusitosi n gusu bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu. Bɛrɛ minɛ nsan n ŋgumi ti m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm bo ba yo yarada, na m ni bɛrɛ saŋga yo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu juma n a. Bu ti minɛm bo bu ma m ahoresi.

ref: Your own Epaphras, who serves Christ Jesus, sends his greetings. He always prays hard that you may fully know what the Lord wants you to do and that you may do it completely.
text: Ipafarasi gusu bo i ti am be na i ti Kristo akɔɔ n bisa am dɔŋgu. Saŋga kɛrɛ i sere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro ma am wɔ, adena am la jinakun­su na am su Nyɛmɛ ni nahɔrɛ, na am yarada yo bii­wa, na am de i nɔaniɛ ni kabo Nyɛmɛ koro n kɛrɛ.

ref: I have seen how much trouble he has gone through for you and for the followers in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
text: Mini mmɔɔ ni m tii koro di i daani, kabo i yi anyum­boro yo juma ma ambɛrɛ, ni Lawodikaya­fɔm, ni Hɛrapolisi­fɔm barasu.

ref: Our dear doctor Luke sends you his greetings, and so does Demas.
text: Luka bo ya koro i na i ti dɔkɔtiɛ ni, i ni Demasi kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu.

ref: Give my greetings to the followers at Laodicea, especially to Nympha and the church that meets in her home.
text: Am bisa ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo Lawodikaya ni, ni Nimfa, ni Kristo jama n kɛrɛ bo bu nati tiɛn bu nɔɔ i dika n dɔŋgu.

ref: After this letter has been read to your people, be sure to have it read in the church at Laodicea. And you should read the letter that I have sent to them.
text: Nzɛn am kaanri ahin kadasi n wieeri, am yo aniɛ na am fa ma Lawodikaya­fɔm na bu gusu kan i. Na am boro am nyi na am gusu kan m kadasi n bo bɛrɛ Lawodikaya­fɔm fa ma am ni.

ref: Remind Archippus to do the work that the Lord has given him to do.
text: Am se Akipusi ka i boro i nyi, na i yo juma n bo Mibiɛ n fa maari i ni, na i wie i.

ref: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL. Don't forget that I am in jail. I pray that God will be kind to you.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii sufa m saa sukɛrɛ ahin kɛrɛɛ­wa n bisa am dɔŋgu a. Má am ma am wara fi ka m wo dan­saraka nu wɔ. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

ref: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God the Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ. I pray that God will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Mini Pɔɔlu, ni Silivanusi, ni Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n bo am wo Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu na am ti ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ minɛm ni. Nyɛmɛ buka am, na i ma am ahoreda. Kabo Tɛsalonika­fɔm woo­wa ni bu yarada tiiri barasu ni

ref: We thank God for you and always mention you in our prayers. Each time we pray,
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ ya da Nyɛmɛ asi ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti wɔ, na ya susere Nyɛmɛ ma am,

ref: we tell God our Father about your faith and loving work and about your firm hope in our Lord Jesus Christ. The Thessalonians' Faith and Example
text: na ya suteŋge am su ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, kabo am fa am yarada fa yo juma barasu ni, ni kabo am la koro, na am fa yo juma barasu ni, ni kabo am bita am tama n kenken fa kɔ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɔ ni.

ref: My dear friends, God loves you, and we know he has chosen you to be his people.
text: Ya ninbaam, Nyɛmɛ koro am, na ya si ka wɔ yi am ka am yo i tiɛŋgu dɛɛ minɛm, ya si ka i ti sɔ ɔ,

ref: When we told you the good news, it was with the power and assurance that come from the Holy Spirit, and not simply with words. You knew what kind of people we were and how we helped you.
text: dama saŋga bo ya kaanri labari kpa n kereeri am ni, wɔ ti má nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ŋgumi wɔ, ama, i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko n nu wɔ, ye am siiri tenle su ka i ti nahɔrɛ a. Saŋga bo ya woori am dɔ ni, am ŋuuri kabo ya wo tiiri barasu, i fiteeri ka ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye ya woori sɔ a, adena i buka am.

ref: So, when you accepted the message, you followed our example and the example of the Lord. You suffered, but the Holy Spirit made you glad.
text: Na am asɔn ya ni Mibiɛ n dɛɛ wo n su, haari ni bo am diiri wahara agaya n kɛrɛ ni, am deeri labari kpa n wɔ, ni ahorejɔ n bo i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ ni.

ref: And because of you, the Lord's message has spread everywhere in those regions. Now the news of your faith in God is known all over the world, and we don't have to say a thing about it.
text: Má ka Makɛdoniya ni Akaya­fɔm tiiri Mibiɛ n labari n fiteeri am dɔ n ŋgumi, ama, yarada n bo am yoori Nyɛmɛ ni, i jɔrɛ abara ayɛ deke kɛrɛ. Nyini ti, ya la má jɔrɛ be bo ya kan kun.

ref: Everyone is talking about how you welcomed us and how you turned away from idols to serve the true and living God.
text: Nyini minɛm n kɛrɛ kan kabo am deeri ya barasu, saŋga bo ya baari am dɔ ni, ni kabo am kaakiiri am sin boori amɔɛm na am su Nyɛmɛ bo i ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni,

ref: They also tell how you are waiting for his Son Jesus to come from heaven. God raised him from death, and on the day of judgment Jesus will save us from God's anger.
text: na am ŋminda i Wa Yesu, yiri fíte Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro ba a, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i yiwee n nu n a, yiri yí ya fite jina cɛɛn dɛɛ kekereke n nu a.

ref: As you remember, we had been mistreated and insulted at Philippi. But God gave us the courage to tell you the good news about him, even though many people caused us trouble.
text: Am si kabo bu boori ya, na bu wuraari ya nyinsɛ Filipiya miɛ kpiri n nu ka, ye ya nya baari am dɔ Tɛsalonika a. Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ama ya ahoresi na ya koro akan i labari kpa n akere am, haari ni bo minɛ dɔŋgu asoro má ya su mmɔɔ ni.

ref: We didn't have any hidden motives when we won you over, and we didn't try to fool or trick anyone.
text: Nɔaniɛ n bo ya kan kereeri am ni, i ti má ka ya mini atin wɔ, wara i ti má ka ya la sunsunni tiɛ be wɔ, i sa su ti má ka ya koro ka ya dadaka be wɔ.

ref: You also know we didn't try to flatter anyone. God himself knows what we did wasn't a cover-up for greed.
text: Am si tenle su ka ya ba abo má am anɔɔfiɛ wɔ, na ya ba akan má labari kpa n ka ya nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su wɔ. Nyɛmɛ wo ya daani,

ref: We were not trying to get you or anyone else to praise us.
text: ya yo akoro má kansi am dɔ, wara be dɔ.

ref: But as apostles, we could have demanded help from you. After all, Christ is the one who sent us. We chose to be like children or like a mother nursing her baby.
text: Má kabo ya ti Kristo jaramasam ni, má ya koro se am ka am yo deke kɛrɛ ma ya. Ama, saŋga bo ya woori am dɔ ni, ya woori bɛnlɛ wɔ, kabo bara baa­wu­fɔ niɛ i nbaam dika n wɔ.

ref: We cared so much for you, and you became so dear to us, that we were willing to give our lives for you when we gave you God's message.
text: Koro bo ya fa koro am n dɛɛ ti, ye ya kaanri Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n maari am a, na ya gusu akun ya ŋu ama am, dama ya koroori am agaya n dɛɛ ti.

ref: My dear friends, you surely haven't forgotten our hard work and hardships. You remember how night and day we struggled to make a living, so we could tell you God's message without being a burden to anyone.
text: Nahɔrɛ, ya ninbaam, am teŋge su kabo ya boroori ya nyi yoori juma nahɔrɛ su, ya yoori juma wiɛsu, ni kɔŋguɛ, adena má ya fa ya fie fie be, saŋga bo ya kaanri Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n maari am ni.

ref: You also know we did everything for you that parents would do for their own children.
text: Am si ka saŋga bo ya woori ni ambɛrɛ ni, ya bitaari am kabo sɔnɔ bita i nbaam n wɔ.

ref: We begged, encouraged, and urged each of you to live in a way that would honor God. He is the one who chose you to share in his own kingdom and glory.
text: Ya maari am ahoresi, na ya dada am ahore, na ya yɛ am nyi ka am wo kabo i ti ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm wo barasu n a. Yiri fereeri am a, ka, am ba yo i dɛɛ minɛm bo i níɛ am dika, na am bá nya i dɛɛ jirima ni.

ref: We always thank God that you believed the message we preached. It came from him, and it isn't something made up by humans. You accepted it as God's message, and now he is working in you.
text: Na ya gusu da Nyɛmɛ asi saŋga kɛrɛ, dama saŋga bo am deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo ya kan kereeri am ni, am ade má i ka i fite adamande dɔ ɔ, ama, am ade i ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ, na i ti sɔ ɔ nahɔrɛ su, ye i wo ambɛrɛ yarada­yo­fɔm ahore su yo juma a.

ref: My friends, you did just like God's churches in Judea and like the other followers of Christ Jesus there. And so, you were mistreated by your own people, in the same way they were mistreated by their people.
text: Ya ninbaam, sam bo bu yo tuuri Kristo jamam bo bu wo Yudiya asiɛn n su ni, nyini sam ye ba tu ambɛrɛ gusu n a. Kabo am tiɛŋgu minɛm akere am wahara barasu ni, sɔ ye Yuda­fɔm yo fa kereeri Kristo jamam n a.

ref: Those evil people killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and they even chased us away. God doesn't like what they do and neither does anyone else.
text: Bɛrɛ kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ye bu kaaki kuunri ya Mibiɛ Yesu, na ba kere ya wuwuruwa, na ba yo sa bo i sun má Nyɛmɛ nu, na ba yo adamande kɛrɛ kpɔ­fɔm.

ref: They keep us from speaking his message to the Gentiles and from leading them to be saved. They have always gone too far with their sins. Now God has finally become angry and will punish them. Paul Wants To Visit the Church Again
text: Na ba koro ka bu kanda ya ka má ya kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, adena má bu nya tii­de. Na nyini satiɛ n aba abuka bu satiɛ n kɛrɛ su akɔ asin su. Ama, kisa, Nyɛmɛ afa yaa bu ŋu, i sudi bu jɔrɛ. Kabo má Pɔɔlu koroori ka i kɔ niɛ Tɛsalonika­fɔm busu ni

ref: My friends, we were kept from coming to you for a while, but we never stopped thinking about you. We were eager to see you and tried our best to visit you in person.
text: Ya ninbaam, saŋga bo ya kpaaki yakiiri am kaan sɔ ni, ya sunsunni yiri woori am dɔ ɔ, na ya wara ado am agaya, na ya boro ya nyi ka ya ba niɛ am busu.

ref: After all, when the Lord Jesus appears, who else but you will give us hope and joy and be like a glorious crown for us?
text: Ya koro ka ya kpie ya nyi ba am dɔ, dama saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá kpie i nyi ba ni, ya lá ahoresi ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, na ya lá ahorejɔ, na ya káaki koro fɔ ka ya nya teremasu i dɔ.

ref: You alone are our glory and joy!
text: Nahɔrɛ, ambɛrɛ ti, ye ya la ahorejɔ koro bo ya hoe n a.

ref: Finally, we couldn't stand it any longer. We decided to stay in Athens by ourselves
text: Kabo ya ni ambɛrɛ akpaaki acɛ n dɛɛ ti, ya wara ado am agaya, nyini ti, ya ŋa yɛrɛ ka Atensi miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: and send our friend Timothy to you. He works with us as God's servant and preaches the good news about Christ. We wanted him to make you strong in your faith and to encourage you.
text: Na ya sunma ya niwa Timoti ka i ba niɛ am busu. Ya ni yiri saŋga yo juma fa kɔ Kristo labari kpa n kan su, ye ya sunmaari i ka i ma am nya ahoresi, na i ma am jina kenken fa kɔ am yarada n su.

ref: We didn't want any of you to be discouraged by all these troubles. You knew we would have to suffer,
text: Adena má am be ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki i fa kɔ ahin wuwuruwam bo bu sukere am n ti. Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka, nyini sam bo bu tú yɛrɛ yarada­yo­fɔm ni, Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa lɛɛ.

ref: because when we were with you, we told you this would happen. And we did suffer, as you well know.
text: Dama saŋga bo ya toko wo am dɔ ni, ya yo seeri am ka bu bá kere ya wuwuruwa. Kabo am si ni, nyini lɛɛ ye wɔ tu ya n a.

ref: At last, when I could not wait any longer, I sent Timothy to find out about your faith. I hoped Satan had not tempted you and made all our work useless.
text: Nyini ti, ma koro amindi má, ye m sunmaari Timoti na i ba niɛ ka am toko bita am yarada n kenken n a. Beni, kisa Sitana akoro anya atin adadaka am, wɔ ma ya juma n bo ya yo i am dɔ n kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: Timothy has now come back from his visit with you and has told us about your faith and love. He also said that you always have happy memories of us and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
text: Ye kisa, Timoti akpie i nyi afa labari aba ama ya fa kɔ kabo am yarada, ni am koro n ti barasu. Wɔ se ya ka am la sunsunni kpa fa ma ya saŋga kɛrɛ, na am koro ka am ŋu ya kabo ya gusu koro ka ya ŋu am ni.

ref: My friends, even though we have a lot of trouble and suffering, your faith makes us feel better about you.
text: Nyini ti, ya ninbaam, haari ni bo ya wo kekereke nu na ya sudi wahara n kɛrɛ ni, am yarada n ama ya ahoresi.

ref: Your strong faith in the Lord is like a breath of new life.
text: Na nzɛn am jinaari kun su, fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su, ya koro da na ya daafi.

ref: How can we possibly thank God enough for all the happiness you have brought us?
text: Kisa ya si má kabo ya da Nyɛmɛ asi fa ma am mmɔɔ, fa kɔ ahorejɔ bambaka n bo wɔ fa ama ya, am dɛɛ ti.

ref: Day and night we sincerely pray that we will see you again and help you to have an even stronger faith.
text: Kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ ya sereeri Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su wɔ, ka, i ma ya ba ŋu ya ŋu, na ya kere am dekem bo i kpiɛ am su fa kɔ am yarada n su.

ref: We pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus will let us visit you.
text: Ya sere ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu ka bu teke atin ma ya, na ya koro ba am dɔ.

ref: May the Lord make your love for each other and for everyone else grow by leaps and bounds. This is how our love for you has grown.
text: Na ya sere Mibiɛ ni, ka, i ma am la koro buka su, na am fa koro am beŋgu ni minɛm kɛrɛ kɔ sin su, kabo ya gusu koro am ni.

ref: And when our Lord comes with all his people, I pray he will make your hearts pure and innocent in the sight of God the Father.
text: Na i ma am nya ahoresi, na am jina kenken, na am ba yo minɛ casim bo be koro jaraki má am, ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá baari ni i tiɛŋgu minɛm kɛrɛ ni.

ref: Finally, my dear friends, since you belong to the Lord Jesus, we beg and urge you to live as we taught you. Then you will please God. You are already living that way, but try even harder.
text: Ya ninbaam, ya yo kereeri am kabo i ti ye ni ka am wo barasu na i yo Nyɛmɛ fiɛ. Ya si ka am wo nyini wo n wɔ nahɔrɛ su. Ama, ya sere am jande wɔ, ya Mibiɛ Yesu duma n nu, ka, am wo wo bo i kɔ tara sɔ.

ref: Remember the instructions we gave you as followers of the Lord Jesus.
text: Dama am si nɔaniɛ n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu fa maari ya na ya gusu fa maari am ni.

ref: God wants you to be holy, so don't be immoral in matters of sex.
text: Nyɛmɛ koro ka am wo wo casi wɔ, na má be yo sakaraya.

ref: Don't be a slave of your desires or live like people who don't know God.
text: Má am ma am konviki wo sakaraya sereya nu kabo minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n yo ni.

ref: You must not cheat any of the Lord's followers in matters of sex. Remember, we warned you that he punishes everyone who does such things.
text: Kabo i nya ti sɔ ni, má Kristo susu­fɔ be se ka i bu i niwa Kristo susu­fɔ ŋgbɛɛn fa kɔ nyini sam su. Ya yo seeri am wɔ ni nahɔrɛ kekereke, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori sɔ, Mibiɛ ni cín i mi sui.

ref: So if you don't obey these rules, you are not really disobeying us. Instead, you are disobeying God, who gives you his Holy Spirit.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri ahin keree­wa ni, i ti má ka i sukete adamande wɔ, ama, i sukete Nyɛmɛ wɔ, yiri ma am i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a.

ref: We don't have to write you about the need to love each other. God has taught you to do this,
text: Tɔ, fa kɔ kabo am koro am ninbaam ni, ya la má jɔrɛ be bo i ti ye ni ka ya kɛrɛ ma am, dama Nyɛmɛ akere ambɛrɛ ni am tii kabo am koro am beŋgu barasu.

ref: and you already have shown your love for all his people in Macedonia. But, my dear friends, we ask you to do even more.
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka, am koro ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu ni. Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ya susere am wɔ, am koro bu koro bo i kɔ tara sɔ.

ref: Try your best to live quietly, to mind your own business, and to work hard, just as we taught you to do.
text: Am yo aniɛ na am wo bɛnlɛ, na am niɛ am tiɛŋgu juma bo am yo i, na am fiɛn aŋuye na am di, kabo ya du mɔ seeri am ni.

ref: Then you will be respected by people who are not followers of the Lord, and you won't have to depend on anyone. The Lord's Coming
text: Nzɛn am yoori sɔ, nyini má minɛm bo bu nya yo má Yesu yarada n bu am a, na am niɛ má be anyumbaa fa kɔ am sa koroo­wa su. Kabo Mibiɛ ni bá kpie i nyi ba n jɔrɛ

ref: My friends, we want you to understand how it will be for those followers who have already died. Then you won't grieve over them and be like people who don't have any hope.
text: Tɔ, ya ninbaam, ya koro ka am si deke bo i ti nahɔrɛ fa kɔ minɛm bo ba wu n dɔ, adena má am ahore saaki ka minɛm bo bu la má tama be ni.

ref: We believe Jesus died and was raised to life. We also believe that when God brings Jesus back again, he will bring with him all who had faith in Jesus before they died.
text: Ya yo yarada ka Yesu wuuri wɔ na wɔ teŋge bekun, nyini ti, ya kaaki la yarada ka Nyɛmɛ téŋge minɛm bo bu yoori Yesu yarada na ba wu ni, na i má bu su Yesu su ba.

ref: Our Lord Jesus told us that when he comes, we won't go up to meet him ahead of his followers who have already died.
text: Keree­wa bo ya sufa kere am kisa n fite Mibiɛ n dɔ ɔ, ka, yɛrɛ bo ya toko la ŋgɔɔ ni, cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ n ba baari ni, yɛrɛ, ya dú má bɛrɛ n bo ba wu n mɔ.

ref: With a loud command and with the shout of the chief angel and a blast of God's trumpet, the Lord will return from heaven. Then those who had faith in Christ before they died will be raised to life.
text: Dama Mibiɛ n ni i tii bá fite aŋgoro n nu jura na i má nɔɔ, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ kpiɛn n tíɛn, na bu kán Nyɛmɛ awiɛ ni. Na minɛm bo bu yoori Yesu yarada na ba wu n dú mɔ teŋge bu yiwee n nu.

ref: Next, all of us who are still alive will be taken up into the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the sky. From that time on we will all be with the Lord forever.
text: Nyini saŋga, na Nyɛmɛ fá bɛrɛ, ni yɛrɛ bo ya toko la ŋgɔɔ ni, na ya kpá Mibiɛ n atin aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu. Na ya kɔ́ wo Mibiɛ n dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Encourage each other with these words.
text: Nyini ti, am fa ahin nɔaniɛm fa si am beŋgum ahore.

ref: You surely know that the Lord's return will be as a thief coming at night.
text: Dama ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si tenle su ka cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ ni bá kaaki ba ni, i yó kabo awiefɔ ba kɔŋguɛ ba wɔ n wɔ.

ref: People will think they are safe and secure. But destruction will suddenly strike them like the pains of a woman about to give birth. And they won't escape.
text: Saŋga bo minɛm ba suse ka tana­laifiɛ ni ahoreda wo bɛrɛ ni, nyini saŋga n nu ye kekereke kába ba tu bu a, kabo bara ba kutu ka i wu baa na i ti barasu ni. Nyini kekereke ni, sɔnɔ be kóro fite má nu.

ref: My dear friends, you don't live in darkness, and so that day won't surprise you like a thief.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ, ya ninbaam ni, am wo má awosin nu bo Mibiɛ n ba cɛɛn ni bá fura am nu, kabo awiefɔ ba fura sɔnɔ nu na i wɔ n wɔ.

ref: You belong to the light and live in the day. We don't live in the night or belong to the dark.
text: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ ti minɛm bo am wo wein n nu wɔ, kabo wiɛsu ti ni. Ya wo má kɔŋguɛ nu, wara awosin nu.

ref: Others may sleep, but we should stay awake and be alert.
text: Nyini ti, má am ma ya daafi kabo nyiŋgam daafi ni, am ma ya nyunu wo su na ya tara ya ŋu.

ref: People sleep during the night, and some even get drunk.
text: Kɔŋguɛ ye minɛm daafi a, kɔŋguɛ gusu ye minɛm nu nzan bo a.

ref: But we belong to the day. So we must stay sober and let our faith and love be like a suit of armor. Our firm hope that we will be saved is our helmet.
text: Ama, yɛrɛ wo wein nu wɔ, nyini ti, am ma ya tara ya ŋu, na ya fa yarada ni koro fa kanda ya ŋu ka bulaari pɛpɛrɛpɛ bo ya fa kanda ya hoe su, na ya fa ahoresi ka ya nya tii­de yo ka bulaari kere bo ya fa butu kanda ya tii.

ref: God doesn't intend to punish us, but wants us to be saved by our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ ayi má ya ka i fá yaa na i cin ya sui wɔ, ama, i yiiri ya ka ya nyá tii­de, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti wɔ.

ref: Christ died for us, so we could live with him, whether we are alive or dead when he comes.
text: I wuuri yɛrɛ dɛɛ ti wɔ, na saŋga bo i bá ba ni, nzɛn ya la ŋgɔɔ o, wara nzɛn ya wu wɔ o, ya ni yiri sáŋga wo a.

ref: This is why you must encourage and help each other, just as you are already doing. Final Instructions and Greetings
text: Nyini ti, am ma am beŋgu ahoresi, na am buka am beŋgu na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu ni, kabo kisa na am suyo ni. Fɔ­tu jɔrɛ

ref: My friends, we ask you to be thoughtful of your leaders who work hard and tell you how to live for the Lord.
text: Ya ninbaam, ya sere am wɔ, ka, am bu mɔ­fɔm ni, bɛrɛ bo bu yo Nyɛmɛ juma ni anyum­boro ma am ni, na bu niɛ am su, na bu kere am Nyɛmɛ atin ni.

ref: Show them great respect and love because of their work. Try to get along with each other.
text: Am ma bu jirima nahɔrɛ su, ni koro, fa kɔ bu juma n bo bu yo i n dɛɛ ti. Am ma tana­laifiɛ wo am ni am beŋgum dɔ.

ref: My friends, we beg you to warn anyone who isn't living right. Encourage anyone who feels left out, help all who are weak, and be patient with everyone.
text: Ya ninbaam, ya kaaki sere am wɔ, ka, am bɔbɔ nviɛya­fɔm ni, na am ma sɛrɛ­fɔm n ahoresi, na am buka daburuka­fɔm ni, na am fa sawɔrɛ ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

ref: Don't be hateful to people, just because they are hateful to you. Rather, be good to each other and to everyone else.
text: Má be se ka i fa i beŋgu satiɛ bo i yo i fa tɔ i mi kaari, ama, saŋga kɛrɛ am ma am sunsunni la ka, am yo sakpa ma am beŋgum ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

ref: Whatever happens, keep thanking God because of Jesus Christ. This is what God wants you to do.
text: na am da Nyɛmɛ asi, haari fa kɔ sa kɛrɛ bo i tu am. Kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka am yo lɛɛ, dama am ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n ti.

ref: and don't have anything to do with evil.
text: na am kete sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ pepere. Awieeri su dɛɛ ansɛ yoo­wa

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will make you completely holy. And may your spirit, soul, and body be kept healthy and faultless until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
text: Ya sere Nyɛmɛ, yiri n bo i ma ahoreda n ka, i ŋmisi am ahore­mbaa, ni am ŋuniɛ, ni am sunsunni kɛrɛ casi, adena am wo wo bo fin be mata má am, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo bá kpie i nyi ba ni.

ref: The one who chose you can be trusted, and he will do this.
text: Yiri bo wɔ fere am ni, yiri la dabuŋu, i yó nyini sam n kɛrɛ.

ref: Friends, please pray for us.
text: Ya ninbaam, am sere Nyɛmɛ ma ya gusu,

ref: Give the Lord's followers a warm greeting.
text: na am bisa ya ninbaam kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ni koro.

ref: In the name of the Lord I beg you to read this letter to all his followers.
text: M susere am wɔ, am niɛ Mibiɛ n ti, na am kan ahin kadasi n kere ya ninbaa yarada­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ na bu ti.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
text: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am.

ref: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God our Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu, ni Silivanusi, ni Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n bo am wo Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu na am ti ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ minɛm n a.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! When Christ Returns
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am ahoreda. Kabo Yesu bá di minɛm jɔrɛ barasu ni

ref: My dear friends, we always have good reason to thank God for you, because your faith in God and your love for each other keep growing all the time.
text: Ya ninbaam, wɔ yo daka ka ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, ambɛrɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ. Atin wo nu ka ya yo sɔ, dama am yarada n buka su wɔ nahɔrɛ su, na koro bo am koro am beŋgu n gusu subuka su sɔ ɔ.

ref: This is why we brag about you to all of God's churches. We tell them how patient you are and how you keep on having faith, even though you are going through a lot of trouble and suffering.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ya fa am fɔ sɔ ɔ, deke bo Kristo jamam wo kɛrɛ. Ya fɔ fa kɔ kabo am ayo yarada na am la mindi fa kɔ wuwuruwa n kɛrɛ bo minɛm sukere am, ni wahara n kɛrɛ bo am wo i nu ni.

ref: All this shows that God judges fairly and is making you fit to share in his kingdom for which you are suffering.
text: Nyini kere ka Nyɛmɛ di minɛm jɔrɛ ni i nahɔrɛ atin su a, na i se ka ambɛrɛ ti ye ni ka am wura i fɛmɛya n nu a, wahara n bo am adi i n dɛɛ ti.

ref: It is only right for God to punish everyone who is causing you trouble,
text: Nahɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, i má minɛm bo bu sukere am wahara n gusu di wahara,

ref: but he will give you relief from your troubles. God will do the same for us, when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven with his powerful angels
text: na i má ambɛrɛ bo am sudi wahara n ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ nya ya ŋu. Nyini sam kɛrɛ bá yo sɔ saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá fite aŋgoro ba ni i mɛrɛkɛm, ni bu yiko bambaka ni,

ref: and with a flaming fire. Our Lord Jesus will punish anyone who doesn't know God and won't obey his message.
text: ni sin, na i bá cin minɛm bo ba kete Nyɛmɛ n sui, ni bo ba de má ya Mibiɛ Yesu labari kpa ni.

ref: Their punishment will be eternal destruction, and they will be kept far from the presence of our Lord and his glorious strength.
text: Nyɛmɛ cín bu sui haari bu duma bá bo má, na bu ŋú má Mibiɛ ni, ni i yiko bambaka ni.

ref: This will happen on the day when the Lord returns to be praised and honored by all who have faith in him and belong to him. This includes you, because you believed what we said.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n bo i ba baari n ye i minɛm kɛrɛ wúra i kpiri a, na nyini minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu yo i yarada n má i jirima. Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ wo nyini minɛm n nu, dama am ayo labari n bo ya fa maari am n yarada.

ref: God chose you, and we keep praying that God will make you worthy of being his people. We pray for God's power to help you do all the good things you hope to do and your faith makes you want to do.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ya sere Nyɛmɛ ma am saŋga kɛrɛ n a. Ya sere i ka i ma am wo wo bo i ti ye ni ka i dɛɛ minɛm bo wɔ fere bu n wo ni. Na ya kaaki sere i ka i fa i yiko n buka am, na am yo am kunnu koroo­wa, ni juma kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa kɔ wie.

ref: Then, because of the undeserved grace of God and our Lord Jesus Christ, you will bring honor to the name of our Lord Jesus, and he will bring honor to you.
text: Nzɛn am nya yoori sɔ, nyini má minɛm ma ya Mibiɛ Yesu duma n jirima a, am dɛɛ ti, na i gusu má am nya jirima. Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n má i yo sɔ a.

ref: When our Lord Jesus returns, we will be gathered up to meet him. So I ask you, my friends,
text: Tɔ, ya ninbaam, m sujɔjɔ am dɔ fa kɔ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ba n su wɔ, ni kabo ya tíɛn ya nɔɔ na ya ni yiri kɔ́ yɛ n wɔ. Ya sere am wɔ,

ref: not to be easily upset or disturbed by people who claim the Lord has already come. They may say they heard this directly from the Holy Spirit, or from someone else, or even that they read it in one of our letters.
text: ka, má am kaaki am sunsunni wara am birisi, dama nbem ŋa Mibiɛ n adu mɔ aba asin n dɛɛ ti. Haari nzɛn be ŋa wɔ de nɔaniɛ Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ, wara nzɛn be jɔjɔɔri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ, wara nzɛn be seeri ka yɛrɛ akɛrɛ i kadasi n nu ase sɔ a, má am de i.

ref: Don't you remember I told you this while I was still with you?
text: Am teŋge má su ka saŋga bo m woori am dɔ ni, m yo seeri am ahin jɔrɛm kɛrɛ, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: You already know what is holding this wicked one back until it is time for him to come.
text: Kisa, am si deke bo i kanda i na wɔ nya ba má ni, má ka saŋga n aju bo i yí i ŋu fite fofoe nu.

ref: His mysterious power is already at work, but someone is holding him back. And the wicked one won't appear until this someone is out of the way.
text: Ama, sɔnɔtiɛ n bo i bu má ka pui wo bɛrɛ ni, yiri suyo i asiri nu juma wɔ kisa, má ka yiri n bo i kanda i ni, aba acin ayaki atin ni.

ref: Then he will appear, but the Lord Jesus will kill him simply by breathing on him. He will be completely destroyed by the Lord's glorious return.
text: Dɔ na sɔnɔtiɛ n nyá yi i ŋu fite fofoe nu, ama, saŋga n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá ni, na i fíta kun i, na i baawa n dɛɛ wein n gusu yó i ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: When the wicked one appears, Satan will pretend to work all kinds of miracles, wonders, and signs.
text: Tɔ, kabo sɔnɔtiɛ ni bá ba n yaa. I bá ni Sitana dɛɛ yiko n na i bá yo alekutura sam beberebe fa dadaka minɛm ka nyini sam fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ,

ref: Lost people will be fooled by his evil deeds. They could be saved, but they will refuse to love the truth and accept it.
text: na i bá bu kɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn barasu barasu dadaka minɛm bo bu ŋgɔɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn ni. Bu ŋgɔɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn, dama ba koro má ka bu su atin n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n su na bu nya tii­de.

ref: So God will make sure they are fooled into believing a lie.
text: Nyini ti, na Nyɛmɛ tá bu nyi, adena bu yó deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kɔrɛ n yarada.

ref: My friends, the Lord loves you, and it is only natural for us to thank God for you. God chose you to be the first ones to be saved. His Spirit made you holy, and you put your faith in the truth.
text: Ya ninbaam, ambɛrɛ bo Mibiɛ n koro am ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya da Nyɛmɛ asi ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti saŋga kɛrɛ, dama fite bumboori n ye Nyɛmɛ yiiri am ka am nya tii­de a. Deke bo i ma am nya tii­de n la ka, Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma am yo Nyɛmɛ minɛ casim a, na am bita nahɔrɛ atin, na am yo yarada.

ref: God used our preaching as his way of inviting you to share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Labari kpa n bo ya kan kereeri am na am deeri n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ fereeri am ka am nya tii­de a, na am nyá ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be.

ref: My friends, this is why you must remain faithful and follow closely what we taught you in person and by our letters.
text: Nyini ti, ya ninbaam, am bita nahɔrɛ atin n kenken, bo ya kaanri i na ya kɛrɛɛri i maari am ni, má am kaaki am sin bo i.

ref: God our Father loves us. He treats us with undeserved grace and has given us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope. We pray that our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father
text: Ya sere ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ni i tii, ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i koro ya, na i dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n nu, na wɔ ma ya ahoresi saŋga kɛrɛ, ni tama kpiri ni,

ref: Finally, our friends, please pray for us. This will help the message about the Lord to spread quickly, and others will respect it, just as you do.
text: Ya ninbaam, jɔrɛ bo wɔ ka n la ka, am sere Nyɛmɛ ma ya, na Mibiɛ n labari kpa n sandi bara yɛ ndende na bu de i ni jirima, kabo am deeri i ni.

ref: Pray that we may be kept safe from worthless and evil people. After all, not everyone has faith.
text: Am kaaki sere Nyɛmɛ, na i yí ya fite minɛ tiɛm saa nu, dama má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ yo labari kpa n yarada a.

ref: But the Lord can be trusted to make you strong and protect you from harm.
text: Ama, Mibiɛ n la dabuŋu, yiri má am faŋga, na i yó am kanda bo Sitana koro yo má am pui.

ref: He has made us sure that you are obeying what we taught you and that you will keep on obeying.
text: Ya Mibiɛ n ama ya ahoresi ka am suyo deke n kɛrɛ bo ya ma am nɔɔ ka am yo i ni, am kɔ su suyo i saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: I pray that the Lord will guide you to be as loving as God and as patient as Christ. Warnings against Laziness
text: Ya sere Mibiɛ n ka i ma am si kabo Nyɛmɛ koro am barasu, ni kabo Kristo mindiiri barasu ni. Wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ yo juma

ref: My dear friends, in the name of the Lord Jesus, I beg you not to have anything to do with any of your people who loaf around and refuse to obey the instructions we gave you.
text: Ya ninbaam, ya maari am nɔɔ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n nu ka, má am fa am ŋu gɔ ya ninbaam bo bu koro má ka bu yo juma, na bu ti nviɛya­fɔm n ŋu, ba kete ka bu su má nɔaniɛ n bo ya fa maari am n su lɛɛ.

ref: and we didn't accept food from anyone without paying for it. We didn't want to be a burden to any of you, so night and day we worked as hard as we could.
text: Ya di má sɔnɔ be diire ŋgbɛɛn. Ama, ya yoori juma wɔ nahɔrɛ su, wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ, adena má ya bo be kaari.

ref: Now we learn that some of you just loaf around and won't do any work, except the work of a busybody.
text: Má pui ti ye ya se sɔ a, dama ya ti labari ka am nbem wo bɛrɛ bo bu ti nviɛya­fɔm wɔ, bu yo má juma be, se bu nati ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, na bu sufa bu nɔɔ bɔ minɛm jɔrɛ nu.

ref: So, for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ, we ask and beg these people to settle down and start working for a living.
text: Nyini ti, ye ya sutu bu fɔ ni anyum­boro su ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n nu n a, ka, bu yo aniɛ tara bu ŋu, na bu yo juma na bu koro bita bu ŋu.

ref: Dear friends, you must never become tired of doing right.
text: Ama, ya ninbaam, ambɛrɛ yiri, má am ma am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am ni sakpa­yo.

ref: Be on your guard against any followers who refuse to obey what we have written in this letter. Put them to shame by not having anything to do with them.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri nɔaniɛ n bo ya kɛrɛ i ahin kadasi n nu afa ama am ni, am yi kɔrɛsi na am si i mi. Má am dodo i mi, adena nyinsɛ ti i.

ref: Don't consider them your enemies. Instead, speak kindly to them as you would to any other follower. Final Prayer
text: Ama, má am fa i mi yo am kpɔ­fɔ, am tu i fɔ ka am niwa a. Awieeri su dɛɛ ansɛ yoo­wa

ref: I pray that the Lord, who gives peace, will always bless you with peace. May the Lord be with all of you.
text: Ya sere Mibiɛ n ni i tii, yiri bo i ma ahoreda ni, ka, i ma am ahoreda saŋga kɛrɛ fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ nu, na i wo am kɛrɛ dɔ.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to all of you.
text: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am kɛrɛ.

ref: From Paul. God our Savior and Christ Jesus commanded me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, who gives us hope.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. M ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa a, Nyɛmɛ bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, yiri ama m nɔɔ ka m yo nyini juma n a, i ni Yesu Kristo bo i ma ya tama ni.

ref: Timothy, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind and merciful to you. May they bless you with peace! Warning against False Teaching
text: M sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma wɔrɔ Timoti wɔ, a ti ka m wa a nahɔrɛ su, dama mini ni wɔrɔ yo yarada kun wɔ. Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka u, na bu si u aŋunfɔ, na bu ma u ahoreda. Am si am ŋu sa ni minɛm bo bu keree­wa kɔ má atin su ni

ref: You needed to warn them to stop wasting their time on senseless stories and endless lists of ancestors. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help anyone to do God's work that can only be done by faith.
text: Se bu na bu yaki kɔrɛ jɔrɛm, ni nana dumam beberebe ni. Nyini jɔrɛm fa akuruwa ba a. Bu koro buka má sɔnɔ na i yo deke n bo Nyɛmɛ koroori ka i yo i. Be koro yo má kabo Nyɛmɛ koro, má ka i mi ayo i yarada.

ref: You must teach people to have genuine love, as well as a good conscience and true faith.
text: Deke n bo i ti na m maari u ahin nɔaniɛ n la ka, i fa koro ba a, nyini koro n fite ahore­mbaa fofoe wɔ, ni sunsunni kpa, ni yarada nahɔrɛ su.

ref: There are some who have given up these for nothing but empty talk.
text: Nyini minɛ n nbem amini ahin keree­wam ni, na bu subo anzama ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses. But they don't know what they are talking about, even though they think they do.
text: Bu koro ka bu yo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, bu kan i ni ahoresi wɔ, ama, bɛrɛ ni bu tii si má bu tiɛŋgu nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu.

ref: We know the Law is good, if it is used in the right way.
text: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni ti kpa a, nzɛn ya bitaari i kabo i ti ye ni ka ya bita i ni.

ref: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me the strength for my work because he knew he could trust me.
text: M da ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo asi ka, yiri ye wɔ ma m faŋga, na m koro yo m juma n a. M da i asi ni kabo wɔ bu m ka m la dabuŋu, na wɔ yi m ka m su i ni,

ref: I used to say terrible and insulting things about him, and I was cruel. But Christ had mercy on me because I didn't know what I was doing, and I had not yet put my faith in him.
text: haari ni bo m yo jɔjɔ wuraari i ŋgasi nu, na m kpiɛɛri i nzukɔ, na m kereeri i wuwuruwa ni, ama, nyini n kɛrɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ siiri m aŋunfɔ ɔ, dama m yo nya yo má i yarada, nyini ti, m yo si má sa bo m yo i ni.

ref: Christ Jesus our Lord treated me with undeserved grace and has greatly blessed my life with faith and love just like his own.
text: Na yiri Mibiɛ n ama ma di i ahɔrɔma akɔ asin su, na wɔ ma ma yo i yarada na m koro minɛm, kabo ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti.

ref: “Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners.” This saying is true, and it can be trusted. I was the worst sinner of all!
text: Ahin nɔaniɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, i ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ de i ni bu saa nnyɔ, yiri la ka, Yesu Kristo baari durunya n nu ka i ba de satiɛ­yo­fɔm tii ji wɔ. Mini ti sɔnɔ bo m satiɛ taraari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ dɛɛ a.

ref: But since I was worse than anyone else, God had mercy on me and let me be an example of the endless patience of Christ Jesus. He did this so that others would put their faith in Christ and have eternal life.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ siiri m aŋunfɔ. I yoori sɔ ɔ, adena Yesu Kristo koro kere kabo i la sawɔrɛ beberebe ni mini bo m satiɛ sunnu taraari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ dɛɛ ni. I yoori nyini sɔ ɔ, adena i fa m fa kere minɛm bo bu ba ka sin ni, na bu yo i yarada nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: I pray that honor and glory will always be given to the only God, who lives forever and is the invisible and eternal King! Amen.
text: Yiri ti Fɛmɛ Kpiri a, na i fɛmɛya n la má awieeri, i wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na be gusu koro ŋu má i, yiri ŋgumi kpein ti Nyɛmɛ a, i ti ye ni ka minɛm kɛrɛ kansi i na bu ma i jirima. Ami.

ref: Timothy, my son, the instructions I am giving you are based on what some prophets once said about you. If you follow these instructions, you will fight like a good soldier.
text: M wa Timoti, m sufa ahin nɔaniɛ n fa wura u saa n nu wɔ lɛɛ, i su kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm du mɔ jɔjɔɔri i fa kɔ u dɔ n su wɔ. Fa nyini nɔaniɛm ni yo ka u biɛsɔya neŋgem na a fa kun luɛ nyunyumi,

ref: You will be faithful and have a clear conscience. Some people have made a mess of their faith because they didn't listen to their consciences.
text: na a bita u yarada ni sunsunni kpa. Nbem akete ka bu fa má sunsunni kpa na bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, nyini ti, bu yarada bo bu yo yoori i ni aka ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: Two of them are Hymenaeus and Alexander. I have given these men over to the power of Satan, so they will learn not to oppose God.
text: Bɛrɛ nbem la Himɛnasi ni Alɛkizanda, bɛrɛ ye ma fa bu awura Sitana saa nu ka i kere bu ŋgɔnlɛ, adena bu sɔnsɔn kabo má bu wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu kun.

ref: First of all, I ask you to pray for everyone. Ask God to help and bless them all, and tell God how thankful you are for each of them.
text: Deke bo i du mɔ la ka, m susere am ni anyum­boro wɔ, ka, am sere Nyɛmɛ ma minɛm kɛrɛ, fa kɔ deke bo i ti bu miɛɛ­wa su, na am da i asi fa ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

ref: Pray for kings and others in power, so we may live quiet and peaceful lives as we worship and honor God.
text: Sere Nyɛmɛ ma nfɛmɛm, ni bo bu wo yiko woo­biri n kɛrɛ, na ya koro wo ni tana­laifiɛ, na ya woo­wa kere ka ya su Nyɛmɛ atin n su ni nahɔrɛ, na ya wo jirima wo ni ya beŋgu.

ref: This kind of prayer is good, and it pleases God our Savior.
text: Nyini nyɛmɛsere n ti kpa a, na i sun Nyɛmɛ nu, yiri ti ya Tii­de­fɔ a.

ref: God wants everyone to be saved and to know the whole truth, which is,
text: Yiri koro ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nya tii­de a, na bu si nahɔrɛ atin ni.

ref: There is only one God, and Christ Jesus is the only one who can bring us to God. Jesus was truly human, and he gave himself to rescue all of us.
text: Dama ya kɛrɛ si ka Nyɛmɛ kun n kpein wo bɛrɛ a, na sɔnɔ kun be gusu wo bɛrɛ bo i yo adamande ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu a, yiri la Yesu Kristo,

ref: This is why God chose me to be a preacher and an apostle of the good news. I am telling the truth. I am not lying. God sent me to teach the Gentiles about faith and truth.
text: Nyini ti ye Nyɛmɛ yiiri m ka m ba yo Yesu jaramasa, na m kan i labari kpa n kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na m kere bu keree­wam bo bu ti nahɔrɛ ni. M sukan nahɔrɛ wɔ, m bu má kɔrɛ.

ref: I would like for women to wear modest and sensible clothes. They should not have fancy hairdos, or wear expensive clothes, or put on jewelry made of gold or pearls.
text: M koro ka nbaram gusu di suŋguruya nyunyumi, bo i sɛ su. I ti má ye ni ka bu di bu tii­ŋmiɛ suŋguruya kɔ sin su, na má bu wura sika, wara aferem bo bu la gɔ agaya, wara suturam bo bu la gɔ agaya.

ref: They should be silent and not be allowed to teach or to tell men what to do.
text: M soro má su na bu kere nbiɛsɔm keree­wam, wara bu la yiko niɛ nbiɛsɔm su. Bu dɛɛ la ka, bu mɔ bu nɔɔ.

ref: After all, Adam was created before Eve,
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri Adam wɔ ka, ye i nya ba yiiri Awa.

ref: and the man Adam wasn't the one who was fooled. It was the woman Eve who was completely fooled and sinned.
text: Má Adam ye woo dadakaari i a, ama, i dakaari Awa wɔ, na wɔ kete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: But women will be saved by having children, if they stay faithful, loving, holy, and modest.
text: Ama, nzɛn nbaram laari yarada, ni koro, ni wo casi, na bu bita bu ŋu nyunyumi saŋga kɛrɛ, bu nyá bu tii­de fite bu baa­wu nu.

ref: It is true that anyone who desires to be a church official wants to be something worthwhile.
text: Ahin nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i boro i nyi na i yo Kristo jama n mɔ­fɔ, i mi koro juma kpa lɛɛ.

ref: They must not be heavy drinkers or troublemakers. Instead, they must be kind and gentle and not love money.
text: I ti má ye ni ka i nu nzan agaya, wara i ti aluwa koro­fɔ, ama, i yo i ŋu bɛnlɛ, na i koro tana­laifiɛ. I kaaki ti má ye ni ka i koro ŋwaa agaya.

ref: Church officials must be in control of their own families, and they must see that their children are obedient and always respectful.
text: Wɔ yo daka ka i koro bita i awuru­fɔm nyunyumi, na i ma i nbaam de i nɔaniɛ na bu ma i jirima deke kɛrɛ nu.

ref: If they don't know how to control their own families, how can they look after God's people?
text: Dama nzɛn sɔnɔ koro bita má i awuru­fɔm, i mi yo sɛ koro bita Kristo jama n a?

ref: They must not be new followers of the Lord. If they are, they might become proud and be doomed along with the devil.
text: I ti má ye ni ka i mi ti Yesu susu­fɔ fɔfɔrɛ, adena má i wura i ŋu kpiri na Nyɛmɛ di i jɔrɛ yo i ŋgbɛɛn kabo i yoori Sitana ni.

ref: Finally, they must be well-respected by people who are not followers. Then they won't be trapped and disgraced by the devil. Church Officers
text: Wɔ kaaki ayo daka ka i mi ti sɔnɔ bo minɛm bo bu ti má Kristo jama n nu­fɔm ma i jirima, adena, má Sitana nya i taraa­biri na i yo sa be bo i di nyinsɛ.

ref: And they must have a clear conscience and hold firmly to what God has shown us about our faith.
text: Wɔ yo daka ka bu subita Kristo jama n keree­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi akere n kenken, na bu la má ŋgasi be bu sunsunni nu.

ref: They must first prove themselves. Then if no one has anything against them, they can serve as officers.
text: I ti ye ni ka bu du mɔ fiiti bɛrɛ Kristo jama n buka­fɔm ni na bu niɛ. Na nzɛn bu la má ŋgasi be, am ma bu yo buka­fɔm n juma ni.

ref: Women must also be serious. They must not gossip or be heavy drinkers, and they must be faithful in everything they do.
text: I ti sɔ ɔ nbaram gusu dɔ. Wɔ yo daka ka bu ti minɛm bo bu la anyunuyi, na má bu susaaki minɛ duma. Wɔ yo daka ka bu bita bu ŋu, na bu la dabuŋu deke n kɛrɛ nu.

ref: Church officers must be faithful in marriage. They must be in full control of their children and everyone else in their home.
text: Kristo jama n buka­fɔ, i ti ye ni ka i ja bara kun n kpein, na i koro bita i nbaam, ni i dika­fɔm nyunyumi.

ref: Those who serve well as officers will earn a good reputation and will be highly respected for their faith in Christ Jesus. The Mystery of Our Religion
text: Nyini buka­fɔm bo bu yo bu juma na i kɔ atin su ni, bu kpini duma kpa ma bu ŋu wɔ, na bu kaaki koro jɔjɔ ni ahoresi kabo bu yo Yesu Kristo yarada ni. Keree­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi akere Kristo jamam ni

ref: I hope to visit you soon. But I am writing these instructions,
text: Kabo m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma u ni, m la tama ka m bá u dɔ kisa.

ref: Here is the great mystery of our religion: Christ came as a human. The Spirit proved that he pleased God, and he was seen by angels. Christ was preached to the nations. People in this world put their faith in him, and he was taken up to glory.
text: Be koro kete má ka ya nyɛmɛsu atin n bo i wo asiri nu la má aŋusu, ka o, “Na wɔ kaaki adamande ayi i ŋu fofoe nu. Na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n akere ka wɔ yo sa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu. Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm aŋu i. Na i susu­fɔm n akɔ ajɔjɔ i jɔrɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu. Na durunya n nu­fɔm dɔŋgu ayo i yarada. Na Nyɛmɛ afa i akɔ awo i bi aŋgoro, jirima kɛrɛ woo­biri ni.”

ref: God's Spirit clearly says that in the last days many people will turn from their faith. They will be fooled by evil spirits and by teachings that come from demons.
text: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ajɔjɔ wein ka, ahin awieeri su saŋga n bo ya wo nu ni, minɛ nbem yáki bu yarada atin ni, na bu su ajinim bo bu dadaka minɛm su, ni bu keree­wam ni.

ref: They will also be fooled by the false claims of liars whose consciences have lost all feeling. These liars
text: Nyini keree­wam sɔ fite minɛm bo bu ti dadaka­fɔm na bu nati bu kɔrɛ n dɔ ɔ, bo bu sunsunni akaaki haari na bu yo satiɛ mmɔɔ, na bu sunsunni ma má bu ka ba yo satiɛ.

ref: What God has said and your prayer will make it fit to eat. Paul's Advice to Timothy
text: dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ni nyɛmɛsere ma nyini diire n yo casi wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: If you teach these things to other followers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus. You will show you have grown up on the teachings about our faith and on the good instructions you have obeyed.
text: Nzɛn a faari ahin keree­wam kere ya ninbaam ni, nyini kere ka a ti Yesu Kristo amusui­su­fɔ kpa. Nyini keree­wam bo a du mɔ ade bu ni, na a yo bu yarada, na a si ka bu ti nahɔrɛ, na a su bu su ni, bu ti ka diire bo i ma u faŋga n wɔ.

ref: Don't have anything to do with worthless, senseless stories. Work hard to be truly religious.
text: Ama, kete jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bo bu kɔ má Nyɛmɛ atin su ni, na a boro u nyi na a sɔnsɔn sa bo i kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin n su nahɔrɛ su.

ref: As the saying goes, “Exercise is good for your body, but religion helps you in every way. It promises life now and forever.” These words are worthwhile and should not be forgotten.
text: Nyini nɔaniɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo i ti ye ni ka ya de i na ya yo i yarada.

ref: We have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of everyone, but especially of those who have faith. This is why we work and struggle so hard.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ya boro ya nyi, na ya yo juma ni nahɔrɛ su, dama ya daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu, yiri wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, yiri ti minɛ kɛrɛ Tii­de­fɔ a, maala bo bu yo i yarada ni.

ref: Teach these things and tell everyone to do what you say.
text: Fa ahin keree­wam n fa kere bu, na a ma bu su bu su.

ref: Don't let anyone make fun of you, just because you are young. Set an example for other followers by what you say and do, as well as by your love, faith, and purity.
text: Má a ma be bu u ŋgbɛɛn ka a ti gbɛfɛnɛ n dɛɛ ti, ama, ma yarada­yo­fɔm ni ŋu u sa­yoo­wam na bu sɔn u su. Bɛrɛ la u nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa, ni deke bo a yo i, ni kabo a koro minɛm ni, ni u yarada n bo a la i ni, ni u woo­wa casi ni.

ref: Until I arrive, be sure to keep on reading the Scriptures in worship, and don't stop preaching and teaching.
text: Yo aniɛ na a sukan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n na Kristo jama n ti, na a sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, na a sukere minɛm Nyɛmɛ atin ni, na m nya ba.

ref: Remember these things and think about them, so everyone can see how well you are doing.
text: Yo ahin juma ni anyum­boro na a fa u ŋu ma nyinim kɛrɛ, adena minɛm kɛrɛ ŋu kabo u nyɛmɛsu n kɔ atin su barasu.

ref: Be careful about the way you live and about what you teach. Keep on doing this, and you will save not only yourself, but the people who hear you.
text: Si u ŋu sa ni u keree­wam ni. Kɔ su suyo ahin sam ni, dama nzɛn a nya yoori sɔ, wɔrɔ ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ti u nɔaniɛ ni nyá tii­de.

ref: Don't correct an older man. Encourage him, as you would your own father. Treat younger men as you would your own brother,
text: Má a tiɛn kpiɛn ŋu, ama, jɔjɔ i dɔ kabo a jɔjɔ u si dɔ ni, na a tu ngbɛfɛnɛm fɔ ka u ninbaam,

ref: and treat older women as you would your own mother. Show the same respect to younger women that you would to your sister.
text: na a fa nbara nkpiɛnm yo ka u nim, na a fa tarawam ni nsuŋgurum yo ka u ninbaam, na u ni bɛrɛ wo ni ahore­mbaa casi.

ref: Take care of any widow who is really in need.
text: Bita nkuna­fɔm bo bu la má buka­fɔ be n ni jirima.

ref: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should learn to serve God by taking care of her, as she once took care of them. This is what God wants them to do.
text: Ama, nzɛn kuna­fɔ la nbaam, wara anumam, i ti ye ni ka bu suniɛ i dika kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni. Nyini kere ka, bu sufa bu wu­fɔm ŋkatɔ tɔ bu kaari wɔ lɛɛ. Dama nyini n sɔ yo Nyɛmɛ fiɛ a.

ref: A widow who is really in need is one who doesn't have any relatives. She has faith in God, and she keeps praying to him night and day, asking for his help.
text: Nzɛn kuna­fɔ wo i ŋgumi na i la má buka­fɔ be, wɔ fa i tama kɛrɛ awura Nyɛmɛ saa nu lɛɛ. Na i susere Nyɛmɛ jande wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ ka i buka i.

ref: A widow who thinks only about having a good time is already dead, even though she is still alive.
text: Ama, kuna­fɔ kɛrɛ bo i nati yo i kunnu koroo­wa, haari ni bo i la ŋgɔɔ mmɔɔ ni, i ti ka wɔ wu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Tell all this to everyone, so they will do the right thing.
text: Fa ahin keree­wam kɛrɛ kere nkuna­fɔm, ni bu dika­fɔm, adena má be ŋu bu ŋgasi.

ref: People who don't take care of their relatives, and especially their own families, have given up their faith. They are worse than someone who doesn't have faith in the Lord.
text: Ama, nzɛn be cɛcɛ wo má nu ka i niɛ i lamɔsim dika, maala i dika­fɔm, i ti ka i mi akete Kristo atin n lɛɛ. Nyini saŋga ni, sɔnɔ bo wɔ yo má yarada tara i mi co lɛɛ.

ref: For a widow to be put on the list of widows, she must be at least
text: Kuna­fɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya ju má afɔ abura­sɛn, má a kɛrɛ i duma buka Kristo jama nkuna­fɔm su. Bekun, na i ti bo wɔ du mɔ aja biɛsɔ kun wɔ,

ref: She must also be well-known for doing all sorts of good things, such as raising children, giving food to strangers, welcoming God's people into her home, helping people in need, and always making herself useful.
text: na minɛm di i daani ka i yo sakpa a, má pui ti, i ti bo wɔ ta nbaam ataari kpa, na wɔ de wɔfɔm i dika, na wɔ yo i ŋu kaan ka i su Nyɛmɛ minɛm, na wɔ buka minɛm bo bu woori kekereke nu, na wɔ su amusui kpa.

ref: Besides, they will become lazy and get into the habit of going from house to house. Next, they will start gossiping and become busybodies, talking about things that are none of their business.
text: Bekun, nyini ma bu koro ba yo nviɛya­fɔm na bu ba sunati awuru awurum nu, i ti má ka bu yo nviɛya­fɔm ŋgumi wɔ, ama, na bu sukpura tere, na bu sufa bu nɔɔ suwura minɛm jɔrɛ nu, na bu sujɔjɔ jɔrɛm bo wɔ ti má ye ni ka bu jɔjɔ bu.

ref: I would prefer that young widows get married, have children, and look after their families. Then the enemy won't have any reason to say insulting things about us.
text: Nyini ti, mini dɔ, nzɛn nkuna­fɔm toko ti tarawam, i ti ye ni ka bu ja na bu wu nbaam, na bu bita bu awurum, na má ya kpɔ­fɔm koro ŋu ŋgasi be bo bu koro nya atin jɔjɔ ya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kabo bu koro.

ref: Look what's already happened to some of the young widows! They have turned away to follow Satan.
text: Dama nkuna­fɔ nbem adu mɔ akete Kristo atin n asu Sitana su.

ref: If a woman who is a follower has any widows in her family, she should help them. This will keep the church from having that burden, and then the church can help widows who are really in need. Church Leaders
text: Ama, Kristo susu­fɔ bara kɛrɛ bo nkuna­fɔm wo i dika, i ti ye ni ka yiri ni i tii niɛ bu dika, na má bu fa bu fie fie Kristo jama n ni, adena Kristo jama n koro niɛ nkuna­fɔm bo bu la má buka­fɔ be fieo n su.

ref: Church leaders who do their job well deserve to be paid twice as much, especially if they work hard at preaching and teaching.
text: Kristo jama ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu yo mɔ­fɔ juma nyunyumi ni, i ti ye ni ka bu nya bu ŋkatɔ busu nnyɔ, maala bɛrɛ bo bu yo bu juma ni anyum­boro ka bu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ na bu kaaki kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ atin n ni.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “Don't muzzle an ox when you are using it to grind grain.” You also know the saying, “Workers are worth their pay.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Nzɛn a bita toora subobo ŋgani, má a cici i nɔɔ.” Na ba kaaki akɛrɛ ka, “I ti ye ni ka juma­yo­fɔ nya i ŋkatɔ.”

ref: Don't listen to any charge against a church leader, unless at least two or three people bring the same charges.
text: Má a se ka a de jɔrɛ be bo ba kpiɛ asɔ Kristo jama n buka­fɔ n kpiɛn be, má ka minɛ nnyɔ, wara nsan adi jɔrɛ n daani ka i ti nahɔrɛ.

ref: But if any of the leaders should keep on sinning, they must be corrected in front of the whole group, as a warning to everyone else.
text: Ama, bo bu yo satiɛ ni, se bu nahɔrɛ jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, adena nyiŋgam kɛrɛ soro sɛrɛ.

ref: In the presence of God and Christ Jesus and their chosen angels, I order you to follow my instructions! Be fair with everyone, and don't have any favorites.
text: M suma u nɔaniɛ lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo ni mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ nyunu, ka, a de ahin keree­wam ni, na má a fa tantambu­nu, wara má a koro be tara be fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo a yo i n su.

ref: Don't be too quick to accept people into the service of the Lord by placing your hands on them. Don't sin because others do, but stay close to God.
text: Má a koro nde na a fa u saa nana sɔnɔ be ŋu, na a sere Nyɛmɛ ma i mi ka i yo Nyɛmɛ juma, má a ma u saa n wo be dɛɛ satiɛ yoo­wa n nu. Ma u woo­wa yo casi.

ref: Stop drinking only water. Take a little wine to help your stomach trouble and the other illnesses you often have.
text: A la má laifiɛ saŋga saŋga n dɛɛ ti, má a nu nzue ŋgumi, ama, nu nzan mmɔɔ kaan, na i buka u fa kɔ akunnuyaari n bo i yaki má u ni.

ref: Some people get caught in their sins almost at once, even before the time of judgment. But other people's sins don't show up until later.
text: Minɛ nbem satiɛ yoo­wa kɛrɛ kaba da fofoe nu wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋu i, nyini satiɛm ye Nyɛmɛ fá di bu jɔrɛ a, ama, na nbem gusu dɛɛ satiɛ bo bu yo i bá fite fofoe nu kasin.

ref: If you are a slave, you should respect and honor your owner. This will keep people from saying bad things about God and about our teaching.
text: Kristo jama nbaam bo bu ti akɔɔm ni, i ti ye ni ka bu ma bu mibiɛm jirima nyunyumi, adena be koro saaki má Nyɛmɛ duma, wara ya keree­wam ni.

ref: If any of you slaves have owners who are followers, you should show them respect. After all, they are also followers of Christ, and he loves them. So you should serve and help them the best you can. False Teaching and True Wealth These are the things you must teach and tell the people to do.
text: Akɔɔm bo bu mibiɛm gusu ti yarada­yo­fɔm ni, i ti má ye ni ka bu bu bu mibiɛm ŋgbɛɛn ka bu kɛrɛ ti yarada­yo­fɔm n ti, ama, i ti ye ni ka bu su bu tara mibiɛm bo bu ti má yarada­yo­fɔm ni, dama bɛrɛ n bo bu sudi bu amusui n kpɛrɛ n ti bu ninbaam bo bu koro bu wɔ. Keree­wa bo i kɔ má atin su ni ŋwaa koro awaraye jɔrɛ Fa ahin keree­wam n kan kere u jama n na a se ka bu yo bu.

ref: Those people who disagree are proud of themselves, but they don't really know a thing. Their minds are sick, and they like to argue over words. They cause jealousy, disagreements, unkind words, evil suspicions,
text: Nyini minɛm n ti kpirikoro­fɔm wɔ, na bu kaaki si má pui. Na bu la sunsunni tiɛ, na bu se akuruwa ni minɛm fa kɔ nɔaniɛ keree­wam bu. Nyini ma bu si kɔrɛ a, na bu di aluwa, na bu kpiɛ bu beŋgu nzukɔ, na bu la sunsun­mase fa kɔ bu beŋgu dɔ,

ref: and nasty quarrels. They have wicked minds and have missed out on the truth. These people think religion is supposed to make you rich.
text: na bu nati suse akuruwa. Bu la má sunsunni kpa, na nahɔrɛ wo má bu dɔ. Na bu sunsun ka nzɛn sɔnɔ su Nyɛmɛ atin su, i nyá kpɛrɛ wɔ.

ref: And religion does make your life rich, by making you content with what you have.
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nyunu yi deke bo i la i, na i su Nyɛmɛ atin su, i mi nyá kpɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: We didn't bring anything into this world, and we won't take anything with us when we leave.
text: Dama ya fa má pui aba má durunya n nu fɛn. Ya kaaki koro fa má pui fite má fɛn.

ref: So we should be satisfied just to have food and clothes.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn ya la diirem ni suturam, i ti ye ni ka ya nyunu yi bu.

ref: People who want to be rich fall into all sorts of temptations and traps. They are caught by foolish and harmful desires that drag them down and destroy them.
text: Ama, minɛm bo bu nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su agaya, na bu koro ka bu nya ajɛkɛ beberebe ni, bu nyini neŋge koro n sɔ bu ŋgɛɛ wɔ, na i nya bu taraa­biri fa kɔ bu konvikiya sunsunni ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn n su. Nyini ma bu kpaasi a, na bu ka ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: The love of money causes all kinds of trouble. Some people want money so much they have given up their faith and caused themselves a lot of pain. Fighting a Good Fight for the Faith
text: Dama ŋwaa koro awaraye ti satiɛ kɛrɛ bunlui a. Nbem wo bɛrɛ bo bu koro ŋwaa agaya, nyini sɔ ma bu kaaki bu sin bo Kristo atin n wɔ, na i fa bu wura ahoresaaki bambaka nu. Kabo Pɔɔlu maari Timoti nɔɔ ka i yo i juma barasu ni

ref: Timothy, you belong to God, so keep away from all these evil things. Try your best to please God and to be like him. Be faithful, loving, dependable, and gentle.
text: Ama, wɔrɔ Timoti, a ti Nyɛmɛ sɔnɔ ɔ, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka má a dodo nyini sam kɛrɛ be. Boro u nyi na a yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, na a su nyɛmɛsu atin n su, na a yo i yarada, na a koro minɛm, na a la mindi, na a wo bɛnlɛ.

ref: Fight a good fight for the faith and claim eternal life. God offered it to you when you clearly told about your faith, while so many people listened.
text: Boro u nyi na a tu ŋgoni kpa bita u yarada n kenken. Nyini má a di nasara, na a nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, ahin ŋgɔɔ n ye Nyɛmɛ fereeri u ka i fá ma u a, na wɔrɔ ni u tii diiri i daani minɛ beberebe nyunu ni.

ref: Now I ask you to make a promise. Make it in the presence of God, who gives life to all, and in the presence of Christ Jesus, who openly told Pontius Pilate about his faith.
text: Nyɛmɛ ma deke n kɛrɛ ŋgɔɔ a, na Yesu Kristo gusu jinaari Gomina Pɔntiyo Pilato nyunu diiri daani kpa, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m suma u nɔɔ Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo nyunu n a,

ref: The glorious God is the only Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords. At the time that God has already decided, he will send Jesus Christ back again.
text: Nyɛmɛ afa nyini saŋga n bo i koro ka Yesu ba n afa ase, nyini saŋga n juuri, ye Nyɛmɛ má i ba a. Yiri ŋgumi niɛ deke n kɛrɛ su ye kansi wo i dɔ a. Yiri ti nfɛmɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim kɛrɛ Fɛmɛ a, na i ti mibiɛm kɛrɛ Mibiɛ.

ref: Only God lives forever! And he lives in light that no one can come near. No human has ever seen God or ever can see him. God will be honored, and his power will last forever. Amen.
text: Yiri ŋgumi kpein wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, na i woo­biri n bo i wo n ti wein wɔ, na be koro dodo má nyini wein ni. Be nya ŋu má i cɛɛn be da, be gusu koro ŋu má i wɔ mmɔɔ cɛɛn be cɛɛn be. I dɛɛ la jirima ni faŋga, saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

ref: Warn the rich people of this world not to be proud or to trust in wealth that is easily lost. Tell them to have faith in God, who is rich and blesses us with everything we need to enjoy life.
text: Se bo bu la ajɛkɛ durunya n nu fɛn n ka má bu fɔ, na má bu fa bu tama gɔ nyini ajɛkɛm bo bu cɛ má n ŋu, ama, bu fa gɔ Nyɛmɛ ŋu, yiri bo i ce ya deke n kɛrɛ kɔ sin su n na ya fa yo ya koroo­wa ni.

ref: Instruct them to do as many good deeds as they can and to help everyone. Remind the rich to be generous and share what they have.
text: Se bu ka bu yo sakpa dɔŋgu, na bu ce aceerim, na deke kɛrɛ bo bu la i, na bu sukpaaki nu ma nyiŋgam.

ref: This will lay a solid foundation for the future, so they will know what true life is like.
text: Nzɛn bu nya yoori sɔ, i ti ka ba tiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ abo i bu afa ase fa kɔ fa ba lɛɛ, adena bu nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i ti ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su ni.

ref: Timothy, guard what God has placed in your care! Don't pay any attention to godless and stupid talk that sounds smart but really isn't.
text: Timoti, niɛ deke bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama u ka a niɛ i dika n su nyunyumi. Cin u ŋu yaki minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ jɔjɔɔ­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn na bu bu má Nyɛmɛ, na bu sunsun ka bu si wɔ, ama, bu si má pui.

ref: Some people have even lost their faith by believing this talk. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: Dama nbem afun ajina su ka bu la si, nyini dɛɛ ti, ba mini Kristo atin ni. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

ref: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus. God himself chose me to be an apostle, and he gave me the promised life that Jesus Christ makes possible.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. M ti Yesu Kristo jamarasa a, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu, yiri Nyɛmɛ asunma m ka m kɔ kan kere minɛm kabo i yo seeri ka minɛm bo bu ni Yesu Kristo yo kun ni, bu nyá ŋgɔɔ.

ref: Timothy, you are like a dear child to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Christ Jesus will be kind and merciful to you and will bless you with peace! Do Not Be Ashamed of the Lord
text: M sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma wɔrɔ Timoti bo a ti ka m wa koroo­wa n wɔ. Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka u, na bu si u aŋunfɔ, na bu ma u ahoreda. Kabo Pɔɔlu maari Timoti ahoresi ka i yo juma barasu ni

ref: Night and day I mention you in my prayers. I am always grateful for you, as I pray to the God my ancestors and I have served with a clear conscience.
text: M da Nyɛmɛ asi, yiri ye m su i ni sunsunni kpa, kabo m nam yo suuri i ni. M sere Nyɛmɛ sɔ ɔ, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu, na m nati suda i asi ma u.

ref: I remember how you cried, and I want to see you, because this would make me truly happy.
text: M teŋge su kabo a yo suunri ni nyunvuin ni, m wara ado u, m koro ka m ŋu u, adena m ahore jɔ.

ref: I also remember the genuine faith of your mother Eunice. Your grandmother Lois had the same sort of faith, and I am sure you have it as well.
text: M wara fi má u yarada n bo a yo i nahɔrɛ su n sa, nyini yarada n gusu ye u nabara Loyisi, ni u ni Yunisi laari i a. Ma yo yarada ka wɔrɔ mmɔɔ la i sɔ ɔ.

ref: So I ask you to make full use of the gift God gave you when I placed my hands on you. Use it well.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n fa ceeri u ni, saŋga bo m faari m saa nanaari u ŋu, na m sereeri Nyɛmɛ maari u ni, m koro ka m teŋge u su ka a kaaki yɛ i su fa yo juma.

ref: Don't be ashamed to speak for our Lord. And don't be ashamed of me, just because I am in jail for serving him. Use the power that comes from God and join with me in suffering for telling the good news.
text: Nyini ti, má a ma nyinsɛ ti u ka a kan Mibiɛ n jɔrɛ n kere minɛm, wara má a ma nyinsɛ ti u ka m wo dan­saraka nu, Kristo dɛɛ ti. Ama, wɔrɔ mmɔɔ di wahara fa kɔ labari kpa n kan su, kabo m diiri i ni, na Nyɛmɛ ma u faŋga na a koro mindi de i.

ref: God saved us and chose us to be his holy people. We did nothing to deserve this, but God planned it because he is so kind. Even before time began God planned for Christ Jesus to show kindness to us.
text: I deeri ya tii ji wɔ, na wɔ fere ya ka ya yo i dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm, má ka ya sakpa bo ya yo i n dɛɛ ti, ama, i dɛɛ kunnu koroo­wa ni i ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti a. Saŋga bo i nya yi má durunya ni, ye i yoori ya nyini sakpa n a, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: Now Christ Jesus has come to offer us God's gift of undeserved grace. Christ our Savior defeated death and brought us the good news. It shines like a light and offers life that never ends.
text: Ama, kisa na Yesu Kristo, yiri bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ n aba durunya n nu ni, ya ŋu Nyɛmɛ koro ni. Labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, ya si ka Kristo akunma yiwee faŋga, na ya koro nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: My work is to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher.
text: Nyɛmɛ ayi m ka m yo Yesu jaramasa na m kan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n kere minɛm,

ref: This is why I am suffering now. But I am not ashamed! I know the one I have faith in, and I am sure he can guard until the last day what he has trusted me with.
text: nyini dɛɛ ti ye m di ahin waharam n kɛrɛ n a. Ama, nyinsɛ ti má m, dama m si sɔnɔ bo ma daafi i ŋu ni, na labari kpa n bo wɔ fa ama m ni, m si tenle su ka i koro niɛ i su fa kɔ ju haari jina cɛɛn.

ref: Now follow the example of the correct teaching I gave you, and let the faith and love of Christ Jesus be your model.
text: Bita nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ bo ma kan akere u na i ti nahɔrɛ n kenken, na a yo yarada na a koro minɛm, kabo ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti.

ref: You have been trusted with a wonderful treasure. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit, who lives within us.
text: Bita labari kpa n nyunyumi. I ti aceeri kpiri wɔ, ye Nyɛmɛ afa awura u saa nu a. Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i wo ya dɔ n buka u, na a bita i nyunyumi.

ref: You know that everyone in Asia has turned against me, especially Phygelus and Hermogenes.
text: A si ka yarada­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni, fa saŋga ni Figelosi ni Hɛmogenesi n kɛrɛ, aŋmati ayaki m.

ref: I pray that the Lord will be kind to the family of Onesiphorus. He often cheered me up and wasn't ashamed of me when I was put in jail.
text: M sere Nyɛmɛ ka i si Onesifɔrɔsi dika­fɔm kɛrɛ aŋunfɔ, dama saŋga kɛrɛ yiri titi m nyunu a. Nyinsɛ ati má i ka m wo dan­saraka nu,

ref: Then after he arrived in Rome, he searched everywhere until he found me.
text: ama, i ba juuri Roma miɛ kpiri n nu fɛn ni, i niɛniɛɛri m busu wɔ ni anyum­boro, haari na wɔ ba aŋu m.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will ask God to show mercy to Onesiphorus on the day of judgment. You know how much he helped me in Ephesus.
text: M sere Nyɛmɛ ka i si Onesifɔrɔsi aŋunfɔ, jina cɛɛn. Wɔrɔ ni u tii kaaki si buka bo i fa bukaari m barasu, saŋga bo m wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu ni.

ref: Timothy, my child, you must let Christ Jesus make you strong by his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔrɔ m wa, ahɔrɔma n bo Yesu Kristo ma a diiri i n dɛɛ ti, má a yɔrɔki u ŋu, yo u ŋu kenken.

ref: You have often heard me teach. Now I want you to tell these same things to followers who can be trusted to tell others.
text: Jɔrɛ n bo a tiiri i m dɔ, na minɛ beberebe woori dɔ tiiri i ni, fa wura minɛm bo bu la dabuŋu n saa nu, na bɛrɛ gusu koro fa kere nyiŋgam.

ref: As a good soldier of Christ Jesus you must endure your share of suffering.
text: Kabo a ti Yesu Kristo dɛɛ soja kpa ni, yo siriya na wɔrɔ mmɔɔ di wahara n kabo nyiŋgam di i barasu ni.

ref: And farmers who work hard are the first to eat what grows in their field.
text: Dɔdɔɔri­fɔ bo i yo juma nahɔrɛ su ni, wɔ yo daka ka yiri du mɔ nya diire fɔfɔrɛ n be di a.

ref: If you keep in mind what I have told you, the Lord will help you understand completely.
text: Sunsun fa kɔ deke bo m suse u n su, na Mibiɛ n buka u na a ti i kɛrɛ bu.

ref: Keep your mind on Jesus Christ! He was from the family of David and was raised from death, just as my good news says.
text: Teŋge su fa kɔ Yesu Kristo jɔrɛ n su, ka i fiteeri Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu n nu wɔ, ye i wuuri a, na wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu, m labari kpa n lɛɛ, sɔ ye m kan i a.

ref: And because of this message, I am locked up in jail and treated like a criminal. But God's good news isn't locked in jail,
text: Labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, ye m di wahara n a, haari ba wura m jɔrɔkɔ ase ka m ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n yiri, i wo má jɔrɔkɔ nu.

ref: and so I am willing to put up with anything. Then God's special people will be saved and given eternal glory because they belong to Christ Jesus.
text: Nyini ti, minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ka bu ti i dɛɛ minɛm n dɛɛ ti, ma mindi ade deke n kɛrɛ, adena bɛrɛ mmɔɔ nyá tii­de n bo i fite Yesu Kristo dɔ ni, na i má bu jirima n bo i la má awieeri Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni.

ref: Here is a true message: “If we died with Christ, we will live with him.
text: Ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo i ŋa, “Nzɛn ya ni yiri saŋga wuuri, ya ni yiri bá saŋga nya ŋgɔɔ.

ref: If we don't give up, we will rule with him. If we deny that we know him, he will deny that he knows us.
text: Nzɛn ya mindi suuri i, ya ni yiri bá saŋga di i fɛmɛya n a. Nzɛn ya keteeri i, yiri gusu bá kete ya.

ref: If we are not faithful, he will still be faithful. Christ cannot deny who he is.” An Approved Worker
text: Nzɛn yɛrɛ la má dabuŋu, yiri la dabuŋu wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, dama i koro kete má i ŋu.” Kristo jama n mɔ­fɔ bo Nyɛmɛ da i ŋu ni

ref: Don't let anyone forget these things. And with God as your witness, you must warn them not to argue about words. These arguments don't help anyone. In fact, they ruin everyone who listens to them.
text: Teŋge u minɛm su fa kɔ nyini jɔrɛm n su, na a se bu ka Nyɛmɛ wo u daani, ka má bu se akuruwa fa kɔ nɔaniɛ keree­wam bo i la má kpɛrɛ, i saaki minɛm bo bu so bu sui ti nyini nɔaniɛ n wɔ.

ref: Do your best to win God's approval as a worker who doesn't need to be ashamed and who teaches only the true message.
text: Boro u nyi na a fa u ŋu ma Nyɛmɛ juma na i sun i nu. Na a yo u juma nyunyumi na má nyinsɛ ti u, na a kere keree­wa bo i ti nahɔrɛ na i kɔ atin su nyunyumi.

ref: That sort of talk is like a sore that won't heal. And Hymenaeus and Philetus have been talking this way
text: Nyini keree­wam sɔ ti ka kambue bo i wie má na i tɛrɛki saŋga kɛrɛ wɔ. Minɛ nnyɔm bo bu kere nyini keree­wam n la Himɛnasi, ni Filetosi,

ref: by teaching that the dead have already been raised to life. This is far from the truth, and it is destroying the faith of some people.
text: bɛrɛ kere ka saŋga bo minɛm wu na bu teŋge bu yiwee nu asin n a. Ahin keree­wam n ti kɔrɛ wɔ, nyini keree­wa n sɔ ma minɛ nbem ŋuniɛ ayɔrɔki bu fa kɔ bu yarada bo bu la i n su wɔ.

ref: In a large house some dishes are made of gold or silver, while others are made of wood or clay. Some of these are special, and others are not.
text: Ya si ka awuru kpiri nu la taalɛ ni ayaba barasu barasu kɛrɛ, bu faari jɛtɛ ni sika yoori nbem a, na nbem gusu ti baka ni yakati dɛɛ. Bu fa nbem fa yo juma cɛɛn­kpiri cɛɛn, na bu fa nbem gusu yo juma cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn.

ref: This is also how it is with people. The ones who stop doing evil and make themselves pure will become special. Their lives will be holy and pleasing to their Master, and they will be able to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: Nzɛn sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yi i ŋu fiteeri nyini kɔrɛ keree­wam n nu, Nyɛmɛ fá i mi yo ka cɛɛn­kpiri ayaba a, dama wɔ fa i ŋu ama Mibiɛ ni, na i yo juma kpa bo i kun kpɛrɛ ma i, má pui ti, wɔ sɛsɛ i ŋu su ka i yo juma kpa ma i Mibiɛ ni.

ref: Run from temptations that capture young people. Always do the right thing. Be faithful, loving, and easy to get along with. Worship with people whose hearts are pure.
text: Tara u ŋu fa kɔ ngbɛfɛnɛm konviki tiɛ sam su. Boro u nyi, na a yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, na a yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na a koro minɛm, na a la tana­laifiɛ, nyini má a buka minɛm bo bu su Nyɛmɛ ni ahore casi n su a.

ref: Stay away from stupid and senseless arguments. These only lead to trouble,
text: Ama, má a fa u nɔɔ bɔ sinnzinya jɔrɛm n nu, ni bisaa­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm. A si ka nyini n sɔ kpura minɛ tii wɔ.

ref: and God's servants must not be troublemakers. They must be kind to everyone, and they must be good teachers and very patient.
text: Wɔ yo daka ka má Mibiɛ n akɔɔ n suse akuruwa. I ti ye ni ka i yo aye ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ, na i koro fa sawɔrɛ kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n nyunyumi.

ref: Be humble when you correct people who oppose you. Maybe God will lead them to turn to him and learn the truth.
text: Haari minɛm bo bu se i akuruwa ni mmɔɔ, i ti ye ni ka i fa sawɔrɛ ni bɛrɛ, na i kere bu atin bo bu fa i, beni, a ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ má bu kaaki bu wo na bu si nahɔrɛ atin ni.

ref: They have been trapped by the devil, and he makes them obey him, but God may help them escape.
text: Nyini ma bu kaaki fa sunsunni kpa na bu ŋmati fite toroku n nu a, bo Sitana n asɔ atara bu na i ma bu yo i koroo­wa ni.

ref: You can be certain that in the last days there will be some very hard times.
text: M koro ka a si ka, fa kɔ durunya n awieeri, i yó kekereke.

ref: People will love only themselves and money. They will be proud, stuck-up, rude, and disobedient to their parents. They will also be ungrateful, godless,
text: Minɛ yó anyii sam, na bu kóro ŋwaa agaya, na bu fɔ́, na bu súnsun ka bɛrɛ ŋgumi wo bɛrɛ a, na bu kpíɛ minɛ nzukɔ, na bu dé má bu wu­fɔm nɔaniɛ, na bu sí má aye, na bu kéte Nyɛmɛ woo­wa casi n jɔrɛ,

ref: heartless, and hateful. Their words will be cruel, and they will have no self-control or pity. These people will hate everything good.
text: na bu kóro má minɛm, na bu sí má aŋunfɔ, na bu sáaki minɛm duma, na bu lá má tara­buŋu, na bu yó wi, na bu kpɔ́ deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa.

ref: They will be sneaky, reckless, and puffed up with pride. Instead of loving God, they will love pleasure.
text: Na bu lá má dabuŋu, na bu yó sa bo bu sunsun má bu, na bu lá karambani agaya, na bu kóro bu tii di tara nyɛmɛsu,

ref: Even though they will make a show of being religious, their religion won't be real. Don't have anything to do with such people.
text: na bu yó bu ŋu ka bu su Nyɛmɛ atin n su wɔ, ama, na bu kéte Nyɛmɛ yiko ni. Má a dodo nyini minɛm sɔ.

ref: Some men fool whole families, just to get power over those women who are slaves of sin and are controlled by all sorts of desires.
text: Bu nbem nati wura awuru awurum nu wɔ, na bu bo nbaram bo bu la má tara­buŋu n anɔɔfiɛ na bu su bu su. Nyini nbaram sɔ satiɛ­yo akunma bu, na bu yo bu kunnu koroo­wam.

ref: These women always want to learn something new, but they never can discover the truth.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ bu ti keree­wam wɔ, ama, bu koro si má nahɔrɛ atin n fieo.

ref: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, these people are enemies of the truth. Their minds are sick, and their faith isn't real.
text: Kabo Janesi ni Jambaresi yo keteeri jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo Moyisi yo jɔjɔɔri ni, sɔ ye ahin kɔrɛ kere­fɔm n gusu akete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a. Bu sunsunni yo má juma, na ba kete yarada atin n su su ni.

ref: But they won't get very far with their foolishness. Soon everyone will know the truth about them, just as Jannes and Jambres were found out. Paul's Last Instructions to Timothy
text: Ama, bu juma koro kɔ má i nyunu, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bá si ka bu ti sinnzinm wɔ, bu si má pui. Kabo minɛ yo siiri ka Janesi, ni Jambaresi yo ti sinnzinm ni. Pɔɔlu awieeri su dɛɛ nɔaniɛ bo i fa maari Timoti ni

ref: Timothy, you know what I teach and how I live. You know what I want to do and what I believe. You have seen how patient and loving I am, and how in the past I put up with
text: Ama, wɔrɔ yiri, a ŋu m keree­wa, ni m woo­wa, ni m sunsunni bo m taraari i ni, ni m yarada ni, ni m sawɔrɛ ni, ni m koro, ni m mindi ni,

ref: In fact, anyone who belongs to Christ Jesus and wants to live right will have trouble from others.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i su Nyɛmɛ atin su na i ni Yesu Kristo yo kun, i mi ŋú wuwuruwa,

ref: But evil people who pretend to be what they are not will become worse than ever, as they fool others and are fooled themselves.
text: na minɛ tiɛm ni kere­fɔ kɔrɛ­fɔm yo satiɛ kɔ sin su, bu dadaka minɛ, na minɛ dadaka bɛrɛ gusu.

ref: Keep on being faithful to what you were taught and to what you believed. After all, you know who taught you these things.
text: Ama, wɔrɔ yiri, nahɔrɛ atin n bo bu fa kereeri u, na a yoori i yarada nahɔrɛ su ni, su i su sɔ, dama a si minɛm bo bu kereeri u nyini atin ni.

ref: Since childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures that are able to make you wise enough to have faith in Christ Jesus and be saved.
text: A si ka fite u kaan nu kɛrɛ, ye a sɔnsɔɔnri Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kɛrɛ n ye a siiri i a. Nyini koro ma u alecira a, na a yo Yesu Kristo yarada na a nya tii­de.

ref: The Scriptures train God's servants to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: Nyini n kɛrɛ koro ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su Nyɛmɛ kpama bi a, na i koro yo juma kpa barasu kɛrɛ.
