ref: When Christ Jesus comes as king, he will be the judge of everyone, whether they are living or dead. So with God and Christ as witnesses, I command you
text: M suma u nɔaniɛ lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo n nyunu, yiri bá di ŋgɔɔ­fɔm ni yiwee­fɔm n jɔrɛ a, na i bá di fɛmɛ na i niɛ i minɛm su.

ref: to preach God's message. Do it willingly, even if it isn't the popular thing to do. You must correct people and point out their sins. But also cheer them up, and when you instruct them, always be patient.
text: Boro u nyi kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, sukan i saŋga kɛrɛ. Teŋge yarada­yo­fɔm su fa kɔ keree­wa bo i kɔ atin su. Jaraki bu na bu yaki satiɛ­yo. Jɔjɔ bu dɔ na bu yo sakpa buka su. Na a yo sawɔrɛ ni nyini n kɛrɛ na a kere bu nyunyumi.

ref: The time is coming when people won't listen to good teaching. Instead, they will look for teachers who will please them by telling them only what they are itching to hear.
text: Dama fa kɔ fa ba, minɛm kóro má ka bu so bu sui, na bu ti Kristo keree­wa n bo i kɔ atin su ni. Ama, bu sú bu dɛɛ koroo­wam su wɔ, na bu tíɛn kere­fɔm dɔŋgu nɔɔ bo bu kere bu dekem bo bu koro ka bu ti i ni.

ref: They will turn from the truth and eagerly listen to senseless stories.
text: Bu káaki bu sin bo nahɔrɛ atin ni, na bu só bu sui ti kɔrɛ jɔrɛm.

ref: But you must stay calm and be willing to suffer. You must work hard, telling the good news and to do your job well.
text: Ama, wɔrɔ yiri, tara u ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, na a mindi ni wahara di, na a boro u nyi kan Yesu labari kpa n jɔrɛ, na a yo u juma n kɛrɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka a yo i ni.

ref: Now the time has come for me to die. My life is like a drink offering being poured out on the altar.
text: Mini yiri, saŋga n aju bo bu yí m ŋgɔɔ ni, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, saŋga n aju bo m fíte durunya n nu fɛn.

ref: I have fought well. I have finished the race, and I have been faithful.
text: Ma boro m nyi atu ŋgoni Nyɛmɛ atin n su, ma yo m juma awie, ma kan labari kpa n ni m yarada kun.

ref: So a crown will be given to me for pleasing the Lord. He judges fairly, and on the day of judgment he will give a crown to me and to everyone else who wants him to appear with power. Personal Instructions
text: Kisa Nyɛmɛ bita teremasu aceeri ŋminda m, dama wɔ de m ka m yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ɔ. Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo, yiri di minɛm jɔrɛ ye i kɔ atin su a, yiri fá nyini teremasu aceeri n ce m jina cɛɛn a, má mini ŋgumi, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro Yesu na i tana ŋminda ka i ba n a. Pɔɔlu ni i tii jɔrɛ lɛɛ

ref: Come to see me as soon as you can.
text: Yo aniɛ na a bra m dɔ kisa kisa.

ref: Demas loves the things of this world so much that he left me and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
text: Dama Keresensi akɔ Galatiya awɔrɛ n nu, ye Titosi akɔ Dalamatiya awɔrɛ n nu. Demasi aŋmati ayaki m, wɔ kɔ Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu, kisa i koro ka i wo kabo durunya n nu­fɔm wo n wɔ.

ref: Only Luke has stayed with me. Mark can be very helpful to me, so please find him and bring him with you.
text: Luka ŋgumi wo m dɔ a. Fa Marako na u ni yiri bra, dama i koro buka m ni juma ni.

ref: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
text: Ma sunma Tikikusi Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu.

ref: When you come, bring the coat I left at Troas with Carpus. Don't forget to bring the scrolls, especially the ones made of leather.
text: A ŋa a ba, bita m ayɛrɛ tanlɛɛ n ba ma m, bo m yakiiri i Tɔrasi miɛ kpiri n nu, Kapusi dika ni. Fa m kadasim n gusu bita m nu, maala bo ba fa neŋge­ndɔrɛ ŋuma ayo bu ni.

ref: Alexander, the metalworker, has hurt me in many ways. But the Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
text: Alɛkizanda bo i ti tunmi n ayo m satiɛ agaya, Mibiɛ ni tɔ́ i kaari fa kɔ sa bo wɔ yo i n su a.

ref: Alexander opposes what we preach, so you had better watch out for him.
text: Si u ŋu sa ni yiri, dama wɔ jina su kekereke akete nɔaniɛ n bo ya kan i ni.

ref: When I was first put on trial, no one helped me. In fact, everyone deserted me. I hope it won't be held against them.
text: Ciciri su bo m sukan na m de m tii jɔrɛ dii­biri ni, be ajina má m sin, bu kɛrɛ ŋmati yakiiri m wɔ. M sere Nyɛmɛ ka i fa bu ŋgasi ce bu.

ref: But the Lord stood beside me. He gave me the strength to tell his full message, so that all Gentiles would hear it. And I was kept safe from hungry lions.
text: Ama, na Mibiɛ n ajina m sin a, na wɔ ma m faŋga, na ma koro akan labari kpa n akere minɛm, na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ akoro ati. Na wɔ de m afite minɛm bo bu koro ka bu kun m n saa nu.

ref: The Lord will always keep me from being harmed by evil, and he will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. Praise him forever and ever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: Yiri Mibiɛ ni yí m fite jɔrɛ tiɛ kɛrɛ nu a, na i fá m kɔ wura i fɛmɛya n nu aŋgoro ni laifiɛ a. Jirima bambaka ti i dɛɛ saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami. Awieeri su dɛɛ ansɛ yoo­wa ni

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
text: Bisa Pirisila ni i hun Akuyila fa tu Onesifɔrɔsi dika­fɔm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma m.

ref: Erastus stayed at Corinth. Trophimus was sick when I left him at Miletus.
text: Irasitusi kaari Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ma yaki Torofimosi gusu Miletosi miɛ kpiri n nu, dama wɔ la má i ŋu.

ref: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, and Claudia send you their greetings, and so do the rest of the Lord's followers.
text: Yo aniɛ na a bra ka, na fufuku nya ju. Ya ninbaam bisa u dɔŋgu, bɛrɛ la Ɛyabulusi, ni Pudensi, ni Linosi, ni ya niɛma­bara Kalodiya, ni ya ninbaam nyiŋgam kɛrɛ.

ref: I pray that the Lord will bless your life and will be kind to you.
text: Ma Mibiɛ n wo ni wɔrɔ. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

ref: From Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. I encourage God's own people to have more faith and to understand the truth about religion.
text: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. M ti Nyɛmɛ akɔɔ ɔ, na m kaaki ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa. Nyɛmɛ ayi m na wɔ sunma m ka m kɔ buka minɛm bo wɔ yi bu n na bu yarada yo bii­wa, na bu si nahɔrɛ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin n su.

ref: Titus, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and Christ Jesus our Savior will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! What Titus Was To Do in Crete
text: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma wɔrɔ Titosi a. A ti m wa a, nahɔrɛ su, dama m ni wɔrɔ kɛrɛ asaŋga ayo Yesu Kristo yarada. Ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ Yesu Kristo buka u, na bu ma u ahoreda. Juma bo Pɔɔlu ŋa Titosi yo i Kereti asiɛn n su ni

ref: I left you in Crete to do what had been left undone and to appoint leaders for the churches in each town. As I told you,
text: Deke bo i ti na m yakiiri u Kereti asiɛn n su n la ka, m koro ka a yo juma n bo wɔ wie má n wie, na a sɛsɛ i su, na a yi Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm se miɛm kɛrɛ su, kabo m seeri u ni.

ref: Church officials are in charge of God's work, and so they must also have a good reputation. They must not be bossy, quick-tempered, heavy drinkers, bullies, or dishonest in business.
text: Nyɛmɛ afa i juma n awura nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm saa nu n dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka, má bu la ŋgasi be. I ti má ye ni ka bu ti aŋukere­fɔm, wara bu fa yaa ndende, wara bu nu nzan agaya, wara bu ti aluwa koro­fɔm, wara bu koro ŋwaa agaya.

ref: There are many who don't respect authority, and they fool others by talking nonsense. This is especially true of some Jewish followers.
text: Dama minɛm dɔŋgu wo bɛrɛ bo ba kete keree­wa bo i ti kpa ni, na bu sujɔjɔ jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn na bu sudadaka minɛm, maala yarada­yo­fɔm bo bu toko bita Yuda­fɔm sa­yoo­wa ni.

ref: But you must make them be quiet. They are after money, and they upset whole families by teaching what they should not.
text: Ma bu mɔ bu nɔɔ, dama bu sufa nyini keree­wa n bo i ti má ye ni ka bu fa kere ni, sufa saaki awuru­kpa lɛɛ. Ŋwaa dɛɛ ti ye bu yo sɔ a.

ref: It is like one of their own prophets once said, “The people of Crete always tell lies. They are greedy and lazy like wild animals.”
text: Haari bɛrɛ Kereti­fɔm mmɔɔ dɛɛ tiɛŋgu si­fɔ be yo seeri ka, “Bɛrɛ Kereti­fɔm ti kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ dada kɛrɛ, na bu ti ka boro neŋge tiɛm, na bu ti nviɛya­fɔm, ni ayaa kpiri­fɔm wɔ.”

ref: This surely is a true saying. And you should be hard on such people, so you can help them grow stronger in their faith.
text: Kabo i yo seeri n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, jaraki bu, na bu yarada yo faŋga,

ref: Don't pay any attention to any of those senseless Jewish stories and human commands. These are made up by people who won't obey the truth.
text: na má bu su Yuda­fɔm nana jɔrɛm su, na má bu su deke bo minɛm bo ba kete nahɔrɛ n akpama i n su.

ref: Such people claim to know God, but their actions prove they really don't. They are disgusting. They won't obey God, and they are too worthless to do anything good.
text: Bu ŋa bu si Nyɛmɛ, ama, bu sa­yoo­wa kere ka bu si má i, dama bu sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ ti ciire wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na bu de má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na bu koro yo má sakpa be.

ref: Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
text: Se nbiɛsɔ anyunuti­fɔm ka, bu bita bu ŋu, na bu yo sa bo i la anyunuyi, na bu la tara­buŋu. Kaaki se bu ka, bu ma bu yarada yo faŋga, na bu koro minɛm nahɔrɛ su, na bu la mindi nahɔrɛ su.

ref: Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper,
text: Se nbara anyunuti­fɔm gusu ka, bu ma bu woo­wa kere ka bu soro Nyɛmɛ. Nyini la ka, má bu susaaki minɛ duma, na má bu ma nzan ja bu. Kaaki se bu ka, bu kere keree­wa kpa,

ref: so the younger women will be loving wives and mothers.
text: na bu tu tarawam fɔ ka, bu koro bu hunm ni bu nbaam,

ref: Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
text: na bu la tara­buŋu, na bu ma bu woo­wa ti casi, na bu bita bu awurum nyunyumi, na bu yo sakpa, na bu de bu hunm nɔaniɛ, adena má be koro bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
text: Tu ngbɛfɛnɛm gusu fɔ ka, bu tara bu ŋu,

ref: Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
text: keree­wa kpa, na be koro kan má jɔrɛ be. Kere bu sɔ, adena minɛm bo bu kete ya keree­wa n di nyinsɛ, dama bu ŋu má ŋgasi be bo bu fa saaki ya.

ref: Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners
text: Se akɔɔm gusu ka, bu de bu mibiɛm nɔaniɛ, na bu yo sa kɛrɛ bo bu mibiɛm koro, na má bu se jɔrɛ be.

ref: or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior. God's Kindness and the New Life
text: Kaaki se bu ka, má bu wɔ awie mumuku, ama, bu kere ka bu la dabuŋu deke kɛrɛ nu. Nzɛn bu yoori sɔ, bu woo­wa kpa ni kere minɛm ka, Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, i dɛɛ keree­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: God has shown us undeserved grace by coming to save all people.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ ayi i ahɔrɔma afite fofoe nu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nya tii­de.

ref: He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
text: Yiri wu maari ya, adena i ma ya nya ya tii, na i yi ya fite ya satiɛ nu, na i kpakpara yi ya fin kɛrɛ, na i yo ya casi, na ya yo i dɛɛ minɛm bo bu nyunu aboro sakpa­yo su.

ref: Teach these things, as you use your full authority to encourage and correct people. Make sure you earn everyone's respect.
text: Ahin keree­wam kɛrɛ, fa kere yarada­yo­fɔm, na a ma bu ahoresi, na a kan bu ndiɛ. Fa yiko n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama u n yo sɔ, na má a ma be bu u ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful
text: Teŋge yarada­yo­fɔm su ka, bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ ma nfɛmɛm ni gomina nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, na bu de bu nɔaniɛ, na bu kpa bu ŋu na bu yo sa kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa,

ref: and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone.
text: na má bu saaki minɛm duma, na má bu se akuruwa ni minɛm, ama, bu yo bu ŋu kaan, na bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

ref: God our Savior showed us how good and kind he is.
text: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, Nyɛmɛ bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, yo faari i sakpa, ni i koro kereeri ya,

ref: He saved us because of his mercy, and not because of any good things we have done. God washed us by the power of the Holy Spirit. He gave us new birth and a fresh beginning.
text: na wɔ nya ade ya tii aji. Má ka ya sakpa yoo­wa dɛɛ ti, ye i deeri ya tii jiiri a, ama, i aŋunfɔ si dɛɛ ti. Kabo i deeri ya tii n la ka, i Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ŋmisiiri ya a, na wɔ ma ya wo fɔfɔrɛ, ni ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ.

ref: God sent Jesus Christ our Savior to give us his Spirit.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ ama ya Tii­de­fɔ Yesu Kristo afa Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya akɔ asin su.

ref: This message is certainly true. These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds.
text: Ahin jɔrɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. M koro ka a kere ahin dekem ni anyum­boro, adena bo bu yo Nyɛmɛ yarada ni, bu fa bu sunsunni kɛrɛ yo sa bo i ti kpa. Ahin keree­wam n la kpɛrɛ, na bu kaaki ti kpa ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

ref: But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
text: Ama, má a fa u ŋu wura akuruwa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn barasu kɛrɛ nu, ni ya nana Yuda­fɔm dumam, ni aluwa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su. Ahinm kɛrɛ ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, bu kunma kpɛrɛ be.

ref: You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are. Personal Instructions and Greetings
text: Si ka nyini sɔnɔ n sɔ akete keree­wa kpa n lɛɛ, na i yo satiɛ bo yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii si ka i ti satiɛ wɔ. Pɔɔlu dɛɛ awieeri su nɔaniɛ bo i fa maari Titosi ni

ref: I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
text: M súnma Aratɛma, wara Tikikusi u dɔ, nzɛn bu be baari, boro u nyi na m ni wɔrɔ sa nu Nikopoli miɛ kpiri n nu. M koro ka m wo dɔ fufuku saŋga a.

ref: When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
text: Zena bo i ti jɔrɛ­di­fɔ ni, i ni Apolosi, bu ba ŋa bu yasu, boro u nyi na a buka bu, na má deke be kpiɛ bu su.

ref: Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: M labari n kɛrɛ lɛɛ. Minɛm bo bu wo m dɔ n kɛrɛ bisa u. Bisa ya danfom kɛrɛ bo bu yo Yesu yarada ni. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

ref: From Paul, who is in jail for serving Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is like a brother because of our faith. Philemon, you work with us and are very dear to us. This letter is to you
text: Mini Pɔɔlu bo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti na kisa m wo dan­saraka nu ni, ni ya niwa Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. Wɔrɔ Filemon bo ya koro u na ya ni wɔrɔ saŋga yo juma ni, ya bisa u dɔŋgu.

ref: and to the church that meets in your home. It is also to our dear friend Apphia and to Archippus, who serves the Lord as we do.
text: Ya kaaki bisa Kristo jama n bo bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ u dika ni, ni ya niwa baa­bara Afiya, ni ya niwa Akipusi bo ya ni yiri kɛrɛ ti ka soja ni, Yesu dɛɛ atin n su ni.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Philemon's Love and Faith
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am ahoreda. Filemon koro ni i yarada n jɔrɛ

ref: Philemon, each time I mention you in my prayers, I thank God.
text: M niwa Filemon, saŋga kɛrɛ bo m sere Nyɛmɛ, m wara fi má u sa, m da i asi ma u wɔ.

ref: I hear about your faith in our Lord Jesus and about your love for all God's people.
text: Dama ma ti kabo a yo ya Mibiɛ Yesu yarada ni, ni kabo a koro Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ ni.

ref: As you share your faith with others, I pray they may come to know all the blessings Christ has given us.
text: M kaaki sere Nyɛmɛ ka i ma wɔrɔ ni bo ba yo yarada na am saŋga su Nyɛmɛ ni la wo fiɛ, nyini ma a si nahɔrɛ su kabo Nyɛmɛ afa deke kpa ama ya, Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: My friend, your love has made me happy and has greatly encouraged me. It has also cheered the hearts of God's people. Paul Speaks to Philemon about Onesimus
text: M niwa, koro n bo a bita i ni, wɔ ma m ahore ajɔ agaya, na wɔ kaaki ama m ahoresi, dama wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ minɛm anya ahoresi. Kabo Pɔɔlu taraari Filemon jaa maari Onisimɔ barasu ni

ref: But I would rather ask you to do it simply because of love. Yes, as someone in jail for Christ,
text: Ama, m koro u n dɛɛ ti, ye m susere u a, ka a buka m, haari ni bo m jina Yesu Kristo dɛɛ jaa nu n ni, na i dɛɛ ti na m kaaki ti dan­saraka kisa ni.

ref: I beg you to help Onesimus! He is like a son to me because I led him to Christ here in jail.
text: Deke bo i ti na m susere u n la ka, m susere u ma m wa Onisimɔ n wɔ, bo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti na wɔ kaaki m wa dan­saraka nu n ni.

ref: Before this, he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
text: Saŋga be, i yo kun má kpɛrɛ be ma má u, ama, kisa, i kun kpɛrɛ ma wɔrɔ ni mini kɛrɛ wɔ.

ref: I would like to keep him here with me, where he could take your place in helping me while I am here in prison for preaching the good news.
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kaan­wa n dɛɛ ti na m wo dan­saraka n nu ni, má m koro ka i wo m dɔ fɛn na i buka m, kabo má wɔrɔ wo fɛn na a buka m ni.

ref: But I won't do anything unless you agree to it first. I want your act of kindness to come from your heart, and not be something you feel forced to do.
text: Ama, m koro má ka m yo deke be bo a soro má su, ama, m koro ka wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii buka m u koroya­nu, má ka i yo ka m miɛ u wɔ.

ref: Perhaps Onesimus was taken from you for a little while so you could have him back for good,
text: Beni, Onisimɔ fiteeri u saa nu ni, wɔ cɛ kaan, adena i ba kaaki ba wo u dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: but not as a slave. Onesimus is much more than a slave. To me he is a dear friend, but to you he is even more, both as a person and as a follower of the Lord.
text: Kisa, i ti má u akɔɔ ŋgumi kun, ama, i tara akɔɔ ɔ. I ti ya niwa koroo­wa a. Koro bo m fa koro i ni, wɔrɔ koro i tara sɔ. I ti u akɔɔ ɔ, ama, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, i kaaki ti u niwa a.

ref: If you consider me a friend because of Christ, then welcome Onesimus as you would welcome me.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn a fa i ka m ti u mata­fɔ, de i kabo má a de m ni.

ref: If he has cheated you or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
text: Nzɛn wɔ do u ŋgasi be, wara i bita u kaari, ma nyini kaari n yo m dɛɛ kaari.

ref: My dear friend and follower of Christ our Lord, please cheer me up by doing this for me.
text: Nyini ti, m niwa, m koro ka a yo ahin sakpa n ma m, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti. Kabo ya kɛrɛ ti nibaa Kristo dɛɛ ti ni, da m ahore.

ref: Epaphras is also here in jail for being a follower of Christ Jesus. He sends his greetings,
text: M lamɔkɔ Ipafarasi bo m ni yiri wo dan­saraka nu, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti ni, i ŋa m bisa u dɔŋgu.

ref: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, who work together with me.
text: Marako, ni Arisitakusi, ni Demasi, ni Luka kɛrɛ gusu ŋa m bisa u dɔŋgu, m ni bɛrɛ saŋga yo juma.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
text: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am kɛrɛ.

ref: Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors.
text: Dawa saŋga Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ, saŋga barasu ni atin barasu barasum, na bu sukan i kere ya nam.

ref: But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son.
text: Ama, ahin awieeri su saŋga n bo ya wo nu ni, Nyɛmɛ afa i Wa n su ye i fa i nɔaniɛ n jɔjɔ ya sɔ a. Nyɛmɛ maari yiri yiiri durunya n a. Na wɔ se ka, awieeri su, deke n kɛrɛ bá yo i dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together. After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven.
text: I jirima woo­wa wein ni kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ti a. Kabo Nyɛmɛ ti ni, i woo­wa ti sɔ ɔ. I nɔaniɛ n bo i la yiko ni, i fa nyini bita durunya n kɛrɛ a. I faari i ŋu fa yoori yoo­wa, adena Nyɛmɛ fa adamande satiɛ ce i. Nyini dɛɛ sin, na wɔ nya akɔ atana aŋgoro, Micɛra Kpiri n saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

ref: He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs. God's Son Is Greater than Angels
text: Nyɛmɛ maari i jinaa­biri bambaka bo i tara i mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ, na wɔ kaaki ama i duma bo i tara bu dɛɛ ni. Kabo Nyɛmɛ Wa n tara i mɛrɛkɛm barasu ni

ref: God has never said to any of the angels, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!” Neither has God said to any of them, “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son!”
text: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ seeri i Wa n ni, ka, “Wɔrɔ ti m Wa a, nyuma, m káaki u Si,” wɔ se má i mɛrɛkɛ n be sɔ. Bekun, i ŋa “M yó u Si, na u gusu yó m Wa.”

ref: When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
text: Sasu bekun, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yo ŋa i sunma i Wa kun n kpein n durunya n nu ni, na wɔ se ka, “Ma Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ su i.”

ref: And when God speaks about the angels, he says, “I change my angels into wind and my servants into flaming fire.”
text: Fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ jɔjɔɔri i mɛrɛkɛm jɔrɛ ni, i ŋa, “Mini, m ma m mɛrɛkɛm yo bu juma kabo aŋuma, ni sin yo bu juma barasu n wɔ.”

ref: But God says about his Son, “You are God, and you will rule as King forever! Your royal power brings about justice.
text: Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ jɔjɔɔri i Wa n dɔ ni, i ŋa, “Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ a, u fɛmɛya biɛ n la má awieeri. A bita u fɛmɛya n ni nahɔrɛ atin su wɔ.

ref: You loved justice and hated evil, and so I, your God, have chosen you. I appointed you and made you happier than any of your friends.”
text: A koro sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, na a koro má sa bo i kɔ má atin su a. Nyini dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ayi u a, ye wɔ ma u ahoreda bo i tara u beŋgum kɛrɛ n a.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “In the beginning, Lord, you were the one who laid the foundation of the earth and created the heavens.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase i Wa n ka, “Ya Mibiɛ, bumboori ni, wɔrɔ yiiri asiɛŋgu a. U sa bo a yoori i dɛɛ ti, ye aŋgoro wo bɛrɛ a.

ref: They will all disappear and wear out like clothes, but you will last forever.
text: I kɛrɛ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, ama, wɔrɔ wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ. Aŋgoro n ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ yó dawa kabo sutura yo dawa barasu n wɔ.

ref: You will roll them up like a robe and change them like a garment. But you are always the same, and you will live forever.”
text: A pɔ́ɔndi bu ka sanvuin bo wɔ do wɔ, na a bá kaaki i fɔfɔrɛ. Ama, wɔrɔ kaaki má fieo, na u afɔ la má awieeri.”

ref: God never said to any of the angels, “Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you!”
text: Na Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase i Wa n ka, “Ba tana m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su fɛn, haari m bá ma a kunma u kpɔ­fɔm na a nana bu kɛrɛ ŋu.” Kabo i seeri sɔ ni, wɔ nya ase má i mɛrɛkɛ n be sɔ.

ref: We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka ya bita Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo ya du mɔ ati i nyunyumi, adena má ya mini.

ref: So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true.
text: Nzɛn yɛrɛ gusu keteeri nyini nɔaniɛ kpiri n bo i kere kabo Nyɛmɛ de ya tii ji ni, ya yo sɛ koro fite nu na ya nya má sui­cin a? Ahin tii­de n bo ya kan i jɔrɛ ni, Mibiɛ n ni i tii du mɔ kan kereeri minɛm a, na bo bu tiiri i n adi i daani, ase ya ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to. The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
text: Nyini saŋga n na Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ adi i daani abuka su, yiri la bo i yoori alekutura sa barasu barasum kereeri i yiko, na i maari minɛm nyaari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim kabo i koro barasu ni. Yesu ti adamandem gɔrɔ ɔ

ref: We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels.
text: Nahɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ afa má durunya fɔfɔrɛ n bo ya kan i jɔrɛ, na i suba ni, awura má i mɛrɛkɛm saa nu.

ref: Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God, “What makes you care about us humans? Why are you concerned for weaklings such as we?
text: Ama, be akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n awɔrɛ be nu, ka, “Ya Micɛra Nyɛmɛ, adamande gusu ti nzu wɔ, ye u cɛcɛ wo i nu a? Adamande wa ti nzu wɔ, ye a niɛ i dika?

ref: You made us lower than the angels for a while. Yet you have crowned us with glory and honor.
text: Kabo a yoori i ni, a ma i jinaa­biri kpiɛɛri su saŋga kaan afiɛn ni u mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ, na a nya awura i kpiri na a ma i jirima bambaka,

ref: And you have put everything under our power!” God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control.
text: na a fa deke kɛrɛ awura i saa nu.” Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu kabo Nyɛmɛ afa deke n kɛrɛ awura adamande saa nu ka i niɛ i dika ni, nyini kere ka deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i ti má i dɛɛ. Ama, kisa, kabo i ti ni, ya nya ŋu má i sɔ.

ref: What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
text: Ama, ya ŋu Yesu kabo Nyɛmɛ yoori i ni, i ma i jinaa­biri kpiɛɛri su saŋga kaan afiɛn ni i mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ wɔ. Na i dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n nu na wɔ wu sɔnɔ kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti. Kisa, wahara n bo i diiri i na i wu n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ awura i kpiri, na wɔ ma i jirima bambaka.

ref: Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory.
text: Nyɛmɛ yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, yiri la deke n kɛrɛ yiko a. I tiiri ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ kpini atin bo i minɛ beberebe ba nya i jirima woo­wa n be. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i ma Yesu ba diiri wahara n a, adena i yo minɛm mɔ­fɔ bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, dama yiri ma bu nya tii­de a.

ref: Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters.
text: Yiri Yesu bo i yo minɛm casi ni, i ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo bu casi n saŋga Si wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, i la má i nyinsɛ na i fere bu ka i ninbaam n a.

ref: He even said to God, “I will tell them your name and sing your praises when they come together to worship.”
text: Na wɔ se Nyɛmɛ ka, “M bó u duma kere m ninbaam. Na saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ, m dí jue kansi u duma.”

ref: He also said, “I will trust God.” Then he said, “Here I am with the children God has given me.”
text: Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, “Mini, m daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu,” Na wɔ kaaki ase bekun ka, “Mini ni nbaam bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ni, yɛrɛ yaa.”

ref: We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death.
text: Kabo nbaam n ti adamande ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye yiri mmɔɔ kaakiiri adamande a, adena i wu na i yo sɔnɔ bo i la yiwee yiko ni ŋgbɛɛn, yiri la Sitana a,

ref: But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying.
text: na i de minɛm tii ji, bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu ŋgɔɔ su na bu soro yiwee n dɛɛ ti, na ba kaaki akɔɔm ni.

ref: Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants.
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, má mɛrɛkɛm ye i buka deeri bu tii a, ama, Abraham busu nu­fɔm ye i buka deeri bu tii a.

ref: He had to be one of us, so he could serve God as our merciful and faithful high priest and sacrifice himself for the forgiveness of our sins.
text: Nyini kere ka, wɔ yo daka ka i woo­wa ti ka i ninbaam i deke n kɛrɛ nu wɔ, adena i yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn bo i la dabuŋu na i si aŋunfɔ fa kɔ i nyɛmɛsu n nu, na i fa i ŋu yo yoo­wa ma minɛm na Nyɛmɛ fa bu satiɛ ce bu.

ref: And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.
text: Yiri ni i tii yo diiri wahara, na ba sɔ i ŋgɛɛ na bu niɛ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i koro buka minɛm bo bu sɔ bu ŋgɛɛ na bu niɛ n a.

ref: My friends, God has chosen you to be his holy people. So think about Jesus, the one we call our apostle and high priest!
text: M ninbaam, ambɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi am ni yɛrɛ ka mata­fɔ casim ni, am fa am sunsunni se Yesu ŋu, yiri bo Nyɛmɛ asunma i na i ti ya sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n ni. Ya su yiri su a, ye ya di i daani kere minɛm a.

ref: But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house.
text: Kabo sɔnɔ bo i ta awuru n la jirima tara awuru n ni i tii ni, sɔ ye Yesu la jirima tara Moyisi a.

ref: Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
text: Awuru kɛrɛ la i ta­fɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ ti deke n kɛrɛ tii a.

ref: But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope. A Rest for God's People
text: Ama, Kristo yiri ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a, yiri la dabuŋu Nyɛmɛ awuru n nu koro bita Nyɛmɛ minɛm a. Nzɛn ya bita ya tama n ni ahoresi, yɛrɛ ti i awuru n nu­fɔm a. Nyɛmɛ minɛm ŋumi­yi n dɛɛ jɔrɛ

ref: It is just as the Holy Spirit says, “If you hear God's voice today,
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n se ni, “Nyuma, nzɛn am tiiri kabo Nyɛmɛ suse,

ref: “Then God got tired of them and said, ‘You people never show good sense, and you don't understand what I want you to do.’
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye m faari yaa bu ŋu a, ye m seeri ka, ba mini atin n a, dama ba yo má m sa koroo­wam ni.

ref: God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ”
text: Na yaa ati m, na ma tan ndiɛ ka, bu ny má atin wura má asiɛn n bo má m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu ni.’”

ref: My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God.
text: M ninbaam, am si am ŋu sa na má am be ahore­mbaa yo kekereke, na i kaaki i sin bo Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ atin ni.

ref: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
text: Cɛɛn kɛrɛ bo ale acin na bu fere i ka, “Nyuma,” am sutu am beŋgu fɔ, na má am ma be dadaka am na am yo satiɛ na am sui yo tan­tanantan.

ref: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
text: Nzɛn ya koro bitaari bumboori su nɔaniɛ n kenken haari kɔ juuri i awieeri, nyini n kere ka ya ni Kristo saŋga yo juma kun wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn like those who rebelled.”
text: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, “Nyuma, nzɛn am tiiri kabo Nyɛmɛ suse, má am yo am sui tan­tanantan, kabo am nam yo yoori bu sui tan­tanantan.”

ref: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
text: Ŋmam yo tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ye bu yoori bu sui tan­tanantan n a? Má minɛm kɛrɛ bo Moyisi yo yiiri bu fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
text: Ŋmam ye Nyɛmɛ yo taanri ndiɛ ka, “Bu nya má atin wura má asiɛn n bo má m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu n a?” Má bɛrɛ n bo bu yo keteeri i nɔaniɛ ni, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
text: Deke bo i ti na ya ŋu ka ba wura má asiɛn n su na bu nya ŋumi­yi n la ka, ba yo má i yarada.

ref: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
text: Nyini ti, am ma ya si ya ŋu sa, na má be fun nyini ŋumi­yi n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma ya na atin toko wo bɛrɛ ka ya nya i ni.

ref: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
text: Dama yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ati labari kpa n kabo bɛrɛ mmɔɔ yo tiiri i ni, ama, bɛrɛ ayo má i yarada, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ kun má kpɛrɛ ama má bu n a.

ref: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
text: Ama, yɛrɛ bo ya yo Yesu yarada ni, ya nya nyini ŋumi­yi n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni. I ti kabo Nyɛmɛ seeri n wɔ, i ŋa, “Na yaa ati m, na ma tan ndiɛ ka, bu nyá má atin wura má asiɛn n bo má m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu ni.” Fite saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya n kɛrɛ wieeri ni, i juma awieeri lɛɛ.

ref: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ le­so cɛɛn n jɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n awɔrɛ be nu, ka, “Nyɛmɛ yo yiiri i ŋumi, i juma­yoo­wa kɛrɛ nu wɔ le­so cɛɛn ni.”

ref: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
text: Ya kaaki aŋu ka wɔ jɔjɔ nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ka, bu nya má atin wura má asiɛn n bo m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu.

ref: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
text: Bo bu du mɔ tiiri labari kpa n ni, ba wura má nyini ŋumi­yi n nu, dama ba yo má Nyɛmɛ yarada. Ama, atin toko wo bɛrɛ bo nbem nya nyini ŋumi­yi ni.

ref: If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest.
text: Nzɛn má Yosuwa koro maari Yisareli­fɔm nyaari atin wuraari asiɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu, má Nyɛmɛ ayo akaaki ase má ka cɛɛn n be wo bɛrɛ bo i ma i minɛm nya ŋumi­yi kun.

ref: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
text: Ama, kabo i ti ni, ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wo bɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa ma i minɛm.

ref: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nya ŋumi­yi Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i mi yi i ŋumi fa kɔ i juma su, kabo Nyɛmɛ yo yoori i juma wieeri na i yiiri i ŋumi n wɔ.

ref: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma ya boro ya nyi, na ya nya ŋumi­yi n bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa ma ya ni. Ama, má am ma ya be kete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, adena má ya fun ŋumi­yi ni, kabo Yisareli­fɔm nbem yo keteeri na bu fuunri i ni.

ref: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n la ŋgɔɔ na i la faŋga. I kaaki wo na tara takobe, i kpiɛ wura sɔnɔ ahore­mbaa su wɔ, ni i ŋumi, ni i awiɛwiɛ nu, na i kpiɛ kpaaki buwe sasuu­biri, na i kɔ kpura bɔrɔkɔ nu, na i fiiti sɔnɔ sunsunni nu, na i si deke bo i ahore­mbaa koro, deke bo i ti kpa, ni bo i ti má kpa.

ref: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
text: Deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fiɛ Nyɛmɛ, dama deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i na i nyu­mbaa ŋu má i. Yiri ye ya kɛrɛ bá kan ya sa­yoo­wa kere i a. Yesu ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn n a

ref: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
text: Kisa, na ya la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn bo i la má aŋusu, na wɔ kɔ aŋgoro, na wɔ wura Nyɛmɛ dika n nu ni, yiri la Nyɛmɛ Wa Yesu, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye am ma ya bita i dɛɛ atin n bo ya ŋa ya yo i yarada n kenken.

ref: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma ya fa ahoresi, na ya cin dodo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya tanaa­biri ni, yiri ti ahɔrɔma­fɔ a, adena i si ya aŋunfɔ, na saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya ba koro buka, na i buka ya.

ref: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
text: Yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii koro si minɛm aŋunfɔ bo ba si má na ba mini atin ayo satiɛ ni, dama yiri mmɔɔ faŋga akpiɛ su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
text: Be koro wura má i ŋu kpiri na i se ka i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn, má ka Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ayi i mi, kabo i yo yiiri Aarɔn ni.

ref: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, Kristo gusu awura má i ŋu kpiri ayo má i ŋu Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn, se Nyɛmɛ yiiri i a, na wɔ se i ka, “Wɔrɔ ti m Wa a, nyuma, m káaki u Si.”

ref: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
text: Saŋga bo Yesu wo durunya n nu fɛn ni, i yo sereeri Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro su, na wɔ bo afere Nyɛmɛ na wɔ sun, ka, i de i fite yiwee n nu. I yo yoori i ŋu kaan, na i soro Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ade i sere.

ref: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
text: Haari ni bo i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, Nyɛmɛ maari i diiri wahara, adena i si kabo i deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

ref: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ yiiri i ka i yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn, kabo Mɛlɛkisidɛki yo ti barasu n a. Fɔ­tu fa kɔ kabo má ya fa ya yarada n ji ni

ref: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
text: Má ya la deke dɔŋgu bo ya kan kere am fa kɔ jɔrɛ bo ya jɔjɔ i n su, ama, i ti kekereke kabo ya kan teke am nyi, dama am koro ti má i bu ndende.

ref: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
text: Saŋga bo am boori i bu ka am su Nyɛmɛ ni, am acɛ nu, má am aju bo am sukere minɛm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, ama, am toko tana koro ka bu kere am dekem bo ba du mɔ afa akere am, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n su. Am toko nu nyufini wɔ, am nya koro di má diire nɔnɔɔri.

ref: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nu nyufini toko ti baa­kan wɔ lɛɛ, i nya koro si má deke bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
text: Nyini ti, má am ma ya ka Kristo ciciri su nɔaniɛ keree­wam ŋgumi su, ama, am ma ya boro ya nyi na ya kɔ mɔ. I ti má ye ni ka ya sukan nyini jɔrɛm ni saŋga kɛrɛ. Nyini jɔrɛm ni la kaaki bu wo, fa kɔ bu satiɛ su na bu yo Nyɛmɛ yarada ni,

ref: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
text: ni kabo bu biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue barasu, na bu fa bu sa nana yarada­yo­fɔm ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ yo bu kisi, ni kabo minɛ ba wu na bu teŋge bu yiwee nu ni, ni kabo Nyɛmɛ ba di minɛ jɔrɛ na be koro kaaki má i kun ni.

ref: Let's grow up, if God is willing.
text: Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ soroori su, ya koro kɔ mɔ bo i tara sɔ.

ref: But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
text: Ama, na ba kaaki bu sin abo i, i ti kekereke bo nyini minɛm ŋusu koro kaaki ba i dɔ bekun, dama bu sa­yoo­wa kere ka bu sukaaki kpama Nyɛmɛ Wa n baka jaanu n su bekun wɔ lɛɛ, na bu suwura i nyinsɛ minɛm nyunu.

ref: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
text: Asiɛn kɛrɛ bo nzue tɔ saŋga kɛrɛ na minɛ dɔ su, na i yo diire nyunyumi, Nyɛmɛ wura nyini asiɛn n abaraka beberebe.

ref: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
text: Ama, asiɛn bo buwe ni wura ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm fi su na i kunma kpɛrɛ, i cɛ má, Nyɛmɛ yó i ŋgbɛɛn, na i awieeri su, sin bá yara i kɛrɛ.

ref: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
text: Ama, m ninbaam, haari ni bo ya kan sɔ n kɛrɛ ni, ya la ahoresi fa kɔ ambɛrɛ dɔ, dama am bita atin kpa n wɔ, yiri la tii­de n bo am anya i ni.

ref: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
text: Nyɛmɛ ti kpa, i wara fi má sakpa n bo am yo i ni, kabo am abuka i minɛm, na am toko buka bu ni. Nyini kere ka am koro i a.

ref: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
text: Ya koro ka am kɛrɛ boro am nyi haari kɔ ju awieeri, adena deke n bo am la i tama n yo yii­wa.

ref: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
text: Má am ma nviɛya ti am, ama, minɛm bo bu yo yarada na bu la sawɔrɛ, na bu nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma bu ni, am su bu dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam su. Deke bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó i ni, i kaaki má

ref: No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham,
text: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ seeri Abraham ka i bá yo i kisi ni, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i tara yiri Nyɛmɛ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i faari i duma taanri ndiɛ maari i a.

ref: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
text: Na wɔ se i ka, “M yó u kisi, na u busu nu­fɔm bá sunnu beberebe.”

ref: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
text: Na Abraham amindi haari na wɔ ba anya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i má i ni.

ref: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
text: Nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋa i tan ndiɛ, i tan deke bo i tara i mi n wɔ. Deke bo akuruwa nya yoori na bu taanri ndiɛ, wɔ kun akuruwa n kɛrɛ nɔɔ lɛɛ.

ref: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
text: Nyɛmɛ yo koroori ka i kere i minɛm ka deke bo i bá ma bu ni, i wɔrɔ má i nɔɔ i busu, nyini ti ye i kaaki taanri ndiɛ n a.

ref: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
text: Deke bo i seeri ka i yó i ni, ni i ndiɛ taan­wa n ti deke nnyɔ lɛɛ, bo bu kaaki má. Nyini kere ka Nyɛmɛ koro bu má kɔrɛ a. Nyini ti ye yɛrɛ bo ya ba i dɔ ka ya nya tii­de n la ahoresi bita ya tama n bo ya la i ni anyum­boro n a.

ref: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
text: Dɔ ye Yesu adu ya mɔ awura, ade ya dɛɛ jinaa­biri a. Yiri ti ya Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n bo i la má de­fɔ fieo, kabo Mɛlɛkisidɛki busu nu n ti n a.

ref: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
text: Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni, yiri yo ti miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Salem dɛɛ fɛmɛ a, na i kaaki ti Micɛra Kpiri sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ. Saŋga bo Abraham kɔ kuunri luɛ ni nfɛmɛ nna nbem na i kunmaari bu na i sukaaki kɔ awuru ni, yiri Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni kpaari Abraham atin ye i yoori kisi gɔɔri i ŋu n a.

ref: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
text: Na Abraham ayi dekem kɛrɛ bo i kun luɛ n na i deeri i ni, deke buru kɛrɛ nu, na wɔ yi i kun kun ama Mɛlɛkisidɛki. I duma Mɛlɛkisidɛki n bu la ka, fɛmɛ bo i sa­yoo­wa kɔ atin su. I kaaki ti Salem fɛmɛ wɔ, i bu la ka, fɛmɛ bo i fa tana­laifiɛ ba.

ref: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
text: Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni, be si má i si, wara i ni, wara i busu nu­fɔm, wara kabo bu wuuri i, wara kabo i wuuri. I yo ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo wɔ la má de­fɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ Wa n ti n wɔ.

ref: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kun bo i wo i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu n kere ka, deke buru kɛrɛ nu, bu yi kun kun fa ma Levi­fɔm, bu ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n dɛɛ ti, haari ni bo bu kɛrɛ ti minɛ kunm na bu ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm ni.

ref: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
text: Mɛlɛkisidɛki yo fite má Levi­fɔm dɛɛ busu n nu, ama, Abraham yo yiiri deke n kɛrɛ buru nu kun kun maari i wɔ, na wɔ yo kisi agɔ Abraham ŋu, yiri Abraham ye Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i bá yo i kisi n a.

ref: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka, sɔnɔ bo i yo kisi gɔ minɛm ŋu n tara bo bu de kisi ni.

ref: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
text: Nyini ti, saŋga bo Abraham yiiri deke n kɛrɛ nu buru kun kun maari Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni, ya koro se ka i wa Levi bo i ni i busu nu­fɔ yo deeri nyini n sɔ ni, bu minɛm gusu mmɔɔ atɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, i yo wo Levi­fɔm bo bu ti i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n saa nu a. Ama, nzɛn má Levi busu nu­fɔm dɛɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm dɛɛ juma n koro yoori deke n kɛrɛ bo má i ti ye ni ka bu yo i ni, má Nyɛmɛ ayi má i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo i ti ka Mɛlɛkisidɛki. Má i kaaki yi i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo i fite Aarɔn dɛɛ Levi­fɔm busu n nu ba.

ref: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ akaaki anɔ fɔfɔrɛ fa kɔ i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm dɛɛ atin n su, wɔ yo daka ka i kaaki i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n gusu.

ref: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
text: Kabo bu kɛrɛɛri Mibiɛ n ŋusu jɔrɛ ni, i fite anɔ barasu be nu a, nyini anɔ n nu­fɔ be ayo má Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ juma be fieo.

ref: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka i fite Yuda dɛɛ anɔ n nu wɔ, ama, fa kɔ Yuda dɛɛ anɔ n nu, ya ti má ka Moyisi kɛrɛɛri ka bu be ba yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ.

ref: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
text: Deke bo i ma ya ti jɔrɛ n bu nyunyumi n tara nyini su n la ka, Nyɛmɛ ayi i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo i ti ka Mɛlɛkisidɛki ama ya.

ref: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
text: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri Yesu ka i yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ ni, i taanri ndiɛ wɔ, ka, “Mini Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n atan ndiɛ, m wɔrɔ má m nɔɔ, wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo a la má de­fɔ fieo a.”

ref: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye i koro de minɛm tii ji fieo n a, bɛrɛ bo i ma bu cin dodo Nyɛmɛ ni, dama yiri wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ de bu nyɛmɛsere, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

ref: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn nyiŋgam yo ti ni, yiri ti má sɔ. Bɛrɛ dɔ, ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, bu du mɔ yo yoo­wa ma bu ŋu fa kɔ satiɛ bo ba yo i wɔ ka, na bu nya ba yo ma minɛm satiɛ bo ba yo i. Ama, yiri yiri, yoo­wa kpe­kun n kpein ye i yoori i a, yiri la bo i faari i ŋu yoori i ni.

ref: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
text: Deke n bo m koro ka m se n kɛrɛ la ka, ya la ahin Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n bo i tana aŋgoro, Micɛra Kpiri n saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

ref: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
text: I yo i Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn juma n dika n nu wɔ, bo i ti Nyɛmɛ seree­biri nahɔrɛ su, Nyɛmɛ ni i tii taari nyini dika n a, má adamande taari i a.

ref: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
text: Nzɛn má Yesu wo durunya n nu, má i yo má Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ fieo, dama minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ adu mɔ ayi bu ka bu yo sa­yoo­wa n wo bɛrɛ wɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere lɛɛ.

ref: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
text: Juma n bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm yo i ni, i ti kabo i wo aŋgoro n wɔ, ama, i ti má kun. Moyisi dɛɛ n gusu yo ti sɔ ɔ, nyini ti ye saŋga bo i ŋa i bo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue n ye Nyɛmɛ seeri i ka, “Si nu, na a yo deke n kɛrɛ kabo m kereeri u buka n su ka a yo i ni.”

ref: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
text: Nzɛn má sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ du mɔ diiri i di n koro yoori deke n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka i yo ni, má i kaaki fa má sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ na i di i di kun.

ref: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
text: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ aŋu i minɛm ni ŋgasi, na wɔ se bu ka, “Micɛra n ŋa o, saŋga suba bo m fá sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ na m dí i di ni Yisareli­fɔm ni Yuda­fɔm.

ref: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
text: I ti má ka dawa dɛɛ sɛsɛ­su ni, bo m yo faari i na m diiri i di ni am nam ni, saŋga bo m yiiri bu fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni. Ba yo abita má i kabo m yo seeri bu ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m cɛcɛ awo má bu nu n a. Kabo Micɛra n seeri lɛɛ.

ref: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Sɛsɛ­su n bo m bá di i di ni Yisareli­fɔm la ka, fa kɔ fa ba, m fá m nɔaniɛ n wura bu sunsunni nu, na bu bíta i bu ahore su, mini yó bu Nyɛmɛ a, na bu yó m minɛm. Kabo Nyɛmɛ seeri lɛɛ.

ref: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
text: Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i bá kere i lamɔkɔ ka i si Micɛra ni. Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bá si m, fa i tarakam kɔ tu i nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ.

ref: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
text: M sí bu aŋunfɔ, na m fá bu satiɛ bo ba yo i n fa ce bu, na m téŋge má i su fieo.”

ref: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
text: Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ yo kaanri sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ be jɔrɛ ni, i bu la ka, ciciri su dɛɛ n ayo dawa. Deke kɛrɛ bo i nya yoori dawa wieeri, i cɛ má, i busu míni wɔ.

ref: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
text: Bu yo boori nambue be wɔ, na ba fa sanvuin ati i nu nnyɔ. Anɔ nu dɛɛ sɔɔ ni, fitana takaa­biri, ni teebiri, ni kpɔnɔ bo bu fa ma Nyɛmɛ n wo dɔ, bu fere bɛrɛ n ka Sɔɔ Casi.

ref: Behind the curtain was the most holy place.
text: Mɔ dɛɛ sɔɔ ni, bu fere i ka Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ.

ref: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
text: Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bo ba mata i sika n wo dɔ, nyini yoo­biri n su ye bu kunkun tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani fa ma Nyɛmɛ a. Alaka n bo ba mata i sika n gusu wo dɔ, nyini alaka n kere kabo Nyɛmɛ faari sɛsɛ­su diiri i di n a. Deke bo bu wo alaka n nu n la sika torosɛ n bo ba fa diire n bo bu fere i ka mana n awura nu ni, ni Aarɔn kpama n bo i fiɛfiɛɛri nyawa ni, ni yabuɛ pɛpɛrɛpɛ nnyɔm bo Nyɛmɛ akɛrɛ i nɔaniɛ maa­wa burum n agɔ su ni.

ref: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
text: Kabo i ti ni, bu nya sɛsɛ nyini neŋgem kɛrɛ su wieeri, nyini saŋga, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm suwura sɔɔ n bo i wo anɔ nu n wɔ, na bu suyo bu Nyɛmɛ juma saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn ni ŋgumi koro wura sɔɔ n bo i wo mɔ n nu a. Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ, i wura dɔ kpe­kun wɔ, nzɛn i ŋa i wura dɔ gusu, i bita neŋge­ndɔrɛm mbunja a, na i du mɔ yo yoo­wa ma i ŋu ka, na i nya yo ma minɛm dɛɛ satiɛ bo ba yo i na bu si má ni.

ref: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
text: Deke bo i ti na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n fa nyini atin n sɔ ni, na i kere ya ka, saŋga bo bu yo boori nyini nambue na bu sɛsɛ deke n kɛrɛ su ni, Nyɛmɛ nya teke má atin na minɛm kɛrɛ koro wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu.

ref: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
text: Dama nyini yoo­wa atin n kere diire dii­wam, ni deke nuu­wam, ni bu yoo­wam kabo bu ŋmisi bu ŋu na bu yo bu ŋu casi ŋgumi wɔ. Nyini n kɛrɛ la i saŋga nyaari wɔ, yiri la bo bu suyo i sɔ ɔ, haari kɔ ju saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá yo deke n kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

ref: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
text: Má naanim ni sirim dɛɛ mbunja ye i fa wuraari nambue n nu a, ama, i faari i tiɛŋgu mbunja a, na wɔ fa awura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu kpe­kun n kpein, na wɔ fa ayo yoo­wa ama ya, adena ya koro nya tii­de n bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
text: Minɛm bo fin yo wo bu ŋu, na bu koro cin dodo má Nyɛmɛ ni, bu fa sirim ni tooram mbunja ye bu fa yɔyɔkɔti gɔ bu ŋu a, na bu yara nambaa, na bu fa nzuin n gɔ bu ŋu. Nyini saŋga, ba kpakpara bu fin ayo bu casi lɛɛ.

ref: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
text: Sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni, i ti kabo sɔnɔ toko la ŋgɔɔ na i kere kabo bu ba kpaaki i aja nu barasu n wɔ, má ka wɔ wu ka, ye bu koro di i aja n a.

ref: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
text: Saŋga bo i toko la ŋgɔɔ ni, be koro di má i aja ni, má ka wɔ wu ka.

ref: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
text: Nyini saŋga, Moyisi du mɔ kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n kɛrɛ kereeri minɛm n wɔ, kabo bu wo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu n ka, na i nya fa tooram ni sirim mbunja na i saŋga nzue nu, na i fa nyawa ni bɔɛ ndɔrɛ kɔkɔrɛ bo ba wu i tani, na i fa bo nu, na i fa yɔkɔti gɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kadasi n su ka, na i nya yɔkɔti gɔ minɛm kɛrɛ ŋu,

ref: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
text: Sɔ ye Moyisi yo fa mbunja n yɔkɔti gɔ nambue n su a, ni neŋge n kɛrɛ bo bu bita yo juma ma Nyɛmɛ n gusu.

ref: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue ni, ni dekem n kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni na bu ferema ni aŋgoro dɛɛm ni, wɔ yo daka ka bu fa neŋge­ndɔrɛ mbunja yo bu casi. Ama, neŋgem n bo bu wo aŋgoro n yiri, wɔ yo daka ka bu fa deke bo i mbunja tara neŋge­ndɔrɛm dɛɛ fa yo bu casi a.

ref: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
text: Kristo awura má dika casi n bo i ferema ni aŋgoro dɛɛ n nu a, bo minɛm taari i ni, ama, i wuraari aŋgoro dɛɛ n ni i tii nu a, ye kisa, wɔ de ya jinaa­biri Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
text: Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn ni yo wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu wɔ, na i fa neŋge­ndɔrɛ mbunja n yo yoo­wa. Ama, Kristo yiri awura má aŋgoro dɛɛ dika n nu ka i sufa i ŋu yo yoo­wa saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga.

ref: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
text: Nzɛn má i ŋa i sufa i ŋu yo yoo­wa saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga, má i sudi wahara a saŋga kɛrɛ, fite kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya n ba ju ni nyuma. Ama, kabo i ti n la ka, ahin saŋga n bo deke n kɛrɛ aba awieeri su ni, na wɔ ba kpe­kun n kpein, afa i ŋu ayo yoo­wa, adena Nyɛmɛ fa adamande satiɛ ce i.

ref: We die only once, and then we are judged.
text: Nyɛmɛ akpama i ka adamande kɛrɛ wu kpe­kun wɔ, nyini sin, na Nyɛmɛ dí i jɔrɛ.

ref: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
text: Kabo Kristo gusu yoori lɛɛ. Kpe­kun n kpein ye i faari i ŋu yoori yoo­wa, adena i de minɛ dɔŋgu satiɛ bo ba yo i ni. Nyini sin, i káaki ba bekun. Má minɛ satiɛ bo ba yo i n dɛɛ ti ye i bá, ama, i bá de minɛm bo bu niɛ i atin nu n tii ji wɔ.

ref: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, i ferema ni deke kpa n bo Nyɛmɛ ŋa i fá ba n wɔ, ama, má yiri lɛɛ. Nyini ti, yoo­wa n bo bu yo bu saŋga kɛrɛ akoro ama má bɛrɛ bo bu cin dodo Nyɛmɛ ni, ati má kabo má i koro ka bu ti ni.

ref: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
text: Nzɛn má nyini yoo­wam n kɛrɛ koro yiiri minɛm n bo bu su Nyɛmɛ n fiteeri bu satiɛm nu, má bu sunsunni ma má bu cɛɛn be ka ba yo satiɛ, má bu yaki nyini yoo­wam wɔ.

ref: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
text: Dama tooram ni sirim mbunja koro yi má satiɛ bo i wo adamande ahore su ni, cɛɛn be.

ref: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo Yesu koro ka i ba durunya n nu fɛn ni, na wɔ se Nyɛmɛ ka, “A koro má ka minɛ fa aceerim ni neŋge­ndɔrɛm n yo yoo­wa ma u, ama, adamande ŋuniɛ­mbaa ye a fa ama m ka m fa yo yoo­wa n a.

ref: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
text: Na wɔ kaaki ase Nyɛmɛ ka, “Mini yaa, ma ba ka m ba yo u koroo­wa n wɔ, kabo ba kɛrɛ m ŋusu jɔrɛ u nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ kadasi n nu ni.”

ref: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
text: Na wɔ se Nyɛmɛ ka, “Mini yaa, ma ba ka m ba yo u koroo­wa n wɔ.” Nyini la ka, Nyɛmɛ akun ciciri su dɛɛ yoo­wam kɛrɛ nɔɔ lɛɛ, ye wɔ fa fɔfɔrɛ aba.

ref: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
text: Ama, Yesu yiri yoori yoo­wa kpe­kun n kpein wɔ, bo i yo juma saŋga kɛrɛ, fa kɔ minɛ satiɛ bo bu yo i su. Na wɔ nya akɔ atana aŋgoro Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

ref: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
text: Fite nyini saŋga ni, i ŋminda kabo Nyɛmɛ ba ma i kunma i kpɔ­fɔm na i nana bu kɛrɛ ŋu.

ref: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
text: Nyini yoo­wa kun n dɛɛ ti, wɔ kpakpara minɛm fin kɛrɛ na wɔ ma ba ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka bu ti ni, saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
text: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n gusu kaanri i sɔ kereeri ya. Wɔ du mɔ ase ka,

ref: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
text: “Micɛra ŋa, sɛsɛ­su n bo m ni bɛrɛ adi i di fa kɔ saŋga bo i suba n la ka, m fá m nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n wura bu sunsunni nu, na m kɛ́rɛ i gɔ bu ahore su.”

ref: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
text: M ninbaam, Yesu dɛɛ mbunja n ti ye ya la ahoresi kɛrɛ ka ya koro cin dodo Nyɛmɛ a, na ya koro wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu a.

ref: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
text: Tɔ, kisa na ya la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bambaka bo i niɛ yɛrɛ n bo ya ti Nyɛmɛ minɛm n su ni.

ref: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma ya cin dodo Nyɛmɛ ni ya ahore­mbaa kun, na má ya yo sika be. Yesu mbunja n akpakpara ayi satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo ya ahore su a, na má ya sunsunni nu suma ya saŋga kɛrɛ ka ya yo satiɛ. Nyɛmɛ nzue n gusu bo ba fa abiɛ ya ni ama ya ŋuniɛ ayo casi a.

ref: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
text: Tama n bo ya ŋa ya la i ni, am ma ya bita i ni ya saa nnyɔ, má am ma ya fa sika wura nu, dama ya si ka Nyɛmɛ yo deke n bo wɔ fa sɛsɛ­su adi i di ka i yo i ni.

ref: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
text: Am ma ya sutu ya beŋgum fɔ, na ya buka ya beŋgu, na koro wo ya afiɛn na ya yo sakpa.

ref: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
text: Kabo i ti ye ni ka ya sutiɛn ya nɔɔ na ya su Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, má am ma ya yaki i kabo nbem suyo barasu ni, ama, am ma ya sutu ya beŋgu fɔ na bu nya ahoresi buka su, dama ya kɛrɛ ŋu ka Micɛra n dɛɛ cɛɛn n adodo lɛɛ.

ref: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i yo fa maari Moyisi ni, nzɛn sɔnɔ be keteeri na i yoori satiɛ, na minɛ nnyɔ wara nsan diiri daani ka i ti sɔ, nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm si má i mi aŋunfɔ, bu kun i wɔ.

ref: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
text: Ye nzɛn i nya ti sɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i buuri Nyɛmɛ Wa ni ŋgbɛɛn na i sunsun ka Yesu mbunja n bo i yo ya casi n ti ŋgbɛɛn, na i jɔjɔ wura Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i si ya aŋunfɔ ni ŋgasi nu, sui­cin barasu bɔnɔ ye am sunsun ka Nyɛmɛ fá cin i a?

ref: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
text: Dama ya si ka Micɛra n ase ka, “Mini cín bu sui a. Mini tɔ́ bu kaari a,” na wɔ kaaki ase ka, “Mini dí m minɛm jɔrɛ a.”

ref: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
text: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a to Nyɛmɛ saa nu, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, i la sɛrɛ agaya.

ref: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
text: Saŋga be bu yo kpiɛ am nzukɔ, na bu kere am wuwuruwa jama nu. Na saŋga be gusu na am asaŋga adi wahara ni am beŋgum bo bu kere bu wuwuruwa ni.

ref: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
text: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, am asi dan­sarakam aŋunfɔ. Bekun, haari nzɛn minɛ fiinri am neŋgem kɛrɛ bo am la i ni, am ahore ti jɔɔ­wa a, dama am si ka am la deke bo i tara nyinim bo ba de am dɔ ni, nyini deke n yiri wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
text: Kisa, wɔ yo daka ka am la mindi, adena am yo Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa, na am nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ afa sɛsɛ­su adi i di ka i ma am ni.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Wɔ ka kaan, i cɛ́ má, yiri n bo i suba ni, i bá.

ref: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
text: M sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su m dɔ, i nyá ŋgɔɔ, i yarada n dɛɛ ti, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kaakiiri i sin boori m, m ahore jɔ má i mi ŋu.”

ref: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
text: Yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti má minɛm bo bu kaaki bu sin, na bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni, ama, ya ti minɛm bo ya yo Nyɛmɛ yarada na ya nya tii­de wɔ.

ref: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
text: Dawa­fɔm yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i se ka i nyunu ayi bu sa­yoo­wa n a.

ref: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
text: Ya yo Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye ya koro fa si ka Nyɛmɛ yo jɔjɔɔri nɔaniɛ wɔ, ye i fa yiiri durunya n a. Nyini ma ya si ka, dekem bo ya nyu­mbaa koro ŋu bu ni, Nyɛmɛ afa deke bo ya koro ŋu má i ayi bu n a.

ref: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
text: Nowa yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i yo deeri kabo Nyɛmɛ bɔbɔɔri i fa kɔ sam bo bu bá yo n su n a. I deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kpaari lee bambaka n ye i ni i dika­fɔm wuraari nu ye ba wu má n a. I yoori sɔ n dɛɛ ti, nyini kere ka minɛm bo bu kaari durunya n nu n ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm a, ama, Nowa yiri, i yarada n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ɔ.

ref: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
text: Abraham boroori i nyi niɛniɛɛri miɛ kpiri n bo Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ata i na i la má awieeri n a.

ref: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
text: Abraham yi Saara mmɔɔ yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, haari ni bo wɔ yo buke na i ti ŋɔrɔka ni, yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i koro wuuri baa n a. I yo yarada ka Nyɛmɛ yó deke n bo i seeri ka i yó i ni.

ref: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Haari ni bo má Abraham ti ka sɔnɔ bo wɔ wu ni, yiri nyini sɔnɔ kun n ye i busu nu­fɔm asunnu ka asara­mbaam bo bu wo aŋgoro n a, ni kabo kunma ŋmiɛ sunnu na i la má nɔ n a.

ref: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
text: Nyini minɛm kɛrɛ yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada a, haari bu ba wuuri. Ba nya má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó ma bu, bu ŋgɔɔ su ni. Ama, yarada dɛɛ n ti, bu ŋuuri deke n ka i wo dede wɔ, na ba de i ni ahorejɔ, na ba soro su ase ka bu ti wɔfɔm wɔ, wara nanatiiri­fɔm wɔ, durunya n nu fɛn.

ref: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
text: Nyini minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu seeri sɔ ni, i kere ka, bu boro bu nyi niɛniɛ bu tiɛŋgu asiɛn busu wɔ.

ref: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
text: Nzɛn má bu faari bu ahore­mbaa seeri asiɛn n bo ba yo ayaki i n su ni anyum­boro, má bu koro nya atin bo bu kaaki kɔ nyini asiɛn n su bekun.

ref: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
text: Ama, bu yo koroori asiɛn be bo i tara bo bu yo yakiiri i n wɔ, yiri la Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ woo­biri n bo i wo aŋgoro ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i ti má nyinsɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ka bu fereeri i ka bu Nyɛmɛ n a, dama yiri ye wɔ sɛsɛ miɛ kpiri be su ase bu a.

ref: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
text: haari ni bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i ka, “Izaki dɛɛ ti, ye a nyá busu nu­fɔm a.”

ref: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
text: Abraham siiri ka Nyɛmɛ koro teŋge Izaki fite yiwee nu. Nahɔrɛ, i yarada n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Izaki yiwee nu, dama Abraham dɔ, Izaki ti ka sɔnɔ bo wɔ wu ye wɔ teŋge a.

ref: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
text: Jakɔbu yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo i ba sudodo ka i wu ni, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ayo Yosefu nbaam nnyɔ ni kisi, na wɔ miɛ i kpama su ayasu asu Nyɛmɛ.

ref: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
text: Yosefu yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo i ba suwu ni, ye i jɔjɔɔri ka, Yisareli­fɔm bá fite Ijipiti asiɛn n su n a, na wɔ kere kabo bu yo i fuin n barasu.

ref: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
text: Moyisi si, ni i ni yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, bu wuuri i ni, ba soro má nɔaniɛ n bo Ijipiti­fɔm fɛmɛ n fa maari ni, na ba fa i afiɛ sara nsan, dama bu ŋuuri ka i yo nyumi agaya.

ref: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
text: Moyisi yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye saŋga bo i ba biiri biɛsɔ, i keteeri ka má bu fere i ka fɛmɛ wa baa­bara n dɛɛ wa n a.

ref: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
text: I koroori kabo i ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm saŋga di wahara n tara bo i di fiɛfiɛ n bo i cɛ má na i do Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi ni.

ref: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
text: Yiri dɔ, nzɛn bu kpiɛɛri i nzukɔ kabo bu kpiɛ Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ni, nyini la kpɛrɛ tara deke kɛrɛ bo má i nya i Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni, dama i nyunu yo boroori aceeri n bo i bá nya i Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni su a.

ref: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
text: I yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i yo kpamaari Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n a, na wɔ kaaki ase Yisareli­fɔm ka, bu kun nbɔmbaam, na bu fa mbunja bɔrɔki bu sɔɔ anɔm su, adena Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n bo i kun n ba baari, na má i kun Yisareli­fɔm nbaa­biɛsɔ nkpiɛnm n a.

ref: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
text: Yisareli­fɔm yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo bu bara yɛɛri Yeriko miɛ kpiri n le­so ni, na kobe n bo ba ta abara ayɛ miɛ n kɛrɛ atɔ.

ref: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
text: Tɔ kisa, m si má kabo m se kun, m la má saŋga bo m kan fa kɔ Gɛdɛyɔn, ni Balaki, ni Samson, ni Yɛfɛta, ni Fɛmɛ Davidi, ni Samuwɛli, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n jɔrɛ n su.

ref: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
text: Nbara nbem yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge bu lamɔkɔm bo ba wu n yiwee nu ama bu. Ama, na ba kere nbem wahara akun bu. Ba saaki má bu nɔɔ na bu yaki bu nu ji, dama bu si ka bu bá teŋge bu yiwee nu, na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má aŋusu.

ref: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
text: Na ba yo bu nbem anzasi afin bu ŋgbere, na ba cici bu nbem jɔrɔkɔ, na ba fa bu awura dan­saraka nu.

ref: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
text: Na ba tu nbem yabuɛ abo akun bu, na ba kpiɛ ati nbem nu nnyɔ, na ba fa takobe akun bu, na yaari ati nbem na bu sufa nbɔɛm ni sirim ŋuma sufa yo bu suturam kata bu ŋu, na minɛm akere bu wahara ayo bu ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ.

ref: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
text: Bu ti minɛ kpam bo bu woo­wa tara durunya n nu­fɔm woo­wa, bu wo má woo­biri kun, bu nati yereŋgi boro kpantain n nu wɔ, ni bukam su, na bu suwura yabuɛ buɛm, ni asiɛŋgu kunma n nu suda.

ref: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
text: Nyɛmɛ nyunu yo yiiri nyini minɛm kɛrɛ sa­yoo­wa, bu yarada n dɛɛ ti, ama, ba nya má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó ma bu ni.

ref: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ma yɛrɛ deke kpa nahɔrɛ su, kana deke n bo i koro ka i yo n yo yii­wa, má ka ya ni bɛrɛ abo kun na i ma ya kɛrɛ ti kabo i koro ka ya ti ni.

ref: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
text: Nyini minɛm bo bu yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n sunnu, ba bara ayɛ ya, na bu niɛ kabo ya su Nyɛmɛ atin n su. Nyini ti, am ma yɛrɛ gusu yaki deke kɛrɛ bo i kanda ya na ya koro su má Nyɛmɛ ni, na ya yaki satiɛ n bo ya koro i agaya ni, na am ma ya fa anyum­boro na ya su atin n bo i ti ye ni ka ya su i n su.

ref: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
text: Am ma ya suniɛ Yesu. Yiri ti ya mɔ­fɔ a, na i ma ya yo Nyɛmɛ yarada kabo wɔ yo daka ka ya yo ni. I yo diiri wahara baka jaanu n su, na ba wura i nyinsɛ, ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ, i cɛcɛ awo má nu, dama i niɛ ŋuuri fiɛfiɛ n bo i bá di i ni, nyini ti, ye kisa, i tana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su i fɛmɛya n nu n a.

ref: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
text: Am niɛ kabo i yo diiri wahara satiɛ­yo­fɔm saa nu, na ba koro má i jɔrɛ, na i laari mindi barasu a. Am su i su, adena má am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am.

ref: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
text: Kabo satiɛ­yo­fɔm kere am wuwuruwa na am mindi ni, am nya mindi ju má juu­biri bo bu yi am ŋgɔɔ.

ref: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
text: Kabo am ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam na wɔ tu am fɔ asi am ahore ni, má am ma am wara afi nyini ni, bo i ŋa, “M wa, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ tu u fɔ, má a kete, nzɛn i cin u sui gusu a, má a ma u ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki u.

ref: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
text: Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ acin má am sui kabo i cin bo bu ti i nbaam kɛrɛ sui ni, nyini la ka, am ti má i nbaam nahɔrɛ su, am ti ka soma nbaam wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
text: Ya sim bo bu wuuri ya ni, bu ba yo, bu cin ya sui wɔ, na ya ma bu jirima. Nzɛn i nya ti sɔ yiri, wɔ yo daka ya de ya Si Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ tara bu dɛɛ, adena ya nya ŋgɔɔ.

ref: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
text: Saŋga bo ya yo ti nbaatumam ni, ya sim yo cin ya sui saŋga kaan wɔ, kabo i ti ye ni ka bu yo ni, ama, Nyɛmɛ yiri cin ya sui, i yo i ni kpa dɛɛ ti wɔ, i koro ka ya nya i dɛɛ woo­wa n be wɔ.

ref: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
text: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu cin ya sui ni, i ti ka ya ahore ti saakii­wa a, i yo má ya fiɛ, ama, nyini n kɛrɛ sin, minɛm kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ cin bu sui na bu si ka ba yo sa bo i kɔ má atin su, bu nya ahoreda na bu wo wo kpa.

ref: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
text: Nyini ti, má am ma am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am, ama, am ma am kpɛkpɛrɛ yo kenken.

ref: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
text: Am susu Nyɛmɛ atin n su, na am nya faŋga, adena má am gbaraki buka su. Fɔ­tu kabo wɔ yo daka ka má ya kete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ni

ref: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
