text: Am kɛrɛ boro am nyi na am tana laifiɛ ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ, na am wo wo bo i ti casi, dama wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a ti má casi, a ŋú má Nyɛmɛ.

ref: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
text: Am si am ŋu sa, na má be fun Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni. Am kaaki si am ŋu sa, na má be fa sunsunni tiɛ be ba saaki minɛ dɔŋgu na bu kaaki bu sin bo Nyɛmɛ.

ref: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
text: I tɔɔri i baawu kpiɛn jinaa­biri n dɛɛ sin, saŋga bo i seeri i si ka, i yo nyini kisi gɔ i ŋu ni, na i si akete, haari ni bo i sun ni nyunvuin ni, wɔ koro akaaki má deke n bo wɔ yo i n kun.

ref: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
text: Dama ba koro ade má nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari bu ni, i ŋa, haari nzɛn neŋge­ndɔrɛ be faari i ŋu kaanri buka ni, bu tu i yabuɛ bo kun i.

ref: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
text: Am aba jama n bo bu ti ka Nyɛmɛ nbaa­kpiɛnm, na ba kɛrɛ bu duma Nyɛmɛ dika n nu ni dɔ a. Am aba Nyɛmɛ dɔ, yiri bo i di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ ni. Am aba dawa­fɔm woo­biri ni, bɛrɛ bo bu yoori sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ na wɔ ma ba ti kabo i koro ka bu ti n wɔ.

ref: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
text: Am aba Yesu dɔ ɔ, yiri ti afiɛn dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ a, yiri ye wɔ fa sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n aba. I mbunja n bo wɔ fite agɔ ni, ayo ya casi a, i la kpɛrɛ tara Abɛli dɛɛ.

ref: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
text: Am si am ŋu sa, Nyɛmɛ sujɔjɔ am dɔ, má am kete i, dama saŋga bo i yo jɔjɔɔri Yisareli­fɔm dɔ durunya n nu fɛn na bu keteeri ni, wɔ yaki má bu. Nyini ti, nzɛn i wo aŋgoro sujɔjɔ ya dɔ, na ya keteeri, ya yo sɛ koro fite nu a?

ref: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
text: Nyini saŋga, i konvi ŋgumi mmɔɔ ma asiɛn kpisi wɔ. Ama, kisa, wɔ se ka, “M kpísi asiɛn n ni aŋgoro n kɛrɛ kpe­kun buka su.”

ref: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
text: Kabo i seeri ka i kpísi i kpe­kun buka su n kere ka, deke n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yi bu ni, i bá kpisi yi bu, adena dekem bo be koro kpisi má bu n bá ka.

ref: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
text: Nyini ti, kabo Nyɛmɛ ama ya atin ka ya ni yiri saŋga di i fɛmɛya n bo be koro kpisi má i ni, am ma ya da i asi. Am ma ya kaaki da i asi, na ya bu i, na ya soro i ahore, na ya su i, kabo wɔ yo daka ka ya su i ni.

ref: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
text: Tɔ, kabo Kristo dɛɛ ti na am ayo ka nibaa kun ni, am sukoro am beŋgu.

ref: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
text: Am teŋge su na am sude wɔfɔm. Nbem yo yoori sɔ ɔ haari na ba de mɛrɛkɛm wɔfɔ, ama, ba si má ka bu ti mɛrɛkɛm.

ref: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
text: Má am ma am wara fi minɛm bo bu wo dan­saraka nu n sa, am fa i ka am ni bɛrɛ ye ba saŋga anyi am a. Na má am ma am wara fi minɛm bo bu sudi wahara n gusu sa, am fa i ka ambɛrɛ sudi wahara n a.

ref: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
text: Am ma jirima wo am ajaam nu, na biɛsɔ n ni bara n kɛrɛ bita bu ŋu, na má be yo ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, dama Nyɛmɛ bá di minɛm kɛrɛ jɔrɛ bo bu kpini nbaram, ni bo bu kɔ somaya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn n kɛrɛ.

ref: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
text: Má am ma am nyunu boro ŋwaa koro su agaya, ama, am ma am nyunu yi deke n bo am la i ni, dama Nyɛmɛ ase ka, “M yáki má am ŋgbɛɛn, m káaki fa má am ji má.”

ref: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
text: Nyini ti, am ma ya fa ahoresi, na ya se ka, “Micɛra ni ti m buka­fɔ, m soro má sɛrɛ. Adamande koro yo m nzu wɔ?”

ref: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
text: Am niɛ am mɔ­fɔm bo bu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n kereeri am ni. Am niɛ kabo bu woori wo kpa barasu, na am sɔn bu yarada bo bu yoori i n su.

ref: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
text: Yesu Kristo kaaki má, kabo i yo ti ni, i toko ti sɔ ɔ, na i ti sɔ ɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
text: Má am su keree­wa fɔfɔrɛ barasu nbem su, na bu ma am mini atin kpa ni. I ti ye ni ka ya de Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n bo i ma ya ahoresi ni, na má ya de atin bo bu ciirem kere fa kɔ diire dii­wam su, bo bu su nyini ciirem su ni, i buka má bu.

ref: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Yesu gusu wuuri miɛ n ahɛmɛ n a, adena i koro fa i mbunja n yo minɛm casi.

ref: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
text: Dama durunya n nu fɛn, ya la má miɛ be bo ya suwo nu saŋga kɛrɛ, ama, ya boro ya nyi suniɛniɛ miɛ kpiri n bo i bá ba n wɔ.

ref: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Má am yaki kpa­yo, am buka am beŋgum saŋga kɛrɛ, dama nyini saraka yoo­wam sɔ, ye Nyɛmɛ koro a.

ref: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
text: Am de am mɔ­fɔm nɔaniɛ, na am su atin n bo bu fa kere am su, dama bɛrɛ sukpiɛ daafiri am busu n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ bá bisa bu kabo bu yoori bu juma n barasu n a. Nzɛn am deeri bu nɔaniɛ, bu yó bu juma ni ahoreda a, ama, nzɛn am ade má bu nɔaniɛ, nyini ma bu yo bu juma ni ahoresaaki a, na nyini sɔ gusu buka má am.

ref: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
text: M sere am wɔ, am boro am nyi sere Nyɛmɛ ma m, na i buka m, adena i cɛ má, na m kaaki ba am dɔ bekun. Awieeri su dɛɛ nyɛmɛsere ni

ref: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
text: Nyɛmɛ ahoreda­fɔ n ateŋge ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo i yiwee n nu. Nyɛmɛ faari Yesu mbunja n faari sɛsɛ­su diiri i di n ti ye Yesu yoori ya sasa­fɔ kpiri n a. Nyini sɛsɛ­su n la má awieeri.

ref: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
text: Nyini ti, m sereeri Nyɛmɛ ka i ma am deke kpa n kɛrɛ bo i ti ye ni ka am nya i, na am yo i sa koroo­wa, na i kaaki ya wo, na ya yo minɛ n bo i ahore jɔ ya ŋu. Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, ye i yo nyini n kɛrɛ a. Yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

ref: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
text: M ninbaam, m sere am wɔ, am yo sawɔrɛ, na am so am sui, na am de ahin jɔrɛ n bo i wo ahin kadasi n nu, na m sufa tu am fɔ ni. Dama jɔrɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am n sunnu má.

ref: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
text: M koro ka am si ka, ya niwa Timoti wo má dan­saraka nu kun, ba yaki i, nzɛn i ba tuuri m nde, na m ba suba, m ni yiri kɛrɛ bá niɛ am busu.

ref: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
text: Am bisa am mɔ­fɔm kɛrɛ ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma ya. Ya ninbaa yarada­yo­fɔm bo bu wo Itali asiɛn n su n kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu.

ref: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
text: Mini Yakobo bo m ti Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ akɔɔ ni, m bisa Kristo jamam n kɛrɛ dɔŋgu, bɛrɛ bo ba sandi abara ayɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu ni. Yarada­yo ni alecira jɔrɛ

ref: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
text: M ninbaam, nzɛn gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ be tu am, am ma am ahore jɔ.

ref: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
text: Am ma am mindi n sun am nu, adena am woo­wa kɛrɛ ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni na má pui kpiɛ am su.

ref: If you are that kind of person, you can't make up your mind, and you surely can't be trusted. So don't expect the Lord to give you anything at all. Poor People and Rich People
text: Nyini sɔnɔ n bo i yo sika sɔ ni, i sunsunni ti nnyɔ nnyɔ ɔ, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i si má i jinaa­biri. Má i mi n sunsun ka i nya pui fite Mibiɛ n dɔ. Fɔ­tu fa kɔ Kristo jama n yaari­fɔm ni neŋge­fɔm dɔ

ref: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
text: Tɔ, ya niwa bo i ti yaari­fɔ, na Nyɛmɛ awura i kpiri, i ti ye ni ka i mi ma i ahore da.

ref: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
text: I ti sɔ ɔ, kabo wiɛ ba bi, na i kɛkɛrɛ n bo kpɔnkpɔn wura pum na i wu, na i nyumi wie ni. Kabo neŋge­fɔ n gusu ni i neŋge n ti lɛɛ. Saŋga bo i nyunu aba aboro i juma su ni, i ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bá wo má bɛrɛ kun. Fɔ­tu fa kɔ saŋga bo am wo gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ nu ni

ref: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
text: Tere­fiɛ wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ tu i, na i mi saaki má i nɔɔ n dɔ. Nyini sɔnɔ n koro mindi ŋuuri nyini gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ n awieeri, i nyá ŋgɔɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, nyini la aceeri n bo i yo seeri ka i fá ma bɛrɛ bo bu koro i ni.

ref: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
text: Ama, deke bo i ma sɔnɔ wura satiɛ nu n la i mi ni i tii dɛɛ konvikiya a, nyini ma i cin dodo satiɛ­yo a,

ref: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
text: na nyini konvikiya n nya cin i wura satiɛ nu na i mi yo satiɛ be, na nyini satiɛ­yo n nya ba bi wieeri, i fa yiwee ba a.

ref: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má am daka am ŋu.

ref: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
text: Aceeri kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa na i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, i kɛrɛ fite aŋgoro ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, yiri yiiri deke n kɛrɛ bo i ta aŋgoro n a. I wo ti i wo wɔ, na i ma má awosin yo má na i kaaki deke be.

ref: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
text: Dama yaa kere má sɔnɔ na i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
text: Nyini ti, am yaki sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, ni satiɛ kɛrɛ yo. Am yo am ŋu kaan fa ma Nyɛmɛ, na am de i nɔaniɛ n bo wɔ fa awura am ahore­mbaa su ni. Dama nyini nɔaniɛ ni koro de am tii ji a.

ref: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
text: Dama nzɛn be tiiri nɔaniɛ n na i yo má kabo i kere ni, i mi ti ka sɔnɔ bo i fa niɛɛnu niɛ, na i ŋu i ŋu wɔ,

ref: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
text: Sasu bekun, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i su Nyɛmɛ atin su, na i koro tara má i nɔɔ, i mi daka i ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, na i nyɛmɛsu atin n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
text: M ninbaam, kabo am yo Yesu Kristo yarada ni, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ Jirima­fɔ ni, má am fa tantambu­nu ni am beŋgu.

ref: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
text: Ma m fa i ka, nzɛn minɛ nnyɔ nbem ba wuraari am tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na kun wura sutura kpakpa ni sika ŋgaa, na nyiŋga n ti yaari­fɔ na i wura sutura titii­wa,

ref: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
text: na nzɛn am nu kun se bo i wura sutura kpakpa n ka, “Jande, ba tana ahin tanaa­biri nyunyumi n su,” na i nya se yaari­fɔ n ka, “Wɔrɔ, ka jina dɔ, nzɛn má nyini, ba tana m jaa bu fɛn,”

ref: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
text: nyini kere ka am sufa tantambu­nu atin lɛɛ, na am sunsunni tiɛ ma am sufa kpakpaaki­nu ba lɛɛ, má sɔ?

ref: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, am ti fɛn. Má Nyɛmɛ yiiri yaari­fɔm bo bu wo durunya n nu n ka bu ti ajɛkɛ­fɔm i dɔ, bu yarada n dɛɛ ti a, na bu nyá fɛmɛya n bo wɔ se ka i fa ma minɛm bo bu koro i n a, nzɛn sɛ?

ref: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ bu yaari­fɔm ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Ŋmam kere am wahara n a? Ŋmam kaaki cin am kɔ jɔrɛ dii­birim n a? Má neŋge­fɔm n a?

ref: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
text: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.” Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa bambaka n lɛɛ. Nzɛn am yo sɔ, nyini ti kpa a.

ref: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
text: Ama, nzɛn am laari tantambu­nu, am ayo satiɛ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka am ti ketema­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
text: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a suuri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ su, ama, na a saakiiri i kun n kpein mmɔɔ, a saaki nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

ref: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
text: Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ se ka, “Má a kpini u beŋgu yi” ni, yiri akaaki ase ka, “Má a kun sɔnɔ” n a. Ama, nzɛn haari a kpini má u beŋgu yi, ye a kuunri sɔnɔ, a yo ketema­fɔ fa kɔ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su lɛɛ.

ref: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
text: Dama jɔrɛ­di cɛɛn, minɛm bo bu si má bu beŋgum aŋunfɔ ni, Nyɛmɛ si má bɛrɛ mmɔɔ aŋunfɔ. Ama, aŋunfɔ si­fɔm ni, bɛrɛ dí nasara, jɔrɛ­di cɛɛn ni. Fɔ­tu fa kɔ yarada ni i sa­yoo­wa

ref: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
text: M ninbaam, nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋa i yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na i sa­yoo­wa kere má ka i la yarada, nyini yarada ni kóro de i tii ji, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, nyini ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
text: na nzɛn am nu kun seeri i ka, “Kɔ ni ahoreda. Nyɛmɛ ma u na a di, na i ma a nya sutura.” Nzɛn a seeri sɔ, na a ma má i mi koroo­wa ni, i kpa la bɔnɔ?

ref: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn a la yarada n ŋgumi, na u sa­yoo­wa kere má ka a la yarada, i ti yarada ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
text: Be koro se ka, “Ahin sɔnɔ n yiri yo Nyɛmɛ yarada wɔ, ama, ahin n yiri yo sakpa a.” Tɔ, ye sɔnɔ yo sɛ kere ka i yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, bo i yo má sakpa a? Mini yo sakpa a, nyini kere ka m yo Nyɛmɛ yarada.

ref: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
text: A ŋa a yo yarada ka Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi wo bɛrɛ a. Nfan. Haari ajinim mmɔɔ si i sɔ ɔ, na bu ŋuniɛ subobo bu ni sɛrɛ.

ref: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
text: A ŋu má i? I yarada yoo­wa fa saŋga ni i sakpa yoo­wa kɛrɛ yoori kun wɔ. Na i sa­yoo­wa n akere ka i yarada n ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti n wɔ.

ref: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
text: Tɔ, m ŋa am aŋu i e, ka sɔnɔ sa­yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ de i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, má ka i mi yarada ŋgumi ti.

ref: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
text: M ninbaam, má am dɔŋgu yo am ŋu kere­fɔm. Dama am si ka yɛrɛ bo ya ti kere­fɔm ni, ya jɔrɛ dii­wa bá yo kekereke agaya a.

ref: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
text: Am ma ya niɛ kpɔŋgɔ. Ya fa karafi wura kpɔŋgɔm nɔɔ ɔ, na ya kunma bu faŋga na bu yo deke kɛrɛ bo ya koro ka bu yo i ni.

ref: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
text: Am ma ya niɛ lee bambaka gusu, i ni i bambaka n kɛrɛ ni, haari nzɛn aŋuma bambaka subo i kɔ deke be, lee kan­fɔ n koro fa napɔku baka n bo i ti kaan ni lee ni, na i kan i kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo i koro.

ref: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
text: I ti sɔ lɛɛ ni taferema n gusu, i ni bo i ti kaan aŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu n kɛrɛ ni, i koro saaki deke dɔŋgu. Niɛ kabo sin ti barasu a, haari i ti kaan sɛ, i koro je boo bambaka.

ref: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
text: Adamande kunma deke n kɛrɛ faŋga, i ajaa­nnam o, i laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm o, i cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm o, ni jombaam o. Na wɔ koro ata bu kɛrɛ.

ref: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
text: Ama, taferema yiri, sɔnɔ be nya koro kunma má i faŋga. I ti deke tiɛ wɔ, bo be koro tara má i. I ti ka wɔ yi ni bɔrɛ bo i kun wɔ.

ref: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
text: Nyini anɔɔ kun n ye kansi ni saaki fite nu a. M ninbaam, wɔ yo má daka ka i ti sɔ.

ref: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
text: Bura kun koro tiɛ nzue kpa, ni nzue tiɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, i koro yo má.

ref: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
text: M ninbaam, am si ka damba baka koro su má ŋguin­mbaa. Cumi baka gusu koro su má damba. I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, bura bo i nzue ti lalaraki, i koro tiɛ má nzue bo i yo fiɛ. Fɔ­tu fa kɔ alecira bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni

ref: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
text: Ŋma wo am nu bo i la alecira ni si a? I ti ye ni ka i mi kere ka i ti sɔ ɔ, fa kɔ i woo­wa kpa su, na i yo sakpa, na i wo bɛnlɛ, nyini kere ka i la alecira.

ref: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
text: Ama, nzɛn am susi kɔrɛ ahore­mbaa su, na am suyo wi, na am bita anyii sunsunni, má am bita fɔ ka am la alecira, na má am bu kɔrɛ jaraki nahɔrɛ n sɔ.

ref: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
text: Nyini alecira n sɔ fite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, i fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ, i nati má Nyɛmɛ atin su, i ti Sitana dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
text: Deke bo kɔrɛsi, ni anyii wo, kpakpaaki­nu ni satiɛ yoo­wa barasu kɛrɛ wo dɔ ɔ.

ref: Why do you fight and argue with each other? Isn't it because you are full of selfish desires that fight to control your body?
text: Nzu ti ma am di aluwa, na am suse akuruwa am ŋuŋu nu a? I fite ka am sunsunni ti tiɛ, fa kɔ am ahore­mbaa koroo­wa beberebe n bo i wo am ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu, na i kpiɛ am daafiri n dɛɛ ti a, má sɔ?

ref: You want something you don't have, and you will do anything to get it. You will even kill! But you still cannot get what you want, and you won't get it by fighting and arguing. You should pray for it.
text: Am koro deke nbem, na am nya má bu n ti, ye am koro ka am kun am beŋgum n a. Am konviki wura minɛm neŋge nu, na am nya má i n ti, ye am se akuruwam, na am di aluwa n a. Am sere má Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye am nya má am koroo­wa n a.

ref: Yet even when you do pray, your prayers are not answered, because you pray just for selfish reasons.
text: Deke bo i ti na am sere Nyɛmɛ na am nya má i n la ka, am sere ni sunsunni tiɛ wɔ. Am sere dekem ka am fa yo am kunnu koroo­wa a.

ref: You people aren't faithful to God! Don't you know if you love the world, you are God's enemies? And if you decide to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am su má Nyɛmɛ ni, am si má ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro durunya n nu neŋgem agaya, ti Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Nyini ti, sɔnɔ bo i fa i ahore­mbaa se durunya n nu neŋgem su, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

ref: In fact, God treats us with even greater kindness, just as the Scriptures say, “God opposes everyone who is proud, but he blesses all who are humble with undeserved grace.”
text: Ama, ahɔrɔma n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya n sunnu. Nyini ti, ye ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Nyɛmɛ kete kpirikoro­fɔm wɔ, ama, na i fa i ahɔrɔma n ma tarakam.”

ref: Surrender to God! Resist the devil, and he will run from you.
text: Nyini ti, am fa am ŋu ma Nyɛmɛ. Am kete Sitana, na i ŋmáti yaki am.

ref: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Clean up your lives, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you people who can't make up your mind.
text: Am cin dodo Nyɛmɛ, na i gusu cín dodo am. Ambɛrɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm, am yi am saa fite satiɛ nu. Ambɛrɛ anɔɔ nnyɔ nnyɔ­fɔm, am yo am ahore­mbaa casi.

ref: Be sad and sorry and weep. Stop laughing and start crying. Be gloomy instead of glad.
text: Am satiɛ dɛɛ ti, am ma am ahore saaki, na am sun. Am fa am siri fa kaaki sun, na am fa am ahorejɔ fa kaaki ahoresaaki.

ref: Be humble in the Lord's presence, and he will honor you. Saying Cruel Things about Others
text: Am da am ŋu asiɛŋgu ma Nyɛmɛ, na yiri bá wura am kpiri. Fɔ­tu fa kɔ kabo má ya jaraki ya beŋgum ni

ref: My friends, don't say cruel things about others! If you do, or if you condemn others, you are condemning God's Law. And if you condemn the Law, you put yourself above the Law and refuse to obey either it
text: M ninbaam, má am jɔjɔ tiɛ ma am beŋgu. Wɔrɔ bo a jɔjɔ tiɛ ma u lamɔkɔ, wara a jaraki i, a jɔjɔ tiɛ afa akɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ajaraki i lɛɛ. Nzɛn a nya yoori sɔ, a nya ti má sɔnɔ bo i de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n lɛɛ, ama, a yo u ŋu ka jɔrɛ­di­fɔ lɛɛ.

ref: or God who gave it. God is our judge, and he can save or destroy us. What right do you have to condemn anyone? Warning against Bragging
text: Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi ti i nɔaniɛ n kpama­fɔ a, na yiri ŋgumi kaaki ti i jɔrɛ­di­fɔ a, yiri bo i koro de minɛm tii ji na i kaaki koro ma bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni. Wɔrɔ la ŋma na a jaraki u beŋgu a? Fɔ­tu fa kɔ yanfɔya atin su

ref: You should know better than to say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to the city. We will do business there for a year and make a lot of money!”
text: Am ti fɛn, ambɛrɛ bo am se ka, “Nyuma, wara ahunma, ya kɔ́ ahin miɛ n su, na ya kɔ́ yo afɔ kun dɔ, na ya yó juma, na ya nyá ŋwaa beberebe ni.”

ref: What do you know about tomorrow? How can you be so sure about your life? It is nothing more than mist that appears for only a little while before it disappears.
text: Ambɛrɛ, haari am si má sa bo i bá yo am ahunma mmɔɔ. Am ni am ŋgɔɔ ti ka sɛ? Am ti ka bɔrɛ wɔ, bo i tɔ na i cɛ má, na wiɛ ba tiɛ, a ŋu ka wɔ kpɔnkpɔn ni.

ref: You should say, “If the Lord lets us live, we will do these things.”
text: Deke bo i ti ye ni ka am se n la ka, “Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ diiri su, ya wó bɛrɛ, na ya yó ahin ni ahin.”

ref: Yet you are stupid enough to brag, and it is wrong to be so proud.
text: Ama, kabo i ti ni, ambɛrɛ koro kpiri wɔ, na am sufɔ. Nyini yanfɔya sɔ n kɛrɛ ti má kpa.

ref: If you don't do what you know is right, you have sinned.
text: Tɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i si sakpa bo i yo i, na i yo má i, i mi yo satiɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: You rich people should cry and weep! Terrible things are going to happen to you.
text: Ambɛrɛ neŋge­fɔm, am ti fɛn. Sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm bo bu bá tu am n dɛɛ ti, am sun na am subo awie.

ref: Your treasures have already rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
text: Am ajɛkɛ n kɛrɛ susaaki, na nvɔrɛm sumuka am suturam n kɛrɛ.

ref: Your money has rusted, and the rust will be evidence against you, as it burns your body like fire. Yet you keep on storing up wealth in these last days.
text: Am sika ni am jɛtɛ kɛrɛ suwura ŋgambulaari, na bu ŋgambulaari wuraa­wa ni bá ma Nyɛmɛ jaraki am, na i káaki ba yara am ka sin, am ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu. Am atiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ afa ase fa kɔ durunya n awieeri wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: You refused to pay the people who worked in your fields, and now their unpaid wages are shouting out against you. The Lord All-Powerful has surely heard the cries of the workers who harvested your crops.
text: Sasu bekun, am akete ka am tɔ má juma­yo­fɔm ŋkatɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu tambu am fiesu neŋge mbaa ni. Am ti kabo bu kan am ndiɛ a, bu awie bo n kɛrɛ ato Micɛra Yiko­fɔ n sui nu.

ref: While here on earth, you have thought only of filling your own stomachs and having a good time. But now you are like fat cattle on their way to be butchered.
text: Kabo am wo durunya n nu fɛn ni, am adi fiɛ, na am ayo am kunnu koroo­wa. Am adi ayo ŋgbiŋgbiri ka toora dɔdɔɔri­fɔm bo bu sukɔ ka bu kpiɛ bu kɔm ni.

ref: My friends, be patient until the Lord returns. Think of farmers who wait patiently for the autumn and spring rains to make their valuable crops grow.
text: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, am yo sawɔrɛ ŋminda fa kɔ cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ ni bá ni. Am niɛ kabo dɔdɔɔri­fɔ yo sawɔrɛ ŋminda, haari kabo bu fiesu diire bá yo kpakpa barasu. I yo sawɔrɛ ŋminda kpanyini nzue kɔ tu takoku wɔ.

ref: Be patient like those farmers and don't give up. The Lord will soon be here!
text: Am gusu yo sawɔrɛ. Am ma am ahore si, dama Mibiɛ n ba saŋga n adodo.

ref: Don't grumble about each other or you will be judged, and the judge is right outside the door.
text: M ninbaam, má am wo waraki su, sukan am beŋgu ndiɛ, adena má Nyɛmɛ bá di am jɔrɛ. Am ti fɛn, yiri Jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n lɛɛ, yiri jina anɔ n nu n a.

ref: My friends, follow the example of the prophets who spoke for the Lord. They were patient, even when they had to suffer.
text: M ninbaam, am ma ya teŋge su, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n bo bu yo jɔjɔɔri Micɛra n duma n nu ni. Am su bu jaa yii­wa su, kabo bu yo yoori sawɔrɛ mindi diiri wahara ni.

ref: My friends, above all else, don't take an oath. You must not swear by heaven or by earth or by anything else. “Yes” or “No” is all you need to say. If you say anything more, you will be condemned.
text: M ninbaam, jɔrɛ bo i buka i kɛrɛ su n la ka, má am tan deke be ndiɛ fieo. Má am se ka am tan asiɛŋgu, wara am tan aŋgoro. Nzɛn u jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu la ka ɛɛn, am se ka, “Ɛɛn.” Na nzɛn u jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu la ka ai, am se ka, “Ai.” Adena, má Nyɛmɛ di am jɔrɛ na i yo am ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: If you are sick, ask the church leaders to come and pray for you. Ask them to put olive oil on you in the name of the Lord.
text: Nzɛn be wo am nu bo i la má laifiɛ, i ti ye ni ka i mi fere Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm na bu ba sere Nyɛmɛ ma i, na bu nunuka i ŋguin, Mibiɛ n duma n nu.

ref: If you have faith when you pray for sick people, they will get well. The Lord will heal them, and if they have sinned, he will forgive them.
text: Nyini nyɛmɛsere n bo bu sere i ni yarada ni, i má tukpaki­fɔ n nya laifiɛ, na Mibiɛ ni má i yasu. Na nzɛn haari wɔ yo satiɛ mmɔɔ, Nyɛmɛ fá i satiɛ n ce.

ref: If you have sinned, you should tell each other what you have done. Then you can pray for one another and be healed. The prayer of an innocent person is powerful, and it can help a lot.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am kan am satiɛ bo am ayo i kere am beŋgu, na am sere Nyɛmɛ ma am beŋgu, adena am nya laifiɛ. Sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, i nyɛmɛsere la faŋga, na i yo juma nahɔrɛ su.

ref: Elijah was just as human as we are, and for three and a half years his prayers kept the rain from falling.
text: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n bo bu fere i ka Eliya ni, i yo tiiri adamande ka yɛrɛ wɔ, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro su, ka, má nzue tɔ, na nzue n atɔ má asiɛn n su afɔ nsan ni sara nsɛn.

ref: But when he did pray for rain, it fell from the skies and made the crops grow.
text: Na wɔ kaaki asere Nyɛmɛ, ka, i ma nzue n tɔ, na wɔ tɔ, na asiɛn n diire mbaa kɛrɛ akaaki ayo.

ref: My friends, if any followers have wandered away from the truth, you should try to lead them back.
text: M ninbaam, nzɛn am nu kun miniiri nahɔrɛ atin ni, na am nu kun be faari i kpieeri i nyi baari atin n su,

ref: God the Father decided to choose you as his people, and his Spirit has made you holy. You have obeyed Jesus Christ and are sprinkled with his blood. I pray that God will be kind to you and will keep on giving you peace! A Real Reason for Hope
text: Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ du mɔ ayi am ye i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama am ayo i dɛɛ minɛm a, adena am de Yesu Kristo nɔaniɛ ni, na i mbunja n ŋmisi am na i yo am casi. Nyɛmɛ buka am na i ma am ahoreda kɔ sin su. Deke bo ya la i tama ni

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is so good, and by raising Jesus from death, he has given us new life and a hope that lives on.
text: Am ma ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Si. Wɔ si ya aŋunfɔ agaya n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ ma ya yo minɛ fɔfɔrɛ, ye ya la tama wo ni ŋgɔɔ a, dama i teŋgeeri Yesu Kristo i yiwee n nu wɔ.

ref: God has something stored up for you in heaven, where it will never decay or be ruined or disappear.
text: Na am nyá aceerim bo Nyɛmɛ afa ase aŋgoro aŋminda ka i fá ma am ni. I ti aceeri bo i saaki má, i yo má fin, na i kaaki wie má.

ref: You have faith in God, whose power will protect you until the last day. Then he will save you, just as he has always planned to do.
text: Am ayo Kristo yarada n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ la yiko bo i sasa am haari ni bo am bá nya tii­de n bo wɔ sɛsɛ i su ka am bá nya i awieeri su saŋga ni.

ref: On that day you will be glad, even if you have to go through many hard trials for a while.
text: Nyini kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, ye am la ahorejɔ n a, haari nzɛn wɔ yo daka ka am ahore saaki kaan fa kɔ kekereke barasu barasu n bo kisa am suŋu i ni.

ref: You have never seen Jesus, and you don't see him now. But still you love him and have faith in him, and no words can tell how glad and happy
text: Am nyu­mbaa nya ŋu má yiri Yesu ni, ama, am koro i, haari ni bo am nyu­mbaa koro ŋu má i kisa ni, am yo i yarada a, na am ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su, na am si má kabo am se barasu,

ref: you are to be saved. This is why you have faith.
text: dama am anya am yarada n kpɛrɛ, yiri la tii­de n bo am anya i ni.

ref: Some prophets told how God would treat you with undeserved grace, and they searched hard to find out more about the way you would be saved.
text: Nyini tii­de n jɔrɛ ye dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm fiitiiri i nu na bu si i n a, ye bu kan kereeri minɛm fa kɔ sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ bá yo ma am n su n a.

ref: The Spirit of Christ was in them and was telling them how Christ would suffer and would then be given great honor. So they searched to find out exactly who Christ would be and when this would happen.
text: Na Kristo Awiɛwiɛ n bo i wo bu dɔ n adu mɔ akere bu kabo Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i n bá diiri wahara ni, ni kabo i woo­wa ti kpiri barasu ni, i wahara di n sin. Na ba boro bu nyi na bu si kabo yiri Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ti barasu, ni saŋga n bo nyini sam ni bá yo ni.

ref: Be alert and think straight. Put all your hope in how God will treat you with undeserved grace when Jesus Christ appears.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am sɛsɛ am sunsunni su, na am tara am ŋu, na am fa am tama kɛrɛ wura sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ bá yo ma am n nu, saŋga bo Yesu Kristo bá yi i ŋu fite fofoe nu ni.

ref: Always live as God's holy people should, because God is the one who chose you, and he is holy.
text: Ama, am ma am woo­wa kɛrɛ ti casi, kabo yiri Nyɛmɛ woo­wa ti casi ni, yiri bo wɔ yi am ka i dɛɛm ni,

ref: That's why the Scriptures say, “I am the holy God, and you must be holy too.”
text: dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Mini, m ti casi wɔ, nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka ambɛrɛ gusu ti casi.”

ref: You say that God is your Father, but God doesn't have favorites! He judges all people by what they do. So you must honor God while you live as strangers here on earth.
text: Kabo am sere Nyɛmɛ, na am fere i ka am Si ni, am tara am ŋu na am bu i fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo am yo i am ŋgɔɔ su ni, ahin durunya n bo i ti má am woo­biri n nu ni, dama kabo i di minɛm jɔrɛ fa kɔ bu kɛrɛ sa­yoo­wa su ni, i tambu má nu.

ref: You were rescued from the useless way of life you learned from your ancestors. But you know you were not rescued by such things as silver or gold that don't last forever.
text: Am si kabo Nyɛmɛ ade am tii aji afite am sa­yoo­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm n bo am nam yo fa maari am n nu ni. Deke n bo wɔ fa ade am tii aji n ti má ka sika wara jɛtɛ bo bu koro ka ŋgbɛɛn ni,

ref: Christ was chosen even before the world was created, but because of you, he did not come until these last days.
text: Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri i ka i ba yo sɔ ka, ye i nya ba yiiri durunya n a. Ama, ahin awieeri su saŋga n bo ya wo nu n ye Nyɛmɛ ma wɔ fite fofoe nu, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti a.

ref: And when he did come, it was to lead you to have faith in God, who raised him from death and honored him in a glorious way. This is why you have put your faith and hope in God.
text: Yiri dɛɛ ti ye am ayo Nyɛmɛ yarada n a. Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu, na wɔ wura i kpiri, nyini dɛɛ ti ye am yo yarada na am la tama fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ n a.

ref: You obeyed the truth, and your souls were made pure. Now you sincerely love each other. But you must keep on loving with all your heart.
text: Kabo am ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ, na wɔ yo am casi ni, nyini ma am koro am beŋgu bo dadaka wo má nu a. Nyini ti, am sukoro am beŋgu ni am ahore­mbaa fofoe ni nahɔrɛ su.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Humans wither like grass, and their glory fades like wild flowers. Grass dries up, and flowers fall to the ground.
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Adamande kɛrɛ ti ka wura a, i cɛ má na i wu. Na bu nyumi kɛrɛ ti kabo wura pum yo nyumi barasu n wɔ, i cɛ má na i yɔrɔki na i kpɔnkpɔn.

ref: But what the Lord has said will stand forever.” Our good news to you is what the Lord has said.
text: Ama, Micɛra n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.” Nyini nɔaniɛ n ti labari kpa n bo ba kan akere am n a.

ref: Be like newborn babies who are thirsty for the pure spiritual milk that will help you grow and be saved.
text: Kabo baa­kan koro nyufini ni, am gusu, am koro Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo i ti ka nɔnɔ casi ni, nyini buka am ye am koro nyi na am nya tii­de a,

ref: You have already found out how good the Lord really is.
text: dama ambɛrɛ ni am tii si kabo Micɛra n ti kpa barasu.

ref: Come to Jesus Christ. He is the living stone people have rejected, but which God has chosen and highly honored.
text: Am bra Kristo dɔ, yiri ti ka yabuɛ n bo i la ŋgɔɔ n a. Yiri ye fata­fɔm ayi i atu n a. Ama, Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i la anyunuyi.

ref: And now you are living stones being used to build a spiritual house. You are also a group of holy priests, and with the help of Jesus Christ you will offer sacrifices that please God.
text: Ambɛrɛ gusu, am ti ka yabuɛm n bo bu la ŋgɔɔ n wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ sufa am ta awuru n bo i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo nu, na am yo i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ casim, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi buka am na am yo yoo­wa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: It is just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a choice and precious cornerstone. No one who has faith in this one will be disappointed.”
text: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, “Am ti fɛn, m sufa yabuɛ bo i la anyunuyi sufa bo Siyon miɛ kpiri n bu, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, i mi dí má nyinsɛ.”

ref: You are followers of the Lord, and this stone is precious to you. But it isn't precious to those who refuse to follow him. They are the builders who tossed aside the stone that turned out to be the most important one of all.
text: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ bo am ayo i yarada ni, am anya deke bo i la anyunuyi ni, ama, minɛm bo bu yo má i yarada ni, ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Yabuɛ n bo bondo ta­fɔm n faari i tuuri ni, yiri ye wɔ kaaki bondo n jaa yabuɛm n kɛrɛ tii a.”

ref: But you are God's chosen and special people. You are a group of royal priests and a holy nation. God has brought you out of darkness into his marvelous light. Now you must tell all the wonderful things he has done. The Scriptures say,
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ, am ti daanfiɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi am wɔ, am ti sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm bo am su Nyɛmɛ bo i ti am fɛmɛ n wɔ, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛ casim wɔ, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ awuru­nbaam wɔ. Yiri ye wɔ yi am ka am kan i sakpam kere minɛm a, dama wɔ yi am afite awosin nu, afa am awura i dɛɛ wein n bo i la má aŋusu n nu.

ref: “Once you were nobody. Now you are God's people. At one time no one had mercy on you. Now God has treated you with kindness.” Live as God's Servants Should
text: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Saŋga be, am yo ti má i dɛɛ minɛm, ama, kisa, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛm wɔ. Nyini saŋga, Nyɛmɛ yo si má am aŋunfɔ, ama, kisa, wɔ si am aŋunfɔ.” Kabo Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm woo­wa ti barasu ni

ref: Dear friends, you are foreigners and strangers on this earth. So I beg you not to surrender to those desires that fight against you.
text: M danfo kpam, am ti wɔfɔm wɔ, ni minɛm bo bu la má woo­biri durunya n nu fɛn, nyini dɛɛ ti, m sere am ni anyum­boro wɔ, má am ma am nyunu boro am aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa su, dama am aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wam sukoro ka bu kunma am wɔ.

ref: Always let others see you behaving properly, even though they may still accuse you of doing wrong. Then on the day of judgment, they will honor God by telling the good things they saw you do.
text: Am ma am woo­wa ti kpa, na nzɛn minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n jaraki am fa kɔ satiɛ be su, bu ba ŋu am sakpa n bo am yo i ni, na bu kánsi Nyɛmɛ, cɛɛn n bo i bá niɛ i minɛm su ni.

ref: The Lord wants you to obey all human authorities, especially the Emperor, who rules over everyone.
text: Ya Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, am de nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ nɔaniɛ, fɛmɛ bo i niɛ adamande kɛrɛ su ni,

ref: You are free, but still you are God's servants, and you must not use your freedom as an excuse for doing wrong.
text: Am ti minɛm bo am ade am tii wɔ, má am niɛ ka am anya am tii n dɛɛ ti, na am sufiɛ yo satiɛ. Ama, am ma am woo­wa ti kabo Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm woo­wa ti barasu ni.

ref: Respect everyone and show special love for God's people. Honor God and respect the Emperor. The Example of Christ's Suffering
text: Am bu sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sɔnɔ, am koro am ninbaam kɛrɛ, am soro Nyɛmɛ, na am bu Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri ni. Kabo ya sɔn Kristo wahara dii­wa n su ni

ref: Servants, you must obey your masters and always show respect to them. Do this, not only to those who are kind and thoughtful, but also to those who are cruel.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti akɔɔm ni, am de am mibiɛm nɔaniɛ, na am bu bu nahɔrɛ su, má ka bo bu ti bɛnlɛ na bu yo am sakpa ni ŋgumi, ama, haari bo bu ti tiɛ ni mmɔɔ, am bu bu.

ref: God will bless you, even if others treat you unfairly for being loyal to him.
text: Nzɛn am ayo má satiɛ be, na bu kere am wahara na am mindi deeri i, ka am soro Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, nyini ti kpa a.

ref: You don't gain anything by being punished for some wrong you have done. But God will bless you, if you have to suffer for doing something good.
text: Nzu kpɛrɛ ye am nyá i a, nzɛn am yoori satiɛ na bu boori am na am mindi deeri i? Ama, nzɛn am yoori sakpa, na bu kere am wahara, na am mindi deeri i, nyini ti kpa Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

ref: After all, God chose you to suffer as you follow in the footsteps of Christ, who set an example by suffering for you.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ afere am a, dama Kristo ni i tii adi wahara ama am, nyini kere am ka i ti ye ni ka am sɔn i su di i wahara n be.

ref: Christ did not sin or ever tell a lie.
text: Bu ŋa, yiri, “Wɔ yo má satiɛ be, wɔ kaaki abu má kɔrɛ be.”

ref: Although he was abused, he never tried to get even. And when he suffered, he made no threats. Instead, he had faith in God, who judges fairly.
text: Bu kpiɛɛri i nzukɔ ni, wɔ wɔrɔ má nzukɔ ama má bu. Bu kereeri i wahara ni, wɔ se má bu ka bu ŋú i. Ama, yiri faari i deke kɛrɛ maari Nyɛmɛ wɔ, yiri bo i di jɔrɛ bo i kɔ atin su ni.

ref: Christ carried the burden of our sins. He was nailed to the cross, so we would stop sinning and start living right. By his cuts and bruises you are healed.
text: Ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ye Kristo deeri ya jinaa­biri diiri wahara wuuri baka jaanu n su a. I yoori sɔ, adena i kere ka ya wu wɔ, fa kɔ ya satiɛ su, na ya la ŋgɔɔ bo ya koro yo sakpa. Wahara n bo Kristo diiri i ŋuniɛ nu n dɛɛ ti, ye am anya laifiɛ a.

ref: You had wandered away like sheep. Now you have returned to the one who is your shepherd and protector.
text: Am yo ti ka nbɔɛm bo ba mini wɔ, ama, kisa, am akaaki aba yiri bo i ti am sasa­fɔ na i niɛ am dika n dɔ.

ref: If you are a wife, you must put your husband first. Even if he opposes our message, you will win him over by what you do. No one else will have to say anything to him,
text: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ nbaram gusu, am de am hunm nɔaniɛ. Na nzɛn bu nbem nya yo má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n yarada, na am woo­wa ti kpa, nyini ma bu yo yarada, má ka am nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n dɛɛ ti,

ref: because he will see how you honor God and live a pure life.
text: ama, nzɛn bu ŋuuri ka am woo­wa ti casi, na am ma bu jirima, nyini ma bu kaaki bu wo na bu su Nyɛmɛ a.

ref: Don't depend on things like fancy hairdos or gold jewelry or expensive clothes to make you look beautiful.
text: Deke bo i kere ka am yo nyumi ni, i ti má kabo am yo am ŋu di am tii­ŋmiɛ suŋguruya ni, ni kabo am wura sikam, ni kabo am kara sutura kpakpam n barasu ni,

ref: Long ago those women who worshiped God and put their hope in him made themselves beautiful by putting their husbands first.
text: Dama kabo dawa saŋga, nbaram nbem bo bu ti casi na bu la tama ni Nyɛmɛ n yo yoori bu ŋu nyumi lɛɛ, bu deeri bu hunm nɔaniɛ wɔ,

ref: For example, Sarah obeyed Abraham and called him her master. You are her true children, if you do right and don't let anything frighten you.
text: kabo Saara gusu yo yoori lɛɛ, i deeri i hun Abraham nɔaniɛ wɔ, na wɔ fere i ka “M awuru­fɔ­biɛ.” Nzɛn am yoori sa bo i ti kpa na am soro má sɛrɛ, am akaaki Saara nbaam wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: If you are a husband, you should be thoughtful of your wife. Treat her with honor, because she isn't as strong as you are, and she shares with you in the gift of life. Then nothing will stand in the way of your prayers. Suffering for Doing Right
text: Ambɛrɛ nbiɛsɔm, i ti ye ni ka am gusu yo sɔ. Am si kabo am fa ŋgɔnlɛ bita am yim barasu. Am si ka nbaram faŋga ni am dɛɛ sɛ má, nyini ti, am bu am yim. Dama am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ sáŋga nya Nyɛmɛ aceeri n a, yiri la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni. Am bita bu nyunyumi, adena má deke be kanda am nyɛmɛsere. Kabo yarada­yo­fɔm diiri wahara fa kɔ sakpa dɛɛ ti

ref: Don't be hateful and insult people just because they are hateful and insult you. Instead, treat everyone with kindness. You are God's chosen ones, and he will bless you. The Scriptures say,
text: Nzɛn be yo am satiɛ, má am yo tiɛ tɔ i kaari, na nzɛn be kpiɛɛri am nzukɔ, má am wɔrɔ nzukɔ ma i, ama, kabo am yo la ka, am yo bu kisi, dama deke n bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ afere am la ka, i yó am kisi wɔ.

ref: Give up your evil ways and do right, as you find and follow the road to peace.
text: I mi yaki satiɛ­yo, na i yo sa bo i ti kpa, i mi boro i nyi na i sukpini tana­laifiɛ busu.

ref: The Lord watches over everyone who obeys him, and he listens to their prayers. But he opposes everyone who does evil.”
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ nyu­mbaa ta minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ni, na i so i sui ti bu nyɛmɛsere, ama, i koro má ka i nyu­mbaa ŋu bo bu yo satiɛ ni.”

ref: Can anyone really harm you for being eager to do good deeds?
text: Nzɛn am nyunu aboro ka am yo sa bo i ti kpa, ŋma koro yo am pui a?

ref: Even if you have to suffer for doing good things, God will bless you. So stop being afraid and don't worry about what people might do.
text: Ama, nzɛn am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n dɛɛ ti, ye am sudi wahara, am tere­fiɛ lɛɛ, má am soro bu, má am ma am ahore saaki.

ref: Honor Christ and let him be the Lord of your life. Always be ready to give an answer when someone asks you about your hope.
text: Ama, am ma Kristo yo am Mibiɛ n na am bu i am ahore su. Na am kpa am ŋu ŋminda saŋga kɛrɛ, na nzɛn be ba bisa am fa kɔ deke n bo i ti na am la tama ni, na am si kabo am bɔ i nu barasu,

ref: Give a kind and respectful answer and keep your conscience clear. This way you will make people ashamed for saying bad things about your good conduct as a follower of Christ.
text: ama, am jɔjɔ bɛnlɛ, na am bu i mi, na am si am sunsunni nu ka am ayo sakpa, adena nzɛn minɛm jɔjɔɔri am ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn fa kɔ am wo kpa n su ka am su Kristo dɛɛ ti, na bu dí nyinsɛ.

ref: You are better off to obey God and suffer for doing right than to suffer for doing wrong.
text: Dama nzɛn am yo sakpa na am di wahara na i ti Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu, i ti ye ni bo am yo satiɛ na bu kere am wahara.

ref: Christ died once for our sins. An innocent person died for those who are guilty. Christ did this to bring you to God, when his body was put to death and his spirit was made alive.
text: Ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti ye Kristo gusu diiri wahara kpe­kun n kpein a, na yiri bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n awu, yɛrɛ bo ya ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm dɛɛ ti, na i yo ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu. Bu kuunri i ni, na Nyɛmɛ ama i ŋgɔɔ awiɛwiɛ n nu,

ref: Christ then preached to the spirits that were being kept in prison.
text: na wɔ nya akɔ akan jɔrɛ akere awiɛwiɛm bo bu wo dan­saraka n nu ni.

ref: They had disobeyed God while Noah was building the boat, but God had been patient with them. Eight people went into that boat and were brought safely through the flood.
text: Dawa saŋga, bɛrɛ yo keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a, na Nyɛmɛ ayo sawɔrɛ aŋminda, saŋga bo Nowa sukpa lee bambaka n ni. Nyini lee ni, minɛ kaan sɔ wuraari nu n a, bu mɔcuɛ nyaari tii­de, ye nzue n adi má bu a.

ref: Those flood waters were like baptism that now saves you. But baptism is more than just washing your body. It means turning to God with a clear conscience, because Jesus Christ was raised from death.
text: Nyini nzue n ti deke bo i kere fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛɛ­wa bo i ma am anya tii­de nyuma n wɔ. Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛ ti má deke bo i yi am ŋuniɛ su fin, ama, i kere ka am si am sunsunni nu ka, am la má ŋgasi be Nyɛmɛ dɔ, dama Yesu Kristo teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Christ is now in heaven, where he sits at the right side of God. All angels, authorities, and powers are under his control.
text: Yiri ye wɔ kɔ aŋgoro akɔ atana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n a, ye i la yiko niɛ mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ su a, ni yiko­fɔm, ni nfaŋga­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ.

ref: Christ suffered here on earth. Now you must be ready to suffer as he did, because suffering shows that you have stopped sinning.
text: Tɔ, kabo Kristo diiri wahara i ŋuniɛ n nu ni, i ti ye ni ka am gusu kpa am ŋu bita nyini sunsunni ni, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri wahara i ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu yo má satiɛ kun.

ref: It means you have turned from your own desires and want to obey God for the rest of your life.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, má am yo am kunnu koroo­wa, ama, am yo kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni, haari am ŋgɔɔ awieeri.

ref: You have already lived long enough like people who don't know God. You were immoral and followed your evil desires. You went around drinking and partying and carrying on. In fact, you even worshiped disgusting idols.
text: Saŋga be am yo yoori am kunnu koroo­wa, deke bo minɛm bo bu si má Nyɛmɛ n yo i ni, wɔ sɛ su sɔ. Am yo tara má am ŋu, na am yo su konvikiya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm, na am nu nzan bo, na am nu nzan yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kabo nzan nu­fɔm yo ni, na am susu amɔɛm, deke bo Nyɛmɛ koro má i ni.

ref: Now your former friends wonder why you have stopped running around with them, and they curse you for it.
text: Kisa na am ni bɛrɛ saŋga wo má nyini wo ŋgbɛɛn ni, jɔrɛ n amiɛ bu wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye bu kpiɛ am nzukɔ n a.

ref: The good news has even been preached to the dead, so that after they have been judged for what they have done in this life, their spirits will live with God.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bo ba wu n gusu tiiri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kaan­wa n a, adena haari ni bo bu wuuri kabo adamande kɛrɛ wu ni, bu nyá ŋgɔɔ awiɛwiɛ n nu kabo Nyɛmɛ la ŋgɔɔ ni. Nyɛmɛ amusui­su­fɔm kpam jɔrɛ

ref: Everything will soon come to an end. So be serious and be sensible enough to pray.
text: Deke n kɛrɛ sukɔ awieeri lɛɛ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, má am fa am ŋu ji, am tara am ŋu na am koro sere Nyɛmɛ.

ref: Most important of all, you must sincerely love each other, because love wipes away many sins.
text: Sa n bo i tara deke n kɛrɛ n la ka, am koro am beŋgu ni nahɔrɛ su, dama koro kata satiɛ dɔŋgu su wɔ.

ref: Welcome people into your home and don't grumble about it.
text: Nzɛn am beŋgum aseke am wɔfɔ, am de bu, má am ŋuŋuti.

ref: Each of you has been blessed with one of God's many wonderful gifts to be used in the service of others. So use your gift well.
text: Nyɛmɛ ama am kɛrɛ aceeri barasu barasu, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka am fa nyini aceerim buka am beŋgu. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ suyo i juma nyunyumi kabo Nyɛmɛ afa ama am ni.

ref: If you have the gift of speaking, preach God's message. If you have the gift of helping others, do it with the strength that God supplies. Everything should be done in a way that will bring honor to God because of Jesus Christ, who is glorious and powerful forever. Amen. Suffering for Being a Christian
text: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ama u aceeri ka a kan i jɔrɛ, si ka Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ye a sukan i a, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ama u aceeri ka a fa buka minɛm, buka bu ni faŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama u ni, adena am sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ nu na Nyɛmɛ nya kansi, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Yiri la yiko ni jirima saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami. Kabo i ti ye ni ka Kristo susu­fɔm di wahara barasu ni

ref: Be glad for the chance to suffer as Christ suffered. It will prepare you for even greater happiness when he makes his glorious return.
text: Ama, kabo am sudi Kristo dɛɛ wahara n bo i diiri i n be ni, am ma am ahore jɔ, adena cɛɛn n bo Kristo jirima woo­wa bo i la má aŋusu n bá fite fofoe nu, am ahore jɔ́ na i yó am fiɛ.

ref: Count it a blessing when you suffer for being a Christian. This shows that God's glorious Spirit is with you.
text: Nzɛn bu kpiɛɛri am nzukɔ, ka am ti Kristo dɛɛm ti, nyini la ka am tere­fiɛ lɛɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am dɔ lɛɛ, yiri bo i la jirima woo­wa n na i la má aŋusu ni.

ref: Don't be ashamed to suffer for being a Christian. Praise God that you belong to him.
text: Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ sudi wahara ka i ti Kristo susu­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, má i mi ma nyinsɛ ti i, ama, i mi kansi Nyɛmɛ, ka i ti Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: God has already begun judging his own people. And if his judgment begins with us, imagine how terrible it will be for those who refuse to obey his message. The Scriptures say,
text: Dama saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ dí minɛm jɔrɛ. I dú mɔ bo i bu di i tiɛŋgu minɛm jɔrɛ wɔ ka. Ye nzɛn i nya du mɔ diiri yɛrɛ jɔrɛ, minɛm n bo ba ka na ba de má i labari kpa ni, bu wíe sɛ wɔ lɛɛ?

ref: Church leaders, I am writing to encourage you. I too am a leader, as well as a witness to Christ's suffering, and I will share in his glory when it is shown to us.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am wo Kristo jama n nu, na am ti nkpiɛnm ni, mini mmɔɔ ti Kristo jama n kpiɛn kun wɔ, ma ŋu kabo Kristo diiri wahara barasu ni, na m bá nya i dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be, saŋga bo i bá fite fofoe nu. Nyini ti, m susere am jande wɔ,

ref: Just as shepherds watch over their sheep, you must watch over everyone God has placed in your care. Do it willingly in order to please God, and not simply because you think you must. Let it be something you want to do, instead of something you do merely to make money.
text: am niɛ Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo am dɔ n su, kabo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ niɛ i nbɔɛm su barasu ni. Am niɛ bu dika nyunyumi, má ka bu miɛ am wɔ, ama, am koroya­nu, dama Nyɛmɛ koro ka am yo i a, má am yo i ka ŋwaa kpini dɛɛ ti, ama, am yo i ka am nyunu aboro ka am yo i wɔ.

ref: Then when Christ the Chief Shepherd returns, you will be given a crown that will never lose its glory.
text: Nzɛn am yoori sɔ, cɛɛn bo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpiɛn n bá fiteeri fofoe nu, am nyá aceeri bo i la má aŋusu na i wie má fieo ni.

ref: All of you young people should obey your elders. In fact, everyone should be humble toward everyone else. The Scriptures say, “God opposes proud people, but he helps everyone who is humble.”
text: Ambɛrɛ gusu bo am nyunu nya ti má ni, am bu nkpiɛnm ni, na am de bu nɔaniɛ. Am kɛrɛ yo am ŋu kaan na am buka am beŋgu, dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, “Nyɛmɛ kete kpirikoro­fɔm wɔ, ama, na i fa i ahɔrɔma n ma tarakam.”

ref: Be humble in the presence of God's mighty power, and he will honor you when the time comes.
text: Nyini ti, am da am ŋu asiɛŋgu fa ma Nyɛmɛ yiko­fɔ ni, na saŋga n ba juuri, yiri wúra am kpiri a.

ref: God cares for you, so turn all your worries over to him.
text: Deke kɛrɛ bo i miɛ am, am fa tu Nyɛmɛ nyunu, dama yiri cɛcɛ wo am nu a.

ref: Be on your guard and stay awake. Your enemy, the devil, is like a roaring lion, sneaking around to find someone to attack.
text: Am tara am ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, má am fa am ŋu ji. Dama am kpɔ­fɔ Sitana ni, i sansan am kabo jara sansan ŋuundi koro sɔnɔ na i kpɛsɛ i n wɔ.

ref: But you must resist the devil and stay strong in your faith. You know that all over the world the Lord's followers are suffering just as you are.
text: Am yo Nyɛmɛ yarada jina kenken na am kete Sitana, dama am si ka am ninbaam bo bu wo durunya n kɛrɛ nu n gusu sudi wahara n bo am sudi i n be n wɔ.

ref: But God shows undeserved kindness to everyone. That's why he appointed Christ Jesus to choose you to share in his eternal glory. You will suffer for a while, but God will make you complete, steady, strong, and firm.
text: Nzɛn am diiri wahara saŋga kaan su, Nyɛmɛ sɛ́sɛ am su yo am dɔŋgu a, yiri má am jina kun su a, na i wúra am faŋga, na i kpáma am. Buka kɛrɛ fite yiri Nyɛmɛ dɔ a, Kristo dɛɛ ti ye wɔ fere am ka am bá nya i dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n bo i la má awieeri n be a.

ref: God will be in control forever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: Yiri la yiko saŋga kɛrɛ a. Ami. Awieeri su dɛɛ nɔaniɛ ni

ref: Silvanus helped me write this short letter, and I consider him a faithful follower of the Lord. I wanted to encourage you and tell you how kind God really is, so you will keep on having faith in him.
text: Ya niwa Silivanusi, m si i ka i la dabuŋu am dɔ, yiri bukaari m ye ma kɛrɛ ahin kadasi kaan n sufa ma am n a, na am ahore si, na m ma am si ka jɔrɛ n bo ma kɛrɛ ama am ti Nyɛmɛ aceeri wɔ nahɔrɛ su, na am jina kenken.

ref: Greetings from the Lord's followers in Babylon. They are God's chosen ones. Mark, who is like a son to me, sends his greetings too.
text: Kristo jamam bo Nyɛmɛ asaŋga ayi am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ, na bu wo Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni m wa Marako kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. I pray that God will give peace to everyone who belongs to Christ.
text: Am bisa am beŋgu dɔŋgu ni koro. Nyɛmɛ ma ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am ti Kristo dɛɛm ni ahoreda.

ref: From Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ. To everyone who shares with us in the privilege of believing that our God and Savior Jesus Christ will do what is just and fair.
text: Mini Simon Petoro bo m ti Yesu Kristo akɔɔ na m kaaki ti i jaramasa ni, mini sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ama am ayo Kristo yarada n a, kabo wɔ ma yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ayo i yarada ni. Yesu Kristo bo i ti ya Nyɛmɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, i sakpa yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti, ye ya kɛrɛ ayo i yarada.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to you and will let you live in perfect peace! May you keep learning more and more about God and our Lord Jesus. Living as the Lord's Followers
text: Am Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ buka am agaya, na i ma am ahoreda buka su. Minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ afere bu, na wɔ yi bu n jɔrɛ

ref: We have everything we need to live a life that pleases God. It was all given to us by God's own power, when we learned he had invited us to share in his wonderful goodness.
text: Nyɛmɛ la yiko, nyini yiko n nu, wɔ ce ya deke kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka ya la i na ya nya ŋgɔɔ na ya su i nahɔrɛ su. Ya si i n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ yo nyini n kɛrɛ ama ya n a. Yiri ye wɔ yi ya ka ya nya i woo­wa kpa ni i jirima n a.

ref: God made great and marvelous promises, so his nature would become part of us. Then we could escape our evil desires and the corrupt influences of this world.
text: Nyini n dɛɛ ti, wɔ se ka i cé ya aceerim bambaka bo bu la má aŋusu, adena i yi ya fite durunya n nu satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wura minɛ konviki na i saaki bu n nu, na ya nya Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ woo­wa n be.

ref: If you keep growing in this way, it will show that what you know about our Lord Jesus Christ has made your lives useful and meaningful.
text: Nzɛn am la nyini woo­wam ni, na am woo­wa ti sɔ nahɔrɛ su saŋga kɛrɛ, nyini kere ka, kabo am asi ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ni, am woo­wa la kpɛrɛ, i ti má ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: My friends, you must do all you can to show God has really chosen and selected you. If you keep on doing this, you won't stumble and fall.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaam, kabo Nyɛmɛ afere am na wɔ yi am ni, am boro am nyi, adena am woo­wa kere ka am ti i dɛɛ minɛm. Nzɛn am yoori nyinim sɔ, am kpáasi má fieo.

ref: Then our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will give you a glorious welcome into his kingdom that will last forever.
text: Nyini má am nya aceeri bambaka bo am la atin, na am wura Yesu Kristo dɛɛ fɛmɛya woo­biri n nu a, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ na i fɛmɛya n wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

ref: You are holding firmly to the truth you were given. But I am still going to remind you of these things.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i wo m ahore su ka m suteŋge am ahin jɔrɛm su saŋga kɛrɛ n a, haari ni bo am si bu na am ade keree­wam bo bu ti nahɔrɛ n na am bita bu kenken n ni.

ref: In fact, I think I should keep on reminding you until I leave this body.
text: Mini dɔ, wɔ yo daka ka m suteŋge am su sɔ, saŋga bo m toko la ŋgɔɔ ni.

ref: And our Lord Jesus Christ has already told me that I will soon leave it behind.
text: Dama m si ka i cɛ́ má, m bá wo má bɛrɛ, kabo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo yi kereeri m ni.

ref: This is why I am doing my best to make sure that each of you remembers all of this after I am gone. The Message about the Glory of Christ
text: Nyini ma m boro m nyi yo deke kɛrɛ bo m koro yo i n a, adena saŋga bo m bá wo má bɛrɛ, na am koro teŋge nyini jɔrɛm su saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: When we told you about the power and the return of our Lord Jesus Christ, we were not telling clever stories someone had made up. But with our own eyes we saw his true greatness.
text: Ya yo kan kereeri am ka, kabo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo la yiko na i káaki ba barasu ni, bo ya kan sɔ ni, i ti má ka ya faari ŋgɔnlɛ boori ya tiɛɛnu kaanri i a, ama, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii nyu­mbaa ŋuuri kabo i la yiko bambaka barasu ni.

ref: We were there with Jesus on the holy mountain and heard this voice speak from heaven.
text: Yɛrɛ ni ya tii tiiri kabo akonvi n fite aŋgoro ni, saŋga bo ya woori i dɔ, Nyɛmɛ buka n su ni.

ref: All of this makes us even more certain that what the prophets said is true. So you should pay close attention to their message, as you would to a lamp shining in some dark place. You must keep on paying attention until daylight comes and the morning star rises in your hearts.
text: Nyini n kɛrɛ ma ya la ahoresi buka su, ka jɔrɛm bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo kaanri bu n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Nyini ti, am boro am nyi na am de kabo bu yo seeri ni, na am su i su. Nɔaniɛ n bo bu jɔjɔɔri i n ti ka fitana bo i taari awosin nu n ti n wɔ, i teke am nyi kɔ ju cɛɛn bo Kristo káaki ba ni, na i fa wein n ba teke am nyi nahɔrɛ su, kabo alecin na asaakojo tiɛ fa wein ba ni.

ref: The prophets did not think these things up on their own, but they were guided by the Spirit of God.
text: Dama má adamande koroya­nu ye bu kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a, ama, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni maari bu nɔɔ ye bu kaanri nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka bu kan i n a.

ref: Sometimes false prophets spoke to the people of Israel. False teachers will also sneak in and speak harmful lies to you. But these teachers don't really belong to the Master who paid a great price for them, and they will quickly destroy themselves.
text: Kabo minɛ kɔrɛ­fɔm woori Nyɛmɛ minɛm nu dawa saŋga ni, bɛrɛ bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, sɔ ye kere­fɔ kɔrɛ­fɔm bá wo am gusu nu a. Bu fíɛ wɔ, na bu fá bu dɛɛ keree­wam kere am, keree­wa bo bu ma am mini Nyɛmɛ atin ni, haari na bu káaki bu sin bo Mibiɛ ni, yiri bo wɔ tɔ bu tii su ŋkatɔ ni. Nyini ti, bu sa­yoo­wam má Nyɛmɛ yo bu ŋgbɛɛn anyumbaa kata ni i teke.

ref: Many people will follow their evil ways and cause others to tell lies about the true way.
text: Na minɛ dɔŋgu sɔ́n bu sa­yoo­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm su. Bɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, minɛ bá bu atin n bo i ti nahɔrɛ ni ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: They will be greedy and cheat you with smooth talk. But long ago God decided to punish them, and God doesn't sleep.
text: Nyini kere­fɔ kɔrɛ­fɔm ni, bu nyunu aboro neŋge su dɛɛ ti, bu bó bu tiɛɛnu jɔrɛ kan ma am a, na bu fá am kpini. Ama, Nyɛmɛ daafi má, wɔ du mɔ adi bu jɔrɛ, i bá yo bu ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: God did not have pity on the angels that sinned. He had them tied up and thrown into the dark pits of hell until the time of judgment.
text: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu yo yoori satiɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ ayaki má bu. I faari bu tuuri cɛɛma sin n nu wɔ, na wɔ bo bu tobari ase awosin n nu, ka bu suwo dɔ haari jina cɛɛn n bo i bá cin bu sui ni.

ref: And during Noah's time, God did not have pity on the ungodly people of the world. He destroyed them with a flood, though he did save eight people, including Noah, who preached the truth.
text: Ya si ka, dawa saŋga, minɛm bo bu soro má Nyɛmɛ ni, wɔ yaki má bu, i cin bu sui wɔ, na wɔ ma wɔmbu bambaka adi bu kɛrɛ, ama, na wɔ de Nowa tii aji, i ni minɛ nso nbem, yiri kan kereeri minɛm deke bo i ti sakpa na i kɔ atin su n a.

ref: God punished the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah by burning them to ashes, and this is a warning to anyone else who wants to sin.
text: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ yo saakiiri Sodom ni Gomora miɛm ni, i faari sin jiiri su yaraari bu wɔ, na i kɛrɛ aje atɔ nzuin. I yoori sɔ ni, i kere kabo i bá yo minɛm n bo bu soro má Nyɛmɛ n wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Lot lived right and was greatly troubled by the terrible way those wicked people were living. He was a good man, and day after day he suffered because of the evil things he saw and heard. So the Lord rescued him.
text: Nyini sɔnɔ n bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ni, yiri ni nyini minɛm ni yo saŋga wo a, cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na i suŋu sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm bo bu yo bu ni, na i sui suti jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bo bu jɔjɔ bu ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ di wahara ahoresaaki nu, yiri bo i ahore ti fofoe ni.

ref: This shows that the Lord knows how to rescue godly people from their sufferings and to punish evil people while they wait for the day of judgment.
text: Tɔ, nyini n kɛrɛ kere ka Nyɛmɛ si kabo i yó na i dé minɛm bo bu su i atin su n fite kekereke nu a. Na i si kabo i sucin minɛm bo bu su má i atin su n sui, kɔ ju saŋga bo i bá di bu jɔrɛ ni.

ref: The Lord is especially hard on people who disobey him and don't think of anything except their own filthy desires. They are reckless and proud and are not afraid of cursing the glorious beings in heaven.
text: Bɛrɛ n bo i cín bu sui tara minɛm kɛrɛ n la bo bu su aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa ni daŋgba sam su, na bu kete ka má Nyɛmɛ niɛ bu su ni. Nyini minɛm ni, bu tii ti kekereke, bu soro má be. Haari ŋgɔɔ­fɔm bo bu wo aŋgoro na bu la jirima woo­wa ni, bu kpiɛ bu nzukɔ ɔ, bu soro má sɛrɛ.

ref: Although angels are more powerful than these evil beings, even the angels don't dare to accuse them to the Lord.
text: Haari Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu la yiko ni faŋga tara nyini minɛm ni, bu kpiɛ má nyini ŋgɔɔ­fɔm ni nzukɔ na bu jaraki má bu kere má Mikun ni.

ref: These people are no better than senseless animals that live by their feelings and are born to be caught and killed. They speak evil of things they don't know anything about. But their own corrupt deeds will destroy them.
text: Nyini minɛm sɔ, kabo bu koro, ye bu yo a, bu ti ka boro neŋge tiɛm bo minɛ tara bu na bu kpiɛ bu kɔm n wɔ. Bu kpiɛ nzukɔ fa kɔ dekem n bo bu si má bu n su wɔ. Kabo minɛ ma boro neŋge tiɛm ka ŋgbɛɛn ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ má bu gusu bá ka ŋgbɛɛn a.

ref: They have done evil, and they will be rewarded with evil. They think it is fun to have wild parties during the day. They are immoral, and the meals they eat with you are spoiled by the shameful and selfish way they carry on.
text: Kabo bu ma minɛm di wahara ni, bɛrɛ gusu bá di wahara sɔ ɔ. Bu konvikiya sam bo bu yo bu ni, i yo bu fiɛ wɔ, bu yo bu wiɛsu wɔ, bu fiɛ má su. Bu ti daŋgba­fɔm wɔ, na bu wura am nyinsɛ saŋga bo am ni bɛrɛ tiɛn am nɔɔ sudi diire ni, bu sunsunni kɛrɛ la ka bu daka am.

ref: All they think about is having sex with someone else's husband or wife. There is no end to their wicked deeds. They trick people who are easily fooled, and their minds are filled with greedy thoughts. But they are headed for trouble!
text: Bu nyunu boro minɛ yim kpini su wɔ, na bu kunnu yi má bu satiɛ­yo. Bu dadaka minɛm bo bu nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin n nyunyumi n wɔ, na bu yaki i, adena bu su bu dɛɛ keree­wam n su. Bu ahore­mbaa abi neŋge kpini su wɔ. Nyɛmɛ ayo bu ŋgbɛɛn awie lɛɛ.

ref: They have left the true road and have gone down the wrong path by following the example of the prophet Balaam. He was the son of Beor and loved what he got from being a crook.
text: Ba yaki atin n bo i ti kpa ni, ba mini. Atin n bo Baalam bo i si la Bɛori n yo bitaari i ni, ba sɔn i su lɛɛ. I yo koroori ŋwaa bo minɛm ma i n wɔ, na i yo satiɛ.

ref: These people are like dried up water holes and clouds blown by a windstorm. The darkest part of hell is waiting for them.
text: Nyini minɛm ti ka bura bo i tiɛ má nzue wɔ, na bu kaaki ti ka nzue munaa­wa bo aŋuma fa i sin n wɔ. Bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ awosin n bo i la má aŋusu ase bu n a.

ref: They brag out loud about their stupid nonsense. And by being vulgar and crude, they trap people who have barely escaped from living the wrong kind of life.
text: Bu fɔ yanfɔya a, na bu sujɔjɔ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, na bu fa aŋuniɛ koroo­wa na bu fa dadaka minɛm bo ba kaba afite kɔrɛ jɔrɛm nu na ba nya acɛ má ni.

ref: When they learned about our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they escaped from the filthy things of this world. But they are again caught up and controlled by these filthy things, and now they are in worse shape than they were at first.
text: Minɛm bo bu si Yesu Kristo ni, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, na i ma ba yaki durunya n nu dɛɛ daŋgba sam ni, na ba kaaki akpie bu nyi ama nyini sam atara bu akunma bu bekun ni, bu awieeri ti tiɛ tara bumboori ni.

ref: They would have been better off if they had never known about the right way. Even after they knew what was right, they turned their backs on the holy commandments they were given.
text: Nzɛn má ba si má woo­wa bo i kɔ atin su ni, má i ti ye tara bo ba kaaki bu sin abo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo ba du mɔ ade i ni.

ref: What happened to them is just like the true saying, “A dog will come back to lick up its own vomit. A pig that has been washed will roll in the mud.”
text: Kabo bu yoori sɔ ni, nyini kere ka ahin anyundɛrɛm ni ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo bu ŋa, “Caa kaaki fa deke n bo wɔ fi i n di bekun wɔ,” na bekun, “Nzɛn a taraari afa biɛɛri i nzue, i kaaki kɔ wura natiɛ nu wɔ bekun.”

ref: what God's prophets said would happen. You must never forget what the holy prophets taught in the past. And you must remember what the apostles told you our Lord and Savior has commanded us to do.
text: M koro ka am teŋge su fa kɔ nɔaniɛm bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo seeri bu dawa saŋga ni, na am kaaki teŋge su fa kɔ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Mibiɛ n bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ n kaanri i, na i jaramasam seeri am ni.

ref: But first you must realize that in the last days some people won't think about anything except their own selfish desires. They will make fun of you
text: Jɔrɛ bo i tara bu kɛrɛ na am si i n la ka, fa kɔ durunya n awieeri su, minɛ nbem bá ba, na bu susu aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa su, na bu yó am anzasi fa kɔ am yarada n su,

ref: and say, “Didn't your Lord promise to come back? Yet the first leaders have already died, and the world hasn't changed a bit.”
text: na bu sé ka, “I seeri ka i káaki ba bekun, ye i wo ni? Ya nam wo má bɛrɛ kun, ama, deke n kɛrɛ toko ti i tii wɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya n ba ju ni nyuma.”

ref: They will say this because they want to forget that long ago the heavens and the earth were made at God's command. The earth came out of water and was made from water.
text: Bu si tenle su, ama, ba kete ka bu teŋge má su ka, dada saŋga, Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔɔ jɔjɔɔri ye aŋgoro wo bɛrɛ a, na wɔ jɔjɔ na wɔ kpaaki nzue n ni asiɛn n nu, na asiɛn n afite nzue n nu.

ref: Later it was destroyed by the waters of a mighty flood.
text: Nyini nzue n ye i fa saakiiri durunya n a dawa saŋga, saŋga bo wɔmbu nzue n diiri deke n kɛrɛ ni.

ref: The Lord isn't slow about keeping his promises, as some people think he is. In fact, God is patient, because he wants everyone to turn from sin and no one to be lost.
text: Kabo Micɛra n seeri ka i yo ni, minɛ nbem bita kan ka i ti niniki. I ti má ka i ti niniki wɔ, ama, i la mindi ni ambɛrɛ wɔ, i koro má ka be ka ŋgbɛɛn, ama, i koro ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kaaki i wo wɔ.

ref: The day of the Lord's return will surprise us like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a loud noise, and the heat will melt the whole universe. Then the earth and everything on it will be seen for what they are.
text: Ama, Micɛra n cɛɛn ni, i bá kabo awiefɔ ba fura nu na i wɔ n wɔ. Nyini cɛɛn ni, aŋgoro ni yó naŋmiɛ faŋga su, na i busu míni, na sin yára deke kɛrɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni, na i ká ŋgbɛɛn. Na asiɛn n ni deke kɛrɛ bo i wo asiɛn n su n bá wo má bɛrɛ.

ref: You should look forward to the day when God judges everyone, and you should try to make it come soon. On that day the heavens will be destroyed by fire, and everything else will melt in the heat.
text: na am ŋminda fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ cɛɛn ni, na am boro am nyi yo sa bo i ma i ba ndende. Nyini cɛɛn n ye sin yára aŋgoro n kɛrɛ na i ká ŋgbɛɛn n a, na i kɛkɛrɛ má deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n koro.

ref: But God has promised us a new heaven and a new earth, where justice will rule. We are really looking forward to this!
text: Ama, ya ŋminda sa bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i bá yo ni, ka, i yí aŋgoro ni asiɛn fɔfɔrɛ, dɔ ye bu nati yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ŋgumi a.

ref: Don't forget that the Lord is patient because he wants people to be saved. This is also what our dear friend Paul said when he wrote you with the wisdom God had given him.
text: Am teŋge su ka ya Mibiɛ mindi n dɛɛ ti, ye minɛ nyá tii­de a. Sɔ ye ya niwa koroo­wa Pɔɔlu yo kɛrɛ maari am a, Nyɛmɛ ceeri i alecira n a.

ref: My dear friends, you have been warned ahead of time! So don't let the errors of evil people lead you down the wrong path and make you lose your balance.
text: M danfo kpam, ambɛrɛ, am du mɔ si nyini ni. Nyini ti, am si am ŋu sa, adena má am sɔn minɛm bo bu bu má be n su, na am mini atin n bo am su i su ni.

ref: Let the gift of undeserved grace and the understanding that come from our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ help you keep on growing. Praise Jesus now and forever! Amen.
text: Ama, am kɔ su, na am subita Yesu Kristo dɛɛ atin ni, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, adena am koro di i ahɔrɔma, na am si i buka su. Yiri Yesu Kristo ni, i ti ye ni ka minɛm kɛrɛ wura i kpiri, kisa ni saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

ref: The one who gives life appeared! We saw it happen, and we are witnesses to what we have seen. Now we are telling you about this eternal life that was with the Father and appeared to us.
text: Nyini nɔaniɛ n la Yesu bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni, i yo fiteeri fofoe nu wɔ, na ya nyu­mbaa aŋu i, nyini ti ye ya di ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n daani kan i kere am n a. Yiri bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n yo woori i Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ye wɔ yi i ŋu akere ya a.

ref: We are telling you what we have seen and heard, so you may share in this life with us. And we share in it with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text: Deke n bo ya ŋu i, na ya ti i ni, ye ya sukan i kere am n a, adena ya ni ambɛrɛ la wo fiɛ ni ya beŋgu, na ya kɛrɛ la wo fiɛ ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni i Wa Yesu Kristo ni.

ref: We are writing to tell you these things, because this makes us truly happy. God Is Light
text: Deke n bo i ti na ya sukɛrɛ ahin dekem ma am n la ka, adena ya nya ahorejɔ nahɔrɛ su. Kabo i ti ye ni ka ya bita wein atin ni

ref: Jesus told us God is light and doesn't have any darkness in him. Now we are telling you.
text: Ahin labari n bo ya yo tiiri i Nyɛmɛ Wa n dɔ n lɛɛ, ye ya sukan kere am n a, ka Nyɛmɛ ti wein wɔ, na awosin haari kaan sɔ wo má i dɔ.

ref: If we say we share in life with God and keep on living in the dark, we are lying and are not living by the truth.
text: Nzɛn ya ŋa ya ni yiri la wo fiɛ ni ya beŋgu, ama, na ya toko wo awosin nu yo satiɛ, ya ti kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ, ya bita má nahɔrɛ.

ref: But if we live in the light, as God does, we share in life with each other. And the blood of his Son Jesus washes all our sins away.
text: Ama, nzɛn ya bita wein atin kabo yiri Nyɛmɛ atin n ti wein ni, ya ni yiri la wo fiɛ ni ya beŋgu lɛɛ, na i Wa Yesu mbunja ni kpakpara yi ya satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo ya ŋu ni. Nyini ti, am ma ya bita wein n atin ni.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we are fooling ourselves, and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Nzɛn ya ŋa ya ti má satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ya dadaka ya ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, na nahɔrɛ wo má ya ahore su.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we make God a liar, and his message isn't in our hearts.
text: Nzɛn ya ŋa ya yo má satiɛ, ya yi Nyɛmɛ kɔrɛ­fɔ lɛɛ, na i nɔaniɛ n wo má ya ahore su lɛɛ.

ref: My children, I am writing this so you won't sin. But if you do sin, Jesus Christ always does the right thing, and he will speak to the Father for us.
text: M nbaam, m koro má ka am yo satiɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛ n ma am n a, ama, nzɛn am be yoori satiɛ, ya la sɔnɔ be bo i yo ya ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu, yiri la Yesu Kristo bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

ref: When we obey God, we are sure we know him.
text: Am ma ya de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, nyini kere ka ya si yiri Nyɛmɛ lɛɛ.

ref: But if we claim to know him and don't obey him, we are lying and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa, i si Nyɛmɛ, na i de má i nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, i mi ti kɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ, na nahɔrɛ wo má i ahore su.

ref: My dear friends, I am not writing to give you a new commandment. It is the same one you were first given, and it is the message you heard.
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ ye m sukɛrɛ ma am n a, ama, nɔaniɛ maa­wa dawa n bo am du mɔ tiiri i n a. Nyini nɔaniɛ maa­wa dawa n la jɔrɛ n bo am yo tiiri i saŋga bo am boori i bu suuri Yesu su ni.

ref: But it really is a new commandment, and you know its true meaning, just as Christ does. You can see the darkness fading away and the true light already shining.
text: Tɔ, ama, nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, i kaaki ti nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ wɔ bekun, na ya ŋu ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, fite kabo Yesu Kristo wo ti ni, ni kabo am wo ti ni, dama awosin n susin, ye wein nahɔrɛ su wo ta su.

ref: If we claim to be in the light and hate someone, we are still in the dark.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i wo wein n nu, na i kpɔ i niwa, i mi toko wo awosin nu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: But if we love others, we are in the light, and we don't cause problems for them.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i niwa wo wein n nu wɔ, i yo má sa be bo i ma i niwa bo i jaa kpaasi.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because your sins have been forgiven in the name of Christ.
text: M nbaam, deke bo i ti na m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma am n la ka, Nyɛmɛ afa am satiɛ ace am, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

ref: Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you have defeated the evil one.
text: Sim, deke bo i ti na m sukɛrɛ ma ambɛrɛ n la ka, am si Yesu, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ kana Nyɛmɛ ba yiiri durunya n kɛrɛ ni. Ngbɛfɛnɛm, deke bo i ti na m sukɛrɛ ma ambɛrɛ n la ka, am akunma Sitana n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because you have known the Father. Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you are strong. God's message is firm in your hearts, and you have defeated the evil one.
text: M nbaam, deke bo i ti na m toko kɛrɛ ma am n la ka, am si ya Si Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti. Sim, deke bo i ti na m toko kɛrɛ ma am n la ka, am si Yesu bo i wo bɛrɛ durunya n bumboori n kɛrɛ ti. Ngbɛfɛnɛm, deke bo i ti na m toko kɛrɛ ma am n la ka, am yarada n ti faŋga, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wo am ahore su, na am akunma Sitana n dɛɛ ti.

ref: Don't love the world or anything that belongs to the world. If you love the world, you cannot love the Father.
text: Tɔ, má am koro durunya n ni, ni deke n kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro durunya ni, i mi koro má ya Si Nyɛmɛ lɛɛ.

ref: Our foolish pride comes from this world, and so do our selfish desires and our desire to have everything we see. None of this comes from the Father.
text: Dama deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo durunya n nu fɛn na i wura sɔnɔ konvi, ni deke bo sɔnɔ nyu­mbaa ŋu i na i koro i, ni deke bo sɔnɔ nya i na i fa fɔ, i fite má ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, i kɛrɛ fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ.

ref: The world and the desires it causes are disappearing. But if we obey God, we will live forever. The Enemy of Christ
text: Durunya n ni deke kɛrɛ bo i wura minɛ konvi kɛrɛ la awieeri, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo Nyɛmɛ sa koroo­wa, i nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri. Kabo ya si ya ŋu sa ni Kristo kpɔ­fɔ n ni

ref: Children, this is the last hour. You heard that the enemy of Christ would appear at this time, and many of Christ's enemies have already appeared. So we know the last hour is here.
