text: M nbaam, durunya ni sukɔ awieeri lɛɛ. Am yo tiiri ka nzɛn durunya n ba sukɔ awieeri, Kristo kpɔ­fɔ ni bá, ama, kisa Kristo kpɔ­fɔm dɔŋgu ayi bu ŋu fofoe nu. Nyini ti, ya si ka durunya ni sukɔ awieeri lɛɛ.

ref: These people came from our own group, yet they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But they left, which proves they did not belong to our group.
text: Nyini minɛm n bo bu ti Kristo kpɔ­fɔm na ba fite ya nu ni, nahɔrɛ su, bu ti má ya minɛm. Nzɛn má bu ti ya minɛm, má bu ka ya dɔ ɔ. Ama, ba fite ya nu, nyini ye ya si ka, bu be mmɔɔ ti má ya minɛm a.

ref: Christ, the Holy One, has blessed you, and now all of you understand.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, Kristo n afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura am ahore su, na am si deke bo i ti nahɔrɛ ni.

ref: I did not need to write you about the truth, since you already know it. You also know that liars do not belong to the truth.
text: Má ka am si má nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye m sukɛrɛ ma am n a, ama, am si i, na am si ka kɔrɛ koro yo má nahɔrɛ.

ref: And a liar is anyone who says Jesus isn't truly Christ. Anyone who says this is an enemy of Christ and rejects both the Father and the Son.
text: Ŋma ti kɔrɛ­fɔm kɛrɛ tii n a? Kɔrɛ­fɔ n la sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa Yesu ti má Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i ni, yiri n bo i yo sɔ n akete ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni i Wa n lɛɛ, i mi ni ti Kristo kpɔ­fɔ n a.

ref: If we reject the Son, we reject the Father. But if we say we accept the Son, we have the Father.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, akete i Si Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ lɛɛ. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa Kristo ti Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, i mi n ade i Si Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ lɛɛ.

ref: I am writing to warn you about those people who are misleading you.
text: M sukɛrɛ ahin nɔaniɛ n ma am, na am si am ŋu sa fa kɔ minɛm bo bu koro ka bu dadaka am ni.

ref: But Christ has blessed you with the Holy Spirit. Now the Spirit stays in you, and you don't need any teachers. The Spirit is truthful and teaches you everything. So stay one in your heart with Christ, just as the Spirit has taught you to do. Children of God
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo Kristo afa ama am wo am dɔ. Nyini ti, i ti má ye ni ka be fa keree­wa be kere am kun, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni kere am deke kɛrɛ a, na deke kɛrɛ bo i kere am ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, kɔrɛ wo má nu. Nyini dɛɛ ti, am wo ni Kristo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n akere am ni.

ref: Children, stay one in your hearts with Christ. Then when he returns, we will have confidence and won't have to hide in shame.
text: M nbaam, kisa am wo ni Kristo, na cɛɛn n bo i ba yiiri i ŋu fiteeri fofoe nu n na ya la ahoresi, na má nyinsɛ ti ya.

ref: Think how much the Father loves us. He loves us so much that he lets us be called his children, as we truly are. But since the people of this world did not know who Christ is, they don't know who we are.
text: Am niɛ koro bo ya Si Nyɛmɛ fa koro ya barasu a. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i fere ya ka i nbaam n a, i ti nahɔrɛ ka ya ti i nbaam wɔ. Durunya n nu­fɔm si má ya Si Nyɛmɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bu si má ka ya ti i nbaam n a.

ref: My dear friends, we are already God's children, though what we will be hasn't yet been seen. But we do know when Christ returns, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is.
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, kisa ya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nya yi kere má ya kabo ya bá ti barasu, ama, ya si ka Yesu bá baari, ya tí kabo i ti n wɔ, dama ya ŋú i kabo i ti barasu.

ref: Everyone who sins breaks God's law, because sin is the same as breaking God's law.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kete­fɔ ɔ, dama satiɛ­yo ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kete wɔ.

ref: and people who stay one in their hearts with him won't keep on sinning. If they do keep on sinning, they don't know Christ, and they have never seen him.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo ni Yesu, koro suyo má satiɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i toko yo satiɛ, nyini kere ka, wɔ nya ŋu má i lɛɛ, na i mi kaaki si má i.

ref: Anyone who keeps on sinning belongs to the devil. He has sinned from the beginning, but the Son of God came to destroy all that he has done.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ, i mi ti Sitana dɛɛ sɔnɔ ɔ, dama fite durunya n bumboori n kɛrɛ ye Sitana n yoori satiɛ a. Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ Wa n baari durunya n nu la ka, i ba yo Sitana n juma ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: God's children cannot keep on being sinful. His life-giving power lives in them and makes them his children, so they cannot keep on sinning.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ wa koro suyo má satiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ woo­wa n wo i mi dɔ, na Nyɛmɛ ti i Si n dɛɛ ti, i koro suyo má satiɛ.

ref: You can tell God's children from the devil's children, because those who belong to the devil refuse to do right or to love each other. Love Each Other
text: Deke bo ya fa si minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam, ni bo bu ti Sitana n nbaam n la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo má sakpa, wara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i niwa, i mi ti má Nyɛmɛ wa. Kabo ya koro ya beŋgu barasu ni

ref: Don't be like Cain, who belonged to the devil and murdered his own brother. Why did he murder him? He did it because his brother was good, and he was evil.
text: Má am ma ya yo ka Keen, bo i yo ti Sitana dɛɛ wa ni, na i kuunri i niɛma ni. Nzu ti ye i kuunri i niɛma n a? I sa­yoo­wa ti má kpa n dɛɛ ti, na i niɛma n dɛɛ n ti kpa n ti.

ref: My friends, don't be surprised if the people of this world hate you.
text: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, nzɛn durunya n nu­fɔm kpɔ am ka am yo sakpa n dɛɛ ti, má am ma i yo am alekutura.

ref: Our love for each other proves we have gone from death to life. But if you don't love each other, you are still under the power of death.
text: Kabo yɛrɛ koro ya ninbaam n ti, nyini ma ya si ka ya fite yiwee n nu anya ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i niwa toko wo yiwee nu wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: If you hate each other, you are murderers, and we know murderers do not have eternal life.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kpɔ i niwa ti sɔnɔ kun­fɔ ɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Am si ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kun sɔnɔ la má ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: We know what love is because Jesus gave his life for us. This is why we must give our lives for each other.
text: Yesu deeri ya dɛɛ jinaa­biri wɔ, na wɔ tɔ i ŋgɔɔ awu ama ya, nyini ma ya fa si kabo koro ti barasu a. Nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka ya gusu tɔ ya ŋgɔɔ ma ya ninbaam, haari nzɛn i yo yiwee mmɔɔ.

ref: If we have all we need and see one of our own people in need, we must have pity on that person, or else we cannot say we love God.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la neŋge, na i ŋu ka i niwa wo yaari nu, na i si má i aŋunfɔ ka i buka i, Nyɛmɛ koro wo má i mi n ahore su lɛɛ.

ref: Children, you show love for others by truly helping them, and not merely by talking about it.
text: M nbaam, má am ma ya jɔjɔɔ­wa n ŋgumi kere ka ya la koro, ama, am ma ya sa­yoo­wa kere ka ya koro ya ninbaam nahɔrɛ su.

ref: When we love others, we know we belong to the truth, and we feel at ease in the presence of God.
text: Nyini ma ya fa si ka ya su nahɔrɛ n su a, na i ma ya la ahoresi Nyɛmɛ dɔ a,

ref: But even if we don't feel at ease, God is greater than our feelings, and he knows everything.
text: na nzɛn ya sunsunni maari ya ka ya yo satiɛ, ya si ka Nyɛmɛ tara ya sunsunni ni, na i kaaki si deke n kɛrɛ.

ref: Dear friends, if we feel at ease in the presence of God, we will have the courage to come near him.
text: Nyini ti, m ninbaa koroo­wam, nzɛn ya sunsunni ase má ya ka ya yo satiɛ, ya la ahoresi Nyɛmɛ dɔ lɛɛ.

ref: He will give us whatever we ask, because we obey him and do what pleases him.
text: Na deke kɛrɛ bo ya sereeri i, ya nya i wɔ, dama ya de nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari ya ni, na ya yo sa bo i sun i nu.

ref: God wants us to have faith in his Son Jesus Christ and to love each other. This is also what Jesus taught us to do.
text: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari ya n la ka, ya yo i Wa Yesu Kristo yarada, na ya koro ya ninbaam ni, kabo wɔ se ya ni.

ref: If we obey God's commandments, we will stay one in our hearts with him, and he will stay one with us. The Spirit he has given us is proof that we are one with him.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n wo ni Nyɛmɛ, na Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ wo ni i mi ni. Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo wɔ fa ama ya ni, ma ya si ka Nyɛmɛ wo ni yɛrɛ a.

ref: Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world,
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má am yo minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ŋa Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n wo bu dɔ n yarada, ama, am yo bu na am niɛ ka bu keree­wa n fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ, nzɛn i fite má. Dama kɔrɛ­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n asandi abara ayɛ durunya n nu.

ref: But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri ka, Yesu akaaki má adamande ni, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n wo má i mi dɔ, ama, i dɛɛ keree­wa n fite Kristo kpɔ­fɔ n dɔ ɔ. Yiri ye am yo tiiri ka i bá durunya n nu, ye kisa wɔ ba n a.

ref: Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world.
text: M nbaam, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm wɔ, ye am akunma nyini kɔrɛ­fɔm bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan­fɔm ni, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i wo am nu n la yiko tara Sitana n bo i wo durunya n nu­fɔm nu ni.

ref: We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
text: Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm wɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i si Nyɛmɛ de kabo ya kere n wɔ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti má Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ de má kabo ya kere ni. Nyini ye ya fa si minɛm bo bu la Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n bo i kere nahɔrɛ n a, ni bo bu bu kɔrɛ n fite Sitana dɔ n a. Ya koro ya beŋgu kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ya ni

ref: My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him.
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, am ma ya koro ya niwa, dama koro fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i beŋgu ti Nyɛmɛ wa a, na i mi si Nyɛmɛ a.

ref: God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him.
text: Nyɛmɛ ti koro wɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i beŋgu si má Nyɛmɛ.

ref: God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life.
text: Kabo Nyɛmɛ kereeri i koro fa kɔ ya dɔ n la ka o, wɔ sunma i kunnu Wa kun n kpein durunya n nu, adena yiri dɛɛ ti na ya nya ŋgɔɔ.

ref: Real love isn't our love for God, but his love for us. God sent his Son to be the sacrifice by which our sins are forgiven.
text: Kabo koro ti lɛɛ, má ka yɛrɛ koroori Nyɛmɛ a, ama, yiri koroori ya a, na wɔ sunma i Wa, na wɔ de ya jinaa­biri na wɔ fa i ŋu ayo tiɛ afa ama Nyɛmɛ, adena Nyɛmɛ fa ya satiɛ ce ya.

ref: Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ya sɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya gusu koro ya beŋgu.

ref: No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
text: Be nya ŋu má Nyɛmɛ, ama, nzɛn ya koroori ya beŋgu, Nyɛmɛ wo ya dɔ lɛɛ, na nyini ma i dɛɛ koro n wo ya dɔ na i ti kabo i koro ka i ti n a.

ref: God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us.
text: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ni, nyini ma ya si ka ya wo ni yiri a, na i gusu wo ni yɛrɛ a.

ref: God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him.
text: Na yɛrɛ aŋu kabo ya Si Nyɛmɛ n asunma i Wa n ka i ba yo durunya n nu­fɔm Tii­de­fɔ, ye ya di i daani a.

ref: God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tekeeri i nɔɔ seeri ka Yesu ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, Nyɛmɛ wo i mi n dɔ, na i mi n gusu wo ni Nyɛmɛ lɛɛ.

ref: and are sure God loves us. God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us.
text: Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii si kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ya barasu, ye ya yo yarada ka i koro ya. Nyɛmɛ ti koro wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i beŋgu ni, i mi wo ni Nyɛmɛ, na Nyɛmɛ wo ni yiri.

ref: A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro wo i dɔ soro má sɛrɛ, dama koro bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, i fɔn yi sɛrɛ wɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i soro sɛrɛ, Nyɛmɛ koro wo má i dɔ kabo má Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, dama sɛrɛ ni sui­cin nati a.

ref: But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see?
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i koro Nyɛmɛ, na i koro má i ninbaam ni, i mi ti kɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ. Nzɛn i koro má i niwa bo i nyu­mbaa ŋu i ni, i yó sɛ koro Nyɛmɛ bo i nyu­mbaa ŋu má i n a?

ref: The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
text: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Yesu afa ama ya n la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro Nyɛmɛ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi koro i ninbaam gusu.

ref: If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yarada ka Yesu ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ asunma i ni, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ wa a, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koroori baa n wu­fɔ ni, i koro baa n gusu.

ref: We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
text: Nzɛn ya deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, nyini kere ka ya koro i a. I nɔaniɛ maa­wam n gusu ti má kekereke,

ref: Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory.
text: dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ wa kunma durunya n wɔ. Deke bo ya fa kunma durunya n la kabo ya yo Yesu yarada n wɔ.

ref: No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God. Who Jesus Is
text: Ŋma koro kunma durunya n a? Bo i koro kunma durunya n la sɔnɔ bo wɔ yo yarada ka Yesu ti Nyɛmɛ Wa ni. Daani dii­wa fa kɔ kabo Yesu Kristo ti Nyɛmɛ Wa ni

ref: In fact, there are three who tell about it.
text: Deke nsan di i daani a.

ref: We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son.
text: Sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ ni i tii adi i tiɛŋgu Wa n daani a. Ya de minɛm daani dii­wa, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka ya de Nyɛmɛ daani dii­wa, dama i daani dii­wa n la faŋga tara minɛm dɛɛ.

ref: If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori Nyɛmɛ Wa n yarada, i mi la daani di­fɔ, yiri la Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i wo i ahore su ni, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo má i yarada ayi Nyɛmɛ kɔrɛ­fɔ lɛɛ, dama wɔ yo má daani n bo Nyɛmɛ adi i fa kɔ i Wa n su yarada.

ref: God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son.
text: Nyɛmɛ daani dii­wa n la ka o, wɔ ma ya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, nyini ŋgɔɔ n wo i Wa n dɔ ɔ.

ref: And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life. Knowing about Eternal Life
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ni Nyɛmɛ Wa n ayo kun, i mi la nyini ŋgɔɔ ni, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de má Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, i la má ŋgɔɔ. Ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n jɔrɛ

ref: All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life.
text: M koro ka ambɛrɛ bo am ayo Nyɛmɛ Wa n yarada n si ka am la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma kɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ama am n a.

ref: We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him.
text: Ya la ahoresi ni Nyɛmɛ, ka, deke kɛrɛ bo ya sereeri i, na i ti i dɛɛ koroo­wa, i de ya sere wɔ.

ref: And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
text: Ya si ka deke kɛrɛ bo ya sereeri i, na i de ya sere, ya si ka wɔ yo i ama ya lɛɛ.

ref: Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋu ka i niwa be ayo satiɛ be bo i kpaaki má i ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn fieo, i mi sére Nyɛmɛ ma yiri n bo wɔ yo satiɛ ni, na Nyɛmɛ má i ŋgɔɔ. Ama, satiɛ be wo bɛrɛ bo i kpaaki sɔnɔ ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn fieo, m suse má ka am sere Nyɛmɛ ma minɛm bo bu yo nyini satiɛ n wɔ.

ref: We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
text: Ya si ka minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam koro suyo má satiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ Wa ni ŋminda i a, na Sitana koro yo má i mi pui.

ref: We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
text: Ya si ka durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ wo Sitana n saa nu wɔ, ama, yɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛm wɔ.

ref: We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
text: Ye ya kaaki si ka Nyɛmɛ Wa n aba na wɔ ba ateke ya nyi wɔ, adena ya si yiri n bo i ti Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su ni. Ye ya wo ni Nyɛmɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n ni i wa Yesu Kristo ni. Yiri ti Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su n a, na i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.

ref: Children, you must stay away from idols.
text: Tɔ, m nbaam, am tara am ŋu, na má am su amɔɛm.

ref: From the church leader. To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth.
text: Mini bo m ti am kpiɛn ni, sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛ n fa ma wɔrɔ bara bo Nyɛmɛ ayi u, ni u nbaam n a. M koro am nahɔrɛ su, má mini ŋgumi koro am a, ama, bo bu si nahɔrɛ atin n kɛrɛ koro am wɔ,

ref: We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
text: dama nahɔrɛ n wo ya dɔ ɔ, na i bá wo ya dɔ ɔ, saŋga kɛrɛ bo i la má awieeri.

ref: I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love. Truth and Love
text: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni i Wa Yesu Kristo buka ya, na bu si ya aŋunfɔ, na bu ma ya ahoreda, na ya su nahɔrɛ atin su ni koro. Am koro am beŋgu na am si am ŋu sa ni dadaka­fɔm ni

ref: I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do.
text: Ma ŋu ka am nbaam nbem bita nahɔrɛ n kabo ya Si Nyɛmɛ afa i nɔaniɛ n ama ya ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo m fiɛ agaya.

ref: Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do.
text: Kisa, bara, m susere am wɔ, ka am ma ya koro ya beŋgu. Ahin ti má nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ ye m sukɛrɛ ma am n a, ama, i ti nɔaniɛ maa­wa kun n bo ya tiiri i saŋga bo ya boori i bu suuri Yesu su n wɔ.

ref: Love means we do what God tells us. And from the beginning, he told you to love him.
text: Koro n bo m sujɔjɔ i jɔrɛ n la ka, ya wo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kere ni. Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo am du mɔ tiiri i n la ka, am koro am beŋgu.

ref: Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ.
text: Dadaka­fɔm beberebe asandi abara ayɛ durunya n nu, bu ŋa Yesu Kristo akaaki má adamande aba má durunya n nu. Bɛrɛ ti dadaka­fɔm wɔ, na bu ti Kristo kpɔ­fɔm.

ref: So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward.
text: Nyini ti, am si am ŋu sa, na má deke n bo ya yi anyum­boro afa akere am n ka ŋgbɛɛn, ama, na am ba nya i kɛrɛ ŋkatɔ.

ref: Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su má Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa n su, na i kere i dɛɛ keree­wa kɔ sin nyini ŋu, Nyɛmɛ wo má i mi dɔ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa n su, ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni i Wa n wo i mi n dɔ lɛɛ.

ref: If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them.
text: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i baari am dɔ, na i bita má Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa ni, má am ma i mi wura am dika, má am yo i ansɛ ni ba mmɔɔ.

ref: I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
text: Má m la jɔrɛ beberebe bo má m se am, ama, m koro má ka m kɛrɛ i kɛrɛ wura ahin kadasi n nu, ama, m la tama ka m bá niɛ am busu, na ya ŋú ya ŋu, na ya bó anzama, bo ya ahore jɔ ni nahɔrɛ su.

ref: From the church leader. To my dear friend Gaius. I love you because we follow the truth,
text: Mini bo m ti u kpiɛn ni, sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛ n fa ma wɔrɔ m dɔn Gayosi bo m koro u nahɔrɛ su n a.

ref: dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit.
text: M dɔn koroo­wa, m nyɛmɛsere la ka, Nyɛmɛ ma u aŋuniɛ­mbaa nu laifiɛ, na u deke kɛrɛ kɔ atin su nyunyumi, kabo u nyɛmɛsu ni sukɔ atin su ni.

ref: It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth.
text: Dama ya ninbaa nbem baari fɛn, na ba se m kabo a bita nahɔrɛ ni, na wɔ yo m fiɛ agaya. M si ka a su nahɔrɛ atin n su wɔ.

ref: Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before.
text: M dɔn koroo­wa, a bita nahɔrɛ kabo a nati yo sakpa ma u ninbaam bo bu ba u dɔ wɔfɔ ni, haari ni bo a si má bu ni mmɔɔ.

ref: They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve.
text: Bɛrɛ bo a buka bu ni, ba kan kabo a koro bu n akere Kristo jama n bo bu wo fɛn ni. Boro u nyi na a buka bu kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni, na bu koro kɔ deke bo bu sukɔ ni.

ref: When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower.
text: Kristo dɛɛ ti, bu nati kan i nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm wɔ, na bu koro má pui minɛm n bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n dɔ.

ref: I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us.
text: M yo kɛrɛɛri jɔrɛ be maari Kristo jama ni, ama, Diyoterefesi bo i koro ka i kere ka yiri ti mɔ­fɔ n atana su, wɔ bɔ má jɔrɛ be nu.

ref: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
text: Nyini ti, nzɛn m baari, m yí i sa­yoo­wa fite fofoe nu, dama wɔ jɔjɔ jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn abu kɔrɛ asɔ ya, má nyini ŋgumi, wɔ kete ka i de má Kristo minɛm wɔfɔ, na nzɛn nbem koro ka bu de minɛm wɔfɔ mmɔɔ, i soro má su, na i koro ka i fɔn yi bɛrɛ bo bu koro ka bu de wɔfɔm n fite Kristo jamam n nu ni.

ref: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
text: M dɔn koroo­wa, má a sɔn satiɛ­yo su, ama, sɔn sakpa­yo su. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sakpa ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ si má Nyɛmɛ.

ref: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ di daani kpa fa kɔ kabo Demetirɔsi bita nahɔrɛ n wɔ, na i woo­wa gusu kere ka i bita nahɔrɛ wɔ. Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ di i daani wɔ, ye a si ka kabo ya se n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Kasin dɛɛ ansɛ yoo­wa ni

ref: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
text: Má m la jɔrɛ beberebe bo má m se u, ama, m koro má ka m kɛrɛ i kɛrɛ wura kadasi n nu.

ref: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
text: M la tama ka m bá niɛ u busu kisa, na ya ŋú ya ŋu, na ya bó anzama.

ref: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
text: Nyɛmɛ ma u ahoreda. U danfom kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn bisa u dɔŋgu. Wɔrɔ gusu bisa ya danfom kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma ya.

ref: From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James. To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
text: Mini Juda bo m ti Yakobo niɛma na m ti Yesu Kristo akɔɔ ni, sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo ya Si Nyɛmɛ koro am na wɔ fere am, na Yesu Kristo bita am ni.

ref: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
text: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má m suboro m nyi wɔ, na m kɛrɛ kadasi ma am fa kɔ tii­de n bo m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ anya i n su ni. Ama, wɔ yo daka ka m du mɔ kɛrɛ am, na m sere am jande ka am boro am nyi tu ŋgoni bita am yarada n kenken, nyini yarada ni, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ afa ama i minɛm kpe­kun n kpein n a.

ref: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
text: Dama minɛm nbem awɔ awura am nu bɛnlɛ, nyini minɛm ni, bu soro má Nyɛmɛ, bu kaaki Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama ya n wɔ, na bu su bu dɛɛ konviki koroo­wa atin n su, na bu kete Kristo. Yiri ŋgumi ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni ya Mibiɛ a. Dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛɛri bu jɔrɛ, ka, Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ na i yo bu ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them.
text: Haari ni bo am adu mɔ asi ahin jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ ni, m koro ka m teŋge am su wɔ, fa kɔ kabo Micɛra n yo yiiri Yisareli­fɔm fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn su ni, ama, kasin na wɔ ma bo ba yo má i yarada n kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm nbem bo bu keteeri ka bu wo má bu yiko woo­biri ni, na bu yakiiri bu jinaa­biri ni, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ awura bu jɔrɔkɔ bo bu fite má nu kun, afa bu ase awosin liim nu n a, ka bu wó dɔ, haari fa kɔ ju jɔrɛ­di bambaka n cɛɛn.

ref: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
text: Sɔ ye Sodom ni Gomora­fɔm, ni miɛm bo bu bara yɛ bu n mmɔɔ yoori a, bu faari bu ŋu wuraari sakaraya sereyam nu wɔ, na bu suyo konviki sam bo bu kɔ má atin su. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ acin bu sui, afa bu awura sin n bo i je saŋga kɛrɛ n nu. Nyɛmɛ yoori sɔ ɔ, na minɛ ŋu i, na bu fa tu bu ŋu fɔ.

ref: The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels.
text: Sɔ ye nyini minɛm nati cin alɛlɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn a, na bu suyo daŋgba sam, na bu kete Nyɛmɛ yiko ni, na bu kpiɛ ŋgɔɔ­fɔm nzukɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu wo aŋgoro na bu woo­wa la jirima ni.

ref: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
text: Haari Mikali bo i ti Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm n kɛrɛ tii ni mmɔɔ, wɔ yo abo má su ka i jaraki Sitana kpiɛ i nzukɔ, saŋga bo Moyisi wuuri na bu seeri akuruwa fa kɔ i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n busu ni. Ama, kabo i seeri i n la ka, “Nyɛmɛ bisa u.”

ref: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
text: Keto, keto, bu ŋú kekereke. Dama ba fa satiɛ atin n bo Keen faari i ni, na ŋwaa dɛɛ ti, bu su Baalam dɛɛ dadaka atin n su wɔ, na ba kete nahɔrɛ atin kabo Kora yo keteeri ni. Nyini ti, kabo i kaari ŋgbɛɛn ni, bɛrɛ gusu ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ ɔ.

ref: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
text: Nzɛn am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ sudi anɔɔsɛ diire, bu sata am afiɛn kabo fin ta sutura n wɔ, dama bu ni ambɛrɛ saŋga di a, na bu san má nya, bu koro ka bɛrɛ ŋgumi kunnu yi a, bu la má kpɛrɛ be. Bu ti ka nzue bo i muna na aŋuma fa i sin n wɔ. Na bu kaaki ti ka baka bo i su má suu­wa bakam su saŋga, na i wu kpɔrɔ na i bu tɔ n wɔ.

ref: Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
text: Bu nyinsɛ sam bo bu yo bu ni, bu ti kabo tieku nzue bo na i yasu fufuku n wɔ. Bu kaaki ti ka asara­mbaa bo bu jeŋgbe bara yɛ n wɔ. Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ awosin liim su aŋminda bu ka bu kɔ́ wo dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

ref: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
text: Sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Henoki, na i tɔ ya na Adama su nso su ni, Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ n maari i wɔ, na wɔ yo ajɔjɔ nyini jɔrɛ n ka, “Am ti fɛn, Micɛra ni suba ni i mɛrɛkɛm akpii akpii buru,

ref: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
text: na i bá di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ, na i cín bu sui fa kɔ bu satiɛm bo ba yo bu n su, ni kabo bu jɔjɔɔri ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn na bu keteeri Nyɛmɛ ni. Bu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, nyini ti, ye bu keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a.”

ref: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
text: Nyini minɛm ni sɔ, bu ŋuŋuti wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na bu sujaraki bu beŋgu, na bu susu bu konvikiya sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bo bu bita i n su, na bu sufɔ yanfɔya, na bu subo minɛm anɔɔfiɛ, adena bu nya bu dɛɛ sa koroo­wam. Jɔrɛ bɔbɔɔ­wam ni atin keree­wam ni

ref: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ, m danfo kpam, wɔ yo daka ka am teŋge su fa kɔ jɔrɛ n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo jaramasam yo seeri am ni.

ref: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
text: Ahin minɛm sɔ, bu sutiti am nu wɔ lɛɛ, bu su adamande dɛɛ ŋgɔnlɛ su a, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo má bu dɔ.

ref: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ, m danfo kpam, am boro am nyi na am ma am yarada casi n buka su, na am sere Nyɛmɛ, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu,

ref: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
text: na am ma Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koro n wo am dɔ. Na am ŋminda fa kɔ saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo bá si am aŋunfɔ, na am nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

ref: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
text: Nbem wo am nu bo bu la sika bu sunsunni nu, am si bu aŋunfɔ,

ref: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
text: nbem gusu, am cin yi bu fite sin nu, na am de bu tii ji, na nyiŋgam gusu, am si bu aŋunfɔ, ama, am si am ŋu sa haari na má am ma bu sutura fin mmɔɔ ta am. Awieeri su dɛɛ nyɛmɛsere

ref: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
text: Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi ti ya Tii­de­fɔ a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Yiri koro bita am na am kpaasi má a, yiri koro fa am ba jina i jirima biɛ n nyunu a, na am la má ŋgasi be na i ma am ahorejɔ a. Yiri ye i ti ye ni ka ya kansi i na ya wura i kpiri a, yiri ti yiko­fɔ a, yiri ti faŋga­fɔ a, yiri yo woori bɛrɛ a, yiri toko wo bɛrɛ kisa ni saŋga kɛrɛ a. Ami.

ref: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
text: Ahin jɔrɛ n ye Nyɛmɛ seeri Yesu Kristo ka i yi kere i akɔɔm fa kɔ sam bo i cɛ má na bu bá yo n a. Na Yesu asunma i mɛrɛkɛ i akɔɔ Yohane dɔ, na i kɔ se i jɔrɛ ni.

ref: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
text: Jɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo yi kereeri Yohane na i ŋuuri i ni, nyini ye wɔ di i daani akɛrɛ i n a.

ref: God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kaanri ahin kadasi ni, tere­fiɛ wo i dɔ. Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri i jɔrɛ na bu deeri i, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama saŋga n adodo bo nyini sam kɛrɛ bá yo. Kabo Yohane kɛrɛɛri kadasi yoori Kristo jama n ansɛ ni

ref: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
text: na wɔ yo ya i fɛmɛya n nu minɛm na ya yo i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, na ya su i Si Nyɛmɛ. Yiri Yesu Kristo ni, yiri ti ye ni ka ya wura i kpiri a, i dɛɛ la yiko saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

ref: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
text: Am niɛ, i fíte aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu ba, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyu­mbaa bá ŋu i, fa saŋga ni minɛm bo bu wɔɔri i kpiɛ n kɛrɛ. Yiri ti, durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ sún. I ti sɔ ɔ. Ami.

ref: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
text: Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri bo i wo bɛrɛ ni, na i yo woori bɛrɛ, na i bá wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n ase ka, “Mini ti mɔ­fɔ a, na m kaaki ti kasin­fɔ.” Kabo Yohane ŋuuri Kristo ka alɛlɛ nu ni

ref: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
text: Mini Yohane ti am niwa bo m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ saŋga di wahara ni mindi Yesu dɛɛ ti, na ya ti i fɛmɛya n nu minɛm ni. Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo ma kan i ni, ni Yesu daani n bo ma di i n dɛɛ ti, na ba fa m akɔ aji miɛ be bo i wo lui bambaka n bo i wo tieku n nu na bu fere i ka Patamosi n su.

ref: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
text: Ya Mibiɛ n dɛɛ cɛɛn ni, na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura m ŋu, na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga m sin, ka awiɛ dɛɛ su.

ref: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ deke n kɛrɛ bo a ŋú i n wura kadasi n nu, na a fa ma Kristo jama asɛsɛ nsom bo bu wo ahin miɛ kpirim n su ni, bɛrɛ la Ɛfɛso, ni Samirina, ni Pɛrɛgamon, ni Tiyatira, ni Saradisa, ni Filadɛlɛfiya, ni Lawodikaya.”

ref: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
text: Na ma ŋu be ka adamande wa ni, i wo bu afiɛn, i wura tanlɛɛ tintiin yorom, na wɔ fa sika dambara abo i hoe su.

ref: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
text: I tii­ŋmiɛ kɛrɛ ti fofoe papasi ka gise wɔ, na i nyu­mbaa kɛrɛ je ka sin fofoe.

ref: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
text: Na i jaa gbagbaa kɛrɛ boro ka jawii bo ba fa awura sin nu na wɔ boro sonsoen, na i konvi bo awie kabo nzue beberebe soŋgi na i bo awie barasu ni.

ref: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
text: I bita asara­mbaam nso i saa fɔmbɔrɔ nu, na takobe nana afite i nɔɔ nu. I nyunu je kabo wiɛ ba bi na i je n wɔ.

ref: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
text: M ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na ma kaba ada i jaa bu asiɛŋgu ka ma wu dɛɛ su wɔ, na wɔ fa i saa fɔmbɔrɔ anana m ŋu, na wɔ se m ka, “Má a soro sɛrɛ. Mini ti deke kɛrɛ bumboori ni i awieeri a.

ref: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
text: Mini ti bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n a. Niɛ fɛn. M yo wuuri wɔ, ama, kisa, ma teŋge yiwee nu. M la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri. Mini bita yiwee ni Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri kadombaa a.

ref: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
text: Nyini ti, kɛrɛ sam bo a ŋu bu ni, ni bo bu suyo kisa ni, ni bo bu bá yo ahin dɛɛ sin ni.

ref: I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
text: Asara­mbaa nsom bo a ŋu bu m saa fɔmbɔrɔ n bu la ka, Kristo jama asɛsɛ nsom n mɛrɛkɛm. Na fitana takaa­biri nsom bo bu ti sika ni gusu bu la ka, Kristo jama asɛsɛ nsom ni.”

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, ‘Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m bita asara­mbaa nsom m saa fɔmbɔrɔ nu ni, na m kaaki nati fitana takaa­biri nsom bo bu ti sika n afiɛn n dɔ ɔ.

ref: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
text: M si am sa­yoo­wam, m si kabo am ayo am juma ni anyum­boro barasu, ni kabo am la mindi barasu ni, na m si kabo am de má satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni. Na m kaaki si kabo am ayi kɔrɛsi aŋu minɛm bo bu ŋa bu ti m jaramasam, ama, bu woo­wa kere ka bu ti má sɔ, na am aŋu ka bu ti kɔrɛ­fɔm ni.

ref: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
text: Mini dɛɛ ti, am ayo sawɔrɛ amindi am wahara nu, am nya fie má, am toko su m su.

ref: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
text: Ama, jɔrɛ kun bo m fa jaraki am la ka, kisa am koro má m kabo am yo koro m ni.

ref: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
text: Nyini ti, am bu am tii nu fɔ na am si deke bo am akpaasi ni, na am kaaki am wo, na am yo sam bo am yoori bu bumboori ni. Nzɛn am akaaki má am wo, m bá cin yi am fitana takaa­biri n deke bo i taka ni.

ref: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
text: Ama, am sa kun bo i yo m fiɛ n la kabo am koro má Nikolaita­fɔm sa­yoo­wam ni, mini mmɔɔ koro má bu sa­yoo­wam ni.’

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Samirina miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, ‘Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m ti deke kɛrɛ bumboori ni i awieeri ni, na m yo wuuri na ma teŋge yiwee nu ni dɔ ɔ.

ref: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
text: M si kabo am adi wahara agaya barasu, ni kabo am ti yaari­fɔm ni, ama, am ti neŋge­fɔm wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Na m si kabo minɛm kpiɛ am nzukɔ ni, bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ama, bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ŋgba, bu ti Sitana dɛɛ jama n wɔ.

ref: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
text: Am dí wahara kisa, ama, má am soro sɛrɛ. Am ti fɛn, Sitana wúra am nbem saraka na i yó am na i níɛ na am ŋú kekereke le­buru. Ama, am su m ni nahɔrɛ haari am bá wu, na m tɔ́ am ŋkatɔ, yiri la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.’

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Pɛrɛgamon miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, ‘Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m la takobe nana n dɔ ɔ.

ref: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
text: M si deke bo am woori ni, am woo­biri n ti Sitana fɛmɛya tanaa­biri wɔ, ama, am toko su m ni nahɔrɛ wɔ, am ayaki má m yarada­yo, haari ni saŋga bo bu kuunri Antipasi bo i diiri m daani nahɔrɛ su am miɛ n nu, deke bo Sitana wo ni.

ref: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
text: Ama, m la jɔrɛ nbem bo m fa jaraki am, am nbem bita Baalam keree­wa atin n wɔ. Yiri yo kereeri Fɛmɛ Balaki, ye i maari Yisareli­fɔm yoori satiɛ diiri tiɛ niɛ, na ba yo sakaraya.

ref: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
text: Am nbem gusu ti sɔ ɔ, bu su Nikolaita­fɔm dɛɛ atin keree­wa n su wɔ.

ref: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
text: Nyini ti, am kaaki am wo, má nyini, m bá am dɔ kisa, na m fá takobe n bo i wo m nɔɔ nu kun luɛ ni minɛm bo bu yo sɔ ni.’

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, m má i mi diire n bo bu fere i ka mana * ni, na i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu ni. Na m káaki ma i mi yabuɛ fofoe be, ba kɛrɛ duma fɔfɔrɛ su, be koro si má duma n bo i wo su ni, má ka yiri n bo i de i ni si i a.” Nɔaniɛ n bo bu fa maari Kristo jama n bo i wo Tiyatira ni

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Tiyatira miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, ‘Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini Nyɛmɛ Wa n dɔ ɔ, mini bo m nyu­mbaa je ka sin fofoe, na m jaa gbagbaa kɛrɛ boro ka jawii dɛɛ su ni.

ref: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
text: M si am sa­yoo­wam, m si kabo am la koro barasu, ni kabo am su m ni nahɔrɛ su ni, ni amusui bo am su i ni, na m kaaki si kabo am la mindi, na m si kabo kisa am boro am nyi tara bumboori ni.

ref: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
text: Ama, jɔrɛ bo m fa jaraki am la ka, am amɔ am nɔɔ ye am niɛ Yɛzɛbɛli, bara n bo i yo i ŋu ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ni. I kere m akɔɔm wɔ na i daka bu, na bu suyo sakaraya, na i ma m minɛm di tiɛ niɛ.

ref: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
text: Ma se i ka i kaaki i wo na i yaki somaya kɔ ni, wɔ kete ka i kaaki má.

ref: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
text: Nyini ti, m má i da tukpaki kekereke, na i ni minɛm bo bu yo sakaraya n dí wahara agaya, nzɛn ba kaaki má bu wo ayaki má sam bo bu ni yiri yo bu ni.

ref: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
text: Na m kún i nbaam, na Kristo jama asɛsɛ n kɛrɛ sí ka, mini fiiti si deke bo i wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ahore su, ni i sunsunni nu. Na m fá sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sa­yoo­wa tɔ i kaari.

ref: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
text: Ama, ambɛrɛ Tiyatira­fɔm nbem bo am aka, na am su má i keree­wa atin n su ni, ambɛrɛ asɔnsɔn má deke bo nbem fere i ka Sitana asiri nu sa bambaka ni. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, m sɔ́ má am sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri buka má su.

ref: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
text: Ama, atin bo am bita i ni, am bita i kenken haari saŋga bo m bá kaaki ba.

ref: I will give each of them the same power my Father has given me. They will rule the nations with an iron rod and smash those nations to pieces like clay pots. I will also give them the morning star.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara na i yo m sa koroo­wa kɔ juuri awieeri, m má i yiko kabo m Si ama m yiko ni, na i níɛ daanfiɛ daanfiɛ kɛrɛ su. Na i bíta bu kekereke kabo naŋkasi fa bulaari kɔrɔma tu bo i naanim barasu ni, na i fúfu bu kabo bu fufu yakati­sɛ barasu ni. Na m má i asaakojo.’

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Saradisa miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, ‘Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m la Nyɛmɛ awiɛwiɛ busu nso n na m bita asara­mbaam nso m saa nu dɔ ɔ. M si am sa­yoo­wam, am wo ka am la ŋgɔɔ, ama, am ti fuinm wɔ.

ref: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
text: Nyini ti, am yasu na am bita deke n bo am toko la i n kenken, má nyini, i wú wɔ. Dama ma ŋu ka am sa­yoo­wam n akpiɛ su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

ref: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
text: Am teŋge su, kabo am yo tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na am deeri i ni, am kaaki am wo na am bita i, nzɛn am ayasu má, am si má saŋga bo m bá ni, dama m bá fura am nu ka awiefɔ dɛɛ su wɔ.

ref: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
text: Ama, m la minɛ nbem bo bu wo Saradisa Kristo jama n nu, bo ba saaki má bu sutura ayo má bu fin, bu sa­yoo­wam nu. Bɛrɛ bá wura tanlɛɛ fofoe na m ni bɛrɛ sáŋga nati a, dama bu ti ye ni sɔ ɔ.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, i wúra nyini tanlɛɛ fofoe n be sɔ ɔ. Na m yí má i mi duma fite má ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu, ama, m bá jina m Si ni i mɛrɛkɛm nyunu, na m sé bu ka i ti m dɛɛ sɔnɔ ɔ.’

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Filadɛlɛfiya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, ‘Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m ti casi na m ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ, na m bita Fɛmɛ Davidi kadombaa n bo i kere ka m la i dɛɛ yiko n be wɔ, na nzɛn m tekeeri anɔ, be koro tu má i, na nzɛn m tuuri i gusu, be koro teke má i.

ref: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
text: M si am sa­yoo­wam, am niɛ, ma teke anɔ ama am bo be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro tu i. M si ka am la má faŋga agaya, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, am fite má m sin, am toko bita m nɔaniɛ n ni nahɔrɛ wɔ.

ref: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
text: Am yaki bo bu su Sitana n na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, bu ti kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ, bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ŋgba. M má bu ba kutu am nyunu na bu si ka m koro am.

ref: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
text: Am ade m nɔaniɛ ni, na am amindi n dɛɛ ti, m yí am fite kekereke n bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa yo durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ na i niɛ ni.

ref: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
text: M cɛ́ má, m káaki ba kisa. Atin n bo am bita i ni, am bita i kenken na má be fin am ŋkatɔ ni.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, m fá i yo jantaraka Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu na i wó nu dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ. Na m kɛ́rɛ Nyɛmɛ duma mata i, na m kɛ́rɛ Nyɛmɛ miɛ n duma, miɛ n bo i fite aŋgoro Nyɛmɛ dɔ na bu fere i ka Yɛrusalem fɔfɔrɛ, na m káaki kɛrɛ m duma fɔfɔrɛ n gusu mata i mi ŋu.’

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Lawodikaya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, ‘Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m ti Ami na m la dabuŋu, na m di daani ni nahɔrɛ su n dɔ ɔ. Mini ni Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yo woori bɛrɛ a, ye ya yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a.

ref: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
text: M si am sa­yoo­wam, am ti má niniki, am kaaki ti má kɛkɛrɛ, nzɛn má am ti niniki wara kɛkɛrɛ, má m koro.

ref: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
text: Ama, am ti bɔbɔrɔki n dɛɛ ti, m púuti am m nɔɔ nu butu.

ref: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
text: Am ŋa am ti neŋge­fɔm wɔ, deke be kpiɛ má am su, ama, am si má ka am la aŋunfɔ, na am si má ka am ti yaari­fɔm, na am kaaki si má ka am ti anyinsinm, na am kaaki si má ka am nati am ŋuniɛ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

ref: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
text: Nyini ti, m koro ka m tu am fɔ, na am bra m dɔ ba to sika kpa bo ba fa awura sin nu na wɔ fite casi ni, adena am yo neŋge­fɔm nahɔrɛ su, na am ba to sutura fofoe m dɔ, na am fa kata am ŋu, na má minɛ ŋu ka am nati am ŋuniɛ ŋgbɛɛn na i la nyinsɛ. Na am ba to anyumbaa ayiri m dɔ, na am fa wura am nyu­mbaa nu na am koro ŋu asi nyunyumi.

ref: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
text: Bo m koro bu ni, bɛrɛ ye m jaraki bu na m cin bu sui a, nyini ti, am boro am nyi na am kaaki am wo.

ref: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
text: Am ti fɛn, m jina sɔɔ anɔ n sin subobo, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti m konvi ŋga na i tekeeri anɔ ni, m bá wura i mi dɔ, na m ni yiri sáŋga di diire.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, m má i atin na i bá tana m bi m fɛmɛya biɛ n su, kabo ma di nasara na m ni m Si saŋga tana i fɛmɛya biɛ n su ni.’

ref: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
text: Nyini sin, na wɔ yo m ka alɛlɛ dɛɛ su, na ma ŋu ka anɔ be ateke aŋgoro. Na ma ti akonvi ŋga ka awiɛ dɛɛ su sujɔjɔ m dɔ, i ti akonvi n bo i du mɔ jɔjɔɔri m dɔ n wɔ. I ŋa, “Fun bra aŋgoro fɛn, na m kére u sam bo bu bá yo ahin sin.”

ref: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
text: Jina nu n bɛrɛ na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aba awura m ŋu na ma ŋu ka fɛmɛya biɛ wo aŋgoro, be tana su.

ref: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
text: Bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su je piɛpiɛu kabo cɛncɛɛri nbem je barasu wɔ, nbem je fofoe na nbem je kɔkɔrɛ, na i fɛmɛya biɛ ni, deke be je piɛpiɛu aŋgoro kabo nzue ba muna na ayi kpiɛ nu ni.

ref: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
text: Fɛmɛya biɛ abura­nyɔ ni nna nbem taka bara yɛ i fɛmɛya biɛ ni, na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm abura­nyɔ ni nna tana biɛm su, bu wura tanlɛɛ fofoem na bu butu sika kere.

ref: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
text: Na fɛmɛya biɛ ni sukpɛsɛ ka nzue dɛɛ su, na i sutiɛntiɛn, na i suyo naŋmiɛ. Fitana nso taka fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu wo je su, bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ awiɛwiɛ busu nsom ni.

ref: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
text: Deke be gusu da fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu ka nzue bambaka dɛɛ su, i nyunu da cain ka niɛɛnu wɔ. Deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nna nbem gusu abara ayɛ fɛmɛya biɛ ni, bu la anyumbaa beberebe, bu nyunu ni bu sin n kɛrɛ.

ref: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
text: Ciciri su dɛɛ n ti ka jara a, nnyɔ su n ti ka toora, nsan su n nyunu­awa ti ka adamande wɔ, na nna su n ti ka kajiri bo i sutu landi.

ref: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
text: Bu kun kun kɛrɛ la laŋkpɛtɛ nsɛn nsɛn wɔ, bu la anyumbaam bu ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu, ni bu ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ su. Na bu sudi jue kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ, ka, “Casi, casi, casi la ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri, i yo woori bɛrɛ, na i toko wo bɛrɛ, na i bá wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni.”

ref: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
text: Nzɛn bu nya sudi jue na bu fa kansi, ni jirima, ni ansɛ ma yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su ni, na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga bo i la má awieeri ni,

ref: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
text: “Ya Micɛra ni ya Nyɛmɛ, wɔrɔ ti ye ni ka a de kansi a, ni jirima, ni yiko. Dama wɔrɔ yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, u koroya­nu ye a maari deke n kɛrɛ la ŋgɔɔ a.”

ref: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
text: Na ma ŋu ka kadasi nyanyaa­wa be wo yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n saa fɔmbɔrɔ n nu, kɛrɛɛ­wa wo i kunnu ni i sin kɛrɛ su, ba fa wiɛ mɛnɛ amiɛ amata i busu nso.

ref: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
text: Na ma ŋu Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ faŋga­fɔ be ka i subo ndoori faŋga su ka, “Ŋma ti ye ni bo i koro cin ŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ n na i teke kadasi n a?”

ref: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Ama, aŋgoro, ni asiɛŋgu, ni bo bu wo asiɛn bu, be awo má bɛrɛ bo i koro teke kadasi ni, na a ba kan ka i koro niɛ nu.

ref: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Be awo má bɛrɛ bo i ti ye ni bo i koro teke kadasi ni, na a ba kan ka i koro niɛ nu n dɛɛ ti, na ma sun ni nyunvuin ŋgba.

ref: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
text: Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nu kun, ase m ka, “Má a sun. Niɛ fɛn, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma kpiri ni, bo bu fere i ka Yuda­fɔm busu nu jara n adi nasara, i koro cin ŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nso n na i teke kadasi ni.”

ref: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
text: Na ma ŋu bɔmbaa be, i ti ka ba kpiɛ i kɔm wɔ, i jina fɛmɛya biɛ n afiɛn. Deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n abara ayɛ i. I la mbiɛ nso wɔ, i nyu­mbaa gusu ti nso wɔ. Bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ awiɛwiɛ busu nsom bo wɔ sunma bu durunya n kɛrɛ nu ni.

ref: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
text: Na wɔ kɔ yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n dɔ, na wɔ de kadasi n afite i saa fɔmbɔrɔ nu.

ref: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
text: Na ba di jue fɔfɔrɛ ama yiri Bɔmbaa n ka, “Wɔrɔ ti ye ni ka a de kadasi ni, na a ŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ n a, dama wɔrɔ ye bu kpiɛɛri u kɔm, ye a fa u mbunja ade minɛm tii aji a. Adamande kɛrɛ, ni daanfiɛ kɛrɛ, ni aniɛm kɛrɛ, ni asiɛn barasu barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni, bu nbem ayo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm.

ref: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
text: A fa bu ayo u fɛmɛya n nu minɛm, na bu yo u sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, na bu niɛ durunya n kɛrɛ su.”

ref: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
text: Na ma niɛ aŋu Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm beberebe, akpii akpii buru nɔ, ka, ba bara ayɛ fɛmɛya biɛ ni, ni deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni. Na ma ti ka

ref: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
text: ba yɛ bu konvi su sudi jue, ka, “Bɔmbaa n bo bu yo kpiɛɛri i kɔm ni, yiri ti ye ni ka i de yiko a, ni neŋge, ni alecira, ni faŋga, ni jirima, ni kpiri, ni kansi a.”

ref: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
text: Na ma ti ka deke kɛrɛ bo bu wo aŋgoro, ni asiɛŋgu, ni bo bu wo asiɛn bu, ni bo bu wo nzue nu, ni deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu, ka bu sudi jue, ka, “Yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ni Bɔmbaa ni, bɛrɛ ti ye ni ka bu de kansi a, ni jirima, ni kpiri, ni yiko, saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga.”

ref: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
text: Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam n ase ka, “Ami.” Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n akutu asu Nyɛmɛ ni Bɔmbaa ni.

ref: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
text: Na ma ŋu ka Bɔmbaa n aba acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nso n bo bu fa mataari kadasi n nu kun. Na ma ti deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam n nu kun konvi ŋga, kabo nzue tiɛntiɛn barasu ni, ka, “Fite.”

ref: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
text: M niɛɛri na ma ŋu ka, kpɔŋgɔ fofoe lɛɛ. Bo i fun i n bita taa ni awiɛ, na ba butu i fɛmɛya kere, na wɔ kɔ ka luɛ­kun­fɔ na i di nasara.

ref: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nnyɔ su ni, na ma ti ka deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnyɔ su ni suse ka, “Fite.”

ref: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
text: Na kpɔŋgɔ kɔkɔrɛ afite, na ba ma i fun­fɔ n takobe tintiin, na ba ma i yiko ka i yi tana­laifiɛ fite durunya n nu, na minɛm kun bu beŋgum.

ref: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
text: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nsan su ni, na ma ti ka deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nsan su n suse ka, “Fite.” M niɛɛri na ma ŋu ka, kpɔŋgɔ biri lɛɛ. I fun­fɔ n bita deke bo bu bita sunsun diire ni i saa nu.

ref: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
text: Na ma ti akonvi be ŋga deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam n nu, i ŋa, “Ŋgani ayaba ŋgboroko kun ti juma­yo­fɔ le­kun ŋkatɔ ɔ. Na abuwe ayaba ŋgboroko nsan ti juma­yo­fɔ le­kun ŋkatɔ. Ama, má am saaki ŋguin ni nzan ni.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nna su ni, na ma ti ka deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nna su n suse ka, “Fite.”

ref: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
text: M niɛɛri na ma ŋu kpɔŋgɔ nzuin faa, i fun­fɔ n duma la Yiwee, na bo bu fere i ka Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri n gusu su i su. Na Nyɛmɛ ama bu durunya kpaaki nu nna buekun yiko ka bu fa luɛ, ni ahɔɛ, ni tukpaki, ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ tiɛm, na bu kun bu.

ref: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
text: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nnu su ni, na ma ŋu minɛm awiɛwiɛ Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bu, bɛrɛ la minɛm bo bu yo kuunri bu fa kɔ bu Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kaan­wa, ni bu daani dii­wa n dɛɛ ti.

ref: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
text: Na bu sutiɛn faŋga su ka, “Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ casi, nahɔrɛ­fɔ, cɛɛn nzu ye a dí minɛm bo bu yo kuunri ya n jɔrɛ na a cín bu sui ma ya?”

ref: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ awura bu kun kun kɛrɛ sutura fofoe, na wɔ se bu ka, “Am yi am ŋumi kaan ka, na bu bá kun am beŋgu akɔɔm, ni am beŋgu Kristo­fɔm kɛrɛ kɔ wie ka, kabo bu yo kuunri am barasu ni, na m dí bu jɔrɛ.”

ref: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
text: Na ma ŋu ka Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nsɛn su ni, na asiɛn akpisi faŋga su, na wiɛ akaaki biri didiki ka nambiri ŋuma, na sara n akaaki kɔkɔrɛ ka mbunja dɛɛ su.

ref: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
text: Na asara­mbaam afite aŋgoro ati atɔtɔ kabo aŋuma ba kpisi damba baka na i poorim titi tɔtɔ barasu ni.

ref: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
text: Na aŋgoro n kɛrɛ anyanya kabo bu nyanya biɛ ni, na wɔ mini, na bukam, ni nzue nu lui bambakam kɛrɛ ayasu bu tanaa­biri.

ref: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
text: Na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm, ni tere­tere­fɔm, ni soja nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni neŋge­fɔm, ni nfaŋga­fɔm, ni akɔɔm, ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ akɔ afiɛ yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim buɛm nu, ni yabuɛ bambakam bu, bukam su.

ref: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
text: Na ba se ka, “Má bukam bereki gɔ ya ŋu, na má yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim kata ya ŋu, má i ti ye, adena má sɔnɔ n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ŋu ya, na má Bɔmbaa n gusu cin ya sui.

ref: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
text: Dama cɛɛn bo Nyɛmɛ ni Bɔmbaa ni cin sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sui n aju, ye ŋma koro jina bu nyunu a?”

ref: After this I saw four angels. Each one was standing on one of the earth's four corners. The angels held back the four winds, so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree. These angels had also been given the power to harm the earth and the sea. Then I saw another angel come up from where the sun rises in the east, and he was ready to put the mark of the living God on people. He shouted to the four angels,
text: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ be ka i fite wiɛ afiiri, i bita deke be bo Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n fa se i minɛm nyaari. Na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su ajɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛ nnam bo Nyɛmɛ ama bu yiko ka bu saaki asiɛn n ni tieku n dɔ,

ref: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
text: na wɔ se bu ka, “Má am se ka am saaki asiɛn n ni tieku n ni bakam ni, má ka ya ba ase Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ ŋuma su nyaari awie ka.”

ref: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
text: Na bu suse ka, “Ami. Kansi, ni kpiri, ni alecira, ni ansɛ, ni jirima, ni yiko, ni faŋga ti ya Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.”

ref: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
text: Nyini sin na ma ŋu jama beberebe, bu la má nɔ, bu fite durunya asiɛn n kɛrɛ su, ni adamande kɛrɛ nu, ni durunya daanfiɛ n kɛrɛ nu, ni durunya aniɛm kɛrɛ nu, ka, bu jina yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ni Bɔmbaa n nyunu. Bu wura tanlɛɛ fofoe yorom, bu bita kpakpa nyawam bu saa nu.

ref: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
text: Na ba bo afere faŋga su ase ka, “Nyɛmɛ bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ni Bɔmbaa n ama ya nya ya tii a.”

ref: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
text: Na mɛrɛkɛm n kɛrɛ ajina abara ayɛ fɛmɛya biɛ, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam ni, na ba dada bu nyunu su asiɛŋgu Nyɛmɛ bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n nyunu, na ba su i.

ref: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
text: Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nu kun abisa m ka, “Ŋmam ye a ŋu ka bu wura tanlɛɛ fofoe yorom n a, ye bu fite ni wɔ?”

ref: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
text: Na ma se i ka, “M mibiɛ, wɔrɔ si a, mini si má.” Na wɔ se m ka, “Bɛrɛ ti minɛm bo ba fite anyum­boro kekereke nu n a, bɛrɛ ye ba ŋmisi bu sutura Bɔmbaa ni mbunja n nu ayo bu fofoe n a.

ref: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bu jina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, i fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu ye bu su i kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu n a, na Nyɛmɛ bo i tana i fɛmɛya biɛ n su n wó bu dɔ na i yó bu kanda.

ref: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
text: Ahɔɛ ni nzuehɔɛ tí má bu kun cɛɛn be, wiɛ bó má bu, na i kɛkɛrɛ gusu wúra má bu.

ref: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
text: Dama Bɔmbaa n bo i wo fɛmɛya biɛ n afiɛn ni yó bu sasa­fɔ a, na yiri fá bu kɔ bura nzue bo i tiɛ na i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a. Na Nyɛmɛ núnuka bu nyunvuin kɛrɛ bu nyunu.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
text: Saŋga bo Bɔmbaa n cin ŋmaŋgiiri wiɛ mɛnɛ nso su n ni, na deke kɛrɛ atɔ amuna aŋgoro n nu, haari miti abura­san.

ref: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
text: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ nsom bo bu jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu ni, na ba fa awiɛ fitaawa nso ama bu.

ref: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
text: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ nyiŋga ka i bita sika ayaba, bo bu fa ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani gɔ nu na bu kunkun i ni, na wɔ kɔ ajina Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n bi. Na ba ma i ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani beberebe ka i fa buka Nyɛmɛ minɛm nyɛmɛsere su, na i fa kunkun Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bo bu fa sika mata su, na i wo fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu ni.

ref: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
text: Na ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani n ŋmisini n ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm nyɛmɛsere n ayasu mɛrɛkɛ n saa nu afun akɔ Nyɛmɛ nyunu.

ref: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ayi sin Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n su, na wɔ fa awura ayaba n nu, na wɔ yi, na wɔ fa atu asandi durunya n nu. Na nzue sukpura sutiɛntiɛn, na i sujeje, na i sukpɛkpɛsɛ, na asiɛn n akpisi. Awiɛ nso n bo bu kan i n jɔrɛ

ref: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsom n bo bu bita awiɛ fitaawam n asɛsɛ bu ŋu su ka bu kan awiɛm ni.

ref: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i du mɔ n akan i dɛɛ awiɛ ni. Na nzue yabuɛ, ni sin, saŋga ni mbunja atɔ durunya n nu, na sin aje durunya n kpaaki nu nsan buekun, ni bakam kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun, ni wura mɔnɛ kɛrɛ.

ref: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i tɔ su nnyɔ su n akan i awiɛ ni, na deke be, i ti ka buka bambaka wɔ, ye wɔ so sin su a, na ba fa i atu tieku n nu, na tieku n kpaaki nu nsan buekun akaaki mbunja.

ref: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
text: Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo tieku n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun awu, na lee bambakam bo bu wo tieku n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun gusu asaaki.

ref: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i tɔ su nsan su n akan i awiɛ ni, na asara­mbaa bambaka bo i je sin ka wura sin afite aŋgoro aba atɔ kunmam ni nzuetinm bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun nu.

ref: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
text: Asara­mbaa n duma la Wiwi, na nzue n bo i wo durunya n nu n kpaaki nu nsan akaaki wiwi, nzue n ti wiwi n dɛɛ ti, na minɛm dɔŋgu bo bu nuuri nyini nzue n awu.

ref: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i tɔ su nna su n akan i awiɛ ni, na deke be aba atɔ wiɛ, ni sara, ni asara­mbaam kɛrɛ kpaaki nu nsan buekun su asaaki bu, na ba kaaki biri tuum. Wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ n kpaaki nu nsan buekun, ayo má wein.

ref: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
text: Na ma niɛ, na ma ŋu ka kajiri be sulaandi tu aŋgoro, na ma ti kabo i tiɛn barasu ni, i ŋa, “Oi, keto, keto, keto wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo durunya n nu n ŋu, kabo mɛrɛkɛ nsanm bo ba ka n koro ka bu kan bu awiɛm ni, na bu tiiri.”

ref: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnu su n akan i awiɛ ni, na ma ŋu ka asara­mbaa be afite aŋgoro n aba atɔ asiɛŋgu. Na ba ma i kadombaa ka i teke kunma n bo i kɔ ŋgoŋgo na i la má awieeri ni.

ref: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
text: Na wɔ teke nyini kunma n ni. I tekeeri i ni, na ŋmisini afite kunma n nu, i ti ka sin bambaka tɔrɔɔ­wa ŋmisini wɔ. Na ŋmisini n bo i fite kunma n nu n ama wiɛ ni aŋgoro n kɛrɛ asi biri didiki.

ref: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
text: Na lalom afite ŋmisini n nu asandi ade asiɛn n su, na Nyɛmɛ ama bu yiko, ka bundaanim dɛɛ su.

ref: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka má bu di wura ni diire mbaa wara bakam, ama, bu kan minɛm n bo bu la má Nyɛmɛ anyaari see­wa bu ŋuma su ni.

ref: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
text: Wɔ ma má bu yiko ka bu kun minɛm wɔ, ama, bu kere bu wahara sara nnu. Wahara ni ti kabo bundaani kan sɔnɔ na i yo ya n wɔ.

ref: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
text: Nyini saŋga ni, adamande sún kpini yiwee busu na bu wú, ama, bu nyá má i.

ref: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
text: Lalom n ti ka kpɔŋgɔm bo ba cici bu ka bu kɔ luɛ dɛɛ su wɔ, deke be wo bu tii su, ka sika kere dɛɛ su, na bu nyunu­awa ti ka adamande dɛɛ su.

ref: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
text: Bu ndɔrɛ ti tintiin kabo bara bo i la tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin ti n wɔ, na bu jee ti ka jara dɛɛ.

ref: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
text: Deke be kata bu hoe su pɛpɛrɛpɛ ka bulaari dɛɛ su, na bu laŋkpɛtɛm suyo naŋmiɛ kabo kpɔŋgɔm ni bu torokum n beberebe suŋmati kɔ luɛ na bu yo naŋmiɛ n wɔ.

ref: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
text: Bu lɔ, bo bu fa kan n ti ka bundaani dɛɛ su wɔ. Bu la yiko bu lɔ n nu, bo bu fa kere minɛ wahara sara nnu.

ref: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
text: Bu fɛmɛ n la mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ kunma ŋgoŋgo n bo i la má awieeri n su ni. Hiburu aniɛ nu bu fere i ka, Abadon, ye Giriki aniɛ nu bu fere i ka, Apolin, i bu la ka, saaki­fɔ.

ref: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
text: Tɔ. Ciciri su dɛɛ ahoresaaki n asin, nnyɔ wo sin suba.

ref: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsɛn su n akan i awiɛ n na ma ti akonvi be ŋga ka i fite Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bue nna n kɛrɛ, ba fa sika amata su na i wo Nyɛmɛ nyunu ni.

ref: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
text: Na akonvi n ajɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛ nsɛn su n bo i bita awiɛ n dɔ, i ŋa, “Kɔ mɛrɛkɛ nnam bo bu wo kunma kpiri n bo bu fere i ka, Yufarati n dɔ, na a nyaŋgi bu ji.”

ref: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
text: Na wɔ nyaŋgi mɛrɛkɛ nnam bo ba du mɔ akpa bu ŋu aŋminda nyini cɛɛn n gbingbin na bu kun minɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun ni.

ref: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
text: Deke n bo i yo m ka alɛlɛ ni, m ŋuuri kpɔŋgɔm, ni bo bu fun bu ni, ka bulaari pɛpɛrɛpɛ be kata bu hoe su, nbem boro kɔkɔrɛ ka sin wɔ, na nbem ti biri ka bura nzue, na nbem ti ka ndɛrɛ nzue, kpɔŋgɔm tii dika ti ka jara tii wɔ. Na sin, ni ŋmisini, ni ferewita sufite bu nɔɔ nu.

ref: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
text: Ahin deke kekereke nsanm ni kuunri minɛ n bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun n a, bɛrɛ la sin, ni ŋmisini, ni ferewita n bo i fite bu nɔɔ nu ni.

ref: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
text: Dama kpɔŋgɔm n yiko kɛrɛ wo bu nɔɔ, ni bu lɔm nu wɔ. Bu lɔm ti ka woom wɔ, bu la atiim, ye bu fa kere minɛ wahara a.

ref: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
