text: Minɛm bo ahin wahara n akun má bu na bu kaari ni, ba kaaki má bu wo, ba yaki má amɔɛm su, ni dekem bo ba fa sikam, ni jɛtɛm, ni jawiim, ni yabuɛm, ni bakam ayo bu ka bu amɔɛm, nyini dekem koro ŋu má asi, na bu koro ti má asui, na bu koro nati má ni, ba yaki má bu su.

ref: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
text: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ faŋga­fɔ be bekun ka i fite aŋgoro sujura ba. Na yinyiŋgu akata i ŋu ka tani dɛɛ su. Na ayi bo i kpiɛ aŋgoro n abara ayɛ i tii, i nyunu ti ka wiɛ wɔ, na i jaam ti ka sin taferema.

ref: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
text: I bita kadasi kaan be i saa nu, i ti tekee­wa a. Na wɔ fa i jaa fɔmbɔrɔ atiɛn tieku n su, na wɔ fa i jaa biɛ atiɛn asiɛn n su.

ref: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
text: Na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su kabo jara ŋuundi ni, na nzue tiɛɛn­wa nso n abɔ i nu.

ref: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
text: Nzue n tiɛɛnri kpe nso ni, má m ŋa m kɛrɛ wɔ, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga aŋgoro. I ŋa, “Má a kɛrɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n bo nzue tiɛɛn­wa nso n ase ni. Má a kan kere be.”

ref: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo m ŋuuri i ka i jina tieku n ni asiɛn n su, n ayɛ i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su akere aŋgoro,

ref: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
text: na wɔ tan Nyɛmɛ bo i la ŋgɔɔ na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, yiri bo i yiiri aŋgoro, ni asiɛn, ni tieku, ni deke kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni, na wɔ se ka, “Nyɛmɛ ŋminda má kun.

ref: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
text: Ama, saŋga n bo mɛrɛkɛ nso su n ba sukan i awiɛ n ye Nyɛmɛ asiri nu jɔrɛ ni yó yii­wa, kabo i yo seeri i akɔɔm bo bu ti i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni.”

ref: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
text: Na akonvi n bo i jɔjɔɔri m dɔ aŋgoro n nu n ajɔjɔ m dɔ bekun, i ŋa, “Kɔ de kadasi kaan n bo wɔ teke na i wo mɛrɛkɛ n bo i jina tieku n ni asiɛn n su n saa nu.”

ref: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
text: Na ma kɔ mɛrɛkɛ n dɔ, na ma se i ka, “Fa kadasi kaan n ma m.” Na wɔ se m ka, “De na a kpɛsɛ i, i yó u nɔɔ nu mamari ka wiɛ wɔ, ama, i to u kunnu, i yó wiwi wɔ.”

ref: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
text: Na ma de kadasi kaan n mɛrɛkɛ n dɔ, na ma kpɛsɛ i. I ti mamari m nɔɔ nu ka wiɛ wɔ, m miiri na wɔ yo wiwi m kunnu.

ref: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Kaaki jɔjɔ sam bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka bu bá tu minɛ barasu dɔŋgu, ni daanfiɛ barasu dɔŋgu, ni nfɛmɛm dɔŋgu.”

ref: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
text: Ama, má a sunsun awɔrɛ n bo i bara yɛɛri Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n kɛrɛ na ba ta kobe abara ayɛ i n buka su, dama ba fa nyini fofoe n ama minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, na bu náti saaki Nyɛmɛ miɛ casi ni, sara abura­na ni nnyɔ afiɛn.

ref: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
text: Daani­di­fɔ nnyɔm ni, bɛrɛ la ŋguin­mbaa baka nnyɔm, ni fitana takaa­biri nnyɔm bo bu jina Nyɛmɛ bo i dɛɛ la asiɛn nyunu ni.

ref: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
text: Bu la yiko bo bu koro kanda nzue aŋgoro na má nzue tɔ, saŋga bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan su ni, na bu la yiko bo bu koro ma nzuem kaaki mbunja, na bu kaaki la yiko bo bu koro ma alɔɔba tɔ durunya n nu, saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu koro.

ref: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
text: Nzɛn bu ba diiri bu daani kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n wieeri, na boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo i fite kunma n bo i kɔ ŋgoŋgo na i la má awieeri ni kún luɛ ni bɛrɛ, na i kúnma bu, na i kún bu.

ref: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
text: Na bu fuin ká da miɛ bambaka n atin afiɛn. Nyini miɛ n nu­fɔm sa­yoo­wa ti tiɛ wɔ, nyini ti ye bu fa bu sunsun Sodom­fɔm wara Ijipiti­fɔm n a. Nyini miɛ n nu ye minɛm kpamaari bu Mibiɛ baka jaanu n su n a.

ref: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
text: Bu fuin ni ká da bɛrɛ le­san ni wiɛ sin, na minɛm, ni daanfiɛm, ni aniɛm, ni asiɛnm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu, bu nu nbem sín na bu niɛ bu, na bu dú má su na be se bu.

ref: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
text: Bu yiwee n dɛɛ ti, durunya n nu­fɔm síri taari, na bu gɔ́ agɔrɛ, na bu cé bu beŋgum aceerim, dama nyini Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nnyɔm bo ba wu n akere bu kɛrɛ wuwuruwa.

ref: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
text: Le­san ni wiɛ sin n dɛɛ sin, na Nyɛmɛ ayi i ŋumi bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n agɔ bu ŋu, na ba yasu ajina, na sɛrɛ ati sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo bu ŋuuri bu ni.

ref: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
text: Na bɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nnyɔm n ati akonvi bambaka be ka i jɔjɔ aŋgoro nu, i ŋa, “Am fun bra aŋgoro fɛn.” Na ba wura yinyiŋgu n nu akɔ aŋgoro, bu kpɔ­fɔm jina suniɛ bu.

ref: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
text: Jina n nu bɛrɛ na asiɛn akpisi faŋga su, na miɛ bambaka n kpaaki nu buru buekun afufu, na wɔ kun minɛ akpii nso, na sɛrɛ ati minɛm bo bu kaari ni, na bu sukansi Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni.

ref: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
text: Tɔ. Na nnyɔ su dɛɛ ahoresaaki n gusu asin, bo i tɔ su nsan su ni suba kisa. Awiɛ nso su n jɔrɛ

ref: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nso su n akan i awiɛ ni, na akonvim atiɛn faŋga su aŋgoro n nu, bu ŋa, “Durunya n nu fɛmɛya kɛrɛ faŋga ti ya Micɛra ni Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n dɛɛ wɔ. I dɛɛ fɛmɛya ni wó bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a.”

ref: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
text: Bu ŋa, “Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Kpiri bo a la má aŋusu ni, wɔrɔ bo a wo bɛrɛ na a yo woori bɛrɛ ni, ya da u asi, dama a de u yiko bambaka n ye a suniɛ durunya n kɛrɛ su a.

ref: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
text: Durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ afa yaa, ye saŋga aju bo u gusu ahore ayasu, na a dí minɛm bo ba wu n jɔrɛ. Na saŋga n akaaki aju bo a tɔ́ u akɔɔm ŋkatɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, ni u minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu su u ni, nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam kɛrɛ. Ye saŋga n akaaki aju bo a sáaki bɛrɛ n bo bu saaki durunya ni.”

ref: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n bo i wo aŋgoro n ateke, na ma ŋu Nyɛmɛ alaka n bo i kereeri ka wɔ fa sɛsɛ su adi i di n ka i wo dɔ, na nzue sukpɛsɛ na i sutiɛntiɛn, na i suyo naŋmiɛ, na asiɛn sukpisi, na nzue yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbiri sutɔtɔ.

ref: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
text: I bita akunnu, na baa­wu yaari afa i na i subo awie.

ref: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
text: Na alekutura sa be gusu ayo aŋgoro dɔ, m ŋuuri woo tiɛ kɔkɔrɛ bambaka be, i tii ti nso wɔ, na i la mbiɛ buru, fɛmɛya kere nso butu atii nso kun kun n kɛrɛ su.

ref: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
text: Na wɔ fa i lɔ abaandi asara­mbaam kpaaki nu nsan buekun aji asiɛŋgu. Na wɔ ba ajina bara n nyunu, adena i wuuri baa n na i kaba fa baa n mi.

ref: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
text: Na bara n awu baa­biɛsɔ, bo i bá niɛ daanfiɛ daanfiɛm kɛrɛ su, na i bíta bu kekereke kabo naŋkasi fa bulaari kɔrɔma tu bo i naanim barasu ni. Na ba fa baa n akɔ yiri Nyɛmɛ ni, i fɛmɛya tanaa­biri n dɔ.

ref: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
text: Na bara n aŋmati akɔ boro kpantain awɔrɛ be nu, bo Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ i su ama i na bu suniɛ i dika haari kɔ ju cɛɛn akpii kun ni yaa nnyɔ ni abura­sɛn.

ref: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
text: Tɔ, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ Mikali ni i jamam, ni woo tiɛ ni i dɛɛ jamam sukun luɛ ni bu beŋgu aŋgoro.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
text: Na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga ka i sutiɛn faŋga su aŋgoro. I ŋa, “Kisa, ya ŋu kabo ya Nyɛmɛ n la faŋga na i de i minɛm tii ji, na i di i fɛmɛya ni. Kisa, ye Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n akere i yiko a, dama ba fɔn ayi Sitana aŋgoro aji durunya n nu fɛn, yiri bo i ti saaki­fɔ na i jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu na i jaraki ya ninbaam kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu ni.

ref: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
text: Bɔmbaa n dɛɛ mbunja, ni bu daani dii­wa ye bu fa kunmaari i a, ba soro má bu yiwee, bu soroori su wɔ, ka bu wu.

ref: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
text: Nyini ti, aŋgoro ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am wo aŋgoro ni, am ma am ahore jɔ, dama Sitana la má am faŋga kun. Ama, ahoresaaki bá tɔ asiɛn n ni tieku n su, dama Sitana ajura am dɔ. Yaa ti i agaya, dama i si ka i saŋga sunnu má kun.”

ref: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
text: Saŋga bo woo tiɛ n ŋu ka ba yi i aŋgoro aji asiɛŋgu ni, na wɔ nya ŋmati afɔn bara n bo i wuuri baa­biɛsɔ n ka i tara i.

ref: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
text: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ama bara n kajiri bambaka laŋkpɛtɛ nnyɔ, adena i tu kɔ boro kpantain n awɔrɛ n bo wɔ sɛsɛ su ama i ni, na i niɛ i dika afɔ nsan ni kpaaki nu, na má woo tiɛ n tu i.

ref: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
text: Na woo tiɛ n apuuti nzue afite i nɔɔ nu ka wɔmbu nzue dɛɛ su, na má i sɔ bara n kɔ.

ref: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
text: Woo tiɛ n atu má bara n dɛɛ ti, na yaa ati i agaya, na wɔ kaaki ka i kɔ kun luɛ ni bara n busu nu­fɔm, bo ba de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n na bu di daani ni Yesu labari kpa n ni.

ref: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
text: Na woo tiɛ n akɔ ajina tieku n nɔɔ su.

ref: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
text: Na ma ŋu boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ be ka i sufite tieku n nu, i la atii nso wɔ, na i mbiɛ ti buru, mbiɛ n kɛrɛ butu fɛmɛya kere, ye duma kɛrɛɛ­wa be bo i kpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ n wo atii kun kun n kɛrɛ su.

ref: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
text: Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo m ŋuuri i n ti ka awɔba a, ama, i jaa ti bambaka, na i la faŋga, na i nɔɔ ti ka jara dɛɛ. Woo tiɛ n afa i dɛɛ yiko, ni i fɛmɛya, ni i faŋga afa ama i.

ref: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
text: Na ma ŋu ka ba kpiɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n kun tii, i ti ka ba kpiɛ i kɔm wɔ, ama, kani n awie, na wɔ yo durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ alekutura na bu susu i su.

ref: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
text: Na bu kɛrɛ sukutu na bu su woo tiɛ ni, dama wɔ fa i yiko ama boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ ni, na bu sukutu na bu su boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n gusu, na bu suse ka, “Ŋma ti ka boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n a? Ŋma ni yiri koro kun luɛ na i kunma i a?”

ref: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
text: Na i jɔjɔ sukpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ, na i susaaki i duma ni i dika ni, ni bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ dika n nu aŋgoro n kɛrɛ.

ref: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
text: Na wɔ nya yiko ka i kun luɛ ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm na i kunma bu, na wɔ kaaki anya yiko na i niɛ adamande kɛrɛ, ni daanfiɛ kɛrɛ, ni aniɛm kɛrɛ, ni asiɛn barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu.

ref: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
text: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni su i wɔ, má ka minɛm n bo ba du mɔ akɛrɛ bu duma agɔ ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu, na durunya n boori i bu ni. Kadasi n ti Bɔmbaa n bo ba kpiɛ i kɔm n dɛɛ wɔ.

ref: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
text: “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka bu wura i dan­saraka, bu wúra i wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka bu kun i ni takobe, bu kún i wɔ.” Nyini dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm fa sawɔrɛ ni mindi ni yarada na bu fa su Nyɛmɛ.

ref: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
text: Na ma ŋu boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n be bekun ka i fite asiɛn n nu, i la mbiɛm nnyɔ, bu ti kabo Bɔmbaa dɛɛ ti n wɔ, ama, i jɔjɔ kabo woo tiɛ n jɔjɔ n wɔ.

ref: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
text: I jina bumboori dɛɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n jinaa­biri wɔ, na i la i yiko n kɛrɛ, na i sumiɛ durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ka bu su bumboori dɛɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ bo ba kpiɛ i tii ni, i ti ka ba kpiɛ i kɔm wɔ, ama, kani n awie.

ref: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
text: Na boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ nnyɔ su n ayo alekutura sam beberebe, na i ma sin fiteeri aŋgoro ba tɔɔri asiɛŋgu wɔ, na minɛm aŋu i.

ref: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
text: Alekutura sam n bo i nya yiko yoori bu bumboori dɛɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nyunu n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ dadaka durunya n nu­fɔm. Na wɔ se bu ka bu ta amɔɛ na i ferema ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo bu faari takobe fɔndɔɔri i ni, ama, i toko la ŋgɔɔ.

ref: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
text: Na boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ nnyɔ su n anya atin, na wɔ ma amɔɛ ŋumi, na wɔ nya ŋgɔɔ na i koro jɔjɔ na i koro kun minɛm kɛrɛ bo ba kete ka bu su má i ni.

ref: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
text: Na i sumiɛ minɛ barasu kɛrɛ, nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam, neŋge­fɔm ni yaari­fɔm, minɛm bo ba de bu tii, ni akɔɔm, na wɔ ma ba se bu nyaari bu saa fɔmbɔrɔ wara bu ŋuma su.

ref: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la má nyini nyaari see­wa n be, koro tɔ má deke be na i kaaki koro to má deke be gusu, dama nyini nyaari see­wa n ti boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n duma, wara i duma lamba.

ref: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
text: Sɔnɔ koro si má boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ lamba n bu, má ka i mi la alecira, i ti adamande duma a. I lamba n ti yaa nsɛn ni abura­sɛn ni nsɛn wɔ.

ref: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
text: Na ma ti ka minɛ nbem sudi jue aŋgoro, i yo naŋmiɛ kabo nzue soŋgi na i yo naŋmiɛ n wɔ, na i yo kabo nzue tiɛntiɛn na i yo naŋmiɛ ni, na i sun kabo bu bo biɛku na i sun barasu ni.

ref: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
text: Na minɛ akpii yaa ni akpii abura­na ni nnam n jina fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu, ni neŋge ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam ni, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nyunu, na bu sudi jue fɔfɔrɛ. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro sɔnsɔn i, má ka bɛrɛ ŋgumi bo Nyɛmɛ ade bu afite durunya n nu­fɔm n nu ni.

ref: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
text: Bɛrɛ la minɛm n bo bu ni bara be ada má, na bu ti casi ni. Bɛrɛ la bo deke kɛrɛ bo Bɔmbaa n kɔ na bu su i su ni. Bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ ade bu tii aji afite durunya n nu­fɔm nu n a, na ba yo i ni Bɔmbaa n dɛɛ ciciri su minɛm.

ref: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
text: Kɔrɛ be mmɔɔ wo má bu nɔɔ nu. Na bu kaaki la má ŋgasi be. Mɛrɛkɛ nsanm jɔrɛ

ref: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
text: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ be bekun ka i wo tu su aŋgoro, na i sukan labari kpa n bo i la má awieeri n jɔrɛ kere adamande kɛrɛ, ni daanfiɛ kɛrɛ, ni aniɛm kɛrɛ, ni asiɛn barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni.

ref: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ayɛ i konvi su atiɛn faŋga su agaya ase ka, “Am soro Nyɛmɛ na am wura i kpiri, dama saŋga n aju bo i dí minɛm jɔrɛ. Am su Nyɛmɛ bo i yiiri aŋgoro ni asiɛn, ni tieku ni nzue ni.”

ref: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnyɔ su n afite asu ciciri dɛɛ n su, na i suse ka, “Wɔ kpaasi, Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n akpaasi, yiri bo i maari durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ nuuri i nzan n na bu yoori sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn ni.”

ref: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsan su n afite agɔ ciciri dɛɛm su. Na i sujɔjɔ faŋga su suse ka, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i suuri boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni i amɔɛ ni, na wɔ se bu ŋuma su wara bu saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n nyaari,

ref: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
text: Nyɛmɛ fá yaa bu ŋu, na i cín bu sui agaya, na i má sin ni ferewita tɔ bu ŋu na bu dí wahara agaya, i mɛrɛkɛ casim n ni Bɔmbaa n nyunu.

ref: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
text: Na ŋmisini sufite nyini sin n bo i kere bu wahara n nu kɔ aŋgoro saŋga kɛrɛ, i la má awieeri. Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu suuri boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni i amɔɛ n na wɔ se bu nyaari ni, bu nya má ŋumi­yi, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ.”

ref: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
text: Nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm la mindi, bɛrɛ bo bu de i nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, na bu bita bu yarada n kenken fa kɔ Yesu dɔ ni.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
text: Na ma ti ka akonvi be sujɔjɔ aŋgoro suse m ka m kɛrɛ ka, “Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm n bo bu suuri Mibiɛ n na bu wuuri n dɔ fite kisa fa kɔ.” Na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n asoro su ka, “I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo ba wu ni, bu yí bu ŋumi, bu ŋkatɔ ye bu sudi i n a, dama bu sakpa­yo su bu su a.” Kabo Nyɛmɛ dí durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ jɔrɛ ni

ref: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
text: Na ma niɛ aŋu yinyiŋgu fofoe be aŋgoro n su, be tana su, i ferema ni adamande wɔ, i butu sika fɛmɛya kere, i bita gɔrɔku nana i saa nu.

ref: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
text: Na yiri n bo i tana yinyiŋgu n su n afa i gɔrɔku n ada awura durunya n nu na wɔ kpiɛ alɛ­mbaa ni.

ref: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n be bekun afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru bo i wo aŋgoro n nu, i gusu bita gɔrɔku nana be.

ref: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n be bekun bo i niɛ sin su afite Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n dika, na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su ase nyini mɛrɛkɛ n bo i bita gɔrɔku n ka, “Fa u gɔrɔku n fa kpiɛ durunya n nu baka suu­wam gɔ, dama baka suu­wa n aboro.”

ref: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n afa i gɔrɔku n ada awura durunya n nu akpiɛ baka suu­wam n kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ agɔ kunma bambaka n bo bu miɛmiɛ yiiri baka suu­wa nzue n nu ni. Nyini kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ahore­mbaa ayasu na i cin minɛm sui.

ref: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
text: Na ma ŋu alekutura sa bambaka be aŋgoro, ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ nso nbem bita kekereke barasu nso n bo bu ti awieeri su dɛɛ ni. Nyini kekereke n dɛɛ sin, Nyɛmɛ fá má yaa kun.

ref: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
text: Na ma ŋu deke be, i ti ka nzue bambaka a, i je ka niɛɛnu wɔ, wɔ saŋga ni sin. Na ma ŋu minɛm bo ba kunma boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni, ba kɔ ayaki i ni i amɔɛ n ni i duma lamba. Bu jina tieku n bo i je ka niɛɛnu n su, bu bita biɛkum n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama bu ni.

ref: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
text: Na ba di Nyɛmɛ akɔɔ Moyisi ni Bɔmbaa n dɛɛ jue, ka, “Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri, wɔrɔ ti adamande kɛrɛ fɛmɛ durunya n nu a. U sa­yoo­wa ti bambaka na i ti alekutura. U sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ kɔ atin su wɔ, na i ti nahɔrɛ.

ref: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
text: Micɛra, ŋma wo bɛrɛ bo i soro má u a, na i wura má u kpiri a? Dama wɔrɔ ŋgumi ti casi a. Adamande kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu, bu bá kutu u na bu kánsi u, dama u nahɔrɛ sam n afite fofoe nu.”

ref: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
text: Nyini sin, na ma ŋu Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue n nu, na i wo aŋgoro n ka bu suteke i.

ref: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsom n bo bu bita kekereke nsom n afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, bu wura sutura fofoe papasi, ba fa sika dambara abo bu hoe su.

ref: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
text: Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam nu kun, afa sika ayabam n nso afa ama Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ nsom n ni. Ayabam n ayi ni Nyɛmɛ ahore­mbaa yasu, bo i kere ka Nyɛmɛ bo i la ŋgɔɔ na i la má awieeri n afa yaa agaya.

ref: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ yiko ni i jirima woo­wa n ama Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n ayi ni ŋmisini, bo be akoro awura má awuru n nu, fa kɔ ju saŋga bo mɛrɛkɛ nsom fa kekereke nsom n fa jiiri durunya n nu n kɔ wieeri.

ref: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
text: Na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, i sujɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛ nsom dɔ, i ŋa, “Am kɔ na am kɔ kaaki Nyɛmɛ ahore yasu ayaba nsom n gɔ durunya n nu.”

ref: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ ciciri su n akɔ akaaki i ayaba n agɔ durunya n nu, na kani tiɛm, bo bu yo ya agaya, akpiɛ minɛm bo bu la boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nyaari see­wa n na bu su i amɔɛ n ni.

ref: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnyɔ su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ tieku nzue n nu, na tieku nzue n akaaki ka sɔnɔ bo wɔ wu mbunja. Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ kɛrɛ bo bu wo tieku n nu awu.

ref: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsan su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ kunmam nzue ni buram nzue nu, na i kɛrɛ akaaki mbunja.

ref: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
text: Na ma ti ka mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ nzue n su n ase ka, “Nyɛmɛ, wɔrɔ ti casi a, wɔrɔ bo a wo bɛrɛ na a yo woori bɛrɛ ni, kabo a cin minɛm sui sɔ ni, u sa­yoo­wa akɔ atin su.

ref: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
text: Dama ba kun u dɛɛ minɛm, ni u nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm na bu mbunja afite, ye u gusu ama bu nzuem n akaaki mbunja na bu nu. I ti ye ni ka a yo bu sɔ ɔ.”

ref: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nna su n akaaki i ayaba, n agɔ wiɛ n ŋu, na Nyɛmɛ ama i kɛkɛrɛ agaya suyara minɛm ka sin dɛɛ su.

ref: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
text: Na sin n kɛkɛrɛ ayara minɛm, na ba kpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ, yiri bo i la yiko na i ma bu di nyini wahara ni, na ba kete ka bu kaaki má bu wo na bu ma i jirima.

ref: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnu su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n fɛmɛya biɛ n su, na i fɛmɛya asiɛn n kɛrɛ asi biri didiki. Na minɛm sukan bu saa, yaari n ti.

ref: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
text: Kambue n ni yaari n bo i wo bu ŋu n dɛɛ ti, ye bu sukpiɛ Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni nzukɔ a, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ba kaaki má bu wo na bu yaki bu satiɛ ni.

ref: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsɛn su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ kunma kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Yufarati n su, na nzue n kɛrɛ anu, na wɔ kpiɛ atin ama nfɛmɛm bo bu fite wie afiiri ni.

ref: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
text: Na ma ŋu awiɛwiɛ tiɛ nsan nbem, bu ti ka pɔntirim wɔ, kun sufite woo tiɛ n nɔɔ nu, kun sufite boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nɔɔ nu, na kun gusu sufite sɔnɔ bo wɔ yi i ŋu ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n nɔɔ nu.

ref: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
text: Bu ti Sitana dɛɛ awiɛwiɛ barasu nbem wɔ, na bu suyo alekutura sam, na bu kɔ durunya n nu nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ dɔ kɔ tiɛn bu nɔɔ, na bu cici luɛ ŋminda ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri n jina cɛɛn.

ref: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
text: Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, “Am ti fɛn, m ba ni yó am fura nu ka awiefɔ dɛɛ su wɔ, tere­fiɛ wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋminda fa kɔ m ba n dɛɛ su, i fa má i ŋu ti má, na i wura i sutura, adena má i nati i bu ŋgbɛɛn, na má minɛ ŋu i, na nyinsɛ ti i.”

ref: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
text: Na Sitana awiɛwiɛm n atiɛn nfɛmɛm nɔɔ dika n bo bu fere i Hiburu aniɛ nu ka, Amagɛdon n ni.

ref: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ nso su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ aŋuma n nu. Na akonvi bambaka be ajɔjɔ afite fɛmɛya biɛ n nu, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, i ŋa, “Wɔ yo awie.”

ref: There were flashes of lightning, roars of thunder, and the worst earthquake in all history.
text: Na nzue sukpɛkpɛsɛ, na i sutiɛntiɛn, na i suyo naŋmiɛ, na asiɛn sukpisi faŋga su. Kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri adamande ni, be nya ŋu má nyini asiɛn kpisii­wa n ŋusu sɔ.

ref: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
text: Na miɛ kpiri n ati nu nsan, na miɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu n atɔtɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n jɔrɛ su, nyini ti ye wɔ fa yaa i ŋu, sucin i sui n a.

ref: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
text: Na lui bambakam kɛrɛ bo bu da nzue afiɛn, ni bukam kɛrɛ amini.

ref: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ nsom n nu kun, bo i bita ayaba nsom n nu kun, aba m dɔ, aba ase m ka, “Bra fɛn, na m kére u kabo Nyɛmɛ cín bara sakara tiɛ n sui ni, yiri bo i tana kunma nzue beberebem n su ni.

ref: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
text: Yiri ye durunya n nu nfɛmɛm akpini i afa ayo bu soma, na wɔ fa i sakaraya asa durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ, na bu gusu akpini i afa ayo bu soma.”

ref: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
text: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu, na mɛrɛkɛ n afa m akɔ boro kpantain nu. Na ma ŋu bara be ka i tana boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ kɔkɔrɛ be su, duma kɛrɛɛ­wa nbem bo bu kpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ n wo boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ su. I la atii nso, na i mbiɛ ti buru.

ref: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
text: Bara n adi suŋguruya anyɔnyɔndi i ŋu, i wura sutura kɔkɔrɛ nyunyumi, na wɔ wura i kɔm, ni i saam, ni i suim jɔrɔkɔm, ni lalankundim, ni afere nyunyumim, ni sikam, ni afere fofoem bo bu la gɔ agaya. I bita sika kɔpu i saa nu, wɔ yi ni daŋgba ni i sa­yoo­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn n wɔ.

ref: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
text: Ba kɛrɛ i asiri nu duma i ŋuma su, nyini duma n bu la ka, Babilɔn miɛ kpiri, bo i ti sakaram kɛrɛ ni, na i kaaki ti saa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn yo­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu n ni.

ref: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
text: Na ma ŋu ka bara n anu Nyɛmɛ minɛm mbunja abo, ni minɛm bo bu di Yesu daani n dɛɛ ti na ba kun bu ni gusu mbunja abo. M ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na wɔ yo m alekutura.

ref: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n abisa m ka, “Nzu ti ye a ma wɔ yo u alekutura? M kére u asiri nu jɔrɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ fa kɔ bara n ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo bara n tana su, na i la atii nso ni mbiɛm buru ni.

ref: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
text: Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo a ŋuuri i ni, ti deke bo i yo woori bɛrɛ wɔ, ama, kisa i wo má bɛrɛ kun. Wɔ ka kaan na i fíte kunma ŋgoŋgo n bo i la má awieeri n nu, na i kɔ́ di wahara na i ká ŋgbɛɛn. Na durunya n nu­fɔm bo bu duma wo má ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu, fite saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ nya yi má durunya ni, bu ba ŋuuri i, i yó bu alekutura, dama i yo woori bɛrɛ, ye kisa, i wo má bɛrɛ kun, na i bá kaaki ba cɛɛn be.

ref: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ fa i alecira ni sunsunni na i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu. Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n tii nso n ti buka nso bo bara n afun atana su wɔ. Bu gusu kaaki ti nfɛmɛm nso wɔ.

ref: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
text: Bu nu nnu akpaasi, nsɛn su n toko wo bɛrɛ, nso su n nya ba má, ama, i ba baari, i cɛ má, i fɛmɛya ni kpáasi wɔ.

ref: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
text: Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo i yo woori bɛrɛ, ama, kisa i wo má bɛrɛ kun ni, yiri ti fɛmɛ n bo i buka su mɔcuɛ su n a, ama, i ti nfɛmɛ nsom nu kun wɔ, na i kɔ́ di wahara na i ká ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
text: Mbiɛ burum bo a ŋuuri bu n ti nfɛmɛ burum bo bu nya di má fɛmɛya, ama, bu nyá yiko na bu dí fɛmɛ buka boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n su saŋga kaan sɔ.

ref: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
text: Nyini nfɛmɛ burum n yó bu nɔɔkun, na bu fá bu faŋga ni bu yiko kɛrɛ na bu fá ma boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ ni.

ref: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
text: Bu kún luɛ ni Nyɛmɛ Bɔmbaa ni, ama, i ni i minɛm ni kúnma bu, dama yiri ti mibiɛm kɛrɛ Mibiɛ, na i ti nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ Fɛmɛ. I minɛm bo bu wo i dɔ n la bo wɔ fere bu ni, ni bo wɔ yi bu na bu jina i sin nahɔrɛ su ni.”

ref: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
text: Mbiɛ burum bo a ŋuuri bu ni, ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ ni, bu bá kpɔ bara sakara ni, bu dé i neŋge kɛrɛ na i ká i bu ŋgbɛɛn, na bu dí i ŋuniɛ­mbaa kɛrɛ, na bu yára bo wɔ ka n sin.

ref: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
text: Dama Nyɛmɛ maari bu yoori kabo i koro a. I maari bu yoori bu nɔɔkun na bu fa bu fɛmɛya n kɛrɛ buka boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ, na i dí fɛmɛya n kɔ ju saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni bá yo yii­wa ni.

ref: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
text: Bara n bo a ŋuuri i n la miɛ kpiri n bo i ti durunya n nu nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ tii n a.”

ref: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
text: Nyini sin, na ma kaaki aŋu mɛrɛkɛ be ka i fite aŋgoro sujura, i la yiko agaya, na i woo­wa n ata wein abara ayɛ durunya n kɛrɛ.

ref: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
text: Na wɔ yɛ i konvi su atiɛn faŋga su ka, “Wɔ kpaasi. Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n akpaasi. Wɔ kaaki awiɛwiɛ tiɛm, ni ajinim, ni deke ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm, ni anuma tiɛm woo­biri wɔ.

ref: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
text: Dama durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ nuuri i nzan ye bu kɔɔri somaya yiri dɔ a. Na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ kɔ kpiniiri yiri yoori bu soma. Na kabo i la ajɛkɛ na i koro tara má i ŋu ni, durunya n nu wata­fɔm gusu nya bu ajɛkɛ fite i dɔ ɔ.”

ref: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
text: Na ma kaaki ati akonvi be ŋga bekun, ka i sujɔjɔ aŋgoro n nu, i ŋa, “M minɛm, am fite miɛ n nu, na má am yo bu satiɛ yoo­wa n be, na má wahara n bo i suba bu dɔ n tu am.

ref: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
text: Dama miɛ n nu­fɔm satiɛ yoo­wa n aju Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro, na i wara koro fi má bu satiɛ yoo­wa n sa.

ref: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
text: Sam bo bara n yoori bu ni, yo i gusu sɔ, tɔ i kaari i ŋusu nnyɔ. Ma i ŋu kekereke tara kabo i yo ma minɛm ŋuuri kekereke ni.

ref: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
text: Kabo i yo wuraari i ŋu kpiri na i diiri i tii barasu ni, kere i kekereke na i ahore gusu saaki sɔ. Dama i ŋa, ‘Ma di fɛmɛ, m ti má kuna­fɔ, m ahore sáaki má cɛɛn be.’

ref: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
text: Nyini dɛɛ ti, le­kun ye kekereke bá tɔ i ŋu a. Yiri la tukpaki, ni ahoresaaki, ni ahɔɛ. Na sin yára i, dama Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n bo i cin i sui n la yiko agaya.”

ref: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
text: Na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm bo bu kɔ kpiniiri i na bu ni yiri diiri bu tii ni, bu ŋu kabo sin je i, na bu ŋu kabo ŋmisini yasu kɔ aŋgoro barasu, na bu sún, na bu bó awie.

ref: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
text: Kekereke n bo wɔ tu i n dɛɛ ti, i ma bu sɛrɛ wɔ, na bu jina dede, na bu nya suse ka, “Keto, keto, am niɛ ahin miɛ bambaka ni. Babilɔn bo i ti miɛ kpiri n lɛɛ ye ba kaba acin i sui, ye i kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn, anyumbaa kata ni i teke n nu bɛrɛ n a.”

ref: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
text: Na wata­fɔm bo bu wo durunya n nu ni susun, na bu sukoko miɛ ni, dama be to má bu gɔɔri n kun.

ref: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
text: Be kaaki to má bu toro neŋge barasum bo bu la nva­nvani, ni ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani, ni baka nyunvuin nva­nvani­fɔ, ni nzan, ni ŋguin, ni kpɔnɔ samɛnɛ, ni diire, ni naanim, ni nbɔɛm, ni kpɔŋgɔm, ni kpɔŋgɔ torokum, ni akɔɔm, ni domuɛm.

ref: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
text: Na wata­fɔm n ase i ka, “Dekem bo a yo koro bu agaya n kɛrɛ aŋmati ayaki u, u ajɛkɛm, ni u neŋge kpakpam kɛrɛ bo a la bu akɔ ayaki u, a nyá má i be kun.”

ref: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
text: Wata­fɔm bo bu yoori neŋge­fɔm nyini miɛ n dɛɛ ti ni, bu kɔ́ jina dede wɔ, dama sɛrɛ ti bu fa kɔ kekereke n bo wɔ tɔ miɛ n nu ni, na bu ahore saaki agaya na bu sun.

ref: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
text: Na bu se ka, “Keto, keto, am niɛ ahin miɛ bambaka ni. I yo kara sutura barasu barasu nyunyumi, na i la sika jɔrɔkɔm, ni aferem barasu barasu bo bu yo nyumi na bu la gɔ agaya ni.

ref: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
text: Nyini neŋge n kɛrɛ akaba aka ŋgbɛɛn, anyumbaa kata ni i teke n nu bɛrɛ n a.” Na lee bambaka kan­fɔm, ni lee­fɔm, ni lee wura­fɔm, ni bo bu juma nati tieku nzue n su n kɛrɛ akɔ ajina dede.

ref: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
text: Na bu susasa tuturi gɔ bu tii su na bu kere ka bu ahore asaaki agaya, na bu susun, na bu suse ka, “Keto, keto, am niɛ ahin miɛ bambaka n ni. Dama minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu la lee bambakam na bu juma nati tieku nzue n su n nya bu neŋgem fite nyini miɛ n dɛɛ ajɛkɛ n nu wɔ. Ye wɔ kaba aka ŋgbɛɛn, anyumbaa kata ni i teke n nu bɛrɛ n a.

ref: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ faŋga­fɔ be n afa yabuɛ bambaka, na wɔ fa atu aji tieku nzue n nu, na wɔ se ka, “Sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ fá Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n fa ji wuum a, na i busu be bá ŋu má.

ref: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
text: Be ti má deke be ŋga miɛ n nu kun, i biɛku kan­fɔ o, i jue di­fɔ o, i tere kan­fɔ o, ni awiɛ kan­fɔm. Na juma­yo­fɔ barasu be mmɔɔ gusu wo má miɛ n nu kun, be ti má ŋgani san yabuɛ be ŋga miɛ n nu kun.

ref: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
text: Na fitana haari kun n kpein je má miɛ n nu cɛɛn be na be ŋu i. Be ti má konya ni konyafan kuyigindi ŋga miɛ n nu kun, dama wata­fɔm bo bu yo wo nyini miɛ n nu yo ti nfaŋga­fɔm wɔ durunya n kɛrɛ nu, na miɛ nu­fɔm yo boboyaya sam dadaka durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ.

ref: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
text: Nyɛmɛ acin miɛ n nu­fɔm sui a, dama bɛrɛ yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ minɛm, ni i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a, ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo ba kun bu durunya n nu ni, bɛrɛ yiiri bu mbunja.”

ref: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
text: Nyini sin, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga aŋgoro, ka jama beberebe akonvi, bu ŋa, “Haleluya, ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, ya Mi Nyɛmɛ n ama ya nya tii­de a, yiri ti kpiri ni yiko­fɔ a.

ref: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
text: I di jɔrɛ ni i atin su, ni nahɔrɛ wɔ. Wɔ di bara sakara n jɔrɛ, yiri bo i maari durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ kpini nbaram na bu kɔ somaya na bu yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm kɛrɛ, na wɔ cin i sui, dama yiri yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm n a.”

ref: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
text: Na akonvi be afite fɛmɛya biɛ n nu, i ŋa, “Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm kɛrɛ, tarakam ni nfaŋga­fɔm, bo am soro Nyɛmɛ ni, am kɛrɛ kansi i duma.”

ref: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
text: Na ma ti akonvi be naŋmiɛ ŋga, i ti ka jama beberebe suyo naŋmiɛ wɔ, na i yo naŋmiɛ, kabo nzue soŋgi na i yo naŋmiɛ ni, na i kaaki ti kabo nzue tiɛntiɛn na i yo naŋmiɛ ni, i ŋa, “Haleluya, Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri n adi i fɛmɛya n kisa.

ref: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
text: Am ma ya ahore jɔ na ya yo ya kunnu koroo­wa, na ya wura i kpiri, dama saŋga n aju bo Bɔmbaa ni já i konya ni, konya n gusu asɛsɛ i ŋu su aŋminda i.

ref: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
text: Nyɛmɛ ama i sutura casi fofoe papasi ka i fa kara.” Sutura fofoe papasi n la Nyɛmɛ minɛm sakpa yoo­wam ni.

ref: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ase m ka m kɛrɛ ka, “Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo Bɔmbaa n abaandi bu ka bu ba di konyaya diire n be i dɔ.” Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, “Ahin nɔaniɛ ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.”

ref: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
text: Na ma kutu i jaa bu asiɛŋgu ka m su i, na wɔ se m ka má m yo sɔ, ama, m kansi Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi. Na wɔ kaaki ase m ka, “M ti u beŋgu akɔɔ, ni u ninbaam bo bu di Yesu daani kere minɛ n be wɔ.” Jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kaanri i ni, bu diiri ahin Yesu n daani wɔ. Sɔnɔ n bo i fun kpɔŋgɔ fofoe n jɔrɛ

ref: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
text: Na ma ŋu ka aŋgoro ateke, kpeŋga na ma ŋu kpɔŋgɔ fofoe be, bu fere bo i tana su n duma ka, Bo i la Dabuŋu ni Nahɔrɛ­fɔ, i di minɛm jɔrɛ kabo i ti ye ni ka i di bu nahɔrɛ su wɔ, na i kun i luɛ ni nahɔrɛ su.

ref: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
text: I nyu­mbaa kɛrɛ je ka sin fofoe, na i butu fɛmɛya kere beberebe i tii su. Duma kɛrɛɛ­wa be mata i, be si má nyini duma ni, má ka yiri ŋgumi si a.

ref: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
text: I wura sutura yorom barasu be bo ba fa i awura mbunja nu, na bu fere sɔnɔ n duma ka Nyɛmɛ Nɔaniɛ.

ref: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
text: Na Nyɛmɛ sojam bo bu wo aŋgoro n fun kpɔŋgɔ fofoe su i su, bu wura sutura casi fofoe papasi.

ref: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
text: Takobe nana bo i fá kunma durunya n nu­fɔm wo i nɔɔ nu, na i bíta bu kekereke kabo naŋkasi fa bulaari kɔrɔma tu bo i naanim barasu ni, na i tíɛntiɛn bu su kabo bu fa baka suu­wa gɔ kunma nu na bu miɛmiɛ yi i nzue ni, dama Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n afa yaa agaya.

ref: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
text: Ba kɛrɛ i duma i sutura n su, ni i jaa sɔɔwa su ka, “Nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ Fɛmɛ ni mibiɛm kɛrɛ Mibiɛ.”

ref: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
text: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ be ka i jina wiɛ n su, na wɔ yɛ i konvi su atiɛn faŋga su ase anumam kɛrɛ bo bu tu aŋgoro na bu di niɛ n ka, “Am bra na am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ na am di ahin diire beberebe n bo Nyɛmɛ sufa ma am ni,

ref: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
text: Na ma ŋu boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni durunya n nu nfɛmɛm, ni bu sojam, ka, ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ acici na bu kun luɛ ni yiri n bo i tana kpɔŋgɔ fofoe n su ni, ni i jama nbaam.

ref: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
text: Na ba tara boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni kɔrɛ­fɔ n bo i yo i ŋu ka i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni. Yiri yo yoori alekutura sam boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nyunu, na wɔ dadaka minɛm bo bu deeri boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n anyaari see­wa na ba su i amɔɛ ni. Na ba fa boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni kɔrɛ­fɔ n ni bu ŋgɔɔ aji sin n nu, bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni.

ref: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
text: Minɛm bo bu kaari ni, na yiri n bo i tana kpɔŋgɔ n su n afa takobe n bo i wo i nɔɔ nu n akun bu kɛrɛ. Na anumam kɛrɛ adi niɛ na bu kunnu ayi.

ref: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
text: Na ma ŋu fɛmɛya biɛm, Nyɛmɛ ama bo bu tana sum n atin ka bu di minɛm jɔrɛ. Na ma kaaki aŋu bɛrɛ bo bu yo wuuri Yesu ti ni. Minɛ yo kpiɛɛri bu tii a, dama bu yo kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ na ba di Yesu daani n dɛɛ ti. Bɛrɛ keteeri ka bu su má boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni i amɔɛ n a, bɛrɛ la bo bu kaaki keteeri ka bu de má i nyaari see­wa bu ŋuma wara bu saa su n a, ye ba teŋge bu yiwee n nu anya ŋgɔɔ, na bu ni Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n saŋga di fɛmɛya afɔ akpii.

ref: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
text: Bɛrɛ n bo bu du mɔ teŋgeeri bu yiwee nu ni, na bu ti minɛ casim n ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, bɛrɛ ŋú má yiwee nnyɔ su n kun, bɛrɛ yó Nyɛmɛ ni Kristo Tii­de­fɔ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm a. Na bu ni yiri sáŋga di fɛmɛya n afɔ akpii. Kabo Nyɛmɛ kunmaari Sitana ni

ref: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
text: Na i kɔ́ dadaka adamande barasu barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni, Gogi ni Magogi asiɛn n kɛrɛ, na i tíɛn bu kɛrɛ nɔɔ na bu kɔ́ kun luɛ. Bu sunnu beberebe ka kunma ŋmiɛ wɔ.

ref: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
text: Na ba tɛrɛki abara ayɛ durunya n deke kɛrɛ, na ba bara ayɛ miɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ koro i agaya ni, deke bo i minɛm wo ni, ama, na sin afite aŋgoro aba ayara bu.

ref: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
text: Na ba fa Sitana n bo i dadaka bu n aji cɛɛma sin n nu, bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni, deke bo bu faari boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni kɔrɛ­fɔ n bo i yo i ŋu ka i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n jiiri nu ni. Dɔ na bu dí wahara, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu saŋga kɛrɛ. Kabo Nyɛmɛ dí minɛm jɔrɛ awieeri su ni

ref: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
text: Na ma ŋu fɛmɛya biɛ bambaka fofoe be, ni bo i tana su ni. Na aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ amini i nyunu, bu wo má bɛrɛ kun.

ref: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
text: Na ma ŋu minɛm bo ba wu ni, nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam kɛrɛ, ka bu jina fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu. Na ma kaaki aŋu ka be suteke kadasim, na be gusu suteke kadasi bo i ti ŋgɔɔ dɛɛ ni. Nyini kadasim ye bu fa diiri minɛm bo bu wuuri n jɔrɛ a, bu sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ bo bu yo yoori wo nyini kadasim n nu.

ref: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
text: Na tieku n awɔrɔ minɛm bo ba wu n ama, na Yiwee, ni Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri n awɔrɔ minɛm bo ba wu n ama, na Nyɛmɛ afa bu sa­yoo­wa bo bu yo yoori adi bu jɔrɛ.

ref: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
text: Na wɔ fa Yiwee, ni Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri n aji sin n nu, bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni. Ahin sin ni ti yiwee nnyɔ su n a.

ref: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
text: Na ba fa sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i duma awo má ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu aji sin ni.

ref: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
text: Na ma ŋu aŋgoro fɔfɔrɛ ni asiɛn fɔfɔrɛ, dama aŋgoro dawa n ni asiɛn dawa n kɛrɛ wo má bɛrɛ kun, na ma ŋu ka tieku nzue n gusu wo má bɛrɛ kun.

ref: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
text: Na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga fɛmɛya biɛ n nu, i ŋa, “Am niɛ fɛn, Nyɛmɛ woo­biri wo adamandem dɔ, na i wó bu dɔ. Na i fá bu yo i minɛm. Yiri ni i tii wó bu dɔ na i yó bu Nyɛmɛ a.

ref: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
text: Yiri núnuka bu nyunvuin kɛrɛ bu nyu­mbaa nu a, yiwee wo má bɛrɛ kun, ahoresaaki wó má bɛrɛ kun, minɛm sún má kun, na minɛm dí má yaari kun, dama deke dawam kɛrɛ asin.”

ref: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
text: Na bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ase ka, “Niɛ fɛn, m suyo deke kɛrɛ fɔfɔrɛ.” Na wɔ kaaki ase m ka, “Kɛrɛ ahin nɔaniɛ ni, dama i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, na i la dabuŋu.”

ref: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, “Wɔ yo awie, mini ti mɔ­fɔ a, na m kaaki ti kasin­fɔ, mini ti deke kɛrɛ bumboori ni i awieeri a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo nzuehɔɛ tiiri i, m má i nu nzue n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni, na i mi tɔ́ má kaari.

ref: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri nasara, m má i ahin aceerim ni, na m yó i Nyɛmɛ, na m fá i yo m wa.

ref: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
text: Ama, sɛrɛ­fɔm, ni minɛm bo ba yo má m yarada, ni daŋgba yo­fɔm, ni minɛ kun­fɔm, ni nbara kpini­fɔm, ni somaya kɔ­fɔm, ni bo bu di ayiri ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, ni munzue­yi­fɔm, ni amɔɛ su­fɔm, ni kɔrɛ­fɔm kɛrɛ, bu woo­biri la cɛɛma sin n bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni. Nyini yiwee ni ti yiwee nnyɔ su n a.” Yɛrusalem fɔfɔrɛ n jɔrɛ

ref: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ kun aba m dɔ, i ti mɛrɛkɛ nsom bo bu bita awieeri su dɛɛ kekereke ayaba nsom nu kun wɔ, na wɔ ba ase m ka, “Bra, na m kere u Bɔmbaa n konya ni.”

ref: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
text: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu, na mɛrɛkɛ n afa m akɔ buka bambaka n bo i ti lenleŋge n tii su, na wɔ kere m Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n bo i ti casi ni, i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro sujura ba.

ref: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
text: Na i je na i ta wein kabo Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa n ti ni, na i sujeje kɔkɔrɛ ni sosofura nzue ka afere nyunyumi barasu be bo i da cain ka niɛɛnu.

ref: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Ba ta kobe bambaka bo i wo aŋgoro abara ayɛ miɛ ni, na i la anɔ buru ni nnyɔ, anɔm kɛrɛ su la mɛrɛkɛ kun kun bo bu jina ŋminda. Ba kɛrɛ Yisareli anɔ barasu buru ni nnyɔ n duma agɔ anɔ n kun kun kɛrɛ su.

ref: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
text: Kobe n bue nna n kɛrɛ la anɔ nsan nsan, nsan wo wiɛ afiiri, na nsan wo wiɛ atɔɔri, na nsan nsan wo gbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bue nnyɔ n kɛrɛ.

ref: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
text: Miɛ n kobe n ajaari see­wa yabuɛ n ti buru ni nnyɔ ɔ, ye bu nya taari kobe n taka su. Ba kɛrɛ Bɔmbaa n jaramasa buru ni nnyɔm duma agɔ yabuɛ n kun kun kɛrɛ su.

ref: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Bu faari yabuɛ nyunyumi barasu be bo bu fere i ka jasapa ni taari kobe n a, na ba fa sika bo i da cain ka niɛɛnu, ata miɛ n ni i tii.

ref: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Bu faari afere fofoe nyunyumi barasu be fa yoori anɔ buru ni nnyɔm n a. Bu faari sika fa yoori miɛ n atin natii­wa kɛrɛ a, na i da cain ka niɛɛnu.

ref: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
text: Ma ŋu má Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru be miɛ n nu, dama ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri n ni Bɔmbaa n ade Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n jinaa­biri a.

ref: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
text: Miɛ n koro má wiɛ ni sara na bu ta wein ma i, dama Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa ni ti i wein a, na Bɔmbaa n ti i fitana.

ref: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
text: Na durunya n nu adamande barasu kɛrɛ sunati i wein nu wɔ, na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm fa bu ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ ba wura nu.

ref: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
text: Miɛ n anɔ ni téke wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, dama ka kɔŋguɛ wo má dɔ.

ref: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
text: Ama, deke kɛrɛ bo i ti má casi wúra má miɛ n nu, ni minɛm bo bu su amɔɛm, ni minɛm bo bu bu kɔrɛm ni. Má ka minɛm bo bu duma wo Bɔmbaa ni ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu ni ŋgumi wúra nu a.

ref: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n akaaki akere m kunma nzue bo i ma ŋgɔɔ, i da cain kabo niɛɛnu ti n wɔ, i soŋgi fite Nyɛmɛ ni Bɔmbaa n fɛmɛya biɛ n nu wɔ,

ref: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
text: na i susoŋgi miɛ n atin n afiɛn ni. Kunma n bue nnyɔ n kɛrɛ, baka n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n jinjina ahɛmɛ. Na i susu suu­wa barasu buru ni nnyɔ afɔ kɛrɛ, sara kɛrɛ ni i suu­wa. Na bu sufa i nyawa n tuntun durunya n nu adamande kɛrɛ tɔrɛm.

ref: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
text: Deke bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i nɔɔ ŋgbɛɛn wó má miɛ n nu. Ama, Nyɛmɛ ni Bɔmbaa n fɛmɛya biɛ ni wó dɔ, na i akɔɔm n súsu i.

ref: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
text: Bu ŋú i nyunu­awa dika kabo i ti barasu, na i kɛ́rɛ i duma gɔ bu ŋuma su.

ref: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
text: Awosin wó má dɔ, be koro má fitana dɔ, na bu kaaki koro má wiɛ dɔ, dama ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ ni i tii yó bu wein a. Na bu dí fɛmɛya bo i la má awieeri. Kabo Yesu seeri ka i cɛ́ má, i káaki ba ni

ref: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
text: Na mɛrɛkɛ n akaaki ase m ka, “Ahin nɔaniɛm n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, na i ti deke bo sɔnɔ yo i yarada. Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bo i faari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wuraari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm nu ni, yiri asunma i mɛrɛkɛ n ka i ba se i akɔɔm fa kɔ sa bo i yó kisa bɛrɛ n a.”

ref: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
text: Mini Yohane tiiri, ye m ŋuuri ahin deke n kɛrɛ, m tiiri na m ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na ma kutu mɛrɛkɛ n bo i kereeri nyini dekem n jaa bu ka m su i.

ref: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
text: Na wɔ se m ka, má m yo sɔ, ama, m kansi Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi. Na wɔ kaaki ase m ka, “M ti u beŋgu akɔɔ, ni u ninbaam bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm, ni bo bu de nɔaniɛ n bo i wo ahin kadasi n nu n be wɔ.”

ref: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
text: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu ŋa, “Am so am sui. I cɛ́ má, m bá kisa, na m bá tɔ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kaari kabo i juma­yoo­wa ti barasu.

ref: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
text: Mini ti mɔ­fɔ a, na m kaaki ti kasin­fɔ, mini ti deke kɛrɛ bumboori ni i awieeri n a.”

ref: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
text: Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo ba ŋmisi bu suturam ayo casi n dɔ, adena bu koro nya atin di baka n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n dɛɛ suu­wa ni, na bu fa miɛ n anɔ n wura miɛ n nu.

ref: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
text: Ama, daŋgba yo­fɔm, ni bo bu di ayiri ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, ni bara kpini­fɔm, ni somaya kɔ­fɔm, ni minɛ kun­fɔm, ni amɔɛ su­fɔm, ni bo bu bu kɔrɛ bu nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ni bu sa yoori nu n kɛrɛ koro wura má miɛ n nu.

ref: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
text: “Mini Yesu asunma m mɛrɛkɛ n ka i ba se am ahin jɔrɛ n a, ambɛrɛ bo am wo Kristo jamam n nu ni. Mini ti Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a, na m ti asaakojo bo i ta ni.”

ref: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
text: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni konya n ŋa, “Bra.” Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti ahin, ma i mi gusu se ka, “Bra.” Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo nzuehɔɛ ati i, na i koro ŋgɔɔ nzue ni, i bra na i ba de nu ŋgbɛɛn. Awieeri su n jɔrɛ

ref: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
text: Mini Yohane subɔbɔ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti ahin kadasi n nu jɔrɛ fa kɔ sa n bo i bá yo n lɛɛ, ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari nɔaniɛ be bukaari su, Nyɛmɛ fá kekereke n kɛrɛ bo i wo ahin kadasi n nu n buka i mi su.

ref: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
text: Yiri n bo i di ahin jɔrɛm kɛrɛ daani n ase ka, “Nahɔrɛ. I cɛ́ má, m bá kisa.” Ma i yo sɔ. Ya Mibiɛ Yesu, bra. Ami.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
text: Ma ya Mibiɛ Yesu buka Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ. Ami.
